# Thread: San Francisco thermogenerator

### hellojtm — 2012-03-14T23:23:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/san-francisco-thermogenerator.1/post-1>

My name is Jesse and I live in San Francisco. I'm not quite 40 years old and have been immersing myself in diet/fitness for the past couple of years, although much more intensely since January 2011.

Definitely coming around to circadian/seasonal eating and living. Dr. Kruse is presenting the best thoughts on this subject that I have heard since reading Lights Out. My wheels started spinning the same way when I read that but Dr. Kruse has the knowledge and experience and also did the work to show the way. Looking forward to exchanging notes on here.

### Enkidu — 2012-03-14T23:30:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.2/post-5>

Hiyas, I'm Michael from Texas. I'm currently struggling to get everything to fit into Dr Kruse's leptin rx with CT.

Currently I'm eating strict auto-immune flavored paleo and have my meal timings pretty close to where they should be. The downside is I currently work noon-9pm, and I'm working to change my hours but that's going to take a bit of time. CT-wise I'm just starting out. I've been taking 60 degree showers for the past week, I'm about to head into the tub and lower the temperature down to the 50-55 range.

### Rojo — 2012-03-14T23:59:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.2/post-21>

Welcome, Michael!

Is your username from the Gilgamesh epic? Perfect for a paleo man!

Rojo

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T00:30:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.2/post-71>

Welcome! I follow auto-immune paleo, too.

### Enkidu — 2012-03-15T00:57:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.2/post-128>

Rojo,

Sure is, and exactly as I thought. I spend far too much time with epic literature. I was also considering the frost giants from Norse mythology or a notable member of the fomori from Celtic since both of those groups are linked to older pantheons and have a link to the cold. Ultimately, I decided on Enkidu because of the significance of Gilgamesh on the western canon.... Yeah, I put way too much thought into my username.

Birdy,

Thanks for the welcome! I'm doing it heal my leaky gut.

### Jane — 2012-03-15T00:57:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.2/post-129>

Hi Michael,

It's great to meet you!

I am overweight and hypothyroid so I live winters in the land of coconut oil!

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T16:33:58+0200

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T04:21:52+0200

### Valorie33 — 2012-03-18T19:56:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-my-name-is-valorie.3/post-2428>

I am feeling really frustrated right now.

Thursday evening I was helping to move a couch up a very steep set of outside stairs in the cold and rain(which really didn't seem to bother me) and I think I may have broken or seriously sprained my wrist.

The only part of me that is getting CT for the last couple of days is my wrist, my food choices have been horrible, you never realize how much you use BOTH hands to do things. My bf is out of town for work, my son is not much for cooking, however he did go out and get me some free range eggs, so this morning I had eggs and oysters, so I am hoping that today things are better and I can get back on track.

I know I need to go to the clinic to get this wrist checked out. I just hate to go to any doctor since the time that I went in for pneumonia and the doctor said (in a room full of other patients) that I should just lose weight, that it wasn't helping me. He actually said it like I hadn't noticed that I needed to lose weight and he was telling me this great observation that only he could know. At the time I was so humiliated I couldn't speak, if I knew then what I know now... I could have told him that following doctors advice and eating low calorie low fat diets ALL my life got me where I was that day, and that until he really knew what he was talking about, he should shove it !!!

But still I let his words keep me from getting this wrist looked at.......until now !! Off I go !!

### Birdy — 2012-03-19T04:29:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-my-name-is-valorie.3/post-2584>

What a jerk! Hopefully you won't let it get you down for long.

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-14T23:39:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-tn.5/post-8>

Excited to see this forum starting! I've come over from MarksDailyApple.

I've been eating a primal diet for 10 weeks, 5 of those following the Leptin Rx to become LS, and have been practicing cold thermogenesis (CT) through either bath or spot-icing for the later two weeks.

Currently working 2 jobs, sometimes until 11pm which has made staying with circadian rhythms and light difficult, but hoping it's not much longer. Looking forward to learning more about these topics along with optimal health.

### aug19 — 2012-03-14T23:43:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-tn.5/post-11>

I cant wait to start Leptin Reset once we are through moving this month. It is going to be great learning from each other!

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-14T23:44:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-tn.5/post-12>

Yes it is, and welcome to the board. Good luck with the move too (I've done it a few times and can be quite traumatic!)

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T00:24:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-tn.5/post-56>

Welcome!! Souldancer, are those blue-blocking sunglasses on your emoticon or are you still midday?

### TrishaMariaPena — 2012-03-15T03:34:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-tn.5/post-389>

Hi there! What part of TN are you in? I'm in Hermitage/Nashville. I too work a different schedule but more by choice. I unofficially have Delayed Sleep Pattern Syndrome, where you sleep prefectly fine but just not during the same hours as most people. My optimal time to sleep is about 4a to noon. Luckily I have worked nights most of my career (25+ years) and have made the most of it but right now I have to be at work at 10a which is not where I want to be. Hopefully I will be freelancing soon where I can set my schedule back to what's best for my body. (OK, I know Dr. K disagrees with not sleeping during normal night time but DSPS is real and I have been this way my whole life.) I actually DO have the blue-blocker sun glasses and wear them around the house when I need to get sleepy. The best thing though is the f.Lux software from [http://stereopsis.com/flux/](http://http://stereopsis.com/flux/) that makes the color of your computer's display adapt to the time of day, warm at night and like sunlight during the day. Cuts out all that excessive blue light. It's wonderful and free. Enjoy!

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-15T15:12:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-tn.5/post-714>

I had not heard of that particular sleep condition before - fascinating. I wonder if a friend of mine has that - she's been as you have all the time I've known her which is most of her adult life. I use f.lux at home and at my day job (although it doesn't affect much there because of the hours). Can't put it on my evening job's computer, unfortunately. But maybe that's a good thing because I still have to drive home after the shift. Oh, and I'm in Nashville also - Donelson! Very close to you indeed. We're right there next to Dr. Kruse.

### hazyjane — 2012-03-16T01:54:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-tn.5/post-1162>

Hey Clabbergirl! I didn't realize you were in Nashville. I'm here too!

# Thread: Greetings from Springfield, IL!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-springfield-il.7/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rojo — 2012-03-14T23:47:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-springfield-il.7/post-16>

Hello all,

I've been following Jack's blog for three months. I love his insights as well as his sometimes chaotic writing style. I teach composition at a community college so I thought I had seen it all, but Jack is one of the most original writers I've ever seen. His style is perfectly suited to his ideas: a bit wild and rough but with a good heart and brilliant ideas.

I was a paleo/crossfitter for 3 years, but have really cut back on the crossfitting recently. I'm still a big Robb Wolf fan. Now I do Olympic weightlifting 2 days/week and throw in a high intensity WOD every once in a while.

I'm following the Leptin RX (will probably be on it for a while) and doing CT (very slowly). I've got a mild case of Raynaud's and probably a bad 06/03 ratio because I don't tolerate cold well at all. Even with my mildly cool showers and baths, though, I've seen a difference.

But I'm in this for the long haul: I want to optimize longevity, health, and intelligence. I don't care about 6 pack abs anymore. I'd be interested to hear from other folks in central IL.

Keep chillin!

Rojo

### mmuskopf@gmail.com — 2012-03-15T00:08:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-springfield-il.7/post-36>

Hey Rojo,

I've been following the blog for about a month now or so and am still catching up on all the old posts. Like you, follow Robb Wolf fairly closely as well as Dr. Bernstein since I am T1D...anyway, I am near Belleville, IL which is not too far from you (like 30 min from STL).

Just thought I'd say hello as a fellow SoIL native!

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T00:16:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-springfield-il.7/post-45>

Welcome! Hey, do you still have snow????

### mmuskopf@gmail.com — 2012-03-15T01:12:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-springfield-il.7/post-150>

Lol, nope. I think the high temp was 80F here today!

### Enkidu — 2012-03-15T01:17:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-springfield-il.7/post-160>

Hey Rojo,

I only really started investigating Paleo a few months ago. Started with Mark Sisson's Primal Blueprint, since then I've become a huge Robb Wolf fan myself. I look forward to hearing more from you.

# Thread: Hi from Melbourne, Australia
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-melbourne-australia.11/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kiera2 — 2012-03-14T23:59:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-melbourne-australia.11/post-20>

I'm so excited to see this new forum. Thanks Jack!

I've been following the Leptin Rx for 4 months, CT for 3.5 weeks. I lost 10Kg in 10 weeks, then started CT and stalled. But doing CT has _cured_ my degenerative disc disease! Yes, cured it. In 2 weeks. I had been severely physically limited since 2003; couldn't do any gardening or heavy housework; couldn't lift more than 2Kg.

Four weeks ago, without thinking, I moved a pot of broth to the back burner, and was laid out with a migraine for the rest of the day. That is what always happened if I lifted too much, or not close in to my body.

Yesterday I held 4Kg at arm's length. I felt no buzzing down my leg, and felt no after effects. I can't believe how strong I feel. I am eternally grateful for the work Jack has done, and his willingness to share it with anyone who wants to know how to live an optimal life.

### Souldanzer — 2012-03-15T00:26:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-melbourne-australia.11/post-61>

Hey! Coriander!!

I'm having similar results re: spine issues. It's so awesome I can now bend to my left again, which means so much better turns skiing

I'm now regularly submerging up to my chin... thinking it might help the chronic headaches

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T00:27:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-melbourne-australia.11/post-65>

Hi Coriander!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

### Jane — 2012-03-15T00:53:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-melbourne-australia.11/post-120>

Hi Coriander and Souldanzer,

Fantastic! I have a herniated disc in my neck and one in my lower lumbar region that causes numbness and tingling in my left leg. You give me inspiration!

Jane

### fitness@home — 2012-03-15T03:46:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-melbourne-australia.11/post-400>

Hi Coriander. Your progress is great!

### Rojo — 2012-03-15T03:50:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-melbourne-australia.11/post-410>

Welcome, Coriander! It's good to see you over here!

### Kiera2 — 2012-03-15T03:59:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-melbourne-australia.11/post-420>

Jane, the first symptoms I noticed, in 2003, were numb patches on my legs; and in about 6 months I couldn't feel heat or cold on my left, from my bra down to my toes. I had such pain in my hip I was limping. But there was nothing wrong with my hip.

In only the second week of CT I noticed I could feel the cold in patches on my leg. Then in the next two days, it felt totally normal! I could feel the cold all over. Then I realised I no longer had the buzzing down my leg when I lifted.

I am stunned that CT has worked this fast. Maybe because I was following the Leptin Rx for months already? I had also been following the GAPS diet for 2 years. I can only guess that my O6:O3 ratio must be good; I can't get it tested in Australia.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-15T04:00:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-melbourne-australia.11/post-422>

Hey Coriander!!! good to see you over here from MDA

### caroline — 2012-03-15T04:56:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-melbourne-australia.11/post-497>

Hi Coriander - I have been following your progress - you are doing so well

### mumone — 2012-03-15T05:06:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-melbourne-australia.11/post-516>

Hi Coriander

ive followed your progress over at MDA, u r an inspiration

Im - Leptin Rx/ Keto paleo/ Leaky Gut Rx

and CTing every day and its doing something -hard to define just what yet but i can feel my body changing- Im trying to get my chin in to try and reduce the double chins i seem to have gained  its a very interesting non linear process.... no weight loss yet (just perpetual gain)

Anyway its grreat to be here with you all, you are a constant source of inspiration for me.

### Mindymoo — 2012-03-16T10:46:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-melbourne-australia.11/post-1347>

Hi all - great to see some fellow Aussies on here!

Have been following the MDA threads for a while, which is how i found Dr Kruse - amazing stuff! Have always followed a fairly clean diet, but have really struggled with an extra 20 pounds that doesn't want to let go! Have been crossfitting for the last 10 or so months (& am totally addicted) which is where i first started hearing about Paleo, although it wasn't that disimiliar to how i was already eating! Then i found Primal which really seemed to resonate with me.

Was already on the coconut oils etc, doing some IF and, while not following a low fat diet, after reading Primal Blueprint i really upped my fat intake. Within about 2 weeks, while the scales didn't move, my clothes got looser & about 3 people commented on how much weight i had lost! Then i found Dr Kruse (only about 2 months ago) and started applying some of his principles - upping my b'fast protein, no snacking etc. Lost 2 kilos in less than a month!!

Am now on holidays travelling for a month (home end of next week) so it has been next to impossible to follow, but i don't feel like i have put on much, if any weight. It's also given me heaps of time to read, read, read all this amazing info - can't wait to get home & really get stuck into it & trying the CT!

Have learnt so much from reading the threads & look forward to continuing on the journey with all of you

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-16T22:00:53+0200

# Thread: Hello from North Carolina!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-carolina.15/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### aug19 — 2012-03-15T00:01:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-carolina.15/post-26>

Hi, I am Michelle. I live in North Carolina. We are smack dab in the middle of moving from the Charlotte area to the Greensboro area. I found Dr. Kruse about a month ago and have been insatiably reading his materials. I am also in graduate school and it has been hard to concentrate on what I should be reading for what I want to be reading (and re-reading) Quilt papers. Some of his papers I have to read more than one time to absorb 100%. I need this leptin reset bad.

My story is long -

In 2008 I felt I was in the prime of my health. Recently losing 80 pounds (260-180's) by working out 2 hours a day and eating a low carb diet. After 7 months I found myself suffering from heart racing, exhaustion, strange sensations like waking up feeling like a lightening bolt was going through me at the first moment of waking up, any kind of adrenaline being uncomfortable. My marriage was great, kids were great doctors just wanted to tell me I was depressed. Couldn't help me in any other way so I began to read everything I could get my hands on.

Miraculously met a compounding pharmacist. He did saliva test on me and cortisol was wacked. Put me on some adrenal fatigue vitamins from Dr. Wilson (?). Introduced me to an open minded doctor in my area that did every test under the sun including spectracell to find out a lot of what I was deficient in. She worked with me for two years. At the same time a few of my family member's sons (13 of them on the Autism Spectrum) were showing great results from a GF/CF diet. With nothing to lose I experimented on myself with it and got some relief from whatever was going on.

I lived on the GAPS diet from 2010-2011 until in June I ran across the Paleo diet. I started reading everything I could get my hands on regarding this and was relieved that there were more people who ate like me in the world. Through MDA I found Jack Kruse.

We are in the middle of moving but come April 2nd I cant wait to start Leptin Reset with CT.

I know I have some leaky gut issues, some inflammation issues and who knows what else. Can't wait to get a full set of labs to show a before/after.

I will be watching this forum daily

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T00:20:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-carolina.15/post-49>

Hi Michelle!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

### Jane — 2012-03-15T01:53:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-carolina.15/post-214>

Hi Michelle,

It's interesting how we have to take our health into our own hands while still paying ungodly premiums for CW health insurance. I hope to some day be so Optimal that I can cut back on everything but catastrophic emergency care.

### jreyn07@live.com — 2012-03-15T01:56:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-carolina.15/post-221>

go forward and succeed!

### Martin — 2012-03-15T13:24:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-carolina.15/post-661>

> **aug19;27 wrote:** Put me on some adrenal fatigue vitamins from Dr. Wilson (?).

Hi there! I grew up in Charlotte. I still think it's home sometimes.

Are you referring to Dr. Larry Wilson and his work with hair analyses and mineral balancing? If so, I got the first relief from my lousy adult health life with his protocol. Good stuff, but left a lot of meat on the bone for me.

all the best,

Martin

### Lyndra — 2012-03-16T16:29:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-rockies.19/post-1456>

> **Souldanzer;1236 wrote:** I just looked at the Boulder Res website and swimming is not allowed at this time of the year
>
>
>
> How'd you do it?

The Polar Bear Plunge is a special event held on January 1 every year and so isn't subject to the usual rules about no swimming. Though I wouldn't call what I did "swimming"! My first year I did a little bit of swimming back and forth in the hole they cut out of the ice because I had friends filming me so I needed to show off. In subsequent years, I got out as fast as my numb feet would let me. You could probably go in from the north shore right now or at nearby Coot Lake if you really wanted to get that ice cold water experience. I'll be sticking with the tub for now.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-rockies.19/post-4151>

> **Cavemam;4146 wrote:** Then we can head to SHINE for Elixers!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-03-23T00:53:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-rockies.19/post-4693>

> **Lyndra;4159 wrote:** We just tried to get hold of them and it took 2+ days to get back to us. Usually they are more responsive. Maybe they had a staffing issue...

So is the 3 mins at what temp? Can we up temp for less $ hehe. Can more than one person fit in there? I envision "KRAZY KRUSE DAY" where we can all meet there for our 45 mins of therapy!

### SimonM — 2012-03-23T01:24:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-rockies.19/post-4723>

> **Cavemam;4150 wrote:** So they have a cryotherapy unit at the cafe of life next to Whole Foods. Wonder if we could work out some deal. I know they normally do 3 min type things. . .wonder, wonder. . .Simon, your the athlete correct? Ever done that?

Done ice cold baths for legs and we sometimes soak in the Creek after runs.

Actually planning on checking this out Monday or Tuesday with my conditioning coach. The testimonials from athletes sound good. As Souldanzer says, it is $45 for first 3 minutes, then 5 treatments for $300. There's a thread somewhere else on this forum with other options (there is one in Denver).

The price is a little shocking for a 3-minute treatment; if I had a unit it would be $10 a pop and get a line out the door. But who knows how much liquid nitrogen costs? Cafe of Life are notorious for hard selling, too, so be on guard

"The Cryosauna uses gasiform nitrogen to lower the client’s skin surface temperature from normal body temperature to 30-32 degrees in 30 seconds or less and keeps it that way for two-three minutes."

### Marsha — 2012-03-23T04:02:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-rockies.19/post-4801>

> **Cavemam;4721 wrote:** So is the 3 mins at what temp? Can we up temp for less $ hehe. Can more than one person fit in there? I envision "KRAZY KRUSE DAY" where we can all meet there for our 45 mins of therapy!

Now that would be fun! But the video with Dr.Oz on the belowzerocolorado.com site will give you an idea of how big the thing is and how it works. I bet the 3minutes is pretty specific, no matter what condition you're in -might come out too frozen to warm up if you stay in longer!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-17T01:12:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-rockies.19/post-24723>

Ok Guys! I cleaned out my inbox! In case you tried to PM me I guess I had reached a max. ooops.

So area folks are invited to my house Saturday for Webinar. That means you. Pm me!

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-25T17:22:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-rockies.19/post-27339>

What are you guys doing for Memorial Weekend and the Bolder Boulder 10K ?

Running the Bolder Boulder 10K was one of my \*very important\* goals for AN recovery... and I did it last year in under 53min!!!

I'm assuming running a 10k without training isn't the smartest thing to do for me right now (Dr K?). But it's really messing with me! I feel like I'm going backwards

Then I was planning on getting away with a friend to Shoshoni Ashram - until I realized that meant even higher altitude and veggie diet, which neither I'm supposed to do right now.

I'm tempted to run the Bolder Boulder anyways b/c of my stubborn mind in this case. Considering I also can't be part of the happily intoxicated crowd on the sidelines which I would have accepted as secondary choice . What else is there around here as a getaway that doesn't include exercise, alcohol, altitude, or vegetarian food?? I'm entirely out of the loop if it can't involve altitude and/or exercise.

### Lyndra — 2012-05-25T18:11:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-rockies.19/post-27352>

I can sympathize about feeling like you are on the sidelines while everyone else is active. Would it work for you to walk the race? There are special waves were everyone is walking so you'd be less likely to feel like you weren't doing what you "should" be doing. Ah yes, my infamous "S" word - "should." Should do this, should do that. So much easier for "should" to rule my life rather than dig into the emotions and figure out what I really want. I, too, am struggling; I'm not able to ride much and can't keep up with my friends. I tell myself to think of the long-term and be patient (which sadly is not one of my strengths).

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-25T18:37:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-rockies.19/post-27361>

Sorry to hear you're dealing with similar things, lyndra. I have a friend who has a horse up in Longmont and it's her life...

I'm not feeling a \*should\* in this case at all. I just remember how much fun it was last year and how motivated I was after the race to continue my journey to health. It proved to me that I was doing the right thing. I was so proud of myself when I crossed the finish line ahead of my friends. I know there are walking waves. Even that feels like a step backwards and giving in to "sick" status again.

I had to work hard for 7 years to be able to run the BolderBoulder last year. Yes, it took me that long to reach this particular goal  I realize there is not point to this if I am hurting myself even more

I'm trying to find something else to do instead that feels like an accomplishment to acknowledge all the work I have done since the last BB 10k.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-25T21:24:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-rockies.19/post-27415>

Scott Carpenter Pool opens today. Only 50m pool in Boulder.

Something I \*can\* do

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-25T21:56:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-rockies.19/post-27422>

Any idea on temp there?

>

That makes a lot of sense to me. Celebrate where you are and how far you've come rather than get wrapped up in where are not right now. I'll try to take that advice myself...

We could bring some ice....

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-25T23:52:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-rockies.19/post-27459>

Okay, no BB10k for me this year.

Test run got interrupted at like .... 2 minutes. My head is hating me now. I am learning to respect my limitations

### Lyndra — 2012-05-26T05:11:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-rockies.19/post-27569>

> **Souldanzer;27557 wrote:** Okay, no BB10k for me this year.
>
>
>
> Test run got interrupted at like .... 2 minutes. My head is hating me now. I am learning to respect my limitations

Bummer. Disappointing I'm sure but something you have to go through to get where you are going. I am going to test my own limitations on Sunday when I ride with a friend. We'll see if I limp home myself.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-26T05:18:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-rockies.19/post-27572>

> **Lyndra;27667 wrote:** Bummer. Disappointing I'm sure but something you have to go through to get where you are going. I am going to test my own limitations on Sunday when I ride with a friend. We'll see if I limp home myself.

CT the heck outta it before so you'll be entirely numb?

Geez, I'm no help here!! Let's try this again... be gentle with yourself and back off if you need to. Once you're all fixed up you can ride all you want again. ... better? Still learning to let go of the Boulder mentality of finishing the race no matter what. The price might not be worth it.

### Lyndra — 2012-05-26T05:31:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-rockies.19/post-27578>

> **Souldanzer;27670 wrote:** CT the heck outta it before so you'll be entirely numb?
>
>
>
> Geez, I'm no help here!! Let's try this again... be gentle with yourself and back off if you need to. Once you're all fixed up you can ride all you want again. ... better? Still learning to let go of the Boulder mentality of finishing the race no matter what. The price might not be worth it.

Yes, better. So frustrating, though, and especially not knowing whether I'll be better later this year (when I want to race again) or not. Taking it one day at a time is hard but so many times I have rushed through things and regretted it later. Thanks for the advice. It means a lot coming from someone else who is struggling.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-27T03:52:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-rockies.19/post-27781>

I know what you mean about not knowing when/if you're going to be better... I definitely am having my moments with this. A good cry works wonders for me with this - and then I pick myself up and do something to help the issue, like making another phone call, or often I just look for someone inspirational on the web who's successfully healed what I am working on. I need those pictures of success in my mind or I wouldn't get out of bed somedays.

Just got back from getting some serious O2 at Tonic and hanging out with some friends. I stepped it up with super-charged O2 water and a Deer Antler Tonic  This is THE place for me to hang out these days!! That bar has a ridiculous oxygen level in the air b/c so many people are hooked up to O2. I'm feeling like a million bucks now  Well, almost. Much better than this morning. I told Michael to expect me back until my prescription O2 comes through.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-28T18:26:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-rockies.19/post-28047>

> **Lyndra;27667 wrote:** Bummer. Disappointing I'm sure but something you have to go through to get where you are going. I am going to test my own limitations on Sunday when I ride with a friend. We'll see if I limp home myself.

How'd it go?

### Lyndra — 2012-05-28T18:48:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-rockies.19/post-28049>

> **Souldanzer;28146 wrote:** How'd it go?

I had to bail out on riding with my friend as one of my cats is not doing well. He got a little better yesterday but now isn't so good this morning. We may be paying a visit to the ol' emergency vet later on. Big sigh.

I did get out for an easy ride with my husband. I am deliberately not gathering data so as not to be bummed out with how slow I am, but I can still tell I'm slower than a slug. But, I did enjoy it and that is what I am after these days.

What are you doing today?

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-28T19:21:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-rockies.19/post-28058>

Sorry about your cat being sick. I hope he feels better soon. I get so worried when my pup isn't feeling well that my whole world revolves around her until she's better.

Good to hear you are able to still enjoy the riding. I worked in my garden yesterday and couldn't do much either. An hour and I was done! But getting outside and soaking up some sun was good despite. It's all a matter of perspective.

Not sure about today. My friends took off for Shoshoni Ashram without me  Cried a little, then picked myself up and spent 1.5 hrs in a 50 degree bath, shivered for forever, snuggled up in bed and listened to some podcasts I had been wanting to until I fell asleep. I can't read much these days, which is a real bummer, as that is my favorite thing to do really.

Boulder traffic will be a disaster still, so I'll probably stay away from going into town for a while longer. Planning on going to a yin yoga class with a friend in the afternoon. It's supposed to be super gentle, and I'll skip the head stands  Think I might take a walk to Vic's in a bit.

### Hope — 2013-02-22T04:54:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-rockies.19/post-76985>

I am so jealous of all you guys...what a dream to live there eh?? I wish I could talk my hubby into it but he loves DC....serious electron depletion.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-ohio.29/post-74>

It's great that Dr Kruse has a forum all set up for us. This is awesome! I came over from the Marks Daily Apple Leptin Thread and it is wonderful to see so many of the posters here.

I've been low carb for a couple years, then gained weight from hypothyroid (Hashi's) and of course not getting everything working well, got sicker and heavier over time. A cycle of getting despondant and binging and losing over and over was wreaking havoc. After being so ill over the past holidays, I knew I had to find out a better way to get healthy and live a full life again and that search led me from the Hcg forums to the leptin forums and Dr. Kruse. So I have been doing the Leptin Reset, Paleo since Jan 2. I am now also working on leaky gut and doing one last round of hcg.

Glad to be here.

# Thread: Hello from Cambridge, UK.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-cambridge-uk.34/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lamree — 2012-03-15T00:35:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-cambridge-uk.34/post-89>

Good evening everybody,

My name is Alex and I'm a junior doctor working in Cambridge. As soon as I saw the email about the forum I signed up immediately.

Over the past year I've lost a great deal of weight and improved my lifestyle thanks to people like Martin Berkhan, Robb Wolf and now Dr. Kruse.

Everyday I struggle to spread what I have learned to my patients and to my colleagues. It can be a slow and often frustrating endeavour, but more often than not it is worth it in the end.

I hope to see and be involved in a great many interesting discussions and insights into optimised living, as well as seeing this community blossom!

Kind regards,

Dr. Alex Alamri

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T00:36:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-cambridge-uk.34/post-91>

### rbu5@sssnet.com — 2012-03-15T01:47:42+0200

All the best!

### fitness@home — 2012-03-15T02:04:35+0200

# Thread: Hi
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.41/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jane — 2012-03-15T00:43:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.41/post-107>

I'm from California. I just finished reading my way through the monster Leptin RX thread at Mark's Daily Apple, where I learned tons of info from Dr. Kruse and the stalwart members who have been my inspiration. You all rock!

I was diagnosed with asthma, high blood pressure and Type II Diabetes ten years ago. I've controlled the diabetes through diet for the last two years without meds and rarely have asthma attacks anymore. A few years ago I landed in the hospital for Atrial Fibrillation twice. Though my cardio doc says my thyroid tests look fine, I'm sure my adrenals are shot after 26 years of doing therapeutic foster care and making the hormonal roller-coaster menopause ride. I've had IBS-D dominant since I was 15, which the "specialists" have all told me to just "live" with it. I have about 100 pounds to lose. I've always been a "Night Owl" and have existed on 4 hours of sleep for eons. I believe I can correct all of these issues and get off of all of my meds through Optimal Living.

I went Gluten free 6 1/2 months ago. I'm on my 5th week of the Leptin RX and aiming for Optimal. My first goal is to heal my leaky gut. At this point, most everything is going straight through me so I've eliminated everything from my diet except GF ground beef, bacon, shellfish, fish, Coconut Mana and oil, and a few forkfuls of fermented sauerkraut along with some of the recommended supplements.

I've been Keto for awhile and have lost 20 pounds since starting the Reset and dropped two sizes. The constant pain in my upper abdomen is gone. I feel calmer even in stressful situations. My body composition is changing for the better.

Once my gut calms down and can handle more detox, I plan on adding the CT protocol. Anyway, that's my current plan. Something Dr. Kruse wrote in his last blog really hit home with me:

_If you want to know your pastÃ¢â‚¬Â¦Ã¢â‚¬Â¦..look into your present conditions.

If you want to know your futureÃ¢â‚¬Â¦Ã¢â‚¬Â¦..look into your present actions.

What you do right now determines what you will become.

_

I'm looking forward to sharing the Optimal Journey with you all!

Jane

### Lamree — 2012-03-15T00:48:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.41/post-111>

Hi Jane,

This is so encouraging and a brilliant example of what one can achieve when we take a step back, apply our intellect to a problem and take action - one of Dr. Kruse's messages which you have so poignantly highlighted in you post. Make sure you keep us all updated on your progress and good luck!

Best wishes,

Alex

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T00:52:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.41/post-118>

Welcome!!!!!!!!!!!!!!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Hello from down under
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-down-under.47/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rook — 2012-03-15T01:05:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-down-under.47/post-140>

hi all,

i'm rook from new zealand. looking forward to seeing what great info gets posted up on this forum. i do a bit of a mish mashed approach comprising of a diet pretty close to an archevore prescription with calorie cycling from lean gains and some cold themogenesis from jack kruse/tim ferriss....seems to be working ok at the moment and i'm about 6-8% off my ideal body fat percentage so not too far to go.

### Grottenolm — 2012-03-15T01:09:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-down-under.47/post-144>

Hi Rook! Glad to see another kiwi around these parts  What city are you in? I'm in Auckland ~ been eating paleo for about 2 years, now introducing cold therapy. Looking forward to seeing more kiwis come out of the woodwork!

### Rook — 2012-03-15T01:15:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-down-under.47/post-155>

I'm in Hamilton at the moment. Good work on the consistent paleo. I've been pretty strict with diet for the last 3-4 months at best. Before that I lost weight the traditional way, by completely overdoing exercise. It worked but I felt like crap and was eating next to nothing to maintain it...I much prefer eating properly and doing very sparse but intense exercise

Yeah, hopefully there'll be a few more kiwis around that we can share info with...

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T01:16:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-down-under.47/post-156>

Welcome!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-15T09:59:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-down-under.47/post-635>

Kia ora Rook and Grottenolm, I'm in Christchurch. I've been strict keto paleo for a few months now, doing the CT almost every day and trying to do the circadian cycles, too. I'd LOVE to share info about best places to source supplements, etc. There seem to be so many resources in the USA. Anyone know where you can get 99% chocolate that Dr K recommends so highly here in NZ?

### Rook — 2012-03-15T21:22:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-down-under.47/post-998>

Ah, the 99% stuff. I have a bit of 85% donovans chocolate (made right here in Hamilton) or I get 85% Lindt dark from places like Nosh (not sure if Nosh is down in Christchurch). We have a specialist chocolate shop in a few of the westfield malls up here that also have 100% chocolate but at $12 for 80g it's rather pricey.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-15T22:35:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-down-under.47/post-1054>

> **Rook;999 wrote:** Ah, the 99% stuff. I have a bit of 85% donovans chocolate (made right here in Hamilton) or I get 85% Lindt dark from places like Nosh (not sure if Nosh is down in Christchurch). We have a specialist chocolate shop in a few of the westfield malls up here that also have 100% chocolate but at $12 for 80g it's rather pricey.

Thanks! I used to live in Hamilton years ago. No Nosh in Chch (not much of anything here at the moment!), but I can get 85% Lindt at our supermarket. I'm still in search of the holy grail 99% stuff though....

Where are you buying supplements (assuming you use them)? I've used Health Post online but wonder if there are other/better/cheaper places? (Health Post is pretty great, though.)

### Grottenolm — 2012-03-16T10:09:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-down-under.47/post-1341>

Ah chocolate, how I love thee! I mostly drink 100% coco powder dissolved in water as a treat - the trade aid brand. Sometimes I add a dash of coconut milk. For chocolate, I sometimes eat the green and blacks 85%, available at countdown. However, my sister in melbourne sometimes sends me 100% Koko Black chocolate from Oz. Also, my in laws are German and I ask them to bring 99% chocolate when they come to visit  there will always be a way to get hold of addictive substances, lol!

For supplements I use iherb.com - no hassles with them yet, and postage costs are reasonable.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-19T08:39:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-down-under.47/post-2657>

Interesting that you use iherb.com without issue. A few years ago I ordered from the US and it was picked up by customs. They then added a $100 (yes, that's right, $100) fee for bringing in vitamins, which was more than what they'd cost me to buy. So I've been wary ever since (and when I saw that warning on iherb's site I wondered). On the chocolate front, I think I need my overseas relatives to get on board!

### Rook — 2012-03-20T20:48:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-down-under.47/post-3416>

I can't say that I take a large amount of supplements yet. I take D3 just because I don't get a chance to absorb a large amount of it naturally (doesn't help that i have darker skin) and that's about it at the moment. Since I don't really have any other problems I'm not too worried about supplementing yet but might look into it a bit later on.

### jumcc — 2012-03-24T09:37:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-down-under.47/post-5348>

hi guys, kiwi here too from qtown. I am not too far off my body fat goals but my hormones are stuffed, adrenals and thyroid. I also have high levels of mercury and lead.

I have been eating paleo low carb for about 6 months. Started weights a few months ago and have toned up and now adding CT in is stripping some fat off me.

I found the stuff interesting about the importance on leaving 3-4 hours between eating and sleep for hormones and fat loss so have really nailed that down as i a bit of late night snacker! For CT i have been sitting in the lake here. it's bloody freezing and i have had quite a few funny looks ha. I am not following the leptin reset guidelines. I train in a fasted state in the morning and eat and lunch and dinner, sometimes something in between. I get the best results when i train fasted.

I'm taking what i can from this site and applying it to me, doing one thing at a time. I find when you throw the kitchen sink at things it gets a bit overwhelming. I did go pretty hard, pretty fast with the CT and felt like crap but now feeling much better.

I also order from iherb and have had no problems. i eat the 85% lindt when i want chocy and i also do the cocoa in water trick. All thes chocolate around for easter is not helping my old chocolate habit!!!

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-26T02:29:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-down-under.47/post-6133>

Sitting in the Queenstown Lake... I take my hat off to you (though I hope you've kept yours on!!).

### Rook — 2012-03-26T23:13:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-down-under.47/post-6581>

Wow, that's impressive! I'll bet you get some looks for that.

### LVK — 2012-03-29T00:43:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-down-under.47/post-7979>

Hi All,

I've been eating low carb for about three months now. Lost over 10kg before grinding to a halt. Been sitting about the same weight for a few weeks now. But all good, half way there and feeling loads better.

I live on 10 acres just out of Crofton, NZ. We eat about 30% meat off our own land. Goat, pig, & lamb.

Don't know anyone else besides my family eating low carb. People look at me funny when I tell them what we eat (or don't eat).

Louise


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Greetings from Colorado!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-colorado.49/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Marsha — 2012-03-15T01:14:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-colorado.49/post-153>

My name is Marsha, I live in Broomfield, CO (between Denver and Boulder).

A convoluted story on how I got here: My husband Steve has Secondary Progressive MS and tried Betaseron and a 4year treatment of mitazantrone...used in MS treatments. He reached his limit, but we still visit his hematologist, Dr. William E. Lee, every 6 months or so to visit and check on leukemia etc. Anyway, Dr. Lee a second generation Chinese and rice connoisseur was quite a 'round' man the last time I saw him- but the smartest doctor my husband has seen. Very into research on the latest methods of treatment etc. At our last visit, on Jan 6, 2012, I was flabbergasted by Dr. Lee's appearance! He'd lost at least 60 lbs. and when asked what he had done, he blushed and said he'd join the cult of Crossfit and adopted a lean paleo diet!

The next day I googled crossfit and paleo, settled with Mark's site and Rob's site and finally came to Dr. Kruse! Since then I've tried to digest all this new information and implement as best I can the leptin RX and CT (face dunks so far).

My husband is the hedonist cynic sitting on the sidelines in his wheelchair just watching and laughing at me (face dunks with a kids snorkel must be pretty funny!). He's also diabetic and does watch his sugars. However, he's not interested in giving up his tequila, his English muffins nor rice/potatoes etc. Does love his prime rib, though!

I'm not even here 'cause of him, but for Me! I am going to gain back my energy, my creativity, my slimmer body and maybe change a few lives along the way as I set an example of what a 65 year old can do when she sets her mind to it!

More as we continue, that's enough for today.

Here's something to celebrate along with Jack's sunrises, since it is 3/14 - International pi day (March 14):

[http://www.newscientist.com/blogs/nstv/2011/03/a-musical-interpretation-of-pi.html](http://http://www.newscientist.com/blogs/nstv/2011/03/a-musical-interpretation-of-pi.html)

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T01:19:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-colorado.49/post-166>

Welcome Marsha! Glad you are hear working on yourself. You deserve to be optimal!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Dipping My Toe in the (Cold) Waters of Change!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dipping-my-toe-in-the-cold-waters-of-change.66/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Becky — 2012-03-15T02:08:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dipping-my-toe-in-the-cold-waters-of-change.66/post-240>

Hi, Everyone! Becky here.

Long-time low-carber, part-time bad dieter, on and off successful, but increasingly not. History of serious family health concerns (maternal grandmother weighed 632 lbs. at her early death) and my own mysterious issues, too. Long, long story. Middle-aged for a few more years, overweight, out of shaped, exhausted, sedentary, sleep-deprived, working a stressful, demanding job that will end in a few months. Money is tight.

That is all the negative background out of the way.

The point, though, is that this is a new day, and I am DETERMINED to get on top of these issues no matter what. I will never give up, and I will continue to learn and to do what it takes to reach optimum. I 'know' a lot, some of which isn't true, and I haven't kept up with the blogs and the other forum talk, so I have a lot to learn. I don't always understand everything I am reading, BUT it resonates with me. My own studies were already moving me in this direction, so when I connected with Jack Kruse, I knew he was onto something MAJOR.

I believe I have found my missing puzzle piece. I fully expect to get better and better, younger and younger, stronger and stronger. I will do the work. I will get the results. I hope to share my journey with everyone here.

### fitness@home — 2012-03-15T02:09:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dipping-my-toe-in-the-cold-waters-of-change.66/post-242>

Welcome Becky! I think we are all on a journey and I look forward to mine...

### Molly — 2012-03-15T02:12:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dipping-my-toe-in-the-cold-waters-of-change.66/post-248>

Good Luck. How old are you? I'm 54 and just beginning to learn all of this but it is fascinating. We sound like we might have similar issues if you want to chat.

### Rojo — 2012-03-15T02:14:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dipping-my-toe-in-the-cold-waters-of-change.66/post-252>

Welcome, Becky! Your challenges are tough but you are tougher!

### Becky — 2012-03-15T02:16:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dipping-my-toe-in-the-cold-waters-of-change.66/post-260>

Hi, Molly! Glad to meet you here!

I'm 57. Wow, I almost said I was 54, too! I keep forgetting how old I am! I don't think or feel that old most of the time. Physically, maybe I act even older at times, being so sedentary, but, inside, I feel young, and I do want my vim and vigor back!

I'd be glad to get to know you better.

### Becky — 2012-03-15T02:18:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dipping-my-toe-in-the-cold-waters-of-change.66/post-266>

Hi, Fitness and Rojo. Thanks for the welcomes! I am excited to see what this new venture brings to all of us.

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T02:53:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dipping-my-toe-in-the-cold-waters-of-change.66/post-324>

Nice to see you here!!

### grammasmitty@hotmail.com — 2012-03-15T03:20:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dipping-my-toe-in-the-cold-waters-of-change.66/post-368>

Welcome!!! Wow, at 57 I was at the beginning of my journey after lots of failed diets. (I just turned 61) Right about then I was down from my high of 322# and using hcg as my only tool. So glad I've found Dr. Jack. Hoping keto and CT will now take me the rest of the way!! The bottom line is like you said, NEVER give up!

### Becky — 2012-03-15T15:01:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dipping-my-toe-in-the-cold-waters-of-change.66/post-706>

Hi, Birdy! I'm looking forward to a grand adventure here!

Hello, Gramma Smitty! Glad to know I'm not the only one in the upper age brackets here. That's encouraging! I'd love to hit 60 in the best shape and fitness of my life. I believe it is possible.

I'm curious about the hcg diet, too. What was your experience? I have a few friends who did well on it and swear by it. Checking into it, I realized a connection with leptin, which I had already been reading about. So I immediately knew Dr. Kruse was onto something. I'm looking at the Leptin Rx, CT, etc., as the solution long-term, but interested in the HCG jump-start. Your thoughts?

**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-sd.73/post-279>

welcome all ...looking forward to the posts

### Becky — 2012-03-15T02:25:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-sd.73/post-282>

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T02:54:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-sd.73/post-326>

Welcome!!!!!!!!!!!!!

### dbfact — 2012-03-15T02:56:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-sd.73/post-328>

Hi everyone, I am a registered dietitian specializing in weight control and anti-aging nutrition in the Northern suburbs of IL. I study all aspects of nutrition (to avoid "confirmation bias"), but so far, Paleo is my favorite template for explain concepts to people in a way they understand (and there is a built-in community for motivation and support, and many overlapping signaling pathways with CRON)! While there are so many positive things to say about Dr. Jack, what helps me the most is his palpable encouragement and the "you can (and must) do it" enthusiasm that he exudes in every podcast and post.

Experimenting with CT (3 days in Lake Michigan so far), pretty good for someone who previously hated the cold! I always so I won't recommend something to clients that I don't do myself. So far, I feel great!

(I'm in the C.alorie R.estriction with O.ptimal N.utrition (CRON) cohort at UCSF). We are supposed to get our telomere data any day not (I will compare it to the results of the company Dr. Kruse has mentioned in podcasts).

# Thread: Hiya from Michigan!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hiya-from-michigan.75/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-03-15T02:36:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hiya-from-michigan.75/post-294>

Hey!

I'm so excited about this forum! I have been having a great time reading Dr. Kruses blog and have learned so much. I love learning, have 2 degrees and would love to go back for more. Not sure that will be happening anytime soon though, so I'll have to just learn this way for now .

I've low carb'ed off and on for a number of years. I lost about 40 pounds before my last pregnancy (twins) with homeopathic HCG. Gained about 20 with my twins and almost 20 more since I've been nursing (I ALWAYS gain when I nurse). So, I'm back where I started several years ago (discouraging). Also have thyroid issues and cholesterol is a bit on the high side.

I started the Leptin diet a few weeks ago and also started CT. I did the face dunking and while I did that gradually started making my showers a bit cooler. I've done 4 times in the tub now. The first two I did about 58-60 and the last two have been between 53-55. My husband decided to join me on the diet and CT too, which is really funny to me, but I'm delighted he is doing it!

I hope to learn a lot here and get to know some people! Ciao

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T02:51:03+0200

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-03-16T03:29:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hiya-from-michigan.75/post-1233>

> **blindpig;1044 wrote: Hi, Trisha,
>
>
>
> I just had to comment on your avatar. I clicked on it hoping it would enlarge so I could get a closer look at those adorable twins! Do you mind questions? How old? Boys/girls/boy&girl?? It looks like they might have been October babies....very cute!
>
>
>
> (I just can't help myself...I have girls twins, but they are 38 years old so I have been missing that cute baby stage for way too many years.) I don't mind questions at all! I have 4 boys total (was hoping for a girl and God blessed us with 2 more boys ha). My twins are 9 months old and we found out just recently they're identical. THe doctors told us they were most likely fraternal since they had their own sacs and placentas. We ended up having the genetic testing done since they looked SO much alike.That was kinda a fun little surprise. They are the most laid back good natured babies!
>
>
>
>

I live in the only county in michigan without a natural lake. I think it's about the most bleak and bland part of the state too!

### grammasmitty@hotmail.com — 2012-03-15T02:44:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-utah.78/post-300>

Finally got registered and activated! The internet didn't seem to like me this afternoon

So, learning a new forum, more people to meet, hopefully clarifications on things I am still confused about. Have been "messing" with the reset since last summer, but am down to business now. Got my labs done in January (don't ask - everything in the tank). Did a short round of hcg, then 2 weeks on the reset, then back to hcg combined with some CT. Cool baths (not quite cold yet) 4 times during 6 days and I put an ice pack across my belly every time I sit down. Lost 2" from my belly in those 6 days!!!!

Looking forward to being back on total paleo/keto next week and will continue CT. Have been reading a lot beyond Dr. Jack and finding so much that totally agrees with everything he says . . . well most everything

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T02:47:11+0200

# Thread: Hello from Hong Kong!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hong-kong.85/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ruby — 2012-03-15T03:08:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hong-kong.85/post-347>

Hi,

I'm Ruby, 47 and a mother of 2. I have always been interested in optimal health (I am nowhere near and believe I have quite a few things to tweak although of the face of it I am fine - no weight problems, no long-term medication). Chanced upon Dr. Jack's blog and was hooked, especially on the scientific side (which I love) and the thorough and logical explanations. All the more because he is so dedicated and genuine. Such stark contrast to all the doctors I have so far encountered who, even if they bothered to explain at all, talked to me like I had an IQ of 10. Looking forward to being part of this great community!

### Petra — 2012-03-16T11:31:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hong-kong.85/post-1349>

Hi to all of you in Hong Kong.

I live 'near' in Guangzhou. Did any of you get their telemere measured in HK, already? I wondered where to do that. I'm familiar with the hospitals and check ups in GZ, but I think this test is a tricky one to explain.

Looking for your insights on this one,

Petra

### ruby — 2012-03-17T04:02:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hong-kong.85/post-1847>

> **Petra;1351 wrote:** Hi to all of you in Hong Kong.
>
>
>
> I live 'near' in Guangzhou. Did any of you get their telemere measured in HK, already? I wondered where to do that. I'm familiar with the hospitals and check ups in GZ, but I think this test is a tricky one to explain.
>
>
>
> Looking for your insights on this one,
>
> Petra

Hi Petra,

no, I haven't even attempted to breach the subject of telomeres after failing to convince my doctor to do a proper thyroid profile as recommended by Jack. Most are very dogmatic here but I will keep searching and let you know. Not very optimistic though. Stay in touch - would be great to swap notes.

### ste colombe — 2012-03-25T13:24:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hong-kong.85/post-5747>

> **Petra;1351 wrote:** Hi to all of you in Hong Kong.
>
>
>
> I live 'near' in Guangzhou. Did any of you get their telemere measured in HK, already? I wondered where to do that. I'm familiar with the hospitals and check ups in GZ, but I think this test is a tricky one to explain.
>
>
>
> Looking for your insights on this one,
>
> Petra

Hi Petra, Im in Hong Kong. For Telemere testing you can get it done at the Sanatorium hospital in Happy Valley or with Dr Lauren Bramley in Central.

### Petra — 2012-03-29T06:29:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hong-kong.85/post-8137>

> **ste colombe;5781 wrote:** Hi Petra, Im in Hong Kong. For Telemere testing you can get it done at the Sanatorium hospital in Happy Valley or with Dr Lauren Bramley in Central.

Thanks Colombe!

### JoeBranca — 2012-03-30T02:23:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hong-kong.85/post-8515>

Hello HK folks, I've been to that crazy wonderful island a couple of times, years ago. I am curious, how would you eat optimally out there? I imagine seafood and hot pots would be the main thing, but beyond that almost everything is cooked in some kind of vegetable oil when you eat out? For home cooking can you easily get good proteins like grass fed meats imported from NZ?

### ruby — 2012-03-30T16:27:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hong-kong.85/post-8750>

> **Jack Kruse;8566 wrote:** Welcome Ruby!

Thank you, Dr. Jack!

Hi Joe,

restaurants are tricky indeed. For home cooking, getting hold of coconut oil, milk etc is not a problem. Most lamb here is from NZ. Getting other types of meat, quality eggs and butter involves real "hunting and gathering" around. For me at least getting organic and "alive" veg and herbs is not a problem - live close to an organic farm. It also helps to enforce the seasonality of food

To many local Chinese farmers, especially older ones, this comes naturally - they strictly adhere to "if it's not growing in your garden, you are not meant to eat it right now".


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Hello from Hermitage, TN (yes, Dr. Kruse's hometown!)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hermitage-tn-yes-dr-kruses-hometown.89/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TrishaMariaPena — 2012-03-15T03:24:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hermitage-tn-yes-dr-kruses-hometown.89/post-374>

My name is Trisha and I am an unabashed carnivore! While never really having been skinny (except for in the 80s when I tried the Diamond's fruit diet, never again!) I gained a ton of weight, almost all of it emotional, after becoming disappointed in my marriage in the early 2000s. Finally unable to face another day fat (5'1", almost 190#) I dropped 60 pounds in 2007 and have never looked back! (Yes, I've kept it off.) While on that path I studied everything I could regarding how to keep my new-found health and body. Through my research I came upon what is for me the optimal WOE. I am blood type O+, which are the original carnivores, and that is the basis of my whole WOE. I rarely eat anything processed and usually eat extremely rare beef drenched in coconut oil at almost every meal. I am very boring but that is fine with me. I do other types of proteins of course for variety but nothing makes me happier than a pound of beef! I drink at least half a gallon of decaf green tea every day. I rarely eat out because I do not trust restaurant food (MSG/HFCS is in EVERYTHING!) and do so rarely enough that it does not harm me. Of course getting rid of my cheating, junk food junkie husband helped my health immensely as well.  I also take lots of supplements and am a huge believer in fish oil and aloe. I am mom to four Chinese Crested dogs and they are my loves. I hope to meet Dr. Kruse someday considering he practices right down the street from where I live. How can I make an appointment? ;-p Take care and blessings!

### rbu5@sssnet.com — 2012-03-15T04:06:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hermitage-tn-yes-dr-kruses-hometown.89/post-427>

Hi and welcome TrishaMaria! We need a section for dog lovers. I have three, 2 yorkies and a pom. Glad to have you here! And when you actually meet Doc K in person, you have to tell us all about it!

### LynneM — 2012-03-15T08:49:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hermitage-tn-yes-dr-kruses-hometown.89/post-627>

Trisha, we have a local Paleo group in the Meetup.org site for Nashville!

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-03-15T13:49:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hermitage-tn-yes-dr-kruses-hometown.89/post-669>

Hey Trisha! I love beef, too, although I don't eat it raw! I'm a cattle rancher from South Dakota, so I have tons of cuts at my disposal. I would love for some of your recipes! I'm a boring eater, too...but how can I say beef is boring? It's awesome! Grok on!

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-15T15:09:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hermitage-tn-yes-dr-kruses-hometown.89/post-713>

Welcome to the forum; nice to meet you. I am close to you in Nashville and feel the same way about meeting Dr. Kruse - might happen one day since he's right down the road. Hats off to you for eating beef so close-to-nature. Haven't been able to do that. But I second the eating out part - it's getting harder and harder to enjoy it these days.

# Thread: Minnesota> cold> cold> cold
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minnesota-cold-cold-cold.91/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### robinwoodall — 2012-03-15T03:41:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minnesota-cold-cold-cold.91/post-396>

Hi!

My name is Robin Woodall and I'm from Minneapolis Minnesota. I am an expert of the hCG protocol and just recently published a book that describes why low dose hCG ( through the stimulus of leptin) prevents starvation while eating less than 500 calories. I own a medical hCG clinic and am getting excited to present the concept of CT with our patients (who are willing).

I started taking cold showers a couple of weeks ago and can now tolerate 55 degrees for about 10-15 minutes. I'm hoping this will improve my overall inflammation as an ex-athlete with osteoarthritus. I used to sit in ice baths in college during two-a-days and we'd live with ice backs on our knees, shoulders, and for me- my back. I never thought to use the "ice pack" idea for my overall inflammation....brilliant!

I'm excited to be here and to learn more and more and more and more.........

### jeepifer@gmail.com — 2012-03-15T03:46:51+0200

Hey from South Dakota! I lost 33 lbs. through two rounds of hcg. I've been following the leptin reset (keto-paleo style) since September and added in CT in February. I'm hoping it jumpstarts my adrenals and thyroid again. Just wanted to say hi to my neighbors!

### Szara.loring@gmail.com — 2012-03-17T16:56:37+0200

Way more energy and just all around more, well, everything. I will be well come to thoughts on how to accomplish the CT on a daily basis when the whether gets hot without having to resort to ice baths for hours....

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/southern-ca-desert-area-checking-in.110/post-624>

I don't think we have to do it year round and the shock of going in and out of CT is something to be investigated.

# Thread: Hi from all over SE Asia and San Diego!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-all-over-se-asia-and-san-diego.125/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ninja — 2012-03-15T12:13:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-all-over-se-asia-and-san-diego.125/post-644>

Hi!

I'm originally from Michigan, relocated to San Diego in 2007, and now on a 7 month trip (yay for vacations!) that will include Washington DC for a month, Taiwan for 3 months, South Korea for 12 days, Philippines for a week, and Beijing for 2 weeks. I stumbled upon Dr. Kruse's Leptin Reset/Prescription (LRx) back when I was tried HCG (lost a buncha weight, gained it all back, by the way). When I actually followed the LRx (about 3 weeks late last year), I felt FANTASTIC. All my aches and joint pain went away (huge for me, because I'm aware of a highly likely onset of arthritis because of a history of martial arts and a severe compression fracture in my L2 spine), my recovery from my sports was phenomenal, and I had al the energy in the world while doing my sports (brazilian jiu jitsu, mainly....staying away from my first love, taekwondo, because of my L2).

Once I get back to Cali (and away from all of the "once in a lifetime" tasty treats out here, I will be able to get back into the swing of things. Still trying to figure out how I can keep martial arts in my life while being optimal, if that's even possible. And if it's not possible to be optimal and practice martial arts, then how to be as close to optimal as I can with the martial arts (it's a must in my life).

I am open to questions, answers, new friends, and whatnot. I would love to have people to talk to about this, since my real life friends think I'm nuts

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-03-15T13:46:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-all-over-se-asia-and-san-diego.125/post-666>

Hey Ninja! I'm from South Dakota but was able to speak at a conference in San Diego late last year. I loved it! I'm so jealous of your world travels! Feed us some nuggets of your adventures once in awhile, will you? I don't post much on MDA, just lurk, but I always love what you add to the conversation. Happy to be here on Dr. K's site!

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T16:39:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-all-over-se-asia-and-san-diego.125/post-785>

Hi Ninja!! Enjoy your trek!

# Thread: Howdy from South Dakota!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-from-south-dakota.126/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-03-15T12:16:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-from-south-dakota.126/post-645>

I've got to catch a plane, but I was so excited to see that this forum has been created, that I had to register and say hi!

I'm a rancher from South Dakota, and I freelance write to pay the bills. My highest weight was 177.7; with two rounds of hcg (started last March) couple with the Leptin Reset (keto-paleo started in September), along with CT (started in February), I'm still losing. I weigh 113.4 lbs; I'm in my 20s.

My biggest struggle is my adrenals, thyroid and hashis. I'm very hopeful Dr. K is going to help me turn things around.

I LOVE beef. It's my life. Seriously. If you ever have questions about it, or if you're interested in freezer beef (and live close), I would love to visit.

Grok on and stay cool, new friends! I'm so happy to be here!

### txritchies@att.net — 2012-03-15T16:03:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-from-south-dakota.126/post-746>

Good morning and safe travels!!

### jeepifer@gmail.com — 2012-03-15T17:38:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-from-south-dakota.126/post-827>

Hey, neighbor! I love beef too, dontcha know!

### kc_boxerman@yahoo.com — 2012-03-15T21:37:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-from-south-dakota.126/post-1014>

Hey Paleocowgirl, thanks for dropping me a note - I'm your neighbor in Nebraska. I've just put a deposit on a side of (grassfinished) beef I expect to take delivery of this summer. I'm wondering if you have any recommendations for processing instructions - like a sample or something. This is my first time doing this - I know I want the goodies so I can render tallow, make bone broth, eat the heart (maybe make jerky of it?), but I'm not real experienced preparing any of this stuff. I know it will be dry aged and I'm going to request the better vacuum sealing...

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-04-03T03:57:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-from-south-dakota.126/post-10634>

> **Optim;1015 wrote:** Hey Paleocowgirl, thanks for dropping me a note - I'm your neighbor in Nebraska. I've just put a deposit on a side of (grassfinished) beef I expect to take delivery of this summer. I'm wondering if you have any recommendations for processing instructions - like a sample or something. This is my first time doing this - I know I want the goodies so I can render tallow, make bone broth, eat the heart (maybe make jerky of it?), but I'm not real experienced preparing any of this stuff. I know it will be dry aged and I'm going to request the better vacuum sealing...

Hey Optim,

Sorry I didn't see this earlier. I hope it's not too late to offer up some suggestions.

For processing, it's all based on preference. Personally, we like steaks much more than roasts, so we request that much of the chuck be cut into steaks (chuck steak is the end of the ribeye, so similar taste and fat, but the shape changes). We also don't request much hamburger. It's a common misconception that if you order more hamburger than steaks, you will save money. But, that takes the butcher time, so it's more costly. So, my advice is to keep most of it in steaks.

As far as the "extras," be sure to request he save things like the trim (fat), soup bones, liver, heart, tongue, short ribs, oxtail, etc. You can make jerky with heart, but I just like to slice it really thin and sautee it with CO, salt, pepper, garlic, turmeric and cumin with onions and mushrooms. Oxtail and short ribs are easy in the crockpot. For bone broth, I just keep all the extra bones we eat from steaks in a ziploc bag in the freezer until I have enough to make broth. Then just throw the bones in the crokpot with water, seasoning and a few T of apple cider vinegar and simmer for a day or two.

We go through beef fairly fast, so we don't worry about vacuum sealing -- the paper works well for us. I would skip that step if it will cost you more, jmho.

Good luck! Let me know if you have any other questions!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Hi ya'll! Martin from north Georgia, USA
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-yall-martin-from-north-georgia-usa.134/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martin — 2012-03-15T13:48:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-yall-martin-from-north-georgia-usa.134/post-668>

So good to see that so many people from all over are gathering for what I think is a MAJOR turning point in our lives. I finally see a path out of my imbalances and have already seen and felt a change for the better.

I'm a 5'11" 145 lb. 59 year-old male, retired from the greatest job ever - restoring old Craftsman Bungalow homes in historic neighborhoods in Atlanta. I still miss it, but retiring at age 48 was a miracle that my wife and I pulled off with lots of hard work and frugal living.

We live on 35 acres up in the beautiful north Georgia mountains where I have a large garden (organic, of course), a small orchard, two workshops, big garden shed, and a diesel tractor. Every lad's dream, no?!

Struggled in this life with health issues for as long as I can remember. Mom passed down hypo-T, adrenal stuff, and an interest in improving myself.

I recently found out that I have mold susceptibility haplotype and low alpha MSH haplotype through testing my DNA. I have appointment with great mold doc and have introduced Dr. Kruse's work to him and his biotoxin study group.

I'm on my way out of imbalance.

Martin

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/669=4-IMG_4626.jpg)
