# Thread: Greetings from Missouri
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-missouri.145/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MartiD — 2012-03-15T16:15:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-missouri.145/post-754>

Hi, my name is Marti. I am a 43 yr old Mom with two teenage kids (Lord help me!) I have been overweight off and on for 17 years and I started last Summer with a round of HHCG. My SW was 228 and after 2 rounds I was down to 187 for my lowest drop weight. I did a sudo-LR RX between rounds and immediately went back the RX after round two. I am down to 172 this morning but more significantly I am down two pant sizes from LDW. I am now starting some toning exercising, weights, walking, etc as well as being 3 weeks into CT via Cold Baths. I have been fortunate to not have dramatic detox issues but have been dealing with an allergy issues that came out in the form of hives. I don't believe this is a result of CT because it first appeared in early January before CT, but I have been tested and have a plan of action since apparently I am allergic to everything I breathe!

My original goal in June 2011 was to get down to 185, or a comfortable size 14 which is where I was able to get to 4 years ago before a hystertomy and spinal fusion detoured my stabilization. However, after getting well below that I have readjusted my goals to 160-165. At 5' 6" with a medium frame I realize that I would be more optimal on the BMI chart at 145-155, however, I don't want to get down to a weight I can't maintain. I know I can be a comfortable size 10 at 165. Once I achieve this goal, then I may re-adjust but for now this and becoming LS and optimal are my main goals.

I am grateful for this and other forums that have kept me so focused and taught me so much about my body, foods and overall a healthy way of living. I look forward to "cyber" meeting all of you!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Hi from St. Louis!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-st-louis.149/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### vwaggs — 2012-03-15T17:22:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-st-louis.149/post-812>

I am a 40 yr old mom to two great kids and a very supportive husband. I work in Chihuahua rescue and teach 5th grade. I have always been healthy and involved in sports but right after I had kids I had a hard time taking off the baby weight and keeping it off. Atkins worked for awhile but then I found hCG. I did the homeopathic first and was always hungry so I ordered the Rx stuff but did it subligually. I was always hungry so that was horrible but I lost 30 lbs and hit goal in 2010 and kept 20 of it off. I just tried another round to get the extra 10 lbs off and I was miserable. Nothing else worked so I felt hCG and starving was my only option. Until now. I am finally losing again on the reset and CT without hunger. I am very excited about the overall health benefits! I am finally, after 3 weeks of baths every other night, getting used to the cold and warming up quicker. I love reading the stories of others and their health successes! This has so many possibilites for helping others and I am so grateful that I found Dr. Kruse and his work. Looking forward to having this one place to talk about all these great things and not going back and forth to all the forums.

### lisa32989 — 2012-03-24T16:51:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-st-louis.149/post-5422>

Hi! I just joined. I'm also in St Louis. I started the reset on Feb 7. I'm slowly feeling better. I think its time for some labs to see what's going on!

# Thread: Greetings from Virginia
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-virginia.162/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ChristineKleiber — 2012-03-15T18:49:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-virginia.162/post-881>

same simple three letters, still me here and on MDA, and Robb's website

I'm 47 year old mother of 5 ages 21, 20, 19, 9, & 6.

French, Italian, German and Irish in ancestory, so we can image what epigenitic soup I'm in.

I'm one of those devout Catholics - so where Dr. K talks mindfulness, I insert prayer. It also means that I'm pretty rooted in natural law theory - the earth itself is a revelation of God, and we should be working with it, not against it.

Originally from upstate NY, but haven't been there since I joined the Marine Corps many moons ago and married a now retired Marine. So my health care perspective is military health care and I have quite a bit of experience with it, using MTF's as well as networked providers for primary care. Our 9 year old has a genetic disorder (that's a thread/case study on Robb's forum), so I've been round the specialty provider networks alot too.

I've been a crossfitter since Sept 2008, although I had an ankle injury and surgical repair, so the last year has been working back up to things.

I have the classic symptoms of middle age womanhood. Weight that sticks, sleep that sucks, etcetera, etcetera, etcetera. Basal cell carcinoma has taken some big bites out of my scalp in the last year.

I found Dr. Kruse from a comment on Robb's website, but I had been curious about Leptin for a while from Steve G's blog.

So here I am, happily going LS from LR and saying goodbye to many health issues (current plague going through NoVa excepted)

Short goals - get back to USMC ht/wt standards, have awesome hormone profiles, and see my hair dresser more than my Mohs surgeon this year.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-15T18:55:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-virginia.162/post-886>

good to see you over here!

# Thread: Hello from North Texas
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-texas.166/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### blindpig — 2012-03-15T19:17:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-texas.166/post-904>

Y'all finally broke me down and you're making me post. I think I made an introductory post over on the monster thread at MDA a while back, but I can never keep up with reading so I never posted again. By the time I read something I want to respond to, it's so far in the past, it's irrelevant!

My name is Robin and I discovered Dr. K last October. I started the Leptin Reset on November 2. It has been lifechanging. I have a lot of "issues" (mainly crazy hormones and Hep C) so I haven't moved into post-reset, but I'm not in a hurry because things are working so well for me right now. I have just recently started doing some spot-icing and face-dunking, and as soon as I get a kiddie pool will be doing some cold soaks outside since I don't have a tub.

I finally pinned my husband down and made him listen to Dr. K's interview on Underground Wellness a few nights ago and now have his full support, so I think it will be full steam ahead now.

I want to thank all of you regulars over on the MDA thread. It has been through reading all of your questions and answers that I have learned how to fine-tune things, and you have all inspired me daily. (And I am sure you all know how I feel about Dr. K because you all feel the same...thank you Dr. K for changing my life!)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-long-island-ny.199/post-1373>

Hi everyone!

I've been doing Paleo since 2001 after reading Cordain's first book. fell off the wagon 3 times and regained all the weight, this is my 4th go around. I'm 5'9 medium frame went form 225lb to 184 in about a year eating pretty strict Paleo. Robb Wolf's Paleo Solution was my kick in the butt Feb 2011. while I fall off the wagon occasionally (chocolate is my gateway drug). I am now working my way through all of jacks blog posts from the beginning. looking forward to learning about CT. I am not sure if I am Leptin Sensitive. I look pretty good, but still have a bit of body fat left. (although my wife tells me I do not need to loose any more fat as I will look too skinny) On weekdays I eat only dinner until I am full and I am a bit afraid I eat too little, but I want to trust my body and listen to my satiety so I stop when full). So I think I am leptin sensitive, however when I go off Paleo for any extended time I gain fat fast. (7 day cruise with eating bread and dessert at dinners and 3 meals a day I gained 7 lbs, but lost it all in 2-3 weeks after going back to my normal eating routine) on weekends I eat 2-3 times a day more for social reasons.

I am inspired by Jacks Passion not only for optimizing health , but for his commitment to spreading this message.

As a dentist practicing 15 years, I have noticed the declining health of my patients. its scary. Having seen me lose weight and look better my patients constantly ask me what I am doing. I gove them a list of books and blogs to read, including Jacks!

### Birdy — 2012-03-16T17:44:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-long-island-ny.199/post-1520>

Welcome!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

### BJK77 — 2012-03-19T19:14:39+0200

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-03-19T19:20:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-other-pennsylvanians.213/post-2822>

They don't pay for the "organic" name, but they are definitely pastured, they let my DH walk around the farm to see their habitat and feeding of their "happy chickens" as they called them LOL it is a little farm that has a little cash box next to several doz trays of eggs in a lean-to, we bring our own containers, sometimes family members are there, if not, they work on the honor system.

# Thread: Hi from So. Cal.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-so-cal.215/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ColdBren — 2012-03-16T21:39:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-so-cal.215/post-1660>

Hi everyone. I am so excited about this new forum, I have been addicted to Dr. Kruse's blog and have found some great success with his protocol. I have struggled with nocturnal panic - meaning I have an attack only after falling asleep. I don't have attacks during the day. I have been to docs and on meds for amost 20 years and while the meds helped, my overall health declined. I am off all meds now and doing really well considering everything. I think Dr. Kruse came closest to understanding my problem after hearing one question than all the docs/specialists over the years. My cortisol was a mess and could possibly be the cause of my issues. Anyway, I have a long way to go, but think I am in the right pace with the smartest people to figure out how to get there! Nice to meet everyone.

### Paradox — 2012-03-17T00:01:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-so-cal.215/post-1730>

Hi ColdBren, glad to meet you and everyone else on this forum.. I too am in S.CA but in the low desert, Palm Springs area. I have only in the last three weeks been reading the site and am completely on board. My issues are many due to years and years of conventional medical wisdom and have nearly reversed it this past year with first Primal eating and then since Jan. strict Paleo and now I am working with CT and today was the first day I actually had some complete body sweat! Which is so different for me so it may be kicking in.  can't wait to see what is next.

### ColdBren — 2012-03-17T00:18:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-so-cal.215/post-1736>

Wow, first day congrats!! Hope you take it very gradual, I am learning from myself and others that the first few weeks can send the body and hormones on a roller coaster, great to hear your first day went well!!

# Thread: Just beginning in NC -  Hi!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-beginning-in-nc-hi.216/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### WandaP — 2012-03-16T22:42:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-beginning-in-nc-hi.216/post-1691>

Hi, Everyone! I'm just beginning the Paleo Diet, specifically the Leptin RX! I started 3 weeks ago and have lost 15 lbs. to date. I have at least 125 more to go. Who knows after that? My doctors have not been able to steer me in any direction. My regular GP had me count fat grams for 3 months. I kept it to 15 g. of fat a day and lost 15 lbs. in 3 months. He said that was the best way because it was slow and would stay off. I stalled and quit. I've tried everything out there and have spent many dollars on all kinds of diet books. I quit buying them! I would read them and get started and last about a month or more. I don't intend to look back on this journey. I know it is working, and I definitely know I have a leaky gut. I looked up leaky gut symptoms and nearly cried when I saw I had 2/3 of the symptoms. Why has my doctor not thought of it? I can't wait to go back to him and have him read Jack Kruse's blogs! I'm sure he will take the time, but he might be skeptical at first. Three years ago I had to stop wearing my shoes because I couldn't get my feet in them. They were swollen night and day...and early morning. They would not go down at night. That doesn't even tell you how corroded my legs have been on my shins for 15 years. A dermatologist put me on steroids, and they got better for a while, but off the steroids and back to rash and itching issues. My legs are full of scars and thickened tissue from the scratching I've done over the years. I wish I had taken pictures of them. Only my husband knows the difference. I stopped wearing dresses when the leg and feet swellings would never go down. The doctor upped my blood pressure meds and put me on lasix, too! Not a lot of relief. I was forever surfing the Internet for pictures of other legs like mine to look for remedies. I have never been diagnosed with diabetics, but I know I am prediabetic. I have checked my fasting blood sugar on my parents' meters occasionally. (Both parents are diabetics and still living in their 80's. I have decided to stay with the Paleo Diet because it works and is giving me relief. I didn't want my parents to have to bury their dead daughter! No such thoughts anymore!) Please keep in touch with me and wish me well!

### Chrisgraff — 2012-03-16T23:00:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-beginning-in-nc-hi.216/post-1700>

Welcome to the forum, Wanda! Paleo does work great for keeping glucose in check. Keep up the good work!

### WandaP — 2012-03-16T23:05:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-beginning-in-nc-hi.216/post-1701>

Thanks, Chris. Feedback here will be a great encouragement for me!

# Thread: G'day
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday.233/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lennyaus — 2012-03-17T14:08:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday.233/post-1927>

G'day,

I am Australian. Living in Melbourne at the moment.

I used to be a heavy drinker - like most good old Aussie blokes. I have also had problems with my weight, which in my ignorance never thought that it could have something to do with the effect alcohol had on the liver, which is a vital organ of course, but like most parts of the body are taken for granted. Hell, I even counted calories when I was drinking.

I am 29 going on 30. For as long as I have remembered it has been an issue. Perhaps there was something wrong with me as I remember not being thin ever. However looking back at my year 12 photos... I actually was thin. I was 182 cms and 82 kilograms. For the yanks - that's a shade under 6 foot (I'll claim it) and 180 pounds. But after high school, something happened. Freedom. Liberation from academic studies (not that I was a scholar) and I gained 15+ kilos in the next year. I am currently 111 kilograms (244 pounds) and according to these scales, 29% body fat.

I have never been totally sedentary. I played sports a number of times during the week. Gotta burn those calories you know? Counted calories (even got into "negative" some days on the treadmill). I have tried cleanses. Fasting. Been a member of a number of gyms - and never went. I think I can count 4 memberships of the top of my head. I did use one of the gyms regularly - and I did managed to get down to 92 kilograms from about 105 - but that weights back again (gained it back when I met my fiancÃƒÂ©e. She is worth more than that). I have tried low carbohydrate. I have tried protein modified fasts and also intermittent fasting and now I am trying the leptin reset.

I can't say that I have lost any weight yet. I feel a bit better. I had to cut my nails for the first time in fifteen years (habitual biter, my girl reckons I have an oral fixation). I have been having cold water baths each night under the pretence of recovery aid from sport (train two nights a week and play a game each weekend) as due to being heavy I often pull up sore. I need this to work. I believe in it. I know the original prescription suggested no exercise, however I feel obligated too for the social aspect.

My fiancÃƒÂ©e doesn't believe in any of the nutritional research I have read. You name it and I have probably read it or at least have heard reference too. She doesn't believe me because I am fat. A guy who has read almost everything and is still fat. But she has issues too. OCD. Full blown OCD, not like those annoying people who because they like things in a line, they have self diagnosed themselves with OCD. Anxiety and Panic Attacks brought on by broken routines OCD. She also complains of stomach issues a lot (right after eating sandwiches or stuff - her mums a coeliac but testing has not shown she is - so she will continue eating them and not listen to me. Because I am still fat). Migraines - which incidentally have gone down since she did follow some advice about drinking more water. She is on anti-depressants and also takes the birth control pill. I feel a better diet than what she currently eats (bread, processed soup, pasta, coca cola) would help her immensely. But she won't listen. Because I am still fat. She however is not, she is just under 5'11 and 147 pounds. So I need to make the change that I have been wanting to make for ten years now in order to help the one I love and will marry next year.

I am also playing Australian Rules Football and my enjoyment of which suffers because of my size. Sure, it has its advantages in some ways (falling on people hurts them for example and you can inflict some pain on others) - but I don't train or play football for that reason. I want to actually "play". My size is holding me back here and instead of being a fun social activity with mates from my team, it's a source of anxiety and trepidation. Oh that, anxiety. I do suffer from some forms of that too and my loving fiancÃƒÂ©e want's to talk me into taking anti-depressants and doesn't see why I would not want to. I am hoping my depressed moods (which may be hormone related) and feelings of low self worth are a part of my own personal quilt which will be fixed with time.

Anyway, this has been quite therapeutic and I apologise for making this my post more like a blog post that is the pathetic venting of man who is being undermined by his neolithic thought processes. It gives me hope to see people who have benefited from the information that Dr. Kruse has provided freely from this website and blog. At first I was dismissive when reading, but the avalanche of positive results made it too hard to refuse. Listening to all podcasts he has been on was also illuminating. He did not sound like some crackpot, snake oil salesman. He sounded assured, informed and passionate about what he is doing. That has sold me and I hope I am one of the many who will benefit from his wisdom.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Hello from LI, NY
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-li-ny.247/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### gmel1084 — 2012-03-17T21:50:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-li-ny.247/post-2111>

Hello everyone,

Im relatively new to the subject of hormone optimization but am eager to learn.

Came across this site yesterday.

I have have been athletic my whole life, but despite being very active (I'm on my feet all day work, I have been training intensely in martial arts for years, and also do CrossFit up to 3x/ week), I just can't get to the point with my body where I'm very lean. Coming across the topic of hormone optimization on Mike Mahler's website, I began to wonder if it is Leptin/ Cortisol related. Upon more research, it definitely seems to be the case. With my activity level, I wouldn't be surprised if I'm experiencing adrenal fatigue, HIGH CORTISOL, and LR, IR.

My diet for the most part is good, but I definitely cheat a lot. The last body fat test I got said I was around 13%, but I store most of it in my lower stomach and love handles. For a very active person for years this is frustrating.

I'm 27 years old, 5'7, and 155lbs. Obviously not a bad thing but I'm intrigued by hormone optimization and its role in the fitness industry.

After coming across Dr. Kruse's Leptin RX info I knew I was on the right track.

I try to space out my meals as much as I can, but with the amount of workout volume I do, its hard when you want to optimize your post workout recovery with shakes, and make sure that you're getting enough calories.

Any help is appreciated!

# Thread: MamaGrokkin' on!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mamagrokkin-on.279/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MamaGrok — 2012-03-19T03:17:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mamagrokkin-on.279/post-2546>

I go to Puerto Rico for 3 days and come back and my favorite thread is dead!  I'm kind of afraid of all the time I'm going to be spending on here ... must develop some self-control.

No doubt it was easier to keep up with only one thread, but no doubt that more people will be better served with an entire new optimal forum!

Howdy, y'all!

I'm MamaGrok (I'm probably going to regret that username following me around everywhere, lol) and the LR has changed my life. Last June, I was absolutely at my wit's end after many years of battling sugar addiction, several years of eating traditional foods, over a year of primal eating, and still not being able to get rid of the cravings and binges. They were much further apart, weeks apart, sometimes months apart, but still destroying my health.

Just days after posting this [thread of desperation](http://http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread32223.html), the monster thread at MDA started. I believe it was divine intervention, frankly.

I doubted at first whether doubling my protein intake would make any difference, but once I dove in - wow. Instant death of cravings. They did come back after a long vacation, and then died back again. My binges spread out even more.

But a few things I read and experienced led me to increase my BAB to 65g of protein, and even though even after that monster breakfast, I was still hungry enough to eat it all over again, within a few days, I saw total craving death. I had one more borderline binge (I debated whether to actually call it that, but went with it) at the end of the month. That was October 30, 2011.

I have not had a binge since.

This is totally life-changing for me. I could finally stick to the LR. I could finally stick to primal, to TF, to GAPS, to all the healing protocols I had tried for so many years. I could do them, and I could finally win.

In November, not only did I start feeling full on the 65g BAB, I lost the desire to eat lunch. I have only eaten lunch about 3 times in the five months since, and those were not because I was hungry.

My calories have naturally reduced, and I am naturally IF'ing, as Dr. Kruse predicted. With the effects of crap food out of the way, I have been able to see that dairy is a problem for me, and that even occasional, small amounts of wheat are a problem for me. I've been able to have success with chronic bloating by seeing that I likely have a FODMAP problem. All these food sensitivities stem from my leaky gut, and I've gone a long way toward fixing that.

My lifelong dry skin is gone, and softer than a baby's bottom. It startles me every time I touch it!

For the first time in my life, I don't ever think about food between meals. I easily make a decision about what to eat based on what will nourish me, not my food obsessions, which are virtually eliminated.

I sleep soundly like I did years ago. I don't yet wake with vim & vigor, but I no longer have insomnia or am easily woken and stuck awake after small disruptions.

I don't find myself screaming at my children at five or six o'clock every afternoon. Praise be to God. I just cannot tell you how much of a blessing this is.

I'm sure there is more I'm unjustly forgetting right now. Brain fog is still a battle.

I've stopped gaining weight, and in fact lost 12 pounds and almost four dress sizes. I'm wearing clothes I last wore in 2004 or earlier, and actually rocked my high school cheerleading uniform a few weeks ago.

2012 is the year for the end of chronic bloating, binge eating disorder, chronic fatigue, borderline depression, hypothyroidism, and much more. This is the year I return to living fully. Thank you, Dr. Kruse.

### Kiera2 — 2012-03-19T03:41:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mamagrokkin-on.279/post-2557>

Yay, good to see you made it over here. Hope you enjoyed your brief holiday without kids!

Welcome! I was Mama2Groklets on MDA but had a namE change with the move! You're journe is definitely an inspiring one (I was also a binge eater). Looking forward to reading more posts from you.

### Groketteliveson — 2012-03-19T13:04:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mamagrokkin-on.279/post-2679>

Hi Mamagrok, glad to see you back. Hope your vacation was good!

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-19T14:26:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mamagrokkin-on.279/post-2694>

LOVED the 3 days away. It was a wonderful 15th anniversary with my love.

I should mention for those new to this that it took me at least FIVE times (really, I think it was more) trying the reset to be able to persevere through the 8 weeks. Every time I started, I'd have a binge or enough off-plan eating (little bit of this little bit of that little bit more of this...) that I'd see all the progress I'd made reverse (Dr K is absolutely right-on with the rule of thumb that a decent sized indiscretion = 10-14 days backwards). I'd lose two weeks,, then two more,, then have to restart the counter again. Over and over and over again.

I finally got it done. Jan 1 - Mar 1. 8 weeks, LR accomplished! (My first attempt had been in June!)

My point - if you have cravings that do you in, NEVER give up. Never never never never never give up. It was increasing the protein again and again (while being sure to eat plenty of fat and plenty of other nutrient-dense foods) that enabled me finally to stick to this and get the healing done. And yes, I'd tried sticking to many other dietary protocols, but none of them had been able to stop the bingeing. Finally, finally, success.

# Thread: Hi from Seattle
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-seattle.292/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Toni1675 — 2012-03-19T18:01:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-seattle.292/post-2781>

My name is Toni and I've lived in Seattle now for 7 years orignally from sunny CA. For 6 years when winter has rolled around here I find reasons to try and get back to Ca except for this year....

My story is I was a athlete all through school and once that stopped the eating didn't and neither did the weight gain. At my largest I was 330lbs in 2003. I never noticed how big I had gotten until I was at a ride at Disneyland and I couldn't fit. Then and there I actually sat back and counted my daily calorie intake. It came to an astounding 12000 calories a day....holy crap! I went to the Dr. for help and they actually told me I should have gastric bypass surgery. I told them to go to hell!! I thought to myself..I'm an athlete I can do this on my own, so I did. I dropped my calorie intake to 2000 a day but kept all my favorite foods like pizza, burger, and fries but dropped my weight to 240 in 9 months. I'm 6'1" female that carries 240lbs pretty good so I was happy but because I didn't know anything about types of food being bad I started to gain weight back. By 2005 I was 280...for the next 5 years it bobbed between 240-285 no matter how much I ran or excersiced. I was also diagnosed with on set of diabetes so I was now taking 2000 mg of Metformin and I had to eat every 2 hours.

I was miserable...until June of 2010 Paleo entered my life. The first 2 weeks were pure HELL for me. I went through withdrawals so bad because of my bread addiction and my soda addiction. After that I felt amazing! I started running more and more triathlons and then crossfit. Oh how I loved crossfit! My weight was down to 210 and I was feeling stronger and stronger so I started going to CF 6 days a week. I lasted that way until May of 2011 when something happened...I started getting more injuries and just feeling like crap.

I stopped CF in June 2011 and just started lifting heavy and was feeling ok so I bought a rower for those rainy Seattle days but nothing was helping it was getting worse. In Dec 2011 I entered a 100k rowing challenge and made it to 45000k until I hit my wall in mid Dec. I just stopped everything all I wanted to do was sleep, which was crappy sleep and eat.

I was not happy cause I was entering winter months in Seattle and I knew I wouldn't see the sun for awhile so depression was kinda setting in until I decided to try snowboarding again. I know this sounds crazy but it changed my whole mindset. Before I hated being in the cold, wet conditions but now I craved it!! I went every weekend and some week days too. I didn't understand it but I didn't care I was happy again and eating good and was feeling better as days went by.

On the way home one Sunday of snowboarding at Mt Baker my partner was listening to a podcast and Dr Jack Kruse was on. I liked what this guy had to say it was very interesting to me how he spoke and made things very simple. I was already Paleo but I couldn't for the life of me loose my belly fat and no one else could help me either. The next day at work I found his website and became obsessed.

I am now 2 1/2 weeks into the Leptin RX and things are changing. My waist line is getting leaner and I have absolutely NO cravings...its kinda nuts!! Oh did I mention I'm also 2 1/2 weeks in to CT with bath water temps at 50 degrees I have worked up to an hour. It feels amazing. My skin is changing and my energy is through the roof!! Thank you so much Dr. Kruse for giving everyone this amazing information!! You are changing lives everyday!!!

I will keep you all updated as I have new changes. But for the first time in 7 years I love living in Seattle because its not cold anymore to me. I can wear shorts and t shirt on 40 degree days and feel great!!

Good luck to everyone

T


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

### esam5 — 2012-03-22T19:07:38+0200

happy happy joy joy

# Thread: Why we need to work our local groups
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-we-need-to-work-our-local-groups.302/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SimonM — 2012-03-19T23:38:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-we-need-to-work-our-local-groups.302/post-2958>

> Most of us arenÃ¢â‚¬â„¢t lucky enough to have a community of Paleo friends living in our neighborhood. Some of us are the only Paleo people we know in real life. This is why events like PaleoFX are so important. Sure, weÃ¢â‚¬â„¢re learning. Sure, weÃ¢â‚¬â„¢re meeting people that have changed our lives. Sure, we might learn a new recipe or two, but itÃ¢â‚¬â„¢s really meeting people that are just like us that is the true value of an event like this for me. We know that our ancestors lived in communities, were raised by the village and had support from all sides. We donÃ¢â‚¬â„¢t live in that world anymore. WeÃ¢â‚¬â„¢re alone in our boxes, separated from family by oceans and even further isolated from society because we wear weird shoes and donÃ¢â‚¬â„¢t eat the same food as everyone else. I think joining a CrossFit box is akin to joining a Church for some of us: itÃ¢â‚¬â„¢s the only community we have.

[http://www.primalgirl.com/2012/03/19/paleo-fx-2012-five-people-who-will-change-your-life/](http://http://www.primalgirl.com/2012/03/19/paleo-fx-2012-five-people-who-will-change-your-life/)

Very well put by an official "starfish", Tara aka Primal Girl. You've got to check out [her journey](http://http://www.primalgirl.com/about/). Thanks Tara!

# Thread: hi from freezing finland :)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-freezing-finland.315/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### eva — 2012-03-20T14:34:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-freezing-finland.315/post-3199>

im a 31 yo powerlifter that was diagnozed with MS 11 years ago. i refusedthe medications and decided to find a way to fixmyself.soi started to study biochemistry and evolutionary molecular chemistry (in adition to my major in geology) and firstly dumperall gluten and lofat dairy +seed/vegetable oils and processed foods. in 2004 i went mainly keto paleo. i have used cold showers a lot in my life but about 6 months ago i started to use them daily to combat DOMS. now im in my 3 week of official CT with 2 x 20 min cold showers daily and icepacks.

### Inger — 2012-03-20T15:28:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-freezing-finland.315/post-3214>

Hi Eva!

I am in Finnland too. Your story is uplifting Eva!

You are crazy to do 20 min. cold showers!!! Huh. I do cold showers too as we have no bathtub, but much less, about 10 min. at most, and then I shower my legs first for 4 minutes, then my arms for another 4 minutes and then my body for 2 minutes. Takes long time for me to warm up after..bbrrr..

Mostly I do 7-8 minutes though. Two times / day. How cold is your tap water Eva?

I live in Kirkkonummi and here our tapwater is only 5,5 degrees celcius.

Inger

### eva — 2012-03-20T16:35:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-freezing-finland.315/post-3244>

great to here im not the only lunatic in this country

the only issue with taking showers so long is it gets boring - have to get me some waterresistant ipod thingy and some books to listen to . and the looks i get in the gym shower are to die for ofcourse. it has taken me a long time to get to 20 minutes. i started 6 months ago like you just showering legs and arms. when i read DR. Ks CT 3 weeks ago i started just walking under the cold shower straight - so now i start with overall shower and then alternate front and back depending on my skincolour. the water i get here (herttoniemi) is between 4-5 celsius. i used to live in kirkkonummi also for a couple of years 2008-2010.

### Inger — 2012-03-20T16:55:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-freezing-finland.315/post-3251>

Thank you Eva! You give me hope.. I guess I have to just take my time and not push too hard.

In 6 months I will do 20 minutes too I promise.

What are you looking for with the skin color? That it does't turn white? My skin get bright red after about 1-2 minutes in the shower.

Alternating front and back sounds nice, I'll try it that way tomorrow.

I love Kirkkonummi, nice to hear you have been living hier too! I bet you loved to go swimming in lake Meiko too..

I will do my CT there as soon as the ice melts, believe me.

### eva — 2012-03-20T21:10:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-freezing-finland.315/post-3436>

yes, definitely take your time. no point in rushing it. im also waiting for the spring so can go and dip at the beach that is close here! by the skincolour i mean that i have noticed that after i get numb i might turnfrom cherry red to more of a plum colour.i guess im a little afraid to go overboard now afrter i got a coldnip (from icepacking granted), so im cautious now.

### Adora — 2012-03-20T17:51:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/syracuse-ny-diabetic.321/post-3288>

Hi I'm from Syr. NY and a type 1 diabetic. I've had a very hard time with going low carb. I love the cold, dark, sleep stuff. Are there any other diabetics out here? How is the adaptation process for you? Advise for diabetics?

### Chrisgraff — 2012-03-22T20:05:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/syracuse-ny-diabetic.321/post-4567>

I was a new T2 until the Leptin reset diet stopped it "cold"

Of course, T1 is an entirely different disease. There are many diabetics (including T1) on [www.diabetesforum.com](http://http://www.diabetesforum.com/diabetes-diet-nutrition/) that have had very good results eating a high fat/low carb diet. Maybe your answer lies between these two websites.

...and welcome to the forum!

# Thread: So how did you hear about Dr. Kruse?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 55
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MamaGrok — 2012-03-22T05:09:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-4253>

MDA? HCG forum? Friend? Facebook? Googling? Paleo conference? Paleo blog? Doctor?

And my big question ... are there any of you out there who know him because he was your doctor (or friend or family member) first? (Would LOVE to hear your experiences!)

I'll go first - the monster thread at MDA was my first exposure to leptin & to Kruse. It hit home and passed all my tests (science, personal experience, common sense, gut reaction...), so I hung around to ask questions for a while, then dove in. The rest is history.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-22T05:13:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-4256>

Monster-thread on MDA was my entry-ticket too!

### Kiera2 — 2012-03-22T05:23:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-4264>

I had been following Weston Price for over a year, then the GAPS diet for 2 years. It was from Kristina, over on the GAPS list, that I first heard the term 'paleo'. Then I read Primal Body, Primal Mind, and it was when I was googling for paleo info that I stumbled across Dr Kruse's blog. Well, that had me intrigued immediately, and I started the Leptin Rx straight away, and kept reading. It was Dr Kruse who introduced me to MDA, and it took me over two weeks back in November to catch up on that thread.

### Birdy — 2012-03-22T05:39:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-4270>

I read some of his posts on paleohacks, and then heard him on Jimmy Moore.

### ColdBren — 2012-03-22T05:43:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-4273>

An HCG forum, so glad I stumbled upon it!

### Casey — 2012-03-22T06:12:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-4274>

MDA. I read Mark's posts daily. I browsed the forum threads a few times - each time I'd see the Leptin Reset AKA monster thred. Seeing that people were constantly posting in it I thought I'd check it out. I ended up registering, started the protocol straight away, and spent the first few days reading the entire thread as well as Dr K's website.

### Sueville — 2012-03-22T06:16:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-4275>

From his first interview with Jimmy Moore which lead me to MDA and PaleoHacks.

### Martin — 2012-03-22T13:22:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-4298>

My hands and feet hurt so bad that I posted on the Georgia Weston Price forum and a member responded and said I needed to check out what Dr. Kruse had to say on his site about peripheral neuropathy.

I think about this often. How another person took the time to pass on some information to help another.

Paying forward.

Martin

### JeffieJoe — 2012-03-22T14:00:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-4305>

I actually stumbled across his blog long ago, but to be honest it was too deep for me at that very moment. I then heard him mentioned on the hcgdieters group and read about him on the MDA forum about the same time. Which all pointed me back to his blog. I would not consider myself stupid and probably have more common sense then the average person; however, Dr. Kruse REALLY makes me think. Which I love at times and others I really am too tired to think about it and I want the bullet points with fact and details to back it up.  Am I the only one?? Really, I'm not being critical as I truly love all the Dr K has done and is doing. I just need to get my brain optimal too

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-22T14:36:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-4321>

Don't worry, JeffieJoe, I'm the same way. And I can't tell you how many times I've encountered something, knew it was for me but "not yet", and then found it pounding me from all directions some other time, when God decided it was time for me!

> **Coriander;4290 wrote: I had been following Weston Price for over a year, then the GAPS diet for 2 years. It was from Kristina, over on the GAPS list, that I first heard the term 'paleo'. Then I read Primal Body, Primal Mind, and it was when I was googling for paleo info that I stumbled across Dr Kruse's blog. Well, that had me intrigued immediately, and I started the Leptin Rx straight away, and kept reading. It was Dr Kruse who introduced me to MDA, and it took me over two weeks back in November to catch up on that thread.
>
>
> I'm impressed that anyone would pick up Dr. Kruse's blog off google and actually read it (prior to the new geek/non-geek format)! I think he would have struck me as a looney-tune had I not already been exposed to his ideas through MDA and his personal interaction on the monster thread. That said, another alternative would be to see him as an eccentric genius, which is closer to my perception now, lol. (or maybe, really truly, just one with bad typing  )
>
>
>
> Martin;4326 wrote:** My hands and feet hurt so bad that I posted on the Georgia Weston Price forum and a member responded and said I needed to check out what Dr. Kruse had to say on his site about peripheral neuropathy.
>
>
>
> I think about this often. How another person took the time to pass on some information to help another.
>
>
>
> Paying forward.
>
>
>
> Martin

Wow - that was me.  Ssshhh, don't give away my name; this is a publicly searchable forum and I like being anonymous so I can really put a lot more of me out there in my journal & such. Has this helped you any in the PN department? I have a dear friend with a little girl really battling it and always keep my ears perked for info on it.

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-22T15:17:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-4343>

Jimmy Moore podcast. I quickly followed on Facebook. Much of the detailed writing is above my head since I don't have a science background, but the advice has been very helpful.

### ruby — 2012-03-22T15:22:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-4347>

via Dr. Davis and Wheat Belly. Got two birds of paradise  with one stone. so lucky!

### texasmom4 — 2012-03-22T15:32:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-4355>

Colleen started a thread on the big hcg diet forum about Dr. K... I bookmarked it a long time ago but didn't really start digging in until mid-January. Read hundreds of pages of the thread on the MDA forum, too.

### debbiemikus — 2012-03-22T15:37:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-4361>

I first heard of JK on paleohacks and have since been devouring his every word. Genius is the perfect description of his work. Sometimes I have to read and reread but it all makes perfect sense and I am so very grateful that he is sharing his knowledge with us. I look forward to each new post and I guess you could say " I hang on his every word". If he says it works, I will try it.

### David — 2012-03-22T15:48:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-4367>

> **ruby;4375 wrote:** via Dr. Davis and Wheat Belly. Got two birds of paradise  with one stone. so lucky!

### Ane — 2012-03-22T18:01:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-4467>

Coleen's thread on the HCG forum.

### TerrierMom — 2012-03-22T20:20:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-4579>

I read Tim Ferriss' book 4 hour body (always a fan of the most results for the least effort) and was just getting ready to slow-carb, was browsing Tim's forums and found a post on LEPTIN that led to Dr K's blog. (THANK YOU to whoever posted that!!)

After reading for a while I realized that as a morbidly obese person, slow carbs and "cheat days" were NOT in my future if I wanted to get well. So I hustled over here for some more reading. Got Sisson's and Wolf's books, read them, and am trying to figure this whole thing out. So much to learn! So glad I started with the Leptin RX and not Slow Carb! SO GLAD Dr K has his OWN forum. And such a great community ready to help each other!

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-22T20:43:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-4593>

Someone on a thyroid forum posted a link to Dr. K as a resource. I clicked, I read and read some more, then jumped in, drank the kool-aid and now I'm hooked! What he has done for me was to emphasize the whole body and got me away from fousing on one or two organs. I'm ever grateful to whoever that person was on the thyroid forum.

### Owl — 2012-03-22T22:34:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-4628>

Maile on the hcg forum told me about him and his work on leptin. That led me to Paleohacks and MDA, and I started the leptin reset last July.

### judithzthompson@hotmail.com — 2012-03-22T23:20:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-4652>

Some time during 2010 - I was low-carbing (still am), found Jimmy Moore via Atkins, saw Jack post comments on Jimmy's facebook - he took no prisoners, his attitude and honesty impressed the heck out of me. I said then that this guy was about to turn the world on its head. I garner my knowledge in numerous ways - I read his blogs (understanding very little initially as I'm no scientist by a long shot, but I do have common sense), then reading the comments/answers explain alot - I may leave it there even I don't quite get it - the follow up blogs usually pull it all together for me and I get the "ah hah" moment.

### Huck — 2012-03-23T04:02:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-4799>

Someone started a thread on LowCarbFriends about Dr Kruse, the Leptin Rx, and the MDA thread. Cravings had killed my weight loss every time I tried for almost 10 years, so I was ready to try just about anything to beat them. For me, my cravings were gone after the 1st BAB.

### interludium.env@gmail.com — 2012-03-23T05:11:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-4838>

I discovered Jimmy Moore in 2009 and it was after that when I heard Dr Kruse on Jimmys program. I was pretty thrilled when Jimmy asked a question of Dr Jack when he recently appeared on Jimmy's show "Ask the Low Carb Experts".

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-23T06:17:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-4864>

A friend recommended a site about 'fixing your metabolism.' That woman mentioned WheatBelly. And then I started Googling. I found Dr Kruse that way. I started reading his blog and then he mentioned MDA which led to me finding the monster thread.

### donkjellberg — 2012-03-23T09:40:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-4884>

Trawling Jimmy Moore's podcast archive. Kruse rocks!

### dkenward@yahoo.com — 2012-03-23T12:11:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-4891>

I had become obsessed with the blog Hyperlipid. There they do a lot of debunking the endless stream of studies that seek to validate the Lipid Hypothesis. I had almost zero biology in school because it did not interest me at the time. But I found it fascinating that something so ingrained in our culture as true was by any objective measure as imaginary as Santa Claus!

One of the frequent commenters on Hyperlipid mentioned Jack's blog. It was something like, "I got this tip from him that if you drink green tea it suppresses your appetite. But don't bother with reading his blog because it's just another ketogenic diet." That was all the endorsement I needed! Before I knew it I was cooking BAB and gobbling down coconut oil.

### quelsen — 2012-03-23T17:25:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-4991>

I was on the HCG forum and the MDA forum and i had been really working hard on figuring out why HCG works and saw the monster thread and didnt want to read its 400 pages LOl so i read the quilt and then hid blogs and more white papers and journals and i had my first Dr Crush after hating on all allopathians for half a decade

### L8F — 2012-03-23T19:12:17+0200

What is all this "Leptin" yammer on MDA threads? is how I got there. Plus regular style Primal didn't work out for me weight-wise so I figured why not...then I started reading like a lunatic.

### ChristineKleiber — 2012-03-23T19:35:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-5066>

total internet grace. I was cruising Robb Wolf's site and one of his questions for a podcast mentioned the Leptin reset. Since I had heard about this Leptin before via MDA and links to Stephan G, I wanted to read what this doc had to say about Leptin.

And I stayed because the science is hard. The amazing Emil T. Hofman was my chemistry professor in college. If he ever heard of me giving in because it was making my hair hurt, he'd probably change my grade. And that is something I value almost more than the diploma.

Fun read - if you like biographies

[http://magazine.nd.edu/news/25982/](http://http://magazine.nd.edu/news/25982/)

### Susan M. — 2012-03-23T19:59:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-5079>

Found comments made by Dr. Kruse on the various LC/Paleo/Primal blog sites and knew then that this was a bright man. I was really tickled when Jimmy Moore had him on a podcast. I was even more thrilled when he started his own blog last year. I never had science in H.S. (except for two months of biology) and probably would not have liked it at the time anyway. But NOW! Oh my goodness! I am having a fantastic time reading all of his blog and responses in the comment sections. I am 58 years old, and I am loving learning so much about how the human body works, and is supposed to work. Fascinating stuff! I was not sure whether he was brilliant or crazy, but now I am voting for brilliant as well as extremely caring and giving. BTW, a quote I saw somewhere about his methods I have adopted, "I drank the Kool-Aid and I am totally on board!"

### Larry — 2012-03-23T20:44:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-5097>

I found Dr. Kruse late last summer and spent about 10 minutes looking at his website. At that time I wasn't ready in my journey to learn the science and quite honestly I forgot about him. I "re-found" him via Jimmy Moore's Best of the Year Podcasts & I am hooked. He is the first guy who I believe has it completely figured out. Many people have parts of it right but Dr. Kruse has the whole package!

### Optimalbound — 2012-03-23T21:56:35+0200

### Valorie33 — 2012-03-23T22:39:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-5148>

I heard about the Leptin Rx on the Atkins forum. I had lost almost 50 lbs on Atkins however I stalled and had stayed the same for over 6 months. A lady named Rebecca (who was actually in the Atkins book) started a topic on the reset and I started back in July. I did hit 50 lbs at one point, now I bounce back and forth about 6 lbs. I am pretty discouraged but I know that hormones are my issue and I am trying to work with a Naturopath to get tested.

When I contacted her and said I was committed to what I am currently doing and just need some hormone help, she agreed to work with that, however the first visit she saw what I was eating and immediately wanted me to add an apple and almonds to my day. I tried it for a week, gained 6 lbs and have been really struggling to get clean ever since. I NEED to stay below 20 gr of carb just to maintain my loss. She also wanted me to cut the beef and pork, she said they were inflammatory ? I have been trying but I don't think it is helping me.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-24T04:03:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-5288>

I kind of stumbled upon Dr K 's blong in late May 2011 by accident. I had been searching google for a specific thread on MDA I was looking for and not having any success using the search function on MDA. And BAM. Read Levee 1 and I was hooked. Especially since work was super slow and if I don't engage my brain well I get dangerous b/c I start developing projects to work on outside the scope of what i should be doing. Started the leptin Reset the Tuesday after memorial day and haven't looked back.

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-24T04:06:35+0200

### PrimalPam — 2012-03-24T04:36:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-5309>

This will sound silly, but about 2 years ago, there was a tiny little blurb in "Self" Magazine about reasons you may not be losing weight...leptin was listed. I googled "Leptin & Hunger", and came upon Dr. Michael Eades. I devoured his blog (still do), but felt like I still may be missing something. Dr. Eades is a LC advocate, but I wanted more info on leptin. So I googled again last fall, and came up with a link on dietforhumans.com to Dr. Kruse. I haven't stopped reading since...

And I am just so thankful that I found Dr. Kruse last fall, because it would be overwhelming to try and get caught up now! LOL

### Birdy — 2012-03-24T05:24:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-5328>

I had already been following Dr. Rosedale, so I perked up when I heard "leptin". In the early days, we had to slog through every post (sometimes 3-4 times), as that was before the summaries. There was already lots of controversy on paleohacks, but eventually things established themselves on MDA. People over at PH didn't believe he was a real doc and made him change his name. For awhile, the whole MDA forum was abuzz with it, but then it settled down to the main monster thread. I was sold after reading the rT3 post, hormones 101 I think. By the time AHS11 came around, there were already tons of people doing the LReset successfully, and he ruffled more than a few feathers there. By then, lots of people were fully engulfed in paleo2.0.

### kevinrutherford@msn.com — 2012-03-24T21:39:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-5535>

I first stumbled across Dr k on the MDA form I use to post there a lot but stopped visiting regularly I still eat pb but one day I was board and stumbled across the monster thread

### ste colombe — 2012-03-25T13:18:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-5746>

I heard his first interview on Jimmy Moores podcast. Then started reading and re reading and then digesting...... and voila. I love the new format, I do end up by reading the "geeks unite" eventually but its good to get the basics in "normal language". Its a shame there are not more doctors out there with the time or inclination to open their minds and read more. Most a so well meaning and over worked but you just want to scream at them when they give a sweet to your child after a consultation... or just want to prescribe the blabla for whatever. Noone here in Hong Kong is on the page, so its good to have this site and to be following a doctor really wanting to make a difference and to shout about it.

### LinD — 2012-03-25T16:06:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-5757>

> **ColdBren;4299 wrote:** An HCG forum, so glad I stumbled upon it!

Same here, specifically, Grammy1952 (Colleen).  After the first round of hCG, I started the Leptin Reset about three Weeks into P3. Did extremely well once implementing BAB to maintain 30# weight loss.

Considered a second round of hCG more as an experiment to alleviate low back pain that was there before hCG, but at some point, I realized disappeared while on hCG. Not only the back pain, but an M.S. symptom of numbness on my side & abdomen went away, too.

But then a new MS symptom developed with numbness in both feet! This was six weeks after ending hCG injections. And the back pain returned. While I didn't have much more weight to lose (10sh or so #), I decided on the second hCG round.

After the second round of hCG (middle of November 2011), I couldn't wait to get back on BAB. (After the second hCG round, you'd never catch me complaining about getting all the protein grams in for BAB!)

Results of the experiment--the back pain was alleviated, but the numb feet remains. I've never had a symptom hang around SO long! It's been since Sept 18, 2011. I am hoping CT will help resolve this. I'm tired of feeling like I am walking around in sand.

I know the MS could be worse, but after 22 years since first diagnosed, I don't want to suddenly go downhill. You wouldn't know if you met me that I suffer from this disease, though I am a little whacky these days... Paleo eating, cold baths, and all. Thanks, Dr. K!

~ Valiantly keeping the 40# loss! ~

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-25T19:22:42+0300

### Slone07 — 2012-03-26T02:40:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-6142>

I learned about Dr. Kruse through the monster thread at MDA. In fact, I'm still reading it and have a long way to go! I started the Leptin Reset about four days ago and since I'm dedicated, thought I would come over here to support his site while I log my progress.

It's kind of funny (and sad!) to me...I "know" so many of you already from reading that thread (and cheering you on) and you know absolutely nothing about me! I hope to change that.

### Powder — 2012-03-26T04:17:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-6192>

I am honestly not sure. I was just finishing up my 4th complete round of P90x, and still had a little belly fat and remainder of man boobs that just wouldn't go away. A friend of mine had read Wheat Belly, and was raving about it. I was too cheap to buy the book (at the time), and so I started reading what I could online. I was ready to get really serious about nutrition, because exercise alone, and the diet that I was on, wasn't getting me over that last hump. I knew something was still "off", and nutrition just seemed to be that piece. I was eating pretty good at the time, but had no real structure, and only my gut (pun intended) to tell me what was right or wrong.

So, in about a two week span from the end of January into February, I bought Wheat Belly, started reading, continued to read what I could online, and stumbled across this site. Been hooked ever since, and I never even finished Wheat Belly. I just passed it on to somebody else, to hope that it could be an entry point for them as well. That being said, I still point everybody I can to this site.

### nonchalant — 2012-03-26T04:50:44+0300

### Susan M. — 2012-03-26T04:54:43+0300

### Sherry — 2012-03-26T16:24:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-6333>

My hormones have been out of whack for years and after 15 years of BHRT and no real help I ditched them. So, I was looking for some natural way to reestablish balance when I came across some information about leptin and couldn't find enough factual background on how it was important so I googled it along with hormone balancing and Dr. K's website came up. This was in December and I still have not completely read each of his posts but I have a huge dent in the list now. I started the leptin Rx on Jan 1. I started the CT the day he wrote about it in February. While my hormones have not magically balanced, things are better. Oh wait, they're worse. OK, now they're better again. I'm trying to avoid BHRT again as I'm 56 and think my body should be fine the way nature intended but I'm keeping an open mind as I learned to trust Dr. K's input after just a few blogs. And, with the benefits of CT I'll just wait and see.

### Tizawee — 2012-03-27T03:42:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-6733>

I have a friend who lives in South Africa. Sometime last year she found out she had leaky gut issues. I have celiac disease and a whole host of food allergies that I attribute to leaky gut. So when she emailed me a link to some Kruse guy's web site in January and asked me what I thought, I had to check it out. (There's alot of bad info out there about leaky gut.) Lo & Behold, the information was good. After I read the page, I saw something called Leptin Reset. Wondering to myself, "What is a leptin and why does it need a reset switch," I read that too. I was hooked. Before I knew it I was wading through the MDA Monster Thread, the Quilt, CT and seeing references to Dr Kruse and posts by him on other web sites I visit. I began LR on Feb 23rd and haven't looked back. Two weeks ago I got my husband started on it as well. I am currently face-dunking... DH is waiting to see if I drown before he tries it.

### freesia — 2012-04-04T05:39:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-11144>

I saw Leptin mentioned on a Fibromyalgia forum a while back and a couple of those gut (stomach?) hormones as well. Filed it away then noticed journals on MDA talking about Leptin Reset. How I missed the monster thread I have NO idea!

Anyway, googling Leptin Reset in the past couple of weeks brought me here quicksmart and I kinda fell in love with some of Dr Kruses other ideas too. So I signed up and I'm 4 days in to LR with CT just getting onboard.

### skline@peak.org — 2012-04-04T06:59:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-how-did-you-hear-about-dr-kruse.352/post-11163>

Monster thread, like so many others. I resisted opening it for awhile, which just meant that I had a long reading task once I did.

Told my sister about it, and directed her to some of the blog posts. She set out way late in the day to march through the whole Monster Thread, and she just as finished the MDA part of it and signed up here. She also has started CT with good results. Her low carb high BG numbers are considerably reduced. Her weight is the same and her pants are looser.

I'm still just nibbling at the edges of cold, which sounds really wimpy, but I felt I needed to get over that super-high-pressure concert (which turned out to be incredible!) and to mop up other loose ends before loading another stress on top of what is already there.

# Thread: and this just in....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/and-this-just-in.353/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mixolydia — 2012-03-22T07:35:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/and-this-just-in.353/post-4278>

Hi everyone! I'm so glad I found this group!

I was reading some of what Dr. Kruse wrote and was relieved to see that he addressed the issue of cortisol and reverse T3...One of the things that causes this is fasting...which is why the Paleo diet seems to be contraindicated for me...

I was wondering (I've googled to no avail) if there are medications that can prevent the t4 metabolism into straight t3...

I don't actually expect an answer, I'm just voicing a concern...I've just returned from 3 days of seeming insanity thanks to elevated cortisol+my attempting to use iodine/iodide drops-as per Dr. Bernstein. I'd very much prefer to NOT take the t3 med. and just get my thyroid addressed with the iodine/iodide route, but can't do that if it's going to do the T4->rt3 waltz!

I'd like to try the Paleo diet but there must be a way to stabilize my hormones before the fact as the fasting would induce rt3-again?

### Kiera2 — 2012-03-22T07:58:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/and-this-just-in.353/post-4281>

Hi! I have followed the Leptin Rx since last November. There's no need for any fasting; why are you concerned?

How much iodine did you take? I think Dr Bernstein uses a lot. Did you take selenium for a while first? You really need to be sure you have enough before adding iodine.

### debbiemikus — 2012-03-22T15:30:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/and-this-just-in.353/post-4350>

Hi Coriander, Have you been following the LR because you like it or are you still resistant?

>

I'd very much prefer to NOT take the t3 med. and just get my thyroid addressed with the iodine/iodide route, but can't do that if it's going to do the T4->rt3 waltz!
>
>
>
> I'd like to try the Paleo diet but there must be a way to stabilize my hormones before the fact as the fasting would induce rt3-again?

I was just getting ready to start this but was going to wait until the weekend. Did you give up or are you just feeling better?

### Kiera2 — 2012-03-23T04:21:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/and-this-just-in.353/post-4812>

> **debbiemikus;4378 wrote:** Hi Coriander, Have you been following the LR because you like it or are you still resistant?

I like it. I'm eating a smaller BAB, and stopped eating lunch ages ago. Although I added a small lunch back about a week ago. We're heading into winter and it seems that cooler weather combined with CT is making me hungry. I think I'll add more fat at breakfast and see if that makes a difference.

### Slone07 — 2012-03-22T17:42:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/joining-to-log-my-leptin-reset-journey.362/post-4446>

Followed over from MDA's Monster Thread, which I'm still reading (page 150 or so). Started the leptin reset (diet) yesterday. I've been eating low carb and/or Paleo for a few years, so it's just a matter of changing the timing of meals and quitting snacking for me (and sleep...my biggest downfall...I'm exceedingly nocturnal). So far, so good, though.

I'm excited about this forum and am eager to do my part in making this worthwhile to Dr. Kruse so that the information continues to spread.

Thanks for reading,

Slone

# Thread: I'll go next
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ill-go-next.408/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kevinrutherford@msn.com — 2012-03-24T22:26:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ill-go-next.408/post-5552>

Hi all, Kev from MDA. If you want to know more about me you can check out my journal. [http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread9234.html](http://http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread9234.html). I had lost a lot of weight on the PB but stalled at 205lbs. I knew enough about leptin to suspect that it was my issue, but nothing I found told me what to do about it. Some said, eat more carbs, others IF, others said relax and don't try and eventually it weightless would start again. I had previously read the warrior diet so I integrated that into the PB. For over a year I ate primal, warrior diet style, and go no where, even put on about 20 lbs. One day I was browsing MDA and stumbled across the monster thread. it didn't take long and I was on the leptin reset. I am still reading through the monster thread, I'm on post 9081 so i have a way to go to finish it.

On Jan 6th I was 228lbs, started the leptinRX and today I am back to my low of 205lbs. I started working out about a month ago after several false starts, I lifted got sore and waited another 10 days to try again. eventually I lifted and had no soreness and knew it was time to get going on heavy weights 2-3 times a week and sprints 1-2 times a week. I started CT 2 weeks ago with Ice packs and face dunks, I just emerged from my 2nd bath 40 minutes at 58 degrees without any problems other than being board, now I am ice packing my upper body that wasn't submerged.

I am still eating the LeptinRX but plan on moving to the post LeptinRX plan soon.

### fitness@home — 2012-03-25T00:32:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ill-go-next.408/post-5591>

Hi Kev. What part of Ohio are you from? I am originally from SE MI, very close to the OH state line.

Sounds like things are falling into place for you!

### kevinrutherford@msn.com — 2012-03-26T04:12:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ill-go-next.408/post-6189>

I am from the Dayton area, but i make it up north to the Cleveland area pretty often.

### dheedude — 2012-03-29T01:19:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ill-go-next.408/post-7988>

I'll go next to the place which I can relax and I feel the breeze of the air. I'm dheedude happy to be here. Any way do you have any idea about [hot tub accessory](http://http://www.aluminumspacovers.net)?

# Thread: Hey..from the other side of the world...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-from-the-other-side-of-the-world.423/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### gemstar — 2012-03-25T08:34:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-from-the-other-side-of-the-world.423/post-5727>

I'm located in Israel. Been playing with low carb for many years. I have picture of me with my daughter who is now 28 looking great from being on the good ole' Atkins plan. I did CALP (carbo addicts life plan) and lost weight. But of course the yo-yo effect was always a part of life. I now am low carb and some days almost paleo. I still have issues, and eventually will try to do LR.

I 'envy' you Americans and your grass fed local beef/lamb/chicken. The closest thing we have here in Israel is frozen beef imported from South America. There is alot of local beef that some even enjoy free range. But we do not have green pastures all year round, in fact we only have green pastures for 4 months a year in our arid climate. Beef cattle is then fed hay and fermented vegetables and chicken manure. Our lamb is not much better, most of it is NOT even free range, and so is fed grain feed and some hay. Goat meat is probably the most natural meat we have here, but is extremely hard to find.

But even though our meat is toxic, I find a paleo style diet much much healthier than the SID (standard Israeli diet) that mimics the SAD, although most Israelis do eat a lot more vegies and fruits than SAD America. Unfortunately, Israel follows the modern approach to healthy eating, and people speak about eating cholesterol foods with that 'heart attack on a plate' attitude. Mainstream Israeli medicine and populace has totally bought into the cholesterol myth. That is even more annoying sometimes than not being able to find pastured animal produce.

Love and light and SHALOM

Myra in Israel

### bugsy1950@gmail.com — 2012-03-25T11:35:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-from-the-other-side-of-the-world.423/post-5742>

Hi Myra,

JOIN THE CLUB!!!

I live in South Africa and share the same frustrations, can't even find anything "low carb" but managed as best I can, my friends all think I have lost my mind....but I will show them LOL

### Chrisgraff — 2012-03-26T14:09:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-from-the-other-side-of-the-world.423/post-6288>

Welcome to the forum, Myra!

### gemstar — 2012-03-27T08:32:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-from-the-other-side-of-the-world.423/post-6879>

Thanks for welcome......Is there a recipe thread here?? I've been specializing in alternative sweets and baked goods for years. I just made up something AMAZING...that I would like to share with y'all. I'm going to look for the recipe forum.

Hi all,

Im from Hong Kong, hot and humid so fun trying to CT - I have to add ice constantly to the bath to keep it at the right temperature! I've been doing the Leptin RX since the beginning of the year and started CT 2 weeks ago. Thus far, I've not encountered many problems as I've always loved cold showers, so it made total sense to me instinctively, but for 3 days now I now have the most awful headaches (havent had those since I was a teenager). I'm 43 yrs old and a mum of 4, I'm of French origin and trying to fix Adrenal death, hashimotos, get a pink liver and lose a few kgs....
