# Thread: From Central Washington
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-central-washington.452/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sugarbiscuit — 2012-03-27T00:17:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-central-washington.452/post-6623>

Hi All,

I first dieted in college, needing to "lose" weight when I went from 105 up to 131. Ha! I though I was fat then, but it was just beginning. Achieved this weight loss and then some (lowest 96 lbs) using a combo of starvation and recreational um, medications.

I eventually got back up to a healthier 125 and maintained for a while, gained up to about 135 slowly over the next few years. Then \*BAM\* I gained 40 pounds in about 3 months. That's when I got my PCOS diagnosis. The weight continued to creep up to top out at 198.

I've tried Body for Life, Atkins, low-fat, calorie restriciton. I hired a personal trainer for 8 months & lost a whopping total of 18 pounds during that time. Not to mention how tired I always was. I went on metformin and got pregnant & lost an additional 12 pounds in the first 4 weeks. (168 when I found out I was preggers).

For the last couple of years, I've been researching keto & paleo. I read Gary Tabues Good Calories, Bad Calories which led me to the Primal Blueprint and MDA. I also really enjoy reading Hyperlipid. That's how I ended up here.

The whole HCG/pregnancy connection really has my interest piqued. It was fairly obvious to me that whatever was going on with me during pregnancy was allowing me to shed weight, and fast. The whole paleo/keto thing makes sense, and I really like that Dr. Kruse breaks down his theories on a molecular level. I really need to know "why" about things and his posts fulfill that for me.

Now, to do the leptin reset and initiate CT. I **really** want to, but something, just something is holding me back. I wish I could figure out the psychological component. It so simple really....right? "Just do it." Why can't I?

### nonchalant — 2012-04-05T23:25:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-central-washington.452/post-11912>

Hi Sugarbiscuit. Were you able to start CT at all?

You might try cool spongebaths, to ease into it.

### .lina — 2012-04-05T23:40:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-central-washington.452/post-11921>

oooh you should just go for it, dear! especially the leptin reset was a great experience for me

### Melinda — 2012-04-06T01:13:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-central-washington.452/post-11974>

Hi Sugarbiscuit, start with the Leptin Reset to get your basic circadian clock working. After 2-6 weeks, start slow with the face dunks. I think face CT feels wonderful and makes the skin look so pretty! I notice I have more desire to CT in a sunny situation like near my bathroom window in the afternoon when the sun pours in, not so much in the morning when it's in shade. You might want to consider a different "handle", Sugarbiscuit is the opposite of where you want to be going. Start now and keep a journal, I've had so many positive changes including weight loss but also better sleep and daytime energy.

### Sugarbiscuit — 2012-04-06T23:48:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-central-washington.452/post-12280>

Haha, I really deliberated whether or not to use Sugarbiscuit, but its a name I've used for a long time, so I just stuck with it. To top it off, my real name is Kandy. It's not so much a food reference as a throwback to my Southern heritage (DH calls me "Sugar" and we both call my DD "biscuit").

Anyhow, I decided to try the LR again...started on Monday. It's going okay. I started freaking out a little last night with the cravings, but managed not to binge on anything somehow. I hit a rough patch at the grocery store today, too, smelled like they were baking bread...ugh! So far, I have not hit the normal crippling "carb flu" I get when I try to start low-carb.

My biggest question was about whether CT should occur simultaneously or if I should wait a few weeks as Melinda suggested. I tried face dunks when I first read the CT posts, but I got horrible brain freeze. I am currently not doing CT. I do wonder, though, is there a point at which I will be able to tell I am ready for CT? Like when the brain freeze goes away, or when I am LS?

### nonchalant — 2012-04-07T00:04:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-central-washington.452/post-12286>

I'd try it periodically, and if the brain freeze goes away, then proceed cautiously and slowly.

It takes a while to get rid of excess Omega 6 oils in the tissues.

### Melinda — 2012-04-07T00:17:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-central-washington.452/post-12291>

Hi SB, (if I may use initials, using your name might trigger an insulin response, ha, ha). When I started low/very low carb, I made sure I had plenty of protein and fat at each meal. I don't remember snacking much but if I couldn't resist now, I'd have a couple of TBP of coconut Manna by Nutria. To this day (4.5 yrs later) I don't go into grocery stores unless I'm well fed. Most non-paleo food is not tempting anymore but after a lifetime of hunger, I'm not willing to let that demon out of the bag again. I'd just do light CT for a while like ice (not gel packs) packs on your big parts while you watch tv. And like Nonchalant says, try the face once a week to check for progress. Good luck, may the low carb flu pass you by.

### Sugarbiscuit — 2012-04-07T03:39:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-central-washington.452/post-12342>

Holy cow, I never thought of that. My whole life people have been saying "Kandy" to me all day long. No wonder I'm so fat. Perpetual cascade of insulin...

(just kidding, I know where the perpetual cascade of insulin has really been coming from: SAD with a southern exaggeration)

Thanks for the input everyone!

# Thread: Two weeks into Leptin Rx: Relapsed, huge binge! Discouraged.....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-weeks-into-leptin-rx-relapsed-huge-binge-discouraged.504/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Maria Anna — 2012-03-29T01:30:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-weeks-into-leptin-rx-relapsed-huge-binge-discouraged.504/post-7993>

Hi,

My name is Maria Anna, I'm 5,4 and 115lbs. Managed to come down from 145lbs. I kept on having cravings though, and usually wake up in te middle of the night, hungry and eat. I suffer from nocturnal sleep related eating disorder.

So I started the Leptin Reset two weeks ago. At first it felt fantastic! No hunger, no cravings whatsoever. But yesterday night, after going to bed I was so hungry and craving wheat and sugar that I binged on everything I could get my hands on.

I feel discouraged now. I don't want to give up this new way of eating but how do I stay on track?

It's also that I know that according to Kruse, a relapse puts you back two weeks in time.... So I basically have to start all over again....

Haven't I made any progress then?

### hellojtm — 2012-03-29T01:40:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-weeks-into-leptin-rx-relapsed-huge-binge-discouraged.504/post-8000>

Hi Maria,

In the words of Art DeVany, "there is no failure, only feedback". Just live in the moment and plan for the future. All you can do now is go back to what was working, i.e. the leptin reset was working for you. If you feel you need to tweak it then propose some tweaks on the forum that would make it more sustainable to you and see what others have tried.

I disagree with what Dr. Kruse wrote about two weeks. The reason is that you lost 30lbs!! Did you gain that back or even a fraction of it? Probably not. Maybe your metabolism will be slightly worse (insulin resistant) for a few days.. maybe not. We don't know.

You know what a cyclic ketogenic diet is? Lots of people lose tons of weight with that diet and it has a 1 or 2 day a week high carb day. This seems to work well for active people.

I know Dr. K's approach is deeper than this and to really get there you'd eventually need to be full keto and do cold therapy etc.. during winter. But you have 6+ months to get it dialed in... during summer I doubt you're going to pull off a 100% ketogenic diet, especially with sleep issues involved. (fix those and watch the carb cravings melt).

best of luck!

jesse

### Maria Anna — 2012-03-29T01:45:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-weeks-into-leptin-rx-relapsed-huge-binge-discouraged.504/post-8003>

Hi Jesse,

Thanks for replying so quickly. I just read what you wrote and I need some time to digest it - pardon the pun!

M.

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-29T05:02:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-weeks-into-leptin-rx-relapsed-huge-binge-discouraged.504/post-8099>

Hi, Maria Ann. That is virtually the same time I had my first (and only) binge since starting about a month ago. Just get right back on track - you can do it. Stick with a monster breakfast, and fill up on healthy protein and fat.

I think Mark Cisson says that it takes the body 21 days to switch from burning sugar/carbs to burning fat for fuel. So if you can stick with it for a bit longer, I think those cravings will start to ease away.

Also, try looking at things as an experiment. Did you do anything that day differently? Can you experiment with different snacks when this happens to see if you can avoid a binge? So there is no failure; there is only results from your experiment. If you don't get the outcome you want, then you have to adjust the variables.

### Birdy — 2012-03-29T17:20:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-weeks-into-leptin-rx-relapsed-huge-binge-discouraged.504/post-8267>

I did not find Dr. K's assessment of the amount of time to continue to be on-target in my case. In my experience, it really DOES get easier and easier with each restart. I am at the point where I can feel my body slipping back into sub-optimal. I don't test much, especially in frequency, but I seem to be able to tell when the rt3 is fluctuating. I start to get cold, hungrier, and have more cravings. I try to beat this back by really concentrate on sleep, stress and careful eating, and plenty of fat. You might try being very strict with your diet. I had to get rid of all dairy for a time, including whey, and sausage, bacon because they have additives and sugars. Green tea has really helped with mood and cravings, so you might try drinking some throughout your day.

### Maria Anna — 2012-03-31T12:39:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-weeks-into-leptin-rx-relapsed-huge-binge-discouraged.504/post-9146>

Thanks so much for the input, everyone! I'm back on track again after a two day binge. Feels good to have my BAB down! Bit worried over a dinner party in a week from now. Doesn't start after 7pm, so one rule broken already then. But we'll see how it works out.....

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2012-03-31T16:44:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-weeks-into-leptin-rx-relapsed-huge-binge-discouraged.504/post-9217>

Don't worry about that dinner party Maria Anna. I usually eat before I attempt this kind of event, and once there I have a salad. I think it's better to have veggies after 7:30 than have junk and alchool. It's not that bad to have food late at night if you have no choice, but you have to eat the good stuff. Focuss on the time being and on trying to make everyday a success, one meal at the time, and one night at the time.

I also have sleep problems, and its getting better with the reset. If I wake up in the night and can't go back to sleep, I usually eat, but most of my binges happen day time. With BAB (up tp 80-85 gram of protein!), I have no cravings at all and the hunger is more easy to manage at night. Do you always wake up at the same time? Like 2 or 3 o'clock? It's what I do, and it's a cortisol problem...

Everybody here with eating disorder lose it from time to time, but you haven't lose all progress you have done. This time will be easier, and it will probably happen again. This is just NORMAL!! Read this, it,s really interesting: [http://primitivestimulus.com/2011/04/moments-of-weakness/](http://http://primitivestimulus.com/2011/04/moments-of-weakness/)

Have a good day, and don't hesitate to come back if you need any support!

K

### Maria Anna — 2012-04-01T11:27:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-weeks-into-leptin-rx-relapsed-huge-binge-discouraged.504/post-9727>

Thanks K! It's so good to have all your support. I also assume that my waking up has something to do with cortisol. Doctor's gonna run a test. It's a blood test though - no saliva test. We'll see what comes out....


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Leptin Reset Protocol for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-protocol-for-hashimotos-thyroiditis.505/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Alimac — 2012-03-29T02:09:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-protocol-for-hashimotos-thyroiditis.505/post-8012>

Hi all,

I have Hashimoto's thyroiditis and am creator of the Hashitmoto's 411 group on facebook. I am an advocate for first and foremost addressing diet as a first step in treating autoimmune disease like Hashimoto's which in turn helps to address the underlying conditions that cause autoimmune responses. I've heard from a few of my members who've done this protocol with incredible results. I have been gluten free for about 2 years now and have done an elimination diet to determine other food sensitivities. That was lifechanging but occasionally I have setbacks mostly due to adrenal fatigue but i'm currently feeling like I need to get an ever tighter reign on my diet to address insulin resistance which is keeping some unwanted lbs on and creating symptoms again. I'd love to hear from any others with Hashimoto's or any other autoimmune disease who are on this protocol. I'm especially curious about the cold showers! With cold intolerance being a symptom I just can't imagine doing it but I'll give it a try. : )

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-29T02:17:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-protocol-for-hashimotos-thyroiditis.505/post-8014>

CT fixes cold intolerance. Seriously! Look around the forum, and hopefully some of those with Hashi's (not rare around here!) will share their experiences. (BTW, I strongly recommend cold baths over cold showers - you get used to it and it's quickly tolerable, instead of constantly being bombarded by new waves of cold water and warmer air.)

Be sure to read the first CT blog (something like "rewiring the leptin reset" or something), b/c it has the protocols for easing into the cold water.

### Slone07 — 2012-03-29T02:39:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-protocol-for-hashimotos-thyroiditis.505/post-8022>

I have Hashi's and so far in this first week on the reset, I feel like a totally, brand new person. Haven't done any CT yet, as I am so used to being an icicle that I have a pretty knee jerk reaction to the thought of purposely touching ANYthing cold...but we'll see. I've been reading about it and it sounds incredibly promising.

I'm eager to hear what others have to say...

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-29T02:59:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-protocol-for-hashimotos-thyroiditis.505/post-8030>

Hi Alice -

I am a member of Hashimotos 411 and you are wonderful. I have done most everything you have listed above too. For me the Leptin reset was a great way to bring my "whole body" back to the focal point and work toward an optimal state. This is so much more than just the thyroid. However, within 1 month of being on the leptin reset I lowered my thyroid meds as well as went off my BP medicine.

About the col intolerance I would have said the same thing in January. But who would have thought that in February I would be jumping into a cold bath almost every day. My body warms up differently than it used to and blankets have come off the bed. I am up to about 45 min in 55 degree water but just recently added ice blankets to my chest and back. Brrrrr. But it took me almost six weeks to get to 45 min so go slow. Now it's much colder and I shiver a lot but I just listen to a podcast or read a book and time goes by fast. I'm rooting for ya!!

### Alimac — 2012-03-29T04:05:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-protocol-for-hashimotos-thyroiditis.505/post-8063>

Thanks Patty! We have alot of great members who share alot of really helpful, even life altering information! I was able to go off my meds completely after going gluten free and the elimination diet. My thyroid levels normalized naturally and I felt really good. Problem is Adrenals are a huge problem for me and stress is at an all time high right now which of course throws off blood sugar and everything else. I'm still off meds though my latest labs suggest I could use some tweaking but I just don't tolerate thyroid meds. Synthetics make my hair fall out and natural dessicated either just doesn't do anything for me or makes me a raving $itch! My diet is pretty clean but I know I need to really reign it in and I think this is where I need to be right now. I'm all about sharing any info that can be helpful to others like us so I hope at some point you may not mind sharing your experience with the leptin reset on the hashi's page. : ) I'm looking forward to jumping in here and I have alot of reading to do here. I love research!

### Alimac — 2012-03-29T04:08:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-protocol-for-hashimotos-thyroiditis.505/post-8066>

Thanks Mama. I'll start with the cold showers before work tomorrow and friday which will hopefully wake me up! It's been tough dragging myself out of bed lately!

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-29T04:51:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-protocol-for-hashimotos-thyroiditis.505/post-8092>

> **Alimac;8106 wrote:** Thanks Patty! We have alot of great members who share alot of really helpful, even life altering information! I was able to go off my meds completely after going gluten free and the elimination diet. My thyroid levels normalized naturally and I felt really good. Problem is Adrenals are a huge problem for me and stress is at an all time high right now which of course throws off blood sugar and everything else. I'm still off meds though my latest labs suggest I could use some tweaking but I just don't tolerate thyroid meds. Synthetics make my hair fall out and natural dessicated either just doesn't do anything for me or makes me a raving $itch! My diet is pretty clean but I know I need to really reign it in and I think this is where I need to be right now. I'm all about sharing any info that can be helpful to others like us so I hope at some point you may not mind sharing your experience with the leptin reset on the hashi's page. : ) I'm looking forward to jumping in here and I have alot of reading to do here. I love research!

Yes you do have a lot of reading to do. I'm still trying to get into some of the docs older blogs that have valuable tidbits I need to learn. Enjoy reading the CT series blogs. He talks in there how doing the colt thermogenesis will create a situation where the hypothalamus takes charge and this process helps the thyroid and adrenals. He has said it time and again the CT is really good for thyroid and adrenal issues. I think this would be great for you. Follow what Mama Grok said about reading the blog to get the proper protocol and it will not be so hard. It will be shocking but just remember when you first had to give up certain foods but you felt better.

I am currently working on my estrogen dominance as that came to the forefront after four weeks of CT. I gained about 4# back which put the focus right back on those hormones. They are crying to get balanced out. I will gladly post some things on Hashimotos 411 after I get myself straightened out. I feel if I told them now that I do cold baths in 55 degree water and have gained back 4# there would be any takers to try this!! LOL

There are a lot of great people who can help with any questions. I consider myself a newbie. I've been watching his blog since Late November but didn't start the reset until Jan 10 and just went to post reset this week. Good luck and enjoy your research!

### hazyjane — 2012-04-02T18:30:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-protocol-for-hashimotos-thyroiditis.505/post-10346>

Hey Alice! It's neat to see you here! I'm in your 411 group and we're actually FB friends (I'm Erin F.)

I haven't posted in the 411 group in awhile (it gets old reading about so many people who just won't change their diet or med-centric views...)

I've been having some neat results with Leptin Rx and CT. I still have some adrenal fatigue- mostly just low morning cortisol, but I do feel a lot more energetic and motivated all around. My hair is getting thicker since CT, too (YAY!!!!!) My hair is one of my thyroid gauges.

Have you tried any of the Apex thyroid support supplements? Maybe you'd tolerate them better than meds and maybe they'd do more for you than glandulars... I haven't used them, but I've had good results with other Apex supps and I'm going to start on their new-ish Glutathione Recycler in place of the Oxicell cream, since CT has lowered my inflammation so much that I don't think I need Oxicell anymore.

PS- have you checked out Gena Lee Nolin's (of Bay Watch!) "Thyroid Sexy" FB page? I have to say that I'm impressed with her and her open-mindedness to using diet and inflammation control/immune modulation for Hashi's.

### Tucker — 2012-04-03T16:10:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-protocol-for-hashimotos-thyroiditis.505/post-10751>

Hi Alice,

This is my first post on the forum. I feel the need to be a Starfish as Dr. Kruse encourages us to be.

I have Hashimotos as well. Been dealing with it since 2008. There were times when I felt like I couldn't muster up enough energy to take my dog for a walk. It was bad. Have done salivary testing and it showed I was in Adrenal fatigue.

Now since 2011 I started working with Dr. Datis Kharazian and have followed his protocol since Aug. 2011. No gluten, no dairy, no coffee and using the Apex supplements. ( Glutathione Recycler, X-FLM & X-Viromin, Nitric Balance) I take other supplements but those are the big hitters. Dr. Datis had me do the test to find out if I am TH1 or TH2 dominant regarding the autoimmunity. I showed no real bias to either one so I take both the X-FLM & X-Viromin. So this protocol helped me tremendously. It cleared the brain fog, I had more energy, my hair stopped falling out. But man the scales would not budge. I would hover around 193-198lbs. It was so frustrating that the weight wouldn't budge especially since I was being so good with my diet and the supplements. I did an elimination diet and everything.

OK, now I have been to the 411 forum and would see people talk about Dr. Kruse. So I checked him out and started reading his mammoth blog posts. I was floored. I really think this guy will get the Nobel prize one of these days. Everything I was reading just made sense to me. I also listened to all of his pod casts he had up on his site. I was PUMPED to try this protocol. It made sense that obesity is caused by brain inflammation. My brain is inflamed because of the autoimmunity and that is why the scales aren't budging. So I jumped right in, literally I went straight to the tub.

March12, 2012 I started the Leptin Reset protocol and Cold Therapy. The first day I did an 18 minute CT in the tub with 54 degree water. COLD!!!!!!!!! Now I do two CT sessions a day for 25-30 minutes. Within 5 days I broke through a plateau and hit 188lbs on the scale. I was amazed. Then the next 2 weeks I was feeling like a mess due to hormonal dumps from the CT. I got bummed out for a while and was wondering why I wasn't seeing any progress. But just a few days ago I think a switch turned on and the weight started dropping. This morning the scales said 185lbs. Now the day I started the scales were 190lbs so it's only 5lbs over 3 weeks. But you have got to remember that I had not seen anything below 190lbs for a couple of years.

I believe it is the CT that is giving me the biggest bang. CTing is challenging at times but I feel so much better afterward. The Kharrazian protocol had helped tremendously with reducing the brain fog but the CT has totally wiped out the brain fog. I also feel like the CT has improved my gut. I don't have the bloating that I had before. I feel it is helping my adrenals as well, since I have more energy since the CT implementation. This is what I think is going on, the CT is reducing inflammation through out the whole body but especially the brain. So now the hypothalamus can work correctly and function the way it was designed too. Also, it must be having an impact on the Vagal nerve, now my brain and gut can have a more clear conversation with each other. That is why the bloating has reduced and my digestion feels better. I could be completely wrong on all of this but all I know is I feel better than I did before the Hashimotos. In a way the Hashimotos has been a blessing because by it making me feel absolutely like shit for so long it forced me to go out and find answers. Pre-Hashimotos I thought I was healthy and eating correctly but I wasn't. Now I"m on my way to being Optimal and better than before. This is all very exciting and right now I feel I"m a success story in progress because I'm not done yet. I really think the CT can stop autoimmunity in its tracks. All I ever here is once you turn on an autoimmune gene you can't turn it off. I say bullshit, that is limited thinking. Thinking that maybe only applies to a warm adapted mammal. I'm turning into a cold adapted mammal and I will turn off the autoimmune gene. My thoughts are changing which in turn my DNA is changing. EPIGENETICS!!!!

So yes, I would highly recommend to anyone with Hashi's to give the Leptin Reset and Cold Therapy a try. They need to be patient and determined. Believe me I have always been cold in my life and embracing cold therapy did not give me a warm fuzzy feeling inside. But I love the thought of doing a bio hack on myself. The cool thing is it's working.

That is my very long story about all of this. I will have more breakthroughs to post in the future.

Hope this can give someone some encouragement to give this a try. When I was at my worst it was really scary to think is this how life is always going to be? If someone feels that way today let me tell you NO! If you put the effort into yourself you will get better and most likely feel the best you have ever felt.

JUST DO IT!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

### Tucker — 2012-04-03T16:18:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-protocol-for-hashimotos-thyroiditis.505/post-10754>

Also, one other major bonus from the CT.

Prior to staring CT the previous 3 menstrual cycles I would have a hormonal migraine the first day of my cycle. My last menstrual cycle that started 4 days after my first CT session I didn't have a headache and had absolutely no menstrual symptoms at all.

Now that is a MAJOR Bonus.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-04-13T19:52:31+0300

Great to hear the success. I have Hashimotos and have been freezing my butt off! LOL Unfortunately, I have no weight to lose in my butt (it's all in my belly)! I started doing CT and was doing spot CT in the morning and cold baths at night. Then I went on vacation and couldn't do cold tubs. I continued with spot CT and face dunks. But since returning, I've totally regressed. The tubs were so hard and I haven't been in for three days. My figures and toes go white on a regular basis  I've been thinking of trying the Apex Glutathione Recycler and Nitric Balance. Glad to hear those have helped you, Tucker. I want to feel warm! It's hard to convince my brain that getting cold is the way to get warm! LOL But I'm gonna keep trying

# Thread: Lurker....coming out..lol
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lurker-coming-out-lol.654/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cantweight — 2012-04-04T19:08:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lurker-coming-out-lol.654/post-11347>

Three very short years ago I thought it may just be the end for me.

Over the years I had been diagnosed with Fibromyalgia, CFS, LUPUS, Hasimotos, BiPolar Disorder, PCOS, and laundry list of other ailments. Surgeries and ER trips were commonplace. At the ripe old age of 35 I felt I was dying.

Pain was so severe I crawled up the stairs at night to tuck my kids into bed, I was losing my ability to speak properly. Couldn't spell my own name at times and was unable to comprehend simple instructions. I ran unexplained fevers of 104 degrees for 2 years straight. Multiple bouts of shingles plagued me. Migraines and arthritis made everyday unbearable. Exhaustion that left me in tears was all I knew. Who wouldn't be depressed with all that?? I often wondered how long I would be with my family physically and mentally. I had never been so scared.

I was on so much medication....wellbutrin, vicodin, diclophenac, plaquenil, synthroid, .....a list so long I can not even remember!!

In the winter of 2009 after 10 years of this insane decline I had had enough. I went off all meds and hauled ass to a holistic doc as my last resort. All the finest docs in the DC metro area had failed to help me. Yet a few visits with this doc had me on adrenal support, natural thyroid, and finally a diagnosis...Lyme disease.

3 years later I am a new person. Curing myself was more of an exorcism than a healing process, but one I'd do again in an instant to have regained such a wonderful quality of life.

Throughout his healing process I have run the gamut from GF to Vegan to HCG to Juicing to Paleo. My whole family has not had fast food in 3 years. No dyes, nitrates, HFCS, .....all that crap....gone.

However I am still walking around at 240lbs. How this was possible was beyond me!

Enter Dr Jack. Wk 5 of Leptin Rx....I feel great have lost 8lbs, stomach getting flatter by the day. Second week of CT up to 30 min baths at 58 degrees. (my tap doesn't get any colder)

Besides the stomach flattening the biggest change I have noticed is my ability to go up and down stairs effortlessly. Sounds silly, but often I would go step by step in the early morning or late evening. My body was tired and maybe a little sore, now I get a little giddy when I am practically skipping up and down the steps any time of day.

I am thrilled about this and the possibilities of what lies ahead. The knowledge and camaraderie shared on this forum is invaluable.

Cheers to you all!

To a life filled with bacon and sex

### .lina — 2012-04-04T19:12:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lurker-coming-out-lol.654/post-11350>

reading that gave me goosebumps! awe-inspiring stuff, mate

### Jim — 2012-04-04T19:30:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lurker-coming-out-lol.654/post-11359>

WOW. So happy life is on the upswing for you. Loudoun County where I live just made Lyme disease (and the ticks that carry it) public enemy #1. We have the highest infection rate in Virginia. There will be a big education campaign for health professionals too. Good luck on the weight loss.

### cantweight — 2012-04-04T19:45:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lurker-coming-out-lol.654/post-11367>

.lina....your intro post moved me to post my own intro. Thank you.

Jim....Loudoun County resident myself. Hello neighbor  Its all about the docs...their mis-information cost me and my family years. Almost cost me my life. So glad people are finally waking up....I hope this coalition does what it needs to and not what mainstream med usually does. Fingers crossed.

### colleencoble — 2012-04-04T21:31:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lurker-coming-out-lol.654/post-11419>

That is absolutely terrific! Love hearing your story!

### PaulaRichards — 2012-04-05T00:39:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lurker-coming-out-lol.654/post-11509>

Thanks for sharing & good for you!!!

### .lina — 2012-04-05T12:18:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lurker-coming-out-lol.654/post-11660>

> **cantweight;11436 wrote:** .lina....your intro post moved me to post my own intro. Thank you.

oh! haha, goosebumps again! i am such a sap

### Martin — 2012-04-06T15:08:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lurker-coming-out-lol.654/post-12087>

I hear you, cantweight.

Conventional Wisdom medicine may have failed you, but you are at the right place now.

There is a way through. I'm glad you are on your way.

Martin

# Thread: Hi, Jazz here with a client's query
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-jazz-here-with-a-clients-query.688/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jasbinder — 2012-04-06T07:35:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-jazz-here-with-a-clients-query.688/post-12067>

Hi Dr. Jack,

Great to be a part of the community here and to learn more about your work. This is Jazz, from Melbourne, Australia. I work as a Personal Trainer and have been following Sean Croxton's work from a while now. That's how, I came to learn about you.

I need a help re a Client's condition. She recently wrote this to me:

"Thank you for helping me with my food allergies.

I'm sure you already know all this, just in case you don't I'll quickly explain it.

So a stage 4 is a strong positive and a stage one is just a positive. My doctor said I can eat stage 1's and 2's. 2's in moderation though.

Barley 3+

Durum Wheat 3+

Rice 3+

Rye 3+

Wheat 4+

Cow's Milk 4+

Egg White and Yolk 4+

Sheep Milk 3+

Goat's Milk 4+

Harocit Bean 1+

Kidney Bean 3+

Peas 1+

Potato 1+

Tomato 2+

Orange 1+

Plum 1+

Almond 2+

Cashew Nut 2+

Hazel Nut 1+

Peanut 3+

Chilli Pepper 2+

Mint 1+

Mushroom 1+

Brewers/Bakers Yeast 2+

My DHEA levels are at 7.8 and they are suppose to be between 0.95 and 11.7umol/L. I need to get my prescription renewed, my natropath doctor said I need to take the supplement every day of the rest of my life.

I want to look into this further, he told me a lot of things like if I continue to eat the food I am allergic to I will get cancer and alzheimer's disease. "

Dr. Jack, it will be awesome if you can guide me so as to help out this young girl (22 yrs old).

Many Thanks.

Regards,

Jazz (Jasbinder Gulati)

### Kiera2 — 2012-04-06T10:16:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-jazz-here-with-a-clients-query.688/post-12073>

Hi Jazz, Jack recommends the paleo diet, either Mark Sisson, or Robb Wolf would be a good guide. There are only three things on your list that I eat - cows milk, eggs and mint. I don't think it would be at all difficult to follow Mark Sissons dietary guidelines whilst leaving out those three.

I think your client would do well to follow Robb Wolf's autoimmune diet, which restricts eggs, tomato, eggplant, capsicum, paprika and chilli powder.

There are no meats on your list; is your client a vegetarian?

# Thread: Pacific swimmer -- almost!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pacific-swimmer-almost.1092/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rosekelly — 2012-04-24T02:27:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pacific-swimmer-almost.1092/post-18215>

Hello All, and Hello Dr. Kruse,

I've been lurking since this forum opened up, following along with you all and having a great time with the protocols. I'm a 57 year old woman, weigh 200 pounds, generally feel pretty good and now feel much better. I've taken the plunge to join your conversation for a couple of reasons.

1\. I'm LOVING CT and LeptinRx, and I want to add my voice to the appreciation for Dr. Kruse. I'm addicted to my cold baths and look forward to making them colder and longer as I continue to adapt. This weekend I drove 45 minutes to the Pacific ocean at Santa Cruz and jumped in, and I wasn't cold! But the waves were ferocious, so next time I'll pick a calmer beach and spend some good quality time in the cold, cold sea.

2\. Last week I was told by a doc that I have endometrial cancer. Apparently, this is not that serious, although I have yet to see the oncologist. I was also told the usual treatment is removal of the uterus and ovaries. I wouldn't have minded before, but now that I understand the important role of hormones in health, I don't like the idea of having no ovaries. If they must go, I will need help figuring out how to supplement for optimal health, which I assume will still be possible for me.

Thanks, and hello!

I'm planning to try the boardwalk beach. Have you swum there? People do swim there regularly, and I remember swimming there as a child. I was at Bonnydoon this weekend, beautiful but too rough. I felt like I would be taking my life in my hands to get in deeper than waist-high.

# Thread: Joy's Journey
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/joys-journey.1100/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### vjoywilliams@sbcglobal.net — 2012-04-24T17:41:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/joys-journey.1100/post-18366>

Thought I would finally take the plunge and introduce myself in the proper place. I have been on the Rx Leptin reset since March 4. That's when I started phase 3 of the hCG protocol. The last three weeks I have added face dunks and (last 9 days) ice packs. I've done a couple of cold baths, but no ice. I live in Arkansas, so I imagine the water was only about 60-65 degrees f.

I am just a regular boring middle aged school teacher with some weird health issues ( and always on a diet and steadily getting fatter and fatter). I am 59 years old. No major health problems until I turned 50, then it's been downhill. 7 years ago, I had terrible back pains and went to the ER room. I ended up having a CT scan in order to confirm a diagnosis of kidney stones. At that time I was told that my abdomen was full of small deposits of calcium. But I also had a growth in my pancreas. I had 67% of pancreas and spleen removed because of a very fast growing adenoma. It tripled in size in the 3 weeks between the time it was discovered and removed. 3 months ago I had a thyroidectomy, 2 spots of papillary carcinoma with fibulary variants. no need for chemo or radiation - Oddly enough the same surgeon performed both operations! I take thyroid replacement hormone and a variety of supplements and other prescriptions, including Metformin. I have lost 80 pounds over the last year. I took hCG and followed the original protocol. The thyroid stuff happened between round 2 and round 3. I still am having kidney problems, have kidney infections a couple times a year. And a large hernia around my belly button. I am hoping the CT will help heal both problems.

Joy

### caroline — 2012-04-25T04:09:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/joys-journey.1100/post-18585>

welcome - you have come to a great place - there are so many knowledgeable people here who are so willing to help

### vjoywilliams@sbcglobal.net — 2012-04-25T17:49:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/joys-journey.1100/post-18720>

> **caroline;18658 wrote:** welcome - you have come to a great place - there are so many knowledgeable people here who are so willing to help

I couldn't agree more. I am very impressed with this forum. I've learned more in the last 3 months than any other time. I never knew how poorly I have been feeding and caring for both myself and my family. Thank you so much for opening up my eyes. For an update, I have been doing ice packs on my lower abdomen and hernia area. I have noticed that the hernia (which is roughly 3 inches in diameter and sticks out about one and half inches) seems to be shrinking! For anyone who wonders why I have so many small calcium deposits in my abdomen, I believe that my calcium supplementation caused it. For about 15 years I took calcium tablets - the coral kind. I did not know that calcium in this form is not absorbed. It is useless without a citrate. All the kidney stones I have passed and my frequent kidney infections are attributed (by me) to my body trying to expel all the little calcium deposits. I had another flare up last week. I am hoping that the ice will help with this situation.

### Mei — 2012-04-25T19:04:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/joys-journey.1100/post-18763>

Wow, Joy, keep up the amazing work and you won't be a "boring middle-aged school teacher with some weird health issues", you'll be a happy, healthy, gorgeous teacher with information very few teachers out there have. My mother teaches counseling at a college and she's started slipping real nutrition into her courses, starting with Mark Sisson's modified food pyramid. So far, two of her students and one of their roommates have changed their lives, lost weight and started taking control of their health after that one little clue. That's three lives out of a few hundred, but each of them may affect someone else. Not trying to preach, here (okay maybe a little) but always keep in mind the power you have as an educator. Congratulations on all the success, and everything to come!

# Thread: New to LR, well, formally at least. Some Qs.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-lr-well-formally-at-least-some-qs.1377/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### paisley777 — 2012-05-10T06:13:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-lr-well-formally-at-least-some-qs.1377/post-22766>

Hello all.

(It's a long post....)

I found my way to LR by way of HCG and Grammy1952.

LR protocol came as a bit of a surprise as it seems I have naturally fallen into many of the LR habits on my own.

History:

Always been overweight but never been higher than 160# (5'2). When I hit that number I decided to change things and found HCG in Fall 2010.

After R1 â€“ lost 20. Stabilized and never gained it back.

R2 â€“ lost 23 â€“ didn't stabilize for a few reasons. Went too fast adding variety, traveling and traveling food.

Now... Just finished R3 (VERY STRICT) and lost 10# (ran out of hcg). Was stabilizing fine for 11 days and then jumped 2#. (TOM may have been a factor). I got worried so I started LR officially 3 days ago. Now down 1# & eating 2 meals.

I had just read about LR maybe 5 days before all that, but decided to read more. Came as a surprise...

Before 2010 I was a night owl. Exhausted in the mornings. Very carb heavy food, processed everything. Cravings.

But, my R1 P3 in 2010 looked amazingly like LR and I have kept these habits. I loved eating a BAB of bacon, eggs, a little cheese (1-2oz), and heavy cream in coffee. Could often skip lunch. I still enjoy sweet things, but don't 'crave' it. Don't snack a lot. I have never liked working out in the morning and usually worked out after getting home ~4-6pm. (not exercising now though) Eat dinner by 7pm.

Now I wake up naturally 5:30 â€“ 6:30. Almost never need an alarm. Feel awake in the morning too. Really sleepy by ~9 - 9:30.

So....

I have another 10-15# to go. No HCG. I now live and work in South Korea, so access to tests and Drs (communication issues, cultural issues) are not available. Also, even if I could find a test source to ship here, my budget is very small.

???

From what I've read it seems I may be less LR than I was after I started naturally making some of these changes, but I don't think I'm all the way there yet. Does this make sense???

Since I don't have nearly all the LR symptoms and I may have some LS... can I start HIIT exercise? I really want to but I have been afraid to do anything that'll further mess up stabilization & now LR. If I do start to exercise, how will I know if it's too soon and I should stop?

Nice to meet you all.

Thanks!

Paisley

### colleencoble — 2012-05-10T22:14:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-lr-well-formally-at-least-some-qs.1377/post-22984>

Glad you made it, Paisley! I use my real name here but I'm Grammy1952 over on the hcg forum.  I have said all along that I believe hcg got me partially leptin sensitive. I always ate just the 2 meals allowed on hcg which I think helped. Plus the action of hcg on the hypothalamus. just read everything here and monitor your symptoms since you can't get any help over there. Dr. K has some posts about symptoms and when to start HIIT and what time of day to do it as well. Good luck!

### paisley777 — 2012-05-11T03:19:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-lr-well-formally-at-least-some-qs.1377/post-23064>

Thank you for the reply Collen.

I have appreciated your time and posts on the HCG forum.

One confusing point... the sweat thing. First - if you are not exercising, then how can you know if you sweat different when you exercise? lol

Also, when doing searches, I have seen MANY posts - some say the change is you will sweat more BUT others by those who have completed the reset say they used to sweat but hardly do at all anymore.

So, I still don't know what to make of that LS marker. :/

### colleencoble — 2012-05-11T15:12:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-lr-well-formally-at-least-some-qs.1377/post-23142>

I've only heard people who are LS remark that they sweat more. I never used to sweat at all but I do a little now. And you know me--I'm allergic to exercise. LOL So it's just a difference I've noticed that's very minor since I don't do much exercise either.

# Thread: Hello from NH
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-nh.1459/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bigknitwit — 2012-05-14T22:56:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-nh.1459/post-23997>

Hi all,

I've been reading, re-reading, and re-re-reading Jack's blog posts, as well as the forums, since I stumbled into the information gem about 7 weeks ago. I had doing yet another google search about adrenal fatigue, or Hashimoto's, or biotoxin illness, or food sensitivities causing weight gain. I was tired of reading the same old information, and had been looking for a fresh approach for quite a while. Boy did I hit a goldmine!!

I've been officially doing the leptin reset for 6 weeks, but had allready incorporated everything except the BAB for a month prior to finding the leptin RX on my own. I started CTing about 4 weeks ago, and do daily 54ish degree baths for 30ish minutes almost fully immersed (I am stuggling to get much past the 30 minute mark). I also do cold showers almost daily. My afterdrop recovery times are improving, but I do still feel a strong afterdrop response getting out of the tub, so I guess I'm not cold adapted yet.

6 weeks into the leptin RX, I am still struggling with never feeling full/satisfied. I feel like I could keep eating and eating. I finally decided to come out the shadows of lurkdom and try to take a more active role on the forum.

### ealachan — 2012-05-14T23:07:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-nh.1459/post-24001>

I wave to you from Vermont!

I was a bit confused by your post - you DO now incorporate the BAB, or you still do not?

### nonchalant — 2012-05-14T23:35:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-nh.1459/post-24011>

Hi Bigknitwit, welcome to the forum!

You may want to think about increasing the amount of protein you are eating.

### SeaHorse — 2012-05-14T23:50:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-nh.1459/post-24018>

Another wave from Vermont!

What foods are you sensitive to? I'm still trying to figure this out and it can make a big difference in bloating weight gain and appetite as I'm sure you know...I'm working with a really helpful ND here in Vermont if you are looking for somebody..

### bigknitwit — 2012-05-15T01:36:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-nh.1459/post-24055>

> **ealachan;24083 wrote:** I wave to you from Vermont!
>
>
>
> I was a bit confused by your post - you DO now incorporate the BAB, or you still do not?

Yes, for the past 6 weeks I do the BAB. For a month prior to that, I was trying to reign in my appetite and had restricted myself to 3 low carb meals/day only.

### bigknitwit — 2012-05-15T01:53:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-nh.1459/post-24058>

> **SeaHorse;24100 wrote:** Another wave from Vermont!
>
>
>
> What foods are you sensitive to? I'm still trying to figure this out and it can make a big difference in bloating weight gain and appetite as I'm sure you know...I'm working with a really helpful ND here in Vermont if you are looking for somebody..

Ughh... I used to be one of the those people who could eat anything she wanted and remain weight stable and slim. That disappeared after my 4th baby was born. I struggled to drop anything after she was born (which had been effortless with the older 3). Then I had a rapid weight loss with illness (13 pounds in 10 days) followed by an elimination diet down to 6 foods for 6 weeks, trying to figure about baby's allergies/food issues (she was nursing and having all kinds of skin reactions, especially around her mouth). After that, things were never the same for me. I'm not sure if it's an allergic reaction, an insulin issue, or both, but when I eat a food I cannot tolerate, I gain weight - like 2-3 pounds overnight. And the weight stays on and accumulates quickly. I gained 15 pounds in 3 weeks while trying to figure out the food issues. Now, I am left with meats (not cured, sadly), vegetables (no mushrooms or starchy veggies), cantaloupe, and a few other fruits in very small portions. Everything else causes the dramatic overnight water retention. It's very frustrating. I am seeing an environmental physician who has tested me and confirmed I have had biotoxin (mold) exposure. I just did more testing to quantify how much and then he will create a detox program for me. I am hopeful that this may be at the root of my issues. How's that for a long answer??!

### SeaHorse — 2012-05-15T14:55:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-nh.1459/post-24165>

I can relate to this too....swelling hands and feet in the am are a sign something was off the day before. Have you looked at oxalates, histamine and salicylates? For me, it explained why certain foods were giving me a reaction...still is a pain to maintain such a limited range of meat and veg. but I'm hoping with the resets and CT that things will start to turn around. I can't take some of the liver support herbs because they are high oxalate...

Hope things start to improve for you soon!

### bigknitwit — 2012-05-15T23:31:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-nh.1459/post-24374>

Interesting... 18 months ago while on the elimination diet for my daughter, I was really suspect of salicylate foods causing her reactions. I could never really pin it down for me. It seems like foods that aggravate yeast overgrowth (like mushrooms and grapes) are a problem. I don't think the sals are an issue (cantaloupe is high and it doesn't cause the extreme water retention). I don't know about histamines.

I have a little theory that each time I've quickly dropped weight (once due to illness, once on a 5 day fast), I've actually just recirculated toxins and made things worse (more food sensitivities). I am doing CT, but in the back of my mind I'm wondering about my body's ability to excrete all that may be released.

# Thread: My Story :)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-story.1480/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Steven — 2012-05-15T22:37:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-story.1480/post-24346>

Hello,

I'm a 28 year old male.

Since the age of 15, I have used a computer everyday for at least 3+ hours per day and stayed up late until 2am. I also ate a diet high in carbohydrates everyday. Even in college I maintained this kind of life style. However, I'm not over weight though. My mom is always yelling at me to sleep earlier....

I didn't pay much attention until a year ago when I developed adrenal fatigue that is causing symptoms such as palpitations, dizziness, ringing in the ears, and cold hands + feet.

I would like to try Dr. Kruse's protocol to see if I can improve my health and get rid of these symptoms so I can live a normal life again!

Thanks for reading.

### nonchalant — 2012-05-15T22:53:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-story.1480/post-24359>

Hi Steven, welcome to the forum! Yes, try F.lux on your computer and jump into the Leptin reset. Glad you are trying this route instead of getting some sort of prescription.

### Steven — 2012-05-18T21:04:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-story.1480/post-25279>

Hi Nonchalant, will look into f.lux.

Thanks for the suggestion!

### TerrierMom — 2012-05-20T18:56:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-story.1480/post-25733>

Good luck Steven. I also get the palpitations etc and observing a good sleep schedule, eating a keto paleo diet (per the leptin rx), and especially tightening up on use of artificial light after sunset, is a tremendous help. Also CT. The more you read here the more you realize our American diet and high tech lifestyle has created biological mismatches that are seriously affecting our health, individually and collectively. And our medical wisdom will prescribe us drugs to treat the symptoms which have side effects of their own....

Here you will learn how you got the way you are and how to fix yourself. If you can get some labs done that will really help to understand what is going on. Read up anything JK has written on adrenal fatigue and leaky gut (surprise! They are related!) .... The good news is.... This can be fixed! It will require you to do some things differently though

Speaking for myself, my health has improved so dramatically I can't imagine living the way I did before!! Keep us posted and don't be afraid to ask questions... People here are wonderfully willing to help.

My recommendation... Start at Jacks first blog post and read it and all the comments (serious wisdom there as he answers questions). And keep reading all the blog posts.... May take a who,e but seriously worth your time and you will have many 'aha' moments!!

### David — 2012-05-21T00:58:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-story.1480/post-25844>

> **Krusing\_tptimal\_in\_CA;25817 wrote:** Good luck Steven. ... recommendation... Start at Jacks first blog post and read it and all the comments (serious wisdom there as he answers questions). And keep reading all the blog posts... you will have many 'aha' moments!!

This is great advice and I would add that you should keep a pad close by to take notes as you read as well to note where it appears (sort of your personal index) so you can go back to that blog post at a later time.

# Thread: Low Carb Cruise recovery
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-cruise-recovery.1491/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### David — 2012-05-16T03:58:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-cruise-recovery.1491/post-24480>

First of all, I commend Jimmy Moore for putting this cruise together and everyone involved noted that his staff did a great job throughout the cruise. I do not regret a moment of the time I spent on this cruise, even though my sole reason for attending was to hear **Jack Kruse** speak. I was impressed that the lectures were as high a caliber as I have ever experienced in any venue and the information was certainly well received. Interaction with all lecturers was encouraged and easily done, which was a very great benefit.

However, after a week or so on the Low Carb Cruise, without a bit of cold water for CT (outside of an iced-drink) and somehow gorging on three full meals each day, I managed to gain two pounds or so. I did walk a couple of laps on the half-mile exterior promenade on deck 5 each morning and evening and I was able to spend several hours in the pool one time (all the kids were ashore). The crew wouldnâ€™t allow me to enter the pool before seven AM and the pool water (and the shower, for that matter) was nearly body temperature anyway. I was thankful to have encountered other members of this site, which helped immeasurably in feeling at home.

My pre-cruise stay in Houston was at a classy hotel that had NO ice in the ice machine on my floor, so CT was not possible. However, the first night offered the pre-cruise get-acquainted dinner where I was able to meet Dr. Kruse, so I consider myself to be very fortunate.

By the way, â€œ_Low Carb Cruise_â€￾ refers strictly to the lecture content NOT the menu. I found it fairly challenging to get something close to a Paleo meal, but many others seemed quite satisfied. My GF commented that she was very glad to get home to have â€œreal foodâ€￾ which I took to be a compliment on my cooking.

Two days at home and I have now had several hours of CT, and I seem to be centered once again.

For anyone interested, there were some excellent lectures. I have been informed that all lectures have been videoed and will be available on line. The details are not yet available.

### indigogirl — 2012-05-16T06:15:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-cruise-recovery.1491/post-24517>

Thanks for sharing and welcome back!

### Jillm — 2012-05-20T03:35:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-cruise-recovery.1491/post-25618>

I am trying to talk Jimmy into putting together a cruise out of Brisbane.

### David — 2012-05-21T00:51:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-cruise-recovery.1491/post-25843>

After a six month hiatus on doctors, lab tests and research, but not paleo, I'm back!

Eddie

# Thread: Hello from Maryland
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-maryland.2748/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### primalminx — 2012-07-29T16:54:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-maryland.2748/post-41786>

My name is Kelly and I am starting primal for phase 3 of the HCG diet. This is my first round and plan to do the LR for phase 3. Back ground information, from a chubby kid to a hyper adult. When I was 15 I was shot in the head, I ended up weak on my left side and started lifting weights to build my strength back up. I loved it, I kept at it for many years, had a very physical job, I built modular office systems. I could eat almost anything I wanted and never gained weight, my breakfast was 711 nachos many days. Then I got pregnant, for 61/2 months I barely gained any weight( I was a size 5, 138lbs pre pregnancy) then shot to 198lbs. I worked until I was 8 months pregnant, still unloading trailers, building the furniture. I took over a year to lose the weight. Two and a half years later, my pregnancy with my youngest son changed my body for good. In 1991 at his birth I weighed 215lbs and went up from there to 230lbs. My primary doctor was a Immediate care facility. I worked so many hours that is why I used them and my insurance allowed it. I started getting tired and weak, for two years I was told it was all in my head, I was depressed being a single mother with two kids. My throat was thicker, a bulge in the front, my eyes buggy and my hands trembled like crazy. I couldn't eat when out, I would have to have a bm before I was even done. I would be easily startled, small panic like attacks. Couldn't lift a gallon of milk, I thank my family for all their help. Again, it was all in my head. I went to the ER one day I thought I was having a heart attack, they couldn't register my pulse. No heart attack and an appointment to an endocrinologist. I had radioactive iodine for Graves Disease, it killed 80% of my thyroid. Within a month I started losing weight and getting my strength back, my youngest was just over 3 years old. I never got on thyroid medication until 2004, my doctor and ex husband begged me to, I use to take over 12 caffeine pills daily but I was a size 7. I gave in and started Synthroid but not just that, I also was going to quit smoking, so I started

Wellbutrin and the depo shot all the same week. Pretty darn dumb of me. I gained 20 lbs the first month and 10-20 every time they upped my Synthroid and leveled out. I worked out at the Ymca but ate a ton of carbs, the same amount as prekids but could not use them like I use to. The only time I lost weight was when I went off my Synthroid for almost a year, lost half my hair(tsh was 52.80). I begged my doctor( whom I worked for) to put me on Armour, I knew Synthroid acted weird for me and it turns out many more. Armour was better but I still had a high RT3 to T3 ratio. I started to gain back the 25 lbs I lost while off the medication and went back to 215lbs. A few months ago, he put me on Cytomel 25mcg twice a day. I feel much better but not losing weight, my body temp is still below 97.6 normally but my tsh is now 0.289 and t3 is 145. It seems when I am hyper, I gain weight and slightly hypo I lose weight. I have given up ever getting it right and just realize I can not eat like I use to. I am trying to get more exercise in the form of walking my dog 30 minutes plus in the morning and 20 later in the day. I can not really lift much I have spinal stenosis in the lumbar. Walking like a caveman is about it, swimming hurts( like sleeping on my tummy) but I can tread water and running place doing it. I have lost 22 lbs on hcg( yes, i cheated, it should have been more), 8 1/2 inches. With the LR and eating clean and another round I hope to be at my goal of 138lbs in a year. I am sorry for the wall of text but I guess I wanted people to know yes, sometimes it is medical and not all over eating. I have to admit in my younger/dumber days I use to think fat people were lazy and gluttons. I am also trying to get my sons to go primal and get my 2 year old grandson into walking a ton.

### shilohman — 2012-07-29T17:05:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-maryland.2748/post-41792>

Welcome Kelly, hope this forum is as helpful to you as it has been to me.

### kathylu — 2012-07-29T20:07:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-maryland.2748/post-41823>

Hi Kelly. I don't know what part of Maryland you are in, but I live in Bowie. Please remember it's more important to fix your underlying health issues than it is to drop weight rapidly. Get healthy and the weight will follow. Welcome.

### caroline — 2012-07-30T11:09:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-maryland.2748/post-41916>

Welcome Kelly. There are lots of wonderful, really smart people on this forum so pick their collective brains!

### Jimmy82 — 2012-08-01T09:55:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.2775/post-42228>

Thanks a lot every one for this warm welcome i m really looking forward for the fun to catch up....... Just kidding ..

I hope to see you guys in the chats.......

Off course this is no doubt a great forum...

# Thread: Hello from Kentucky!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-kentucky.2912/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### adameads — 2012-08-08T05:29:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-kentucky.2912/post-43585>

I've had great success with the Primal Blueprint, but am looking for something to give me an extra boost. I came across Dr. Kruse on a podcast (fatburningman) while sleeping last night, and I spent most of today listening to Dr. Kruse's various speeches/talks/interviews on YouTube/iTunes/etc. Needless to say, I'm VERY intrigued.

Is it really as simple as continuing to follow the Primal Blueprint diet and adding CT?

I did a five minute bath tonight using full-cold tap water. It's been hot here, so I don't think the water is as cold as it could be. It wasn't that difficult. I've always been a fan of ending my showers with cold water in the summer because, at least in my head, it slams shut the pores. I seem to sweat less when I do this...maybe now I'll discover the true answer as to why?

A little about me:

6'1"

31 years old

SW: 251 (12/28/2011)

CW: 213 (have been as low as 207)

The last month my diet has been bad because of a move to a new home, busier at work (overnights at hotels), etc. When I'm eating clean, it's eggs and meat or leftovers for breakfast. Lunch/dinner consists of some of the following: GF beef, pastured pork/chicken, sardines, eggs, avocado, seasonal veggies from our CSA, a little fruit. I eat a Larabar three or four days a week. I take 10,000 IU of D3 three or four days a week, and 5,000 IU the rest.

Looking forward to learning from the community.

Thanks!

Adam

### Kiera2 — 2012-08-08T11:55:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-kentucky.2912/post-43609>

> **adameads;43876 wrote:** I've had great success with the Primal Blueprint, but am looking for something to give me an extra boost. I came across Dr. Kruse on a podcast (fatburningman) while sleeping last night, and I spent most of today listening to Dr. Kruse's various speeches/talks/interviews on YouTube/iTunes/etc. Needless to say, I'm VERY intrigued.
>
>
>
> Is it really as simple as continuing to follow the Primal Blueprint diet and adding CT?

Hi Adam, welcome! I think the answer is that it depends on where you're starting from, and what your goals are. This recent blog from Jack shows how to eat optimally [http://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-6-epi-paleo-rx/](http://http://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-6-epi-paleo-rx/) Good luck!

### caroline — 2012-08-08T12:12:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-kentucky.2912/post-43610>

Welcome to the forum and good luck!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Hi from New York
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-new-york.2980/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bonniesherbals — 2012-08-11T17:39:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-new-york.2980/post-44332>

Hi everyone, just wanted to introduce myself. I'm Bonnie Rogers and I'm an herbalist and a wellness coach. I'm so excited to have found this forum as so much of what Dr Kruse mentions is what I recommend.

Our bodies were designed to be alkaline, however with the common diet people are eating and the stress that abounds in our world most of our bodies are acidic and filled with disease. My goal is to assist people in making small shifts that create powerful changes in their bodies. By just changing one small thing miracles can happen.

I want people to feel comfortable in their bodies, it doesn't matter if you are thin or overweight, small or large you should feel comfortable in who you are and learn how to release what doesn't serve you. Every Body is Beautiful ([http://wheredidmybodygo.com](http://http://wheredidmybodygo.com)) is the name of a workshop that I am presenting with a few other women to allow women to learn to release old messaging.

I'm so excited to learn from this forum ways that people are working to make a difference in their own health, the health of their families and the health of their communites.

### caroline — 2012-08-12T05:40:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-new-york.2980/post-44471>

Welcome to the forum - lots to learn!

# Thread: Hi from South Island, New Zealand
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-south-island-new-zealand.3326/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### amber_tyrrell@hotmail.com — 2012-08-29T10:18:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-south-island-new-zealand.3326/post-48035>

Hello everyone,

I am a 42 year old mother of 4 children aged between 16 and 8 years old. I've been very overweight for a very long time and have been on lots of different diets which work for a while and then I just can't seem to stick to them. I came across an interveiw with Dr Jack about 4 weeks ago by 'accident', and I have been reading and listening to as much as I can since then. I started the paleo diet on Monday 27th Aug (2 days ago), and started the face dunking as well. I have been working very hard gardening in the last two days and usually by the second night I am KNACKERED!!! But tonight I have good energy, my knees would normally be hurting like hell, but they are fine. I'm so excited about this - the cold thermogensis seems like the 'missing link' that I have been searching for all these years. Time will tell I guess, but it just makes complete sense to me.

We live in the foothills of a mountain and are 420 metres above sea level with amazing views over the valley on a 100 acre property where we are endeavouring to be as self sufficient as possible - grow our own veges, and raise our own meat, etc. The paleo way of eating is therefore perfect! I just found myself an old cast iron bath that I bought for $40 and I'm going to put it next to our water tank so that I can fill it straight from there (and it will double as a water trough when I need it for that). We are just heading into spring here, and the days are getting longer, so I will be in the cold bath looking at the view in the evenings! Sounds like bliss - not so?!

I feel like a shift has happened inside of me - that I am starting a new way of living, so that I can live the rest of my life with no inflammation and use my body to its fullest capacity. I'm very excited!! Thank you Dr Kruse!!

### freesia — 2012-08-29T10:45:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-south-island-new-zealand.3326/post-48036>

Hi Amber. I envy your cast iron bath with a view set-up!

I'm from the North island and I've been doing the Leptin Reset and Cold Thermogenesis since early April. Seafood heavy diet since July.

I don't have excess weight but I did actually lose a few pounds overall and gain some muscle. Inflammation seems to have improved, fitness definitely has...out of sight (!) and adrenal fatigue too.

I look forward to reading about your progress!

### amber_tyrrell@hotmail.com — 2012-08-29T12:47:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-south-island-new-zealand.3326/post-48037>

Hi Freesia - nice to see a fellow kiwi here. I'm slowly navigating my way around the website - i have much to learn!

### TribalSpice — 2012-08-29T13:31:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-south-island-new-zealand.3326/post-48043>

Hi Freesia and Amber,

I am in the far western state of NZ, also know as the Gold Coast . I love the idea of your bath. 100acres would be amazing!

I know the feeling.  Welcome Amber!

Welcome Amber to this great forum and keep reading, reading, reading and enjoy the brain pain!!

### amber_tyrrell@hotmail.com — 2012-08-30T09:37:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-south-island-new-zealand.3326/post-48316>

Hi TribalSpice - so your on that little island to the west! Can't wait to get my bath - will have to post a pic of it when its set up (if I can work out how to put pics on here!)

### amber_tyrrell@hotmail.com — 2012-08-30T09:40:13+0300

Thank you Caroline - its one thing I like about this site - you certainly couldn't get bored as there is just SOOOO much information - I'm a little befuddled at the moment, but as the brain fog clears from being on the paleo diet I'm sure I'll be able to work it all out! (I'm near the end of day 4 and today I have such clarity of mind for the first time in ages)

Hurray! Finally another Kiwi! I have been on board with Dr K (epi paleo, CT, circadian cycles, etc etc etc) since the beginning of 2012 and my life has taken fantastic turns as a result. I am in Christchurch. Where on the South Island are you? (It is hard to get the seafood when you're rural?) I am spinning my wheels trying to find local docs who can support me as I get the testing, BHRT, etc that I need... NZ is bliss but it's also in the dark ages when it comes to what the Americans have available in this realm! So glad to have you on board!! Lauren

### amber_tyrrell@hotmail.com — 2012-08-31T00:20:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-south-island-new-zealand.3326/post-48449>

> **KiwiLauren;48917 wrote:** Hurray! Finally another Kiwi! I have been on board with Dr K (epi paleo, CT, circadian cycles, etc etc etc) since the beginning of 2012 and my life has taken fantastic turns as a result. I am in Christchurch. Hi Lauren! I am in the Waitaki Valley in North Otago. Yes this is all such new stuff - no one has heard about it around here, and I'm still so new to it that I don't even know what epi-paleo is! We have a fantastic doctor here who is of American decent, and she is amazing at researching new stuff. If you know exactly what tests you need to have done, then I could ask her if she is able to do them. We are about 3 hours drive south of Chch. I go to Oamaru once a week to get groceries, seafood is rather expensive - can you stick to meats and have seafood occasionally?

### KiwiLauren — 2012-08-31T04:33:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-south-island-new-zealand.3326/post-48505>

Thanks for the potential heads-up about working with your doc. I may come back to you on that. I need someone to support me with BHRT and this is proving hard to find. Dr K is so good that I currently know more than the doctors I'm talking to!

Oamaru is my fantasy CT place! Have you CT'd there? I will some day.

There is a lot to learn when you come on board. Dr K began discussing the epi-paleo diet a while back... basically to be optimal we must eat a seafood-heavy diet. There is an entire webinar about it. Seafood at least 5 times per week, with shellfish being number one and fish number two. And raw better than cooked. I have been totally capitalising on roe season... $6.95/kg and I eat it raw every day! There is a lot of info out here. The other big thing, if you haven't got there yet is the light cycles. Light is more important than diet. So no artificial light and definitely none after dark. We just made it through an entire winter with nothing but candlelight and no screens of any kind after sunset. It's been fantastic actually.

Keep in touch! (And feel free to PM me if that's easier.)

Lauren

### KiwiLauren — 2012-08-31T04:34:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-south-island-new-zealand.3326/post-48506>

PS I should add that some of us are eating seafood 7-14x week and others are doing much less. The 5 times a week was just a general number, not something Dr K has prescribed.

### FreeRangeKiwi — 2012-09-01T03:25:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-south-island-new-zealand.3326/post-48680>

> **KiwiLauren;48917 wrote:** Hurray! Finally another Kiwi! I have been on board with Dr K (epi paleo, CT, circadian cycles, etc etc etc) since the beginning of 2012 and my life has taken fantastic turns as a result. I am in Christchurch. Where on the South Island are you? (It is hard to get the seafood when you're rural?) I am spinning my wheels trying to find local docs who can support me as I get the testing, BHRT, etc that I need... NZ is bliss but it's also in the dark ages when it comes to what the Americans have available in this realm! So glad to have you on board!! Lauren

Hey guys, thought I'd let you know you're not the only Kiwis! I've been addicted to Jack's blog since June 2011. If you want to join Primal/Paleo Dunedin or the Dunedin Polar Bears Club on fb here are the links [https://www.facebook.com/groups/188990581234244/](http://https://www.facebook.com/groups/188990581234244/) and [https://www.facebook.com/DunedinPolarBears](http://https://www.facebook.com/DunedinPolarBears). The water in Dunedin harbour has been down to 5.2 degrees this year and I'm trying to get down there daily although sometimes I have to resort to the bath at home. I have gone from 124kg to 88kg and am always warm. Last time I was this weight I was 16 and could run 100m in around 15seconds. I swam competitively too. I am now 33, can run 100m in around 12seconds and can swim faster than when I trained and only need to breathe every 14 strokes (once per short-course length). I don't not do any training other than lifting weights once or twice a week or whenever I can be bothered.

I have got a friend doing CT and he's an avid reader too. We are both in the Physical Education department at Otago Uni. I'm doing postgrad training as an exercise physiologist (looking at hyponatraemia) - might be setting up a CT study - will keep you posted. My friend is one of the neurophysiology lab techs in the Physed Dept. There is heaps of research going on in the department on extreme environments, but it is all to do with performance. I hope to change that as I'm into clinical exercise and metabolism.

Good to hear there are others... I heard from someone in Queenstown when they commented on Jack's FB page. Sing out if you're a silent observer!

### freesia — 2012-09-01T04:43:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-south-island-new-zealand.3326/post-48681>

Hi FRK

I LOVE that you might be setting up a CT study. (Otago does great work!)

I have ME/CFS...have had it all my adult life so my response to CT likely isn't exactly typical, but in my own way I have made fantastic gains.

Thanks for introducing yourself here....great to know others are discovering how well we can do when we embrace Winter conditions as we're designed to!
