Hi FreeRangeKiwi, wow so good to know there is someone close to us here. You have done so well to lose 40kgs! How long did that take you? I'm very excited about this whole paleo/ct regime - its so different and yet makes so much sense. I got an old cast iron bath today and have put it by our water tank, will be cleaning it out tomorrow and giving it a try. I am only 6 days into my face dunking, but I can't wait. I've lost 3 kilos in 5 days, so feeling good about that. Just hope I can keep it up. Do you follow the circadian rythyms like KiwiLauren does? I'm interested to learn more about it.

Great to know you're out there! Funny that we all seem to be situated on the south island. I did some ocean CT over winter... i wish I had a club (you should have seen the looks of those walking along the beach all rugged up and me in the water all by myself in my togs (with my hat on of course). I'm guessing you get those looks regularly. I no longer complain about the typical Kiwi uninsulated house in winter. It's built in daily CT for me. Like Amber, I'd be keen to know if you're following circadian cycles and epi-paleo diet, too. If your peers think you're weird swimming in the ocean, just wait til you have to stay home past 5pm with the lights off! How many do you have in your Polar Bear club?? (Btw, I tried to reply yes to your friend request on this forum but am not sure I did it correctly).

### amber_tyrrell@hotmail.com — 2012-09-03T13:47:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-south-island-new-zealand.3326/post-49161>

I spoke to KiwiLauren on the phone last night - thank you for answering so many questions for me Lauren... I had lots and still have many more. I have been doing the paleo diet for one week today, plus face dunking and I have lost 3.8 kg! (8.3 pounds) I'm stoked about that!

I wanted to share a little story that I told Lauren last night about ct. I grew up on a farm here in the south island of New Zealand, and as a child we did A LOT of swimming in the rivers, and even now in the summer I always take my kids to the river to swim rather than the local swimming pool, the rivers are colder but much more fun and we love being in nature. About 20 years ago I backpacked around the world by myself starting my journey in the States and traveling up the west coast and into Canada. I hitched a lift with a couple of Australians and we were camping in the Rockies. One morning after not having showered for 3 days I decided I was going to wash my hair in a river (somewhere in the Rockies in Alberta) and this river was glacial fed, so it was FREEZING. I must have been in the water for 10 minutes or so and it really was so cold it took my breath away. But after I got out and dried myself by skin was tingling all over and I felt so warm and ALIVE!! I often tell people that it was the best I have ever felt in my life! On another occasion (about 2 years ago), my husband and I slept the night out in the hills behind where we live, and in the morning I jumped into a stream that was also freezing, but only up to my waist as it was just too cold. I then got out and drip dried in the sun, and my skin started tingling from the waist down, and that was where I was warm - I was cold from the waist up! So I jumped back into the stream and went under this time as I knew I would be warmer all over and feeling alive afterwards.

So now that I am learning about cold thermogenesis for the first time, I can call on my own experiences to back up the benefits that ct does for us!

### caroline — 2012-09-03T23:45:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-south-island-new-zealand.3326/post-49245>

What a great story - thanks for sharing

# Thread: I'm back
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-back.3430/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Primal Dream — 2012-09-03T23:17:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-back.3430/post-49238>

Hello everyone,

I have read Dr. K. since the monster MDA thread. I started the LRx several times and stuck with it in January 2012. Good stuff started happening. Then I took off on essentially a 5 month road trip. I started off well but found it hard to maintain on the road. I went back to bad habits.

When the CT stuff came out I bought a bunch of stuff to get me started, but with traveling, it was hard (then I lost my bag of stuff when I got home).

So I returned this summer and then had to travel some more because my mother was ill (she lives 800 miles away). I'm finally settling in back home, and needing so badly to get back to LRx and into CT again.

I am really wanting to be serious this time. I lost about 30 lb. when I first started the primal blueprint the summer of 2010. I have kept a good 25 of that off, but I'm still wanting to lose another 20 lb but, more important, to get to optimal health.

I am 63, have been overweight for pretty much all of my adult life. Thankfully I'm no longer obese, but I really want to be optimal.

I need lots of help! I will be starting a journal in the next couple of days and hope you will help me on my journey.

Thanks! No more excuses!!

### caroline — 2012-09-03T23:39:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-back.3430/post-49243>

Welcome back Primal Dream! You are way ahead of the game - you know what to do and that it really works! I look forward to hearing all about your journey. Sometimes life does get in the way.

### Primal Dream — 2012-09-04T19:48:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-back.3430/post-49397>

Thanks for the welcome, Caroline, and for helping me bust through the shame that I didn't stick with it before.

Now it's onwards and upwards. I now have lots of seafood to help me on my way. And seaweed.

I now need to get into the discomfort of the BAB. But I know from experience that it will only take a few days to begin to feel like normal.

### caroline — 2012-09-05T03:41:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-back.3430/post-49517>

Hi Primal Dream - we have all been there - absolutely - onward and upward! please keep posting and let us know how you are doing. It helps everyone.

### nonchalant — 2012-09-07T00:30:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-back.3430/post-49864>

Hi Primal Dream. No shame at all. Just information. Glad to have you with us.

# Thread: Stacy from CA
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stacy-from-ca.3640/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### stacyj — 2012-09-13T21:18:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stacy-from-ca.3640/post-51491>

I have been lurking around the forum for some time. Been too busy reading to post, lol!

I've been relatively healthy my entire life, cough cough.....Not too bad for having endured the SAD for most of my life. I have 6 wonderful kids (my youngest is 16, so not really kids anymore) and three grand daughters.

I gained a tremendous amount of weight several years ago, not good. Had been somewhat successful following a low carb diet- lost almost 50 pounds. Then, got into a MVA and things have spiraled down from there. Hormones were changing prior to that, but have definitely been changing fast the last couple of years. Add to that the back and neck injuries, which flare up frequently, and being completely dependent on thyroid meds (dont have one anymore)...and what a mess!

I am proof that diet alone cannot fix things, helps, but not as much as I need. Finally getting my back to where I can function- hoping to keep it that way- and now starting on tackling my hormones.

I am a work in progress!

### caroline — 2012-09-14T02:08:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stacy-from-ca.3640/post-51540>

Hi Stacyj. Welcome to the forum and good luck! keep us posted please.

# Thread: Meg from Georgia
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meg-from-georgia.3704/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Meg — 2012-09-17T00:47:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meg-from-georgia.3704/post-52002>

I had my first consultation with Dr. K on Friday and found myself shaking as we ended our session. Iâ€™ve wanted answers for so long on why I feel so crummy but I must admit there is some truth in the saying that ignorance is bliss. And as most of you know Dr. K is not one to promote any kind of ignorance and hold back on truth- no matter how bad it might hurt.

I do want the truth because I know that the truth will ultimately set me free to find more truth. But some of the truth on Friday hit a place in my heart that is raw and afraid. The truth that if I donâ€™t fix what is wrong with me I will end up like my mother- and my mother, at 71 years old canâ€™t remember bits of a conversation we just had, or my birthday or much else that would make her able to function without the constant attention of my father. The Alzheimerâ€™s showed up at age 67. All my life I have fought to not be like my mother â€“ which is another story in itself, and here I am coming to the realization that physically I really am currently headed down a very similar path.

The past 8 months have been hard, lots of physical ailments. But the hardest part has been the mental side. The anxiety, the lack of clear thinking, the fear and irrationality that has taken hold of my mind and has made me feel like a crazy personâ€¦... made me feel like my mother. It is hard to be an advocate for yourself when you are in this kind of state of mind, but somehow I was led to Dr Kâ€™s site and have been eating up as much information as my poor brain can hold. And implementing as much as I can. This Gut/Brain series was written for me! I feel like I know a lot of the regular participants on this forum because I have read and reread many of your entries. The fog has lifted enough for me to pursue last Fridayâ€™s appointment and now to write these words and start to pursue help from those on this site who are running hard after optimal. Because I want nothing less than optimal for the rest of my years and I know I can change my future.

I have much work to do. I have Candida and gut flora issues, wacked out hormones, glucose and cortisol problems. I will be pursuing metformin, cycloset and more BHRT and new doctors. I hope to dig in and start asking questions and I look forward to becoming friends. I know that I will find the answers I need and find the health that I want.

Hi, my name is Meg. I am a wife and mother to four awesome children. I live in Peachtree City GA (ATL area), Iâ€™m a real estate agent, and am a very creative upbeat person. I have an addictive personality, I used to be addicted to sugar but now I am addicted to finding health and truth and optimal!

Today I ate a dozen raw oysters & a half a pound of fish for lunch. There is no turning back!

PS. Dr. Kruse was nothing but kind and sensitive while dishing out honesty during our consult- I would never want to give the impression otherwise and make anyone to hesitate to seek his help.

### caroline — 2012-09-17T01:41:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meg-from-georgia.3704/post-52014>

welcome to the forum and optimum! I am so glad you had such a positive experience with Dr. K. and I look forward to hearing all about your journey. When you post your successes \[and failures\] it helps everyone. There are a lot of really smart,kind people here that are very happy to answer questions or discuss whatever....Good luck

### SeaHorse — 2012-09-17T01:56:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meg-from-georgia.3704/post-52024>

Welcome Meg...all the best with your journey!

# Thread: New to the group
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-the-group.3715/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### REN2BRAINS — 2012-09-17T20:16:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-the-group.3715/post-52172>

Hello all,

I have been reading and reading. I haven't started anything yet.

I have Hashimoto's and one Dr. said I probably have fibromyalgia(which I chose to ignore and not take Lyrica etc.)

I have herniated and bulging disks in my neck. I often fight pain and my endo says all my levels are okay but I still feel rotten a lot of the time.

My biggest concern is my daughter and how to get a 19 year old(college freshman and busy busy busy) to start all of this. She is 5ft7in, 240 pounds. When she was five her hormones went crazy and she gained weight like crazy, 50 pounds in 5 months, with no change in diet. Started growing hair in places a 5 year old shouldn't. It finally stopped but she still can't lose weight and just seems to gain. She has had high uric acid, acne, a DNET brain tumor(It was removed in 2008) and now they are saying Crohn's even though her colon is normal. Blood test have high CRP and serum IGE. Her stomach issues started after a campylobacter infection and they gave her 2 or 3 antibiotics. Hopefully I can find some answers here to help her.

### caroline — 2012-09-18T00:37:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-the-group.3715/post-52228>

Welcome REN2BRAINS. Keep reading and absorbing and ask questions. You have a lot on your plate and it is so important to help your daughter as fast as possible. Have you looked at the easy start guide? The first thing to do is to start the Leptin RX and then go from there. If you have labs - post them and someone will hack them for you and suggest what else you might need. Dr. Kruse will tell you that you need to fix yourself first and then you can help others - lead by example. Read the journals so you can see how others are working at fixing themselves and maybe start a journal yourself. We are all a work in progress and it is hard work but you will get there.

### samerry50@gmail.com — 2012-09-26T01:22:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-the-group.3715/post-54288>

I'm from Tennessee and Dr. Kruse just recently fixed a ruptured disc in my back. i'm new to all of this and just embarking on this journey. Have lots to read and learn. Have started on the Paleo diet as I can figure it out so far from the Paleo Answer he asked me to read and the Paleo Diet book. I have a lot of weight to lose but I not only want to lose weight but to improve the muscles in my back so I don't rupture more discs. Also to improve my overall health. I see there is tons of imformation on this site and I'm just beginning to read so I better get started.

# Thread: Greetings from New Hampshire.. doing research on Leptin for my situation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-new-hampshire-doing-research-on-leptin-for-my-situation.3817/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 28
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cramernh — 2012-09-22T05:32:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-new-hampshire-doing-research-on-leptin-for-my-situation.3817/post-53359>

Hello!

I am a married 38yr old from New Hampshire with a 19yr old heading off to college. I spent the last 15+ years working two industries: culinary and healthcare. Now that I have an empty nest, I remain in my healthcare position. I am an advanced certified medical reimbursement specialist within a large provider/health system network. I use my clinical background to help with the appeals process and battle insurances to get medical claims paid.

When Im not battling for medical claims and the headaches associated with 38220's always needing a modifier 59, I am a bassoonist with a private university's orchestra department.

I keep up with my previous cheffing background as my husband seems to think Thanksgiving occurs 365 days per year, LOL!

I am currently a patient being treated for PCOS, hyperinsulinemia, metabolic syndrome, and dealing with the residual healing of alopecia... I spent a number of years trying to find practitioners who had a background with IR/PCOS and Metabolic syndrome, unfortunately there are not many out there.

I have learned so much about myself from this one Endocrinologist I am seeing however, I truly believe not all the appropriate testing has been done.

I have a very clean dietary intake where I consume 100% clean foods. I have learned about the effects of the bread/grain group of foods and how it has affected me. It is one year that I have been without that family of foods and in my first three months: I have completed erradicated my diabetes risk from 11.2 to 5.2 and successfully sustained that for exactly one year (today makes it one year exactly). My cholesterol also dramatically improved in the first three months, to the point of shocking not just the Endo, but it compelled my Dermatologist and PCP to call me and ask "WHAT DID YOU DO?" They almost did not believe me when I told them that I completely rid my home of all breads, pasta, rice, corn and flour. I havent had a can of soda in over 12 years...

But - the real issue I have going right now is that my weight is NOT dropping. For one year, I have followed the guidance of the Endo: 1600 calories per day, no more than 120g of carbohydrates daily (each meal no more than 40g)... My total cholesterol panel is proof that for one year, I clearly have made excellent food choices... but with all of the exercising I am doing at the gym... I just cannot drop this weight.

I have begged the Endo to consider other testing as I feel there may be other things we simply have not reviewed yet.

I was downright INSULTED that she would even suggest Qsymia to me. I complied with food changes willingly and have successfully stuck to that for one year - Qsymia would be for someone who does not comply with food portions/modifications.. I was angry she would even consider recommending to me a damn drug.

Out of all the tests I have had since being diagnosed, I have never once had leptin considered, glucagon considered and it boggles my mind why this Endo thinks a quickly-approved weight loss drug would help.... Im about to fire her to be honest.

I do hope there are others here like me and Im just wondering about Leptin resistance and whether or not I fit in this category of being possibly considered for...

Thank you for any future replies!

### SeaHorse — 2012-09-22T15:22:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-new-hampshire-doing-research-on-leptin-for-my-situation.3817/post-53385>

Welcome from over the border in Vermont! Have you had a chance to read about the tests suggested for looking at your inflammation levels, hormone profile etc? How about your light cycles and CT? Good food choices are part of the picture, but not the whole picture. Certainly you've come to the right place to get lots of information from the blogs and the forum!

### Huck — 2012-09-22T15:23:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-new-hampshire-doing-research-on-leptin-for-my-situation.3817/post-53386>

Welcome. I wouldn't be to quick to fire her. There's not very many great docs around and yours sounds trainable!

This site is kind of like a treasure hunt. All, or at least most, of the answers you need are here someplace. Read, read, read. And when you can't find it or have questions, just ask.

### Dali Dula — 2012-09-22T17:07:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-new-hampshire-doing-research-on-leptin-for-my-situation.3817/post-53401>

Welcome. I will give you the advice I give everyone if they are not already doing it. Don't consume any calories within 4 hours of sleep. You can find more about this in the blog concerning circadian cycles. I missed this for a long time and once i put it into practice things changed dramatically. good luck. And if you haven't already seen it check out the leptinRX.

### bigknitwit — 2012-09-22T18:30:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-new-hampshire-doing-research-on-leptin-for-my-situation.3817/post-53409>

Welcome  I'm in Southern NH.

### cramernh — 2012-09-23T22:34:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-new-hampshire-doing-research-on-leptin-for-my-situation.3817/post-53690>

> **Dali Dula;54040 wrote:** Welcome. I will give you the advice I give everyone if they are not already doing it. Don't consume any calories within 4 hours of sleep. You can find more about this in the blog concerning circadian cycles. I missed this for a long time and once i put it into practice things changed dramatically. good luck. And if you haven't already seen it check out the leptinRX.

That is a definite.. in fact if I do eat within four hours, I will actually vomit... the most I will have is a glass of water simply for the fact that the glass of water has been the norm for me for many years.

### nonchalant — 2012-09-25T18:17:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-new-hampshire-doing-research-on-leptin-for-my-situation.3817/post-54133>

Hi Cramernh, welcome. With Autumn here, you may want to reduce those carbohydrates way back. And for winter, reduce them even further, close to zero.

For your thyroid, I'd want to know what your Free T3, Free T4, and reverse T3 levels are. Also you should check for antibodies. I don't see a Vitamin D level in your results above. Is the CRP number a regular CRP (which checks for infection) or a highly sensitive (cardiac) CRP?

Your LDL is low, so maybe your thyroid is working very well and converting the cholesterol to various hormones like it should. But you mentioned that you were on prescription medicines. You aren't taking a cholesterol-lowering drug, are you?

I would guess that your weight loss stall is caused by a hormone imbalance, or by toxins hidden away in your fat cells. If your body gets rid of the fat, it would release the toxins as well.

### cramernh — 2012-09-25T18:30:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-new-hampshire-doing-research-on-leptin-for-my-situation.3817/post-54138>

Hiya!

I know that from previous labs (I do my best to keep a copy of each-drives them batty! LOL), I have not seen a Free T3/T4 or the reverese T3 ever done so that is my next step. Thankfully my Vit D levels are within normal limits (had to call the Endo back for that separate result). Unfortunately the CRP is the standard... so Ill be adding HS-CRP to the list.

My LDL is low thanks to getting rid of the bread/grains group of foods as well as potatoes... NOOOOOO no cholesterol-reducing medications because I successfully lowered it by the food changes I made.... Im the only one in the family thus far who has been successful in doing that and never needed the medication for it.

My testosterone/estrogen levels have been stabilized for one calendar year thus far.....

My Endo has no idea what to consider next for testing... thats why she recommended that damn drug the FDA was so quick to approve... I told her "HELL NO are you KIDDING?!" I was insulted by it to be honest.... She admits to having no other patients with an HDL of 86.... I am her first.... despite my diagnoses of PCOS, Insulin Resistance, Metabolic Syndrome, etc....

### cramernh — 2012-09-25T18:33:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-new-hampshire-doing-research-on-leptin-for-my-situation.3817/post-54139>

As of right now, the Endo recommended this:

...and yes, I am very well aware that Endos have no full nutritional training, just the basics to help diabetics and other pts out.

1600 cal per day (almost 300 below my BMR)

no more than 120g of carbohydrates daily - not to exceed 40g per meal

no refined carbs (hence my willingness to just eliminate all breads, pasta, rice, corn, flour and potatoes)

I am an avid rabbit food lover, and I have a 64oz mug of iced water at my side all the time.

I cook 100% clean, 100% of the time....

Ocella, Metformin, Fosinopril, Spironolactone, D3, Calcium Citrate, B complex, Biotin, Fish oil.... That is what I am taking right now

### cramernh — 2012-09-25T18:35:31+0300

### nonchalant — 2012-09-25T22:09:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-new-hampshire-doing-research-on-leptin-for-my-situation.3817/post-54232>

Cramernh, the 50g of protein is just for breakfast. As far as meat goes, make it seafood if possible. Yes to all visible animal fat (but I still have difficulties with visible beef fat myself.) Just eat whatever protein and fat is needed to keep you happy until the next meal, with no snacking. People that regain their leptin sensitivity will move their largest meal to the evening, instead of morning. Happy to have you with us, btw.

### cramernh — 2012-09-25T22:52:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-new-hampshire-doing-research-on-leptin-for-my-situation.3817/post-54240>

Thank you for the clarification... looks like people might benefit from some grilling tips I have as most of the fat from beef melts off if you are good at controlling both direct and indirect heat

I LOVE LOVE LOVE the idea of no snacking..

Im still trying to figure out how the hell Ill be able to get exercise in during the later part of the day... I can definitely do that on the weekends... but during the week is chaotic with my schedules... for the longest time, I had been working out at 5am for an hour because it is convenient to get in there and put a solid hour's worth of exercise in, come to work to shower here, and the voila: ready to start my shift.... Yes, in case you are wondering, I did read on Dr. Kruse's excerpt regarding no exercise before/after breakfast... lol

### cramernh — 2012-09-26T22:16:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-new-hampshire-doing-research-on-leptin-for-my-situation.3817/post-54548>

OK... I just received an email from the Endocrinologist this afternoon.. she confirmed that I did have the HS-CRP, it came back within normal limits. She also confirmed the last thyroid screening done (including reverse) all came back normal as well. She also referenced my hormonal levels are all within normal limits as well from the June 2012 draw (her office didnt send me page three evidently)

Im ready to punch a hole in the wall out of frustration.... I dont know how much more compliant I can be. My head is killing me from doing all kinds of research on my conditions, in fact, Im lucky to understand about 85% of it...

I dont want to throw my hands up on this - I know I have come so far and maintained well, my labs are proof that I am compliant and I just have to keep reminding myself of that fact: if I were jerking around and eating poorly, my labs wouldnt look so damn good like the have been for the last calendar year.

Very frustrating

### SeaHorse — 2012-09-26T22:55:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-new-hampshire-doing-research-on-leptin-for-my-situation.3817/post-54558>

Did she give you the exact figures or just tell you within "normal limits"? This was my downfall for years...not having the labs right in front of me and going by the "everything's fine" I heard from the Dr.'s. Being in the top quartile is very different than being "within normal limits." You could try posting them here and see how other's respond.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-new-hampshire-doing-research-on-leptin-for-my-situation.3817/post-54591>

### cramernh — 2012-09-27T00:35:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-new-hampshire-doing-research-on-leptin-for-my-situation.3817/post-54592>

only within normal limits/normal, etc.

Here is a copy-paste from her email reply to me:

**_I am glad you are doing your research.Here is the breakdown on your questions:Hs-crp- non-specific marker. It helps determine risk for ischemic heart disease. It may help understand the risk but may not be related to what you are dealing with currentlyDHEA- checked in 2010 was normalSalivary cortisol has the same specificity as 24 hour urine cortisol (which is still the gold standard) for ruling out Cushings or cortisol excess. You had the 24 hour urine studies done prior which was normal. TSH and FT4 levels done previously were normal.T3 levels help understand the thyroid disease - but in most cases do not give any further information if your TSH and Free T4 levels are normal. T4 gets converted to T3 in your body and most often checking the T4 levels is a good indicator of how your T3 levels are.Reverse T3 is not helpful and in general is only checked when you have abnormal thyroid function tests from an illness not related to an underlying thyroid condition.Naturopathic literature suggests otherwise - but based on my experience reverse T3 has never been helpful.CLA - natural supplement - which I have never prescribed before and not really have experience with seems to be something that may help (or not) as based on reading that I had done the results in few studies was helpful for weight loss and in some studies it did not show benefit.Based on what I see it seems to be a fairly well tolerated med, however can increase insulin resistance and changes lipids.Unfortunately, I cannot come up with a good answer for your persistent issues. I can only ask you to continue. I will be happy to answer any questions you have_**

### rwhite — 2012-10-18T16:00:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-new-hampshire-doing-research-on-leptin-for-my-situation.3817/post-58911>

Welcome to the forum> I am sure you will get a lot out of it.

### nonchalant — 2012-10-18T22:48:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-new-hampshire-doing-research-on-leptin-for-my-situation.3817/post-59022>

Well, "normal" lab results mean that you are in the same boat as most of the other people in the US.

Your doctor should provide paperwork that was sent from the lab to the doctor, giving the normal range and exactly where on that range you are.

Dr. Kruse does not want people to be in the normal range for HS-CRP. He wants us to be below normal. He wants no systemic inflammation at all, in fact.

### marylouise — 2012-09-26T01:42:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.3916/post-54294>

Hello everyone, I am marylouise. I am 61 years old. I started my new way of eating. March, 2011. I have lost 137.8 pounds, so far. I started off at 300 pounds. Today I weigh 163.8. I would not have lived much longer had I continued to eat refined carbs and processed food. I was on 2 different types of insulin and had to use a go-cart to go shopping. I could not go outside and enjoy Gods beautiful world. I had very limited time left to live. From heart problems to breathing problems.

I need spell-check and it doesn't seem to be here??

I had stopped losing and tried hard to restart weight loss. This went on since March. I have been on LC Induction for one year. Then I tried lowering protein and calories and bamm, I started losing again. On my new woe to date, I have lost 9.8 pounds. I started this new woe 8-21 of this year. And it is fantastic!

I restrict calories and have a UD and a DD. I eat 1.300 calories on my UD and 600 to 650 on my DD. I keep my carbs from around 10 to 25 max. I have started to raise them very slowly. I use a site calculator to record every bite I eat or drink.

I no longer use any aide to control blood glucose

It is a pleasure to met ya'll

I will use my picture as soon as the site will let me.

marylouise

### marylouise — 2012-09-26T06:19:39+0300

### MamaGrok — 2012-09-26T16:14:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.3916/post-54407>

I don't think there's any post minimum for an avatar. Just hit your Settings button at the top of the page and search around.

The WOE here is basically

\- lots of seafood & meat (& eggs if no autoimmune disease)

\- SEASONAL veggies & fruits, so your carbs will be highest in the summer, lowest (nothing) in winter

\- dairy is mainly fermented, and only for those with no autoimmune disease and certain other good health markers

\- plenty of coconut oil, animal fats, & cold olive oil

\- exercise is simple - lift heavy things & sprint once in a while, once you're leptin sensitive (there is a nice page somewhere on the blog explaining how to tell this)

\- cold thermogenesis - starts with face dunking in ice water, moves to cold baths. It is MUCH easier than it sounds (almost all of it thought it horrifying and impossible at first) and the benefits are beyond your wildest dreams

Most of us are tackling these things a little bit at a time, so everyone's in a different place. 100% compliance is not required for discussion!

There is no

\- worrying about carbs in non-produce foods

\- obsessing over weight

\- processed food

The main focus is health, health, health. Labs & symptoms help guide our progress. Weight is only one symptom; it is \*not\* the be-all, end-all. However, many of us have lost a LOT of weight along the path.

As you can tell, there are similarities between Atkins & this, b/c Dr. Atkins hit on the truth that carbs play a major role in weight loss. but Atkins alone (just pure low carb, and maybe a focus on avoiding processed food) is unlikely to hit the other health markers we're changing here, everything from thyroid to sexual dysfunction, eczema to degenerative disc disease.

I'm sure I forgot something, lol.

THIS IS IT! For a week now, I've enjoyed immersing myself in the most comprehensive material regarding my whole health condition. But this site covers all the additional problems:lyme, leaky gut, adrenal fatigue, etc. I'm really grateful to hop aboard and tweak my protocol for a more complete recovery!

Thanks to the things I have done correctly, the body burden is lessened-brain fog, etc, but the tissue damage is increasing-especially the leaky gut. It's really mucosal damage that is sooo subterrain and oxidative, that normally good supports- licorice, aloe and coconut oil actually burn it further. I've made things so much worse by taking the wrong steps which are supposed to be helpful! Hi all, I'm new to ALL of this, including forums! Wanting to become a life lover, and inspiration to my husband and my 12 month old twin girls.
My journey in points:
\-overworked and underpaid!
\-tired and anxious
\-history of binge eating
\-history of binge exercising
\- bad sleeper
\- history of counting calories and weighing food
\- wanting to be understood
\- sick of feeling overwhelmed and tired of not being who I am meant to be!
Would appreciate any tips or tricks on how to become a butterfly. I am doing the leptin reset and reading and reading and adapting as I go. May use this forum as an accountability tool if I can?
My stats are :
5'6
Not overweight but have 3 months pregnant banana shape.
32 years old.
Thanks everyone in advance, Jack I am discovering the wealth of knowledge you share- thank you!

# Thread: G'Day
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday.6229/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sjoshua — 2013-03-08T02:17:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday.6229/post-79567>

Hey all,

I'm new to the forums here, and mainly interested in adding/experimenting with CT in my personal regimen.

A little about me:
I have been slow carb, transitioned to primal since January 2011. By July 2011, I had lost ~30 lbs of fat to achieve a <10% BF, which I have maintained since. Also in July 2011, I began intermittent fasting on a daily basis and
currently only eat 1 meal/day in the evening.

Additionally, I am interested in the impacts of paleo and CT on individuals with cystic fibrosis, which my girlfriend has. She is in the process of transitioning to a more paleo diet currently, and considering CT due to how degrading cystic fibrosis can be.

### caroline — 2013-03-08T10:49:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday.6229/post-79616>

Welcome from down under.....

### ashryn — 2013-03-08T11:49:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday.6229/post-79625>

Welcome! I have a question... If you fast daily, it isn't intermitTent really, is it? What is the rationale behind it?

### sjoshua — 2013-03-09T02:46:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday.6229/post-79761>

Thanks for the welcomes!!

> [ashryn said:](/goto/post?id=79625)
>
> Welcome! I have a question... If you fast daily, it isn't intermitTent really, is it? What is the rationale behind it?

My understanding was "Intermittent Fasting" referred to any eating pattern that possesses (scheduled) altering periods of fasting/feeding. So I believe intermittent would still apply. Maybe a better way of saying it would be "daily intermittent fasting" to avoid confusion?

The rationale is mainly driven by efficiency. I no longer wanted to have to spend the time to make and eat breakfast every morning, so I had eliminated that meal first (note I was already LS at this point). Once I realized how easy it was to do that, I figured why bother having to make a lunch either? Now I only have to prep, cook, clean once/day in the evening when I have the most free time. And, an added bonus is I get to take daily walks on my "lunch" break!

### nonchalant — 2013-03-10T03:13:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday.6229/post-79895>

SJoshua, has your GF started any CT? I was thinking even a cold wet washcloth might be a good way to start.

### sjoshua — 2013-03-12T00:10:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday.6229/post-80104>

> [Nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=79895)
>
> SJoshua, has your GF started any CT? I was thinking even a cold wet washcloth might be a good way to start.

Nonchalant: Sorry for not having replied back to your suggestions in my other thread yet... I have now tried to post twice, but both times recieved an error that a moderator has to approve my posts, so here's hoping they show up soon!

She will be starting CT this week, and I think the washcloth is brilliant as it won't impede her breathing. That will be a good first step!

# Thread: Hello From Ohio
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-ohio.6290/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### gtown937 — 2013-03-11T19:37:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-ohio.6290/post-80070>

Hello Everyone,

I just found this site this past week and have been reading it like it's free cocaine! Here is my story.....

I am a 37 y/o white male, grew up in small town, USA. Typical home....(I think). I have battled IBS since as far back as I can remember as well as depression. I work as an ER nurse (but soon to change...maybe hospice?? need something that does not impact my sleep and I have been in management and want to de-stress !)

The past couple years have been the worst.... Went through Divorce, foreclosure, bankruptcy, job loss, repo and had depression so bad I could hardly function.

I divorced in 2008/2009 and the depression and insomnia went to a new level.... started on a bunch of meds....ended up not able to work...not able to function....and ended up overdosing on Chloral Hydrate (accidental OD). I am grateful to have an awesome family and wife that stepped up and helped me.

I am off all medications and doing much better... The depression has been much better, pretty much gone. The insomnia and sleep still is not great... and energy kind of sucks.... I'm 37 and feel 77.....

A few months ago a friend of mine started talking to me about GAPS. It made sense. Plus, I liked the science and the author being a physician. I was sick of well meaning people suggest I eat a carrot or take Garlic to cure all disease and make me live happily ever after.

But the discussion started me thinking and seeking truth in my inner most being.

I started juicing; I started looking at the benefits of food. I noticed a change for the better even in just doing that. I started researching Gerson Therapy and veganism etc.... I thought they had some valid points and interesting thoughts. Gearson was to restrictive to me, I suppose if I had cancer I might reconsider as I think they know how to starve cancer!! But, to supplement with B-12 because you don't eat meat does not make sense to me.

In 2006 I had weight loss surgery. To be specific, I had the Biliopanctriatic Diversion with a Duodenal Switch. (This is not gastric by-pass...still have fully functioning stomach with pyloric sphincter)

[http://www.duodenalswitch.com/procedure/procedure.html](http://www.duodenalswitch.com/procedure/procedure.html)

The surgical changes results in malabsorption. I only absorb 50% of protein, 50% of complex carbs, 18% of fats 100% of simple sugar and alcoholâ€¦.

At the time, I did not fully appreciate the mind gut relationship. Now it seems as if I did not just have abdominal surgery but brain surgery !

But, I can not go back.... So, in my journey, I hope to gain understanding on how to eat and live (and do so with my unique gut). I have always dealt with bowel issues and depression; The depression became worse after the surgery... also because of major life events.....The medications did not help.... I was on 8 or 9 meds.... for depression, anxiety, insomnia, ADHD....you name it. The bowel issues?? I have pooped my pants more than any 5 year old !! (sorry TMI I know...but it is what it is...)

I am really looking forward to learning this Paleo life and what it means and also, how my surgery has impacted my mind body relationship and what specifically I need to do to reach my optimum life.

I am looking forward to getting to know you and to learn from you. If you have any questions of me, just ask. I donâ€™t mind at all. If someone can learn from me, then great !

Thanks, David

### nonchalant — 2013-03-11T20:08:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-ohio.6290/post-80074>

Hi David, welcome! Glad you are with us.
Do you use ox-bile? Seafood will help you tremendously, it is the densest food you can eat.
You also might really benefit from gathering free electrons from the sun and the earth. I know, it's a really different approach, but it sure can't hurt, and some of us are feeling benefits already.

### gtown937 — 2013-03-11T20:19:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-ohio.6290/post-80075>

Hello,

I do not use Ox-bile....yet.... I read about it in the Gerson Therapy info....so I am taking the HINT that it might be something to investigate further....lol Strangley enough, after the DS surgery seafood is the one thing I can eat a TON of.... I love it.

The gathering of free electrons etc.... I have no clue what you are talking about.....

### nonchalant — 2013-03-11T20:26:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-ohio.6290/post-80076>

Well... the free electrons have to do with quantum mechanics. Dr. Kruses recent blogs discuss it. It's complicated. In the mean time, you could consider walking barefoot outside, and getting some sun. Other than that, just keep reading!

### shilohman — 2013-03-11T20:34:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-ohio.6290/post-80079>

Welcome gtown937,

This site has been a great help to me, my girl friend and many others on this forum. You have come to the right place.

### fitness@home — 2013-03-11T21:24:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-ohio.6290/post-80082>

David,

Welcome! I have family living in Dayton, OH...

Good luck on your journey

### caroline — 2013-03-11T23:35:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-ohio.6290/post-80092>

Welcome David. It is great to have you here. Just keep reading and asking questions. Did you read the quick start guide? There is so much information here but slowly it will start to come together. Pay attention to light cycles. That is so huge! Grounding is huge too - and EMFs - actually everything is here! Good luck!

### gtown937 — 2013-03-12T04:10:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-ohio.6290/post-80143>

# Thread: hello, looking for a coach
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-looking-for-a-coach.6390/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### shauna — 2013-03-18T21:57:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-looking-for-a-coach.6390/post-81015>

Hello,
I'm new here, but not entirely new to these ideas. I am a busy stay at home mom, trying to start my own business- and I just do not have the time to figure out Jack's website by myself! I'm looking to hire a coach who can help me through this process, and can coach me through getting my hormones and health back on track.

Here is a little about me. I am almost 48 years old, not exhibiting perimenopausal symtoms yet, but I know I must be close. I weigh 240 lbs (as of this AM) and my weight continues to creep up. This is my biggest issue. My entire adult life until about age 39- my weight ranged from 145-170.Then I got pregnant-- gained 70 lbs, got pregnant again.. and have 2 lovely children. I thought I settled at 205-220. Tried HCG to lose the rest... got down to 190. and now am back up. I do have some emotional addictive eating problems too I guess.

Huge weight swings as you can see. I have always loved jumping in cold rivers lake and oceans. So I'm attracted to the ideas of CT.

I'm certain that I'm leptin resistant, and am sure that my hormones are out of whack., I eat fairly healthy- paleo/ weston price guided- but then will binge on girlscout cookies, or my kid's snacks. I'd love some help with a coach or practitioner who works with women my ageish, with similar issues.

Any recommendations? Does Jack even work with people directly? I just can't get through everything on his blog. it's too much for me and i have too much other stuff going on. Thanks so much.

Shauna
[sandandsea4g@gmail.com](mailto:sandandsea4g@gmail.com) or [shaunareisewitz@yahoo.com](mailto:shaunareisewitz@yahoo.com)

### caroline — 2013-03-18T22:55:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-looking-for-a-coach.6390/post-81019>

There are easy start guides for new people .... have you checked those out? Dr. K. does consults. Get your labs done and then buy a consult. any more questions - just ask away....
You need to start the leptin reset and pay attention to light cycles and read about C/T and pay attention to EMF's .... and then just jump in.
There is a ton of reading for sure - but the easy start guides will get you going ASAP. Go to the home page for the guides......

Welcome Shauna and good luck!

# Thread: Hello, from Oregon!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-oregon.6408/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### memullen1@yahoo.com — 2013-03-20T12:20:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-oregon.6408/post-81165>

I'm a 48 yo female with Lyme disease and Fibromyalgia (with PTSD). I started a dairy free paleo diet again, am taking Mag Malate, Mag Threonate, high dose fish oil (1325 mg tid), Armour Thyroid 60mg, Kelp. I haven't started CT yet. Which protocol should I start first? Is there a way to combine them?

### fitness@home — 2013-03-20T13:49:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-oregon.6408/post-81169>

Welcome! There are many here combining protocols. I would do the Leptin Reset protocol with BAB (big @ss breakfast) and Epi-Paleo. Eating more seafood is better than supplementing with a lot of fish oil. Think fish oil in its natural package!

Begin minding your light cycles and wear blue-blockers after dark. You can also start becoming aware of the amount of EMFs in your environment. Turn off wifi at home, unplug appliances that are not in use, remove TVs and other electronics from your bedroom. Some people here turn off the breakers in their homes at night.

Ease into CT when you start and read, read, read. If you have questions post them on the forum and you will receive feedback.

Good luck on your journey!

p.s. Forgot to add drink lots of springwater, especially if you have flouridated "city" water.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-20T15:38:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-oregon.6408/post-81176>

Welcome memullin! Glad to have you here.
Fitness@home gave some great advice. Fixing your circadian rhythm will allow your body to coordinate the release of various hormones at the right time and allow you to repair your body at night, when all the conditions are right. Excellent sleep is the most important thing, so that you can repair and also regenerate your energy from ATP.

One other small thing to add at the beginning, along with seafood and good water, is sunlight and walking barefoot. So you can work on the Leptin reset protocol, but add those other factors, so that you get a head start on the other protocols.

### memullen1@yahoo.com — 2013-03-20T19:34:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-oregon.6408/post-81201>

Thanks you two! I forgot to add that I'm 5'6 1/2" and 255 lbs. 15 of which I gained on my overnight job as a gluten free chef this past year. Also, about 9 months ago I had a total hysterectomy and am without a gallbladder. It gets worse! 2 or 3 days back into Paleo I got my first "smart" phone. After feeling remarkably better on the diet I suddenly felt awful again! Problem is, that's how I read Jack's info. So now I've been voraciously reading up on the articles in the middle of the night again, but this time it's closer to my head. I was supposed to be on day by now, but it looks like it's going to another month.

I was out of work for 4 years because I was so sick with the Lyme and severe microcytic anemia (which supposedly is now gone.) When I went back to work my one stipulation was that it had to be day shift. I was hired for day shift but never got to work it. I was immediately put on evenings, had my hysterectomy, and then put on graveyards. This last year has been hell! I don't know I got through it, but God got me through. Now I'm being teased by the promise
of going to day shift and am at the end of my rope. My bosses aren't lyi ng to me. We're still waiting for permits to expand the kitchen. Btw, I've been writing all this with numb fingers and a numb hand from carpal tunnel syndrome.

### fitness@home — 2013-03-20T19:46:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-oregon.6408/post-81205>

If the shift change doesn't happen soon, you may want to consider looking for another position. Your health is important! Shift work is hard on your body.

Do you have someone with a computer/printer setup? You could print out the blogs and read the hard copy version. That is what I do...just easier on my eyes. Of course, I am fortunate because I work in an office and print them out at work.

Hang in there.

### caroline — 2013-03-20T23:15:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-oregon.6408/post-81225>

Welcome - everyone has given you great advice. Hang in there ......

# Thread: Hello

**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cloak — 2013-03-21T01:38:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.6416/post-81244>

I've been reading around here and finally decided to join. I also know MamaGrok and have been encouraged by the progress she has made using the info from this site. I'm a mid 30's female with hashimotos/urticaria and I need to figure out how to get my hives under control. Was diagnosed Hashimoto's when pregnant with kid #2 but it wasn't until I developed chronic hives after kid #3 that I saw a naturopath and learned about being gluten free amongst a lot of other things. I was doing well for awhile and then my hives came back full force. Happy to say that all my dietary changes have gotten my autoantibodies well in the normal range but I still have these awful hives! Looking forward to learning what I can from the wise folks on this forum.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-21T02:10:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.6416/post-81246>

I cloak, glad to have you here. Hives are tough to live with. I have had them as an allergic reaction.
You might try going dairy free as well.

### diane — 2013-03-21T02:40:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.6416/post-81251>

Welcome cloak! Great to hear about your progress, and I hope you can find some help for your hives. That can't be pleasant to live with.

### cloak — 2013-03-21T05:48:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.6416/post-81268>

Thanks, everyone.

Nonchalant, I'm just about everything-free right now it seems. The only grain I eat is rice (the one non-paleo food I'm holding out on) and I'm free of all the AI foods except nightshades and I do eat eggs here and there. I'm off of a whole list of other stuff from my IgG testing. I also can't eat tomatoes, cinnamon, and parsnips because they flare up my psoriasis. Weeding flares it up to and I'm not sure what that is all about. Spring is just bad in general because I'm highly allergic to tree pollens so everything goes out of whack when those are out.

### SeaHorse — 2013-03-21T14:44:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.6416/post-81288>

hi cloak....I've been dealing with histamine and hives off and on throughout my life....I feel your itch as I write this!! I had loads of triggers that all make sense to me now. The last bout I had was my reaction to the antidepressant Elavil a few years ago. It was the clearest way my body could give me the message to get the hell off of these drugs. I had full blown full body hives for six weeks....horrific.

Anyway....If you haven't read the histamine thread, you might find some helpful stuff there and I would encourage you to simplify your diet even more. I've done really well on the seafoods I can tolerate (basically it has to alive (oysters) or eaten right away after cooking (clams, mussels lobster etc). I can't eat canned foods at all or fish that is over a day old.... I eat lots of organ meats, fats, muscle meat and almost no veggies. This has served me very well for the last year. I will be adding some more veggies this summer...but I'm planning on sticking with this for a long time. I need to repair a lot of bone and electron losses.

Have you tried taking lots of quercetin? That's been useful for me too. I believe sleeping grounded has made a huge difference in my allergies...I'm am no longer sneezing every single morning of every day. Feels great to just get up and breath normally.

Soo...lots to process, but I would gently suggest that you might benefit from just giving your body a real rest and lose the rice, the nightshades, eggs and eat really simply and drink amazing amounts of good water, stay away from EMFs and try to ground as much as possible.

Another avenue to read up on is how histamine fits into the methylation cycle...a couple of good threads on that too. Sorry if this is overload...I'm glad you're here and hope you find what you need. All the best, SeaHorse

### nonchalant — 2013-03-21T14:46:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.6416/post-81290>

Cloak, you need some energy.
Sounds like going out into the sun would just aggravate your allergies at this point, though. Yeah, tree pollens are the worst for me, even worse than grass & weed pollens.
I think you should seriously consider grounding. It got rid of the rash I had on the back of my hand. I had it for a year. I think it was psoriasis or eczema. When it was at its worst, it would be swollen, red, and get a silvery dandruff-like covering. I learned to control it by dunking the hand in water with epsom salts, sea salt, and iodine, and then after air drying, putting olive oil on it.

But what finally got rid of the rash was when I started serious grounding at night. Took about a month.

### Shijin13 — 2013-03-21T14:48:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.6416/post-81291>

Welcome!

I'm suffering with you as we move into spring... my allergies are killing me right now...trying to figure out how to tame them... Seahorse is right its a good idea to check out the histmine threads and the methylation threads as well...

### cloak — 2013-03-21T17:54:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.6416/post-81320>

Seahorse, how much quercitin do you take? I took some awhile back but after a couple months of taking a billion things without any results I get frustrated and quit. Trying to find a doable regimen that works seems to always be the biggest challenge. I've read some of the histamine stuff and it makes me cry. LOL No leftovers? No avocado? Do you hear me crying already? LOL I might have to resort to that though. Hoping if I can do the iodine protocol I can avoid more dietary changes. We will see...

I haven't read that much about methylation and I have no idea what grounding is so I will look that up. Thank you for all of the suggestions.

What do you all think about allergy shots? I got them from age 5-23 then I moved to a new state after college and didn't have any allergies there so I never got a new allergist. Now I'm back where my tree pollens are awful but so far I've just been taking Claritin plus Vit. C for 2/3 months in the spring and I'm okay after that.

### SeaHorse — 2013-03-21T18:28:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.6416/post-81328>

Cloak, I take 500 mg 3 times a day...sometimes more. It's really all a big experiment and that is what has worked for me....but I've also changed a million other things. I'm just going on my instinct that the quercetin has been helpful. Good luck with all the reading!

### SeaHorse — 2013-03-24T21:37:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.6416/post-81642>

Here is an interesting link between dehydration and histamine that I hadn't really put together.... fits right in with the current blogs

[http://www.naturalnews.com/032624\_allergies\_hydration.html](http://www.naturalnews.com/032624_allergies_hydration.html)

### BJK77 — 2013-03-24T21:46:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.6416/post-81644>

> [SeaHorse said:](/goto/post?id=81642)
>
> Here is an interesting link between dehydration and histamine that I hadn't really put together.... fits right in with the current blogs
>
> [http://www.naturalnews.com/032624\_allergies\_hydration.html](http://www.naturalnews.com/032624_allergies_hydration.html)

That is really interesting SeaHorse. I suffered from histamine overload for months and couldn't figure out the cause or any helpful treatment. I had tried Quercetin, but I never really felt like it did much. I've never suffered with seasonal allergies, but my histamine issues seemed to primarily stem from eating high histamine foods and I would get the hot/cold feeling, flushing face and severe insomnia.

This has progressively improved over the last few weeks to months. I remember in one of my very first consults Jack suggested getting my DHEA in range would help with this. I've been supplementing DHEA for awhile now and my labs for the most part have still stunk! I did just have them repeated last week though, so I'm anxiously awaiting the results to see if anything has changed.

I'd like to say it's my increased water intake that has been helpful, but I've only been doing that for about 2 weeks now and I noticed the improvement before that.

### Inger — 2013-03-25T08:16:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.6416/post-81680>

Interesting article. It says the body cannot absorb too much water at once so it is better to drink a couple of glasses frequently throughout the day instead of huge amounts at once.
That is how I have been doing too, it feels just right...

### cloak — 2013-03-29T16:10:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.6416/post-82164>

The water/histamine link is interesting. I'm actually drinking more water than I ever have because I had to cut out so many other liquids. Can't say I've noticed a difference but I also have no clue how much I'm actually drinking. Would be interesting to measure and see as the article says.

### cantweight — 2013-04-11T17:36:32+0300

### lisa — 2013-04-01T01:36:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-everyone.6563/post-82393>

I've never posted on discussions like this before. I'm excited to learn. I'm about 6 weeks into the leptin reset but feel as though something isn't working for me. I keep reading everything 2-4 times to "get it". I have a doctorate in nursing and I am having a hard time understanding everything Jack says, but I believe it. I have 3 generations of diabetes in my family and a LONG personal history of yoyo dieting. I haven't done all the labs that Jack recommends but I have it on the list of things to do soon. I know this lifestyle is the one for me, I just need to get it right. I'm looking forward to getting support here.

40 yo female
5'6"/157 pounds (goal is 135)

> 5'6"/157 pounds (goal is 135)

Hi Lisa!
You are in the right place, you'll get a lot of support here. It took me 1 year to lose 10 lbs and then 6 months to lose 20 more lbs. Also my FBG took time to control. It takes years to get broken, so it doesn't take just weeks to fix it. Just be prepared to have a lot of patience. We are here for you when you get frustrated!

### Vicster — 2013-04-02T14:39:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-everyone.6563/post-82590>

Hi Lisa, it's taken me a long time to "get it", as you say, and there are still things that I don't "get"!  I've reread blogs countless times and each time clarity comes. I've also found as much information and insight in the forum from members as I have in the blogs. Read the forum topics that apply to you and ask questions when you need to, the members here are extremely smart, helpful, understanding and supportive. Good luck to you on your journey!

### BJK77 — 2013-04-02T15:13:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-everyone.6563/post-82594>

Welcome Lisa! I've been reading Jack's blogs for over a year now and still don't "get" it all. Eventually though as you continue to read and re-read you'll find yourself putting pieces together and gaining more and more clarity. It's a lot of work, but so very worth it

# Thread: Hey friends!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-friends.6653/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MrPinkies — 2013-04-06T00:02:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-friends.6653/post-83023>

Hi! My name is Kate.. I'm new to the forum stuff, but, I'm getting the hang of it- I think?!??

Anyway... I'm a mom of two kids living with Autism. I married a man with Autism. I probably have Autism, but, after one turns 30 years old- whatever..

A year ago January, it was own personal mission to lose 150-160 lbs. Starting weight was 309 pounds. By June, I was beyond frustrated, beyond hungry, and injured from running (fractured my tibia at the knee). I had a personal trainer, who I love, but, obviously, things didn't work out. It was mid-summer that it was suggested to us by the ABA therapist and an OT to change my son's diet for sensory purposes. Well, ok, back up.. I have a feeling no one is going to understand "sensory purposes". See, when we found this website, EVERYTHING began to make sense to us. My son's nervous system is very very very under-developed. So.. before finding this website, the OT said to me, ok, so, you've had wine before, right? and I said yes, of course.. Ok.. so.. what happens when you have one too many glasses of wine? I get drunk! Right, your behavior changes! I said yes, but in confusion.. She explained that over half of our nervous system in is our gut and certain foods- like wheat and dairy can make nerve endings go "crazy". So... after finding this website, we got excited.. VERY excited... The excitement actually died the first day of the leptin reset. Actually, the first month, I felt like a crack addict with no crack. However, it started to sink into my brain of the addiction to sugar. The sugar I knew about and wasn't being honest, but also, the sugar that I didn't know that would be harmful, etc. This website gave a clear understanding of WHY.. I am incredibly grateful. Seven months later, I am FINALLY under 200lbs, but, I still have a good 40-50lbs to go. I am pretty sure that I no longer have PCOS, my cycles are on time and manageable. On the subject of my husband and kids, there was a very noticeable difference when we changed our lights to amber, orange, and red... My kids SLEEP!!! AMEN! lol We also sold our TV and iPad. We got rid of our Android phones and got cheap pay as you go phones that we keep off and just for emergencies only. We turned off our WIFI and hard wired our computers. My kids are more calm.. They still have their moments, but, over all, more calm. Less meltdowns. Less screaming.. I'm sure there is more to be done!! One day at a time...

I would love to hear thoughts on what you guys pack in your kids lunches???

### BJK77 — 2013-04-06T00:56:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-friends.6653/post-83027>

Welcome Kate! Sounds like you have made some great progress and your entire family is benefitting from the changes you made. I love hearing about the positive effects reduced EMF can have on kids!! We saw a great improvement in my kiddos too

### MrPinkies — 2013-04-06T01:27:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-friends.6653/post-83034>

The lights and the tv were the big ones with the most noticeable difference.. and I mean, night and day kind of difference. We were SHOCKED. My son would get up at 1 and 4am to jump, literally. My daughter would get up at 3am to move around. They wouldn't disturb each other or anything. It has been before they were born since I have slept through the night.. they FINALLY are BOTH sleeping through the night! that makes me one HAPPY Mama to sleep through the night too!!! lol

### caroline — 2013-04-06T01:42:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-friends.6653/post-83037>

Hi Kate .... I can't even begin to tell you how amazing I think you are! You dived into all of this and look what rewards you have reaped! You are an awesome woman and more importantly an awesome wife and mother. You are one smart lady!
And ..... You are sleeping .... Just simply ...WOW....

Please keep coming here and posting and asking questions. - there are a lot of really smart people here who can help if needed. We love, love, love to hear from people who take all of this on board and don't look back.

All of this must have been pretty daunting given all your difficult circumstances - but you didnt say "I can't" you just did!
,

### caroline — 2013-04-06T01:49:35+0300

I really am in awe of how you took all of this and ran with it! I can't even imagine how many other people you are going to help. You are a Quntum Mom - no question.....

### MrPinkies — 2013-04-06T03:15:47+0300

Hi Caroline!

Thank you!

My body literally shut down.  On the other hand, there are days where I crave carbs.. I still battle this addiction to sugar.. but it seems to get slightly easier every day..

### caroline — 2013-04-06T03:27:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-friends.6653/post-83064>

Kate - I don't know if you eat fruit ....but that was huge for me when I gave it up ...... in the long run it is just sugar. It took a few weeks but I don't crave fruit at all now - in fact - I bought an apple the other day and didn't enjoy it at all. That was huge for me - I have loved apples all of my life!

Have you thought of starting a journal here? we would love to follow your progress.....it is amazing when you look back months later and see how far you have come ...... and all your trials and tribulations - successes and failures ...all part of this amazing journey to wellness.

When I started a journal - I was just going to keep track of food and C/T before my next set of labs ... but it has turned into so much more....

### MrPinkies — 2013-04-06T03:51:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-friends.6653/post-83072>

Caroline-

I'll think about it! Where can I go to start one if I decide to do so?

### caroline — 2013-04-06T04:03:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-friends.6653/post-83074>

Kate - just go to the forum page ...it is the third category down ....90 day reset and then - my journal. It is really easy to find and do - just start a new thread!

Just start by posting a few sentences and then soon enough - you will want to go there to post your thoughts - It helps Dr. K. to see how people are doing as well ...he says he likes journals. It really helps to focus our thoughts even when busy. Some people have been keeping a journal for a year and it is amazing to see their progress and everyone is very supportive....

### caroline — 2013-04-06T04:06:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-friends.6653/post-83075>

BTW - I love your inspirational quote - amazing words to live by.

### MrPinkies — 2013-04-06T15:24:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-friends.6653/post-83113>

HA! I found it! Thanks!

Yes, this is one of my FAVORITE quotes... A very special friend of mine told me this quote about a year after my first kids diagnoses. At that time, life was very confusing because at the time of diagnoses, they literally said "your son has autism, good luck" and then showed me the door.. That's the thing about Autism, is, there is no book.. there is no one therapy or one answer. It's called a spectrum for a reason.. All of these kids, that will eventually become adults, are different. Both of my kids are very different from each other and have different struggles. I think I finally (starting my fifth year on this journey with Autism) got my act together. On the tough days, where my kids nervous system completely goes haywire, this is the only quote that gets me through my day because being strong IS the only option I have.. Over time, I have learned to love being strong.

It's interesting that when I explain to other people why I eat the way that I do and do the things I do- like the amber lights, etc, and not owning a tv... the typical response is "I can't do that- it's too hard.. and I wouldn't be able to keep this lifestyle"... but.. the more I think about it.. these things make life EASIER... not harder... We are so programmed to believe things.. and until we "TRY", how will one ever know that it's too hard.. or one would be unable to keep this lifestyle up??!?? I'll tell you what.. my kids put themselves in bed at 8pm.. they are WILLING to go to bed because they get tired at the right time...

AMEN! hahahahahaa...

### caroline — 2013-04-07T01:59:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-friends.6653/post-83154>

Kate - I just love how strong you are and how motivated. Why do people always put things in the too hard box? Surely your life isn't going to end if you give up wheat and sugar and TV.......people seem to be so unmotivated to even try to help themselves but as Jack says - its the brain. They just can't make descisions to save/help themselves

### caroline — 2013-04-07T02:01:06+0300

I really hope that you start a journal Kate .....you have so much intelligence and insight to offer. .....you never know who might see your story and be motivated....
