# Thread: Hi from sunny South Africa
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-sunny-south-africa.6771/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ellen_ZA — 2013-04-14T12:46:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-sunny-south-africa.6771/post-84049>

Hi there

I posted a message under Hormones 101 but Dr Kruse said I must take this to the forum, so here I am

I'm a 38-year old woman and I've been experiencing hair loss slowly since I was 18. The hair loss is in the frontal region of my scalp. It doesn't seem to fall out at a faster rate, but rather, doesn't grow back or miniaturises. I've tried numerous doctors over the years, with no success. I went to a very helpful GP last year as I had a number of symptoms (super-frizzy hair, slight loss of power in my right hand, memory loss, tenderness in my liver area) that were worrying me. He suspected low thyroid and I've been put on Synthroid (can't get Armour or the like in the country I live in). I've never been entirely sure if the hair loss was hormonal or caused by obscene stress that I was experiencing (and which didn't let up well into my twenties), however, I'm now very overweight (heaviest was 126 kg and I'm currently at 96kg / 212 pounds) so if it was stress, I believe the hair loss is now caused by my blood sugar mess.

I had some blood tests done last year before my GP put me on thyroid meds:

\- Ultrasensative-CRP: 1.31,
\- DHEAS: 3.47 (reference is 1.65 - 9.15)
\- Vitamin D: 17.6
\- Insulin Fasting: 7.5 (reference is 2.1 - 10.4)
\- Glucose fasting: 5.1 (reference is 3.5 - 5.5)
\- 17OH Progesterone: 12.2
\- Free T4: 10.1 (reference is 7.2 - 16.4)
\- Free T3: 3.8 (reference is 3.8 - 6.0)
\- S-TSH: 2.01 (reference is 0.37 - 3.50)
\- Testosterone - Total: 0.58 (reference is 0.22 - 2.9)
\- Sex Hormone Bind. Globulin: 43.5 (reference is 26.1 - 110)
\- Free Androgen Index: 1.3 (0.4 - 5.9)

And then others that were done at the same time have been highlighted because they are high:

\- Cholesterol-S - 5.4 (reference is 5.0)
\- LDL Cholesterol - 3.3 (reference is 3.0)
\- Bilirubin Total-S - 28 (rererence is 2 - 20)
\- Bili Unconj.-S - 25 (reference is 2 - 14)
\- ALT (SGPT) - 35 (reference is 10 - 32)

I was diagnosed with the start of non-alcoholic Fatty Liver disease and Hypothyroidism. My GP's response was to put me on Euthyrox (Levothyroxine) at 25 ug, follow the diet recommended by Dr Rosedale and he also suggested some EFT sessions - which I did with him, he's very holistic. I also had to take loads of supplements and a shake drink called Estrium that is manufactured by Metagenics (although at half-dosage).

I have noticed an improvement in symptoms, however my memory loss, while not severe is still not where it was when I was younger (I used to have excellent recall) and I do miss it. I followed Dr Rosedale's diet and experienced a lot of weight loss (went down to 88 kg) but then depression hit badly and my wheels fell off. However, I have been making an effort diet-wise from this year. I've been eating BABs since last year though as I've definitely noticed that it sustains me through the day and I eat as soon as I start to get hungry otherwise my mood drops and I become revolting to anybody around me. I also have slow digestion. I've had no problem cutting out bread, potatoes and pasta, but I've struggled with the cake. I have an incredible sweet tooth.

Just some other things about me:

\- acne started at age 11 and it's never gone away
\- very greasy hair and skin - I cannot go a day without washing my hair otherwise I look terrible (I've tried to reduce my hair washes over extended periods of time, but then I just look terrible for longer)
\- zero ability to handle any stress, so much so, I've had to move out the city and live in the country where the pace is slower
\- ongoing depression (I'm currently on Welbutrin, it manages the symptoms without rendering my libido non-existent)
\- no feeling on bottom portion of my right foot, which I suspect is blood-sugar related
\- my periods are regular, however, when I have been on the pill (I'm no longer on anything), my period would just get less and less each month until eventually it would stop (I've been on Diane and Yaz)
\- I've always looked at least 5 years younger than my age, but in the last 2 years I've noticed rapid aging

I read Dr Kruse's Adrenal Fatigue article after I had posted in Hormones 101, and apart from sleeping well and very rarely getting colds and flu, I can tick everything else he lists there.

I've been a "night owl" since I was a teenager and I regularly have inconsistent sleep lengths. I started getting serious fluid retention (my ankles blow up) from my late twenties if I didn't get enough sleep (5 hours or less), so I do make an effort to have 8 hours - although for some reason I can go through stages when this is difficult to achieve. Until late last year, I was getting in bed by midnight / 1 AM but I've been trying to swing my cycle earlier - it's difficult though because I can have one late night and my sleeping cycle swings back again.

I'm never hungry in the morning - the thought of eating within 30 minutes of waking is just unfathomable. I can't even get out of bed within 30 minutes of waking - it takes me at least an hour to get motivated to get out of bed, so if I wake up at 8, I will only be interested in breakfast by 10:30 / 11. Caffeine-wise, I drink several cups of black tea a day (and have been doing this since I was young) and I go out for one coffee usually every day. I can't tolerate most fruits - apples, pears, peaches, grapes, plums, etc - all make me feel queasy and have done so since I was a child. I also couldn't eat honey for the same reason but this has improved in the last few years. I cannot eat or drink anything with a liquorice smell or taste, so liquorice, aniseed and molasses is out of the question - instant nausea. I also cannot drink rooibos tea as it makes me feel queasy - again, the smell is the reason. Oh, and I'm always thirsty. I drink lots of water (I refill my Brita jug several times a day) but the thirst never really goes away.

Health-wise, I was extremely sick as a baby (born a month premature) and had pneumonia three times before my second birthday. I had my tonsils removed at the earliest age possible and I've been fine ever since. I have also had grommets and my adenoids were removed. I had a burst appendix when I was 25 and the infection didn't go away, so I had to have a laparotomy to clean out everything.

My mother died of breast cancer when she was 41 (I was 21 and she had been diagnosed 4 years previously) and I strongly believe that the cancer was a psychological response to the lifestyle choices my mother and step-father made (which was the cause of the stress that my family was under). My gran got breast cancer in her 70's but she didn't die of it. My step-father died of cancer in my early 30's and the family stress that I've referred to stopped with his death.

I'm not sure if all this is relevant but if stress is a contributor, then I can say with certainty that my stress has been huge from a young age but intensified from the age of 15. My hair loss has overshadowed my entire adult life and I would really love to just move on from the psychological issues that it causes.

Any feedback would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you.

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-14T16:28:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-sunny-south-africa.6771/post-84061>

Hi and welcome.

I'm getting ready to run the kids to swimming - and I'll try to comment later - I have some ideas.... hang in there and start reading .

first place to star is the Leptin reset, using an epi-paleo template...

I have Asperger's syndrome, so socialization is not my strong point, but now that my brain is working better, I expect that will improve as well. # Thread: Howdy Folks
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-folks.6902/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bionaut — 2013-04-28T15:15:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-folks.6902/post-84989>

Hi all,
Long time lurker here. Firstly, I want to thank Dr Kruse and all of the wonderful people in this community. This forum has provided so much insight into what it truly means to be healthy. I have always been deeply interested in biology and biohacking. About 4 years ago I started my journey into weightloss, I pretty much tried the fad diets & workouts you all are familiar with to no avail. I also got a job in place that would make you all run for the hills. I am surrounded by literally thousands of computers and high power equipment for a few hours each day (now I know why I get so thirsty). About 7 months ago I made a commitment to myself to get to the bottom of what was going on with my body. I pulled out my old bio books and got cracking.

I found Dave Aspreys site and started following a bulletproof diet. Within two weeks I had felt my brain fog lift and felt so much better. However, it wasn't enough. I was still 60lbs over weight and still had big time energy issues. Jack's CT blog from Dave's site got me really interested. I dived into Ray Cronise and Jack's work. I thought this was nuts "what kind of crackpot stuff was I reading?"! My preconceived notions of how the body operated were challenged but I tried it with an open mind. In the end I found I was the one who was nuts trying to lose weight fast and not take care of myself.

I began the CT protocol and spent the whole winter without a jacket. This coupled with the leptin reset I got some real results! I have lost 36 lbs without working out. I just recently shifted to a night schedule for the next 1-2 years. I was a little worried at first but with RO water, Epi paleo, ct, a magnetico mat, supplementation, blu blockers, earthing, and sitting in the sun I have tools to cope with the situation. My stress is at an all time low my energy is at the highest point it has ever been and I am happy.

Thanks Everyone

### nonchalant — 2013-04-28T17:22:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-folks.6902/post-84999>

Welcome, Bionaut! You might consider a grounding mat for your mouse at work.

### Dali Dula — 2013-04-29T05:30:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-folks.6902/post-85081>

Nice. How many other lurkers out there? Speak up lurkers. You may be able to help someone with your experience and observations.

### caroline — 2013-04-29T08:12:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-folks.6902/post-85094>

Wonderful results - thanks so much for applying yourself and then telling us your story.... please keep coming back - as Dali said - it helps everyone. You never know when someone is lurking and then reads your story and is inspired to take back their life like you have.....

Is it better to have protein Veg or fruit?? If I do cave...

### caroline — 2013-05-03T09:08:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-new-here.6947/post-85369>

Welcome Alaskaladyie! I would definitely say you are not eating enough for breakfast. You need at least 50gr of Protein.
Some of us needed a lot more than that - I probably had around 80- 90gr some days.
Share with us what you are eating ..... if you are eating enough protein you shouldn't be all that hungry and definitely no snacking. Do you eat at least 4 hours before bedtime?
I personally wouldn't have yogurt and I wouldn't have fruit either - too much sugar - that might be what is kicking your butt right now.
Just up the protein and fat in the morning - 30 minutes after rising. Adding in more fat helps. Have you tried a bullet proof coffee yet?
Please keep posting and let us know how you are going - it helps everyone.......

Maybe start a journal and post what you are eating every meal and we can help you figure this out!
A journal is great to keep you on track and it is fun to look back and see how far you have come.

Maybe - also - re read the leptin R/X just to make sure you are doing it as written .... it is so easy to miss things the first time around! 2 years later I am re reading and going Wow - I didn't see that before - but it is because we have new eyes and understand more and the pieces start to fit together....

### AlaskaLadyie — 2013-05-03T09:19:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-new-here.6947/post-85370>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=85369)
>
> Welcome Alaskaladyie! I would definitely say you are not eating enough for breakfast. You need at least 50gr of Protein.
> Some of us needed a lot more than that - I probably had around 80- 90gr some days.
> Share with us what you are eating ..... if you are eating enough protein you shouldn't be all that hungry and definitely no snacking. Do you eat at least 4 hours before bedtime?
> I personally wouldn't have yogurt and I wouldn't have fruit either - too much sugar - that might be what is kicking your butt right now.
> Just up the protein and fat in the morning - 30 minutes after rising. Adding in more fat helps. Have you tried a bullet proof coffee yet?
> Please keep posting and let us know how you are going - it helps everyone.......
>
> Maybe start a journal and post what you are eating every meal and we can help you figure this out!
> A journal is great to keep you on track and it is fun to look back and see how far you have come.
>
> Maybe - also - re read the leptin R/X just to make sure you are doing it as written .... it is so easy to miss things the first time around! 2 years later I am re reading and going Wow - I didn't see that before - but it is because we have new eyes and understand more and the pieces start to fit together....

**Thank you Caroline for reply.......... I just started this on Monday and I have been having 4 eggs fried in about a T of Butter I also have a scoop of AMP protein powder with unsweetened almond milk and a scoop of Strawberry Shakeology and a hanfull of frozen strawberriesand ice to creat a smoothy shake for breakfast...... I eat about 6oz of protein and a european cucumber for lunch and some celery................ For supper usually some type of meat and a salad with salsa on it or steamed vegtables...............is this Leptin R/X u spoke of the Leptin Reset book or is there a post here on the Forum titled that??????? Thank you**

### caroline — 2013-05-03T09:40:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-new-here.6947/post-85371>

Personally - I would skip the shake stuff - JK is not a fan. Add in some bacon with the eggs. If I remember right - 4 X-large eggs is only about 25 gr protein so you will need at least 4 strips of bacon and then add in some fat. More fat was huge for me. I have 2 BPC in the morning. Skip the fruit. There are lots of paleo books around telling you what to eat - I think Dr. K. recommends Rob Wolfe and some others - Paleo is pretty standard.

Optimum would be epi-paleo which is lots and lots of seafood with oysters at the top of your list.

If you go to the home page there are lots of blogs to read about this and everything else. I just had a look and things seemed to have changed there ..... I am going to go and check it out better and get back to you.

Are you having coconut oil? You may be a little heavy on the carbs from what you said above especially at dinner time.

### caroline — 2013-05-03T10:01:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-new-here.6947/post-85373>

okay ..... go to the home page....at the top ...2nd tab from the left is the getting started guide. Click on it and Just scroll down and choose your topic. If you have more questions please don't hesitate to ask - we have all been there!

### Linz — 2013-05-03T10:15:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-new-here.6947/post-85375>

Welcome Alaskaladyie,

You need to up the fat and include lots of fish and really stick to the meal timing and light use advice and you should find you forget about needing snacks. When I first changed my diet and had the fat a bit wrong I kept a big pot of thick meat/veg soup which was really effective for stopping hunger cravings between meals.

Include fatty bacon and fish/prawns etc cooked in bacon fat, butter or duck fat at breakfast time instead of the strawberries. And try bullet proof coffee as Caroline suggests. For lunch make sure the protein is real food with its own fat included. For supper alternate the meat with fish/shellfish and add bacon fat/ chopped bacon/ warmed olive oil and garlic/coconut oil with chilli and turmeric or butter with parsley/cilantro/basil to your vegetables after steaming. Melt some very dark chocolate with coconut oil, d-ribose or honey and a few nuts and keep in the fridge for something sweet at the end of a meal, perhaps with some raspberries, blueberries, currants, gooseberries or rhubarb.

There is a leptin RX post here with more detail (and great information in the comments) and a post leptin rx with advice on how to adapt the initial rx once you know you have regained leptin sensitivity and a beginners section to point you in the right direction.

Edit - Caroline just gave you most of the same info in far less words.

### Shijin13 — 2013-05-03T15:08:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-new-here.6947/post-85380>

welcome you've gotten great advice. I definitely agree you're not eating enough protein or FAT. you might even consider eating a tablespoon of coconut oil, butter, ghee, bacon or duck fat with each meal.

### AlaskaLadyie — 2013-05-04T02:25:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-new-here.6947/post-85405>

I'll add in the coconut oil not a big fan of bacon as the nitrates in cured meat do not get along well with my stomach and digestive system.. I'll get some shrimp and crab tonight when i get groceries..
I purchased JK's Book the Epi Paleo R/X on my nook today so I can read up on what is allowed and not allowed
I have eaten primal for the last year and 1/2 along with having lost 60# doing hCG but here in the last few months the # have been trying to creep back on ......so I am looking for a solution...
Was sorta late last night when I found this Forum and I was hoping some one could head me to the link of what foods to eat when since I was probably too tired to weed through all those posts......
Thank you all very much for the information and advice..

### Inger — 2013-05-04T08:05:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-new-here.6947/post-85429>

Welcome, Alaskan Lady
The book is great! I have read it many times already. **I would recommend it for ANY newbie here!** So much easier than starting to read the whole blog, as there is so much. The book is very easy to get. Read everything, also the different chapters about Diabetes, Heart disease.. etc., even if you do not have any of these. Lots of gold in there so useful for everyone!

And C/T or CT means cold thermogenesis, = cold baths in the ocean / river / bathtub etc... or other cold treatments. Amazing for healing.

# Thread: Hello from South Africa
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-south-africa.6978/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bugsy1950@gmail.com — 2013-05-05T17:13:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-south-africa.6978/post-85477>

I have visited this site many times in the last year, and realise that I have done things backwards - posted a few questions but never introduced myself.
I stumbled upon this site as I was trying to find out why losing weight had become an impossibility for me.
The more I frequent this site, the more I am aware of the enormity of my ignorance regarding how my body works, but it has captivated my attention and I have learned so much and there is so much more to learn. I am very grateful to Dr K for having dedicated so much of his precious time to educate people interested in living optimally. I am tired of just surviving I want more out of life, but unfortunately I am not young any more and there is so much to learn to turn things around, keeping in mind that when you are "older" the body does not respond as quickly any more, but I believe it is never too late.
I have been advised to start a journal.....not good at that, not much self discipline, plus I hope not to make a fool of myself...
One thing though I have managed to discipline myself to eat as close to paleo as possible. I say as close to because organic meat is impossible to find - the closest is "free range" and once in a blue moon I find some organic onions, carrots or sweet potatoes, so I asked my husband to plant some spinach, red bell peppers and egg plants, often they are attacked by some disease and then.....I have to buy the ones at the supermarket.
I am 62 YO female living in a very beautiful part of the world - the tip of Africa - Cape Town.
So here I am - on board

### Optimalbound — 2013-05-05T22:37:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-south-africa.6978/post-85482>

Welcome, Bugsy!
I've been to Cape Town several times and it is such a wonderful place. I imagined that most of your meat would be grass-fed, but to be honest I never investigated it because you have so much lovely seafood. I saw some really tempting abalones (sea snails, for the rest of you) while scuba diving but unfortunately their numbers are critically low and as you well know, they are illegal to remove from the sea.

### caroline — 2013-05-06T01:11:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-south-africa.6978/post-85486>

Welcome Bugsy! - I am so happy to see you here .....very well written. It is never too late to turns things around if you really want to .... I am proof of this and many others here as well! As I have said to you - there is tons of reading to get thru - but there is no other way if you want to get a handle on all that Dr. Kruse is showing us .....remember - he is the guy with the flashlight!
When you read the blogs - please make sure that you read the comments as well - they are often as important if not more so......then come back here and ask questions. This is tough stuff and Dr. K. likes to hurt our head! I promise - no one thinks anyone else is stupid for their questions and then you can have an "aha moment" when something finally sinks in!

Have you checked out the ...Leaky gut R/X ? and if you read some of the journals - you will see what others are dealing with..... some very tough gut problems.....

Nothing in Life worth doing is easy ... and challenges keep us sharp and motivated and in the game and hopefully keep our neurons firing for a long, long time. Have you read Dr. K's personal story?

Have a really great day - get out and enjoy the sun and sky and nature and see the beauty that is all around you - maybe you could post some pictures? I have always wanted to go to South africa....

### Inger — 2013-05-06T08:12:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-south-africa.6978/post-85503>

Welcome!  I sometimes think free range meats are way better than organic, you might get game too? Like antelopes etc? I always choose wild instead of organic.. organic here at least means heavily grain fed..

I have probably read 70% of the material, as to understanding it..well....60% of it LOL ! I do struggle so with the acronyms....

# Thread: Greetings....help!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-help.7201/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LRichard12 — 2013-05-30T18:44:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-help.7201/post-87144>

Hi my name is Lucas. I am 22 years old, 6foot tall, 150 lbs, around 12-14%
body fat. I have recently (over the past year) lost 100 lbs (from 250 to
150). I was obese my entire life, suffered from high BP, chronic fatigue,
laziness, low motivation, brain fog, poor memory, depression, etc. I then
developed heart palpitations and the chronic fatigue and neuro problems
worsened. I tried everything in the book, had every blood test, have taken
every anti depressant and stimulant the neurologist and GP could throw at
me. I've taken every supplement under the sun (and spent a small fortune
doing so), and had even turned to drugs to help (found hydrocodone and
tramadol worked the best). I've learned to manage and press on, by the
grace of GOD, day by day drinking coffee, yerba mate, green tea, taking
endless combinations of supplements, and eating what I thought was a
balanced diet. Then after coming back from Africa (I am a missionary from
the USA, based in Dublin, serving in West Africa) last year weighing in
over 250lbs (probably closer to 300) and decided that it was time to lose
weight. So I switched to a pure veggie, fruit, and low fat cottage cheese
diet, cut out all added sugars and deserts, ate mueslie cereal, skim mile,
flax seed, goji berries, bannanas, and apple juice for breakfast, etc. I
started jogging and hiking at least 3 mornings a week and eventually joined
a gym. I lost a few lbs and felt a bit better though still fighting with
chronic fatigue, depression, low motivation, waking up tired, and heavily
relient on coffee. Also was still experimenting with antidepressants.

Then I decided to go super low calorie, eating mostly protein shakes, low
fat cottage cheese, and fat free unsweetened organic yogurt (used
maltodextrin laced stevia to sweeten). I dropped a few more lbs, but was
hungry all of the time! Then I was introduced to Atkins (thank GOD). I
switched to Atkins and lost the rest of my wieght fairly quickly, going
down below 150, while building muscle. I still stayed very low calorie
(1200/day) for a while and ate only 3 meals a day, no snacks. However,
every one kept telling me that I needed to up my calorie intake. So I uped
them by adding in 2 snacks of nuts (either almonds or walnuts) and more
olive, walnut, or coconut oil. Up until that point I had full appetite
control, and had no trouble eating less than 1000 calories per day if I
wanted to. Then once I added the snacks and uped my calories I began to get
hungry. However I maintained control.

Then I went to Africa in February. While in Africa my appetite continued to
increase, eventually becoming ravenous. I lost control for a couple of
weeks and gorged on peanuts, gaining a couple of lbs. Then regained control
forcing myself to stay around 1800 calories and <25 net carbs perday. Now I
am back in Dublin and I'm over come by my hunger, my obsession with food,
it is all I can think about, and I cant seem to stay full. I am eating 3
solid meals a day, under 20 net carbs, 100 grams protein, 150 grams of fat
(mostly from olive oil and avacoados), and all of the low carb veggies that
I can ingest. I still seem to be hungry.

Recently I discovered Mark Sission's Primal diet and the paleo diet, read
Mastering Leptin, and the perfect 10 diet. I then came across Jack Kruses
Leptin RX. After reading into it I think that my problem is low leptin. So
I tried a primal refeed (sweet potatoes, berries, low fat organic
unsweetened yogurt, honey, etc) last week, with around 500 grams carb, 50g
fat, 130 or so protein, and nearly 3000 cals, which kind of helped. Then I
started the Leptin RX , eating 50 grams of protein at breakfast (eggs, hard
cheese, almonds, and coconut oil) after which I am still hungry. So I
recently tried another refeed (roast beef (for morning protein) sweet
potatoes, white potatoes, parsnips, low fat organic unsweetened yogurt
(with stevia and FOS to sweeten)). I was really hoping that this would
work! Now I am back to low carb/Leptin Rx and still hungry. I am trying to
maintain nutritional ketosis (for the thereputic and weight loss benifits),
however I find that first thing in the morning my fasting blood ketones are
consistently .3 mmol (they where around 2.0 to 3.0 fasting before leaving
for Africa in Feb) even if I keep protein down to 100grams perday, then creep up to nearly 2.0 by the afternoon. So I'm
trying not to over do the protein (I keep to low 100's; 120 or so from
eggs, hard natural cheese, nuts, and 2 meals of wild salmon a day), eat
high fat (150 or so), and 20 net carbs (from veggies, organic unsweetened
greek yogurt, and nuts). I have been avoiding snacks and processed foods
for over 1 month now, I drink only filtered water, go for everything
organic, and take evey supplement recommended by the lowcarb/paleo books
I've read (I.E. GPC, PS, NAC, Acyl-L-Carnitine, L-Carnosine, vit d3, vit E,
Magnesium, Potasium, Chromium, R-ALA, Calcium, Zinc, Omega 3, 6, 7, 9,
extra DHA and EPA, vit C, green tea w/hoodia, cinnamon, kelp (also eat
Kombu), Licorice root, 5 grams+ natural sea salt, Melatonin (2mg 1 hour
before bed w/ magnesium to sleep), broad spectrum probiotics, and 60 grams
of F.O.S and 30grams of Inulin per day, and an iron free multi vit). Still
have little to no energy, irritable some times, weak, hungry!, obsessed
with food, low motivation, poor work ethic, trouble with memory and
concentration, poor social ability, and craving sweet potatos and chocolate
(off and on). It wasn't like this before. What's my problem and what do you
recommend I do to fix it.

BTW I just ordered PQQ and reservatrol, will add it to my L-Carnosine, NAC,
L Carnitine, RALA, GPC, and PS; hoping that that will help.

Please help. Thank you!

### GlenPDQ — 2013-05-30T23:07:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-help.7201/post-87154>

So you've only been on the Leptin RX for 1 week if I am reading you correctly?
FWIW, coconut oil, MCT oil, and the nicotinic acid form of niacin either helps the body get into/stay in ketosis or mimics ketosis.

These may be helpful questions to ask yourself:
When is your last meal of the day? (No later than 4 or 5 pm?)
What time do you go to bed, and do you get 8 hours of sleep? (circadian rhythm)
Do you limit artificial light exposure after sundown?
What is your exposure to EMF, wifi, satellite phones, 3G/4G/5G phones, cell towers? Do you wear a phone close to your chest?
How high do you live off of the ground?
Is your water non-flouridated? (Most filters don't remove flouride.)
Are you exposed to bromides and chlorine?
Were you exposed to pesticides in Africa?
Do you get sufficient Iodine and selenium?
What type of magnesium do you use? (Oxide form is worthless.)

# Thread: Good morning from the end of the world (almost)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-morning-from-the-end-of-the-world-almost.7468/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lahelada — 2013-06-25T16:06:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-morning-from-the-end-of-the-world-almost.7468/post-88891>

Well, good morning from Argentina. I am glad I have found this forum. Even though I have not been able to read a lot here yet, I already know that I am going to like it here.

So how did I end up here. Physically through google. The contemplation on CT though was started by a musing on our boat as to why I can stand cold temps in just a long sleeved T shirt when others get out the cold weather gear. The conversation made me realise that I have always loved cold temperatures. Even when I was a child much to the chagrin of my grandmother who always swaddled me in layers, something I deeply appreciated as it was easy to secretly take off and put back on just before going home. Same grandmother though who made me sleep under a big eiderdown in an unheated room. It only happened once but one morning I had ice roses on the inside of my window.

To the present: I put myself to researching the subject, thinking that I might eke out a slight acceleration to my pitifully slow weight loss. Apart from all the reading I was reminded of some family history, where a family member cured himself from the left side paralysis caused by a stroke. The doctors had told him that it was for life. He cured himself by doing Kneipp treatments with no additional meds,only cold water immersion treatments in mountain river water. Sebastian Kneipp was ac a priest who had recognised the healing powers of cold water back in the 1800s.Lore has it that he cured himself from TB. The English sources relegate this man to quaint history but his work is still recognised today in Germany. There his cures are accepted as a valid treatment. Medical insurances pay for them and you can guess that they are very picky as indeed they are in other parts of the world.

So what have I been doing. In the beginning nothing much. I took the dog to the park for 10 minutes at a time in summer clothes. The looks ....
When we went sailing with temps around 15 C or 60F I only wore 2 long sleeved T shirts with wind cheater at the ready, if I felt the cold which yesterday I did. But all in all I loved being slightly cold. It is invigorating to me and actually puts me in a good mood. I prefer dry cold though. As I am already very cold adapted without even trying I have the hope that my beige/ brown fat still exists and is only dormant as I am living in a majority hot climate with fat layers to insulate me on top of that "mismatch". These cold air baths have been my routine since June 19, so very recent indeed.

Yesterday I went a step further. I came home from shopping with heavy bags and carrying anything produces me a pain in the neck/shoulder region. It has gotten better with the aid of magnesium, but has not gone away. So when I got in I had my usual shower and thought now is as good a time as any and if the pain goes so much the better. I had fully expected to do a dance when the cold water hit me, but no. It was slightly unpleasant, but yes again I loved it after a while. I was still chicken though and only stayed in for three minutes.The neck pain has not gone away, but it has dulled it to an acceptable level. I don't know what temperature it was but the water comes from an outside tank with pipes exposed to the outside so if it was ambient temp I would think around 65. I will buy a thermometer in the near future to test it.

My stats are 49 years old, currently weigh 190 lbs or 86.5 kilos. I started at 224 back in November with non ketogenic Atkins ( my invention ) and changed to the normal Atkins around the middle of March. Since then I only lost 10 lbs .
I am happy with ketogenic. I think I am quite close to paleo, but need to do more reading to know all that is involved.

So here I am.

### nonchalant — 2013-06-25T17:31:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-morning-from-the-end-of-the-world-almost.7468/post-88897>

Hi Lahelada, glad to have you with us! Sounds like you have a head start on CT.
Do you eat much seafood?

### Lahelada — 2013-06-25T18:13:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-morning-from-the-end-of-the-world-almost.7468/post-88899>

Thank you for the kind welcome !

Nonchalant : Seafood err, makes me run a mile in the opposite direction. Fish I can tolerate well disguised in a strong flavoured sauce or before low carb with loads and loads of batter. Oily fish like sardines or salmon no chance. I can even spot the slightly fishy smell in avocadoes and ground linseed . So completely anti fishoil which is not a good thing I believe in terms of what we would like to achieve.

### nonchalant — 2013-06-25T19:16:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-morning-from-the-end-of-the-world-almost.7468/post-88901>

Hmm. Maybe you could try eating more fish (strongly disguised) and after a few weeks you may start changing your mind? I used to eat seafood once or twice a month, but now I love it. It really is important, for the DHA and iodine and selenium, zinc, etc.

### Lahelada — 2013-06-26T00:57:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-morning-from-the-end-of-the-world-almost.7468/post-88923>

Thank you for the ideas non chalant. I really do not have much hope in that regard. I can see myself increasing my fish intake, but seafood makes me ill just from the smell alone I am thinking nuts and seeds like chia could help with the minerals you mention but I am new to this ,so don't know what are the implications of that. I have a lot of reading to do here.

### caroline — 2013-06-26T02:56:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-morning-from-the-end-of-the-world-almost.7468/post-88930>

Welcome .... and cheers from down under!

### Lahelada — 2013-06-26T16:18:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-morning-from-the-end-of-the-world-almost.7468/post-88976>

Thank you for the welcome Caroline. Looks like we are hemisperial neighbours.

### Anna — 2013-07-15T10:20:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-a-newby.7637/post-90127>

HI Folks - sorry, a newby here - finally have found an area that I am able to post LOL........
Can someone please let me know how I am able to post a question in the Ask Jack Forum please? When I go there is says that my account is awaiting activation???
Ta
Cheers
Anna

### caroline — 2013-07-15T10:54:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-a-newby.7637/post-90130>

Hi Anna and welcome! You are only able to "ask Jack" if you are a silver or gold member......are you? Have you just joined? pm me f you are having a problem - happy to help.....

### Anna — 2013-07-15T11:32:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-a-newby.7637/post-90131>

Thanks Caroline, yes a brand new member although been following Jack for sometime now - so no, doubt I'm a silver or gold member - will search the info to find out how I can become eligible for these privelages. Thanks heaps. Did manage however to find my way into the Newby forum section and post a question there  Cheers.

### caroline — 2013-07-16T00:58:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-a-newby.7637/post-90180>

> [Anna said:](/goto/post?id=90131)
>
> Thanks Caroline, yes a brand new member although been following Jack for sometime now - so no, doubt I'm a silver or gold member - will search the info to find out how I can become eligible for these privelages. Thanks heaps. Did manage however to find my way into the Newby forum section and post a question there  Cheers.

Hi Anna ....Silver and gold memberships .....there is a charge for this. Have a look on the home page. Bronze is free but you can't access "Ask Jack" that is only for paid members. It is very well worth it if you can manage it as it includes the webinars. If you are a gold member you also get the Q&A and the whole back catalogue....

### caroline — 2013-07-16T01:06:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-a-newby.7637/post-90182>

Just go to the ....Getting started guide .... on the home page.

Just wanted to properly introduce myself, my name is Beverly. Good morning to everyone.

He challenged me to not only be my own advocate but keep looking because he (and I deep down) knew there was more going on with me. In pressing along for answers I am some what terrified at what I have and am discovering but I am also excited for a chance to make some major changes in my life. Hope for the future. Changes I will be implementing not only for myself but for my children as well.

### jenaf — 2013-07-26T19:03:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-canada.7727/post-90928>

Hi beverly
Where abouts in Canada are you? I'm in Vancouver... I kinda wonder how many people here are from our part of the world. Dr. Kruse has helped me to help myself tremendously over the last two years, although my own journey has been haltered occasionally, had its ups and downs, but the improvement is incredible. And through me, a lot of this vital information has helped my family and some of my patients too.

Jena

Jen aka beach bunny

### nyseides — 2013-07-27T17:35:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-canada.7727/post-91010>

Hi from Thunder Bay, Ontario. Grew up in Ottawa Valley, then lived in Toronto for many years, and then moved to Northwestern ONtario 13 years ago. Talk about culture shock, lol. Took years before I stopped saying "I am from Toronto" and reclaimed my inner hick, bought a house in the boonies with a wood stove, a big collie, a 4x4, quit smoking, took up xc skiing and organic gardening. I moved into town last year, but to a part of town where I'm surrounded on 3 sides by water, (Lake superior, the Kam river). I'm looking for a doc to help who will write T3 only therapy, northern travel grant will pay to go to toronto or winnipeg. Anyone have OHIP doc in toronto they recommend?

I'm looking for a doc to help who will write T3 only therapy, northern travel grant will pay to go to toronto or winnipeg. Anyone have OHIP doc in toronto they recommend?

Hi Nyseides,

Unfortunately, living in Toronto often feels like the Dark Ages. I have struggled for the last few years to find a Dr. who would work with me locally but so far have been paying out of pocket for Naturopaths. If I come across someone I will definitely let you know.
You could try to look into Dr. Alvin Pettle:
[http://http://www.drpettle.com/](http://http://www.drpettle.com/)

I do not have personal experience with him but know that he charges a fortune.

# Thread: Brand New and Loving Life
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-and-loving-life.8229/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### veelansu — 2013-09-23T22:20:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-and-loving-life.8229/post-95835>

Hello everyone. 49 y/o female. I am new to the forum. I am going to start a diary like everyone else to track my progress. I have been on the Leptin RX for about 40 days, and OMG what a difference a few days make. I have had the most wonderful success with this way of eating so far that I feel like I finally found out what has been going on in my body since the year 2000. That was the year that I was told I had to get a hysterectomy and rather than do some research, I followed blindly. I learned a very valuable lesson to not trust blindly any more. But that surgery, loss of my ovaries, caused havoc on all my hormones and general health. So many things started going wrong. I went from 140 to 210 lbs in under 6 months. I was told it was not my thyroid since my TSH was within range. I was always cold, my hair was falling out, so many other horrible symptoms. I started furiously searching online for relief. I found it in the form of several yahoo groups that got my thyroid under control with the help of a very understanding doctor. The issue was that I still struggled with my weight. I could not understand why...why...would the weight not fall off. This is years of looking for answers, low carb, low cal, low fat, etc. Nothing worked for long, and I would always plateau around 165. Then would regain always ending up around 180-185. So frustrating. Then this past June, I went to the doctor and my Total Cholesterol was 349. Could not believe it. My grandmother died of a heart attack at the age of 52. She was overweight and died with a smile on her face, dancing at a party. Still, I was not looking forward to following in her footsteps. I searched online again but this time with a different mind set. Rather than look for a diet, I searched for what would cause someone to not be able to lose weight, and I found this great site. After 40 days, my total cholesterol is 197. along with other great lab numbers. My weight, I went from 182 to 167. I am still losing and loving life. I am going to post in the lab area to see if I need to do anything else to optimize my heath. I still have some cortisol and aldosterone issues that I am trying to resolve, but the progress so far has really made me a believer. Thanks Dr. Jack, and all the members who provide such valuable information for us to take control of our own health.

### Inger — 2013-09-23T22:29:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-and-loving-life.8229/post-95836>

Welcome to the forum, Veelansu! What great progress you have made, wow! Very kind of you to share it with us.
This is such an exiting, never ending journey

### fitness@home — 2013-09-23T23:36:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-and-loving-life.8229/post-95843>

Welcome Veelansu! Can't wait to follow your journal.

### caroline — 2013-09-24T03:16:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-and-loving-life.8229/post-95879>

Welcome Vee ...what a wonderful story that will give so many hope. It sounds like you are 100% committed and fully engaged! Please do start a journal - it ups the commitment level and it is fun to look back and see your progress. This journey to your own personal n=1 won't always be linear ....we are all a work in progress ....but this is our journey of a lifetime... we don't get a do over.

Do you have Dr. K's book? he goes into a lot of detail about labs etc. Have you listened to his podcasts? you get lots of great information quickly. The webinars and Q&A's are an amazing resource as well. Think of it as the best investment you can make in your health.

### caroline — 2013-09-24T03:17:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-and-loving-life.8229/post-95880>

I love your thread title btw.....that says it all really

### veelansu — 2013-09-24T08:45:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-and-loving-life.8229/post-95913>

Yes, I bought the book the first day. I downloaded all the podcast but have not listened to them yet. I'm trying to absorb the book and the blog posts. So much great information. I am so committed to this. Especially with the great progress I'm making. I have good will power, so I'm being really strict with the program. The journal will help me also to keep myself straight in those moment when I may stray. Keep me honest so to speak. My daughter is a pastry chef and I have a huge sweet tooth so it's been challenging at times, but I've been strong and kept my distance from the restaurant she works at. She also helps by not bringing me anything even when I ask. . She a good kid.

### caroline — 2013-09-24T09:26:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-and-loving-life.8229/post-95917>

Honestly ....it isn't about will power really. Are you doing the BAB? If you are eating enough fat and protein within 30 minutes of rising ...it knocks out all cravings....it absolutely does. I wouldn't thank you for even a bite of cake - wouldn't be able to do it and everyone else will tell you much the same thing.

We had a restaurant for 35 years and I made everything from scratch ....fresh desserts and homemade bread every single day. Now, if I want to treat myself to something it is blue cheese and red wine and seafood ....and I can't wipe the smile off my face

### Determined — 2013-09-24T09:33:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-and-loving-life.8229/post-95919>

I haven't got any willpower and just as Caroline says w the BAB I don't crave anything - except a fetish for oysters as a 'treat' - that's pretty amazing for a big sweet tooth like me! No fruit or anything for now except a bit of dark raw chocolate and cream in my coffee in the morning. Might have to give that coffee up too though as I have an AI disease ( Hashi's) and coffee is very dehydrating as per Jack.

Sounds like u r off to a fantastic start!

### caroline — 2013-09-24T09:34:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-and-loving-life.8229/post-95921>

Listen to the podcasts first....you get so much info quickly and Dr. K. Is really easy to listen to...much, much easier than the blogs. If you join as a gold member you get all the webinars and Q&A"s as well. We will be getting the September webinar this week and then the live Q&A will be a few days later....and you can usually ask questions on anything.

The Q&A usually runs for a couple of hours .....he has been know to go for 4 hours..

BTW.....you definitely dont have to ....
.

### caroline — 2013-09-24T09:37:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-and-loving-life.8229/post-95922>

> [Determined said:](/goto/post?id=95919)
>
> I haven't got any willpower and just as Caroline says w the BAB I don't crave anything - except a fetish for oysters as a 'treat' - that's pretty amazing for a big sweet tooth like me! No fruit or anything for now except a bit of dark raw chocolate and cream in my coffee in the morning. Might have to give that coffee up too though as I have an AI disease ( Hashi's) and coffee is very dehydrating as per Jack.
>
> Sounds like u r off to a fantastic start!

Fetishes eh?? Tell me......

Yeah ....you have to ditch the coffee...I should do it too....we can do it together..?.

### veelansu — 2013-09-24T23:30:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-and-loving-life.8229/post-95972>

I definitely do the BAB, and I am not hungry most of the day. I start getting hungry about 4 or so. Then I get home from work and eat dinner. The thing is that after dinner, I always get a hankering for something sweet. Always. I don't know if it's just a habit, or a real craving. What I have been doing is making an avocado chocolate pudding. Avocado, coconut milk, coconut oil, organic cocoa powder, stevia and some coconut sap. Process it all together, then eat about 1/4 of the batch after dinner. That helps. I don't do this every night, just when I really can't stop myself. My issue with sweets is not about hunger. It's about seeing something and wanting to shove it in my mouth. I've been better these days on the Rx. I can say no, but I still look at them with longing. .

### caroline — 2013-09-24T23:42:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-and-loving-life.8229/post-95976>

Have you tried just a piece of dark Chocolate? I think we are all the same!

Make sure you aren't having dinner too late....I find any later than five and I am screwed \[literally not firguratively .....and I prefer 4:00 - 4:30

### veelansu — 2013-09-25T02:21:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-and-loving-life.8229/post-96008>

I cannot eat that early. I work until 5:30 so I don't get home till 6. I usually eat between 6:30 and 7 depending on what I am cooking and how long it takes. Dark Chocolate has sugar which is not good for me as my glucose is still kind of on the high side. That's why I made the pudding. No sugar, same great taste, low glycemic.

"Your request (#7096) has been updated. Reply to this email or click the link below:
[http://jackkruse.zendesk.com/tickets/7096](http://jackkruse.zendesk.com/tickets/7096)

Team Jack Kruse

Sep 13 14:03 (CDT)

### caroline — 2013-09-24T06:06:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-again.8239/post-95901>

Hi sienaflin ..... so glad you are back! The forum was down for about 9 days - sorry that your account was deleted.....I didn't realise that had happened.

Please start a journal so we can get to know you.....the good, the bad and the ugly  just like the rest of us.....

# Thread: New as of today!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-as-of-today.8307/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### oliveview — 2013-09-30T19:11:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-as-of-today.8307/post-96518>

Hi everyone! I just wanted to introduce myself and say hello. I started today with my 50gm protein breakfast and I am patiently (not really) waiting on my labwork that was drawn on Friday. My goal is restoration of health and ultimately that being expressed as an improved body weight. I've been stopping and starting Paleo over the last few months because I would not see any results. I started reading JK's work and it really hit home with me. I'm excited to get started and I'm "all in"!

### caroline — 2013-09-30T23:49:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-as-of-today.8307/post-96541>

Welcome Olive ....so glad to hear you are all in and committed ....please start a journal in the journal section. It really, really helps to keep on track and motivated ...and we can all lend a hand - and you can help others too.

Have you listened to Dr. K's podcasts? they are a great way to get lots of info fast.....and do you have his book?
The EPI-PALEO RX ......it puts everything into context in an easy to understand way.

Please keep reading and asking questions .....there is a lot to get thru ....probably the understatement of the century

### oliveview — 2013-10-01T01:23:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-as-of-today.8307/post-96552>

Dr K and Caroline, thank you for the warm welcome. Caroline, I have his book but I haven't listened to any podcasts yet. I've been reading his blog posts also. I think I will start a journal, however it's a little intimidating to put it all out there.

### caroline — 2013-10-01T02:12:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-as-of-today.8307/post-96555>

Olive - just start simply.....Just maybe say what you want to accomplish .....and it will take on a life of it's own! It is great to look back at a journal and to see how far you have come....and JK likes journals  Actually, what you said initially is perfect - just transfer to the journal section.

It is great to read other's journals too .... a lot of folks here are dealing with some pretty tough stuff and maybe you can encourage them.....

Also - when you get your lab work back post in the lab section if you want help with it.

### fitness@home — 2013-10-01T02:29:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-as-of-today.8307/post-96561>

> [oliveview said:](/goto/post?id=96552)
>
> I think I will start a journal, however it's a little intimidating to put it all out there.

Olive, take the plunge. It is a bit intimidating, but once you start it really does gets easier. You will get some wonderful feedback and support from others too.

Welcome!

# Thread: Symptoms only the tip of the iceberg
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-only-the-tip-of-the-iceberg.8447/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Julie5 — 2013-10-15T03:03:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-only-the-tip-of-the-iceberg.8447/post-97863>

Hi everyone. I've been reading and asking for a few weeks now, and I'm in awe of Jack's willingness to answer so many questions!

I'm 58 and thought I was generally in good health, except I struggled with insomnia for the last 4 years (severe, can't sleep without drugs). Then I started noticing even the drugs didn't work. I developed a runny nose, a tingly toe, my hair started falling out like crazy (I have to wear a wig now). And no matter what I did, I couldn't get the extra 40 lbs off. In frustration I started searching online and found Jack Kruse. What a blessing that has proven to be! I asked him my first question and he put me on the road to recovery. He suggested i needed to have a serious talk with my doc. Since I knew a regular family doc couldn't do all the tests I knew I needed to do (per Jack's recommendations), I found an MD/naturopath. He had never heard of Jack, but his recommendations and the tests ordered, the ketogenic diet, the supplements suggested and the prescriptions given were right on target. To me, a second doc was verification that what Jack suggests is exactly what I need.

When my tests came back there was only one piece of good news: my HDL is 84. In January I had 5 stents put in my heart, but the cardiologist said there is no heart disease. The naturopath told me that without the high HDL I would have died of a massive heart attack like my brother did at the same age. All my other numbers were either way too low or too high. All my supps/meds now cost me about $250 a month, but I feel that's better than an early death. Everyone in my family died early. I'm the only one left.

I also found out I have 2 genetic mutations that prevent, if I understand correctly, enzymes from being utilized, and I learned I have celiac disease. That was a shocker since I have for many years made all our own homemade whole wheat bread. Now instead of feeling like I am generally healthy, I feel like I am a long way from there.

So for the last 2 weeks I have been eating the leptin RX (except for oysters). We started using oil lamps in our bedroom since the breaker is off in that room and I wear amber goggles in the evening/early morning. Compression shirt comes this week and I will have to wrap my head around being cold. Being cold has always been my least favorite thing, so maybe being cold adapted will help me tolerate our cold winters in eastern Washington.

Since learning all this, at least 2 of our 5 sons are exploring their own health. One, at 36, has osteopenia...way too young. The other, 30, is an elite runner with no health problems, but needs to learn about CT for training.

Hubby is 100% behind me, others are skeptical, some mocking. Tough bananas, I say. That iceberg of illness is gonna melt and I'm going to be OPTIMAL!

Here's to ya, Jack!

### fitness@home — 2013-10-15T03:17:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-only-the-tip-of-the-iceberg.8447/post-97865>

Julie5, I love your attitude! Good luck in your journey...

### caroline — 2013-10-15T03:31:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-only-the-tip-of-the-iceberg.8447/post-97868>

Julie - great post - thanks for sharing! Please consider starting a journal in the journal section. It is a great way to commit to this journey and we can follow along and follow your progress and you can help others too. Just copy this post to a journal. Let me know if you need help finding it or with anything else!

JK likes journals so he can get to know you a little. Please tell us about where you live and how your pregnancies went and your EMF exposure at home and at work etc. etc. It is all massively important .........

### nicld — 2013-10-15T03:52:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-only-the-tip-of-the-iceberg.8447/post-97874>

Julie, great saying "That iceberg of illness is gonna melt and I'm going to be OPTIMAL!" Welcome

### Julie5 — 2013-10-15T04:39:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-only-the-tip-of-the-iceberg.8447/post-97879>

THanks for the welcome! ANd I think I will start a journal. Great suggestion. I AM committed to this. My doc said, after I asked how long I should be on a ketgenic diet, that considering my makeup, the rest of my life. This is the perfect place for support!

### caroline — 2013-10-15T05:11:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-only-the-tip-of-the-iceberg.8447/post-97881>

This is an amazing journey of a lifetime..... welcome aboard!

Have you read Jack's book?

Have you watched his podcasts? they are a great way to get lots of info fast.

if you are a gold member - there is a live Q&A coming up on the 21st.

# Thread: This is My Season for this Journey
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-is-my-season-for-this-journey.8553/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Liveea — 2013-10-24T19:32:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-is-my-season-for-this-journey.8553/post-98889>

Hello,

I am so pleased to be here. I found Dr. Jack through a post on Facebook. I couldn't stop reading. I was on my way to Minneapolis to get Prolotherapy injections in my back for chronic pain that has persisted for 28 years.

I am 53 years old. I have one son, 26. My chronic pain appear one morning out of the blue, sacrum pain and right shoulder blade area and achy all over. Day before there was no pain. Three things that happened prior to the pain that could answer... I was living in the Black Hills and I had a diving accident that most likely gave me a basal skull fracture with the blood that came out of my ears and the mood problem for several months. (I was young, refused medical care-there was a party that night...I was luck to be able to walk). I had a violent fall on my sacrum. And sometime in that year, I went to the nurse's office at the college with a round rash on my low back that the nurse said was shingles but I know it wasn't. It was just a round ring that was annoying......

Fast forward...searching for medical help for the pain, fatigue, depression, weight loss and weight gain, soo much pain... btw...pain disappeared during the last three months of pregnancy. I was diagnosed with Lyme's disease in 95. Finally got someone to dig deeper as to why I had pain. (I knew nothing about Lyme's then) I test positive 3 times. Had treatment after each test the last was IV rocephin, which made me so violently ill I was in the hospital by the 8th day of treatment.

Side bar....I was concerned because I read that Lyme's crosses the blood placenta barrier. So, we tested my son. He came up positive. No symptoms, but I look back and wonder if his learning disabilities could have been caused by this. He was treated with antibiotics for 10 days regardless of symptoms.

In the hospital, the infectious disease doctor became furious. He screamed at me because a GP doc was treating my Lymes. There had not been a reported case in South Dakota at that point. He was so furious that in fact he refused to go any further with testing or treatment. I was left to deteriorate, die or whatever it was on his brain. So, I had a rheumatologist and a physical medicine doctor fight over the fibro diagnosis or myofascial pain syndrome. I won the Myofascial diagnosis.

I have been to many pain clinics, including Mayo Clinic with no resolve. A lot of drugs along the way....not good. A lot of doctors and medical people dismissing me and telling me it was all in my head. No one would even consider touching the Lyme's tests. I had good insurance and begged.

Still fast forward- car accident- xrays revealed 3 thoracic compression fractures, healed. MRI showed old fx of my sacrum, healed. These were all old and never discovered prior to this....right around 1999.

Weight gain and loss of 45-50lbs about every 3 years. I begged for my thyroid to be tested. I was refused....they said it was fibro and peri-menopause. I kept getting sicker. Gave up a wonderful career due to heal and not being able to deal with the stress of the job, pain and mysterious health issues.

So....finally after jumping doctors...I am getting prolotherapy injections in Mpls and it is helping the over stretch ligaments and tendons...spasms are slowly leaving.
Hypothyroid (now) that swings to hyper we suspect when I loose weight. Hashimoto's is under control and the antibodies are normal now. Severe adrenal fatigue, leaky gut, still have chronic pain, in the weight gain mode.... finally awaiting the Lyme's testing after 20+ years, oh, yes, I have Lichen Schlerosus, and in the 90's gallbladder stopped functioning and had to come out, tonsil's were so infected and I had an abscess burst behind my tonsil, so they came out, I had continued doubling over pain that was the same that I had with the gall bladder symptoms for over a year....tested negative for pancreatitis. I have had chronic unrelenting stress that has added to all of this. Have lost both parents and raised my son solo.

I hope I haven't overwhelmed anyone. I started to see that I was loosing the chronological events. I hope I haven't missed anything.... I hate that my teeth are spreading....doing weird stuff with my tongue...tongue thrusting to bottom teeth and they are spreading..... they say that is neurologic....why? Heaven only knows.

Thanks for reading. I just really felt a connection to everything that Dr. Kruse is talking about. I just have to put it in perspective and act on the protocols. I have ordered some more supplements, working on some changes.

I'm not giving up. I know there is an answer. Thank you again.

Liveea

### caroline — 2013-10-24T23:46:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-is-my-season-for-this-journey.8553/post-98913>

Hi Liveea and welcome.

My heart just breaks for you........so much for a body to go thru......but you are here now.

Please, please, please start a journal. If you could move this post to the journal section ....Dr. kruse likes journals - he needs to know about you .... context.... to offer any suggestions. A journal will help you with commitment on this journey to optimal health and we can follow along with you and offer support.

The journal section ......top of page ...click on forum and then go 5 categories down and you will see optimal reset and then journals ... just start a new thread and I will watch for you!

Have you read Dr.K's book? it is a great starting point. Please watch Jack's podcasts - they are a quick way to get information.

Please read the easy start guides on the home page and just dive in and then there is a lot to read! it gets easier over time when we are able to start connecting dots!

If you can manage it - become a gold member then you have access to all the webinars and the live Q&A that follows a few days later. You will get the whole library of webinars.

You have a lot of work to do - for sure ......but everyone helps everyone else here .... and Dr. kruse monitors everything.

There are others here who have or had lyme and they will chime in at some point!

Do you have testing that you can post in the Lab section? ...in the forum - go down 7 categories. There are folks here that are great at reading labs.

Don't jump into supplements until you know more.....Dr K believes we should get everything we need from the epi paleo diet and please read about CT ....it is a massive game changer.

I am so glad you found Dr. Kruse ........


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

He was so furious that in fact he refused to go any further with testing or treatment. I was left to deteriorate, die or whatever it was on his brain.

Gee, guess they didn't want you to mess up their stats.

I have a relative that had severe pain in the gall bladder area for years. He had it removed, and it seemed to help, but then the pain came back just as bad. He was later diagnosed with ulcerative colitis, and whatever they have him on now is helping. Being in constant pain for years.... is a serious thing for sure.

### nicld — 2013-10-25T03:13:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-is-a-new-season-for-this-journey.8555/post-98955>

Welcome Liveea, finally someone in my neck of the woods, well at least closer than the others.

Do you have a hormone doctor that you are seeing now? This site will really be helpful but you will have to be patient as there is a lot to sort through and a lot to learn.

Nicole

### SeaHorse — 2013-10-25T03:18:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-is-a-new-season-for-this-journey.8555/post-98956>

Welcome Leeva....so glad you made it here!

### caroline — 2013-10-25T05:48:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-is-a-new-season-for-this-journey.8555/post-98991>

I did write you a big post - but it isn't showing up................

### Liveea — 2013-10-26T02:19:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-is-a-new-season-for-this-journey.8555/post-99061>

Thank you for the welcome. I have a NP and the GP who oversees her (co-signs labs, etc.). I am just with her for the past two months. We haven't delved into the hormone area. I assume due to the thyroid issues, I went through menopause at 47. I did have labs to see if I was fully menopaused and checked my testosterone. I am menopaused and my testosterone is normal. Still waiting for my labs on my Lyme's which I am grateful is through IGeneX. We haven't begun to do anything with hormones yet.

It is a tough one. Been pretty sick lately. I believe some of the stuff is detoxing me and I just ache and itch and am irritable and teary eyed. Crazy stuff. Really want to get well.... desperate to get well.

# Thread: New Journey/New Start
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-journey-new-start.8585/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Bonniebelle — 2013-10-28T18:43:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-journey-new-start.8585/post-99259>

Hi everyone, I'm new to the site and just wanted to say hello. I'm a wife and mother, interested in getting optimally healthy and losing weight. I live in Birmingham, AL and look forward to getting to know the members and continuing to learn all that I can about how to live a healthier life.

I found Dr. Kruse's site a few weeks ago and have been trying to absorb as much as I can since I found the site. I believe that when the student is ready, the teacher will appear! I feel fortunate to have stumbled onto the site. I'm currently on Day 14 of the Leptin Reset, working on my circadian rhythm, doing CT and just trying to add in what I find is needed as soon as I can. There is just so much information and only so many hours in the day, and patience has never been one of my strong points, lol. I have had several AHA moments while reading Dr. Kruse's blogs and I plan on upgrading my membership shortly so that I can catch some of the webinars.

I started a journal in the Optimal section and you can read about my journey up to this point over there. The way I look at things now, I'm starting a new journey now and plan to transform this caterpillar into a beautiful butterfly one step at a time!

### caroline — 2013-10-28T22:13:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-journey-new-start.8585/post-99279>

Hi Bonnie .... and welcome again! I just read thru your journal. You have made such a great start.

Yes .... baby steps .......and it will all start to come together for you. I think you are already a beautiful butterfly...

### Bonniebelle — 2013-10-28T22:55:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-journey-new-start.8585/post-99283>

Thanks for the welcome and the encourage, caroline!

# Thread: sick of tired

**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3

### Nittygrittydanny — 2013-11-15T21:11:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sick-of-tired.8773/post-100781>

49 yo male who is active, 5'9" 273lbs, works hard and wanting to be the badass ball of energy i was went i got out of the military.

up by 6am most mornings in bed by 10-11pm most nights. i know my diet is for the most part crap but have the disipline to do things right.

lots of reading to do here. look forward to learning.

Danny

### nonchalant — 2013-11-15T22:06:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sick-of-tired.8773/post-100786>

Welcome Danny! Energy is the key to it all.

### caroline — 2013-11-15T22:57:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sick-of-tired.8773/post-100789>

Hi Danny and welcome!

Please go to the home page and follow the easy start guides. Just jump in and start with the leptin R/X using the epi- paleo template.

It would be great for you to start a journal in the journal section - go to the top of this page and click on forum and go down 5 categories to journals.

A journal really helps with commitment and Dr. Kruse likes journals so he can get some context. Please tell us more! Do you have labs that you can post? there is a section for labs and there are many here who can hack them for you - or at least give it a crack!

Do you have Dr. K.'s book? available at amazon - on kindle and paper. It is a great, easy and quick overview of all of this

Please listen to the podcasts - they are listed on the home page. Huge source of information and easy to grasp.

There is a new bootcamp starting with Dr. Tim Jackson .... Heal your hormones - he gives a lot of detail and is very easy to understand. We already have had week one last week - but if you purchase the bootcamp you will get that one too.
Check out the home page.

If you become a gold member you have access to the whole library of webinars and the live Q&A that follows a few days later. You can view them at any time you want and as many times as you want. you certainly don't have to - we will support you in any way we can.

There is a lot here to take in .......just keep reading and asking questions!

# Thread: Hello and Excited to be here!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-and-excited-to-be-here.8807/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Frankie L — 2013-11-20T03:29:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-and-excited-to-be-here.8807/post-101072>

As most people here, I stumbled on Dr. Jack Kruse while looking for a solution to Periperal Neuropathy (caused by anxiety)-I really need to calm down my adrenals.
I can say thus far I am one of Jack's biggest fans and I am so impressed with Jack's knowledge and valuable insight. What a blessing he is! I look forward to meeting new people where we can share our experiences and learn to grow together in peace, harmony and health.

I was a vegetarian but I've now turned Paelo and had my first bit of red meat this evening in almost 4 years! Wow!

I look forward to chatting with you all!

Peace

### caroline — 2013-11-20T05:54:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-and-excited-to-be-here.8807/post-101085>

Hi Frankie and welcome!

Please start a journal. Go to the top of this page and click on forum and then go down 5 categories to the journal section.

Dr. k. Likes journals - it gives him some context and it is great for commitment.

Have you listened to the podcasts yet? They give a great amount of info in an easy to understand format.

There is also a great hormone webinar with Dr. Jackson.

There is a ton of reading....but just jump in and start with the easy start guides for the leptin reset using the paleo template and C/T ......and then come back and ask questions!!

### nonchalant — 2013-11-20T16:14:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-and-excited-to-be-here.8807/post-101105>

Welcome Frankie! Glad you are here with us.

### Inger — 2013-11-20T19:44:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-and-excited-to-be-here.8807/post-101130>

Welcome Frankie!

Ketogenic Diet (no carbs!) loaded with fat and seafood will be great for anxiety, at least it was for me.. and when doing it raw even better...  (my experience)

### Frankie L — 2013-11-22T16:00:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-and-excited-to-be-here.8807/post-101350>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=101085)
>
> Hi Frankie and welcome!
>
> Please start a journal. Go to the top of this page and click on forum and then go down 5 categories to the journal section.
>
> Dr. k. Likes journals - it gives him some context and it is great for commitment.
>
> Have you listened to the podcasts yet? They give a great amount of info in an easy to understand format.
>
> There is also a great hormone webinar with Dr. Jackson.
>
> There is a ton of reading....but just jump in and start with the easy start guides for the leptin reset using the paleo template and C/T ......and then come back and ask questions!!

Will do Caroline..thank you! I will start with a journal and go from there! Thanks everyone!

### Frankie L — 2013-11-22T16:08:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-and-excited-to-be-here.8807/post-101352>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=101130)
>
> Welcome Frankie!
>
> Ketogenic Diet (no carbs!) loaded with fat and seafood will be great for anxiety, at least it was for me.. and when doing it raw even better...  (my experience)

Thank you Inger

Is the ketogenic diet a permanent solution, or is it until the symptoms are corrected? I only justed started to eat grass fed meat in the last few days. I had shrimp yesterday for the first time in 5 years.

Although I am not mentally anxious, my body is, and therefore my hands and feet tingle and vibrate. If I have a cup of coffee....omg...I'm 2 days getting over the effects. I never drink anything with caffeine! My adrenals are so sensitive right now especially in the morning. If a real situation of anxiety presents itself, then OMG my heart pounds out of my chest and my hands tremour for a few hours then settle back down.......I need help!

### caroline — 2013-11-22T23:56:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-and-excited-to-be-here.8807/post-101405>

Please think of this as your permanent lifestyle from here on out......why would you go back to crap????

your body, and most of all, your brain needs the best of everything.

Don't forget about your non native EMF exposure - that will absolutely be the most important thing for you to control.....

### margor_j — 2014-01-04T10:31:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-and-excited-to-be-here.8807/post-104715>

Hi Frankie,

Welcome! Im new to the site to, but I did discover JK about two months ago! i, also, am dealing with completely whacked out adrenals, messed up cortisol and did have problems with anxiety / depression.. as explained in my intro, I was pretty much vegan/ vego for years, and 4 years ago when I met the physician who put me onto a paleo diet - well, i was off anti depressants after two weeks of changing my diet! True story! Never forgotten it, because I never wanted to be 'medicated' but every time i tried to come off them, i fell apart..

Iv done a couple of different protocols for restoring adrenals, but i heard TS Wiley talking about how crap they are as a solution.. then i discovered JK, so im not taking the adrenal extract anymore.. A low carb diet is good for adrenal stres apparently, and the more you read JK and adrenals, your mind will probably be blown away as much as mine!!

 all the best!

Margs
