# Thread: Hi from hopeful and very sick
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-hopeful-and-very-sick.9061/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Daizy — 2013-12-13T00:17:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-hopeful-and-very-sick.9061/post-103258>

From being totally healthy, two prescribed medications 17 years ago,
cascaded in torturous devastating SEVERE symptoms/illness.

A brief summary:

1\. Prescribed fluoroquinolone antibiotic. Fell unwell, palpitations, insomnia
and many more symptoms.
2\. A few months later, prescribed Lariam - caused neurological dysfunction, including ocular toxicity, (inc visual distortion), floaters, halos, and more. Auditory
dysfunction such as hyperacusis, tinnitus, vestibular dysfunction, phototoxicity, severe reduced cognition, agnosia, type of aphasia, and more. Arrythmias, tachycardia daily for many years, up to 200 beats per minute, atrial flutters (not recently), AF, (not recently), bradycardia, very low BP, but can fluctuate to to high, overall too low.

Refused all meds to treat. I was hoping to improve with time, healthy diet and vits and supps. Weak to the point of being spoon fed, worsened to being bedridden/wheelchair bound for many years on/off, with lots of symptoms, including neuropathy.

A year later, unstable angina. Given IV GTN, which caused left ventricular dysfunction (heart failure),
Read a few years back, that IV GTN alters ALDH2 gene which causes LVF. Then, diastolic and systolic heart failure and global hypokinesis. Hypothyroidism, mitochondrial dysfunction, autonomic dysfunction, multiple food allergies (causing malnutrition and suspected scurvy), gallstones, toxic hepatitis. MCS, EMF, vascular (insufficiency, blood clots, right groin valve is dysfunctional, left foot turning blue etc..), and more symptoms.

Had thallium test - refused adenosine over ten years ago. Made me very ill. Angioscan with galdolinium, made me very ill, unstable angina for over two years, extreme headaches, hypo/hyperglycemic attacks, kidneys starting to fail etc.. and low grade on/off activation of CMV, EBV and HHV6 over a period of time, which I believe was activated by antibiotics, in particular amoxycillin, which I took over ten years ago. There is much evidence that antibiotics, in particular, amoxycillin can activate dormant infectious pathogens, in particular, the aforementioned.

There is a long list of symptoms, such as neuropathic pains, muscles pains, joints, bones, etc..

Since, I refused all invasive diagnostic tests, zero medication, no anesthetic (unable to tolerate, even local).Had several attacks of angina, unstable angina, acute coronary syndromes, suspected heart attacks for which I refused all medications. I take Arjuna, Ayurvedic med, no toxicity no reactions, from time to time.

Free radicals so high, that even blending a few vegetables makes me ill. Had oxygen mask once on, and made me iller for about a year. When pollution level is high, makes me very ill, angina, and more.

Many compounds in many foods cause worsening of weird neuro symptoms, and extreme pain in all veins and microvessels. Told some type of inflammatory vasculitis. Inability to metabolize some compounds in foods, makes me feel toxic, as if poisoned, and other symptoms. Found out some of the CYPs enzymes are dysfunctional including GSTS, which explains in part the inability to metabolize so many foods and meds.

Slight improvement since I started bone broths a year ago. Able to add a few more foods to the limited diet.
Unable to eat nuts, seeds, avocados, fish, dairy, I am very low in Omega 3 (as shown in tests), and most nurtrients and minerals.

Since the bone broths has been so helpful, I would like to continue with this. The only meats I can meat at present, are lamb and beef, including liver and kidney. I tried ox heart and could not tolerate. I would love to have been able to eat, it oozes with ubiquinol, as it boils..

Unable to eat all sea foods, except for kombu and dulse seaweeds, unable to tolerate others.

As for vegetables and fruits, very limited, but expanding since starting bone broths.

Tried functional medicine, acupuncture (worsened symptoms), Ayurvedic medicine, (some helpful),
pranayama breathing (made me dangerously ill, specially heart and autonomic), Qi Gong, (made me much
worse), Mudras (type of yoga using finger tips), had the opposite effect, homeopathy, (will try again), antroposophical medicine, (worsened symptoms). Met with several Qi Gong sifus for qi transmission. One caused severe symptoms lasting six months. The second sifu, was helpful, except for the last session suffered hypoxia, and oxygen sat went down to about 90. Similar experience with three other sifus and their prescribed exercises, which consisted of specific gestures, not moves/exercises.

It was explained that ion channels are so messed up, and intracellular communication so dysfunctional, that energy medicine worsens symptoms. Qi transmission practitioners say the Qi is too weak, and too much cell apoptosis. Ayurvedic practitioner says the agni is too dysfunctional. Advised to avoid, meditate for a long period of time, before next attempt.

Sitting in the sun makes me very ill. I read up on this and found out, that it can increase free radicals..this is how sensitive. Unable to tolerate solvents, chemicals, includes essential oils, zero tolerance to coffee, and alcohol. Heat or cold, worsens symptoms considerably. Baseline improves during Spring and Autumnal months.

I don't believe detoxing is safe for me, and would like to reduce free radicals using a nutritional protocol, such as the Paleo protocol here. Interests include chemical compounds in various foods, epigenetic effects, reducing free radicals, best foods to reduce various types of toxicities, and rebuild at cellular level. Looking forward to improve, and exchange info.

### caroline — 2013-12-13T01:13:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-hopeful-and-very-sick.9061/post-103266>

Okay .... now your post showed up! I answered you in your other post ..... pm me if you want.......


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Since the bone broths has been so helpful, I would like to continue with this. The only meats I can meat at present, are lamb and beef, including liver and kidney. I tried ox heart and could not tolerate. I would love to have been able to eat, it oozes with ubiquinol, as it boils..

Unable to eat all sea foods, except for kombu and dulse seaweeds, unable to tolerate others.

As for vegetables and fruits, very limited, but expanding since starting bone broths.

Tried functional medicine, acupuncture (worsened symptoms), Ayurvedic medicine, (some helpful),
pranayama breathing (made me dangerously ill, specially heart and autonomic), Qi Gong, (made me much
worse), Mudras (type of yoga using finger tips), had the opposite effect, homeopathy, (will try again), antroposophical medicine, (worsened symptoms). Met with several Qi Gong sifus for qi transmission. One caused severe symptoms lasting six months. The second sifu, was helpful, except for the last session suffered hypoxia, and oxygen sat went down to about 90. Similar experience with three other sifus and their prescribed exercises, which consisted of specific gestures, not moves/exercises.

It was explained that ion channels are so messed up, and intracellular communication so dysfunctional, that energy medicine worsens symptoms. Qi transmission practitioners say the Qi is too weak, and too much cell apoptosis. Ayurvedic practitioner says the agni is too dysfunctional. Advised to avoid, meditate for a long period of time, before next attempt.

Sitting in the sun makes me very ill. I read up on this and found out, that it can increase free radicals..this is how sensitive. Unable to tolerate solvents, chemicals, includes essential oils, zero tolerance to coffee, and alcohol. Heat or cold, worsens symptoms considerably. Baseline improves during Spring and Autumnal months.

I don't believe detoxing is safe for me, and would like to reduce free radicals using a nutritional protocol, such as the Paleo protocol here. Interests include chemical compounds in various foods, epigenetic effects, reducing free radicals, best foods to reduce various types of toxicities, and rebuild at cellular level. Looking forward to exchange info, and health improvement..

Hello,

I am a 60 yr old man with an auto immune disease that requires IV gamma globulin every 3 or 4 weeks. Its a multifocal blockage of conduction disease. I also have a thyroid disorder that requires a prescrip for synthyroid. The neuros are doing their best to stabilize my condition. Its been about 10 years since I started getting IVIG.

Since reading Dr Kruze's writings and following his advice I am beginning to feel less bothered by inflammation. I especially notice that I get less exercise induced inflammation after workouts. I am also finding my optimal weight easier to attain.

There are so many testimonials here that its encouraging.

Be well, wishing everyone optimal health in the upcoming year,

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-with-hashimotos-mtfhr-mutations-and-leaky-gut-help.9151/post-103951>

Hi michaela and welcome. Congratulations on your daughter .... How wonderful!

You seem to be on top of things ..... Good job!

Have you read Dr. K.'s book? That will give you a quick overview of all of this.

Have you Been to the home page? The easy start guides are so helpful.. Have you seen all the podcasts? They also are a very quick and easy way to dial in.

If you can manage it - become a gold member and then you will have access to all the webinars and the follow up Q&A a few days later where you can ask Jack pretty much anything.

There is a free podcast by Dr. Tim Jackson "Heal your hormones" ....and he has just done a bootcamp for us that was outstanding. I think you can still purchase that.

No question - there is a lot to get thru here .... But just dive in and everyone will help! Keep reading and asking questions.

If you could transfer this to the journal section it will keep track of your journey to optimal!

For the journal section .... Just go to the top of the page and click on forum and then go down 5 categories to journals.

Have a look at a journal by Mr. Pinkies and read what she and her DH have accomplished for themselves and their children.

Where are you in Oz? There is a great thread in the cave ...Aussies and the donut hole.

We have crap water because of the donut hole ....so JK strongly suggests we drink bottled water from the Northern Hemisphere and be low carb all year .... And C/T our butts off!

Have you thought of getting a magnetico sleep pad?

Non native EMF is our biggest problem .....Jack addresses that in almost all of his podcasts at some point

### caroline — 2013-12-21T09:40:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-with-hashimotos-mtfhr-mutations-and-leaky-gut-help.9151/post-103955>

Mikaela .... I love your name! No - there isn't an OZ supplier. There is a forum member Nick who is in Brisbane and he got one for he and his DW and son. You could pm him. It has helped his little son tremendously.

If you want .... post your labs in the lab section. Same thing - go to the top of the page and click on forum and scroll down - 7 categories I think - to labs. There are folks here who are great at reading labs.

I strongly suggest that you listen to Dr. Jackson's free community wide webinar on Heal your Hormones. He gave us an amazing amount of information. Dr. Tim is an expert in hormones and is part of a MTHFR group with Dr. Sterling Hill

For those of us who purchased the bootcamp - Dr. Tim has given us a great price on a package of three consults and can help us get testing here in OZ from a lab in Sydney ...stool testing.

He also highly recommends a book .....Why do I still have Thyroid symptoms when my labs are normal - by Dadas Karazian \[Chiropractor\] I have no idea if that spelling is correct ..... that is how it sounded ...lol

There was also a free download of a book by Ameer Rosic \[spelling?\] it might be still available - go to Amazon. I will check and get back to you. If you want pm me and give me your email and I will send you my link.

### SeaHorse — 2013-12-21T15:17:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-with-hashimotos-mtfhr-mutations-and-leaky-gut-help.9151/post-103960>

Hi and Welcome!
I would agree with Caroline that some consults with Tim Jackson are really worth it. He can take you through a protocol to address the methylation issues. Along with all the protocols that Dr. Kruse outlines I think you are will find things will be better and better for you.

I can't answer your question about the saturated fat and snps but it doesn't make sense to me to limit these good fats.

### nonchalant — 2013-12-21T17:41:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-with-hashimotos-mtfhr-mutations-and-leaky-gut-help.9151/post-103966>

Hi Mikaela, welcome!! Sounds like you are doing great so far. I second Caroline's suggestion for a different water source. I also suggest you get plenty of sunshine if you have been limiting your artificial PUFAs like vegetable oil. And the magnetico is so helpful.

Remember that just because you have a gene, doesn't mean it is being expressed. For instance nuts and eggs may be a problem if some genes are expressing (because they are responding to a stress of some sort), but if the gene isn't expressing, nuts and eggs may be fine. They found that the group of people in Okinawa that regularly live to be 100 years old have the same "bad" genes as the rest of us. The 'bad' genes just aren't active. I agree with SeaHorse that saturated fats (non-trans fats) would not be a good thing to reduce. Good fats are pretty much self limiting.

Glad you are with us!

### Chris Wyllie — 2013-12-26T19:42:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introduction-hello-everyone.9187/post-104258>

Hello,
My name is Chris Wyllie. I'm currently running my own business in Toronto as a Performance Coach and Neurofeedback and Biofeedback Specialist. I am an avid bio-hacker and aspire to optimize my performance in all aspects of my life. I am so thankful to have come across Dr. Kruse's blog and this extremely knowledgeable community. Jack's brilliance and productivity are profoundly inspiring. I'm also intrigued by the high level of expertise and quality of discussion present in the comments and forums. I hope I can offer some valuable contributions as well!

My future plans are to begin an M.B.A /J.D program at an Ivy University in the U.S. starting next year. (I definitely will now be favouring the cooler climates, sorry Stanford!)
For me maximizing productivity, cognition and overall performance is critical. The valuable knowledge on this site can likewise make me a more astute supporter of clients in my business.

I am very interested in continuing to learn more and more about your most effective bio-hacks and how they've elevated your life! People fear what they don't understand. Rather than fearing, I am going to seek to understand as much as I can. As Jack so aptly states, I love that everyone in this community is committed to achieving not only an A, but an A +. I share your same passion.

Recommendations as to initial membership? Right now I'm thinking a Gold Membership to access all the webinars would be the best choice. Have you found them to be a crucial supplement to Jack's Blogs?

I am happy to now be on the road to OPTIMAL!

I just do it when ever I can fit it in.... Sometimes it is a short jump into my 40 degree F pool. Sometimes a cold shower and others ice packs or just going outside early in the morning when it is cold.

Right now I have a room in the house that I keep cold and will go in there to do some stretching and mobility work.
I do my Movnat sessions outside in the cold with minimal clothing....
Sometimes during the day I will just strip down and take a cold shower or a jump in my cold pool.
During the summer months it is ice baths and or jumps in a river when I am out an about.
Basically I just try and fit it in when I can....

# Thread: Nuthin but a hot mess! (with low body temp!)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nuthin-but-a-hot-mess-with-low-body-temp.9248/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### margor_j — 2014-01-04T02:29:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nuthin-but-a-hot-mess-with-low-body-temp.9248/post-104692>

Hi all, from New Zealand!

Im new to posting on this blog, but discovered JK about two months ago, Im gutted that I never found him sooner, and it surprises the crap outta me, given my constant 'health trolling' about adrenal exhaustion etc..

Im almost stuck at where to start, such is the 'quilt' of my ill health..

Heres where Im at now, iv just ordered some labs - sex hormones, melatonin and a cortisol profile (all saliva) and i will try to get vit D and HS CRP, though i recently read on the inet, that its basically not an option in NZ (like lots of things)

Over the past 5 years mission of trying to lose weight (but just keep getting bigger) iv had lots of hormone panels done. My cortisol is always showing its shot to bits - really low by day, and uber high at night. I first got relief from my nightmare when i met the most amazing american doc working at a place i stayed at in thailand - and he took me from a vegan diet straight to paleo, and off coffee (detoxing from that was a b\*tch!) no change for weeks, then after like 3 months, i basically woke up thin! as weird as that sounds, i wouldnt believe it myself if i hadnt see it with my own eyes! ( i was taking adrenal cortex and some herbs because hed run cortisol labs on me and showed adrenal burnout)

And its just been a mare since then. Just before i met that doctor, Id been back to antarctica for work (summer) and that year was when i stopped sleeping well. We were on night shifts for most of the 4 months, and though the rooms are pitch black, it was still 24 hour daylight outside, but not for the whole 4 months. It got to the point where you get up in the dark, and go to bed in daylight - and im a chef, so i was working inside under fluro lights - NONE of that rang any bells but its thru JK that iv been able to string bits together and see a pattern.. SO -

i went back for two more consecutive summers. and Iv also seen with my own eyes, i just got bigger, and bigger and bigger - and i hadnt changed my diet or exercise (except id stopped the chronic cardio) but i was under a lot of stress, and back to 24 hours light, and mixed up shifts...

I used to leave the ice an go and hang out in asia, literally lie about in the air con and just while away HOURS on youtube watching crap - that was my 'rest'! Id go to the gym but other than that.. i was happy laying low - getting massages, taking a wander and watching all that fab junk that i could on my laptop. It was my second time doing that that i REALLY started to have sleep issues - im talking taking sleeping pills and still lying awake all night, bashing heart (that used to start at 10.30 on the dot, some nights so badly that i couldnt lie down..) thank god i found a chinese doctor who gave me some herbal sleepers that worked amazingly, im not sure how i would have survived the last few years otherwise!

A year ago, i self medicated with thyroid extract. Im so sick of low body temp (average around 35.) but continued to swell up and and up and up, low energy, very unstable moods.. basically a hot mess! (back in nz at this point) then i met a naturopath who tested my iodine, saliva and (now i cant remember, blood or urine?) and i barely scarped through, it was DISMALLY low! wow, what a shock! so iv been taking iodine ever since. and she helped me see how the dessicated thyroid wasnt really helping, so i came off it. (and i lost the feeling of being too big for my skin)

the other job i do is as a soigneur - i work with bike teams doing massage / food etc.. which takes me all over the world. this year, in europe for 5 weeks, i didnt take a laptop, just ipad and iphone. hmm i never got a sim for my iphone, i was given a brick phone, i couldnt be bothered with european tv, so never watched it, and basically was kinda busy at races, and naturally following light cycles. ( i was doing some cold blasts in the shower - but i hadnt discovered JK at this stage) but low and behold, i came home 5-7kg lighter! and i got back to miserable nz winter, and spent two weeks in bed recovering jet lag n all, HOURS of blue light.. and HELLO, the weight came back! hmmm

then i eventually discovered JK. so here i am. Its not been the easiest for me to do strict epi paleo fx, but i def do ok cos i dont go for lots of carbage etc, i do the BAB daily, no snacking etc. I did get changes in my sleep for sure. this can be a bit up and down ,depending on whats going on in my life.
what id like to do is get my lab results back and then become a gold member and discuss this all with JK. some days i feel SO desparate and hopeless, it really sucks. (like now! lol) Iv been to hell and not come back yet, like so many others on here.. its funny how ppl still comment on how big i am, or how i should eat or exercise.. i say 'honey! youre so 10 years ago when eating less actually worked for me! if it was that easy, i wouldnt be like this!'

i do the grounding when i fly. My next race is to LA and then colombia, so im going to try CoQ10 for those flights. I listen to all JKs podcasts, and really feel this is the last place i will come to for help, that this will actually help me!

I know therell be things i missed out, and i tried to make this as condensed as i could but yeah.. thats me in a hot-mess nutshell!

oh yeah, i was bulimic for about 8 years when i was younger, and i dabbled in anorexia as well, but found bulimia more fun. and i raced bikes myself, for about 15 years, and talk about chronic cardio! fully low fat, lots of grains etc..

I wanna thank JK again, for what you do, learn and then share. freaking amazing!

HMM - Hormonal Mess Margo! lol

### caroline — 2014-01-04T03:47:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nuthin-but-a-hot-mess-with-low-body-temp.9248/post-104698>

Welcome Margo! what an amazing story you have shared with us!

Could you please start a journal in the journal section ..... Dr. K. needs context to offer suggestions when he can.
Journals really help everyone .... you never know when someone will say something to help you .... or something you say really resonates with someone else! We all help each other on this amazing journey.
A journal really helps with commitment and it is fun and helpful to go back and see your progress.

Did you see the free webinar with Dr. Tim Jackson?

When you become gold member you will have access to all the webinars at any time and even more important, the live Q&A with Dr. Kruse where you can ask him pretty much anything.

Please don't feel desperate .... altho I guess most of us have been there. You can and will turn your titanic around! Lots here have done it.

Just take baby steps - you didn't get broken overnite - so it is going to take awhile! we are very lucky to have JK shining his flashlight for us.

Have you got Jack's book? The Ei-Paeo RX ... it is a great overview of all of this.

How are you doing with the BAB? it worked wonders for me .... I had been a binge eater for most of my life.

You seem to get the non native EMF connection - hugely important ......especially for you with all your travel.

Do you have blue blocker glasses?

Please just keep reading and asking questions...... you have so much to share with all of us .... can't wait!

### Josh — 2014-01-04T03:51:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nuthin-but-a-hot-mess-with-low-body-temp.9248/post-104699>

Welcome! I would recommend reading the blog posts in chronological order. The early ones are good, but it really gets good when you read through the topical ones in order. The concepts emerge and it gets you thinking with him. It is empowering which I feel is his mission, the N=1 thing. The key from your story looks like what we might call keeping your energy or electrons topped up. The system tends to run well when that is in order and otherwise it gets really inefficient and unpleasant things happen, many of which you have already identified. Its simple, but not easy....He lays it all out brilliantly through the blogs, especially EMF into Quantum Biology. Beware of too steep of a gradient as this gets one into trouble, only took me 50 years or so to learn.

### margor_j — 2014-01-04T05:17:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nuthin-but-a-hot-mess-with-low-body-temp.9248/post-104705>

HI guys,
thanks for your positive replies.

Iv been wading through JKs blogs since i discovered them, i have his book and i have downloaded every podcast i can find where JK is interviewed

Caroline - yes, i have blue blocker glasses. I did have my room blacked out, which was pretty mega, but the foils been removed (not my doing  But im not working at the moment, before I was getting up at 4.30 so i could do CT in the cold lake right outside, before I started my shift (that has been reallllyy eye opening for me, because i found i got a huge reduction in body pain / back pain - working as a chef can be really hard on your body! plus, it made a huge difference in how hot the kitchen felt when i got to work each day! )
I also noticed that i have started to sweat again, not hugely, but definitely noticeably. Ever since i can remember, iv been the person who can sit in a sauna and not sweat..

The last race i was away at, i would have the small tv on in the background, but i didnt turn lights on, i could massage in that semi light (natural) plus the little the tv put out, and some from the hallway cos i kept my door open.. anyway, it was amazing how knacked i was by bed time and i really think it was the fact i made the most of natural light... (huge windows, curtains open, naturally getting darker...)

Yeah josh, i wonder about my emf damage also.. i actually have a pendant i wear - not every day, but regularly, to help with emfs. whether it does or not, i dont know, but i figure if it could help, its worth it. I also try to earth every day cos, while i havent been going barefoot alot before hand, the 3 summers in a row down south also coincided with my first iphone, ipad, fancy ipod and spending loads of time on my computer (what else do you do on a blizz day when you cant go outside!)

and caroline yes, i sometimes 'fear' that reality that i may be looking at something that will take a long time to correct because its been a good few years building up..

any thoughts, ideas on raw milk? i have access to it, get it weekly, and here in NZ were lucky to have A2 beta-casein.. i feel like a calf sometimes i just love it so much!
also, coffees SUCH a thorn for me.. but i know i feel so much better if i can go months without having it, its just very hard for me to seem to be able to do.. altho, italy this year did change the way i drink it. and interestingly, in italy i never got the shakes from a coffee!! they do ristretto shots (shorter, so less caffeine) plus, they dont do the big milky mega cup size like over here (how i used to drink it). its more like 2-3 sips and its done, and its not really strong.. but its hard to find ppl in nz who understand how to make that!

thanks for the advice to start a journal, il endeavor to do so!

hmm well, i get frustrated to cos EVERY dam thing iv tried, every pill, exercise, etc etc - where normal people will shed kgs, i get zero change. every single time, its a bit of a joke actually! (but im not laughing!)

Doing cold showers, and packs a few times, did a few ice baths but they didnâ€™t feel right (right idea at the wrong time IYKWIM), so will work back up to them.

We live a couple of miles away from town, EMF situation not too bad, but how did I let the wifi slip past me?  (Studied Beckerâ€™s work and EMFs generally 15 years ago as part of a subtle energy course, the microwave disappeared immediately). Have tightened up on wifi use and darkness/temperature of bedroom at night.

Sleep is much better, I used to kid myself that if I couldnâ€™t sleep it was OK to go and study things I love, which was better for my stress levels than listening to DH snoring (he could snore for Britain,  and he was developing sleep apnea). It wasn't his snoring that woke me up, but it didn't help me trying to get back to sleep. Iâ€™m not sure what change has done what, but heâ€™s also doing the BAB, 7 p.m. cut off for eating (especially carbs), swapped sweet potato for potato and cut down on the grains, and in the last couple of months has lost 30 lb without trying, and the snoring and apnea is soooooooo much better. ### Sue-UK — 2014-01-08T10:06:27+0200

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=105030)
>
> I'm interested in your lunula observations, I only have them visible on my thumbs, what does this suggest? When hubby and I first cut carbs he lost over a stone and I lost almost a stone. Sadly this hasn't stopped off and I feel as though I'm swelling round my middle instead of shrinking

It apparently points to possible malnutrition and/or anaemia. I had a ridge on my right ring finger that also pointed to it which has now gone too. I was interested in the why. Seems mad because Iâ€™ve been eating better for several years, but weâ€™re not what we eat we are what we assimilate.  Iodine has been my light bulb moment, because of its role in making stomach acid. In my case not enough iodine intake and a lot of tea drinking causing losses! Hence why my previous grain free tries werenâ€™t successful , it was less about inflammation (although that is a factor), and more about iodine deficiency and fluoride toxicity.

Iâ€™ve also seen lack of lanula related to low thyroid, but Iâ€™m not convinced yet as Iâ€™ve not found that on a referenced or medical source (and if someone is malnourished, then low thyroid could be the expected effect not a cause?).

# Thread: Kia Ora from New Zealand
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kia-ora-from-new-zealand.9344/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Da-mo — 2014-01-15T01:46:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kia-ora-from-new-zealand.9344/post-105695>

Hello optimizers

I have been lurking around on this site for around 6 months and have been so impressed that I just last week joined up as a gold member. Firstly, I'd like to thanks Dr. Jack Kruse for sharing his genius so freely and helping people with seemingly intractable health problems. I have been able to refer many people to the information he has provided to show them there are solutions for things ranging from diabetes to crohn's.

So a bit about me . . .

I am fortunate enough to live on a 5 acre block in the country in a beautiful setting by a river with my lovely lady (high school sweethearts, 23 years married, 30 years together), two daughters (22 and 20), one son (17), two horses, two labradors and a weird looking goldfish who lives in the horse's drinking trough. There are bush clad ranges a few minutes walk away and a 15 minute walk brings us to a waterfall where we do our CT in preference to using our swimming pool.

As a child born of a Fijian mother and Scottish father, I grew up not far from here during the boom years of a local town. Food was much better back then and sports and outdoors were the norm rather than internet, xbox and playstation. Football (soccer), swimming, tennis, gymnastics and archery were all part of my life through to 20 years old.

From 20 to 35 years old I practiced Chinese internal martial arts. This was meditation based using Buddhist breathing techniques and combining Buddhist and Taoist principles. The martial aspect served to teach how to integrate mind and body - which is a mystical way of saying how to get out of your own way  . In a nutshell, this summed up to Chi follows Yi, which means that energy follows will/intent/focus/attention.

From 35 to 45 years old I fell into a very sedentary lifestyle, made bad health choices and developed osteoarthrosis in my knees. This was partly due to being born club footed, although corrective surgery as a baby enabled me to be a competitive sportsman regardless and go on to compete in gymnastics at a National level. It did however place abnormal stresses on my knees and ankles, resulting in 2 knee surgeries, until the final straws which were probably lack of activity combined with inflammatory foods.

At 45, when my left knee had been hurting constantly to the point it would wake me at night for over a year I decided enough was enough and something had to be done. After reading some information on this site I realised that the long term chronic pain was likely screwing with my cortisol and hormones as well as the disturbed sleep. Lots of physiotherapy and acupunture followed with good but not lasting results. Reading more, I eliminated the inflammatory foods, nightshades and grains from my diet and that was the breakthrough for the osteoarthrosis. My knees are now pain free and do not blow up like balloons anymore.

Such has been the dramatic effect of Dr. Kruse's information that I'm now hooked and have moved on to keep to an epi-paleo template and have added CT into the equation. At 45 I'm now facintated by this perspective of health from a brain signalling perspective and the positive effects are having some flow on effects on other members of my family and even to others I speak with about the profound ideas I have learned here.

My health continues to improve dramatically and I hope some of the damage that was done in my "lost" decade can be repaired. The idea of extending my telemere lengths and stem cell supply is an exciting prospect.

My current mission is to finally kick the smoking habit once and for all. I did stop for a couple of months late last year but that ended up with weight gain of 5kg. 3kg is enough to make a difference to my knees from pain free to constant pain - so it was kind of a choosing between the two evils . . . constant pain with cortisol and hormones and sleep disarray or smoking with all of its ill effects and dopamine problems. Im finding this site lacking in information that might help with some sort of dopamine reset for when I stop smoking. I expect this will depress my dopamine levels for awhile so am interested in learning the effects of low dopamine and also how dopamine levels might be returned towards optimal.

It would be so easy to just gut it out and stop like I did last time but again from reading here I have found out the effects on brain chemistry, leading to bad decision making and choices, fitted in with what I experienced with the weight gains. Finding a way to return to normal dopamine production while minimising bad choices caused by altered brain chemistry is what Im most interested in at this point.

A little about my working environment - its bad, real bad. I do have a plan in place to make it better by transitioning off shiftwork and minimising the time I spend in extremely high EMF (I do shift work as an operator in a power station). Meanwhile, eating well combined with CT and lots of grounding appears to be helping.

BTW - my username "Da-mo" was the name of a monk who crossed the Himalayas from India into China and he had an immense influence on the Chinese monks from whom my line of martial arts descended. Has was also known a Damo or Bodhidharmi and is credited with teaching the Shaolin monks how to integrate mind and body and also as the father of Zen Buddhism. I once learned a technique called the Damo fist set which is a set of postures involving manipulation of energy flow in the arms combined with meditative visualisations - the results were instantaneous and profound and further solidified my experience of mind/body integration. So when Dr. Kruse starts getting into quantum physics, things start to get really exciting.

I would have just used my name - Sandy - as my username but it was already taken.

I look forward to meeting you all in the forums and learning from your experiences.

Sandy (Male BTW, in case you missed that - since Sandy is a unisex kind of name)

### fitness@home — 2014-01-15T02:10:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kia-ora-from-new-zealand.9344/post-105699>

Welcome Sandy! Glad to see you here.

### Josh — 2014-01-15T02:15:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kia-ora-from-new-zealand.9344/post-105702>

Welcome. Do you still practice on any level? What style did you practice, martial or for health and healing or both? Interesting how in the current world teachers are having trouble with students not being able to handle the practices. I figure it is a combination of the distorted resonant environment and all the rest. I have a friend in China whose teacher has gone to villages to find children that are in good shape from having been brought up on a good diet away from modern life to train in his system. I have noticed that my body can only handle so much extra energy as I age and that diet and environment are quite important. I still find it a challenge to recognize my resonant/internal training limit rather than my physical/external limit. The later always leaves me tired and the former always humbles me.

### SeaHorse — 2014-01-15T02:47:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kia-ora-from-new-zealand.9344/post-105703>

Welcome... glad you are here.

### Da-mo — 2014-01-15T03:09:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kia-ora-from-new-zealand.9344/post-105710>

Thanks for the welcom fitness@home

Josh, yes I do still practice mostly on the meditative level - hardly on the physical level although I still do some as useful opportunity presents itself.For example, if I bang my shin or knee on something I might use some meditation to clear the pain and energy blockages. Since doing CT I've discovered I can instantly stop shivering if I drop into meditative breathing, its a fun novelty but I think there is a point to the shivering so dont prevent it for long.

I like the way you describe the internal versus external limits - it shows you have experienced exactly what I mean when I describe mind/body integration. During my 3rd grading which lasted 5 hours, the whole point was to first hit the physical limit then overcome it. SO when you hit the physical limit, you are instructing or willing yourself to continue b ut your body is sending all these pain signals back and your body s in effect arguing or battling with your mind. The resullt feels like a loss of co-ordination, emotional disturbances, loss of balance tec and exhaustion.

For me, getting through this barrier was achieved by a change of context, a paradigm shift. What I did was to visualise that I was holding onto a loved one dangling over a cliff and that there was no way I was going to willingly let go - my strength would have to fail first or my fingers or arms drop off. Shortly after this shift in context, my limbs felt lighter, co-ordination returned and so did energy along with a deeper meditative state in which I was more detached from the physical situation, more of an observer/controller.

I believe in some way that once the body stops protesting the mind/will, a lot of wasted energy that was used up in conflict between the body and mind is released to follow the will or intent - and my experience was that it is an immense amount of energy that can either work for you or be wasted on internal conflict. This was how I experienced mind/body integration and what it means to me.

My experience has caused me to believe that a universal truth applies universally - across all scales of magnitude, and I have read Dr. Kruse make reference to this fractal nature of physics etc. when comparing the quantum scales with the larger scales in biology. So, being that I believe truth and meaning and several layers or "depths" - on a lesser level the mind body integration can be demonstrated in a martial artist who has not learned to fully commit to the strike/block/movement because they have not learned to FULLY release the muscles opposing the movement - that there is also an example of wasted energy and reduced power.

This is kind of what I meant when I described mind/body integration as meaning "getting out of your own way".

We would use a lot of visualisations and often an individual needed a different visual for the same thing depending on what would make it more "real" for them. I the beginning your teacher guides you through these until you reach a point where you have learned enough to be creative and invent your own techniques and visuals.

Toanswer your first question last. I began it for self defence, then it grew into health, then philosohy. I found myself in the philosophy/religion section in the library often. One day I realised that I was disqualifying certain philosophies that I thought did not fit with my beliefs and after that forced myself to grab at least one book thought would be crap at each visit. This forced another paradigm shift where instead of focussing on the differences between them, I would focus on the common threads . . . and that was a huge breakthrough for me - pieces of the puzzle that previously didnt fit started to harmonise and form a bigger picture. It was about this time I started reading books on human perception, artificial intelligence and quantum effects relating to conciousness and sentience. I believe that right now the student is ready and the teacher (DK) has appeared.

If there is one meditation that I still do more than any other is is one that starts off pulling energy in from . . . its hard to describe breifly where but it is like a parallel dimension where all are one . . . . to your center and outward, clearing toxins as it goes before radiating further beyond the body and out into the world carrying compassion? connectedness? . . . When I was first taught this one my teacher warned me that people seem to be attracted to this energy like a magnet and some will unconciously suck it all up like vampires if you let them . . . . but otherwise, it can be really fun and rewarding to use it to light people up and make them feel good.

Apologies for the long posts, there are just some subjects that really get me excited

Thank you SeaHorse - nice to meet you.

### Da-mo — 2014-01-15T08:01:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kia-ora-from-new-zealand.9344/post-105736>

**Hi Josh**

> [Josh said:](/goto/post?id=105702)
>
> Welcome. Do you still practice on any level? What style did you practice, martial or for health and healing or both?

Dont know what happened to my previous reply but I'll have another go . . . .

Yes I do still practice some aspects. I do the breathing meditation and Chi manipulation - specifically:
1\. Filling the lake - increasing energy through the tricky gate (3rd eye position) into the Tantien (lower abdomen area)
2\. Small circulation- recycling through the governing vessel - pirenuem area between scrotum and anus.
3\. Ion arms
4\. Ion legs
5\. Ion back
6\. Grand circulation using Buddhist breathing - 4 stopping points at governing vessel, base of skull, solar plexus and Tantien as opposed to Taoist which only stops at base of skull and Tantein.
7\. 5 gate breathing through tricky gate (3rd eye position) palms of hands and equivalent part of feet.
8\. Talking to the immortal - an astro type meditation that connects with the divine spark
9\. Detox meditation morphing into compassion meditation.

Of these 9. is my favourite and unless Im having trouble I can go straight to that one - the others help buildup to that one if required. Each of these has a visualisation that was either taught to me or that I have modified to make work better for mysef. If you have the right teacher there comes a point at which you can begin to get creative and invent or discover things that were not taught to you. The reason 9. is my favourite is that the compassion meditation is where you radiate energy beyond your own body to the world at large. When I was taught this I was warned that people become subconciously drawn to the energy and some will suck it up like vampires if you let them. But the best thing about it is that you seem to be able to light people up with it which makes it fun and very rewarding.

I dont practise on any schedule nor do I devote time to it, it has become something that I do simultaneously while doing other things - for example if I bang my knee or shin I will use it to deal with the pain and use the pain to draw energy to the injury (pain is your freind when you can use it to train your focus onto an injury site) and again use some of the circulating meditations to clear energy blockages. If Im really onto it sometimes I might use it to give someone else a boost if they need it or if I need them to perform or communicate a little better. Prior to social occasions I often use 9. to charge up both myself and my wife and the result is that we can often light up the other people at a party or such. Once you get a few going they start vibing off eachother and energy levels skyrocket for everyone.

I've recently discovered I can instantly stop shivering during CT by just dropping into breathing based meditation - interesting effect but I think there are good reasons for shivering and so I only do it as a sort of curiousity for a few seconds at a time.

The practice was breathing based meditation and control of Chi energy with martial arts as a useful tool for mind/body integration. For me it started being about self defence, then health and finally philosophy. I view most social interactions as energy flows nowadays and that perspective makes for some interesting insights into how people share, compete for, amplify and diminish eachother's energy. The Celestine Prophecy novels are great books that provide an insightful template for viewing social interactions from this perspective.

When exploring the philosophical phase I would collect books from the library to read. One day I caught myself ruling out certain books because I did not think they would fit with my understanding. From then on I made myself select at least one book each visit that I thought I would disagree with. That small change caused me to start looking for the common threads in all the philosophies instead of the differences and a lot of the puzzle pieces that I had previoulsy discarded started falling into place. After this I started reading books on human perception, quantum effects and metaphysical effects on conciousness and sentience. Hence reading Dr. Kruse's material regarding quantum physics and energy states is very interesting for me.

> [Josh said:](/goto/post?id=105702)
>
> I still find it a challenge to recognize my resonant/internal training limit rather than my physical/external limit. The later always leaves me tired and the former always humbles me.

Those words right there make it clear to me that you have an experience based understanding of the mind/body effect. My 3rd grading was 5 hours long. The whole point of it was to go to the point of physical exhaustion and overcome it. So 3.5 hours into it and the body is protesting loudly through the nervous system against the mind, co-ordination goes out the window as the signals from both battle for energy and right of way and physical collapse seems immenent.

It was at this point that I learned that a change in context, a paradigm shift, could be used to integrate the mind and body. My body was saying if you keep doing that your arm is going to fall off - my mind was saying dont be silly, of course it wont. I then visulaised holding a loved one dangling off a cliff and how I was not going to let go until my arm actually did fall off.

This caused my breathing to return from laboured to relaxed, my limbs to feel light, my co-ordination to return and a new influx of energy. Reflecting later, it seems that we waste HUGE amounts of energy on internal conflict and that if we can integrate mind and body to act congruently we can free up large amounts of energy and power.

If you consider as I do that the nature of the universe is fractal, I also believe that universal truths apply universally and fractally and that is why things can have many layers of meaning without contradiction. For example, a less advanced layer of meaning for this mind body integration is that often a martial artist does not develop anywhere near the power he is capable of in a movement, until he learns to fully commit to the movement and fully release all the muscles opposing the movement. So that is what I mean when I say that mind/body integration is really about learning how to get out of your own way.

> [Josh said:](/goto/post?id=105702)
>
> Interesting how in the current world teachers are having trouble with students not being able to handle the practices. I figure it is a combination of the distorted resonant environment and all the rest. I have a friend in China whose teacher has gone to villages to find children that are in good shape from having been brought up on a good diet away from modern life to train in his system.

An interesting observation that I was not aware of. I have to say that my teacher had to beg and plead for months before his teacher would teach him as he was not interested in teaching anyone. I think that perhaps, in some cases, when the right student comes along it brings out the teacher in someone. That idea kind of fits with another thing I learned deeply and that was that focussing on the end goal can often prevent you from ever acheiving it, but focussing on the journey will often get you there. This proved to be a big block for breaking concrete slabs and clay bricks - the more you wanted to break it, the less likely that it would break, but shifting your intention to perfecting your technique and letting go of the result often ended in a seemingly effortless break.

My interest in horsemanship gives me another perspective on this as well. Many trainers discard horses as untrainable if they do not get the results they normally get given their methods. However, the really talented ones, the horse whisperers if you like, treat each horse as an individual coming from a different place and adjust their repertoire to fit the horse rather than trying a one size fits all approach. THese talented trainers are often able to get spectacular results from horses others have discarded as petfood.

The point is - maybe these teachers need to look to themselves to find the solution and make adjustments for what "is" instead of trying to recreate what "was".

> [Josh said:](/goto/post?id=105702)
>
> I have noticed that my body can only handle so much extra energy as I age and that diet and environment are quite important.

Now you touch on something interesting that I have discovered recently. You are maybe viewing yourself as a storage tank or a fuel tank for energy and that your capacity has diminished. Where intially in 1. above "filling the lake" or chi-ing up I would do the same thing, nowadays I use chanelling more than storage. In other words, I have developed a visualisation technique where energy flows through me rather than to me or from me. Can you see the difference here? I'm not the tank anymore - Im the fuel line. It means that so long as I can draw energy in and allow it to flow through, I dont have to store it because the universe is my fuel tank.

Hope this is not too lengthy or out there - but I have an instinct you get this stuff and its not often I get to communicate with people that either get it or can be bothered with it.

Sandy

### Da-mo — 2014-01-15T23:31:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kia-ora-from-new-zealand.9344/post-105822>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=105768)
>
> I also liked hearing the term "tricky gate" for the pineal gland. I have all sorts of plans for mine, once I get all the fluoride-induced calcification gone.

We never really related the two - and never really referred to it as the 3rd eye either - I just used that term really to identify the location on the forehead.

So far as to why we used that and called it a tricky gate, it had to do with it being a gate most difficult to use which made it a safer option for the beginner in terms of not leaving it wide open to outside influence. By comparison, the gate we caled the bahui on top of the skull was one of the last we were made aware of because it can leave you wide open - I think this had some link to why some religions cover that part of the head.

The 5 gate breathing - that is the gate in the forehead, palms and feet - think about the crucifixion of Christ and which pathways were shut down with the crown of thorns and the nails (although more accurately the nails were driven into the wrists and not the palms). Regardless of whether one believes it actually happened or not - the symbolism is still there. There are further correlations across different philosophies/religions encompassing halos (auras) and group energy dynamics (regular gatherings in churches, mosques, temples etc.). Just like with seemingly contradictory results between warm or cold adapted biology - the total picture is not one or the other - it is both, but only makes sense when you know how they link with eachother.

### sooperb — 2014-01-20T18:19:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-thought.9397/post-106298>

I should say an official Hi to the forum and site. I've actually been around for a few months absorbing the information and picking out the bits that looked relevant (and understandable lol). I have THE book and am almost all the way through. I have made a start on the reset, although OH and I dropped carbs nearly 2 years ago. I am up to face dunking and abdomen icing occasionally, eat two meals a day, gave up sugar completely nearly 3 weeks ago apart from one lapse, and would say I am doing about 80% of the programme. The bits I can't manage are breakfast within half an hour of getting up as the dog has to be walked first, I don't think I am hitting the target for breakfast protein but then again, I need to find somewhere with a protein chart to be sure, any suggestions welcome. I add CO into as much as I can, cook with it in favour of anything else and sometimes eat it just off the spoon although I'm not that keen lol. Otherwise a lot of the protocol is what I've been doing for a long time, I love avocados, I eat nuts but am careful not to eat too many, we are able to buy unpasteurized butter and lots of different cheese although I don't really eat that much. Would cheese be ok for breakfast protein or not?

I have a lot of questions but I'm not sure where the appropriate place is to ask them. I browse other paleo sites, MDA is pretty cool, and have a paleo cookbook. There is some contradiction but for now, in order not to complicate matters I am sticking with Jack (lucky fella eh!)

I have finally started a journal, well, the intro, that took me a while.

I am 5'5" and weigh in at 186 lbs/13st 4.

The downside for me is the weight issue. Albert has pointed out that that is not the starting point and I really appreciated his clarification. That aside and the fact that I am basically pretty healthy but not fit, I would have expected my efforts to have produced some results, weight wise, by now.

### Gagnrad — 2014-01-21T08:09:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-thought.9397/post-106430>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=106298)
>
> ... I don't think I am hitting the target for breakfast protein but then again, I need to find somewhere with a protein chart to be sure, any suggestions welcome.

There are online services that will calculate this for you, though it gets tiresome using them for more than a day or two. The most widely used is FitDay.

Obviously, absolute accuracy isn't important and it's probably easier to get a rough handle on it terms of things like "a piece of beef the size of a pack of cards = around x grams of protein", "a fillet of fish the size of your hand =" ... and so on.

If you want something on paper, there is a set of tables in the back of Nora Gedgaudas's _Primal Body, Primal Mind_, which should be on Amazon. There's another in Ron Rosedale's _The Rosedale Diet_. (It's probably the same one: I think Dr. Rosedale was an advisor for PBPM.) If an electronic copy will do, there should be tables online, too: try an internet search under something like "grams protein chart".

As a rough idea, 2 ounces of cooked bacon would contain about 21g of protein; two large eggs around 13g. (There's fat and water in these foods, of course, so although there's 2 ounces -- i.e. 55g -- of bacon in this example less than half of it is protein.)

I think Dr. Kruse prescribes 50g of protein at breakfast on the Leptin Reset, but I haven't checked recently. You would have to go some to hit that target. As you can see, 2 ounces of bacon and 2 eggs only gets you up to 34g.

### Gagnrad — 2014-01-23T17:00:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-thought.9397/post-106761>

Anyone who wants a set of tables, here's one among the many out there. This is one is done on the basis of grams of protein per 100 grams of foodstuff:

[http://apjcn.nhri.org.tw/server/info/books-phds/books/foodfacts.../html/data/data2d.html](http://apjcn.nhri.org.tw/server/info/books-phds/books/foodfacts.../html/data/data2d.html)

The tables in this case are actually GIFs (image files). But one could find the specific table one wants -- say "seafood" or "meat" -- and save the GIF out. Or paste it in a word-processing program and print from there.

... or perhaps not. It says "per 100 g" but then there are other weights in brackets after each food item.

Here's another set:

[http://www.health-alternatives.com/meat-protein-nutrition-chart.html](http://www.health-alternatives.com/meat-protein-nutrition-chart.html)

And another:

[http://www.ketogenic-diet-resource.com/protein-chart.html](http://www.ketogenic-diet-resource.com/protein-chart.html)

### tellmisty — 2014-02-24T03:15:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-thought.9397/post-111880>

Why do you have to walk the dog first? Perhaps there is a way you can manage that ... I pull everything together the night before so it's easy to eat. I have to take cytomel immediately and then wait 30 min and start eating right at the 30-minute mark; whew! I just rearranged my entire morning schedule to accommodate the Leptin Reset and the other protocols ... this is too important! I have to take care of me so I can take care of everyone else ... just something to think about.

If you're interested in joining the Optimal Klubs, you can find info here: [www.jackkruse.com/register](http://www.jackkruse.com/register).

### sooperb — 2014-02-24T18:07:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-thought.9397/post-111946>

I hear you Misty. I'll think of a way, maybe just by saying, eat first, dog walk after.

### Andrea — 2014-02-25T19:15:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-thought.9397/post-112136>

Very simplified, most meats contain about 20% (by weight) protein. So 100 g of meat will give you about 20 g of protein. However, some sea creatures are lower on protein, like oysters with around 10%. Shrimps are at around 20% though.
Eggs contain about 12% protein.

So, for 50 g of protein for breakfast you could eat for instance a 250 g hamburger, or 500 g of oysters, or 8 eggs (depends on the size)...

### caroline — 2014-02-26T01:15:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-thought.9397/post-112188>

I find now that food is not the focus of my world ...... I just grab something and move on - too many things I want to do..
Food used to be my job and my passion ....24/7

now - If I could have some oysters and shrimp and lobster and salmon and scallops ....I am a happy camper! So - still passion to the max, but now directed to a healthy mind and body ..... and I would lite up like a Christmas tree if I have all that seafood on a plate at the same time!

# Thread: Starting my Leptin Reset diet!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-my-leptin-reset-diet.9415/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mariaelaine — 2014-01-21T18:10:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-my-leptin-reset-diet.9415/post-106476>

Yesterday I turned 33! Maybe it's because my birthday is in January, or maybe it's just my perspective on birthdays, but I've always treated my birthday as my personal New Year holiday. On my birthday for the last three years, I've made the same resolution: to try to be the healthiest me possible.

I'm a moderately athletic person who enjoys boxing (only bags, not people), various aerobic activities and over the last couple of months have begun jogging. I eat pretty cleanly as well. Clean and humane meats, eggs, veggies, whole grains with the occasional dining out cheat. Very little alcohol, no smoking or recreational drugs and no junk/processed foods. The way I eat now is very different than they way I was raised and has taken years of learning and developing good habits, learning to cook and some seriously creative financing as I'm on a very low-income budget.

My good habits (and good genes based on my parents) have given me a generally healthy disposition. As far as the basics of blood pressure, cholesterol, immune response, digestive system, I'm pretty health and always have been. What drives me towards a change is that despite a baseline of general health and an active lifestyle, I've always been overweight. Since adolescence I've carried more body fat than I would seem to need. I also have a good deal of muscle mass, I'm long-limbed, and of a medium build to begin with, but still carry a lot of weight that I'm positive I don't need.

As I get further from 30, I realize that I'll only continue to put on the pounds of fat unless I find a better balance of nutrition that works for me. That's why I'm starting a Leptin Reset diet using the Primal Blueprint Diet as a guideline. It's day two and I'm going to keep a journal using this forum a couple of days a week to help chart my experience and stay focused. I also look forward to reading others' experiences on the forum.

I'm logging my food in the Lose It app for iPhone to keep track of the protein, fat, and carb counts for the day.

Here's to 33!

### caroline — 2014-01-22T00:18:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-my-leptin-reset-diet.9415/post-106521>

Happy belated Birthday Maria! and welcome. There is a journal section - if you cant find it please let me know.

Did you join the free 90 day reset? It would be perfect for you. There is a yellow banner at the top of the home page to register. Dr. Kruse just did the first webinar yesterday - if you join now I think you can still hear it. Please make that a priority - it was a great webinar on epi pale etc, .... please don't miss it.

If you ever need help - just holler!

# Thread: G'day from down under.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday-from-down-under.9549/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ben1983 — 2014-02-02T12:45:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday-from-down-under.9549/post-107882>

Hi all...I just want to introduce myself and give a quick rundown on where I'm at health wise and what I've been doing about it. I am a 30 year old male living in Sydney, Australia. I have been paleo for nearly two years. I have been fairly consistent with my diet during that two year period, however, I have had bouts of massive sugar cravings that last anywhere between several days to several weeks. I haven't had any cravings now for quite some time. I haven't been eating any seafood (until last week) as I had been scared off by much that has been said about radiation in seafood. I read a comment by Jack on facebook that said that much of the talk to do with radiation in seafood is overblown and that's part of the reason why I have decided to start eating oysters (2 a day) and salmon (twice a week).
Up until about a month ago, most of my health concerns were quite minor....slight brain fog from time to time, trouble sleeping on occasion, and the odd day of feeling irritable for no apparent reason, but all in all not too bad.....this changed at the start of this year when I noticed my energy dipping considerably about halfway into my work shifts. This was also combined with a feeling of irritability/agitation. This has progressed to the point where I have huge energy crashes in which I basically just need to sit or lay down and do nothing. I cant even be bothered talking when it hits me. Some days are worst than others. I have never experienced anything like this before, and as far as I can tell, nothing has really changed in my diet as I eat the same things most weeks including beef, beef liver and kidneys, plenty of varieties of vegeteables, eggs, butter, coconut oil....and everything is fresh, organic, free range etc etc. I take very few supplements...I take a synbiotic and a wholefoods multi and I also take one to two diatomaceous earth baths each week for detox.
I have no idea what to do, which is what led me to this site for help. If anyone has any tips as far as where to start reading, then feel free to share. I am looking forward to chatting with some like-minded folks.

### caroline — 2014-02-02T13:16:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday-from-down-under.9549/post-107885>

Hi Ben and welcome! first of all - please start a journal. Dr. K. loves journals ... he needs context if he is able to offer suggestions. It helps you with commitment and we can get to know you!

Go to the top of the page and click on forum and then go down 6 categories and you will see the journal section.

Have you joined in with the free 90 day Optimal reset? Have you read Dr. K's book? Epi-Paleo RX. It will give you a great overview of all of this - available on Amazon in kindle or paper.

The very most important thing is to be aware of the non native EMF in your life ...home, work, car. Did something change in your life at the start of the year?

Have you listened to Dr. K's podcasts? check the home page. He talks a lot about EMF in most of them. Food is only 10% and it seems like you are doing well there.

Have you had labs done? There is a great section in his book about labs.

Please keep reading and asking question....

Are you doing the leptin reset? The BAB will knock out sugar cravings.

I don't know what those baths are? when did you start them? I wonder if you are experiencing detox from them?
If you aren't methylating properly you will be reabsorbing toxins back into your tissues and that could make you very ill.

There is also a free webinar by Dr. Tim Jackson on healing your hormones - in the blog index on the home page.

Sorry - this is a lot to throw at you! I will stop for now and let you catch your breath! There are a lot of great people here working on our own optimal journey and everyone lends a hand .... ask away ...

### caroline — 2014-02-02T13:19:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday-from-down-under.9549/post-107886>

BTW - I am in Wollongong ... we are practically neighbours!

There is a huge thread in the cave ....the aussie donut hole...

### Gagnrad — 2014-02-02T16:24:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday-from-down-under.9549/post-107896>

> [Ben1983 said:](/goto/post?id=107882)
>
> This has progressed to the point where I have huge energy crashes in which I basically just need to sit or lay down and do nothing. I cant even be bothered talking when it hits me. Some days are worst than others.

I should think it certainly could be hypothyroidism -- but doubtless a lot of other things, too. There are a few other signs that tend to go along with hypothyroidism, such as a tendency to lose the outer third of the eyebrows.

I should go to your doctor and tell him or her that you're experiencing extreme fatigue. They'll then take blood samples and run a battery of tests to see what they can find.

### Ben1983 — 2014-02-03T16:52:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday-from-down-under.9549/post-108107>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=107885)
>
> Hi Ben and welcome! first of all - please start a journal. Dr. K. loves journals ... he needs context if he is able to offer suggestions. It helps you with commitment and we can get to know you!
>
> Go to the top of the page and click on forum and then go down 6 categories and you will see the journal section.
>
> Have you joined in with the free 90 day Optimal reset? Have you read Dr. K's book? Epi-Paleo RX. It will give you a great overview of all of this - available on Amazon in kindle or paper.
>
> The very most important thing is to be aware of the non native EMF in your life ...home, work, car. Did something change in your life at the start of the year?
>
> Have you listened to Dr. K's podcasts? check the home page. He talks a lot about EMF in most of them. Food is only 10% and it seems like you are doing well there.
>
> Have you had labs done? There is a great section in his book about labs.
>
> Please keep reading and asking question....
>
> Are you doing the leptin reset? The BAB will knock out sugar cravings.
>
> I don't know what those baths are? when did you start them? I wonder if you are experiencing detox from them?
> If you aren't methylating properly you will be reabsorbing toxins back into your tissues and that could make you very ill.
>
> There is also a free webinar by Dr. Tim Jackson on healing your hormones - in the blog index on the home page.
>

Hi Caroline, I have signed up to the 90 Day Optimal Reset. I have Jack's book, but so far I have only skimmed through certain parts of it. I am taking 10 days off work (annual leave) starting this Friday, so I'll have a good chance then to read it properly, and to read a lot in the forum, too. I'm primarily taking the time off work just to see if it helps my body to recover. I'm just going to take walks on the beach, have massages and read each day.
I'm not currently doing the leptin reset (as far as I am aware of). I am basically just eating paleo with the addition of more seafood and bone broths into my diet as of last week. What does "BAB" stand for?
Actually, now that you mention it, I started those baths only a short time before I started to feel strange. How do I ensure that I am methylating properly so that the toxins are released from my body and not just reabsorbed?
I love the gong! I lived in Figtree for 5 years when I was studying at TAFE and UOW. I still get down there about once a month. What part of the gong are you in?

Also, I may do this as a separate forum question, but I wanted to find out if any Aussies have sourced out which various places we can get all of the recommended lab tests done? I would really like to see where I'm at in regards to what the labs are showing instead of just being able to say "I have huge crashes in energy".

### caroline — 2014-02-04T01:16:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday-from-down-under.9549/post-108204>

Hi Ben .... I live in the North Gong - close to the beach! This is probably the nicest place I have ever lived - I love it here!

I would take a semi educated guess and say to stop those baths ASAP.

Please read Dr. Jacksons blog and listen to his webinar. Read Dr. K's hormones 101 and 102 blogs.

BAB = Big ass Breakfast

this should be your routine...

get up and go outside and look at the sun
have a BAB within 30 minutes of rising \[50gr of protein\]
leave at least 4 hours between meals and before bed

no artificial lites after sunset

R/O water

do you have blue blocker glasses?

I can help you with labs when you are ready - read Jack's book first!

what else do you want to know?

don't forget to get outside as much as possible with as little clothes as possible \[nude is best!\] barefoot - go to the ocean!
hug a tree or two.

Google movnat ...and Erwan Lecorre.....

# Thread: Hello Jack and All.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-jack-and-all.9652/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AllyCat — 2014-02-08T04:33:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-jack-and-all.9652/post-109009>

I wanted to post this in the "Ask Jack" section but I'm still doing the obligatory readings so I have a lot of questions or none at all, depends on how you look at it.

I'm 25. I live in Canada \*brrrr\*.
I was born in Ukraine, when I was 6 I moved to Israel and then finally at 16 I came to Canada,
so when reading Dr. Kruse's post about epigenetics it really struck a cord with me. I've changed my diet drastically so many times, my body become more and more confused.

I developed acne really early on (13-14) and it got increasingly worse. In my 20s it improved but never was under control until I cleaned up my diet, i.e., read Mark Sisson's Primal Blueprint.

I was also skinny in my youth, almost too skinny. Then closer to adolescence was getting heavier (but still normal). The thing is, in the summers I would go to visit my grandma so my diet would constantly shift.
The weirdest thing, whenever I would go to Ukraine all of a sudden my skin would clear up even though I was eating wheat and dairy (which, NOW, I think is the culprit to all my problems).

When I returned I was always 15lbs heavier but quickly lost it all, and gained the acne back
It was like that until the last time I went to visit my grandma. I was 19 and when I came back losing weight was no longer as easy.

Add in Tetracycline for the acne, birth control, junk food and yo yo dieting I gained more and more weight. Then I discovered I had PCOS and weight was impossible to lose and everything I ate gave me indigestion.

I went paleo and lost weight. Very very slowly. about 5lbs in 6 months. Added fasting, exercise and went really low carb and low calorie and lost enough to feel "normal" again. Then got depressed (?!) and gained it all back. Now I've discovered that according to the BMI I'm obese... And I was startled. I tried to get back on the proverbial horse but kept failing.

The cravings are so difficult to deal with, the low carb makes me fee lethargic and I was losing hope. It seemed that what worked before stopped working. It's like I couldn't digest anything!

I read the GAPs diet book and it made sense to me and probiotics helped a little, and then through PaleoCon I found this place!

The more I read the more it makes sense. I always wondered why I would break out eating wheat here but in Ukraine it didn't matter (though I still gained weight). It's difficult to attribute my bad genes to my mother/ grandmother. My mom has great skin, "normal" skin while mine is oily sensitive and acne prone. She can eat A LOT and never gain weight. She's always been thin. So is my grandmother. No problems with ingesting dairy either (which seem to cause me problems). I think the Ukrainan dairy I consumed could have been unpasteurized (grandparents had a lot of farmer friends, they would boil it but it wasn't "processed").

I don't know... Just venting, I guess. Anyone has any ideas about why I didn't win in the gene lottery? ;D

### caroline — 2014-02-08T05:30:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-jack-and-all.9652/post-109012>

Hi AllyCat and welcome!
It would be great if you could transfer this to a journal. Dr k. Likes journals because he needs context to be able to offer any suggestions.
Go to the top of the page and click on forum and go down 6 categories to journals.

Have you been to the home page? There are quite a few podcasts that Dr. kruse has done and , of course, all of his blogs.
Do you have Jacks book? The Epi-Paleo RX. Available from amazon. It will give you a great overview of all of this.

There is a free 90 day optimal reset that we are doing right now - you can still join.

You can only "Ask Jack" if you are a silver or gold member and then you will have access to the monthly webinars.

Have you looked at the easy start guides?

Keep reading and asking questions!

Like many others I had my tonsils removed as a child (tonsillectomy). It seems there is little information out there regarding what we can do nutritionally to address this void in the immune system. Any ideas?

### sooperb — 2014-02-25T18:46:47+0200
