### caroline — 2014-02-26T01:46:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/life-without-tonsils.9906/post-112192>

Hi Surfa and welcome! You are a surfer? where are you located?

btw - sorry don't know the answer to your question ...... maybe an "ask Jack"

### Hope — 2014-02-26T02:43:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/life-without-tonsils.9906/post-112201>

Surfa, I think anything you can do to follow the protocols should help strengthen your immunity. Do you have symptoms or issues now? If you can surf, you must be strong! Surfing is something I can only dream of. If I was skinnier than I am now. No desire to show my big behind on a board I'd fall off on. But I digress.

Have you read about the redox potential? How does yours stack up? The better your battery, the better your system.

If people can live well without spleens, I bet you can live well without tonsils. I have no medical idea, this is just all what my gut is saying....and what i'm learning here. My immunity has been pretty yucky for other reasons (bad magnetism and biotoxins).
Welcome, indeed!

### sooperb — 2014-02-26T16:15:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/life-without-tonsils.9906/post-112257>

My daughter has managed quite well without hers but she gets a never ending stream of colds. That said, she is overweight and thinks good nutritional advice comes on the back of food packets sigh. People wouldn't live long without their spleens if they didn't take a lot of pills though Hope.

### Andrea — 2014-02-26T19:42:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/life-without-tonsils.9906/post-112311>

I wanted to say that I have managed quite well without my tonsils, but then I'm not a health wonder either...

Nevertheless, I used to have colds twice a year - until I started on a low carb high fat diet about 4 years ago. Since then I have had a cold only once (and my body had a very, very good reason to get that cold that time: stress+injury+stomach issues+long airplane trip with lots of coughing and sniffling people onboard...).
Other than the change of diet I supplement with vitamin D from September to April, the dark period here in northern Europe.
So, despite my other problems, my immune system can't be too crappy!

Anyway, I think I agree with Hope about redox potential.

### sooperb — 2014-03-11T16:55:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/life-without-tonsils.9906/post-114801>

I'm especially healthy, I tend to ail nothing. I am very overweight though. I have tonsils, I don't get colds. My daughter doesn't have tonsils and gets colds a lot. She prefers them to the pain and discomfort of tonsillitis. My best friend revealed only yesterday that she doesn't have tonsils, she gets many colds and coughs and chest infections. My husband doesn't have tonsils, he gets colds that don't infect me. I'm beginning to understand about organ removal not necessarily being a good thing. I guess if you don't have tonsils and your inflammation levels are high, you're going to be sicker than most. I don't have my appendix, I can't say I miss it as such, I do wonder though what it contributes? As for my gallbladder, that's gone and I was glad at the time, now I will be forever wondering just how much my weight issues are related to its absence.

# Thread: First time posting, new member.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-time-posting-new-member.10144/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6

### Infinusss — 2014-03-13T19:13:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-time-posting-new-member.10144/post-115212>

Hi, everyone. I was introduced to everything Kruse through hearing him on the Bulletproof Exec podcast. I became interested in his theories and protocols, so I decided to come join the forums and give the Leptin Rx and CT Rx a shot.

Also, within the last week I've been trying to eat way more seafood, such as wild-caught salmon, muscles, clams, shrimp and sushi-grade raw tuna.

# Thread: Hey Everybody!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-everybody.10153/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matty — 2014-03-14T02:11:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-everybody.10153/post-115289>

Aspiring to be a starfish. Spreading knowledge and love. Helping others. Kaizen. Awesomeness.

That pretty much sums me up.

I was 300lbs back in 1999 and dropped to 170 in about a year. Pure determination and very little knowledge. In retrospect I did a lot of damage by doing it wrong and not fixing my brain first. In the years since I've made it my mission to improve myself everyday, to learn something new everyday that will help me make better choices, and to help someone else everyday.

Part of that was to gain back some of the muscle I'd lost after I lost so much weight. I got certified as a personal trainer after spending a few years lifting weights, in a quest to learn more. Now a happy 185, size 32, 5' 11" - I still exercise but not As much, but more intense. I walk a lot, fast on occasion and try to implement everything Dr Kruse recommends.

I've always been big on the idea of returning to a way of life resembling our ancestors. My paternal grandparents from Scotland, and my maternal GPs Mennonite, I grew up on healthy food and fairly traditional fare. Living in Canada I have always loved the cold so CT fit my lifestyle. I have tried to eat ancestraly for quite some time and have read a ton about evolution, Paleo nutrition, biology, etc so when I came across Jack's blog I was hooked immediately.

I've since immersed myself in this blog and forum and finally feel like I should say hello and introduce myself.

I'm a Daddy, first and foremost. 5 yr old Etta and 3 yr old Macy are my everything and why I am so dedicated to learning more about health. I am married to an amazing, beautiful exercise physiologist, Erin, who I am lucky to have but whose eyes are not yet open to this new perception of health. She's coming around one thing at a time, but is in awesome shape so not trying to fix anything.

I have worked as a personal trainer, but my career is in Social Services. I got to 300 lbs through a really fun combination of alcohol, cocaine, cigarettes, eating fast food at 2am, and playing rock n roll in bars. Mostly singing in metal bands, little drums little guitar. My quest to be a rock star left me NOT feeling or looking like one. This was 16-19 years old, dropped out of school, gave up 10 years of football for sex, drugs & rock n roll. No exercise, combined with the above mentioned lifestyle choices was devastating to my body.

So I work in social services trying to help people not make those decisions if they're young, or digging themselves out of the mire if it's too late. I am surrounded by overweight, unhealthy people at work, both clients and colleagues. So I try to pay forward the great fortune I've had in helping myself, and for all the knowledge, love and patience given to me by helpful people like Dr Kruse.

My mother has ALS though she's surviving better than DRs expected, so I'm always trying to help her. My dad had a stroke early in life but has survived and made many changes to improve himself. I'm cognizant of the risks in my genetic inheritance, and learning as much about epigenetics as I can.

I am always learning and adapting. No dogma is too strong to question. I will never stop searching for knowledge and will never stop learning or caring about others. The better I am, the more of myself I can give to those around me, those who need me, those who love me.

I just want to be the best me I can be, Optimal if you will, and I couldn't care less what others may think of my tinfoil hat. Wink wink.

### caroline — 2014-03-14T03:50:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-everybody.10153/post-115302>

Hi Matty - and welcome! Could you transfer this to the journal section? - but don't worry about it if you can't!

It is so great to have all that context! thanks .... you will help so many others.

Where are you in Canada?

Yep .... we all to need to pay this forward......

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-everybody.10153/post-115306>

# Thread: Hello my new friends!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-my-new-friends.10208/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mare — 2014-03-17T22:44:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-my-new-friends.10208/post-116029>

Hi Everyone, I, like all of you have been on the hunt to feel better/optimal. I'm diagnosed Hashi's and have been battling to get well for several years. I'm a single mom with a wicked 14 y/o boy that I love to death and I'm hoping that by finding my way on this journey, I can help him avoid some pain down the road...

Here's a nutshell of my progression thus far:

-   Diagnosed Hashi's 2007-synthroid prescribed, continued to decline, extreme muscle/joint pain, headaches, brain fog
-   August 2010- Found lil' article linking gluten to hashi's quit the stuff and within' 24 hrs, almost all pain resolved (found later that dairy in certain amounts causes' me fits too) Quittin the G was like hell on earth for this philly girl weaned on cheesesteaks, pizza and tastykakes!
-   I eat a fairly clean/paleoish diet, but I'm at a plateau...
-   So basically all the muscle pain is gone, but left with less than stellar energy/stamina and I feel a bit dumber than I once believed....things just take longer to sink in, it stinks
-   Chronic constipation, since childhood (Not breastfed?) Take tons of mag, but it's hit or miss on if I get a movement
-   Last year learned via a Genova labs stool test I have a level 4+ yeast. Spent the last year chasing that beast and after about 10k in alt docs and supplements I don't think it made a dent.
-   My other devilish symptom is cystic acne, it comes and goes and is less severe since altering my diet, but none the less a total downer. PMS, FIBROID at 25, Estrogen dominance?? have labs if anyone can help me interpret??
-   Mom diagnosed with BC at 55 now at 63 it's moved to bone, she's suffered her entire life with hormonal disorders, endo, fibroids, acne, now breast cancer that's taking her life. It's a horror to watch.

Tried to make it to the point! I'm fascinated by the EMF and CT connections. Dr. Jack Kruse is my hero even though he hurts my head daily!! I'm trying to make this very technical info work on a daily basis, would be most interested in the links that give specifics on CT duration, etc. This is the one I dread the most. My temp runs at 97 degrees, constantly bundled up, so the submerging in cold is tough for me, but I will do it to get well...any tips on getting started would be much appreciated

I look forward to joining you all on this path.
Mare

### caroline — 2014-03-17T23:22:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-my-new-friends.10208/post-116037>

Hi Mare and welcome! Please start a journal so we can all follow your journey! and as you probably already know - Dr. K. needs context if he is able to offer suggestions to you. You could just transfer this to the journal section.

Please think about becoming a "Gold" member and then you have access to all the webinars and live Q&A whenever it is convenient for you. Think of it as an amazing investment in your health and future and that of your precious son.

Of course - it isn't necessary - we will all support you in any way we can.

Welcome to our world of brain Pain! This is a great journey to optimal health!

Hi Caroline, Thanks so much for the warm welcome! I'm hoping to join the GOld club soon, just lost my job so I'm being careful now with money, but will definitely look into that membership asap.

This is where my feeling dumber part comes in. When you say add context, what exactly do you mean? I would love any help I can get from Dr. Kruse, I'm feeling pretty alone and frustrated right now. Everything I've tried ends with no changes to the constipation, skin issues and fatigue...

I will def check out the C/T protocol, I think I saw something on face dunks in the forum, but don't recall from Dr....that would be my preference for sure..how long do you do them to start?

I will attempt to move this to the journal section, I"m assuming that where we provide progress updates....

Thanks you again! I'm feeling a little better just having someone on the other line that gets the battle
M-

They like their oysters here in Chastown, so I'll be indulging in them more often...I've been avoiding seafood because my D.O. was concerned with high mercury.

And Steph I hear you on the bread, I'm still waiting for the day I don't crave that Amoroso roll with my cheesesteak!! You're right though pain free is a better option!

Can anyone tell me how I can determine if I'm Leptin Resistant?

My Reverse t3 was 194 in march, 13, my new doc did not pull it but i have no reason to believe things are much different..

### Inger — 2014-03-23T08:15:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glad-to-join-in-im-drnoknow-joshs-dad-55-years-of-psychitric-practice-45-years-of-meditation.10288/post-117324>

Welcome Drnoknow (lol) - nice

To your question... hmm. Maybe the strongest one is I am more centered now. Actually this is pretty awesome!!! Like something in me is so peaceful, I cannot even explain but I like a it a lot. It is almost a physical feeling around my heart and abdomen area. It can be thrown off by something that happens but i regain it fast... like such a strong well-being feeling.
Oh man maybe I understood your question totally wrong?  Anyways, I think life is so much fun - it is like increased love to life. And yeah some increased pain thrown in but it is like the pain belong to the increased joy too so it does not really matter. So - maybe in the end I just feel more alive? And I like that.... I enjoy so much to feel alive - that is.

I feel all the natural darkness and seafood and sunlight has made me more sensitive to life. and, I see more possibilities, like my view is broadening?

### Steph Corby — 2014-03-26T22:38:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glad-to-join-in-im-drnoknow-joshs-dad-55-years-of-psychitric-practice-45-years-of-meditation.10288/post-118135>

Welcome! RE: Subjective experience of life since going EpiPaleo - Well, I thought I knew my body and its reactions to things pretty well BEFORE going Epi-Paleo, but the sensitivity has intensified. Im a lot more scientific about finding causality, and notice the effect of even a tiny change (such as forgetting to turn off wi-fi at night, or too many carbs) as soon as it occurs.

In general- feel much awesome-er all around - little aches and pains are gone, more energy. Also I feel and look younger as well.

Greetings All,
I am very new to most of the information on this amazing website and forum. I was led here by an interest in Leptin, after searching for ways I could improve my health. I have training in craniosacral therapy, massage, and nutrition, but this is whole new ball game. I have been on the GAPS diet for over six months, with less than stellar results, am familiar with Paleo diet etc. and I just finished reading the Epi-Paleo Rx and the Leptin Rx, getting started post. Now trying to figure out where to go!
**Age: 34 (35 in four days)
Sex:** F**
Joined JackKruse.com:** March 2014
**Location:** Minneapolis, MN
**Dx:**
**Surgeries:**LEEP 2000
**Current Rx:**
**Current Therapies / Protocols:**
**Health WINS:**haven't really started yet!
**Health Goals:** to have lots of energy and zest for life
**My Optimal Journal:**

### caroline — 2014-04-06T01:18:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-intro.10452/post-119975>

Welcome Sunshine! what a lovely name for a beautiful young woman!!

Please start a journal in the journal section ..... Dr. K. likes journals so he has context if he wants to offer suggestions.

Do you have Dr' K's book? The Epi Paleo RX ..... it is a great resource to get all of this more easily at first.

Have you listened to Jack's podcasts? they are great too for fast and easy info!

just start the leptin RX ....and then go from there...

your day should look like this..

rise and go outside and look at the sun ... barefoot and as little clothes as possible

Have a BAB within 30 minutes of rising

leave at least 4 hours between meals and before bed ..... absolutely no snacking!

drink only bottled water

avoid fake light after sunset ..... do you have blue blocker glasses?

what is your non native EMF exposure at home? at work?

Sleep is hugely important!

Grounding and sunshine is hugely important!

What can we help you with? we all try to help each other.....and Dr. K. keeps an eye out so we don't self destruct!!!

Follow Kyrakitty's journal .... she has a similar background I think

### sunshine — 2014-04-06T20:11:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-intro.10452/post-120074>

Thank you so much for the welcome! I am starting the leptin reset tomorrow and I have read the book. I am really good with all of it, will start implementing as much as possible. I don't have blue blockers yet and its been really cold here this winter in MN, but as it gets nicer I am willing to go outside with less clothing! My middle name is literally Sunshine, and I need way more of it! I will start a journal and will be getting some labs done in a couple weeks.
I look forward to interacting and learning on this site, for myself and my family (I have two girls, 2 and 9yrs and a self-employed filmmaker hubby-he'll be the biggest challenge to reducing EMF...).
aside: Are there posts that I can't seem to find about how to utilize seafoods for our health while working on preserving the integrity of the oceans? i.e. I have avoided eating seafood because of overfishing and pollution. I know there are sustainable sources, but if there's a conversation about that here, I would love to be a part of that.
Many thanks!

### Inger — 2014-04-06T21:20:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-intro.10452/post-120088>

Welcome Sunshine! Do not forget nude tanning

I think mussels and oysters are very ecological, they actually help the oceans recover, and they do not pollute at all even if farmed

### nonchalant — 2014-04-08T21:06:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-intro.10452/post-120421>

Welcome Sunshine! You have picked the best screen name possible!

# Thread: Seeking Local folks for a Jack Kruse Mastermind Group!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-local-folks-for-a-jack-kruse-mastermind-group.10983/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kyrakitty — 2014-06-03T10:31:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-local-folks-for-a-jack-kruse-mastermind-group.10983/post-129523>

I want to start a local mastermind group of no more than 4 people to study Jacks work in depth to help me/the group to seriously focus on assimilating the old material and then keeping up with the new blogs & webinars.

I want the group small because it will be easier for us to get together schedule wise with less people. Having someone hold me accountable to read through material knowing that we can help each other understand things when we get stuck feels encouraging as I sometimes get discouraged with not understanding many concepts.

My dream is that all the people in my group are smarter than me as they certainly will elevate me and be motivating for me to step up into "who I really am" LOL.... Actually only a handful of requirements:

#1 is to have a burning desire to find an organized way, like creating a study guide, to work through learning the concepts.

#2 we be committed to showing up to the dates we agree on to meet.

#3 all have studied the agreed upon info to be dissected and assimilated.

#4 enjoy teaching the subjects were working on when a member asks for help

Lastly I'm more interested in focusing on helping each other learn Jacks material vs spending our meeting time working on how it applies to our individual N=1. I don't mean our n=1 issues shouldn't come up ever but rather I want to focus on learning the material first and foremost.

I live in San Mateo county, California, specifically in Foster City. Cities close to me are: Belmont, San Carlos, Palo Alto, Redwood City, Woodside, Menlo Park, Atherton, Stanford, Mountain View, Burlingame, Hillsbourogh, Millpitas & San Bruno. SF is relatively close too but bad traffic makes it far.

I am fine to open my home for us to meet at or we can meet at a library or other members homes.

If you're interested or have any input respond here or PM me.

Betsy


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Hello again! Can I please get some help with my labs?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-again-can-i-please-get-some-help-with-my-labs.11521/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 39
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Tms — 2014-08-18T16:49:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-again-can-i-please-get-some-help-with-my-labs.11521/post-138165>

I say hello again, because I was on this thread before but dropped off. I would say I am still new.
I don't have much money for good labs and have a super high deductible. Been working with a doctor on my plan for about 8 months. He dx with hypothyroid and low testosterone. He put me on bio identical testosterone but it did nothing so I stopped it. I am still on 130 grains of nature thyroid but don't see much change there. I've been looking over my labs and the numbers seem strange to me. Maybe there are fine but I just want to be sure I'm getting the right treatment. I tried iodine supplementation but has a terrible detox reaction so stopped it. I would try again though with the right supplementation support... Which I did not do thoroughly enough. I am supplementing with selenium now. Hoping to gather some money to do Genova test so I can get my rt3 number.

Symptoms:
Up early.. Cortisol issues
Mood dysregulation
Anxiety
Digestive/ leaky gut stuff
Chronic fatigue
No sex drive
Hard to lose weight although at an average weight (5'6, 145)

Labs 12/11/13:
Ferritin 13
Testosterone 37
DHEA 316.4
Free t3 2.52
Free t4 0.62
Tsh 1.77

Labs: 3/5/14:
No DHEA numbers
No ferritin numbers
Testosterone 35
T3 2.44
T4 0.72
Tsh 0.72

Labs 6/8/14
DHEA 205.1
Testosterone 23
T3 3.04
T4 1.14
TSH 0.19

I really appreciate any help!
Thanks
TMS

### caroline — 2014-08-20T00:48:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-again-can-i-please-get-some-help-with-my-labs.11521/post-138330>

Don't forget about frozen seafood .... it can often be fresher than stuff sitting in a seafood counter - because it is flash frozen on ships when caught.

Buy whole fish and then use the frames and heads etc for stock....

Develope a relationship with a fish monger/market - there are plenty of less expensive seafood ..... like mussels etc.
Check out Chinese markets

I often buy salmon offcuts - from when they are trimming the salmon to make them look beautiful and perfect for the fish counter!

You must have a Costco etc????

smart meter ....on your house.....

the leptin reset is about timing ..... you need to reset the rolex in your brain .... maybe re read that blog....

What about water? are you drinking good spring water?

>
> I LOVE raw meat and raw fish!!!!! I could eat a slab of raw salmon or steak tartare everyday! Doesn't the source matter?

I try to find the best source always, grassfed, wild.... but i have eaten farmed salmon raw that has been many days in my fridge too.. and old raw mackerel.. But sure fresher is better!
I eat almost only raw... everything! I love it and if you love it too... it is going to be great for your health
when we cook stuff the bacteria will die.. and we need them for our gut, lots of bacteria! Raw is so juicy and good tasting, and there are many delicious recipes too to make of raw meat and seafood. I very much eat the same stuff all the time I love it that much. Just had 5 big oysters and 250 g raw salmon for breakfast, so good

You need to watch out for the smart meter... they are not good for health at all. Very bad in fact. It can give many of the symptoms you mention you suffer from.....

### kcehealth — 2014-08-28T13:41:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ken-from-vancouver-canada.11607/post-139136>

Very interesting podcasts. Caught my attention for sure. Phase 21 of a lifetime of phases.
Psoriasis (down 85%), diabetes (in remission), getting down on weight -50lbs. 25to go but stalled for 8months when this week I went to step on a scale and get body comp checked. No muscle loss but down 10lbs since I last tested 2weeks ago.
Backpacking SE Asia for the last year. Keto for the past month and no rice for a year... In a region of rice. Hehehehe
Interested in the whole emf / blue light issues... My lady is always complaining about tired eyes.
Well... Off to read and lurk around the forum.

### kcehealth — 2014-08-29T02:59:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ken-from-vancouver-canada.11607/post-139200>

On occasion. Right now we're backpacking SE Asia (1yr adventure) and coming home in September.

### kcehealth — 2014-08-29T03:06:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ken-from-vancouver-canada.11607/post-139202>

I'm guessing this is what you mean. A pic of me in the mirror.

### caroline — 2014-08-29T08:07:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ken-from-vancouver-canada.11607/post-139218>

Your trip sounds awesome .... please tell us more.......

How are you managing with food? I come home from Asia a total disaster.....

I keep thinking India ..... but how would I survive that.........

How many cans of sardines can a person pack??????

### kcehealth — 2014-08-29T09:03:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ken-from-vancouver-canada.11607/post-139221>

It was very challenging so we changed our tactic a bit so we could use the markets. So we used Bangkok as our hub for much of Thailand and into Cambodia, Loas and Burma. The big challenge was "no rice" cuz everything comes with rice. And I won't pull any punches... There were times (cuz we didn't know the good markets upon arrival in a new city) we had to partake in KFC since they have HACCP programs (food safety). So out goes the bun and I would eat the grilled breast. We always made sure we had lots of coconut oil. I brought a backwoods camp stove that uses butane or propane so we could cook up bacon (doesn't need refrigeration for a few days). From time to time we ran into a fellow doing roadside BBQ. After using up the meat I'd use the stove again to make broth including some market veg like onion and Choy.

Market buying is critical. The hostel or pension-house will often help you get to the good local markets. That's critical. This caused me to change my eating a bit. Only meat and fat until the evening. Veg sources can be of concern so washing with ACV or even the vinegars from fermented (which is everywhere)

We never got to India. Since it's a vegetarian base but other backpackers say you can find chickens. We were trying to find a way thru Burma but they just opened their borders since 1966 so we just couldn't find buses. Trains in SE Asia and India are definately the way to go. Best resource is seat61.com

At the backpack stores you can find alkalizer that fits into a store bought water bottle.

Also, pickup a cheap phone so you can swap out simcards as you get to each location. We have iPad mini plus a solar charger cuz the power is very very bad in Asia. Plus we have two mini battery packs to recharge devices.

We went super lightweight. 2shirts - lightweight fast drying. 1pants 1shorts. 3socks. 3underwear. 500ml of coconut oil (unless flying). Seasalt.

PS we did find "things" we really wanted. When it got to much in the bag... UPS back home. Better to pay some bucks verses carrying it around for a year.

Oh ya. The seasalt sounds weird but we stopped bathing with soap and bathe with seasalt (I have a small tester from a pet store to make sure it's balanced properly). Seasalt helps keep the skin bacteria in balance. Freshwater rinse after dinner. It also allowed us to use the same salt on food or in it. We found the salt was a major issue during long walking locations plus the heat.

I could go on and on but I'll stop now.

### kcehealth — 2014-08-29T09:06:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ken-from-vancouver-canada.11607/post-139222>

If you're going to do it our way. You Tube was super to find how to get from place to place.

We had almost no success with voice translation apps due to accents. One Malaysian lady downloaded an app she knew worked but it spoke back to me in a very strong Australian accent.

### caroline — 2014-08-29T10:51:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ken-from-vancouver-canada.11607/post-139226>

you have a problem with an aussie accent????

# Thread: Happy to have found this blog & Dr. K
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happy-to-have-found-this-blog-dr-k.11660/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Darleen — 2014-09-05T22:29:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happy-to-have-found-this-blog-dr-k.11660/post-140031>

Hi All, I am new to this forum but have been recently reading and researching on Dr.K's website. I'm am so happy to be here. Everything is coming together and making so much sense. That being said, I could use some input. So I'll try to be as brief as possible, I am 33 y.o. F. Major problem being extreme fatigue and anxiety, and asmtha. Ive had things like ebstein barr, terrible itchy rash all over my body, all before the age of 25. Miscarriage at 32. I've gone primal about one year ago and since then I do feel somewhat better.
A osteopath has diagnosed me with some of the following over the last 8 years or so.
Low cortisol(all day) low progesterone, low dhea, low iron and ferritin, low d3, high Rt3, low t3.
Fasting glucose and LDL has recently gone up, which concerns me.
My main question is which protocols should I be adhering to? I am starting the leptin and CT. My biggest problem is wanting to constantly snack, but I am not overweight. I have always thought the anxiety& panic attacks kept me thin? Anxiety and depression runs deep in my family. I'm also thinking I have a mitochondria issue ?
Thank you all

### nonchalant — 2014-09-05T23:09:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happy-to-have-found-this-blog-dr-k.11660/post-140034>

Welcome Darleen, glad to have you here. Starting with the leptin reset & CT is great.
You have been primal for a year, has that reduced your anxiety? Animal fat sure helped mine. I got additional benefits from eating seafood.

Work on your circadian cycle first, add in CT and then add lots of seafood. Your snacking is probably caused by high EMF. Do you live in a city? Use a cell phone and wireless internet?

### Darleen — 2014-09-05T23:30:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happy-to-have-found-this-blog-dr-k.11660/post-140036>

Hi there nonchalant, thank you for the welcoming! Primal has not helped the anxiety just the energy. I used to sleep constantly, now it's much less. I am now including more seafood. I live on Long Island, Surrounded by wifi and iPhone, iPad . I read on the iPad in bed to fall asleep.. Yikes! I've been turning off the t.v.( we used to sleep with it on until I awoke at about 2am to turn it off) Yes high Emf exposure. I was looking into the blue light blockers but I am not sure of how I'll wear them in conjunction with my prescription glasses. Any ideas?
Also it takes me about an hour to eat, especially breakfast. Is that ok?
Before this leptin rest I would skip breakfast and just have coffee with coconut oil or butter.
I'm still checking out all the blogs..
Thank you again!

### nonchalant — 2014-09-05T23:40:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happy-to-have-found-this-blog-dr-k.11660/post-140037>

Sounds like you're off to a good start. The blue blockers are a bit difficult with glasses, yes. These are my favorite:
[http://www.amazon.com/Uvex-S0360X-U...ti-Fog/dp/B003OBZ64M/ref=cm\_cr\_pr\_product\_top](http://www.amazon.com/Uvex-S0360X-Ultra-spec-SCT-Orange-Anti-Fog/dp/B003OBZ64M/ref=cm_cr_pr_product_top)
They will go on top of some glasses. Or you might be able to put your glasses outside of them. I have done it both ways. At first they seem to make things too dark, but when your night vision improves, they seem to allow too much light in, imo.

It's easy to buy orange goggles online that don't actually block blue light. Or they only block a small part of the blue spectrum.

Just starting your breakfast in 30 minutes is good, as long as you aren't grazing.

### nonchalant — 2014-09-05T23:47:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happy-to-have-found-this-blog-dr-k.11660/post-140038>

One thing else to concentrate on early is good water. Reverse Osmosis or spring water is best. Or at least anything without fluoride.

How did you find this site?

### Darleen — 2014-09-05T23:55:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happy-to-have-found-this-blog-dr-k.11660/post-140039>

No grazing for me, lol I guess I'll just have to get new glasses that fit my new blue blockers  thanks so much nonchalant. I'll be investing in a pair today.

### Darleen — 2014-09-05T23:59:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happy-to-have-found-this-blog-dr-k.11660/post-140040>

I have a berkey filter, that also filters flouride out. I don't remember how I found the site, I read daily about nutrition and stumbled upon gold.

### caroline — 2014-09-06T00:01:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happy-to-have-found-this-blog-dr-k.11660/post-140041>

Welcome Darleen! Is your BAB big enough? 50gr protein?

get all electronics out of your bedroom STAT ...can you turn off the breaker to your bedroom? If I use my ipad after 4:00pm I will have a lousy sleep.

are you leaving at least 4 hours between meals and before going to bed? grounding?

Do you have Dr. K's book? the Epi-Paleo RX ...it is an overview of the first two years of the blog.
Have you listened to all Jack's podcasts? a great way to get fast easy info...he has a great delivery - makes it easier!

When you an manage it ..... become a gold member and you will have 24/7 access to all the webinars and every month there is a live Q&A with Dr. kruse where you can ask him anything!

### caroline — 2014-09-06T00:02:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happy-to-have-found-this-blog-dr-k.11660/post-140042>

Apparently the berkley filter sucks.....

my blue blockers fit over my glasses - no problem ..... very cheap too.

### Darleen — 2014-09-06T00:23:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happy-to-have-found-this-blog-dr-k.11660/post-140046>

Thank you Caroline!
My breakfast has been about 4 eggs(24grams protein) over easy in bacon fat or coconut oil and egg white protein(approx 12grams protein) mixed with water and sometimes hand full grapes or blueberries and 2 strips organic bacon.(4 grams protein) Also coffee with half n half ( is that small amount of milk ok?) I get pretty nauseous when eating breakfast regardless of what it is.
Can or should I replace protein grams with grams of fat like I've been doing? There's 20 grams of fat alone in the eggs.
I'm trying so hard to eat 4 hours before bed. But having some issues, sometimes I skip lunch bc I'm not hungry then come 8-9pm and I'm starved and give in because I don't want to lose weight. Honestly I think a journal will help me keep on top of things.
Yes I'm grounding now.
No book yet but I will def love the read. Where can I find the podcasts?

### Darleen — 2014-09-06T00:25:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happy-to-have-found-this-blog-dr-k.11660/post-140048>

Do you think the berkey is not taking out flouride? I would invest in an R.O. System. I don't like to buy spring water because of the plastics.

### caroline — 2014-09-06T00:35:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happy-to-have-found-this-blog-dr-k.11660/post-140049>

don't get an R/O system ....wrecks your plumbing .... Jack tells us the best thing is to buy bottled water ...and cheapest.

Please start a journal in the journal section ..... Dr. K. needs context to offer you some suggestions.

Have you thought of having a BPC? bullet proof coffee.....check out Dave Asprey's site - the bullet proof executive

the podcasts are listed on the home page I think - gotta check that out.

you are okay with dairy? I often have full fat cream

and btw .... anorexia and obesity are just different ends of the same spectrum ...so the leptin RX is for everyone ...you just have to find your own n=1

### caroline — 2014-09-06T00:46:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happy-to-have-found-this-blog-dr-k.11660/post-140053>

the podcasts .... on the home page - there is a spot to google what you are looking for ...I am not sure if they are all there..
just google podcasts and some do come up ...I just checked.

### Darleen — 2014-09-06T00:49:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happy-to-have-found-this-blog-dr-k.11660/post-140054>

Yes I like BPC and I'm good with dairy as well. Thank you all

### caroline — 2014-09-06T01:09:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happy-to-have-found-this-blog-dr-k.11660/post-140057>

our absolute pleasure .... we are all on this journey together ...Dr. K. has the flashlite...

# Thread: Newbie Alert - Hello
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-alert-hello.11911/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sarah Hodgson — 2014-10-16T12:19:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-alert-hello.11911/post-143997>

Hi all, just wanted to introduce myself \*wavingexcitedly

I do not have any thing in particular that I am trying to treat health wise, of course I have or have had ailments of various kinds throughout my life for one reason or another and I would like to address these with the advice & knowledge I gain from the site. I am well aware that people come to accept these niggles of poor health, rather than strive for optimal health and it is for this reason really that I have stumbled upon the amazing work of DrJK. After years of various reading and making changes here and there, I now feel like I have actually happened across something that will really make a difference and be life changing for not only myself but anyone around me that I can help and who is willing to listen. I am not a creative type rather than academic so I am aware the reading material may be a challenge for me. I have been listening to podcasts over the past couple of days, as I am on the road with my job quite a lot & I have found this to be a really great way of gaining some insight and understanding. I look forward to getting stuck into the forum in a few days and really getting my head into this new and exciting journey!!!!

Thanks, Sarah

### fitness@home — 2014-10-16T12:56:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-alert-hello.11911/post-143999>

Welcome Sarah!

The reading matetial is very challenging to me on a regular basis...and I've been here a long time . What's nice is that when you go back and read older blogs, over time you have learned and they actually become easier to understand.

Hang in there, ask questions and and read, read, read.

### Sarah Hodgson — 2014-10-16T13:33:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-alert-hello.11911/post-144002>

Thank you ladies. Yes, it feels great when something suddenly makes sense so I guess we just need to accept that it is going to take time, and perseverance is key. I am currently listening to podcasts again as I work... I am looking forward to starting CT once I have had the chance to read exactly what I need to do. I doubt I will feel as excited when I am about to plunge myself into cold water teehee. But hey, in for a penny in for a pound!

### caroline — 2014-10-17T01:52:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-alert-hello.11911/post-144063>

Welcome Sarah! yes indeed .... in for a penny - in for a pound!
are you grounding? do you have blue blockers?

Please start a journal in the journal section .... it really helps to keep track of stuff and for motivation ....and fun to look back over at a later date and see our journey to our own n=1

### Sarah Hodgson — 2014-10-17T05:26:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-alert-hello.11911/post-144078>

Hi Caroline. Yes I had been grounding already but not as much as I should. I sat in the sunshine and grounded today as the weather was lovely. I was fortunate enough to have been located with work near to a fish restaurant so had oysters for lunch and I am currently sat with my blue blockers on! Thank you for the referal for the journal, I will have a look now

### caroline — 2014-10-17T05:44:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-alert-hello.11911/post-144080>

Do you have Dr. K's book? The Epi-Paleo Rx ...... it is an overview of the first two years of the blog.

Good job on the oysters and grounding!

Have you see the easy start guide for C/T????

### Inger — 2014-10-17T10:04:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-alert-hello.11911/post-144086>

Welcome Sarah!
I can assure you, living Jacks protocols are going to make a difference... just do it and you will be amazed soon enough

### Sarah Hodgson — 2014-10-17T10:24:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-alert-hello.11911/post-144090>

Thank you both! And not yet, but I will be buying the book next week. I haven't seen the easy start guide yet and I cannot seem to work out where the Journal section is. Yes I can't wait to get stuck in thank you!!

### Johnny Downunder — 2014-11-12T02:13:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/john-from-nz.12127/post-147058>

Hi, I am finally recovering after 15 years of various chronic fatigue type problems. The snowball really started rolling when I couldn't recover from a virus back in 1996. Over the years following that, I ran myself down to complete failure and really only started to come aright this year after following a program based on hair tissue mineral analysis. It involved supplements, spring water, coffee enemas, infra-red light saunas, and a very effective form of meditation.

This forum has been great to read and I look forward to incorporating the techniques into my program. I kind of knew what worked and what didn't but now it makes a lot more sense. Thanks Dr Kruse.

Dr. K. has always said that he won't dumb down the information ...... we need to keep rereading until we get it.

sometimes I will see a blog that I read a year or so ago ....and think I have never seen it before - but of course I had.

Things click and become clearer as we learn more and somethings becomes more relevant to us.

Also, please remember that Dr. K. is writing for Doctors and scientists.......

Jack tells me over and over .... we don't have to speak Chinese to enjoy Chinese food! It is all about commitment and applying protocols 100% if you want results. Nothing worthwhile is ever easy.

# Thread: Hello, lovely peeps!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-lovely-peeps.12276/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kristi Lambert — 2014-12-03T01:28:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-lovely-peeps.12276/post-149053>

[Hi everyone! I have been following Paleo, Wahls, Keto, LCHF for a while and recently came upon interviews with Dr. Kruse, which brought me here. I have been vlogging on Youtube as Lambiepoo for a few years, most recently, my journey with keto for the past 13 weeks. I have been pouring through the blogs and forums here and trying to soak in all the information. I lost about ten pounds before I started vloggin, and 32 pounds or so over the following 12 weeks. I incorporated alot of testing of different macro rations based on what the "gurus" were recommending and noted how my body reacted. I unofficially started the LeptinRx yesterday (kind of a dry run for two days before offically starting tomorrow - Wednesdays are my Youtube check-in days, generally).](http://www.youtube.com/user/lambiepoovegan/videos)

I am a T2D and found that my FBG stayed in the 80s when I kept my protein below 70, while it would shoot up to 100-120 if I went to about 100g. I found myself being scared off from the BAB by the internet folks who talk about gluconeogenesis, so I was heartened to read Dr. Kruse's comment about gluconeogenesis being the gateway drug to the PPP! I have not used any diabetes meds, just a cinnamon extract called Cinsulin (sp?) and hope that I will not have to do so. My FBG, as expected, has gone up to the 120s since I started the LeptinRX. I am hoping it will start dropping natually, and I also want to not that I have not been entering my food into Cronometer this pas week and have stayed LCHF and Keto but enjoyed a few treats such as pork shoulder, Applegate Farms pepperoni and Kerrygold cheese over that past week. Did not measure ketones or blood sugars over five days, so I may sill be recovering from my indulgences. I had been doing a few five day fat fasts recently so who knows how much I'll weigh now that I am rehydrating (hindsight says probably no more fat fasting).

I feel so much better already, sleeping better, aching muscles subsiding. I have my blue blocker glasses on, got my sleep mask. Been using a Colpak on my trapezius, but I got a snorkel and am gonna start my face dunking.

Dr Kruse and all you lovely peeps are so inspiring and thought-provoking! Thanks for being here!

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/kristis-journal.12278/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/kristis-journal.12278/)

Cheers,

Kristi

### Kristi Lambert — 2014-12-03T01:33:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-lovely-peeps.12276/post-149057>

Thanks so much, Dr Kruse!

### nonchalant — 2014-12-03T01:41:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-lovely-peeps.12276/post-149059>

Welcome Kristi! Glad you are going to start the face dunking. Will you be getting some AM sunshine?

### Kristi Lambert — 2014-12-03T01:46:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-lovely-peeps.12276/post-149062>

Thanks! Yes indeed, found a spot this morning across the street and got a couple minutes on my retinas during the "golden hour", at about 7:15 AM. Sunrise was listed to be 7:06 AM. Any recommendations on the best way to sun gaze? I would be most grateful

### nonchalant — 2014-12-03T02:31:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-lovely-peeps.12276/post-149065>

> [Kristi Lambert said:](/goto/post?id=149062)
>
> Thanks! Yes indeed, found a spot this morning across the street and got a couple minutes on my retinas during the "golden hour", at about 7:15 AM. Sunrise was listed to be 7:06 AM. Any recommendations on the best way to sun gaze? I would be most grateful

Sounds great. I found at first that I couldn't look at the sun, even when it was very low on the horizon. So I would just look in the general direction.
Now my eyes are different, and I can look directly at it (if right after sunrise), or later if it is behind a tree and I only see small bits of the sun. Even if obscured by clouds, I go look in the general direction. And glance at the sun often during the day.

Jack has emphasized that resetting your circadian rhythm is the most important part of the Leptin Reset.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-lovely-peeps.12276/post-149088>

Hi from Oz Kristi .... and welcome! You have made an awesome start!!

### Kristi Lambert — 2014-12-03T14:49:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-lovely-peeps.12276/post-149104>

>
> What is your non native EMF exposure? work? home?
>
> What water are you drinking?
>
> C/T will be great for your aching muscles.

Thanks so much!

I have been drinking spring water and RO water, but I love ice cubes and have used filtered water cubes from the fridge so I am gonna start sticking RO water in the freezer and fridge going forward.

I am currently in an emf swamp in this house. I use Dropcams to keep track of my mother and wireless routers so I have a bunch of work to do there to come up with ethernet solutions. This house was built in 1980 so I have some challenges.

I have a grounding mat, but had it grounded by the house electric socket (stopped using it - I could feel it was making me sick) so I need to run a wire directly to the earth.

I bought one of those emf ponchos and a face shield - things are tight money-wise so I will keep adding. I have an emf pendant called a rejuvenizer that I hope is helping me, I got it a couple years ago and never take it off. I am currently caretaking for my mother so at least I am no longer in a work emf swamp.

We have Hunter Spring Park in Crystal River which I hope is getting nice and cold. Thought I might start wading into it, will bring a thermometer and see if it will work.

I would be most grateful for any suggestions

Thanks again for the welcome! I am so excited to have been guided here - feels like I am finally home

The best way to investigate complaints against a doctor to check more than one resource. It also explores whether the physician is licensed in another state to see if there were complaints in the previous points.
Obtaining extra kilos during Christmas [**Choice 9000 Caralluma**](http://www.tripleeffectseyeserum.org/choice-9000-caralluma-weight-loss-and-energy-booster-supplement/) is a nightmare for you every year? Specialist Nutritionist and Personal Trainer Thales Panayiotou suggest ways to control your diet and keep your weight over the festive season.
"You should not let them understand our panic for detoxification and monofagia to achieve fast weight loss," says Mr. Panayiotou. Instead, we should keep our composure and set realistic goals
According to the eminent nutritionist, it is important to realize that diets that promise dramatic results in a short time based mainly on fluid loss rather than fat, so I have no lasting effect and sometimes dangerous to health.

You can also drink tea or coffee, the latter with skim milk and never with sugar, if desired sweetened used saccharin.

# Thread: Mike from Melbourne
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-from-melbourne.12789/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MIKE WILKINSON — 2015-02-03T08:39:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-from-melbourne.12789/post-154582>

Hi all,
1st time poster so hopefully I am following the correct protocol here
I only found out about this website when visiting Josh Lamaro down in Torquay
Josh is very knowledgeable and good bloke very caring
A bit of a background on Mike from Melbourne
Well here we go
First started being concerned about my health many years ago with with a really bad fatigue mainly affecting me after sports
symptoms like elevated heart rate sinuses that I never had before ever and had to stop doing exercise needed lots of sleep etc wasn't probably the classic "fatigue symptoms no aches but something was definitely wrong
Even today I have to be very careful with how much exercise I do
Recently diagnosed with Parkinsons and on a small amount of Sifrol
Main symptoms from the Parkinson's down my right side with the tremor in my arm but the thing that's really quite debilitating at times is my walking movement with my right leg
Really giving me the shits
I'm still very active and working full time
Love to surf when I can and renovating the house
Couple of months ago I found out about the Paleo diet and the effect on autoimmune diseases
Been Paleo for four weeks now and strictly off Gluten
Body seems to be detoxing with my sinuses going rampant off the Gluten
Now reading the auto immune solution by Amy Myers and interested in her program
Before I jump on any program I want to get professional guidance run some blood tests etc
That's about it there's other stuff but that's the main points
Other than that fit and healthy
I figure can't wait for conventional medicine to do its thing with doctors just treating the symptoms drugs profits drugs
Not expecting miracles
Pretty sure I'm in good hands now with Josh getting some baseline markers on my health looking forward to delving further into The mountain of information from Jack Kruse
Happy and Healthy
Mike

### MIKE WILKINSON — 2015-02-03T10:32:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-from-melbourne.12789/post-154589>

Thanks heaps Jack
Sharp learning curve here
Any recommendations on where to start reading on this site ?

### Danny S — 2015-02-05T17:55:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here.12828/post-154817>

50yr old male, overweight sports official, work under both natural and artificial lights. been reading JK for about a year. Ready to get it together.

Danny

### Kristi Lambert — 2015-02-05T18:05:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here.12828/post-154818>

> [Danny S said:](/goto/post?id=154817)
>
> 50yr old male, overweight sports official, work under both natural and artificial lights. been reading JK for about a year. Ready to get it together.
>
> Danny

Welcome

### Danny S — 2015-02-05T18:27:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here.12828/post-154820>

JFYI my avatar pic is my dad at 55 yrs old. He died at 64 from cancer. He was a PALEO guy from childhood. Loved the animal fat, could eat a bushel of Oysters (we live near Apalachacola Fl, former oyster capital of the world), fished the gulf of mexico, grew our own veggies. He was up at day light, worked as a carpenter was 5'6" tall and weighted 135# wet. He smoked a couple packs a day (Pall Mall non filters and Viceroy Filtered) most of his adult life and drank beer or scotch. never was a fan of "sweets".

I am now 50, active most days, always in some sort of pain, have had a couple disc's removed from my neck (c-5/6), never regained feeling in my left thumb or index finger, injured reffing a football game, got whipplashed from a hit from behind and took a foul ball off my umpire mask in a fast pitch softball game that made me decide to get cut.

I now have a grand daughter (5 daughters) and want to be healthy to see each of them graduate college and get married. So thus my active journey will begin!

### nonchalant — 2015-02-05T22:47:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here.12828/post-154852>

Welcome Danny. Your granddaughter sounds like an excellent reason to reach for health.

post test results here.

### Nittygrittydanny — 2015-02-13T18:00:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here.12828/post-155540>

Billy, my day to day is pretty abnormal.

I drive 76miles one way to work daily, ref football, ump baseball and softball, work inside/outside of a lab. My day starts prior to sunrise and ends under some sort of lights (sports field, traffic, home). My breakfast would have to be eaten prior to sun rise and dinner most nights is late. Getting my life ordered this year is my goal. I'm taking this season off of football ref'n (we start in May), as soon as baseball/softball season ends (April) i'll be stepping away from that for a year.

I get Jack'ed reading this stuff and have made small changes, but i'm lazy by nature, so it's a challenge.

### BWJustice — 2015-02-14T21:28:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-needed-for-leaky-gut-and-leptin-reset.12931/post-155623>

I would appreciate any advice on the level of physical activity tolerated during leptin reset and leaky gut fix. I'm a relatively fit T2D and have been eating ketogenic for several years. My blood sugar can reach mid mid 80's after 72 hrs of fasting or 72 hours on coconut oil only. Normally my HB1ac is around125 and I've been off all meds for a year. I was taking 2000mg of berberine daily until I read about it causing mitochondrial oxidative stress. This may not be an issue since it actually seems more effective than the 2000mg of metformin I was on for 10 years. Isn't a certain amount of oxidative stress a good thing in your mitochondria? My main issue has been inflammation with neck and left shoulder pain, as well as severe jaw and headaches. Ice is my friend. Early morning is about the only time I can't control blood sugar. I would like to be physically active and have plenty of time to do so but am afraid to trigger more inflammation with the severe headaches. If you have any experiences to share, I would be greatly appreciative.

### caroline — 2015-02-16T02:59:25+0200

There are no cookie cutter solutions, so I'm beginning to understand how important labs will be, as well as individual strengths and weaknesses, such as fitness levels and habits. I will post a journal and picture soon. Thank You!

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3296087/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3296087/)

To clear the ammonia from all that yeast, molybdenum would be a great move - 300 mg 3 times a day after you eat - also pantothenic acid works well with the moly -

Stop eating any starch - just eat a high fat diet with lots of coconut oil - bugs can't digest fat but love simple sugars -

Protein will convert to sugar also - you might consider niacinamide for energy along with d-ribose until you actually become a fat burner :

### Penny — 2015-02-18T23:58:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-fog.12951/post-155986>

> [kovita said:](/goto/post?id=155972)
>
> Penny, that is intersting, the probiotics... i have no brain fog at all... oppositely, lately I enjoy quite surprisingly sharp brain. I am now on quite high dose probiotics, mainly the baby strains as i am in the third trimester already and would like to infect my child with the best possible diversity and signaling. Should I gove it a try? It is bloody expensove here in Europe!

Hi Kovita

When you are nursing your baby, your milk will contain galactooligosaccharides - a prebiotic that feeds bifidobacterium which will constitute 90% of your infant's gut flora...

In the book "Gut and Psychology Syndrome" Dr. Natasha Campbell Mcbride says the following on page 349:

" _Prepare for Birth and breastfeeding_
It is essential to prepare your birth canal for the baby, which is something women used to do in traditional societies. In order to prepare the birth canal you need to populate it with beneficial flora. To do that, every day after your bath or shower apply a handful of your homemade yoghurt or kefir all over your genital area, as well as all over your breasts and armpits. Let the kefir or yoghurt dry on you before dressing. If you have an unusual vaginal discharge or thrush (which is very common in pregnancy) once or twice a week insert a capsule of a good quality probiotic into your vagina at bedtime and leave it there to dissolve.Alternatively you can use a piece of cotton wool soaked in kefir or yoghurt as a tampon, inserted into the vagina for a few minutes. AS those areas get populated by beneficial flora, tehy will be protected from anything pathogenic, and whn your baby goes through the birth canal it will acquire beneficial flora from you. Populating yhour breasts and armpits with good bacteria will help to prevent mastitis and supplement your baby with probiotics, so carry on with this procedure after your baby is born."

So, I would find a probiotic that had bifidobacterium and acidophilus in it and use that - I would not use Dr. Ohira because it is enterically coated to survive stomach acid - I personally would also avoid any probiotic that has streptococous thermopoulos in it because it can be hijacked by a pathogenic strep...

Making yogurt is not difficult and has trillions of probiotics - if you have a problem with milk protein, strain it out and just use the whey (liquid part) -

Or, assuming the probiotic you are taking is not enterically coated, just use that vaginally -

Just remembered - I use a probiotic soap by Dr. Ohira called Kampuku - the stuff is great - I even use it in my nose and ears - it's $12.00/bar here but lasts a long time - try it

> PERFECT!!!! You know why?? becasue here in Olavarría, i can only get 2 probiotics. One comes with almost zero cultures. But the other only contains S.Boulardii anda a LOT OF IT. I will try it thank you

I am a 48 y.o. G.P. from Bendigo Australia.
I read paleo FX and decided to order some bloods.
To my surprise, my DHEAS and Progesterone were low.
One thought was pregnenolone steal but the T, Cortisol are not too bad.

**Is it worth trying to supplement DHEA??? If so how much???**

I have been in nutritional ketosis for 3 months, and am quite active walking or jogging 10 km daily.
Height 1.83 m wt 88 Kg Dexa fat 28%
BP 110/70 (although systolic can drop to 90 if salt not kept up); resting HR 64

DISH found incidentally on CT coronary calcium score (grrr) which was ZERO.
Have recently had Dx of mild-mod OSA on CPAP.

FULL BLOOD EXAMINATION
(x10 ^9 /L)
HB : 14.4 g/dL (13.0-18.0) WHITE CELL COUNT: 6.4 (4.0-11.0)
PCV: 42.0 % (40.0-54.0) Neutrophils: 59% 3.8 (2.0-8.0)

### Jenny S — 2015-03-23T03:21:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-down-under.13160/post-159248>

Hi Simon - I'm in Bendigo too!

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-04-10T05:46:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-down-under.13160/post-160771>

bun creatinine ratio is 20:1 simon, your cells are dehydrated
without intracellular water micells you cannot carry charge and the results will show in your hormone panels, esp DHEA and Pg. Pg helps keep you hydrated too.
if you are leptin sensitive now is the time to start CT to help your inner battery.

you also need a real thyroid test including ft3, rt3 etc

there are big time calcium efflux problems in bendigo because of the electromagnetic environment there. it shows in all the fibromyalgia cases, and is why they really respond to lyrica. these microwave frequencies also have the effect of cellular dehydration. i have never sen a BUN:Creatinine ratio under 14 in bendigo ever. and i test all my patients. i talk about the "bendigo effect" lots on the QandAs when i call in.
I moved away from there for this very reason, however i still visit to run the practice and feel like shit for 3 days a week as a result.
the effect is especially devastating near your clinic on eaglehawk road because of the long range tower behind the goldmines hotel. i have tested it and it pumps out an astonishing 368mw/cm2 even 1km away. i would say your clinic is less than that distance away from it. ironbark and that top end of golden square near the hospital is especially horrific in terms of EMF. the other bad areas are near osbourne street, there are clusters of frozen shoulder/migraine and fibro sufferers there, and many brain tumour cases too.

if you check your 25-H- vitamin D and it is low with a concurrent high plasma calcium you have your answer, get the fuck out of bendigo like i did.

we have a few mutual patients too, would be good to catch up and talk about some of this stuff..good to see you found your way here, you are already miles ahead of any other GP in Bendigo just for having considered visiting here, so welcome

josh lamaro

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-04-10T06:22:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-down-under.13160/post-160773>

please also obtain a proper VAP lipid test such as the LipoScan through imupro so we can see what is happening properly

# Thread: Aloha from Hawaii ~
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aloha-from-hawaii.13918/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Amy Bilberry — 2015-04-17T04:47:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aloha-from-hawaii.13918/post-161484>

My 11 1/2 year old daughter and I have been through much turmoil over the last few years: I got cancer and went through 5 months of chemo and have been in remission since July 2014. I also ended a very abusive relationship and relocated here to Hawaii and am literally 'starting over' again - for the first time in my life!

I've been studying and re-educating myself on Ancestral Nutrition, Epigenetics and Paleo-Ketogenic diets/lifestyle for almost 5 years now but only had brief periods of sanity in which I was in an environment conducive to starting and maintaining the lifestyle before the $hi# hit the fan so to speak...

Always been about 25+ pounds overweight since adulthood and lost the most weight following the Atkins diet when I was 21. My daughter was born with insulin resistance and had non-alcoholic fatty liver disease at age 6 - we reversed this by diet alone but as she got older, old habits showed up again as life got more stressful. We both lost some weight and regained health and sanity briefly when my ex was away at school and we didn't have to 'deal' with his bipolar/alcoholic mood swings. We both are leptin resistant, pre-diabetic, pyroluria (high/low methylator), thyroid issues and she still has abnormal liver function.

Now, I'm getting settled here in Hawaii and plan to start my own wellness practice and therapy once I've reached my own health goals - hopefully by January of next year

I'm interested in the general key points and protocol breakdown for the BOTH of us - I'm also wondering if CT is safe for my daughter? It's refreshing to know that seafood and sea veggies are the best foods for the protocol and eating simply is the most enjoyable thing about this lifestyle! I would eat the same thing every day for weeks and consequently, due to the high fat content, ate only 1 large meal and 1 small snack each day! This is also a very sustainable diet for obvious reasons.

Anyone try the Cool Fat Burner vest? It looks easier to use although Jack said 'if you have belly/leg/butt fat - you should do ice baths submerging those areas'...

Any and all info is greatly appreciated!!

Alohi (Amy) & Amelie

### fitness@home — 2015-04-17T12:47:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aloha-from-hawaii.13918/post-161508>

Welcome Amy!

Hawaii sounds like a great place to start over. Kind if exciting to do that... I've had to start over a couple of times in my life. You can either let it overwhelm you or embrace it

I see no reason CT shouldn't be safe for your daughter. Nice that you have someone to CT with and keep the motivation going for each other.

Enjoy all the sunshine, seafood, and grounding there...

### caroline — 2015-04-18T03:36:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aloha-from-hawaii.13918/post-161582>

Hi and welcome Amy ...to both you and your daughter and a brand new life! I am sending you a huge Koala bear hug from down under .... we are practically neighbours!

The cool fat burner vest is great.

You are a health care practitioner?
Do you have JK's book? The Epi Paleo RX. Is your daughter following along here? reading etc.?

Please start a journal in the journal section so we can follow along with you on your journey. Dr. K. needs context to be able to direct you. Tell us everything LOL

It would be great if your daughter would start a journal too ...and we could get her perspective on this journey to optimal health ...that would be very neat!

There are others from Hawaii here as well - please connect with them and you can support each other.

### nonchalant — 2015-04-18T17:59:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aloha-from-hawaii.13918/post-161655>

Welcome Amy! I sure hope you are taking advantage of the ocean. Daily trips to the shore would be awesome. Do you drink water that is non-fluoridated?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aloha-from-hawaii.13918/post-170329>

also living in hawai'i.

# Thread: Hello from Charlotte, NC
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-charlotte-nc.14443/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### deallen7 — 2015-05-12T02:10:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-charlotte-nc.14443/post-163889>

Hello, everybody. My name is David and I have been journeying on this quest for optimal health ever since my original "light bulb" moment 4 years ago. As a chronically over-trained, over-stressed, under-nourished, intoxicated and caffeinated college student, I had a LOT of struggles with brain fog, energy, mood, etc. My turning point was stumbling across the documentary "Food Matters" one night and realizing that the food I consume actually does influence my health. It still pisses me off that this was a "light bulb" moment. Something very wrong with being able to live 22 years without realizing that food directly and quickly impacts health. Now, reading this blog, I am realizing that food _definitely_ still matters, but food is not everything! There are so many other forces at play. I can't believe my mom was actually on to something with her fear of the wireless router. As a kid, I used to shake my head when she turned it off every night. I didn't understand! But she did, somehow...

It's been a fascinating month diving deeper into the universe of quantum biology. I feel like I have been craving this deeper and more inclusive understanding of life, and I am sure you have all felt the craving and can understand exactly what I mean. Now that I know _what_ to learn, my goal is to rapidly improve my cognitive health so that I can soak up more knowledge and integrate the knowledge into my life at a faster rate!

Looking forward to continuing this education. If any one can point me in the direction of underweight and low body fat % information/bread crumbs/threads/Rx’s I would greatly appreciate it! My "underweight" and "low body fat" queries in the forum search field have been only slightly helpful

Thanks in advance. Excited to be joining this community.

### betterbrain4me — 2015-06-05T17:41:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/old-fashioned-paper-cookbooks.15119/post-166694>

Thank you all for your replies.

Yes, I guess we have somewhat "fresh" seafood at our local store, but being in Colorado, not sure just how fresh it is by the time it gets to us? Lol! I assume it gets frozen and thawed along the way. Will head to the store shortly to purchase lots of varieties.

I was curious about the vegetables. I read the Wahl's Protocol (written by Dr. Terry Wahl - an MD diagnosed with MS that was able to get herself out of her wheelchair by changing her diet). I was following all of her recommendations when a friend told me about the "Eat Right for your Blood Type" protocol. When I looked at that, it said that all of the vegetables and fish that I had been eating were wrong for my blood type. Ugh! Learning this information is like trying to drink from a fire hydrant. It's overwhelming at times. I just wondered if Dr. Kruse had a "go-to" list of vegetables (besides sea vegetables). When I ran "Ubiquination" in the search bar, it had 20 separate articles that needed to be printed out. Can I find everything that is online here about ubiquination, MS, Adrenal Fatigue - in his book "Epi-Paleo Rx? Just wanting to find all the info on the website in hard copy. Again, at least for me, the less computer, the better.

And thank you for your suggestion, I actually had not seen the forums on MS . I actually stumbled across his site while researching adrenal fatigue. After your suggestion, I did run a search for MS, and am currently trying to print one of the articles. It will be a good jumping off point. And to top it all off, I don't just live in "Mile-High" Denver, I live even higher in the mountains at an 8,000' elevation. Would love to move, but kids in school and jobs currently require me here. Maybe a couple more years and I can get to lower elevation. At least that's the plan. Lots to adjust for in the meanwhile.

### betterbrain4me — 2015-06-05T22:50:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/old-fashioned-paper-cookbooks.15119/post-166706>

Thank you.

I should ask my holistic doc for these tests. I am near broke having to pay out-of-pocket for recent tests (he wanted to check thyroid ABs, a Neurological Autoimmune Reactivity Screen and saliva testing for Adrenals). I'm hoping that this is (just) Adrenal Fatigue/Exhaustion and that I am simply exhibiting symptoms before it truly progresses to full blown MS.

I currently supplement with 2000mg of EPA and DHA fish oil, cook with coconut oil (even add it to my teas, or just let a spoonful dissolve in my mouth). I am also supplementing with a multi vitamin/minerals, Vitamin B-100 Complex, Turmeric, MSM, Vitamin D, Vitamin E, pro biotics, Sea Kelp, and Rhodiola Rosea. I am certainly open to new suggestions, so greatly appreciate the article that you've attached. I have also removed all grains, dairy, soy from my diet.

I just returned from the grocery store and they are out of oysters, but may have more tomorrow. So, instead, I picked up more shrimp, scallops, crab meat, halibut and salmon. Making lots of salads with kale and other mixed greens (dandelion, spinach, etc.). I really feel like the light aspect is HUGE for me. I have been trying to get more natural sunlight - but it has been unusually rainy and gray for the last month (so not cooperating with me). I've been calling around to stores to find an NIR light (to maybe supplement with during this kind of weather?) with absolutely no luck. Will look online, which means MORE computer time. Sigh....

My brain is definitely letting me know that it is unhappy with my previous diet (lots of gluten, dairy and soy) and _way_ too much artificial light. I just hope that I haven't passed the tipping point and that I can get it back on track.

Thanks again for the info. I'll print it out and give to my doc.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/old-fashioned-paper-cookbooks.15119/post-166711>

I am not a doctor. 75yo man
I do not know if you have MS.
MS is serious problem.
What caught my eye is description of MS in the book (below).
Person there had excessive omega3 and suppressed omega6.
In case like that the last thing one need is
supplementing with omega3 sources (fish/krill oils)
and
eating food that is purposely high on omega3.
I think
that it would be good idea to clarify that issue.
\------
I posted page 138 and 132 from book that you can buy here:
[http://www.bodybio.com/storeproduct304.aspx](http://www.bodybio.com/storeproduct304.aspx)
\------
\==================
Regardless of your MS status
it is good idea to clarify your status (and optimize):

Fatty Acids
Circadian Rhythm
Adrenal Fatigue
Steroid hormones
Thyroid hormones
Micronutrients status (vitamins, minerals, other)

For most of the above you would not need much help from doctor,
any doc MD or DO willing to write script would do.
Actually some scripts for testing you could buy online.

You may want to take a look here:

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-7](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-7)

and here:
[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/)

//
I could help if you post
age, height, weight, menstruate(y/n), any missing body parts

# Thread: Hello from Nebraska.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-nebraska.15179/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Brave New World — 2015-06-07T04:48:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-nebraska.15179/post-166894>

Hello! My name is Nicholas and I'm here in hopes of turning things around for my health. I've been struggling to sort things out for about four years now, with minimal success. Severe brain fog and fatigue coupled with a propensity toward depression and suicidal ideation have made it difficult. I've read a great deal and learned much despite this, but while much of what I learn seems effective for others, I've had little success for myself. At this point I've lost all but a grain of hope and coming here is sort of a last ditch effort. At best the information from the blog and Epi Paleo RX seem like a matrix of truth intertwined with great leaps of conjecture and at worst complete fantasy. That being said I'm in a state of desperation and am far more open minded than I used to be. I am doing my best to sort this out but could use some help due to my cognitive impairment. Here's to hoping that I find that which I am seeking and maybe help some others along the way. It's a pleasure join the forum!

(Hope I'm complying with the decorum of the community and approaching this correctly...)

### fitness@home — 2015-06-07T05:00:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-nebraska.15179/post-166895>

Welcome Nicholas!

Can you start a journal in the journal section and give us some more history? Where you live (city or country, house or high rise apartment, etc...). Whats your work or school environment like, potential EMF exposure, blue light exposure - do you utilize blue blockers, flux, etc. What kind of water do you drink - tap, spring, RO...

You will get more feedback by starting a journal. It helps when there is more context (hey, I'm starting to sound like Caroline!)

### Lahelada — 2015-06-07T12:54:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-nebraska.15179/post-166921>

Welcome ! Wishing you lots of success. If it helps the blogs were /are difficult for all of us in the beginning. I would advise you to keep reading and say "what/" a lot . The understanding will come ,at least the basic grip of what s important for you . A quick summary from me would be . Get out into the open, nature if you can away from EMF as much as you can, drink mineral water or RO water ( doc recommends the latter) ,have a go at grounding and coldthermogenesis . Face dunking is a good start.
I saw you started a journal . Just now I have no time to read as I am goingto work but it would be good if you laid out a typical day .I get up at ,take the bus,car,bike at am to .school /work etc.. . Iam in a high EMf zone with 50 gazillion wifis or whatever. It might even begood to refelct on the health of your family right back to your grandma . Welcome . Gotta run and I mean run
