# Thread: Yec'hed mat
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeched-mat.15266/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thomas — 2015-06-09T17:44:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeched-mat.15266/post-167234>

Before you ask, the title of the thread means "bonne santé" in Breton.

Just wanted to say hello to all of you.
Me? I am thomas,26, currently living in brittany. Which is awesome in many ways (we've got the sea! ANd seafood, TONS of it, we're the inventors of canned sardines, and oyster farm of europe lol)

I am not particularly ill, I'd say I am ok... among the annoyances I guess:
\-Hay fever.
\-Cold hands and feet. Hyperhidrosis.
\-Always stay 10-11 hours in bed.
\-Hate for the winter.
But you know what? I don't want to just be ok. I want to be optimal. As good as I can get in this modern world, given the current knowledge without being a billionaire. I want an optimal brain, optimal hormones, optimal physique, I want to enjoy optimal longevity.
I want to help all the people I know&meet get healthier and maybe create a movement that way.

I discovered JackKruse last year I guess. And since then I am hooked. I do love the natural sciences but Jack is a bit dense sometimes...
I love being really connected to nature, the outdoors, our nature, our instincts... So sad I lost that for some years.(I had it for the first 15 years of my life!)

### b612 — 2015-06-10T17:48:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeched-mat.15266/post-167410>

Les Bretons are the nicest French people I have ever met. I love Bretagne. You are so lucky to be living there

### thomas — 2015-08-04T20:35:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeched-mat.15266/post-173936>

I really need for my brain to work..for my life to work... damn gotta fix that. I feel like I use 1% of it's capacity. I am barely able to think sometimes. I clearly have a hard time with the blogs. I think it would be easier If I printed it and focused 100% on it.

nnEMF and blue light is probaly what's fucking me up right now. I think I am mildly EHS. I get migraines(anoying pressure between the eyes) if I stay too long on the computer/indoors, or if I use a hair clipper etc..

Now..Living 10 km from the center of a 200.000 ppl city (600.000 metro) doesn't help obviously (even if it's already better than most people in the country!). Also nearest antena is only 300m away!!! not counting power lines about 5 km away. all neighbors dects/wifi's. even worse.. the house electromagnetic fields.

Living here is a bad idea. I am moving.

### thomas — 2015-08-04T20:53:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeched-mat.15266/post-173942>

Now living close to Rennes. will go farther away from the city, or be a bit more nomadic and explore around, I am not a tree yet, and definitely not to a point where I want to stay in the same area for decades. I don't have to be anywhere at anytime so let's do.

On the other end. Ehs is definitely proof that nnemf=nobueno. Actually most people can't sense nnemf, but I can sense some of it. Maybe it's not an illness but a sense then. It clearly reduce my vitality/sense of being alive.

Part of the reason is I spent too much time on electronics while in my teens. Learned programming around age 12, only a few monthes after getting my own computer. Wanted to work in IT back then. Well I still do a little but only for my own projects.

>

### thomas — 2015-09-01T17:15:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeched-mat.15266/post-176168>

Still in experimental stage but seems consistent..
Found that going to sleep before and about 9pm solar time is incredible. I sleep so exceptionally well. Then I am so alert and awake during the day. Like 10x mental energy. I have been sleeping way too late all my life.WTF. How can that happen? It's like the first time I am that rested in decades. I can think much better and faster with less noise. Happier and more calm. Everyone should try it.
There's a problem with it tho... falling asleep. The only way I can fall asleep that early(without using sleep deprivation which ruins the point) is to be outdoors for hours in the morning (and the day). And then have absolute discipline to relax and be dark at night. well worth it.

alcohol

# Thread: Hello and Thank You
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-and-thank-you.15499/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Daina — 2015-06-15T20:13:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-and-thank-you.15499/post-168285>

Hi, I am new t0 posting and the threads, but have already started my journal. I have been given homework and a lot of food for thought, but thought it appropriate to meet and greet here, good manners!

Thank you to everyone that welcomed me into this kind and caring fellowship of discovery and knowledge. Especially Dr. Jack. I did have a physical in April, but I need to pick up copies of the most recent labs. I am to go back in 6 months for followup labs, but I am going to try and request any appropriate ones that weren't requested. Looking at the order, looks like she is asking for a hormone panel. I am 53 years old, some thyroid issues, hopefully menopausal, last period in March, 40 pounds overweight, 70 pounds from ideal. I have been reading the blog posts for conditions that relate to my conditions and implementing them as able. I have a 16 year old daughter at home with hubs and I, and she has her own set of problems. She is the last child in the house. I have a 33 year old daughter who lives in NO with her daughter and works at a law firm office worker, a 28 year old daughter that is currently at Barry University in Miami taking post bacc courses to apply to med school. She was a captain in the Air Force, but has always wanted to be an orthopedic surgeon so she is following her dreams with my blessing; a 24 year old son who is in the enlisted Navy as a Nuke on an air craft carrier soon to separate in less than a year and wants to compose music scores, a 17 year old son who is totally disabled from CMV from birth and currently lives in a group home 1 hour away who I see every weekend, and a 16 year old daughter who is bi-polar and a dancer. The 33, 17, and 16 year olds are children of my heart; the 28 and 24 year olds are my birth children. The two high achievers were breast fed. The three that I took in and raised were not.

From what I have gleaned, I am doing the leptin reset. My thyroid meds, which i just started in April, .5 mg of westhroid. I split the dosage and take 1/2 in the am, and 1/2 at 3pm. I take 10,000 iu of vitamin d3 5 days a week. I regularly take selenium, lugol's iodine 25mg, 3870mg of fish oil (495 of DHA, 2205 EPA; what i am reading is that i should contemplate switching to krill oil, but i am not sure why yet, so need to study up on it), vitamin b12 5000-10,000mcg, 1200mg magnesium oxide ( i sometimes employ a magnesium spray that I mix up because i can absorb through skin better). Since starting the leptin reset and employing even a little CT, my body warms up quite a bit and fast! When I first started taking the prescribed NDT, i got a bit of a rush of warmth, which I expected. I knew that I would need to recheck back at the docs in case of a need to increase after about 3 weeks. However, just changing what I have changed so far, my symptoms don't seem to dictate an increase.

I asked my son for blueblocker glasses to fit over my glasses for my birthday and they came last week! I was so excited! So I am using those in the evenings. For about 3 weeks I have included the BAB, but I suspect I am not getting enough protein or good fats still somehow, as I am having breakthrough eating after dinner of bad salty things. BAB is usually 4-5 scrambled eggs in coconut oil or kerry's gold butter, and either scallops, bacon or shrimp sauteed in coconut oil. Probably not the best quality of seafood yet (frozen), but I did move from the regular large eggs to organic. Lunch is usually a meat left over from dinner, but I should probably be putting coconut oil on that too to increase my fat intake. Dinner is usually small meat and vegetables. No starches. Until later. Popcorn is my drug of choice. I must start taking coconut oil shots to try and kill that.

I have downloaded flux to my personal computers. I work from home, my home office has two windows, so I try to remember to open the blinds for real light. I have a dog, but don't go outside much, so at lunch i am trying to remind myself to go outside and play for 'recess'. I work on a computer for the full day. I don't have a smart phone, just a slider. I don't trust smart phones. The daughter and hubs have one each. The daughters' phone is taken at bedtime and kept out of her room. No TV in her room. The house has 5 TV, though only one is usually turned on. I rarely watch TV in the bedroom anymore, as I spend time in there sleeping and keeping it as dark as I can. And trying to catch up on reading about my health doesn't leave a lot of time for TV. I have not retrained my SCN (?) to shut down properly yet. I do get to feeling sleepy now at about 9:30. I need to enforce my bedtime. In the mornings, I can feel my systems 'firing' up. It feels like muffled popping behind my facial skeleton. I am sleeping deeply.

I have purchased two icepacks that i refreeze and expose my skin to throughout the day. I go to bed with icepacks. I need to get more. I want to start the face CT. It is really hot in Orlando, daily temps between 85 and 95 degrees in summer. I have the thermostat set to 78 during the day. I didn't realize how much effect environment and cold could have on a human being. Before this month, the a/c was very 'iffy' and having problems. I got it serviced, because even keeping it a cool 85 was difficult. (tongue in cheek, but true) Still have more to update in the house, like upgrading insulation. Now that I am cooling my skin and environment, i really crave some coldness and I didn't think that would be possible. I had begun to hate feeling cold these last years, but I know it wasn't always like that. I remember as a young girl growing up in Orlando, that I loved the winter here. Logically I would say to myself and others, in winter I can always put on more clothes, in summer you can only take off so much before people start to stare! (this was during a time when modesty was encouraged, not frowned upon like today, lol)! So that is some more of my story. Thanks again everyone for making me feel so welcome!

### caroline — 2015-06-16T01:15:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-and-thank-you.15499/post-168300>

Hi Daina .........do you get outside first thing when you rise? Can you have your BAB outside? can you work outside in the morning? like on a patio? Most of us have very long cords!

Maybe sit in an ice bath and work from there?

### Tara_Tuck — 2015-06-22T06:59:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-wisconsin.15711/post-169221>

Hello, my name is Tara and I live in Madison, Wisconsin. I grew up in the Northwoods and find energy and peace being encapsulated by the trees or dipping my feet in the edge of a clear lake. I came across the forum when researching cortisol, hormones, neurotransmitters, and Hashimoto's. I was diagnosed with Hashimoto's this past January by a functional medicine doctor. Since then, my goals have been to get well, change my career, and have a family. I'll write more about my experience, goals, and Rxs in an optimal journal soon.

I've read the Epi-Paleo Rx at a high level and plan to do a deep dive by mapping out concepts on paper. I'm fascinated by various aspects of psychology and am excited to learn how personality and behavior connects with Jack's work.

I really appreciate the "forum force" which to me is the collective abundance of curiosity, passion, encouragement, authenticity, and growth that resides here. It's inspirational and motivates me to keep digging. Keep it going!

Current preoccupations: sand volleyball, thinking about fear, trying new types of fish, and euchre!

# Thread: Hello from Seattle!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-seattle.15768/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mnloeb — 2015-06-24T04:37:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-seattle.15768/post-169423>

Hello everyone,

In advance, thanks for reading the post and offering any suggestions! Here's hoping the post is logical...grateful for your insight.

Anyway, I find myself completely depleted--and am struggling to understand why. Apathy, lying in bed, lack of coordination, fumbling over words/brain fog, gnawing pit in stomach, dehydration. The past couple months have been trying but hopefully I can defeat this--whatever this is.

Have there been any supplements or medications
that have provided relief for the lethargy/brain fog? Life has slowed down--just an odd, disconcerting feeling. I sense that the body is in a mini-revolt.

I am taking GAIA Adrenal Health, a digestive probiotic, L-Lysine, and B-12. Is this sufficient? Going to try to overhaul my diet, namely slash carbs.

Has anyone else struggled with short-term memory or directions? Fortunate enough to pass a Bar exam a couple of years but mental processing isn't there right now.

Grateful for the forum--looking forward to passing it forward once the situation improves. Best of luck to everyone battling this!

Matt

### caroline — 2015-06-24T04:58:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-seattle.15768/post-169427>

Hi Matt and welcome!

Please start a journal in the journal section ....Dr. K. needs context.

Tell us about your mom's health and your grandma and siblings? what kind of birth did you have? breastfed?

Tell us what you are eating? drinking? You need tons and tons of seafood .....

blood work?

nnEMF at work? at home?

How much sunlight and grounding do you get? can you get to the ocean?

C/T???

are you exercising your butt off????????

Do you have Dr. K's book? The Epi-Paleo Rx ...it is the first couple of years of the blog and easier and faster to get the big picture!

Do you listen to his podcasts? fast and easy way to get lots of info.

If you can manage it ...become a "Gold" member. Dr. K. does a webinar every month and a follow up live Q&A where you can ask any question.

keep reading and asking questions!!

Please read it at your leisure and I'm looking forward to meeting all of you.

### Stanislava — 2015-06-26T01:14:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-stranger-in-paris.15840/post-169596>

Hi everyone!

35 years old born in Eastern Europe lived in Paris for last 16 years.

Still trying to understand what doesn't work so I can heal more and more.
I know I can find more answers here. I've read lots of things here but it feels like I need to talk to someone to move in right direction now.

I was not very good for a longtime, but 2 years ago I woke up with tachycardia and probably adrenaline surge but when I stood up I've fainted and hit my head. Since then for weeks it would happen every night waking up half an hour after falling asleep and fainting, nausea...
I started to develop allergies, GI issues with bloating and uncontrolable anxiety panic attacks. My muscels started to get tensed, and I would be medicated for anxiety, sleep, GERD....I would get worst over next ten months also with constant nausea and after visitt to osteopath I've lost my capacity to sleep completely and I felt like under andrenaline surge 24/7 - dx pheochromocytoma symptoms without pheochromocytoma so psychiatric dx. Light and noise sentivity and autistic like symptoms, no ability to handle stress positive or negative at all etc....78 symptoms

REAL DIAGNOSIS

Looked around on internet and went to London dx with hyperadrenergic POTS, mast cell activation disorder and hypermobility.

Diagnosed by functinal endo: hypothyroid, autoimmunes, dysautonomia and mitochondria, insuline resistance,

SYMPTOMS

I did get better a lot (78 symptoms) and don't take anymore meds as I had extreme reaction to meds with pseudoepileptic symptoms; feels good that brain doesn't run on adrenalin and electricity like feeling, now I've only few symptoms:
\- extreme fatigue
\- brain fog
\- inflammation my CPR is just having difficulty going down, back of my head and neck is burning when I eat anything inflammatory even healthy things. When I eat oils my head burns or feels hot as well.
\- loss of sensations nose, cheek it comes and goes (probably allergy or hypoxia)
\- altitude problems can't take a breath while traveling through mountaints in the car
\- hip pain comes and goes.
\- SIBO relapse again as I ate sugar again after a year for a while. Doesn't feel good when gut causes and/worsens tachycardia.
\- my last 28 lbs don't go down. I've lost 55lbs it was water retention, burning sugar not fat, After my morning smoothie I can't move so extremely tired - hypoglycemia? My smoothie: kale or watercress, banana, blueberries, almond milk, chia, chlorella, almond butter.

With fatigue d ribose mito pack helps some and glutathione helps as well. I coudn't hold platess before or pans and my body felt like it lost capacity to produce any energy at all. But I'm nowhere near normally functional even if I go out everyday and walk do simple things. I'm not in form enough to do daily tasks I could when I didn't work for a while and absolutely can't go back to work short term memory as therapist using also EMDR and hypnosis. I would love to know what take or do more for mitochondria or other issues so that fatigue disapears. I heard that POTS is mitochondrial dysfunction and/or neuropathy

DIET

Lowish carbs, lowish histamine, gf/dr free organic diet for about a 11 months.

TREATEMENTS

T3/T4; thyroid plant activator, hydrocortisone20, B12 adeno+methyl 3500mg, B9 folinate calcium800mg, pregnenolone 50mg, 1 pill B complex from thorne research, PQQ, COq10 200mg, d-ribose, acetyl l carnitine, magnesium glycinate, magnesium malate, vitamin D3/K2 2000 UI, zinc 20mg, progesterone, vitamin C + quercetin 4000 mg, borache oil, fish oil, arginine 5ml, alpha lipoic acid, P5P, glycine 1000mg, iron 25mg, maitake mushrooms - polysacharrides to restore collagen and get rid of dysautonomia and Chiari like symptoms, olive leaf extract, grape seed extract, mangosteen, quercetin - trying to manage inflammation and glutathione IV +absorbic acid.

Thank you for reading and listening

### Stanislava — 2015-06-26T03:40:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-stranger-in-paris.15840/post-169603>

Thank you.

Yes, I did 23andme and I've got it interpreted by practionner also my functional endocrinologist who was chef of interne medicine before getting sick 25 years ago does Methylation. That's why I'm taking so many things already.
Lot's of autoimmunes markers, allergy markers, 18x chances for diabetes and mastocytosis, mitochondrial dysfunction 1 and 3 and if you're interested this is my 23andme report:

**Clotting Factors**

You possess a homozygous mutation to CETP that is a protective polymorphism that causes an increase in high density lipoproteins (HDL) and is associated with less cardiovascular problems due to a good ratio of HDL to total cholesterol.

You have a homozygous mutation to Factor XI that is commonly presented as a Factor XI deficiency or Rosenthal Syndrome. This can cause prolonged activated partial thrmoboplastin time (aPTT).

You also have a homozygous mutation to KNGI598T that causes a decrease in high molecular weight kininogen (HMWK) and low molecular weight kininogen (LMWK) that can cause problems in the kallikrein-kinin system and decreased amounts of bradykinin. This can cause an increase in arachidonic acid that can cause inflammation and increase in prostaglandin (PGE2) production. This can cause problems with regulation of blood pressure, sodium homeostasis, inflammation and heart health. This may, in fact, be part of the cause of your POTS-like symptoms. Increased intake of omega-3 fatty acids if tolerated can help to decrease arachidonic acid levels and in this way decrease inflammation.

**Detoxification**

The detoxification panel primarily has to do with tolerance/intolerance to various types of medications. You are heterozygous for CYP1A2 which is associated as being a somewhat slow metabolizer of polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs) that are found in cigarette smoke. Additionally you are somewhat slow at metabolizing caffeine, aflatoxin B1 (a type of mold found on peanuts), and acetaminophen (Tylenol). Your heterozygous status for
CYP2C9 also makes you a somewhat slow metabolizer of phenytoin and tolbutamide. This includes medications including S-warfarin (Coumarin), diclofenac (NSAID), phenytoin (seizures), tolbutamide and losartan (angiotensin receptor blocker).

**Gluten Intolerance**

You possess heterozygous mutations to HLA and HLA-DQA1. Both of these genes are considered to increase the risk of gluten intolerance.

**Immune System**

You possess quite a few polymorphisms related to Immunoglobulin A. This immunoglobulin A is used mostly in mucosal immunity. The heterozygous SNPs to HLA-DPB2 and HLA-DQA2 are often correlated with an increased risk for autoimmunity. The heterozygous SNP in IRF5 is associated with an increased risk for Lupus, Sjogrens, and Rheumatoid Arthritis. Finally, the homozygous SNP to TRAF1 is associated with a complement component 5 deficiency that may be responsible for issues including recurrent infections, liver fibrosis and rheumatoid arthritis.

Immunoglobulin E is related to the allergic response. You possess a homozygous mutation to FCER1A, a gene that encodes the IgE receptor. This is often found in those with exaggerated allergic response (such as in mast cell related disorders). Additional you possess a heterozygous mutation to IL5 that increases your risk for eosinophilic inflammatory diseases and a homozygous SNP to RAG1 that is correlated with immunodeficiency and increased levels of IgE.

A homozygous mutation to TNF (tumor necrosis factor) is a risk factor for autoimmunity, insulin resistance, cancer, food hypersensitivity, asthma and allergy.

**Methylation**

You have a homozygous polymorphism to ACE Del16. This is highly correlated to an increase in blood pressure via decreased conversion of angiotensin I to angiotensin II. This enzyme requires zinc as a cofactor and so adequate zinc intake is important in helping the functionality of this enzyme. It is further exacerbated by homozygous SNPs to NOS2 and heterozygous SNPs to NOS2 and NOS3. These SNPs cause an increase in ammonia (which may be elevated from poor sulfur clearance). The NOS SNPs increase your requirements for the amino acid L-arginine.

You have multiple homozygous SNPs to the CBS enzyme that is used to convert the amino acid homocysteine to cysteine so that it may be used for the generation of the natural antioxidant glutathione which is then used in the detoxification pathway. This polymorphism is associated with an increase in homocysteine levels, as well as decreased sulfur tolerance. While sulfur is a very important mineral involved in detoxification and other metabolic pathways, too much may cause an increase in ammonia levels which can be quite neurotoxic and stressful on the liver which must work to convert ammonia to the less toxic urea to be excreted by the kidneys. Following a reduced sulfur diet and supplementing with molybdenum can improve your sulfur status. I have included a document that details a reduced sulfur diet. The presence of heterozygous polymorphism to the enzyme BHMT will also exacerbate this SNP. BHMT allows homocysteine to be converted back to methionine and also generates the amino acid glycine which is important in detoxification. In your case, there may be homocysteine build up as this amino acid is poorly converted to cysteine and back to methionine in your body. Homocysteine is generally lowered by improving vitamin B12 and folate status. In order to maintain proper detoxification, taking one gram of glycine prior to bed time for a few months may be helpful. You can do one month of glycine intermittently to help maintain adequate levels.

You possess one heterozygous mutation for MTHFR A1298C and are all clear for MTHFR C677T. This riboflavin dependent enzyme is used to convert inactive folate into the active L-methylfolate. Your status indicates that you have a slightly higher than average requirement for folate. In general, the A1298C polymorphism is considered milder than the C677T variation because it does not increase homocysteine. I would recommend 800 mcg of folate, not folic acid. I would recommend half be taken as calcium folinate and the other half as the active L-methylfolate.

You have multiple SNPs related to neurotransmitter synthesis. Neurotransitters are brain chemicals that serve as messengers in the nervous system. Decreased folate availability from the MTHFR A1298C polymorphism and the MTRR A66G homozygous mutation (related to the vitamin B12 recycling system) will generally cause a decrease in the production of BH4 which is the precursor to serotonin, dopamine and norepinephrine. While you may have decreased synthesis of these neurotransmitters, this may be okay because you have multiple homozygous polymorphisms to the enzyme COMT that is responsible for breaking down dopamine and noepinephrine. This polymorphism is highly correlated to obsessive compulsive disorder (OCD) and intolerance of methyl donors including L-methylfolate, methylcobalamin, and trimethylglycine. You do, however, possess a homozygous mutation to VDR-TAQ, which will allow you to tolerate a bit more methyl groups than those who have COMT, but do not have the VDR-TAQ mutation. This will, however, cause an increase in your vitamin D requirements. I would recommend having your levels of 25(OH) D evaluated and supplementing vitamin D to bring your levels up to approximately 80. In regards to methylation, I would recommend generally using a mix of methylated and unmethylated folate and vitamin B12. As mentioned above, 400 mcg of calcium folinate and 400 mcg of L-methylfolate would be a good starting point. I would start with 2,000 mcg of vitamin B12 as hydroxocobalamin and then add an addition 1-2,000 mcg of methylcobalamin. Slowly add methyl donors to tolerance. If you notice increased anxiety then it is important to decrease the amount of methyl donors. You may take 50 mg of niacin to help counteract overmethylation on an as needed basis. It should also be noted that your homozygous mutation to the TCN2 C766G gene decreases vitamin B12 binding and therefore increases vitamin B12 requirements further

You possess multiple heterozygous SNPs to the GAD1 enzyme (glutamic acid decarboxylase). This enzyme is required to convert the excitatory neurotransmitter glutamate to the calming neurotransmitter GABA using vitamin B6 as its cofactor. It is also present in the pancreatic beta cells where it is used in insulin production. This genetic mutation is strongly correlated to both anxiety disorders and to increased blood glucose levels and decreased serum insulin levels. It may be helpful to supplement vitamin B6 as the active pyrodxal-5-phosphate at a dosage of 100 mg per day to help the activity of this enzyme if you have symptoms of either elevated blood glucose levels or anxiety.

You possess heterozygous SNPS to SOD2 and SOD3. These genetic mutations mean that you have a higher than normal requirement for antioxidants.

**Mitochondrial Function**

You possess multiple homozygous mutations to NDUFS7 showing that you have a mitochondrial complex I deficiency. You also have a heterozygous polymorphism to UQCR2 that is correlated with a complex III deficiency. As the powerhouse of the cell, mitochondria are essential in helping to create energy from our foods. These polymorphisms give you an increased requirement for the B-complex vitamins, Coenzyme Q10 and lipoic acid. I would recommend a 100 mg dosage of CoQ10 in combination with additional B-complex vitamins and 600 mg of lipoic acid twice per day.

### JenRN — 2015-06-26T22:06:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-stranger-in-paris.15840/post-169682>

Hello Stanislava, just want to give a word of encouragement here! This site can be a bit overwhelming with information but start with the getting started section as advised by others. Also I found the book to be a big help. You can make a master list of all the suggestions and check off each as you incorporate it. I do not know if its necessary to do them in order or if there is a hierarchy, perhaps a more knowledgeable person here can answer that. I had to just jump in and do what I can. I am also just starting like you.

Also I have spent a lot of time in Paris as I have family sort close to there. I adore your city but it feels highly electrically charged and puts me an edge. I feel it in the air and even before I knew about emf I would have to kick off my shoes and run for the parks. I prefer to walk barefoot everywhere there much to the horror of the very stylish ladies in sky scraper heels....lol. I do wear the cutest ankle and foot chains when I do though!

As gorgeous as they are, the city lights & Tower blinking all night and those florescent pharmacies signs putting out a high watts remind me of NYC which is EMF hell. The UK seems also close but I haven't spent much time there. I am in Los Angeles which is almost as bad and I live a block away from a cell tower. I had to make the big leap of faith and start reducing emf as my family is mocking me (husband is walking around with foil on his head as I type). I just purchased a whole house Schuman generator and it is already making a big difference. I have also been sleeping on a small magnet pad for a month (no difference yet) and I am waiting on a negative ion generator I just got off Amazon (praying for a difference). I have started to wear the blue blockers in the day as well and this feels very comfortable (Lots of comments though lol). Also if mold is a problem you might want to kill it with an ionizer.

I think the biggest thing is CT. Read up here on the many positive experiences on it. The anti inflammatory benefit should do you good and I can personally attest to it clearing brain fog. I hate it though and don't do it often as the 26 C in winter here has completely spoiled me. Anyway, welcome, good luck and know there is hope!

> [JenRN said:](/goto/post?id=169682)
>
> Hello Stanislava, just want to give a word of encouragement here! This site can be a bit overwhelming with information but start with the getting started section as advised by others. Also I found the book to be a big help. You can make a master list of all the suggestions and check off each as you incorporate it. I do not know if its necessary to do them in order or if there is a hierarchy, perhaps a more knowledgeable person here can answer that. I had to just jump in and do what I can. I am also just starting like you.
>
> Also I have spent a lot of time in Paris as I have family sort close to there. I adore your city but it feels highly electrically charged and puts me an edge. I feel it in the air and even before I knew about emf I would have to kick off my shoes and run for the parks. I prefer to walk barefoot everywhere there much to the horror of the very stylish ladies in sky scraper heels....lol. I do wear the cutest ankle and foot chains when I do though!
>
> As gorgeous as they are, the city lights & Tower blinking all night and those florescent pharmacies signs putting out a high watts remind me of NYC which is EMF hell. The UK seems also close but I haven't spent much time there. I am in Los Angeles which is almost as bad and I live a block away from a cell tower. I had to make the big leap of faith and start reducing emf as my family is mocking me (husband is walking around with foil on his head as I type). I just purchased a whole house Schuman generator and it is already making a big difference. I have also been sleeping on a small magnet pad for a month (no difference yet) and I am waiting on a negative ion generator I just got off Amazon (praying for a difference). I have started to wear the blue blockers in the day as well and this feels very comfortable (Lots of comments though lol). Also if mold is a problem you might want to kill it with an ionizer.
>
> I think the biggest thing is CT. Read up here on the many positive experiences on it. The anti inflammatory benefit should do you good and I can personally attest to it clearing brain fog. I hate it though and don't do it often as the 26 C in winter here has completely spoiled me. Anyway, welcome, good luck and know there is hope!

Hey Jen thank you for your kind words.

Yes I'm well aware I need to run from here :-D I can feel it. With pheo like symptoms I was so sensitive to light and noise before that I was in dark room for weeks. And going to doctors in the center was real hell.

Which shumann generator did you get I've seen there are three different ones, one is even for mitochondria.

Do you wear it on you whole time or it's something that protects whole house/ appartement even when on the floor?

About magnetico pad I heard you have to get the most expensive one to see real difference in their range sleep pad. No in my budget just yet. But generator, ionizer. etc I can do.

Thank you for ionizer I will definitely buy one.

I must admit I didn't read about CT. I was more concentrated on leptin, gut, hormones, mitochondria. And with my horrible short term memory, brain fog and especialy as I've POTS it's dizzy when I've to read, the words don't stay put :-D so read Jack is pretty challenging. I don't feel really dizziness much now, only when I've to zoom in and concentrate, then it's pretty bad. I was avid reader before so not being able to read much is real change.

CT means cold shower right. I did it twice today for 2-3 minutes. My inflammation is really strange. I can feel my neck and back of my head inflamed really badly and it gets my brain fog and fatigue even worst. I feel it after food, even healthy fats any fats doesn't matter if it's coconut, ghee is worst, olive is better. Don't start me on any dairy, not good as well. It's kind of strange. I will try digestive enzymes with ox bile. Even if I overdo it or there is stress it comes back. This is one of my biggest issues. It comes and goes very often. It feels like neck flu so often. And being good for absolutely nothing so often is kind of annoying.

If I could get down from 78 symptoms to these few, I know I can get these last symptoms better. Just I'm kind of stuck and it has been a while. So just go away symptoms now. ;-)

### caroline — 2015-06-29T12:41:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-stranger-in-paris.15840/post-169859>

Please read the easy start guide for C/T ....Dr. Kruse is very specific. Start with face dunks. Do you have a bath tub?

### JenRN — 2015-06-30T03:46:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-stranger-in-paris.15840/post-169990>

I am so glad you can relocate, it will be much healthier for you! A few answers:

1\. the Schumann generator is the only whole house one sold on lessemf.com. It plugs into the wall and runs through there. The company is the manufacturer and they were very good answering my million questions. I've had it a few weeks in the living room by a wall with a smart meter and a large tv. So far it just feels a bit more "comfortable" , less prickly in the air.

2\. I got a travel sized pad from promagnets.com for less than a hundred. I'm very skeptical about magnets as I've seen many pain patients use them to no avail (I'm a nurse). I think its placebo honestly but because the Doctor explained it and I'm starting to understand redux , I'm open to starting with this cheaper one first. I spoke at length with the company and educated me on the importance of shape(round) and orientation (north) for recharging the body gradually. I'm sleeping right on top of it for the past month so the jury is still out....
I'm going to try structuring water with it to see if that works next.

3\. CT can be spot treatment also, not just showers. And a soak on the tub. The Doctor really explains it well and gives thorough instructions here so please do read. Perhaps you can ask him directly though about your reaction, you don't want to injure yourself passing out from dizziness.

4 the ionizer is by enerzen I believe off amazon for $99 US. Its for commercial use so I have to leave the room while its working and then air it out but it's been effective for ridding mold smell.

Hope that helps!

# Thread: Los Angeles Newbie
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/los-angeles-newbie.15884/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JenRN — 2015-06-26T21:17:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/los-angeles-newbie.15884/post-169679>

Hello all! I am actually not a "new" newbie as I've been lurking on and off for several years. This however is my first time posting and effort to fully incorporate the JK lifestyle. I have to admit I was originally quite skeptical but as the Doctor has unfolded the biophysics behind the Quilt, I have become a complete believer. It makes sense to me intuitively and empirically.

My background is total geek when it come to physics, epigenetics, physiology and biochem. I am an RN specializing in lifestyle medicine and nutrition for chronic disease management & a trained clinical aromatherapist. My nutrition philosophy never aligned with the evidence based protocols for diabetes and hypertension and now I know why....which leads me to the crossroads I am at professionally....

I will be finishing up a FNP program and really look forward to partnering with MDs who believe in the Quantum approach to health. I can no longer practice the standard- avoid salt, get your 60% carbs and take your pills routine. I believe the Quantum paradigm is needs to part of solution to our health care crisis.

OK, sorry for the rant and back to my personal health journey. I am mid 40’s diagnosed over the last decade with hyperthyroidism, obesity, uterine fibroids, pre diabetes and hypertension. A typical picture for a typical African American female in this country.

Except, I haven’t lived the typical American life style nor have I ever had the typical diseases. I have never eaten SAD, always been active, thin and energetic. Grew up strict vegetarian, then vegan and then high protein (red meat) per blood type as an adult. No drinking, drugs etc. The only health issues I had was uterine fibroids as a teen after starting the pill and a strong family history of hyperthyroidism. I was the one family member who was asymptomatic and just monitored for this.

However, my health took a huge spiral down 10 years ago. Looking back I correlate this to moving back to Los Angeles under duress, and getting both a cell phone and WiFI at home for the first time. Before the move here I spent 12 years in the Midwest living 10 feet from a lake and having ice parties on it all winter. The city wasn’t rural but close enough. I did live L.A. previously before the midwest stint for a few years but I was a kid playing outdoors daily and a lot of the electrical toys/tools didn't exist then. I am also originally from Miami, lived close to the beach and our family ritual was a “spirit cleansing” swim every weekend. (I hated it then, would pay good money for it now).

Anyway within three months of moving here I developed vertigo, intense migraines and carb cravings for the first time. I gained 40lbs literally in weeks and my BP went up . After 15 years, the fibroids I had removed also starting regrowing. I have spent close to 7k out of pocket seeing a world famous endocrinologist whose best contribution was HCG for the weight gain and telling me all about the evils of reverse T3. I stopped seeing him 3 years ago as I continued to get worse and just couldn't afford all the supplements and monthly labs. I now see just my HMO doc who adheres strictly to evidence based protocols only. I have been semi paleo for 5 years (at times I “need” high carbs for hypoglycemic crashes) which is how I discovered this site yet I’ve only lost ten lbs of the 48 I regained after Hcg. I have never done and couldn’t do Crossfit if I tried and instead do yoga, treadmill walks and FIR saunas.

I am now noticing mild memory problems and slowing of thought. Of course my MD thinks this is normal age related changes at 45. I had a CT for the fibroids last month which showed calcium plaques elsewhere and this wasn’t even mentioned until I asked to see the results...when I asked my PCP about it, I was told its common for African Americans and “normal ‘ for my age...

So at this point I am truly scared. I have done all the “healthy things” I have been taught yet I’m headed for an early grave. I feel as if I am de evolving in a system that does not promote life. I truly believe epigenetic switches got pulled by environmental mis matched triggers that until recently I didn't know about or understand. I thank God I am starting to now.

My biggest challenge is following through on what I know is good such as reducing nnEMF and money for testing. School loans payments are in full effect......and it is sooo easy to forget to hit the lights after dark when I am used to computer work at 1 am. I have bought some tools and am making changes but it is still a hit or miss at this point. So I am here to strengthen my resolve by keeping a journal here, getting a deeper understanding of solutions & changes and most of all, to give and take support on this road less travelled. Whatever I can contribute, I will. I am looking forward to this new journey, reversing the damage done and living a full healthy life as meant to be!

### fitness@home — 2015-06-26T21:37:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/los-angeles-newbie.15884/post-169681>

Welcome Jen!

What are your chances of getting out of LA and relocating to a better environment? That big of a change can take time and planning (i.e. saving $). Have you tested your current living space/work space with a trifield meter? It can be eye opening.

Make sure you start a journal

### JenRN — 2015-06-26T22:27:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/los-angeles-newbie.15884/post-169684>

Hello and thank you for the response. Leaving L.A. is the plan in 3 - 5 years due to money and work /school commitments. I would love to go back to Miami as close to the water as possible but my husband is more keen on Texas as he has undeveloped property there out in the boonies. When I first married him that sounded like hell and I had nightmares of sleeping in utter darkness, and having to hunt for food and drinking muddy well water (heaven forbid!!!)..... I am a city girl after all! But now I am open to it as long as I can get to urban areas/ internet by car. My, how my perspective has changed since getting sick.

Is there a meter brand you would suggest? There seems to be so many types that measure all kinds of energies and I can afford only one. I admit to being scared to see the results though....

I will be starting a journal this weekend! I think its really important for being serious about changing.

### nicld — 2015-07-01T16:14:55+0300

Yes, you have opened my eyes to this. It never occurred to me that what we can't see is killing us. I was convinced for years that I had a mystery virus but now I know it's the environment. I am on this site to lean how to survive the damage until I leave this city....

When I first married him that sounded like hell and I had nightmares of sleeping in utter darkness, and having to hunt for food and drinking muddy well water (heaven forbid!!!)..... I am a city girl after all! But now I am open to it as long as I can get to urban areas/ internet by car. My, how my perspective has changed since getting sick.
>
> Is there a meter brand you would suggest? There seems to be so many types that measure all kinds of energies and I can afford only one. I admit to being scared to see the results though....
>

This made me think of a forum member who lived in San Fran ..... never owned a pair of flat shoes! only ever wore high heels.

Now they live in Oregon and she is having the time of her life.

She is spreading the word ...and has a website ....I will try to find it.
her name is ...Elaine Goldman

### Celeste — 2015-07-17T21:52:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/los-angeles-newbie.15884/post-172195>

I am also in LA. And newly back on the forum. We had our house tested and changed some things to make it better. Welcome!

### Penny — 2015-07-17T23:45:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/los-angeles-newbie.15884/post-172197>

You could start at [www.antennasearch.com](http://www.antennasearch.com) - and then [www.clear.com](http://www.clear.com) to convince yourself how hopeless L.A. is - you have to move - especially if you're in Burbank... "radio city" - plus, the whole place is covered in 4G (clear.com will point you to that partiular nightmare) - if I were you, I would be popping BH4 - and embracing the cold... a lot

[http://www.cardiab.com/content/6/1/35](http://www.cardiab.com/content/6/1/35)
And this was really good:
[http://deepblue.lib.umich.edu/bitstream/handle/2027.42/58432/jenkinsg\_1.pdf?sequence=1](http://deepblue.lib.umich.edu/bitstream/handle/2027.42/58432/jenkinsg_1.pdf?sequence=1)

As far as a meter goes, my new darling is this one:
[http://takebackyourpower.net/shop/electromagnetic-radiation-testers/](http://takebackyourpower.net/shop/electromagnetic-radiation-testers/)

If possible, walk the beach between 9:00-11:00A.M. - and spray yourself with epsom salts beforehand...
[https://www.jackkruse.com/tensegrity-7-black-hole-sun-come/](https://www.jackkruse.com/tensegrity-7-black-hole-sun-come/)

For the diabetes, I like the berberines plus bitter melon + gymnema sylvestre + fenugreek - I also like glycine before bed to help with that 2:00AM cortisol spike -

I'm currently also experimenting with fermented papaya - which is all the rage in the Parkinson's community - I would guess it gets rid of misfolded proteins and spikes superoxide and all that biochemistry that I must confess, makes my head hurt

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/16823100](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/16823100)
[http://www.treanime.com.au/references.html](http://www.treanime.com.au/references.html)

In fact, I would say anyone on this forum who has any protein misfolding going on would benefit from fermented papaya... just get a papaya, blast it in a food processor with 1 tsp turmeric+1 tsp cinnamon - then stick it in a mason jar covered in plastic wrap for 5 days - eat 1 tbl with each meal - papaya is a natural protease - truly, if anything can help someone with Parkinson's, it has to be helpful to the rest of us...

[http://www.favabeans.parkinsonsrecovery.com/how-to-make-fermented-papaya](http://www.favabeans.parkinsonsrecovery.com/how-to-make-fermented-papaya)

google "fermented papaya ampk" - there is a boatload of research on it - you can do the same with glycine+ampk too -

If you moved west of Thousand Oaks, you would at least be out of the 4G area - just my opinion

# Thread: Jack Ware in Columbus, Ohio
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-ware-in-columbus-ohio.16030/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jackware — 2015-07-01T05:47:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-ware-in-columbus-ohio.16030/post-170178>

Been a follower of Dr. Kruse for a few years. Have made the switch to eating as much seafood as possible, experimented with CT from time-to-time, spend a lot of time outside in my barefeet, keep my water intake really high, etc.

What made me decide to join the forum today was I need to lose some weight (10-15lbs) but have been having right knee pain for about half a year (after training for a half marathon) and over the last 6 weeks or so have had pain in my left tibia, which I think is a stress fracture. I work as a Logistics Manager in a warehouse and walk on concrete all day. I also just came back from a Key West, Florida vacation where I did a lot of walking on concrete (Duval Street) in minimalist sandals.

Being unable to exercise my lower body or do cardio I wanted to start, for the first time, a rigorous CT program to see what results I could get. Look forward to chatting with everyone. If anyone is in Columbus, OH let me know. I seem to have found a restaurant that does $1 oysters every Monday evening. Maybe we could meet up? Thanks.

### fitness@home — 2015-07-01T13:27:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-ware-in-columbus-ohio.16030/post-170212>

Welcome jackware. Can you please put up a picture as your avatar?

I'm from Michigan originally but now live in Texas. When you say "rigorous CT" what do you mean exactly?

I enjoy CT, but find I need something to help keep my mind busy. Haven't learned to just let my mind go and relax in the tank. Something I need to work on.

### jackware — 2015-07-01T17:15:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-ware-in-columbus-ohio.16030/post-170224>

Where in Texas? I meant a consistent and regular CT practice.

### fitness@home — 2015-07-01T18:49:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-ware-in-columbus-ohio.16030/post-170233>

> [jackware said:](/goto/post?id=170224)
>
> Where in Texas? I meant a consistent and regular CT practice.

We are in NW Houston. Kind of at the outer edge of the city limits.

My husband built me a CT tank and I am loving coming home to it chilled and ready to go. The chiller and pump turn on at about 2 pm and turn off at 7 pm. It is chilled to 55 degrees.

We have cut a piece of foam insulation to fit as a cover. It checked the temp this morning and it was at 57 degrees.

### shah78 — 2015-07-01T20:37:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-ware-in-columbus-ohio.16030/post-170241>

A rigorous CT program will work just fine. Ten hours of 50 degree water per week has allowed me to attain a body composition at age 59 far better than when I was a triathlete thirty years prior. Instead of thirty hours of exercise a week, I walk ten miles a week and go to the gym twenty minutes a month. Yes PER MONTH. Now get in the tub! In fact I'm in my tub as I speak. I only allow myself computer time while I'm in the tub. Keeps me in longer and keeps me off the computer otherwse.

### jackware — 2015-07-02T02:10:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-ware-in-columbus-ohio.16030/post-170272>

Very informative and inspiring reply, and a great avatar picture! I'll update my progress in the CT area. Thanks.

### jackware — 2015-07-14T01:07:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-ware-in-columbus-ohio.16030/post-171871>

Filled up a few more old water bottles yesterday. Now I'm ready to throw in ten of them at my next CT.

### jackware — 2015-07-22T21:37:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-ware-in-columbus-ohio.16030/post-172677>

CT is progressing nicely. Cold showers are now the norm and are enjoyable!

### nicld — 2015-07-26T20:13:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-ware-in-columbus-ohio.16030/post-173105>

> [shah78 said:](/goto/post?id=170241)
>
> A rigorous CT program will work just fine. Ten hours of 50 degree water per week has allowed me to attain a body composition at age 59 far better than when I was a triathlete thirty years prior. Instead of thirty hours of exercise a week, I walk ten miles a week and go to the gym twenty minutes a month. Yes PER MONTH. Now get in the tub! In fact I'm in my tub as I speak. I only allow myself computer time while I'm in the tub. Keeps me in longer and keeps me off the computer otherwse.

I do that at home too. Only on the computer on the weekends during CT.

### nicld — 2015-07-26T20:13:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-ware-in-columbus-ohio.16030/post-173107>

> [jackware said:](/goto/post?id=172677)
>
> CT is progressing nicely. Cold showers are now the norm and are enjoyable!

I just cannot get into the cold showers, so hats off to you. I do a final rinse with cold every morning though.

### jackware — 2015-07-28T04:53:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-ware-in-columbus-ohio.16030/post-173274>

Two weeks now of cold showers. My body hasn't sniffed warm water.

### jackware — 2015-09-05T20:52:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-ware-in-columbus-ohio.16030/post-176557>

Thought it was a time for a new photo. Showing off my beard growth and the shaka in honor of August 2016 being the month my wife and I will be traveling across the country to deliver our vehicle in Oakland to be shipped to Hawaii. Then we'll board an Alaska Airlines flight for a one-way ticket to Hawaii! Fantastic to know it's less than one year away!

### jackware — 2016-03-08T00:54:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-ware-in-columbus-ohio.16030/post-190365>

6 months later and we are still on track for Hawaii! Was excited to see some sun out in Danville, Ohio this morning when I opened my eyes so I lept out of bed and limped to the porch (feet hurt first thing in the morning). When I came back in after a few minutes my wife said, "Did you go out there naked?"

Answer; "Yep!"

It had been over a week without any direct sun and I had been bumming bad. We also started a blog about our journey, if anyone wants to check us out; [www.texawaii.com](http://www.texawaii.com)

Hello, happy to be here.

Recently got interested in the phenomenon of Sun Gazing. I have always been keen on general health, and so have been free of dis-eases for many years, and I'd like to keep it that way.

I have a few disadvantages where I live though, and of course, there are questions.

L. Rijklof

# Thread: Hello from Portland, OR
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-portland-or.16878/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Krysti — 2015-09-18T00:10:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-portland-or.16878/post-177299>

Hi all,

My name is Krysti, I am 29 years old living about an hour south of Portland Oregon. I've been a wall-starfish for a while now. I live on 10 acres, and unfortunately, there's a cell tower about 1.5 miles away from me. We do not have internet at the house, but do have full coverage cell service because of that blasted tower.

I've been on and off reading Jack's block for about 5 years, my eldest sister introduced me. I've also been on and off following the Leptin Reset (I can't seem to fix it, but as per current blogs, I'm extremely blue light toxic). The husband is not at all on board with limiting blue lights, cell use in the bedroom, TV late at night, or any of the other things I'm trying to accomplish.

I'm a Personal Trainer (mostly specializing in postural distortion and elderly), so I move regularly, but don't specifically power lift or any of the other stereotypical gym stuff. The building I work in has wifi and fluorescent lights. I'm surrounded by it.

I've been Paleo for about 5 years now, strict Auto Immune Paleo with Leptin Reset influences. I have celiac disease and let them take my gallbladder about 1 year before I learned of the blog. Now I know better and am frustrated I let them. My main foods are salmon, sardines, oysters and whatever veggies are in season at the farmers market.

I "baby" CT every morning for the last couple minutes of my shower. Turn that baby to cold and stand there for a while. I've yet to do a full dip.

Life goals: Change hospital nutrition. As $ allows I'll be going for a Dietetics degree so I can play the political game with the right papers attached to my wall. My youngest sister died of stage 4 glioblastoma at age 13. We pureed her food in a blender and fed her through her feeding tube once we got home. Mostly fish and veggies. It smelled like cat food. But while at the hospital they gave her "sugar with a multivitamin" as I called it. Similac. The time she needed the best nutrition in her life, they wouldn't let us.

Personal goals: Optimal health for myself and my family. Regulated hormones and circadian rhythm, low body fat percentage when annually appropriate, and endless energy that comes when summer is around.

Thanks all!

### Scompy — 2015-09-18T00:40:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-portland-or.16878/post-177300>

Welcome...! I'm 110% with you on your goals. I'll do what I can too to help change hospital nutrition--I was pulling out my hair seeing people eating the worst possible foods at hospitals before, during and after surgeries. I posted this thread recently: [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/hospital-emf-mitigation.16732/#post-176001](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/hospital-emf-mitigation.16732/#post-176001)

Oregonian myself... raised in Corvallis and I'm an OSU alumni (B.S. in Biology).

### shah78 — 2015-09-18T01:02:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-portland-or.16878/post-177304>

The "full dip" is much easier than the shower "money shot". And two levels of magnitude more effective. Just saying. Welcome.

### Krysti — 2015-09-24T01:58:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-portland-or.16878/post-177705>

> [shah78 said:](/goto/post?id=177304)
>
> The "full dip" is much easier than the shower "money shot". And two levels of magnitude more effective. Just saying. Welcome.

I've put some jugs of water in the freezer to make ice...courage here I come! When I rafted in the grand canyon (50\*F constant H2O temp) I CTd every AM, but also when I was in the GC I awoke and slept with the sun, no alarm clock, wifi, artificial blue etc.

### Scompy — 2015-09-25T04:42:00+0300

# Thread: Hi hi from Brisbane Australia ✌️
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-hi-from-brisbane-australia-%E2%9C%8C%EF%B8%8F.17153/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Haley_Pendragon — 2015-11-24T11:29:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-hi-from-brisbane-australia-%E2%9C%8C%EF%B8%8F.17153/post-182567>

Hi all!

My name is Haley and I'm a 32yo Naturopath in North Brisbane. I'm also studying my post grad Bachelor of Medicines Management with Professional Honours in Complementary Medicine part time.

I currently run a health food store where I spend 5 days/wk, and spend the remainder of my time seeing clients in my clinic ([www.facebook.com/aliveinwonderlandhealth](http://www.facebook/aliveinwonderlandhealth)), writing articles (I'm a paid health blog writer), studying, and of course spending time with my kids (mum of 2 kids under 10)... Occasionally if I'm lucky I'll take my motorbike for a cruise up the mountain.

I was drawn to Dr Kruse's work through the paleo scene... People such as Chris Kresser were on my reading list... Then I began delving deeper, seeing Joshs's (Paleo Osteo's) comments on our practitioner networks and read of Jack's Epi-Paleo Rx - I have been immersed in reading & listening to Jack's work ever since (big podcast fan as I find podcasts easier in my busy life!). I tend to spend the majority of my lunch breaks reading Dr Kruse's work from his blog and I often have "sunlight moments" (no longer known as "light bulb moments") that I share with my colleagues & clients regularly.

My end of year gift list this year consists mostly of books, recommended by Jack on podcasts. I am always open to more reading suggestions as knowledge is power! I have read many of the blogs, and often re-read them.

I'm grateful to live on the east coast of sunny Australia where fresh seafood is rife. My 9yo son is a big fan of oysters in particular!

My main interests lie within mental health (neurotransmitters, anxiety, depression, stress, PTSD), heavy metals, gut health, addiction, diabetes, adhd and improving people's wellness in general through education. Most of it is all linked anyway.

Glad to be here on the forum. Being so busy I will likely not spend too much time 'foruming' but I am always keeping up as best I can.

Thanks for having me!

Warm regards,
Haley ✌️

### Haley_Pendragon — 2015-11-24T22:02:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-hi-from-brisbane-australia-%E2%9C%8C%EF%B8%8F.17153/post-182598>

Thanks! Looking forward to more learning ahead.

### caroline — 2015-11-25T01:18:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-hi-from-brisbane-australia-%E2%9C%8C%EF%B8%8F.17153/post-182616>

Welcome Haley. We have a few members in Brisbane and I am a little south of Sydney.

### Jenny S — 2015-11-25T05:17:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-hi-from-brisbane-australia-%E2%9C%8C%EF%B8%8F.17153/post-182624>

Hi Haley, I'm in Hervey Bay at present.
Sounds like you are very busy but doing well!

### Haley_Pendragon — 2015-12-08T12:33:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-hi-from-brisbane-australia-%E2%9C%8C%EF%B8%8F.17153/post-183683>

> Hi Haley, I'm in Hervey Bay at present.
> Sounds like you are very busy but doing well!

Hi Jenny! That's not too far from me at all.

I'm really interested in finding a good quality magnetic sleep pad here in Australia that I can use for myself & kids, but also that I can refer to my clients. I've been looking through some posts on this from 2013 to try and find suggestions for something like the Magnetico available locally, and if any of you lovely Aussies can give me some guidance on that I would really appreciate it! Thanks!

### Matthew Blackburn — 2015-12-01T07:32:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matt-from-san-diego-ca.17180/post-182947>

**Age:28 Sex:** M **Joined JackKruse.com: Today! 11/30/15**

**Location:** San Diego, CA

**Current Rx:** Cold Thermogenesis. I've been on 100% cold showers for the past 3 weeks. Once I move out of my parents in a month I plan to get a 150gal rubbermaid tub to soak in...

**Current Therapies / Protocols:** Detox maintenance weekly. At least 1x far infrared sauna session, 1 coffee enema, 1 tsp of 100% pure gum spirits of turpentine. Mg bicarbonate daily in 10.5 pH acid free + contaminant free water. 32oz of ozonated water in the morning. Daily AM sun. I've been blocking blue at night since May of 2014. I originally learned about it from Dave Asprey. Occasional blood cleaning with 50-100 microamperes on the radial/ulnar arteries.

**Health WINS:** Access to the webinars! I'm soaking them up. I've been eating oysters daily (3-4) and everytime I eat them like clockwork I feel pressure in my head.. a good kind of pressure. I've been grounding while I sleep for the past four years so I believe i'm increasing electron flow to my brain through the intake of DHA? I was vegan/vegetarian for most of the past five years.

**Health Goals:** I would like to gain strength and mass. I've been pretty stuck at 130.

**My Optimal Journal:** I have a YouTube channel where I share biohacks/detox stuff. The link is [www.youtube.com/astralfish](http://www.youtube.com/astralfish)

Thank you for all that you do, Jack! I've been downloading your stuff nearly nonstop for the past month. Its in line with all of the research that i've been doing over the years.

### Toby King — 2015-12-29T12:22:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-byron-bay-australia.17310/post-185074>

Just checking in with this community again...
To catch everyone up I am a teenager (16), lived in southeast asia, and had a past of adrenal fatigue, inflamation and poor choices.

Loving the Adrenal Rx! The sun has become like a new friend that I check in with every morning ☀️☀️☀️

Continuing with Leptin reset (BAB, 3 meals max, epi-paleo diet). Monitoring and minding my eye-clock as much as possible... A couple of bad mistakes of going out too late and leaving my blueblockers at home.

A few days ago I tried out a friends Google Glass for half an hour or so in the evening. Ironically as I got home I turned on the podcast Jack did with Ben Greenfield where they specifically talked about the reason it has failed and the issues associated with pulsed electromagnetic fields. Turned out I had god-aweful sleep that night and a blinding headache the next morning. Seems my biology was telling me something!

On a disparate note of using technology for good I just purchased a double pack of beta Quantlets! So excited for the possibilities that this might have!

Merry Christmas and happy holidays to all of you!

# Thread: Greeting from Rochester, NY!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greeting-from-rochester-ny.17597/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kingers — 2016-02-23T15:25:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greeting-from-rochester-ny.17597/post-189637>

Hi everyone,

My wife and I live in upstate NY, specifically in the Rochester area. I've been a health coach for a number of years and am fascinated with the information presented by Dr. Kruse.

Over the past few weeks, I've started my first n=1 experiment. At night I have started to use candles to light the house and use orange tinted glasses to block out any of the other blue lights from devices/appliances/etc. In addition, I've been turning off the circuits to our bed room as much as I can. We were sleeping with our bed right against the wall, with sockets directly near our heads. After just a few days, I was and still am dreaming substantially more every single night. I'm taking this as a good sign. Time to do some reading on blue light, melatonin, EMF, and sleep.

Glad to be part of the crew!

Sincerely,
Ken

### Kingers — 2016-02-23T15:34:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greeting-from-rochester-ny.17597/post-189638>

I should add that I work as a software engineer so I'm particularly interested in hacking that part of the environment. We're planning on converting our current wifi set up (which is turned off when not in use) to a hardwired Ethernet set up for our home.

### caroline — 2016-02-23T22:49:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greeting-from-rochester-ny.17597/post-189676>

Welcome Kingers......

# Thread: Greetings from Collingwood, ON

### Sam Richardson — 2016-02-28T19:46:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-collingwood-on.17616/post-189924>

Good afternoon everybody!

If you're in northern Ontario right now you're probably also aware of how beautiful it is outside today. Ran into lots of people cruising down the snowy shores and it was all smiles and love being passed along by everybody. My friend and I, Hershey, are really enjoying what Mother Nature is throwing at us!

I, first and foremost, would like to thank Dr. Kruse for providing this platform to myself and all the like minded individuals who share their stories on their path to optimal health and, ultimately, happiness. This cornucopia of health information has been a blessing in my life, and I cannot express in words how grateful I am that I have found this wonderful group of individuals to help me along my journey. I will share as many of my own experiences I can to do my part in giving back to this community, and hopefully be able to affect someone else's life in the same positive manner mine has been.

Peace and love from Canada.

Sam

Hey guys my name is Adrian, I'm 20 years old from Australia. I've had chronic Gut issues for 2-3 years and recently underwent FMT (fecal microbe transplantation) however it seems as though I'm
Not overcoming my illness.. I've done copious amounts of research as I'm a medical student (had to defer due to constant pain, depression and Brain fog). Recently tests are showing low WBC elevated bilirubin my functional liver detox panel shows insanely slow phase 2 (for all conjugations) and I'm waiting on some gene tests... I'd love some advice as I'm starting to get desperate!
PS born normally and breast fed as a child.. Was a competitive bodybuilder for 4 years whic I know didn't help...
Bless you all x

# Thread: ~~ Like two pine cones kissing noses ~~
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/like-two-pine-cones-kissing-noses.17639/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mobeus — 2016-03-05T10:30:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/like-two-pine-cones-kissing-noses.17639/post-190221>

High Friends

I'm delighted to be here - it's an honor to connect with so many on a similar path. Happy to contribute a little, learn from everyone, and just generally sharing my experience with bio-hacking.

My name is Morgan. I'm 37, living on an island in the salish sea, and i've always been in "good" health. My definition of "good" has been evolving since 2004. But has taken a quantum leap in the last few months. I've spent several years vagabonding around asia/india/europe/iceland/south america. Then i restored a wooden sailboat and cruised for 5 years. Now i'm founding a company to build biologic homes out of cold-fired crystalline ceramics. So many life style changes.

I'm 5' 10" and my weight has fluctuated from about 185 to 155. But mostly between 155-165. I have not eaten processed foods since 2005, i've experimented with a raw diet, spent many months eating only fresh fish (tuna and salmon, still quivering) and rice. In retrospect, the last 6 months may have been my first "bio-hack". I have been eating a lot of potatoes, pork/steak/chicken/bacon, tons of cheese, tortilla chips, sour cream, not enough living foods. I've gained 15 lbs in 6 months. Such is the price of being a penniless entrepreneur 'boot strapping' for two years.

Thankfully I just came across Jack and some pieces fell together - information i have been longing for.

Health Goals for Next 3 months:

1) More wild salmon, more coconut oil, local/organic salads, the more vitality the better...sprouting.
2) Grounded for at least 14 hours/day.
3) Almost no blue light - except i have to look at this screen! How bad is that?
4) High Intensity Strength Training - Doug McGuff: Body by Science.
5) Lots of dancing and running barefoot in the sunshine fields.
6) Singing, stoned, naked, and exalted in the cold rain!
7) Integrating a unified fractal field theory into life experience - Dan Winter.
8) Integrating principles of feng shui into home/work/routine.
9) Practicing non-accumulation to stay in trust and in flow.

Technically i am a full on "newby".

All of the above is in progress. I use a land line and wired internet. Cell rarely. Living on acreage on island with no wifi in range. No bluetooth.

I drink untreated water from a well on my land, and will be experimenting with structuring the water using phase conjugate EM (devices i will build), Flannagan's "crystal water", Sedlemayrs "divinia water", Dan Winter's "Super Imploder", and probably some ORMUS.

With oceans of jOy and light waves of DHA,

Morgan

### Lahelada — 2016-03-05T12:01:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/like-two-pine-cones-kissing-noses.17639/post-190227>

Welcome ! I love your story. You should start a journal. You had me at "restoring a wooden boat"-we did that too/
Looking forward to the next installments of the story.

Hi,
I can remember when I was around 7 to 9 years old, I had a strange habit that I liked to do in the bathtub.
I would put in the plug, turn the cold on slow, lie down and imagine myself in the arctic tundra naked.
For some reason I enjoyed doing that immensely!

I am so grateful to have learned of Dr Jack Kruse, and although I am not obese, I do not feel optimal.

I would be interested if anyone would like to share labs and offer insights / biohacks.
My most recent labs done are an Adrenal Stress Index, Metabolic Assessment and CBC panel.

I am so far taking cold showers, using mocassins and UVEX shades, and eating organ meats like cow tongue, heart and liver to great enjoyment,

Thanks!

### Alper — 2016-03-31T15:23:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-hannover-germany.17754/post-191769>

Hi,

my name is Alper, medical student from Hannover, Germany and I´ve been following Jacks blog for quite a while. Lately I am trying the cold thermogenesis protocol and I am excited for the progress and the results. I´m not sure if I am sensitiv to leptin but I´ll give the epi-paleo diet a try.

Looking forward to read many interesting threads here.

Alper

### Alper — 2023-05-24T14:21:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-hannover-germany.17754/post-321636>

Hey people,

I have been offline for a while. I found my way back to the forum after following some of the podcasts with Jack as well as patreon in the last 6 month.
In the meantime i finished med school and became MD in 2018/19. The light/water/magnestism-story hit me hard in the last couple month again. I realised, even more after med school, working in a hospital, that the perspective of the centralized system is so limited, especially looking at chronic diseases. Except the limited "disease concepts", i got confronted with the shitty light i have to work under, even though, luckily, no night- and evening-shifts for me, cause I work in a psychotherapeutical department and love it.

I try to ground as much as possible, got my self some proper blue-blocking-glases that I wear every evening or when I have to face screens especially later in the day and my sleep (which was shitty for quite a while) has improved just by this so much.

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/white-spots-on-my-skin.19486/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/white-spots-on-my-skin.19486/) this story hasnt changed much i think, even though right now the spots are hard to see because i guess my skin got even more atrophic. I wonder though, if white spots, compared to what you mentioned, Jack, on the australian podcasts with Max, referers to melanin-issues in the somite/dermatom/myotom etc. Would make sense for me, because in the same areas I got quite some (viszeral) fat as well.

Well, nice to be back here.

# Thread: Super pumped to be among you guys!!

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Constant_Growth — 2016-06-16T08:16:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/super-pumped-to-be-among-you-guys.18059/post-196672>

Hi everyone!

I originally found this place a while back, but have only just realized how awesome it is. I was searching for ways to overcome my insomnia initially and that led me to notice all the other cool stuff going on here. I have a friend who's also a big fan and had some of the webinars which I've been listening to over the past few days.

I plan on becoming a premium "Klub" member very soon. I'm a huge fan of Jack and the work he is sharing, so much so that I'm also going to be dedicating a section of my website to note taking of his various programs and webinars, etc.

I've already started taking notes, with my associated plans of actions, if interested in a more thorough intro into what drew me to Jack's work, etc, you can read that here:

[http://healthvibed.com/category/notes/jack-kruse-notes/](http://healthvibed.com/category/notes/jack-kruse-notes/)

I really look forward to discussing the protocols and results with you guys on here, and becoming a very active (and hopefully valuable member and contributor)

I believe it's very challenging to be healthy and live optimally in this modern world, and Jack's information is invaluable while one is attempting to make that journey.

Look forward to getting to you know you all better!

# Thread: Hi everyone! Glad to be on the forum finally! :)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-everyone-glad-to-be-on-the-forum-finally.18159/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Arsim — 2016-07-13T23:09:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-everyone-glad-to-be-on-the-forum-finally.18159/post-198323>

Hi everyone I live on a small island in Michigan called Grosse Ile. After following Dr. Jack Kruse for over two years. I just now made an account lol. Took me sometime to really understand how environment and sunlight dictate so much of our health. All the sun, seafood, & magnetism really helped my mind work better thanks to the dopamine etc... I want to thank Dr. Jack Kruse for everything he has taught me and also everyone on the forum, it has really changed my life. Because of all the amazing things I have learned I transferred from Lawrence technological university to Arizona State University (online & also visited there for 6 months) to become a health sciences lifestyle coach. I used to have terrible acne face, back, & chest. Went the conventional route (dermatologist lol) which made my intestinal issues even worse & my mood. I worked at a health food store for awhile, and was so focused on food/supplementation.... wasted a lot of money on supplements a lot.. I was so eager to cure my acne. The way i got into health was my freshman year learning about government "conspiracies" about fluoride being bad for us. I am so excited to talk to you all! I have spoken to Jack Kruse via twitter (very cool) cant wait to learn more, I have so many questions & things to share. I will make a success story post very soon.
