# Thread: Happy New Member!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happy-new-member.18245/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michelle H — 2016-08-01T18:01:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happy-new-member.18245/post-199192>

I'm Michelle from South Carolina, 58. I am so happy that I found this site and have been reading for weeks. Thank you Jack!

I have been searching for answers for a very long time. I eat healthier and less than everyone I know, yet I could not lose the visceral abdominal fat. It's awful, I look like a Cushing's patient. I knew my doc would prescribe pills, which I don't want. My husband and I are trying to stay prescription-free! I could only drop a pound or two if I exercised 2 hours a day, 1 hour a day just kept me from gaining. I knew something in my system was messed up and thank you Jack for providing the answers!

I started the Leptin RX last week, 2 meals a day hours earlier than normal, BAB, no exercise except slow relaxing swimming or yoga late in the day, coconut manna (wow, yum!). I am down from 180 to 174 already, freaking awesome!

I still have tons and tons to read. So far I'm positive I'm leptin resistant, I'm a sugar burner like Oprah, my circadian rhythm is out of whack from decades of 2nd shift and late eating, my cortisol is probably through the roof from stress from being a small business owner and maybe from long term prednisone use. I've lost and gained the same 40 pounds 4 times. I need this time to be the last. I'm very hopeful.

I guess the next step is to post a journal/history for more feedback? Thanks to all.

### caroline — 2016-08-02T03:27:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happy-new-member.18245/post-199202>

Do you have Jack's book? Are you listening to his podcasts?

Get yourself a Kiniki tan thru bikini (naked is better)and get outside.....

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happy-new-member.18245/post-199206>

Hi Jack & Caroline - I am working my way through the book, I do get lots of sunshine.

I am truly thankful for all of this information. I've always searched for the best answers to try to avoid doctors, hospitals, meds or procedures. Doing it online is so much easier than before the internet when I had to go to the library. I need to get blue blockers - I have much more to read and learn.

### JanSz — 2016-08-02T18:01:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happy-new-member.18245/post-199222>

Michelle from South Carolina, 58
assume menapausal
\---
We are creatures of light (and darkness).
Circadian Rhythm is a must.
When you get of bed look at sun or sky for few minutes.
That light tells your pineal gland that it is a day. Pineal gland under that light will stop producing melatonin.
Lower melatonin will allow cortisol to raise up.
At 7:30AM you should have
cortisol,serum=~20ug/dL
When you follow the light/dark instructions for 2 months and you do not have good cortisol7;30AM
have a blood test
pregnenolone
progesterone
cortisol,serum(7;30AM)(noon)(2:30PM)
DHEAs
E1, E2, E3
freeTestosterone
SHBG

post it
\-----
During day be max time outside.
When indoor use only incandescent lights. (no fluorescent, no LED)
\--
2-3-4 hrs before bed time use red lights only
no cell phone, no tv, no computer
because
they emit blue light that will tell your pineal gland that it is day (wrong signal)
\--
sleep in totally darkened bedroom, more orgasms = better oxytocin=good
\------------

Using clothes is a first step in bad direction.
Minimize use of clothes, no sunscreens, no makeup, expose max skin area to max amount of sun. Take a month of gradual exposure to sun, to allow skin to get used to it. Do not use eyeglasses, contacts, look close to sun, but not directly.
You have enough sun exposure when you have at least:
Vitamin D (25-Hydroxyvitamin D)(250nmol/L=100.16ng/mL)
More is better but no more than 2x of above.
Do not use vitamin A & D supplementation, they are different chemicals than the one you want, must make your own using sunlight (UVa & UVb)
Shoes disconnect us from mother earth. Use solid leather shoes, bare feet better.
To make vit D&A you need to eat sulfur and carotenoid containing foods.
sulfur- garlic, onions, leeks, MSM
carotenoids- carrots, tomatoes, peppers, other nightshades (but no white potatoes).

Carbohydrates,
do not eat those that raise glucose, high glycemic index (high GI), wheat, white potatoes, rice, other
eat carbohydrates (low GI) only in season, preferably those that are grown locally to you
So usually carbohydrates are for your local spring/summer only.

Overall eat lots of fat, beef lard, pork lard, duck fat, coconut oil, red palm oil
bacon with most of the fat is a king

Do not eat statins (to lower high cholesterol).

Check
insulin,fasting
take action if it is higher than 5

Do not eat fish or krill oils.
Dr Jack Kruse goes hog wild on a seafood, specially oysters. That is mostly for the content of DHA.
I do not eat seafood currently, because I did my Fatty Acids Analysis.
This one, at LabCorp:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/)
58 ------- Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

### Michelle H — 2016-08-03T16:43:45+0300

Thanks Jan - see my comments below in red...

Michelle from South Carolina, 58
assume menapausal - yes, finally hit menopause at late age of 57
\---
We are creatures of light (and darkness).
Circadian Rhythm is a must.
When you get of bed look at sun or sky for few minutes.
That light tells your pineal gland that it is a day. Pineal gland under that light will stop producing melatonin.
Lower melatonin will allow cortisol to raise up.
At 7:30AM you should have
cortisol,serum=~20ug/dL
When you follow the light/dark instructions for 2 months and you do not have good cortisol7;30AM
have a blood test
pregnenolone
progesterone
cortisol,serum(7;30AM)(noon)(2:30PM)
DHEAs
E1, E2, E3
freeTestosterone
SHBG

post it - will post when I get tested. I know my circadium rhythm is off, have always been a night owl, second shift worker, late eater, working on changing all of that now
\-----
During day be max time outside.
When indoor use only incandescent lights. (no fluorescent, no LED)
\--
2-3-4 hrs before bed time use red lights only
no cell phone, no tv, no computer
because
they emit blue light that will tell your pineal gland that it is day (wrong signal)
\--
sleep in totally darkened bedroom, more orgasms = better oxytocin=good
\------------

Using clothes is a first step in bad direction.
Minimize use of clothes, no sunscreens, no makeup, expose max skin area to max amount of sun. Take a month of gradual exposure to sun, to allow skin to get used to it. Do not use eyeglasses, contacts, look close to sun, but not directly. I don't use sunscreens and always have gotten lots of sun, so I'm good on this one.
You have enough sun exposure when you have at least:
Vitamin D (25-Hydroxyvitamin D)(250nmol/L=100.16ng/mL)
More is better but no more than 2x of above.
Do not use vitamin A & D supplementation, they are different chemicals than the one you want, must make your own using sunlight (UVa & UVb)
Shoes disconnect us from mother earth. Use solid leather shoes, bare feet better. I am barefoot unless working or hiking.
To make vit D&A you need to eat sulfur and carotenoid containing foods.
sulfur- garlic, onions, leeks, MSM
carotenoids- carrots, tomatoes, peppers, other nightshades (but no white potatoes). These foods are my staples - garlic, onions, tomatoes, peppers, carrots, but I thought I read no nightshades during Leptin RX?

Carbohydrates,
do not eat those that raise glucose, high glycemic index (high GI), wheat, white potatoes, rice, other I have not eaten these for many years.
eat carbohydrates (low GI) only in season, preferably those that are grown locally to you
So usually carbohydrates are for your local spring/summer only.

Overall eat lots of fat, beef lard, pork lard, duck fat, coconut oil, red palm oil
bacon with most of the fat is a king I am adding more fats now.

Do not eat statins (to lower high cholesterol).

Check
insulin,fasting
take action if it is higher than 5

Do not eat fish or krill oils. I have never been a big fish eater, I do take fish oil.
Dr Jack Kruse goes hog wild on a seafood, specially oysters. That is mostly for the content of DHA.
I do not eat seafood currently, because I did my Fatty Acids Analysis.

# Thread: Greeting from Perth, Australia~
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greeting-from-perth-australia.18303/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Videha — 2016-08-21T05:45:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greeting-from-perth-australia.18303/post-199944>

Hello everyone! I am so grateful to be granted approval to join this amazing community. The first time i found the forum & blog-i had a deep & profound feeling of finally being home (hard to explain but that is what i felt)
I look forward to now being able to interact....have been frequently visiting the forum & Jack's blog since i discovered it last week.

Q-Is it best to start a journal first, including blood test results...OR... is it best to post blood work in optimal tests? Thanks for any input.

### Videha — 2016-08-21T06:11:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greeting-from-perth-australia.18303/post-199945>

\* Waves a big Hello to Caroline~ great to see another Ozzie canary here.
I am looking forward to starting my Optimal Journal-will wait til i get advise re my above query tho.

### caroline — 2016-08-21T11:31:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greeting-from-perth-australia.18303/post-199946>

Hi Videha ....and welcome! There are lots of Aussies here!

Please do start a journal in the journal section. Dr. Kruse needs context to be able to direct you.

Tell him about your Mom's health and Grandma"s health and siblings and children, and, of course your health.

Where do you live - city? where do you work?

How is your sleep? your libido? Do you get outside lots?

What do you eat? drink?

Have you read Dr. K's book? it is the first couple of years of his blogs.

Do you listen to all the podcasts? they are a great and easier way to get info.

There is a great thread on the importance of newbies - have you seen it?

If you can manage it - become a gold member and you will have access to the monthly webinars and the live Q&A when you can ask Dr. K. anything.

### Videha — 2016-08-21T14:20:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greeting-from-perth-australia.18303/post-199948>

Thank you Caroline, for your warm welcome! I am so pleased to hear there are a lot of aussies here, am hoping there may be at least 1 or more from west oz.-that would be wonderful!

Re: the journal...have been typing for hours (raining outside) thought i would get it started since weather was bad. I didn't remember much, but the more i typed the more the memory spat out... \*i sure hope there isn't a rule about how long or short it should be. lol I figured the more info the better. Now since reading your list..(thanks) -it will get even longer...lol

Yes, i did read the post on importance of newbies..will re-read when time permits. Being usually a very private person, i never divulge anything about myself on the net, nor been willing to post my pic, ex-stalking victim reasons.... but time has come to rattle the old cage & just go for it.

Like i said in my first email to the contact us address> "I have been avidly & very excitedly reading Dr.Jack's information on the forum & his site. Thank you so much for sharing this very valuable research!! I very much want /need to join the community as i can clearly see he KNOWS his stuff".
Never got any replies to my 3 emails, but,was determined to join so finally found another addy & here i am. Yay!
Haven't seen any membership stuff yet...

p.s. Thanks for spacing out yr txt, that is the one thing i am struggling with when i read J.K's blogs. Very long paragraphs...hard on eyes, especially wearing magnifying glasses and blue blockers. I do my best but sometimes can't finish them....always need to re-read. Nope don't have his book yet. Yes been listening to his podcasts-i comprehend the talks better than reading, he explains things so well.

### Lahelada — 2016-08-21T15:04:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greeting-from-perth-australia.18303/post-199950>

Welcome. Kudos to you for making such healing steps from the get go.
Anything you need just ask.
And no, no rules about the length of your diary.

### Videha — 2016-08-21T15:20:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greeting-from-perth-australia.18303/post-199954>

@ Lahelada, thanks for the welcome. I like your name-very unusual. Thanks also for the link-have read it before will re-read again. Right now tho, i am on a very important misson, typing up the extra points Caroline mentioned. good to hear no limit, gonna be more like a book i think lol

### Videha — 2016-08-21T18:16:29+0300

### Videha — 2016-08-22T05:36:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greeting-from-perth-australia.18303/post-199993>

Thank you Caroline! Just logged in this second to add more to journal.. yes there is more! lol
I really appreciate your encouragement & kind words.

Despite all the physical body issues, my spirit is strong & i work very hard to maintain & share a positive uplifting vibration/energy with all people i encounter in the world. I enjoy sharing what knowledge & life experience i have. All the shopkeepers i interact with have been very open to my input & have made massive changes. I am glad that you can feel it. # Thread: Hello from Springfield!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-springfield.18342/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matthias — 2016-08-29T21:43:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-springfield.18342/post-200479>

Let me tell all you wonderful people a story about a young man who saw the movie _Limitless_ in 2012 and thought that it was based on a real-world drug. The stand-in is a compound called modafinil. In my search for a place I could get it, I came upon Dave Asprey's Bulletproof blog. What impressed me was his presentation of himself as a savvy modern man, and his claimed insistence on peer-reviewed studies regarding his doings.

I discovered that modafinil was a prescription drug, so I had to get it from India. And I gotta say, it actually worked! Having seen the results, I decided that I'd follow Asprey's other instructions for his diet and whatnot. And it worked for a time--I became a better version of myself ("upgraded" in his parlance), but still kept some of my niggling problems. Anyway, at one point I came across his collaboration with Jack Kruse about cold thermogenesis. In the elapsation I'd become hungrier for the _next_ improvement I could give myself, and so I made the leap to reading Jack's blogs about CT that Asprey had linked.

Oh boy was I in over my head.

Dave had linked to Cold Thermogenesis 9, so that's where I tried to start. It was like walking into the middle of a NOVA special that was dialed up to 11. I tried to go back and start reading from Cold Thermogenesis 1 and work from there, but even that was a little... initially frustrating. Fortunately, I'm an engineer and was schooled enough for a cursory read, but--as always for anyone--it was my constant curiosity that got me over the hump.

What was immediately clear was that Jack's level of actual, lower-level understanding (which led to my own follow-up googling & investigating) was so far ahead of the information from where I started. For example, if I'd asked _Why is fluoride bad_? I previously would have been told: _it just is_; where Jack would say **_because it reduces water's dielectric constant to ~20 and that effectively causes dehydration and improper chemistry for the proteins in the cell_** and elaborate on it [with a blog post](https://www.jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-4-ubiquitins-connection-to-light/) after mentioning its effects in other places.

The real tasting of the pudding was just plain **doing** what Jack instructed and seeing what happened--sometimes without even spending that much time familiarizing myself. And dammit, everything he said to do worked--_especially_ the stuff that other people had previously told me was nonsense (like the orange glasses I wear after dusk and at work while under the fluorescent lights and in front of my monitors).

So since about early/mid-2014, I was fully on the hook. No going back. No in-car wifi. No bluetooth. None of this nonsense in my house that would make it worse.

The burden on my heart now is to help my parents. They're both 68, my father is where I got my psoriasis and it's only gotten worse as has his weight, and my mother is in remission from breast cancer after having it surgically removed in 2011. I try my damnedest to convince them with any kind of language and with as much love as I can show them. I want them to be around long enough to see their grandchildren. They might come around, but... right now it's like [Luke 4:24](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Luke+4%3A24&version=KJV).

I eat as much crab as I can get, occasionally splurge on a lobster, always drink Pellegrino when it's on the shelf, and go into Cambridge to get $1 oysters at Red House when I'm seeing friends. (And of friends I haven't yet met--Reuben, my offer for dinner and Malbec is still on the table!). The only thing left is to change more of the environment that had been holding me back. I'm looking forward to keeping a log with you guys about the stuff I try out.

Up and atom, everybody!

P.S. Anybody else from the Northeast is welcome to take me up on my offer as well! I'll meet, and I'll buy. Except New York City. Until I have my Quantlet, I'm not going back. (And even then...)

P. P.S. _Limitless_ is a wonderfully-structured movie, but a fantasy. Get your circadian rhythms and your mitochondria right, and modafinil doesn't even do anything.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: An introduction from the Chicago Burbs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-introduction-from-the-chicago-burbs.18346/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Madison28 — 2016-08-30T08:21:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-introduction-from-the-chicago-burbs.18346/post-200496>

Hello everyone... my name is Sonya and I am going to be 54 years old next month.

I am divorced and live with my 20 year old son and most recently my mother moved in with us. We all get along great and for the most part life has been pretty good.

My health has been a little haphazard over the years however. I had my first daughter at age 24, via a healthy vaginal delivery and two years after that had an emergency appendectomy, a knee surgery, and a horribly painful ectopic pregnancy that almost killed me. I didn't realize until just recently how bad antibiotics can hurt a person's gut.

It was shortly after this that it seemed like I was having to go thru a surgery for one issue or another. I had my gallbladder removed, and a miscarriage that required a D&C which I found later to have a sponge that was left inside of me. After that I had major issues with my bowels and was chronically constipated. This led to a very large polyp inside my bowel, as well as multiple hemorrhoids. Surgery for that removed four fingers worth of tissue and left me in incredible pain. I have had fatty tumors removed behind my knee, a breast lump removed, and I was finally once again pregnant 10 years later after my daughter was born. I was so thankful that this pregnancy resulted in a healthy 10-pound baby boy. My one tube that was left after the ectopic pregnancy was tied off during the emergency C-section so I could get pregnant no more. After years of terrible menses bleeding and cramping I had a partial hysterectomy... leaving in my ovaries.

Finally after life had settled down approximately 2005 I was diagnosed with MS and Arnold Chiari malformation. I still had horrible gut issues but did not know the connection between antibiotics and gut health. After an in Hospital stay for high-dose IV steroid treatment then multiple rounds of steroids here and there the MS was brought under control.

In 2008 after 29 years of marriage my then-husband decided he'd had enough and left. He thought he was too good to support me and the children and has kept me in court 6 out of the last 9 years. The last two years were the most brutal and finally ended in February of 2016. During those last two years my mother moved in with us and was diagnosed with bladder cancer. I was her primary caretaker throughout the three surgeries she had to remove the cancer. It was shortly after that that she had a really terrible case of shingles. To say my stress load was over-the-top is putting it mildly.

When the court case I wrote about above ended on February 1st 2016 my body physically crashed on February 3rd and I have not been the same since. It seems I have chronic fatigue and sleep constantly however I always wake up at 3 a.m. every morning and then stay up for up to 6 hours only to crash again for the rest of the day. My skin is a mess full of acne that I've never had before as well as horribly dry and flaky skin. My hair is falling out. I can't stand for more than 5 or 10 minutes due to extreme pain around my left side kidney. And now I am getting strange boils that are pus filled and very painful, usually in the breast area. I probably have had 20 of them in the past 3 months. I've also gained probably 40 pounds since February.

I am a very happy-go-lucky, joyful person who loves to laugh. I have 3 beautiful grandchildren from my daughter whom I adore. And I also enjoy the memories my mother and I are making together as we are constantly laughing at how silly life can be. What I can't stand is my current state of health and the feeling of hopelessness of never getting better.

I've turned to several different practitioners who want thousands of dollars to help me heal without a guarantee of helping me to feel better. Of course I don't have that kind of money as I live on a limited budget and Medicaid. There has to be another way to help my body heal and I'm ready to learn and do whatever it takes.

I've read a little bit here and there on this website even as I am confused as much of it is over my head LOL. However I want to learn as much as possible and like I said I'm willing to do the work. I'm excited for a whole new future ahead of me.

I'm really sorry this was so long and I'm really happy to meet you all.

Sonya

### caroline — 2016-08-30T11:33:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-introduction-from-the-chicago-burbs.18346/post-200499>

welcome Sonya ......

Have you listened to Dr. K's podcasts? - they are a quick and easier way to get info.

Do you have Jack's book? It is the first couple of years of the blog and much easier to get thru.

Also please get the book by John Ott .....Health and light. It is a small book but a very important book.

You need lots and lots of sunshine and grounding and seafood. and good water ....

Where do you live? your postcode really, really matters

what are you eating? drinking?

Please start a journal in the journal section .....

Do you have all electronics out of your bedroom?

What is your nnEMF situation in your house????? smart meter? cell towers? electricity lines overhead?

Do you have blue blocking glasses?? extremely important

### Madison28 — 2016-08-30T11:55:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-introduction-from-the-chicago-burbs.18346/post-200502>

Hi Caroline...nice to meet you.

In answer to your questions...
No I have not listened to any of his podcasts, and no I don't have his book. I'm not sure which one you are referring to because it looks like he might have written more than one?

I am having a hard time getting lots of sunshine... my vitamin D level is 11.6. I recently got a Sunshine lamp to help me with that, would that help or no?

I live outside of Chicago in the 60538 postal code area.

I am eating a lot of fruit (watermelon, grapes, apples) and then toast and soups, 2 cups of coffee per day, and drinking higher ph water, about 64 ounces of that with lemon in it. I do not eat any meat nor do I eat fish because Seafood has always been kind of yuk to me. I recently learned that I am Mercury poisoned and forgot to put that in my introduction.

No I do not have All Electronics out of my room and need to do that. I wasn't aware of how important that is until visiting this site. The cable modem is in my room and needs to be removed ASAP. I also keep my cell phone in here in case my daughter needs to contact me and I use my computer in here as I've been trying to do research on helping myself to get well. We are not buy any Towers but no I do not have a smart meter. I have to look into doing that but I'm not sure where to start.

Yes I do have the blue light blocking glasses and wear them when the Sun goes down.

I'll try and start the journal sometime today after I figure out where it is.

Thank you so much for your message.

### fitness@home — 2016-08-30T12:22:22+0300

Welcome Sonya. One step at a time...we were all newbies once.

Caroline had good advice. Keep reading, watching podcasts and ask questions.

### caroline — 2016-08-30T12:27:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-introduction-from-the-chicago-burbs.18346/post-200505>

Hi Sonya ......You do NOT want a smart meter.

Please, please, please get all electronics out of your bedroom ASAP. Can you turn the breaker off at nite?

What is on the other side of your bedroom walls? in particular - where your head is?
Is this a house?

why can't you get outside????? nothing will be more important in your healing. your vitD is desperately low.

You need Real sunshine and grounding ....and you need it now!

Please get your butt outside with little to no clothes on .....as soon as you wake - you want the early morning sunshine.

Dr. K. just has one book presently .... The Epi Paleeo RX - please get it. It is a quick route to important information to get you going.

If you click on forum on the left hand top - you will see all the topics come up.

"My optimal Journal" is fifth down I think.

You need to start listening to all Jack's podcasts ASAP

Have you been to the home page and read the getting started stuff?

Your circadian rhythm is a mess ....you need to reset the rolex in your brain!

Jack's story is a light story ..... this is all about the three legged stool ....... light, water, magnetism.

This isn't going to be easy - but you are a survivor.

BTW - where you live sucks really bad .... can you get out of there anytime soon???

Dr. K. will tell you ....you will never get well in the place you got sick.

You have lots to do ..... It isn't going to cost thousands$$$$$ ....but you do need to be very diligent about doing the stuff that counts. Your life depends on it.

Lots of folks here have done it .....or are doing it - so can you.

So - get on it! I want to hear some progress tomorrow - easy stuff ...everything out of your bedroom. Your bedroom is only for sex and sleeping.

### Valuation Man — 2016-08-30T12:52:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-introduction-from-the-chicago-burbs.18346/post-200506>

Hi Sonya, I completely agree with what Caroline just wrote and that is a great start.

I just want to add a couple of more things since like you I have been going through a divorce this past year and all the paperwork is done and it will be final in 16 days and 5 hours . The emotional drain of this really does impact your health and energy. Today, I feel better than I have in over a decade and I have Dr. Kruse to thank. Try to put into practice to what Caroline just wrote and get out in the sun and barefoot as much as possible. Consider a Magnetico and drink a ton of spring feed water. By simply doing these steps my thinking and energy has really improved and I can feel my dopamine levels skyrocketing. It is a much better way to live.

The one additional item I would like to recommend is to purchase Phil Escott's book. He is a member here and writes about his journey in his book. In addition to the bio hacks he did, he also has a great section on the emotional and spiritual aspect on our journeys and how that impacts our health. I understand what you have been through with a recent divorce and this book may be helpful.

[https://www.amazon.com/ARTHRITIS-Ps...s-Autoimmunity-ebook/dp/B01HRLWSRC#nav-subnav](https://www.amazon.com/ARTHRITIS-Psoriatic-Rheumatoid-Arthritis-Autoimmunity-ebook/dp/B01HRLWSRC#nav-subnav)

Best wishes

Tim

### Madison28 — 2016-08-30T15:29:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-introduction-from-the-chicago-burbs.18346/post-200511>

Thanks for the encouraging comments, everyone!! And I love the detailed explanations, Caroline! My brain struggles a bit with stuff lately, so I love things broken down into bite size pieces.

I am a survivor....if you only knew....

Yes I can turn the breaker off until I get the stuff out of my room. I will do that starting today. And no to smart meters, got it.

Where I live does suck however I cannot move at this time. I need my mother's financial support and she is thoroughly against moving. I wish it were not like this because I have told her we BOTH really need to move, and she is against it. Ugh. The money I have coming in does not afford me the opportunity to live on my own. I can try to work on that however at the moment I'm just too sick to get any kind of employment.

I will go back to the home page and read the getting started stuff as well as start listening to all the podcasts. That will be a good start for me today. As far as the Circadian rhythm stuff I wish I knew how to fix that. Any suggestions? I know I need to change the sleep patterns... That being said I am physically exhausted to the point that it's hard to move at all. I was so desperate that I tried different medications offered to me by my dr. And I can not sleep through the night no matter what I try to do...grrr.

To those that mentioned divorces, how did you heal? I want to get to the point one day where this does not affect me at all. I thought my in-laws loved me as if I was their own daughter but I had to come to find out that I was nothing to them. They funded my ex-husband in his divorce proceedings and I can only imagine how much money they all paid after going through three attorneys. On my end I had to invest $55,000 just to survive it all and to keep my son...my ex and family (them) were as low as low can get, they even had me followed, and not once followed a court order until absolutely having to because of the threat of jail. Really darn pathetic... his entire family would walk into the courtroom like piranhas ready to feast on me. I always went alone with my attorney.... and I was as brave as brave could be considering the situation and circumstances.  One day I will heal completely!! I fully believe in karma and they may think that what they did was small beans but in life in general Karma will come back on them one day. The ex hasn't spoken to our children in almost 2 years...! Crazy!

I want to thank you guys for the book recommendations... I am a fan of reading so I will get on that as soon as possible.

Again, thank you for your help and encouragement.

### fitness@home — 2016-08-30T16:02:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-introduction-from-the-chicago-burbs.18346/post-200516>

> To those that mentioned divorces, how did you heal?

I had to forgive first and foremost. Divorce is a two-way street. Both parties play a part in it. During the worst of it, I went to counseling. Helped to have a neutral 3rd party to discuss things with. Although his family will always side with him, they were (and still are) aware of his issues. Nice family and I have no problem with them.

Believe it or not, I got a phone call a couple of years ago from his _next_ wife out of the blue when I was at work. She found me via LinkedIn and called our main reception desk. She introduced herself and said, "Please don't hang up!" Turns out it was far worse for her. She is now divorced also and we are friends. Recently she said, " We have to be there for the next unlucky woman..."

### Valuation Man — 2016-08-30T16:31:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-introduction-from-the-chicago-burbs.18346/post-200518>

I agree with what fitness@home said and you have to forgive him and yourself and get a counselor. The other important thing I did was to be part of a support group. I went to DivorceCare through a church (national organization) and there are other support groups that deals with divorce (look at meetup.com). It was helpful to be in a group where others are going through the same thing and your feelings and struggles are normalized. Also, it was helpful to see others that were further along in the process that provided encouragement that it does get better. You will get better, follow the advice here and find a group and friends that been through the process that can support you

### Madison28 — 2016-08-30T17:58:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-introduction-from-the-chicago-burbs.18346/post-200519>

That's a great idea Valuation Man! When fitness@home mentioned forgiveness, I thought I had done that. It's a battle for me, I keep continually going over for forgiving him over and over again as well as his family. And I've gone to counseling and I thought I had it all done and over with but apparently I don't. You both really nailed it and have some awesome ideas.... thank you so much.

I've seen some of those Divorce Care signs around in our town. I'll that have to get involved in that one too.

Sincerely grateful!

### caroline — 2016-08-31T03:27:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-introduction-from-the-chicago-burbs.18346/post-200536>

I have a different perspective altho I have never been divorced.

I did have to end the life of my beloved DH of many years .....the grief and guilt is paralysing but .....

After three counsellors I realised I/ we were just talking about the same shirt over and over and over. It was helping nothing really ..... It wasn't going to change anything.

Dr. K. Helped me with that ...... Endlessly talking about the same crappy situation gets you no where.

You go over the situation, examine it, turn it over and over and over ......and then put it aside -,and move forward.

You need to get yourself in forward motion .....that is why you are here. The past is the past and it hurts like hell and you can't figure it out .....so be it.

Get on with it girl!

You need to reset the clock in your head .....get outside and lay on the grass - naked if possible. Soak up as much sunshine as possible, drink good water, eat seafood.

Tell us what you have done for yourself today......

Your mother has cancer? And she isn't interested in moving?

You need to show her a thing or two or three......you got lots to do!

### Madison28 — 2016-08-31T04:16:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-introduction-from-the-chicago-burbs.18346/post-200539>

YES! I have lots to do!

Today, I went outside and ran my toes through the grass and soaked up the sun for as long as I could, and it felt ahh-mazing!! I had my three grandbabies today and I'm whooped, but its a good kind of tired.

I can relate to everything you wrote Caroline...re-hashing things over and over gets you no where, and I have to say that's where we were, my ex and me. HOW DO I GET OUT OF THE PAST?

Ohhh... I don't mean to shout but I need to get out of this hell-hole and I need to do it soon. You are right when you say I have lots to do..... I just don't know where to begin.

Thank you for the nudge...I need more of 'em.

### caroline — 2016-08-31T04:44:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-introduction-from-the-chicago-burbs.18346/post-200544>

The past is part of us .....we are like a tapestry - and it makes us who we are now
there must have been lots of good times too ..be thankful for all of the good stuff.

You need to focus on regaining your health - and most of all - your joy? You have children and grand babies to be an inspiration/example for. ....we all need to stop feeling sorry for ourselves - so not productive in any way shape or form.....been there - done that.

Learn from the past and move forward now

It will be hard for awhile because your dopamine levels are low ......so concentrate on all the positive things you need to do for yourself every single day.

One day, if you work hard at all of this, I promise you - the lights will come on!

That doesn't mean that everything, suddenly, will be perfect - it certainly won't be ....but you will see everything with new eyes and a wonderful new perspective, and an open and forgiving heart.

Have you thought of taking up drumming?

Do you listen to music? There are a couple of wonderful music threads here. They are incredible healers.

Just go outside and be .....make yourself sing and dance and laugh. And I promise you - Mother Nature will weave her magic!

One day you will wake up and really, really see.

### caroline — 2016-08-31T04:48:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-introduction-from-the-chicago-burbs.18346/post-200545>

Btw .....start a simple journal today ....

Just put in it at least five things that you have done for yourself that day.

That's it ......you don't have to write the great American novel - not just yet anyway!

### Madison28 — 2016-08-31T06:10:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-introduction-from-the-chicago-burbs.18346/post-200552>

Your comments gave me chills. I've always wanted to be an inspiration to my children and my grandbabies. And even to my mother who has seen a lot of sadness in her life.

No more feeling sorry for myself. You are right there were many happy times and it all wasn't gloom and doom to be sure. I received the greatest blessing of all from that marriage and I'm so proud of what my daughter and son are becoming. They are really great kids I have lots to be thankful for.

I believe it's time for me to heal... Time for me to get to know Mother Nature really well... and time to introduce myself to Seafood. I can honestly say it's time for me to spend time on me to make me the best person that I can become.

Thank you for your words of amazing wisdom.

### caroline — 2016-08-31T08:34:01+0300

Baby steps ......

When you are outside with your grand babies - let them crawl around naked in the grass and sunshine - what an amazing gift you will be giving them.

Your Mother and daughter and son and grandchildren will be watching you ...... You are doing this for you - but you will also be giving your family the gift of life and showing them all the possibilities that Mother Nature is bestowing on all of us.

Hug a tree every day!

Hi @[Madison28](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/18920/)

You have indeed full plate.

You said:

I need my mother's financial support and she is thoroughly against moving.
vitamin D level is 11.6
2005 I was diagnosed with MS

It seems I have chronic fatigue and sleep constantly however I always wake up at 3 a.m. every morning and then stay up for up to 6 hours only to crash again for the rest of the day.

I am eating a lot of fruit (watermelon, grapes, apples) and then toast and soups, 2 cups of coffee per day, and drinking higher ph water, about 64 ounces of that with lemon in it. I do not eat any meat nor do I eat fish because Seafood has always been kind of yuk to me.

I've also gained probably 40 pounds since February.

The money I have coming in does not afford me the opportunity to live on my own. I can try to work on that however at the moment I'm just too sick to get any kind of employment.

\=======================================================================================
Taking into account
that you cannot move, live in Chicago
do not have financial resources
do not like meat or seafood
gained 40 pounds since Frebruary

I suggest the following:
Get at least one, better up to four or six lamps
Exo Terra Solar-Glo High Intensity Self-Ballasted Uv/Heat Mercury Vapor Lamp
[https://www.amazon.com/Exo-Terra-So...UTF8&qid=1474548267&sr=1-1-spell&keywords=exo](https://www.amazon.com/Exo-Terra-Solar-Glo-Intensity-Self-Ballasted/dp/B00101JII8/ref=sr_1_sc_1?s=pet-supplies&ie=UTF8&qid=1474548267&sr=1-1-spell&keywords=exo) terrra 160W

get one
RubyLux ALL RED High Intensity LED Bulb 640 to 660nm
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B01544PUJ2/ref=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o00\_s01?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B01544PUJ2/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o00_s01?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

If you happen to have electrical heating, it would be good idea to use as many as you want of:
RubyLux Infrared Bulb NIR-A Near Infrared Individual Bulb
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00N4JE9U6/ref=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o06\_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00N4JE9U6/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o06_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

\----------------------
Artificial lighting management for a human locked in small room.

Day length=24hrs
Light--14 hrs
dark--10hrs
sleep--8hrs
Use minimum to no clothes 24/7
\----------------------------------------------
Wakeup-6AM
Bed time-10PM
\----------------------------------------------
At 6AM for 2-3 minutes look directly at one Solar Glow 160W
\----------------------------------------------
Light 6AM-8PM using
Exo Terra Solar Glow 160W
\----------------------------------------------
Light 8PM-10PM
using RubyLux ALL RED
\-----------------------------------------------
Night
No light at all, any windows blocked completely from the light
Hand held red light similar to RubyLux ALL RED can be used on the way to the bathroom
\---------------------------------------------
Lay in bed from 10PM to 6AM sleeping or not, no tv, radio or music. Day dreaming/meditating ok.
Do not go to bed in other times unless sick.
\---------------------------------------------
\----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Get selenium, Lugols, vit D

every day eat
one selenium pill
five drops of vit D
ten drops of Lugols

First two months take vitamin D twice a day, latter just one a day
All three above take indefinitely.

Life Extension - Super Selenium Complex & Vitamin E - 200 Mcg - 100 Vcaps (200 VCAPS)
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00IAVRJ2S/ref=oh\_aui\_search\_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00IAVRJ2S/ref=oh_aui_search_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

Biotics Research Bio-D-Mulsion Forte Vitamin D -- 2000 IU - 1 fl oz
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B004HGF69I/ref=oh\_aui\_search\_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B004HGF69I/ref=oh_aui_search_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

J.CROW'S¨ Lugol's Iodine Solution(2 oz.) Four Pack(4 bot.) + Free Additional J. Crow's Dropper
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B01FWM4J84/ref=oh\_aui\_search\_detailpage?ie=UTF8&th=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B01FWM4J84/ref=oh_aui_search_detailpage?ie=UTF8&th=1)

\==================================================================
Do not eat anything made of wheat, rice or white potatoes. No toast, no bread, no pizza, no french fries, etc

Nothing sold in boxes.
Coffee ok, no sugar of any kind, no sweeteners.
Do not eat fruits, watermellon in small amounts ok, bluberry.
garlic, onions, carrots, peppers ok
Drink only spring water.
Eat eggs, butter, cheese, olive oil, as much as you want.
Eat sea salt (according to your taste for it), have to be dirty sea salt, white salt with "sea salt" label does not cut it. I use this
Real Salt Sea Salt - Pouch, 26-Ounce
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000BD0SDU/ref=oh\_aui\_search\_detailpage?ie=UTF8&th=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000BD0SDU/ref=oh_aui_search_detailpage?ie=UTF8&th=1)

Lots of black ground pepper, freshly ground, tablespoon/day is ok.
Lots of ground clovers.
Black peppers and clovers I buy by pounds, real cheap in local Indian supermarket.
Turmeric

Last meal ends 6PM.
\===========================
//
Reconsider animal fats and protein.

# Thread: Help me get started!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-get-started.18349/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 20

### Lola Roman — 2016-08-30T23:25:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-get-started.18349/post-200532>

Hello All!

I'd love some guidance on where to start considering my constellation of symptoms.

Look forward to getting to know the community!
**Age:** 55 **Sex:** F **Joined JackKruse.com:** 8/28/16

**Location:** Oakland, CA, USA

**Dx:** _Longstanding:_ PTSD (complex and shock), insomnia, anxiety, depression, headaches, extreme myopia (-12). Depression not an issue with 5-HTP.
_New (2016):_ autoimmune thyroiditis (have antibodies for Graves and Hashimotos, but recent TSH = 0.44 – 0.68 so closer to expression of Graves), osteopenia, fibroids (small, 9 mm)
Of most concern: sudden vitreous detachments with floaters, both eyes, just happened Aug. 10
Also: hair test shows elevated lead, stool test shows dysbiosis

**Surgeries:** 1975, facial surgery following car accident; 2003, cosmetic surgery

**Current Rx:** Tiny dose of clonazapam, 0.125 – 0.25 mg, for sleep/few nights week (down from 1 mg/night); lots of supplements for sleep; occasional propranolol for anxiety; caprylic acid

**Current Therapies / Protocols:** Gut restoration, detoxing (frequent saunas, coffee enemas + Pectasol), started eating meat/fish (have been nearly vegetarian most my life) and exposing myself to sunlight daily without glasses/contacts, sleeping on grounded sheet, changed home lighting to incandescent or halogen, use blue blockers on computer/phone, experimenting with red/near infrared light

**Health WINS:** Fewer headaches, reduced autoimmune activity, got off Gabapentin and drastically reduced Clonazapam, better energy

**Health Goals:** Take charge and reverse conditions! _Especially_ eye problems.

**My Optimal Journal:** Coming soon!

### Lola Roman — 2016-08-31T19:52:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-get-started.18349/post-200595>

Thank you so much!

I'm not sure a Paleo diet is right for me—but I'm all ears for your thoughts! My thinking:

I never liked meat, was a vegetarian from age 17–34. I read Eat 4 Your Blood Type and I'm A- (compatible with vegetarianism), so I thought my diet was suited for my genetics. But over 20 years ago I added occasional chicken and beef to avoid deficiencies.

Now: Following Weston Price diet. Added daily high-quality pastured animal protein + fat + bone broth to my diet (w/ lots of veggies and far fewer grains/fruit). I hesitate to let go of grains/fruit because I'm underweight (5' 10'' 130 lbs) and can't put weight on. I don't need to burn fat or increase my energy. I don't have food cravings of any kind.

Jack says that diet isn't everything, and my intuition says to focus on my environment, esp. in getting more sunlight, reducing toxic exposures (I've totally detoxed my home/products/diet), doing infrared saunas, and drinking great water.

That said, I'll do any diet that will help me reverse my various conditions.

I drink lots of filtered/remineralized water (use the 10-Stage Filter). Considering a filter upgrade (looking at Pure Effects) to remove fluoride, and also a a shower filter, but choosing the right products is a challenge! My goal is to get spring water locally using findaspring.com. Yay! Do you guys have thoughts on Pure Effects?

I'm extremely concerned about the vitreous detachments/floaters. This seems to be a problem with collagen/elastin/hyaluronic acid/hydration, no? I have very dry eyes and frequent urination. My sense is that water goes through and doesn't get absorbed into the cells. Thoughts on that?

I've listened to MANY of Jack's podcasts, will listen to all!

I don't think I get much blue light now that I've eliminated it from my cell phone/computer (f.lux and Nightshift continuously on). Do I still need blue-blocker glasses?

The sheet is grounded outside in the dirt (not in a power socket). Can it still draw dirty electricity?

In my bedroom I don't have anything plugged in and no electronics.

Yes, I have a smart meter! It's not near my bedroom, but still not good. Can anything be done to mitigate effects?

I have a fairly clear idea of the overall program Jack advocates, and I'm moving in that direction A-SAP! Would love to proceed as surgically as possible to the next step best suited to my biology. What do you think?

Deeply grateful for your time!!!

### Lola Roman — 2016-08-31T20:01:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-get-started.18349/post-200596>

P.S. My insomnia began immediately after surgery in 2003. I already had a PTSD diagnosis and the trauma of surgery put my nervous system over the edge.

I run on the hyper side and am mentally overactive (!) but my 24-hour cortisol is oddly normal. I think the sleep issue is related to H-P-A axis dysregulation, but I'm not in adrenal fatigue.

My experiments with decreasing blue light/increasing natural light haven't helped yet. I also light candles at 8PM and turn off the lights. Shifting to parasympathetic mode seems to be the problem.

Would love to hear your thoughts.

### caroline — 2016-09-01T04:39:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-get-started.18349/post-200617>

You can shield a smart meter pretty easily ..... Lots of stuff on line

Yes .....you still need blue blockers and cover your skin at nite if watching tv etc.

What is on the other sides of your bedroom walls? fridge? Laundry? Dishwasher? Electronics?

Overhead power lines? Electricity in walls? Floors?

Have you seen Michael Neurts Webinars ? He is an electrical engineer and works with people on nnEMF
I understand he does phone consults and you can rent meters from him.

Can you sleep in your backyard? Go camping and see how you feel?

You need to reset the Rolex in your brain....

On rising - get outside, bare foot, little or no clothes.

Have breakfast within 30 minutes of rising ....BAB

Leave 4 hours between meals and at least that or more before bedtime

The leptin reset.....

### Lola Roman — 2016-09-02T04:29:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-get-started.18349/post-200684>

Hi Caroline,
Yes, I love sweet potatoes! Try to eat several times/week.

I'm confused about the leptin reset b/c my thyroid markers suggest this isn't a problem for me. Can you comment on that?

I've lost even more weight over the past two weeks while increasing protein/fat and decreasing carbs. Now at 125 and I'm 5' 10". That's too thin! I think some people do okay or even better on carbs, no?

I'll investigate all of your suggestions and tips. Each day I'm reducing exposures, increasing nutrients (esp. DHA), and increasing outdoor time. The basics, but I also want to add a specific protocol of some kind.

My main point of confusion is whether the leptin reset diet is right for me.

I do think I need to increase my redox potential. Can you point me toward the right info to help me with that?

Yes, I am excited! And thank you SO much for responding to my questions. When finances are better I'll officially join and hopefully can get some more targeted info for my conditions.

Bless you!

### caroline — 2016-09-02T06:23:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-get-started.18349/post-200690>

The leptin reset is to reset your clock primarily. I don't know what to tell you about why you are losing weight. A lot of us here struggle with our weight!
You are eating seafood? Sardines? Salmon? Mackerel? Bacon?

Dr. K. Did a blog on resistant starch - have a look

I understand that you want to be doing something more pro active for you .....let me have a think and perhaps others have some ideas.

I have never had to worry about gaining weight .....I am really good at that!

What is your rT3?

### Jenny S — 2016-09-02T10:03:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-get-started.18349/post-200697>

Hi Lola, I struggle to keep weight on too. I just followed the high fat, low carb diet for years but have found just increasing carbs as well has made the biggest difference. I even eat a little bit of brown rice occasionally now & often have a tspn or 2 of honey with each meal - both seem to help. Good luck.

### Curves — 2016-09-02T19:15:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-get-started.18349/post-200735>

Hi @[Lola Roman](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19348/)! I just moved out of San Francisco a few years ago. Once I started to follow Dr. Kruse's protocols in 2012 I began to get healthier. I lived near Ocean and China Beach so I got in the water regularly. China Beach was easier to navigate because it's just around the corner in the Bay; the waves on Ocean Beach kicked my ass often, but I always got in-- often. I removed all EMF from my apartment, blue blocked and ate epi-paleo. And I felt waaaay better. What I didn't know was that the best was yet to come, but I had to move out of the City to "get there.

Caroline made some awesome suggestions. This is truly the place to get well. You've come to the right place.

### Lola Roman — 2016-09-02T20:24:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-get-started.18349/post-200745>

Hello!

My reverse T3 is 14.7 ng/dL (scale is 9.2 to 24.1). Totally normal range.

I'm eating seafood a few times/week, and small amounts of grass-fed meat every day. Just ate a BAB outside in the sun!

I'm now listening to Jack's last available podcast on iTunes. Got the message about DHA and seafood, blue light, EMF, outdoor time, etc. etc.! I also deeply appreciate his references to meditation, breathing (oxygen!), and gratitude because I believe optimal wellness is a mind/body/spirit game.

Speaking of gratitude, thank you Jenny and Curves for your suggestions and encouragement! It warms my heart!

As far as being proactive, I feel drawn to CT. I frequently do rounds of saunas/cold showers and afterwards is the best I ever feel. I'm just concerned about burning too many calories.

Many thanks!!!

### Lola Roman — 2016-09-02T20:27:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-get-started.18349/post-200746>

@[Curves](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1418/) where did you move to? I would do much better in a more natural environment, that's for sure. I lived 25 years in SF before moving to Oakland. I now have grassy areas and a backyard for sunning, and I'm just a block from Lake Merritt... so beautiful and much healthier, but one day I'll make another move to be closer to nature.

### Curves — 2016-09-02T20:54:51+0300

I now have grassy areas and a backyard for sunning, and I'm just a block from Lake Merritt... so beautiful and much healthier, but one day I'll make another move to be closer to nature.

@Lola I moved to NW Oregon halfway between Portland and the Coast. The Pluses: oxygen rich trees, good clean water, low nnemf. Negative: low natural sunlight all year round. It's beautiful up here and yet, I curse the cloudy skies regularly.

### Curves — 2016-09-02T21:17:18+0300

### Lahelada — 2016-09-03T16:07:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-get-started.18349/post-200774>

@[Curves](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1418/) Hiya, yes it is something I do. I walk around my garden in long armed T shirt and jeans playing in the puddles. Certifiable one looks I am sure but great fun !! I only have fluoridated water so this is a welcome break . I hope anyway.

The reason for full clothing is to get as close to "a being surrounded by water effect "as possible. Cotton a must obviously. I discovered it when a problem had me running around in circles around the loquat tree until 2 am and had the best sleep ever after that even with the problem unresolved. By rights there should be a moat around that tree already.

I extend the practice when the weather is good, but still cold and put on wet shirts. It has happened that I also wear wet jeans inspired by a lack of laundry preparation  Looked at the right way it takes stress out of life twice...

# Thread: Hi from Toronto!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-toronto.18530/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### FM9000 — 2016-10-05T06:05:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-toronto.18530/post-202302>

Hey Everybody!

I discovered Dr. Kruse, through my research on matrix repatterning, which is a manual therapy based on tensegrity. I wasn't sure if matrix repatterning was legit, so I did a bunch of research. I found it interesting as it appears that it is based on improving conductivity of fascia. My searches about conductivity, collagen, cytokines, and more eventually lead me to Dr. Kruse and the EMF-7-Prometheus epic post!

I've had health issues for around 10 years, since a facial trauma that messed up my jaw, which subsequently created more and more health issues, including:

\-IBS
\-Chronic Prostate and pelvic pain issues
\-Muscular-skeletal issues and pain
\-Facial pain + TMJ issues + messed up occlusion
\-Chest/rib inflammation (I have trouble breathing)
\-Seizures
\-Depressive tendencies
\-Brain fog/difficulties focusing
\-Get cold easily
\-and more!

I'm assuming I have central sensitization syndrome and/or fibromyalgia, as I feel my nervous system is hyper sensitive and stuck in sympathetic mode.

I've managed my issues with no help from doctors (all of them told me I have psychological problems), with a primarily vegetarian diet concentrating on organic whole foods and a macrobiotic approach. I do eat a lot of eggs, and occasionally fish. Before I changed my diet, I couldnt eat anything without getting sick, but after some time got to a somewhat functional level.

I love learning, and I'm pretty good at assimilating large amounts of information, but I have to say that I found the amount of information on here overwhelming in both the amount and difficulty in digesting. I also realized that even if I do understand everything on here, it wouldn't be much help unless I can make it actionable.

So, I'm looking forward to connecting and learning how to action from Dr. Kruse's paradigm.

### caroline — 2016-10-05T07:25:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-toronto.18530/post-202305>

welcome ...... can we have a pic please

# Thread: Hello from North of 60
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-of-60.18877/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Samy Colddigger — 2016-12-20T19:36:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-of-60.18877/post-205530>

Im from Northern Canada and love what Jack has taught me about life. As soon as I started reading his blogs everything clicked! I have always wondered about what makes health and what makes illness. This likely has to do with the fact that I had a very disabled sister who was born with hemi-encephalepathy (half her brain wasn't there). She couldnt talk or walk or speak but she could sing. She died when I 6. I recently found out that my mom got a microwave during the pregnacny and used it the entire time. Given that I was born 2 years earlier with strong redox and low heteroplasmy....as well as my older brother.....it is no question in my mind what caused it. Given all the big questions I have, I have always been a learner. I was going down the food guru path for the longest time until I heard about EMF and then discovered Jack....so glad I woke up!

I live in a small cabin with no Wifi and no cell service. I sleep on a grounding sheet at night and eat heaps of oysters and salmon. Have not been sick since I started following Jacks blog about 2 years ago (I immediatley uped my seafood intake)

Now I would love to get Jack to come up a do a talk here somehow. My fellow citizens (of northern Canada) are completely unaware of the dangers and we need someone with authority to tell us its bad (so far, all Medical doctors up here have denied there is any biological effect from nnEMF).

Now I just need help brainstorming on how I can get him up here. I know some doctors around and but dont they will help much, as they are very skeptical of anything I say coming from 'some guy' on the internet). I also have zilch for funding so I was thinking of starting a go-fund-me campaign and perhaps approaching some of the non profits to try and help with funding.

Any big ideas peeps? Or any ideas at all!

### Samy Colddigger — 2016-12-22T10:10:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-of-60.18877/post-205655>

Thanks Caroline! Happy Solstice. I am up to late on my computer as usual but I did celebrate the longest night with a bon fire and some snow rolling. So hopefully I tunneled enough for a decent quantum trade off.

Have had my Cornet RF meter for about a week now and it is so amazing and reassuring to know where the good places are (less than or equal to .0005 mw/m2.) It is however disheartening to see how so many people pollute the environment with their choices. I just found out that the cabin I live at that I thought was out of cell service range is not actually. My old friends with their new friends came over and their phones worked here and the meter spiked into the red danger zone! But I am not too concerned, I wont let it happen to often now. I am thankful for what I have learned from Jack's work and also from members like yourself caroline! You may not know that I have already learned from you over the past year. I send may thanks to all the other member's on here who have made so much effort to convey important and inspiring lessons over the forum!

### caroline — 2016-12-23T08:19:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-of-60.18877/post-205705>

love that ^^^^^^^^

keep going!

# Thread: Finally Diving In! Biohacking Portland, OR.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finally-diving-in-biohacking-portland-or.18892/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shane O — 2016-12-22T23:43:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finally-diving-in-biohacking-portland-or.18892/post-205684>

Howdy Gang!

My name is Shane, I'm 38 yo, living in a suburb of Portland with my terrific wife of 8 years and our 4 kids. Life is a zoo! But wouldn't trade it.

We have been orbiting the Paleo-sphere since 2011, and did find some health improvements there, but I always felt like there was more meat on the bone. I actually tried to read some of Dr. Kruse's blogs back then, but my brain pretty much overheated and I fell into the more shiny and superficial marketing of some of the Paleo gurus. I recently found quite an appetite for Jack's podcasts and have been listening to them on repeat, taking notes, and employing some of the hacks that I gleaned from the audio. The whole family has been wearing Uvex at night, eating much more seafood, and I've been experimenting with CT. Wifi is off at night, as are all phones and devices. Found out the other day that airplane mode doesn't really change the emf output of my phone. Shocking!!!

Thankfully myself, my wife, and the kids are all in reasonable health these days. I want to become an active part of this community to ensure my wife and I are performing optimally, and that the kids are set up to understand the context of our modern world and ramifications for their health and performance as they grow.
The other reason I've joined now is that my mother has just been diagnosed with a golf ball sized glioma. I'll likely post an optimal journal for her prior to my own, as she has a lot coming at her these days and hasn't really bought into the Jack Kruse optimal living lens. We are meeting with a neurosurgeon today (**wish it was Dr. Kruse!**) to learn more about her tumor and options for treatment.

I look forward to connecting with as many of you as possible to learn, share and grow together.

# Thread: Hello from San Francisco (37.7749° N)!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-san-francisco-37-7749%C2%B0-n.18907/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SFBaySwimmer — 2016-12-30T06:26:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-san-francisco-37-7749%C2%B0-n.18907/post-205904>

I discovered Jack Kruse's ideas while Googling about cold water thermogenesis last month. Ended up down so many rabbit holes, bought Epi Paleo RX, reading Nick Lane's recent book and bought Body Electric.

I'm an avid swimmer in the Pacific -- SF Bay Area waters (50 degrees currently). I'm adding in some nude sunbathing to get more UV's and also getting my blood tests done tomorrow, so I can hopefully post those results soon.

Been lurking and reading/listening to Dr. Kruse's podcasts/blogs for the past month. Just got my trimeter for my home and now I'm testing out magnetic & electric measurements everywhere. Surprising to find a wall near my entryway with a ton of magnetic field.

My sleep has been pretty good this month (except for nights with too much social drinking/blue light). It's been part of my habit to get out of bed and into the light down the street -- I stand at the Embarcadero waterfront in my sports bra and shorts and do sun salutations as people walk by in their winter wear.

I wear my blue blocking orange safety goggles around at night everywhere and also inside fluorescent blue light environs. I'm getting used to ppl looking at me funny. Although I've actually gotten a ton of compliments on my glasses -- ppl think I'm really trend forward or I'm from the future. Kinda reminds me of Ali G (Sasha Cohen's character) from the early 2000s. <-- blue blocking, not sunglasses. Gave my entire family the same glasses for Xmas.

Bad side effect, is that it's been hard to focus on work since I'm so worried about my exposure to EMFs, RFs, Blue Light during the day. I feel like half my day is taken up with cold water swimming, sauna, sun exposure. :-/

# Thread: Hello From 44.5192° N, 88.0198° W aka Green Bay, WI
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-44-5192%C2%B0-n-88-0198%C2%B0-w-aka-green-bay-wi.18927/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### the lovely brenda — 2017-01-06T06:06:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-44-5192%C2%B0-n-88-0198%C2%B0-w-aka-green-bay-wi.18927/post-206201>

Hello my name is Brenda! I am 37 and have been suffering external and internal hemorrhoids since I returned from Gerald Pollack's conference on the Physics, Chemistry, and Biology of Water in Sofia, Bulgaria in October. [http://www.waterconf.org/](http://www.waterconf.org/) I drank too much red wine and not enough water while on a 12 hour flight. I then drove 9.5 hours to get to a conference on Hydrogen in Ohio and have been in lots of pain since. I have been searching non stop for something that reduces the pain and swelling. I am probably a grade three. It used to feel like I was shitting a dozen razor blades. I am down to only shitting one to three. I take MSM three times per day to soften my stools. I have been opening my window and sunning my ass the past few days around noon time. I am wondering if neighbors can see me and are enjoying the view. I am on the second floor and not grounded.

I need to figure out what is the optimal water for me. Currently, I filter fluoridated and chlorinated city water with a Berkey counter top system. I am a college student studying Environmental Engineering with a focus on drinking water and waste water. I learn the basics of water in school. Most of what I know I have learned from volunteering at conferences like Tesla Tech ExtraOrdinary Technology Conference in New Mexico [http://teslatech.info/ttevents/prgframe.htm](http://teslatech.info/ttevents/prgframe.htm) or the US Psychotronics Association Conference in Chicago [http://www.psychotronics.org/conference.php](http://www.psychotronics.org/conference.php). I am going to be transfering to studying cellular molecular biology in fall.

I followed Dr. Terry Wahls and her mitochondria stuff since the beginning because so many of the moms of my friends growing up had MS. I practiced sun gazing while I lived in NYC back in 2008. I used it to get off all antidepressants I was on for 13 years and significantly reduced my asthma medication.

I am a fluoride activist and appreciate Jack's simple break down of fluoride. I was one of 5 mothers who petitioned EPA in November along with Food & Water Watch, Organic Consumers Association, American Academy of Environmental Medicine, International Academy of Oral Medicine and Toxicology, Moms Against Fluoridation, and Fluoride Action Network. [http://fluoridealert.org/articles/epa\_fluoride\_petition/](http://fluoridealert.org/articles/epa_fluoride_petition/)

I delivered my last child at home having an orgasmic birth. I potty trained him from birth and he was out of diapers by 3.5 months. I write lots of complaint letters about school food, cosmetic pesticides, and fluoride in drinking water. I volunteer at an indoor grow space and work under LED grow lights. The lights seem to make my skin feel strange. We raise perch and mixed greens salad mixes. I like to do experiments with different types of water and grow plants to compare how tap water differs from vortexed tap water programmed with special frequencies. This is the device I use [http://junglesecretwater.com/](http://junglesecretwater.com/).

One day I hope to have a flying Vision VTOL by Mike Waters for my transportation device and to be disconnected from the current power grid system. [http://cureforglobalcrises.weebly.com/vision-vtol.html](http://cureforglobalcrises.weebly.com/vision-vtol.html)

I also want to reverse all my allergies and to have an ass that is healed up.

I like to teach others and I enjoy meeting new people. This is my facebook. [https://www.facebook.com/thelovelybrenda](https://www.facebook.com/thelovelybrenda)

### caroline — 2017-01-06T07:30:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-44-5192%C2%B0-n-88-0198%C2%B0-w-aka-green-bay-wi.18927/post-206204>

Hi the Lovely Brenda.....

You got quite a problem there girl ..... hope you get answers fast!
