# Thread: Hello from North of 60*
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-of-60.18933/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Imogen Skye — 2017-01-09T01:57:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-of-60.18933/post-206356>

Hi all,
I just did my first cold water bath after 3 weeks of 3x/day face dunking and outdoor CT (in bikini-esque coverage + boots, hat, gloves, -20 t0 -32C). I did 14 minutes of trunk and 8 minutes of legs; my bathtub is near-miniature. I've never towelled off a numb-skin body before; it was surreal.... I couldn't tell if I was still wet or not. My face and belly started producing heat yesterday! I did some spot icing on my belly with frozen meat, just 3x so far!

Two years ago I was in an accident and ended up with a traumatic brain injury that changed my whole life; I had to relearn how to do everything; I still can't drive and I am nowhere near my previous capacity, but I improve every day, and every day is a triumph! I home educate my five children and they are my greatest joy in life, truly. I used to live in the bush and now live in town, but I am preparing to move back to the bush, further south, in the summer, where the light cycle is more moderate and I can relearn homesteading with my children. I am by no means a townie kind of woman; I need the wilderness!

My diet was WAPF for 3 yrs, followed by strict paleo for 4.5yrs and for the last 1.5 yrs I've included once/wk basmati rice and potatoes, which I have not needed since I started the CT protocol, so it has been dropped and I'm back to strict ancestral paleo once again. My freezer is full of organ meats (liver, kidneys, heart, tongue, etc...), wild atlantic and northern pacific fish, and some muscle meats all from grass-fed and pastured animals, and my eggs are from bug-eating ducks and chickens.

I have had success already with CT just from my outdoor exposure and face dunking: a sudden influx of previous lost longterm memory, increased focus and memory recall in both short and longterm, improved strength, drastic reduction in pain, obvious body comp. changes, skin improvement, increased energy and only occasional fatigue with 2x/wk sleep attacks as opposed to the all-day fatigue with sleep attacks 7-12/wk I had before CT. It is changing my life rapidly! I could not lift a 10 litre jug of water before- even pushing it along the floor was exhausting and sometimes impossible- and now I am lifting a 20 litre jug for a short ways! I can lift my youngest children! I haven't been able to do this since the accident. Before the accident I was fit, slender, and had resolved adrenal and thyroid problems several years before (with diet and my Naturopathic Dr's intervention). Pre-accident, I wasn't aware of any health problems at all, felt healthy and strong, just enjoyed being in my body, dancing and doing yoga every day, hiking, walking, playing, etc.... Then the accident changed me into what felt like a frail 90 yr old woman- fragile and "thin as a reed," mildly ill most of the time, and physically weak, very suddenly, besides the new myriad cognitive deficiencies.

I started the protocol because I googled the words (for the umpteenth time) "hypothalamus reset" and saw them in one of the CT posts on Jack's main pages, and resolved to start immediately. The rest of the benefits came as a surprise, which is why I started reading more and finding out that this is all to be expected (as in, no placebo effect because I had no idea about any of it; I just hoped to reset some invisible pathways to get more energy to and from my mitochondria). I knew from my own experience and self-discoveries that I am dealing with mitochondrial disorder and that the hypothalamus is the route to order, but that ultimately this is only a maintenance plan until I can get SUNLIGHT! We just came out of 22 hrs of darkness/day here, and it is rapidly getting lighter. I've been in the sunlight as much as possible in the few hours we're getting now. Being hit at 120kph seems to have knocked my hypothalamus and other parts loose and their functions are all impaired where they didn't show any symptoms before.

I have gone through periods of mild cold-adaptation from living in the Yukon, being in the cold all the time, but post-TBI, I have had super poor temperature regulation, among other things. This is rapidly improving also with CT, which is counter-intuitive, and was my blind spot! I was using an infrared heat lamp to stay warm and keeping my house at 24\*C! Now I sleep in 16\*C and my house is kept at 17\*C and I'm fine; I use IR only rarely now, just when I am too cold and can't warm up and it's too late to eat anything. Sometimes 16-17\*C is chilly if I haven't had enough fat (it is hard to eat enough, but I'm getting better at it), but always tolerable and sometimes even comfortable. I discovered that when I feel chilly, I can eat fat and protein and go outside for 15 minutes and then it is toasty warm in here, haha. That has been my remedy so far.

I'm just so thrilled that this is working! It was for me the missing piece of the puzzle. I am also somewhat amazed at what I figured out from my own experience with my body, which is to say that it can be done even without labs, even though I appreciate others doing so so that I can substantiate my experiential evidence! I could start the CT protocol right away with no caveats because I was at the point of entry already. My body has accepted this and is rapidly adapting.

I cannot wait to see more. I'd be even more thrilled if my body would build more commissures between brain hemispheres that could connect the right and left sides of my body properly; right now they act like choreographed dancers, but are not coordinated as from the same body- too many severed connections. To outsiders it looks like I'm normal, but to me, I have two bodies to coordinate.

Oh! I'm 39 yrs old.

### caroline — 2017-01-09T05:19:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-of-60.18933/post-206368>

Welcome Imogen - what a great story of survival and endurance.

Please start a journal in the optimal journal section and give us up dates often. So many can learn from your strength and tenacity and perseverance.

Give Dr. K. some health history and your Mom's and Gran's too.

5 kiddos - wow that's a handful! How were the births?

You are doing wonderful things for yourself and setting a great example for your children.

How is the nnEMF where you are? Do you all have blueblockers?

Have you read John Ott's book? Health and light ...

Have you read Dr. K's book?

listen to his podcasts?

### Imogen Skye — 2017-01-09T06:40:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-of-60.18933/post-206371>

Thank you Jack, and Caroline

I'll have to check out the journals.... I mean I am just really new here. I started the CTing without reading more than the protocol and article about it, skimmed everything I didn't understand, and went for it. I was in my prior life an avid researcher and still am, but my capacity is lowered so much that even when I read research papers, I skim for just what I came for and unless necessary, I don't read the rest. Usually I can tell if the conclusions are likely to be accurate or on-track based on the skimming. Two decades of paper-reading has given me this skill, I guess. So, I haven't learned very much here yet, and have to take that part much more slowly than my body is adapting to CT and giving me feedback about my diet and light and sleep and so on. I'm more body-based now; I was a brain-vehicle before; now I'm a whole thing! haha  I'd love to have my brain power back though.

My first two births were c-sections for breech and I was uninformed because I basically slept through the first pregnancy- could not stay awake for more than enough time to eat, drink, bathe, and get back to bed. I consented to what I later discovered was a gross misrepresentation of the facts by a obgyn who wanted to get a surgery in before vacation time. Criminal. Then I was surprise-pregnant 4 months postpartum, had a 2nd c-section because I "must," (once a c-section, always a c-section) after which I began my reading in earnest so that when baby 3 came, I was ready for a homebirth, but no midwife would touch me with a ten-ft pole (which is what I was told by one of them), so I had three free births at home and all three babies were robust and healthy, weighing 10lbs 3oz, 10lbs 8oz, and 9lbs, respectively- the last was a girl, the first 4 were boys. They were alert, strong- held up their heads from birth, even the premature csection babies, and all advanced physical and mental development to this day. They were well-muscled and beautifully proportioned. I felt and still feel so lucky.

None of my children have had any major CW health problems, though my 12 yr old is showing adrenal fatigue and I am convinced that too many years in this extreme light cycle has imbalanced him; the symptoms showed up this winter as we headed into deep and abiding darkness.  I have him drinking rhodiola (with other tonic herbs also) and putting a saturated solution made from himalayan and grey sea salts in his water. He gets adrenal "mitts" and they are almost resolved now after CTing for 3 weeks, although I think all of my children's mitochondria are suffering from wacky light cycles that is cumulative over years. When we went south for a year, I couldn't believe how easy it was to be healthy and strong and how brightly-coloured my children were then. Every person who lives up here is pasty-looking, light or dark skin you can see it clearly if you know what to look for.

My 12 yo has been having a detox reaction that I did not expect (I had two hours of feeling a bit sick and urgent, but nothing came of it; I went back to sleep and woke up refreshed); I discovered that his diet at his dad's was not exactly stellar- sugar, chips with sunflower oils, and not nearly enough fats- though he also fed them otherwise similar foods to my menu. He is back on track with their and his diet now. But my son has had several days of diarrhea and heart palpitations and paleness and emotional dread, which is not like him. I've watched him closely; he is leveling out now and feeling better. I gave him liver tonic herbs and foods because I believe his heart discomfort, which came only after eating, was liver-related and it abated when he got the liver-support. He is so thrilled with his new muscle definition that he has no plan to stop CT even though he felt rotten for a few days, haha.

I use f.lux on my computer and only incandescent bulbs and we eat by candlelight or sunlight when its here. I don't know about nnEMFs, but I guess it can't be much given that we are surrounded by mountains and forest and the population is small. We are in Whitehorse, Yukon, so you can see on a map how isolated we are. Within town too it is minimal and we are surrounded by trees even in our neighbourhood. Where we move will have far more people overall, but I'm trading microwave influx for sunlight and hoping for net gains. We'll still be in the wilderness, but overall there is a lot more going on there than here. I have shut down all wireless and bluetooth in my home, and we use cellular with speaker or wired earbuds and everything is shut off at night. Once we're in our forever home, I am not sure what avenue of communication we'll have because landline for phone is unlikely given our expected remoteness. When we lived in the bush we had a telephone on the actual pole that you had to walk to from the cabin, with a little shelter around it to sit, near the Alaska highway. It was fine.

My mum was extremely mal- and undernourished as a child and continued that, by habit, as an adult. She is 5'2" and weighed 95 lbs for most of my life, though she gained a bit in menopause and weighs around 105. Her mum's diet was the same, having come from a war-torn area as a refugee to Canada. She was from Sicily, and my mum was born in Canada a year after they landed. My mum said they ate flour balls rolled in sugar every day for supper except friday when they had pork chops- no breakfast ever, occasional lunch. My mother drank a lot of vodka while pregnant with me, and my dad smoked a lot of weed in the house all through childhood as well as when I was in the womb, and from my sideways research, I am convinced that the weed is what saved me from fetal alcohol syndrome, and ultimately had the opposite effect. \[My IQ drops 80 points when I'm fatigued specifically in areas of memory and concentration, and with this point-loss it still measures 40 points above average (WAIS- IV), to give you an idea of the impact of the TBI and how far from FASD I ended up- I was professionally tested last yr because of my TBI(- EDIT- ack had to look it up- bad memory!)\] My dad is Hungarian and grew up on a traditional diet, grew to his full height at 13yo, 6'3" and weighed since then 175lbs. He also started experimenting with substances at that time and suspects that stunted his growth. He has several uncles who were/are 6'4" to 6'6"." I grew up eating a mix of conventional and traditional Hungarian food (the latter as per my dad's insistence), but I ate enough bad food for damage. I started changing my diet in 1998 and just kept improving it as I learned more and more and resolved the load of health issues I discovered when I moved out and was no longer bathing in cigarette smoke, and the detox began. All things seemingly resolved, I was hit by a truck. I still think it matters, haha. Imagine if I'd been in poor health!

My children are far and beyond my health at any time in my life; they are like Greek gods even while I know they are not optimal because of the environment. I can't wait to get them south. And by south I mean 48.5° N, not anywhere warm, haha.

Will look up John Ott and Dr Kruse's book and podcasts. I have to divide my time between daily life, acquiring life-skills and seeking optimisation of health and wellbeing, and it is much harder now with the new TBI bottleneck, but I do my best.

# Thread: Hello from New Jersey :)

### RoxanneA — 2017-01-28T16:20:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-new-jersey.19000/post-207474>

Hi All,

New to the site and trying to take it all in. I am hypothyroid with Hashimotos, keep gaining weight, near sighted since childhood now having trouble close up as well, and more recently started dealing with some kind of IBS. I'm guilty of so many lifestyle issues that were mentioned in the various posts, it's crazy. I wear glasses, work on pc all day literally, under regular ole LED's. From what I'm gathering, I need to start with fixing the light in my environment first, so here are the couple things I've started doing and a few questions if anybody could help...

1) Blue blocking glasses from sunset to sunrise. I wake up well before the sun rises though, does that matter that I still wear them till sunrise or do I need them off as soon as I'm awake?
2) I've been trying to get out in the morning and walk without my glasses for at least a half hour, if not an hour or two. It's grey and cloudy most days this time of year though.
3) I'd like to change my day time bulbs so something better, what are the best ones for daytime, where I need to see to work.. full spectrum, or the high CRI LED? Any brand recommdations?
4) Ive changed all my night time lighting to red bulbs, does it matter if the bulb is painted red or does it specifically need to be a red light? Brand recommendations?
5) I bought a black light, 365 nm, I'm assuming I should use this from 10 am till about 3pm for normal UVA exposure this time of year?
6) I bought the solar glo 160 W bulb, do I use this when I would normally get UVB, 11 am till 1pm this time of year?

Also, I wake at 4:30 ish, going to bed around 9ish, sun is not up until 7:15 now, do I need to do anything different to make up for the sun difference in am?

Im not sure if my ancestry is needed to optimize my protocol but posting it just in case.
I've also include the lab work from August I had done, for the bun creatine ratio mentioned, shows I'm dehydrated I guess? Not sure why though, I drink constantly

There is nothing magic about 7:30AM
The only natural way is to wait for the sun to raise.
Everything else is already artificial.
To not add to the problem, I think that we should keep constant time when we get out of bed and start next day.
Changing the get out of bed time would result in jet lag. That is not desirable.
So,
I do not see a good reason to wear blue blockers before sunrise.
I get out of bed 6AM and start day by looking at two Solar Glow 160W.
\---
There is another consideration about (amount) of blue light.

When I wake, I'm wide awake.. mind racing ready to read more blog posts  I did stay there this morning, refused to get up or even look at the clock for what seemed like an eternity. Finally got up, and it was 5 am lol I'll try again tomorrow.

All really depends on how badly you want something.
Figure out how much sleep you need, but it have to be between 7-9 hrs. Say 8 hr.
From 7:30Am back out 8 hr, so you have 11:30PM
Go to bed 11:30PM.
Two hrs before that all is slow, no excitements, but sex (orgasm) before bed is highly advisable (any way you can get it is ok).
There is a whole separate thread on benefits of oxytocin, do not miss it.
two hrs before that, 9:30PM, end of any blue light, only red and IR allowed.
You have just finished (9:30PM) your (breakfast+lunch+dinner) your only meal of the day.
....
We still do not know your cortisol (level and profile),
After cortisol and thyroid are in order, you should be able to be full of energy all waking time.

....

> I'm referring to the fact I'm blonde/blue/freckles/fair skin.. I didn't get that way cause they were swimming in Lake Victoria in January  I did join a tanning salon, for $20 I get unlimited tanning a month. I've gone 2 - 3 times a week. My D level increased to 49.9 now on the latest lab. However, there is a disconnect here for me understanding why I'm doing it. The colder locations are in my genes. Just as seasonal eating would be beneficial , wouldn't seasonal UVB go hand in hand with that? Cold Adaptation wasn't done with the presence of UVB. There is a possible problem with tanning saloons.
We are not only after UVb.
We need both UVa and UVb.
Going to tanning saloons take a time.
Waiting for your turn takes time.
Then you have to spend time for actual tanning.
Coming back from then takes time.
Red Sperti should usually take about 15min period.

49.9 is low
You want 100
but 150 is also ok.

Skin must get accustomed to sunlight.
If you look at dr Wunch video, he recommends taking one whole month of gradual exposures to harden skin.
Because you have a white skin, it will take you less time that it takes @[Dia](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/11720/)
Dia have a daughter with light skin, they see big difference in times they need.

I really appreciate you answering all of my newbie questions, thanks Jan

I do go to bed usually between 9 - 11, the odd thing of course is that I wake up the same time regardless. I never need an alarm clock, been like that for a decade. When the time change happens, I then wake up later be default lol And mid summer it's even with the sun!  Listening to some of the podcasts, is what made me think with the thyroid, weight gain, and sleeping schedule... I'm metabolically stuck in summer continuously. I have zero issues falling asleep, and almost always sleep the night through and feel well rested when I wake. Usually can't remember any dreams.. ever.

I'm scheduled for my thyroid labs in 2 weeks. Im on Nature-throid currently. I was taking selenium daily 200mgs, but I got worried with all the seafood I'd be getting to much, so stopped in the last week.

I am going to look into the cortisol tests and fatty acids profile next.

### JanSz — 2017-01-29T15:56:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-new-jersey.19000/post-207524>

> [RoxanneA said:](/goto/post?id=207520)
>
> I really appreciate you answering all of my newbie questions, thanks Jan
>
> I do go to bed usually between 9 - 11, the odd thing of course is that I wake up the same time regardless. I never need an alarm clock, been like that for a decade. When the time change happens, I then wake up later be default lol And mid summer it's even with the sun!  Listening to some of the podcasts, is what made me think with the thyroid, weight gain, and sleeping schedule... I'm metabolically stuck in summer continuously. I have zero issues falling asleep, and almost always sleep the night through and feel well rested when I wake. Usually can't remember any dreams.. ever.
>
> I'm scheduled for my thyroid labs in 2 weeks. Im on Nature-throid currently. I was taking selenium daily 200mgs, but I got worried with all the seafood I'd be getting to much, so stopped in the last week.
>
> I am going to look into the cortisol tests and fatty acids profile next.

I guess improvement on your sleeping patterns is not a high priority.
\-------------
Nature-thyroid, I assume that it is thyroid hormone from pigs, better be.
Chew it before swallowing.
When getting ready for a blood draw for thyroid testing,
make sure that for some long time you were taking it early in the morning or better, right at wakeup time.
On a day of blood draw, take it (after) blood draw.
In that kind of setup, you want your FreeT3= (3.5-4.2) pg/mL
\-------------
Selenium, if you were taking 200mcg/day recently, I would keep it going.
You will find your selenium status after you get spectracell test done.
My own experience with selenium, supported via spectracell, is that I need that 200mcg every day.
When I tried EOD i got low.
If you have stopped selenium for more that 2-4 months, keep it that way, do spectracell test first.

I am worrying more that you will have plenty of selenium to accommodate Lugol.
And with your antibody's you may need more Lugols then usually.
Make sure that you get Lugol's from the link I posted.

\----------------
Wonder about not having or rather not remembering dreams.
That is also my situation, rarely I remember dreams.
I have heard that lack of some vit B's could be the reason.
Now I doubt that, with spectracell it is rather easy to keep track of this, but no dreams.

Let me know when you figure this one out.
\---------------
About fatty acids profile
do not be surprised when they come.
Lots of people have this messed up.
And the way it is messed up is that they already have a high omega3's
The next question then becomes the seafood question.
This is a hot topic,
at least on my list.

Fatty acids are everywhere.
.

I requested to join the facebook group, still waiting to be accepted. Would love to read all those links! Thank you for posting them.

I've been wearing my blue blocking glasses for about a week now, I think im putting them on along with the red lights too early. I'm passing out on my sofa like an hour after I put them on at 8 pm! lol Though I went to bed and fell asleep early, woke up at 3:30 and just stayed in bed till 5 am. I think I misunderstood the directions, thought we had to blue block as of sunset, but that seems to be too early for me, I'm affected by it immediately apparrently. Not sure if lack of melatonin is an issue for me though, I've never had trouble getting tired or falling asleep.

### RoxanneA — 2017-01-30T13:15:55+0200

It's very confusing. I have the dminder app, and in my location there wasn't any UVB since november until just over a week ago. I'll still go to the salon, its cheaper than the machine, and the booth does give UVA and UVB but I'm trying to get outside for a half hour to an hour if its sunny to get it naturally as well. It's a nice break mid day from being on the pc, wish it wasn't so cloudy all the time.

# Thread: Hello from Boulder, Colorado. How is my understanding of why sunlight is important?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-boulder-colorado-how-is-my-understanding-of-why-sunlight-is-important.19100/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mlamz — 2017-02-19T04:43:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-boulder-colorado-how-is-my-understanding-of-why-sunlight-is-important.19100/post-208881>

Hello!

I moved to Boulder, Colorado from the UK four and a half years ago because I realized that the lack of sunlight in the UK was detrimental to my health. I realized this because for seven years, I suffered from cholinergic urticaria, and the only thing that made it better was UVB phototherapy and when I went skiing in France and got a tan. _My health is now fantastic because I have made exposure to sunlight the number one priority in my life._

I have now recently discovered the work of Jack, Gerald Pollock, Stephanie Seneff, Nick Lane and Travis Christofferson. It has been thoroughly eye-opening. From their work, I have attempted to put together a paragraph summarizing the importance of sunlight, and would like to know if I am on the right track.

The paragraph is this:

\--------------------
Sunlight is comprised of infrared, visible and ultraviolet wavelengths of light. The near infrared wavelengths in sunlight penetrate the skin and structure the water within our cells and mitochondria into an “exclusion zone”. This exclusion zone in our cells and mitochondria causes a net negative charge, similar to how a battery functions. The ultraviolet wavelengths in sunlight then acts to supercharge this exclusion zone. It does this through the ability of the skin to create cholesterol sulfate from ultraviolet light. Cholesterol sulfate then attaches to the capillary walls and causes the size of the exclusion zone of our cells to increase. The net negative charge within our cells and mitochondria causes the electron transport chain within our mitochondria to become more efficient. A more efficient electron transport chain means better control of oxidative stress, which is the root cause of aging, chronic disease, and cancer, if we are to believe the oxidation theory of aging and the metabolic theory of cancer.
\--------------------

How does this sound?


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Greetings from Philadelphia
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-philadelphia.19158/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### brookway17 — 2017-03-02T01:17:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-philadelphia.19158/post-209504>

Hi, my name is Pete. I can't believe it took me this long to find Jack's site. I think this is a really interesting approach to health.I have always taken an evolutionary-based approach to health. My current situation is somewhat complex.

All my problems started with a tick bite. I love to walk in the woods. Which turned into a chronic Lyme infection. Which according to our pathetic healthcare system does not exist. The Lyme triggered a very rare neurological autoimmune condition called CLIPPERS. You never Heard of it, most neurologist haven't heard Of it. My immune system is attacking my lower brainstem which controls motor functions in the body. At my worst I was on a walker because of the severe balance problems. I had severe double Vision, you could not understand a word I said, I could not write and I even had difficulty typing.
The neurologist put me on intravenous steroids, 1 g per day for five days. This did help me miraculously. But it took me six months to finally taper off of the steroids. I wanted to get off so I can give my Immune System a chance To clear the Lyme infection. I went on a very strict organic diet similar to Paleo. I was able to go without steroids for about nine months but my symptoms were getting worse. I finally had to go back on intravenous steroids this time one gram per day for three days. But once again a very long slow taper. The good news is that I got rid of the Lyme disease, I haven't had any symptoms in the last five years.
However the clippers is still causing me problems. I am currently on hundred milligrams/D of Imuran& plaqunil. I have tried a lot of stuff in the alternatives space to no avail. One of the big problems with Imuran is that it is reported to cause Squamous cell cancer, due to UVA exposure causing DNA damage. There for I have been avoiding the sun. I do not know how this study was carried out but if you Google Imuran,Squamous cell cancer you get a lot of hits. I am in the process of trying to switch drugs so I can once again go out in the sun. It takes three months for the Imuran to start working and it takes three months to get off of it.

On the exercise side I do yoga every day and high intensity strength training four times a week and I am just starting to do H IIT. I do strength training to try to heal my bones and muscles from all the damage done by the steroids. Because of my balance problem I have fallen several times on sidewalks and parking lots. So far no broken bones.

**Age: 64** **Sex:** M **Joined [JackKruse.com](http://jackkruse.com): Feb 2017**

**Location: bucks county PA**

**Dx: Lyme, CLIPPERS**

**Surgeries: 7/2017 5 cm bladder stone removal
5/2007 hernia**

**Current Rx: Imuran, plaqunil,LDN, prednisone (currently at 20 mg per day trying to get down to 5 mg per day per Day),testosterone cream**

**Current Therapies / Protocols:**
Grounding at night for the last three years
I'm trying to use Curcumin to replace the prednisone
I have started to incorporate the Leptin Rx although I'm not sure I have a Leptin problem (BUN/creatine ratio 16)
I watch the sunrise every morning with bare feet on my concrete front porch in my pajamas. It is pretty cold at sunrise in Philadelphia!
I also walk my dog every day for a mile without my glasses or much clothing on.
A voiding blue light, eliminated all LED and fluorescent lighting from my house.
Working to reduce nonnative EMR in home
Trying to improve my redox

**Health WINS: nothing yet I'll only been at it for two weeks
Several people have told me that I look better. I think it might be too soon to expect results.
**Health Goals: improved balance, coordination, vision, speech.
Regain short term memory(Steroids destroyed mine)
**My Optimal Journal:** \[Insert your link\]****

### JanSz — 2017-03-18T02:06:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-sydney-australia.19165/post-210559>

> [Mayuri said:](/goto/post?id=210556)
>
> Richelle, Hi. Hope you enjoy your new home. We have extreme UV in Oz, is it ok to take in the extreme rays? (In Victoria) So far I've only been doing early-morning and sunset. I'm uncertain on this UV index = Extreme. Thanks.M

If you are able to get some more or less measured exposure,
then,
check your vit D levels.
If those levels are excessively high,
that would be indication that you are getting too high exposure.
\===============
You do not want to get more than twice of this:

Vitamin D (25-Hydroxyvitamin D)(250nmol/L=100.16ng/mL)

### Richelle Jones — 2017-03-18T12:21:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-sydney-australia.19165/post-210577>

> [Mayuri said:](/goto/post?id=210556)
>
> Richelle, Hi. Hope you enjoy your new home. We have extreme UV in Oz, is it ok to take in the extreme rays? (In Victoria) So far I've only been doing early-morning and sunset. I'm uncertain on this UV index = Extreme. Thanks.M

Hi Mayuri...ha...that was Janz not me... UV gets up to 14 here in Agnes but you don't sunbake then...too hot! I am out in sunrise & sunset and I do yoga on my back verandah about 9am to 1030am. The UV is plenty high enough for vitamin D then. I really struggled in Canberra to get enough UV even with naked sunbaking as often as I could....I haven't had any blood tests here yet but I can feel the difference in sunlight exposure on me. Hair, skin, nails and eyesight are improved. Small weight loss. Stress is still high but that is due to move and family crap.

### JanSz — 2017-03-18T14:00:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-sydney-australia.19165/post-210583>

> [Mayuri said:](/goto/post?id=210556)
>
> Richelle, Hi. Hope you enjoy your new home. We have extreme UV in Oz, is it ok to take in the extreme rays? (In Victoria) So far I've only been doing early-morning and sunset. I'm uncertain on this UV index = Extreme. Thanks.M

I have newer been to Australia.
**Latitude of victoria australia is -37.4713**
But I am suspecting that the situation about extreme UV is hyped.
Most people producing that news about extreme UV are sitting in air conditioning offices, do not see sun often enough,
have skin that rarely see sun and is not sun hardened.
I normally live in:
Parsippany NJ latitude 40.8574813
but have spend one whole year in
Angra dos Reis, Rio de Janeiro - RJ. **Latitude 23º 00' 24" S**
Construction work, lots of time outside.
Had a house right on the beach.
So I spend lots of time outside, my wife and her girlfriend spend most of the time on a beach (drinking rum and coca cola).
Kids were indoor, in school.
That was (1979-1980), so 37 years ago, nobody got ant skin problems.
I suspect that we were exposed to higher UV index than what Victoria have.
\----
To compare.
**Desirable vit D levels are:
Vitamin D (25-Hydroxyvitamin D)(250nmol/L=100.16ng/mL)**
Level 2x of above does not cause problems,

People that spend whole live outdoor on low latitude, when tested had:
Massai --119nmol/L
Hadzabe--109nmol/L

I think that is really about:
**having sun hardened skin**
testing vit D blood level (in order to be resistant to propaganda)

### Richelle Jones — 2017-03-18T14:33:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-sydney-australia.19165/post-210585>

Hi JansZ . I live at latitude 24 in Agnes Water Queensland so the UV here is definitley high year round - thats why we moved here. I have DMinder. We moved here in January this year from Canberra which is closer to Victoria and has much lower UV. I have had a pretty good tan for the last year or two so can withstand the sun for quite a while. We live near Bundaberg which produces a very nice rum that we like to have with dry, lime & soda! Our house is also near the beach and we get to see the sunrise and set and do some stand up paddle boarding in between. Agnes Water only has 2000 people and is quite remote. No traffic lights! Its a really beautiful place. Just need to get the 15 year old to love it. He is not loving it yet.

### Richelle Jones — 2017-03-19T00:40:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-sydney-australia.19165/post-210626>

> [Mayuri said:](/goto/post?id=210608)
>
> Oh thank you Richelle. So appreciate you sharing information with me. There is so much information to take in that one has to be sure that they got it right.
>
> I watch the UV index, I know how high the extreme UV can go – I do not lay out in the sun in the high heat. I hope I am correct to protect my eyes in the extreme UV light as well?
>
> We now taken early Sun try to get it least an hour, no less than a half hour, but it seems that we can go for more - perhaps up to 10 AM.
>
> Ozone hole moves around, Hopefully that extreme UV will lighten up as we move into the Fall. I cover up now when on the bicycle, but I was not doing that in early and mid summer – so my Vit D went up. I figured I was only on the bike for 30 minutes but I certainly don't want to be in a microwave, now do I? Nope. Late summer I was more informed.
>
> Sounds like you have a lovely routine going.
>
> I had read that when the UV light goes extreme that the plants produce more tannin. Tannin can block iron uptake I believe. There was a report on a pack of 10,000 wildebeest that were decimated by acacia trees during a drought.
>
> The animals were stripping the acacia trees of what small leaves they had left, and the trees knew that they were going to be destroyed. Apparently then the trees communicated to one another to produce more tannin. I think this communication occurred through a gas that the trees can release. Nonetheless, the grove of acacia trees did just that, increased the tannin in the leaves – and killed off 10,000 wildebeests. The scientists were amazed when they performed inspection on the animals, and confirmed that it was high levels of tannin that killed them.
>
> All this to say that my crops in my veggie garden didn't do so well this year I think it's because of the extreme UV light. Summer came in late but the tomatoes are just medium sized and not as large as I recall them growing in the northern hemisphere. You know? Dr Jack's instructions to us on the Donut hole are right on. Eeks
>
> I lightly steep what tea I drink down under. I like Daintree tea from up north in Australia. I feel relatively safe with it.
>
> I wonder if the fruit we eat contains fluoride?
>
> There is so much information here and when I read the entries I see that Dr Jack has been saving us for quite some time now. Honestly I can't understand how I missed it!!!
>
> That is the downside of being caught in a hurricane I suppose. I had some crazy shit going down these past 4 years. Tried to help with a situation in the family and entered the ring, got the shit kicked out of me. Yet justice is being delivered. I certainly have learnt a serious lesson about how much one can actually take on. (I did say to my father-in-law that going after the thieves would kill us but being a good and honest man he demanded justice.)
>
> Thankfully I am on the mend. I have done really well through treatment and I am remaining optimistic. I am in the process of repairing damaged nerve endings. The healing has been going well but I'm still not at 80% - I have some way to go yet but the information here has helped immensely.
>
> Enjoy your new location!
>
> Everyone here is super! Thanks.

"

"Tannin can block iron uptake I believe" maybe more red wine will reduce my iron?
I often wonder about the water in fruit & fluoride & other pesticides...
I thought the problem with the ozone hole is that it lets in other cosmic radiation that is normally filtered out.
Sounds like life is hectic. I think this middle age time is extremely demanding time when you have teenage children and elderly parents to look after. Add in some natural disasters...some illness for yourself and all the people you have to look after...fun times!

Drain=let pint of blood at the time out. (latter half pint)
They are usually able to check Hgb with pin prick to the tip of the finger (within a minute or two).
keep Hgb within:
man=~(15-16)
women=(14-15)
Test first, then make decision about letting the blood out.
\==
If not able to give donation,
ask doctor
for script for
therapeutic phlebotomy

# Thread: Newbie from Monterey, CA
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-from-monterey-ca.19172/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Barbara Pollard — 2017-03-05T18:46:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-from-monterey-ca.19172/post-209693>

I have an autoimmune disease called Stiff Person Syndrome (SPS). I have had this disease for 20 years and am looking forward to learning how to minimize, or better yet, reversing this disease.

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-10T19:08:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-from-monterey-ca.19172/post-210056>

Hi Barbara

Might be a good idea to start and journal in the Optimal journal section, and give some more info, and any changes you are integrating so far ....

But anyway, welcome!

### caroline — 2017-03-11T02:09:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-from-monterey-ca.19172/post-210083>

welcome Barbara,,,,,,,

As Sue suggested - please start a journal and give Dr. K. some health history - and your Mom's and Gran's and siblings too.

Where do you work?

### fitness@home — 2017-03-11T16:59:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-from-monterey-ca.19172/post-210121>

@[Barbara Pollard](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19827/) ,

I have a friend with this syndrome. She is in her mid 70s, but it started happening about 15 years ago. We worked together.

She manages with medication, but it seems to wan and then the symptoms get stronger. Last fall she was on vacation in Florida when it got worse. A friend had to fly down and drive her car back up to Michigan.

Seeing how it is for her, I understand your desire to heal.

### JanSz — 2017-03-11T18:17:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-from-monterey-ca.19172/post-210139>

> [Barbara Pollard said:](/goto/post?id=209693)
>
> I have an **autoimmune disease called Stiff Person Syndrome (SPS)**. I have had this disease for 20 years and am looking forward to learning how to minimize, or better yet, reversing this disease.

Stiff Person Syndrome-----> (progressive encephalomyelitis with rigidity and myoclonus)

Searching I am ending with membrane problems.

[https://duckduckgo.com/?q=encephalomyelitis phosphatidylcholine&t=ffcm&atb=v47-2&ia=web](https://duckduckgo.com/?q=encephalomyelitis+phosphatidylcholine&t=ffcm&atb=v47-2&ia=web)

\---------------
**CDP-choline (citicoline, cytidine 50-diphosphocholine)**

CDP Choline (250 mg, 60 Caps)
[https://www.vrp.com/CDP250/CDP-Chol...medium=cpc&utm\_term=&utm\_campaign=Ingredients](https://www.vrp.com/CDP250/CDP-Choline-250-mg-60-Caps?utm_source=bing&utm_medium=cpc&utm_term=&utm_campaign=Ingredients)

[https://www.amazon.com/s/ref=nb\_sb\_noss?url=search-alias=aps&field-keywords=Citicoline](https://www.amazon.com/s/ref=nb_sb_noss?url=search-alias%3Daps&field-keywords=Citicoline)

**I use:**
[https://www.amazon.com/Jarrow-Formu...TF8&qid=1489248582&sr=8-3&keywords=Citicoline](https://www.amazon.com/Jarrow-Formulas-Citicoline-Supports-Function/dp/B004JO4EFU/ref=sr_1_3_a_it?ie=UTF8&qid=1489248582&sr=8-3&keywords=Citicoline)

\---------------
**phosphatidylcholine**

**I use:**
[https://www.amazon.com/BodyBio-Phosphatidylcholine-Phospholipid-Complex-Softgels/dp/B0058A9O6I/ref=sr\_1\_2\_a\_it?ie=UTF8&qid=1489248772&sr=8-2&keywords=bodybio pc](https://www.amazon.com/BodyBio-Phosphatidylcholine-Phospholipid-Complex-Softgels/dp/B0058A9O6I/ref=sr_1_2_a_it?ie=UTF8&qid=1489248772&sr=8-2&keywords=bodybio+pc)

**But IV version may be better to start with.**
\========================================
Since we ended here;
get:
**Micronutrient Test (MNT)
CardioMetabolic Risk**
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-5#post-210075](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-5#post-210075)

and
from LabCorp or MayoClinic:

**Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum**

Make corrections to all deficiencies found in all three tests.
Repeat testing
fatty acids every 3 months
micronutrient test every 6 months

////////////////////////////

# Thread: Biohacking in the jungle!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-in-the-jungle.19175/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matt Wilson — 2017-03-06T04:52:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-in-the-jungle.19175/post-209730>

Hola! I’m Matt Wilson, co-founder of the travel company Under30Experiences and host of the Live Different Podcast. I moved here in Costa Rica, to eat fresh fish, live in the jungle away from most EMF, and have my feet grounded in the ocean everyday.

I’m an athletic 31, 5’10” and 150 lbs and need some help deciphering my WellnessFX Comprehensive labs… I’ve done lots of Dr. Kruse’s webinars and was sponsored by Bulletproof, so this is not a newbie ask.

My lifestyle is pretty damn strict, but I am on the computer 5+ hours per day (grounded) and have 15 employees, so there is some stress…. I eat very keto; eggs, fish, avocados, and sauerkraut at most meals. I strength train 3x/week, meditate 30-60min daily, drink R/O, tan my @$$ at 7am every morning, wear my blueblockers, and haven't taken a warm shower in over a year...

I’m a concerned about my Thyroid and hormone panel… T3, Free T3, and IGF1 are a little low, although Testosterone comes in at 1191 ng/dl, Androgen at 520 ug/dL, and SHBG at 60 nmo/L (all high!) Also notable when talking about Thyroid is my Total Iron Binding Capacity is 229 ug/dL (low).

My theory is that low body fat (less than 8%), combined with intermittent fasting, long hours, and lots of travel, has pointed me in the direction of hypothyroidism. This was deduced from listening to Ben Greenfield + Elle Russ’s Paleo Thyroid Solution, but maybe I’m wrong…

I know this drawing of blood is just a snapshot of this a moment in my life, but any insights on this would be appreciated. Gracias + Pura vida!

### Matt Wilson — 2017-03-08T14:00:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-in-the-jungle.19175/post-209862>

Moving this to the thread to the labs section! Thanks all.

### NADme — 2017-05-14T13:19:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-in-the-jungle.19175/post-214025>

Your extensive panel, is that a single comprehensive CPT that Quest offers or did you menu the composite?

Your SHBG is high and while total T is ranged for your age your free T might be better served by addressing your SHBG.

You do not have E2/estradiol on your list. Quest's E2 is not good, Labcorp sensitive:

**Estradiol, Sensitive, LC/MS**
TEST: 140244
CPT: 82670

Also, you need a urine iodine and selenium RBC, I use labcorp for those as well. You cant supplement one without the other or you will imbalance your thyroid. Dont eat too many Brasil nuts which has, if memory serves, 20-80 micrograms of selenium per nut. I was able to reduce thickening and eliminate 1-2 small nodules. Dont use lugals for iodine, i use detoxadine, nascent iodine.

E2, Iodine and Selenium will give you a better picture. Your SHBG at 60 is an issue.

Are you out by the University for Peace in the Jungle? I  CR. Lots of pals in the land of Tico/Tica. Exchange was 120:1 when I fist traveled to CR. Ironic the guy who is the big shot at the University for Peace was the co-author of Agenda 29 and advocate of eugenics.

# Thread: Hello From the Corn Fields, Ohio!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-corn-fields-ohio.19256/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### CFAN — 2017-03-20T19:48:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-corn-fields-ohio.19256/post-210775>

Hi!
So excited to be here!

I have been battling with weight all my life. I have always been in the overweight category, but when the scale said 306, I knew it was time to change. I am a 28 year old female. I tried hardcore exercise at a gym with frustratingly little success, changing my general diet with little sticking power. I fell into the paleo diet with some recent wins, but I still felt like I had no energy. Searching through books online, I found and read Dr. Kruse's book and it was a slap upside the head. So much science! Yet... it seemed like it explained so much!

I am just starting my journey down the rabbit hole, and frankly have no clue how to start using this forum to my benefit (recommendations/help here?), but I am excited for this new path!

### JanSz — 2017-03-20T20:22:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-corn-fields-ohio.19256/post-210779>

We are extremely efficient machine.
Hardcore exercise can only hurt you. It is meaningless as a way to loose weight.
When done indoor in the gym, it takes away precious outdoor time.

Fat is estrogenic.
Fat promotes aromatase,
aromatase promotes estrogen
estrogen makes things grow, including more fat

Body fat can/should be used for energy.
Body fat can be used for energy only if insulin,serum is low (2-3 range)
If insulin is higher than that certain minimum, there is no way to use body fat for energy (even if you are hungry and tired).
Time must pass to let glucose and insulin fall into levels low enough so use can be made of energy stored in body fat.
Practically it takes 12 or even more hours from the last bite of last meal before there is some chance of starting process of using body fat for energy.

Note, people using supplemental insulin
are (guaranteed) to not be able to loose weight.

So the easiest way is to eat within 8hrs window, have a first and last bite within 8hr window, and do not eat in other time (of 24hr period).

Next step is to eat just once/day
Next step is to eat once EOD, Every Other Day.
And so on.
Depending of how badly one wants to loose weight.

On top of above foundation,
come additions on the theme. CT is one of it. No carbs another one.

\-----------------------
Speaking of scale, you do not have to change religion,
but may be interested in habits of
Eastern Orthodox Catholics.
(About 200 days a year of severely limited food intake).

///

### fitness@home — 2017-03-20T20:42:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-corn-fields-ohio.19256/post-210780>

Hi CFAN,

Start a journal in the section I linked below:

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?forums/my-optimal-journal.17/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?forums/my-optimal-journal.17/)

Please give a history - medical, family (especially maternal side), labs if you have them and what your goals are. What steps have you taken to change your habits and health?

There are many people here that will chime in once you provide some background.

I know the blogs seem overwhelming, but just get started and read, read, read. Don't forget to read the comments at the end of blogs as you can learn a lot from others' questions and the answers they received.

# Thread: Old new member
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/old-new-member.19278/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Andrea — 2017-03-26T08:36:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/old-new-member.19278/post-211114>

Hi...

It's time for me to intruduce myself... I've been hanging around here for a while. In the beginning of my journey here, I even wrote a journal, [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/)

However, during the last year or so, when I look at my old journal I don't recognize myself. I have been through all that's written there, but I feel like a different person now.

When I first came here I was a fat, sick, tired, brain dead zombie, feeling like I was 80 - I suppose I looked like 80 - and I was waiting to die.
Now... I feel lighter, I AM lighter, my whole body functions far better. I think differently - and I see the world very differently. I am alive.

So I have decided to change my forum name.
My old name, Rerun, is a nick I've used for ages on the internet. It refers to Peanuts, the old comic strip, where Rerun was the little kid who sat on the back of his mother's bicycle, scared to death. That used to be me.

Now, I don't let my mother - or anyone else for that matter - control me. Only my environment does. Also, I don't want any reruns of my old life...

So bye bye Rerun, here is Andrea.

### JanSz — 2017-03-26T18:04:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/old-new-member.19278/post-211128>

That is powerful, intriguing statement Rerun, I mean Andrea.

Please tell us how you did that.
I am 77yo, but do not think of 80 as a major stopping point.
But one newer knows, may be change of name is also in my cards.

....

### caroline — 2017-03-27T03:03:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/old-new-member.19278/post-211181>

Welcome Back Andrea!!!! love what you have done/ accomplished.....

I am pretty positive if we all went back and re read our journals we would hardly recognise ourselves!

What a quantum journey we are on - Thanks for sharing Andrea!

I assume you are keeping the bike?

### Andrea — 2017-03-27T10:27:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/old-new-member.19278/post-211195>

Jan, you are an amazing man and you seem to be in an amazing shape. There's nothing wrong with 80 - except for when you feel like it when you're 35.
And you know, at your age, you have a far sounder hormone panel and libido than I have ever had - until just recently anyway...

What I have done to change the way I have? I have embraced discomfort.
Being comfortable and doing what's easiest never really got me anywhere but closer to death, faster.

On my way now, I actually got some help from you. I didn't get and use the supplements you suggested, but your suggestions helped me see what was wrong and what I needed to work with. I really do appreciate that you took the time to help me, more than you can imagine. Thank you!
(Ok, I am using a little Lugol's, although topically, on specific parts of my body. But that's it.)

The most significant changes to my life are sunlight, limited fake light, ocean based diet, grounding, and less nnEMF.
For more than a year now I have reduced my time in my nnEMF hell home to less than half. (I call it nnEMF hell, although in reality it's nothing compared to what many are facing in large cities in the US and around the world - but it's more than bad enough for me). Among other things. Most of my time now I spend in far better, nnEMF wise, environment than before.
Also, this winter, from October to December, I spent 4 weeks on places with lower population densities at lower latitudes. I should have gone somewhere in the beginning of this year too, but that I failed, unfortunately.

It's so frighteningly obvious that my so called home is killing me, there's no way to ignore it anymore, so I'm there less and less. For now I keep my head above water. But I do have a very uncomfortable decision to make if I'm ever to reach my potential...

### Andrea — 2017-03-27T10:27:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/old-new-member.19278/post-211196>

Thank you, Caroline!
Yes, I'm keeping the bike for now, although for other reasons than before. It's a means to be out in nature... in sunshine... with certain people...

# Thread: Leaky gut , zapping, black seed oil
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaky-gut-zapping-black-seed-oil.19321/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TANN — 2017-04-07T18:03:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaky-gut-zapping-black-seed-oil.19321/post-211963>

Hello all

My issues are as follows; leaky gut, adrenal health, brain fog, psoriasis, constipation, anxiety, histamine intolerance, difficulty losing weight (currently 15 st 4lb)
Ive had the gut issues for around 15 years but possibly all my life to a lesser extent. Ive made massive progress from where i had palpitations and black outs at my worst. These days i have the occasional skin flare up i feel tired after evening meal and caffeine has been a crutch for a long time. I know i still have a long way to go. I have recently been restoring stomach acid by taking HCL betain/pepsin and have got my BM much better than normal. I am doing low carb high fat and high protein but if i try less than 50g i get really constipated and the skin issues come back. I make my own sauerkraut and i use live apple cider vinegar with each meal.
I find mediation fantastic and do 5 mins pranayama breathing following by 10 minutes deep (mantra) meditation per day.
Recently been on a protocol including L glutamine, NAG, NAC, Ester C, Q10 probiotics (have done this many times before also) I also take Mg theronate at night which helps big time with achy joints and sleep.
With Jacks recommendations I also now make sure im getting more daylight im eating fresh sardines twice per week and i use grounding mat in the car. plus i do sauna/cold showers three times a week.
Recently im using my terminator 2 zapper directly on gut and i have seen very positive results; i have better skin , my energy has improved and less anxiety. Last night i slept for 10 hours when i typically get just 6-7 . I wanted to know others experience of zapping and of course Jacks opinion. (got a horrible feeling he wont condone it!)
Finally i would like to know opinions of black seed oil as well, i believe Jack doesn't recommend it but would like to know more please. It helps me lose weight i can make me a little aggressive as it builds testosterone. I know that it has omega 6 and not 3 which wouldn't be good but i wanted to know is it really that bad?
Thats my introduction and questions for now.
Best of luck to everyone

### Sue-UK — 2017-04-08T13:44:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaky-gut-zapping-black-seed-oil.19321/post-211995>

Hi TANN

"have done this many times before also" on that supplement protocol speaks volumes. Your post mentions lots of supplements and a zapper, and it seems as if you are only just dipping a toe into the water as far as the teaching here goes? Getting more daylight is good .... but how much and when is important, as is where you live (population density, pollution etc). How are you following the circadian light cycle? Eating sardines twice a week is good, how are you planning to improve on that?

Have you read [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/your-first-post-to-read-here-at-jackkruse-com.17829/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/your-first-post-to-read-here-at-jackkruse-com.17829/).

Welcome to the forum.

### Martha Ray — 2019-12-15T03:48:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaky-gut-zapping-black-seed-oil.19321/post-283599>

>
> ... Finally i would like to know opinions of black seed oil as well, i believe Jack doesn't recommend it but would like to know more please. It helps me lose weight i can make me a little aggressive as it builds testosterone. I know that it has omega 6 and not 3 which wouldn't be good but i wanted to know is it really that bad?

I was wondering what Dr. Jack Kruse thought about Black Seed Oil... sorry he does not favor it. Hope it is fine for occasional use. It is the only 'supplement' that **works on my pain** and it usually works within 20-30 minutes. I only take if I am in pretty bad pain and after I've tried CT. usually no more than 3x a month. I am not able to take most Rx meds for pain. At first I hated the taste of the _Amazing Herbs_ liquid **Black Seed Oil** but I've acquired a taste for it and look forward to taking it when I am in pain because it works so well for me.
Of Black Seed Oil..
The folks selling it say ....it will cure anything except death.

I't's worked on any kind of pain I've tried it on.... for muscular skeletal pain the Egoscue Static Back Exercise [https://www.egoscue.com/webmenus/ECiseHTML/30.html](https://www.egoscue.com/webmenus/ECiseHTML/30.html)
and warm baths work sometimes too especially at night before bed.

**Static Back**

1.  Lie on your **back** with your legs up over your inflatable large block or up over a chair/couch.
2.  Your arms are at 45 degrees and your palms up.
3.  Try to relax your upper **back** and notice if your low **back** is flat evenly from left to right.
4.  HOLD.

[**Static Back - Egoscue**](https://www.egoscue.com/webmenus/ECiseHTML/30.html)
[https://www.egoscue.com/webmenus/ECiseHTML/30.html](https://www.egoscue.com/webmenus/ECiseHTML/30.html)

# Thread: Greetings all!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-all.19396/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sun-sybarite — 2017-04-29T07:36:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-all.19396/post-213061>

I am very happy to be here. I am a 39 yr old female from tropical South India. From young age I have faced several small illnesses which have kept my metabolism down and not able to live a full life. Instead of just accepting it as many do and taking a dozen pills daily, I have searched for the answer that feels right and think I have found it here.
Around a year back , I started reading and devouring the blog including this [article](https://www.jackkruse.com/the-holy-trinity-ct-4/) about how Himalayan Monks lived such long healthy and happy lives while tropical southerners are facing an epidemic of diabetes, atherosclerosis and renal diseases. It is so true, I see it happening in my family and all around me. But it was not the case in a century ago. 3/4 of generations back - before there was electricity here - my ancestors who spent the earlier half of the day out in the sun, lived from anywhere to 90-110 yrs and died of old age not any major disease.
Learning from other peoples stories has helped me as much as Dr.Kruse's blog. What I like is the emphasis on nature healing. Because it is accessible but also affordable - sunlight is abundant here!

I will share my journey in the appropriate sections.

### caroline — 2017-05-01T03:29:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-all.19396/post-213136>

### Sun-sybarite — 2017-05-01T08:26:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-all.19396/post-213141>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=213133)
>
> Use it.
> Whole body, nothing covered.
> Use only the clothes that you had been born with.
>
> .

Oh well, I will try to JanSz. Thanks for the advice.
India is over crowded and people are conservative but I try to get sun in the early morning when not many people are around. I wear shorts and really small top that exposes my belly area and keep my eyes open and look at the sun. After 8.30am( its summer here) the sun becomes really unbearable. When I close my eyes, I can see the colors and early morning its green, yellow, pink(combination of red and violet, I'm thinking) but after a while its full on red.

During day time the temperatures can be anywhere from 35 -40 Celsius. Temperatures in summer here increase every year. Maybe its a sign of the global warming. It drives many people indoors away from the sun into air-conditioned homes or cars. Which is sad. I have read in this forum of so many people relocating to sunny places but here it is abundant - just so intense that people go to great lengths to avoid it.

I also try to get evening sun. I enjoy it a lot, makes me happy - and weirdly sleepy!
One thing is - I never used to tan for more than 1 day( my tan was usually gone next day) but now if I get into daytime sun my tan doesn't go away for 4/5 months.

### JanSz — 2017-05-01T15:24:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-all.19396/post-213148>

> [Sun-sybarite said:](/goto/post?id=213141)
>
> Oh well, I will try to JanSz. Thanks for the advice.

>
> During day time the temperatures can be anywhere from 35 -40 Celsius. Temperatures in summer here increase every year. Maybe its a sign of the global warming. It drives many people indoors away from the sun into air-conditioned homes or cars. Which is sad. I have read in this forum of so many people relocating to sunny places but here it is abundant - just so intense that people go to great lengths to avoid it.
>
> I also try to get evening sun. I enjoy it a lot, makes me happy - and weirdly sleepy!
> One thing is - I never used to tan for more than 1 day( my tan was usually gone next day) but now if I get into daytime sun my tan doesn't go away for 4/5 months.

What are your latitude, altitude?
One of the ideas of sun exposure is to get good levels of vitamins A & D.

There are at least two important variables that play major role in this task.
One is latitude the other skin color (actually amount of melanin in the skin)

You are born with darker skin, have to spend more time exposed to sunlight.

The other are vitamins.
They, the vitamins, can be had by exposure to UVa and UVb frequencies.
Vit A needs less attention in this context, just supply carotenes in food.

Vit D presents more problems, it depends on UVb exposure.
Only at equator UVb is present at all the times there is a sunlight.
At higher latitudes UVb content gradually decreases.
(say up to 30 degrees) one of this frequencies (UVb) are present is sunlight only at about two hours before and after solar noon.
If one goes further up on latitude that time is even less.

Overall, excess sunlight will not hurt, deficiency is hurtful.

There are other reasons why particular person may be lacking vit D (after substantial sun exposure).

With all that said, it is much easier to just do blood testing 2-3x/year to figure out where you stand actually.
Because guessing may be deceiving.
You want to have at least:
Vitamin D (25-Hydroxyvitamin D)(250nmol/L=100.16ng/mL)

One must shy away from vit D supplementation. (That is not the same chemical)(vit D vs vit D,sulfate)
If sunlight is scarce, frequent showers can be a factor, they can wash out (freshly made) vit D.
If you expose only say half of your skin area, that is a big negative for which you must compensate with doubling time of exposure.
Private space (on top of roof??) may come handy.
\----------------------
The other very important factor is Circadian Rhythm.
You can think of it as (day=light), (night=absence of light).
First it should be steady, same hours for the day, same hours for the night.
Say if you change your sleep time but 1 or 2 hrs, think of it as a jet lag that people get when traveling across time zones.
At night is good idea to sleep in totally blacken room, not even smallest night light.
Our body knows that there is a day when eyes see blue light.
Keep that in mind, because you do not wand to deceive your body's sensors by looking at TV, computer screen or celphone at the time when normally blue light is absent.
One reason why technology plays havoc with our heath.
\-------------------------
We are creatures of light.
The energy that powers our bodies comes mostly from sunlight (not from food).
Per latest @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) estimates, 2/3 of energy we use is from sunlight.
I bet, in tropics may be more than 2/3
The other factor is that our needs for a type of foods vary with Latitude.
Closer to equator one would (generally) eat more carbohydrates and less seafood.
That would reverse going out farther from equator.
Needs of individual person may vary from averages.
That
should make you aware that discussions on this board started and mostly continue with attention to people living at 20-30 maybe 40 degree of latitude and low altitudes.
You have to keep in mind your location while reading some of discussions.
\-------
Speaking of food
We are mixture of variety of organisms,
most of which are gut bacteria.
Half of the battle is to keep gut happy.
Having bristol score #4 is highly important in this context.
[https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipe...tolStoolChart.png/600px-BristolStoolChart.png](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/9e/BristolStoolChart.png/600px-BristolStoolChart.png)

Solids.
Water, we are mostly water, mostly EZ water, but plain liquid water is important too.
Drinking good water is important.
Observe how your body manages water. Watch pee color.
\-------------------------------------
Body temperature tells about thermal balance.
Too high burns.
Too low, burns less, weight gain.
Deep under the tongue
98.6F= 37.00C
Use accurate thermometer. 1/100F(not C)
Basal Digital Thermometer Fertility Tracking with 1/100th degree Accuracy to Test Basal Body Temperature BBT

Think of thyroid hormones.
They are made of iodine and selenium.
You must have this elements available.

Todays water is usually provided by municipalities.
To keep that water clean the use additives.
Those additives compete with iodine
iodine, iodide is halide
it have to compete with
chloride
fluoride
bromide

You must use (excessively) Lugols to have a chance in this battle.
Do that 10x more if any female in your area have
goiter
fibrocystic breast
uterine fibroids
This is not about family, it is about area that you live in.

Lugols=(iodine)+(potassiun iodide)

..................................
1/day-->Super Selenium Complex, 200 mcg 100 capsules (lef.org)

1 dropperfull/day=40mg/day=16drops
Lugol's Iodine Family Pack -- 6 (2 fl. oz.) bottles
[http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00E3B49R2?psc=1&redirect=true&ref\_=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o04\_s00](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00E3B49R2?psc=1&redirect=true&ref_=oh_aui_detailpage_o04_s00)
\-------
Check your weight:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-5](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-5)
say
5'5"----120#
5'8"----130#

If you could stay on one thread would be easier to have overall status.
..

# Thread: Here comes the Sun! <my intro>
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-comes-the-sun-my-intro.19574/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Laur — 2017-05-31T19:44:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-comes-the-sun-my-intro.19574/post-215182>

I have learned over the years that when my functioning is compromised (low energy and slow brain for starters) that only the sun and ocean can restore me. Going to SW Florida, Tulum (Mexico), and other parts of the Caribbean have always rescued me: I take what I call "emergency" impromptu trips South-to-the-sun to get my mind and body back on track.

I have Hashimotos, Tinnitus (for the past 4 years), SAD, and TMJ. Twenty pounds overweight that I can't seem to ever lose.
My descent into lesser health coincides with my increase in blue light (laptop, etc) over the past 6 years.
fyi: I live in The lower Hudson Valley area of NY.

Last year I discovered the Jack interviews : ) on youtube, starting first with Dave Asprey's interview....and then many others... Jack was articulating what I have always known to be innately true for myself, I need A LOT of the right light to thrive - and I believe I need it more than most (so now I need to find out what's going on mitochondrially <hope that's a word> )

I am a true Newbie here, and I look forward to "getting started!!"

Thanks Jack - and All...
Laura

### caroline — 2017-06-01T10:50:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-comes-the-sun-my-intro.19574/post-215236>

welcome Laura. Dr. K. has a thread .....the importance of newbies - have a look.

if you can manage it - become a gold member and you will have access to the monthly webinars and the follow up live Q&A when you can ask Jack anything.

Now that you mention it ....we used to go to the Bahamas about three times a year - to regain our sanity! I suppose it is really different now.

### Laur — 2017-06-01T17:59:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-comes-the-sun-my-intro.19574/post-215266>

Thanks!
I will take a look at the Newbie thread! Because this is a public forum AND the nature of my profession, I did not say more....however I will say that the impromptu trips South truly were emergencies, because other modalities were failing. The sun, warmth, and the negative ions from the ocean change me in a way that noyhing else can...when things are pretty dire healthwise.
I will also take a look at what the Gold membership entails....
I truly appreciate the feedback!

# Thread: Hi
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.19604/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 163
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### minimal — 2017-06-07T17:38:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.19604/post-215569>

Hi everyone,

I decided to combine three things: simple Hello, my gratitude for having chance to associate with You - rather inquisitive People who are not happy with cookie-cutter prescriptions for everyone. I also have quesiton which I didn't know where to put.

I'd like to pose You question on behalf of someone who needs help much more than I. I'm sure this person would like to interact with You personally but unfortunately doesn't speak English. I'm really sorry for doing that as this is my first post on this forum but I think that if He didn't ask me for this favor I would never leave my comfort zone and would never find power to interact with You. Why? Actually for many reasons. The first is that english is not my mothertongue. I'm also very intimidated with Your knowledge and many times I simply don't comprehend what Jack or many of You are on about. This is not the case of my language skills but rather that in comparision with many of You I feel retarded - sometimes I catch the idea after weeks or months of thinking and reading about it.

Anyway, my acquaintance is in his 50's and was diagnosed with grade 4 patellar chondromalacia.
As far as I know, there is not much to do about it. His story is very long but rather typical. In a nutshell he's very active (maybe not chronic cardio but close to it), overweighted, leptin resistant for sure, he's not epi-paleo nor paleo, doesn't do CT's and doesn't mind EMF's so there is a lot to do... But I'm wondering if anyone here have been there and could pass some ad hoc advice to him that could help convince him that his knee is not just separated problem?

I told Jack's and some others story about being optimal and reversing irreversible to many of my friends including him but they don't get it. Many of them still stuck in many common wisdom paradigms so when I mention about leptin, electromagnetism, CT, EMF, mitochondria and many other things they simply smile under their breath. But in face of surgery or other threatening circumstances people start to be more open.

So, is there anything that could help his condition at least put off the surgery? I would be very grateful for any meaningful reply.

Even if this thread will be left with no answer I would like to thank to Jack for bringing his ideas to the World and to others who spread his message.

Best regards,

Arek

PS: English is not my mothertongue so please don't mind my gramar

### JanSz — 2017-06-08T00:47:09+0300

### caroline — 2017-06-08T01:27:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.19604/post-215594>

### drezy — 2017-06-10T18:50:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.19604/post-215754>

@[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) Nice list

I'll add a philosophy that I've picked up around here too because I like borrowing people's thinking templates.

When dealing with my family, especially the ones who just don't get it, it's helpful to remember Jack's "Half truth" concept because you often have to dance around it and with it a bit.

Your friend claims that kneeling on hard surfaces regularly caused his issue.
Half truth: It's kinda half true and many people are happy enough to get to a half truth.

The bigger picture is probably more like there is an underlying breakdown going on in his connective tissues throughout his body. Why?
Half truth: We could claim that he's just not young anymore and stop at that half truth. The odd thing about half truths is that two of them do not add up to to a whole truth. It's more like they move you half the distance toward (or sometimes away from) your goal.

The me from a year ago would suggest that he start eating bone broth for most meals because he may very well have a leaky gut that lectins are sneaking through and replacing all lectins with bone broth will patch the gut and stop the internal damage.
Half truth: Since you understandably are unable to completely rearrange this man's life and habits could this be true and help? Sure. But see it still stops with the food half truth (paleo/ketogenic). I was happy as a pig (with 6% more bodyfat but still fitter than my colleagues) with that half truth for the last 11 years. Still it's just a half truth and raises the question: Why? Doc's blog post on leaky gut pretty much required me to try to add a new lobe to my sore brain after reading it.

Becoming good at recognizing half truths in yourself and others can make you better at maybe luring yourself and others down the right path as you absorb more of the whole truth.

Sadly there is no button we can press to make your friend:
get morning sun while barefoot on grass\*
get solar noon UV light nude or in a tan-through\*
not watch TV in the evening\*
jump into a CT tub regularly
asses the labs Jan suggests
alter his diet
avoid as much EMF exposure as possible\*

Doc Kruse did a very long interview where near the end he was pressed to condense priorities here they are/were ( [https://selfhacked.com/2015/04/12/i...use-emfs-biohacking-water-dha-and-blue-light/](https://selfhacked.com/2015/04/12/interview-with-dr-jack-kruse-emfs-biohacking-water-dha-and-blue-light/) ):

Dr Kruse’s order of importance:

Circadian Rhythm (marked above with \*)
DHA
Limiting blue light
Water (spring water)
Sleep
Cold
Psychological stress
Exercise
