# Thread: A curious mind
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-curious-mind.19937/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Optimal Health is EZ — 2017-08-01T18:32:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-curious-mind.19937/post-220636>

“In the early fifties I suffered temporarily from a disease of middle age: I used to give philosophical talks about science-how science satisfies curiosity, how it gives you a new world view, how it gives man the ability to do things, how it gives him power-and the question is, in view of the recent development of the atomic bomb, is it a good idea to give man that much power?“

\-Richard Feynman

Dr. Jack Kruse is dooming humanity. He has seen the writing on the wall and is working us towards extinction. If you listen to and follow what he says we will perish.

Richard Feynman in the 50s was one of many curious, genius minds. Inquisitive. Pressing the boundaries of known knowledge. This has been rectified.

The main signaling molecule identified with this disease was treated in an insidious fashion: using invisible energy beamed in an ambient, omnipresent manner around the world. You can’t escape it. Without this human creativity would be thoroughly unleashed.

Human minds created The Bomb. Quantum mechanics is a blind alley - ultimately it is inaccurate. Feynman in the right context with the right understanding could end the world. The world didn’t give him or his mental counterpart this option.

Whether the decision to saturate the ionosphere with ungodly amounts of electromagnetic radiation and thus destroy humanity’s dopamine was purposeful or not the end result is the same: we won’t have time to advance science enough to the point we kill ourselves. The fields of science have stagnated. Dr. Pollack has mused on this subject and now we know the answer: human beings are not what they once were. Technology is killing our brains. Somehow this is accepted perhaps fear of nuclear annihilation is so great we’d rather be dumb and stymied than creative and riding waves of new understanding into future unknown.

Listening to Dr. Jack Kruse will liberate your brain. Are you willing to be this dangerous?

### Optimal Health is EZ — 2017-08-20T18:36:54+0300

# Thread: Becoming Mitochondriac
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/becoming-mitochondriac.19946/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### WildForWesties — 2017-08-03T02:54:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/becoming-mitochondriac.19946/post-220778>

I am excited and grateful to have been led to Dr. Kruse's work and this group of smart, courageous, leading-edge thinkers. I've spent time in a lot of health forums and this one is unique. Reconnecting in a fundamental way with nature and myself as a "being of light" resonates deeply. It feels right and vital and utterly transformative. What a gift.

I have been on a downward health spiral since contracting either EBV or HHV6 (I have both and don't know which started this FGO) in 2001, Lyme in 2005, autoimmune thyroid issues in 2007 and mold exposure in 2009. Prior to 2008 I rarely went to the doctor except for gyn exams - didn't trust them and thought they were worthless since all they ever did was prescribe drugs or say "your labs are fine there's nothing wrong with you". I could have never imagined the depths of the chronic illness rabbit hole I've been living in since. I've tried many things, been to 50 doctors and practitioners, and spent MANY thousands of dollars trying to get well. I \*have\* made significant progress however light, water and magnetism are foundational pieces I've been missing. Through implementing these strategies it feels like my life is about to change in ways I can't imagine. Bring it on!

I'm reading, listening and absorbing as fast as possible, which is probably slower than most since I have absolutely no science background. Doesn't matter! I am 100% committed to fully recovering my health, I love learning and especially love Dr. Kruse's no-nonsense approach and his direct and practical applications of science. I look forward to many fruitful interactions and celebrations of success.

### Robert Marking — 2017-08-03T05:43:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/becoming-mitochondriac.19946/post-220788>

Welcome aboard!!! Like you, I have battled chronic fatigue due to Lyme & coinfections, EBV, and Mold Illness unknowingly for over 45 years and have finally won the battle. I am glad to hear that you have started the Shoemaker Protocol I just completed the protocol recently myself. That's going to be huge for you.

I truly feel that what Dr Kruse says is true, "you can't get well in the same enviroment that you got sick". It's all about minding your mitochondria and doing everything in your power maximize mitochondrial energy while lowering oxidative stress.

### WildForWesties — 2017-08-03T05:58:20+0300

> Welcome aboard!!! Like you, I have battled chronic fatigue due to Lyme & coinfections, EBV, and Mold Illness unknowingly for over 45 years and have finally won the battle. I am glad to hear that you have started the Shoemaker Protocol I just completed the protocol recently myself. That's going to be huge for you.
>
> I truly feel that what Dr Kruse says is true, "you can't get well in the same enviroment that you got sick". It's all about minding your mitochondria and doing everything in your power maximize mitochondrial energy while lowering oxidative stress.

It's SO good to hear you are doing well. It always gives me hope to hear from people who made it to the other side. What else did you do besides the Shoemaker protocol? Did you do the PK protocol? My doc adds that in and it's helping although very expensive. I also completely agree about the role of environment. I'm looking at moving back to Texas where I spent the first 30 years of my life. Until then there is much I can do to improve where I am now.

### Robert Marking — 2017-08-09T07:27:31+0300

Until then there is much I can do to improve where I am now.

Hi WildForWesties, Sorry that it has taken me a few days to get back to you. I hope that you are doing well.

So let me share with you a few things that I am currently doing to combat Biotoxin Mold Illness, Lyme and Co infections and it's symptoms.

First off, I am an Holistic Health Practitioner that utilizes Autonomic Response Testing (ART) which includes Direct Resonance Testing to first detect the specific hidden stressors that are affecting the the body and it's biophoton field.

Stressors can be any of the following...........

1\. **Structural Stessors/Trauma** – muscle, bone, connective tissue, scars, etc (car accidents, blunt trauma, falls, sprains, breaks, crashes, impacts, surgery, burns, dental, etc)

2\. **Emotional Stressors/Trauma** - untimely deaths, abuse, neglect, accidents, events, negative programming, brainwashing, negative thoughts and/or emotion, unresolved psycho-emotional issues, etc.

3\. **Chemical Stessors /Trauma** – viral & bacterial infections, parasites, food allergies & sensitivities, genetic mutations, MTHFR, nutritional deficiencies, biochemical imbalances, vaccines, toxins, pesticides, herbicides, heavy metals, amalgam fillings, etc.

4\. **Electromagnetic Stressors/Trauma -** non-native EMF (electromagnetic fields), WiFi, cell phones, computers, TV, high power lines, x-ray’s, airplanes, florescent lights, LED lights, tanning beds, smart meters, geopathic, etc.

5\. **Spiritual Stressors/Trauma** - Unresolved spiritual conflict or trauma, attachments, entities, etc.

6\. **Other** - unclassified types of trauma

When the body is stressed by any of the above stressors, the cells start to emit incoherent light and this disrupts body's regulation of it's biophoton field which controls all communication between the cells throughout the body. There's 100,000 chemical reactions per second in the cells and these are all controlled by light/photons. In ART this disruption of communication throughout the biophoton field is called "Blocked Regulation".

When a person has "Blocked Regulation" from a chronic Stressor, the Autonomic Nervous System ANS often times is stuck in the Sympathetic Fight or Flight mode. A person will not get well in the Sympathetic Mode for a number of reasons. The initial goal is to get the patient out of Sympathetic Fight or Flight mode and in the Parasympathetic Rest & Healing Mode. The only way this can take place is by detecting the specific stressors affecting the body and biophoton field and addressing those stessors accordingly.

Anything that causes any level of stress on the Autonomic Nervous System for that patient should be addressed and/or removed from the patients environment.

Of course I have addressed my hidden stressors listed above, and here are a few key things that I focus on personally...........

1.  Pure RO water re-mineralized and structured with low surface tension and high zeta potential much like Hunza Water
2.  Sun Exposure
3.  Reduce EMF exposure, I wear a energy balancing pendant that not only blocks harmful EMF, but it also converts EMF into harmonious energy for the body

4.  Avoid all Hidden Food Allergies/Sensitivities/Intolerances , one of the biggest causes of hidden inflammation on a daily basis for most people
5.  Check for Sulfur Metabolism Issues,I would bet that you will have a problem with this, this is very common with chronically ill people, this leads to poor detox pathways and high ammonia levels, I have a severe case myself
6.  Ketogenic/Paleo type of diet for lower inflammation (avoid grains, increasing good fat intake, consume more veggies. moderate protein)
7.  Rectal Ozone Therapy at home, over the past 2 years this has been huge in my battle against mold and Lyme, I feel that it is a must for anyone battling chronic viral and/or bacteria infections and mold illness. Viruses such as EBV, Herpes, Ebola, Coxsackie are especially susceptible to destruction by Ozone Therapy. Ozone modulates the immune system and it also improves oxygen utilization is the cell, which leads to more energy production in the mitochondria. There is a very inexpensive way to do in home rectal ozone therapy.
8.  HBOT (Hyperbaric Oxygen Therapy)/EWOT (exercise with oxygen therapy) Ive been doing HBOT in conjunction with Ozone Therapy for 2 years and this is massive. Because of increased oxygen utilization from Ozone, the hemoglobin has a greater ability to mobilize more Oxygen into the cells where it can be used for energy. It's not how much oxygen you breath, it's how much is utilized for energy production. My personal HBO chamber that I had made is a hard chamber that I use 4 days a week, but if you cant afford a chamber then EWOT has been shown to be as effective and its very simple set up and use, and is very cost effective.
9.  Eng3 makes the Nano Vi which is almost a miracle cure for oxidative damage on DNA & Proteins from chronic stressors including Lyme and Mold exposure. This machine emits "Exclusion Zone Water" that is Bio-Identical to the body's. This EZ water mist mimics the signaling of the Reactive Oxygen Species ROS that is responsible for refolding and repairing damaged proteins in the cells. Once the proteins are refolded and repaired they can be used for producing energy in the cells once again. [www.Eng3corp.com](http://www.Eng3corp.com)
10.  Magnetico Mattress
11.  Near infrared Sauna - detoxing, collagen production, helps form and charge EZ water, mine is Low EMF and home made for less than $120
12.  Supplements to help support mitochondria function, NADH, CoQ10, Folate, B vitamins, Zinc, etc.
13.  Lastly, I also feel that utilizing a technique based on quantum physics called "Destructive Interference" has been one huge key in defeating Lyme and mold. The law of destructive interference says that if you have the exact frequency of something (Freq, A) and then make an inverted copy of that frequency which would be its mirror image (Freq. B) and then emit Freq B into Freq. A, ...the result would be that Freq B will energetically neutralize Freq A. The new noise canceling headphones use this very same technique. So here's how its done with a person. First through ART/Direct Resonance Testing discover the specific strains of infection the patient has, next make an inverse copy of that infection imprinted into a homeopathic solution, next take a laser and laser the inverse frequency of the infection into the biophoton field of the patient. If lasered in at the right place into the biophoton feild, the inverse frequency of the infection will travel throughout the entire biophoton feild energetically neutralizing the infection where ever it may be.

So there is a breakdown off some of the tools that I used to beat Lyme and Mold. I think that the Surviving Mold Protocol was a must in my battle with mold.

Feel free to fire away with any questions!

> [WildForWesties said:](/goto/post?id=220778)

> I am excited and grateful to have been led to Dr. Kruse's work and this group of smart, courageous, leading-edge thinkers. I've spent time in a lot of health forums and this one is unique. Reconnecting in a fundamental way with nature and myself as a "being of light" resonates deeply. It feels right and vital and utterly transformative. What a gift.
>
> I have been on a downward health spiral since contracting either EBV or HHV6 (I have both and don't know which started this FGO) in 2001, Lyme in 2005, autoimmune thyroid issues in 2007 and mold exposure in 2009. Prior to 2008 I rarely went to the doctor except for gyn exams - didn't trust them and thought they were worthless since all they ever did was prescribe drugs or say "your labs are fine there's nothing wrong with you". I could have never imagined the depths of the chronic illness rabbit hole I've been living in since. I've tried many things, been to 50 doctors and practitioners, and spent MANY thousands of dollars trying to get well. I \*have\* made significant progress however light, water and magnetism are foundational pieces I've been missing. Through implementing these strategies it feels like my life is about to change in ways I can't imagine. Bring it on!
>
> I'm reading, listening and absorbing as fast as possible, which is probably slower than most since I have absolutely no science background. Doesn't matter! I am 100% committed to fully recovering my health, I love learning and especially love Dr. Kruse's no-nonsense approach and his direct and practical applications of science. I look forward to many fruitful interactions and celebrations of success.

Ditto!

# Thread: Yo from North cyprus

### Stevie D — 2017-08-04T12:11:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yo-from-north-cyprus.19952/post-220894>

Hi everyone I’m Steve I’m 24 and I live in north Cyprus.

First thing I ever heard of Jack was that his jacket made him a metrosexual and that he likes to blow people’s minds as a hobbie on primal edge.

And I’ve not looked back since.

I was originally born and raised in south London and after leaving school at 16 I was running on a pure sugar based metabolism and was heading from a school with 6 wifi’s into central London for work with god knows what RF flying about.

I was at time in ok physical shape since I had done MMA all my life but the stress of my job lead me to a slow downwards spiral of my body and mental health.

Fast forward to me being 21 and my dad retiring in North Cyprus. I came out for a holiday, sunbathed on the beach, and felt infinitely better. I then went back to London and got instantly worse.

So with the 21st century I can work from home pretty much so I moved out to Cyprus to live with my dad.

Then I started looking into my health and went through all the usual gurus until I found my favourite metrosexual who actually explained things I thought always made instinctive sense.

And now? Now I have a second hand ice cream freezer filled with water, a quantlet, an endless desire to consume seafood if the sun is up, and I watch as many sunrises as is humanly possible.

All in all I think I've done pretty good so far.

Hey [@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) . I am from Cyprus, I was born here. Although there is a good sun here, the environment still sucks due to nnEmf and marine radars. You probably heard of that. I am interested to know why you are asking him to find out about his haplotype and where the latitude of his ancestors is? I did the 23andme test and am waiting for results aswell. Why is it really helpful to know your haplotype regardless of the methylation? And why is it wise to know the latitude of your grandma 10000 years ago?

### JanSz — 2018-05-13T16:02:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yo-from-north-cyprus.19952/post-241113>

> [Antonis said:](/goto/post?id=241103)
>
> Hey [@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) . I am from Cyprus, I was born here. Although there is a good sun here, the environment still suck due to nnEmf and marine radars. You probably heard of that. I am interested to know why you are asking him to find out about his haplotype and where the latitude of his ancestors is? I did the 23andme test and am waiting for results aswell. Why is it really helpful to know your haplotype regardless of the methylation? And why is it wise to know the latitude of your grandma 10000 years ago?

As humans travel from their origins in vicinity of Lake Victoria, their body changed.
It takes 5-10 thousand years to get accommodated to certain latitude (and altitude).
Not long ago most peoples have not travel significant distances. Now we have significant migrations.
Per Jack's teachings everybody would be better of on equator or real near to it, but definitely within tropics.
When trying to thin slice that advice, it should be observed by currently sick people (that are hoping for recovery).
Those that are currently healthy may still fare rather well if they are at or closer to equator relative to their grand grand mother 5-10 tousand years ago.

# Thread: Hello from Myrtle Beach, SC
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-myrtle-beach-sc.19956/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Brad Boyles — 2017-08-04T20:22:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-myrtle-beach-sc.19956/post-220921>

Hello Everyone,

I am excited to have stumbled onto Dr. Kruse. I was tickled to see that there was a forum. I am really looking forward to the opportunity to learn here. I am just really getting a good idea how far this rabbit hole is going to go. Quite deep! I am a slow learner, but I am eager and motivated.

Without going into too much detail about my history, it has been rather challenging. I have for almost all of my life been barely surviving and if it wasn't for the support of my parents, I highly doubt I would even be alive.

So when I read something to the notion of don't just survive, thrive, I am all for that.

So far the behavior modifications I have made: 1. I have dramatically reduced the amount of artificial light that enters my eyeball especially at night and 2. I have gotten my (angle side side) out of bed to see the sunrise with my feet grounded to the earth. I didn't do that today due to inclement weather ' Lighting'.

Thank you, Dr. Kruse, for allowing me to participate in this forum and I optimistically look forward to seeing where this will take me.

b

### JanSz — 2017-08-04T21:44:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-myrtle-beach-sc.19956/post-220935>

If not for my children residing nearby, Myrtle Beach is my favorite location for permanent residence.
I like to vacation in Breaker's Paradise Tower.

..

### caroline — 2017-08-05T03:02:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-myrtle-beach-sc.19956/post-220948>

Hi Brad and welcome. Please start a journal in the optimal journal section and give Dr. Kruse some health history - also your Mom's ad Gran's.

Have you had a 23&me done?

You have made a good start! Do you have blue blockers?

Do you watch Jack's podcasts? especially His Vermont talk a while back? The podcasts are a great and faster/easier way to get Jack's critical info.

Do you have John Ott's book? health and light. It is a quick read

Dr. Kruse is all about the 3 legged stool .....sun, light and magnetism

### Brad Boyles — 2017-08-05T15:12:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-myrtle-beach-sc.19956/post-220969>

Hi JanSz,
Yes, Myrtle Beach isn't a bad place to live. The Breakers is nice. I don't live to from there.

Hi, Caroline,

Thanks! Yes, I will compile a health history. It may take me a while.

No, I haven't done a 23 and me. I need to look into that.

Yes, I have blue blocking glasses that I got on Amazon. I have four different kinds so far for different times of the day. I am learning how to optimally use them. The ones I am wearing in my profile pic, are computer blue blockers which say they block 99 percent of blue light. I am not a savy consumer when it comes to this technology, so I am not sure if that is a true claim. I also got a uvex blue light filter for my phone, but I am not sure how effective it is.

It doesn't really show well, but the reflection on my glasses is Jack's Vermont 2017 talk at the point where Jack has the slide "the monk who sold his Ferrari". I was listening to the talk while I was signing up. Since I needed a human picture, I just took one using my computer. So yes, I love his talks. I have listened to many of them a couple of times.

I just received John Ott's book "Health and Light" yesterday and will begin to read it tomorrow on my day off. I skimmed through it last night before going to bed.

### Brad Boyles — 2017-08-14T23:48:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-myrtle-beach-sc.19956/post-221661>

years ago in around 1999 I was beginning to learn about yoga and I was set on getting a book on chakras. So at Barnes and noble, I was in the eastern philosophy section looking for such a book. I came across a subtle energy book, I thought, opened the book and saw the chapter titled "maxwell's silver hammer".

It just happened by coincidence the Beatles -maxwell's silver hammer on Abbey Road was playing in my car as I was parking my car at Barnes & noble and I still had the song in my head. I ended up getting the book without giving it too much of a review. Later, I wondered why in the french toast did I get that book. I didn't think it was relevant to me.

that book was, of course, Becker's "The Body Electric",

So now I am hearing Becker being mentioned a lot by Dr. Kruse and maybe me getting that book was not some random coincidence. I forgot I had it. I really need to read that thing.

But only after I get a good understanding of the recommended top 5 blogs for beginners. I am just getting my feet wet with the material. I have deferred the John Ott book for a later date.
I must say the webinars are making the process a lot easier.

1\. Leptin R(x)
2\. CT Protocol
3\. Brain gut 11
4\. Cold thermogenesis 7
5\. Energy and epigenetics 4

# Thread: Hello from West Seattle
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-west-seattle.19974/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Bita — 2017-08-07T20:27:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-west-seattle.19974/post-221100>

Hello friends & colleagues!! I am a Naturopathic Medical student studying at Bastyr University in Washington state. Unlike many of my classmates, being a physician has not been the leading motivator in pursuing the topic of medicine for me. In the last 4 years I've become quite curious about & at times obsessed with the foundational truths of life, society, & culture. I see truth as power & the more I can align myself & my loved ones with the foundations of what makes us breathing, feeling, thinking miraculous humans, the more empowered we become. I am so grateful to have been given the opportunity to study the great work of such an innovator as Dr. Kruse. I've never been quick to learn as I have to fully embody what I'm seeking for anything to actually stick. Needles to say that skimming the surfaces of topics & memorizing pathways/terms in a compartmentalized curriculum (my current academic life) does not really work for me. Especially when there is no true source or commonality that ties all of it together.

As of lately I've tried to seek out various mentors from whom I could get some guidance around the many mysteries of life. Ive even studied Shamanic modalities for a year with a well known & talented teacher/Shaman, yet I still am left with confusion around how it is all connected. This is why I'm so excited about the information Dr. Kruse is sharing, as I feel like its slowly bridging many of the gaps in my understanding.

I have two more years of school, yet decades of experience & learning left to go in order to be an effective facilitator in this field. As seen in the archetype of the wounded healer I'm first attempting to utilize the various Dr. Kruse prescriptions to optimize my own health & function. I am so fortunate to be living across the street from the Puget Sound (West Seattle) & have adopted the ritual of bathing in the cool water under the the rays of the morning sun. I am absolutely addicted & am hoping to be motivated enough to carry it through to the colder months of the year as well. Getting my incredibly brilliant & supportive, yet highly skeptical husband to follow suit has been a challenge, as I am still working on my effective & intelligible communication skills around the topic of quantum biology & all its practicalities.

I'm thankful to be a part of this community & am looking forward to learning from all of you through this forum.

In love & light,

\-Bita

### Clayton — 2017-08-07T21:34:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-west-seattle.19974/post-221104>

Awesome to see another ND on here, you will learn a ton about mitochondria here. I practice in BC and even had Jack fly out and lecture at Boucher 2 yrs ago

### Bita — 2017-08-08T00:34:39+0300

Hi Clayton,
Wow... That's great!
I have so much to learn here. Thanks so much for reaching out.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-west-seattle.19974/post-222142>

Hi Clayton!! I just had an idea & I'm curious of your input. I'm currently enrolled in an IV therapy/treatment class & thought of the idea of cooling down the bag to create a similar effect to cooling down the blood flowing through the radial artery as seen in the quantlet. This may be too subtle to have much effect but I read a study in how cold saline was an effective method in reducing fever in febrile children [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3638805/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3638805/)
Any thoughts?

# Thread: Hello from South Carolina
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-south-carolina.19983/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Omelchenko — 2017-08-08T19:46:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-south-carolina.19983/post-221198>

Hello everyone! I'm a long time lurker but finally signed up.

I'm 29 and have recently upended my life trying to chase the optimal life. Fortunately, I work from home so completely changing my surroundings and environment was actually not a tough turn around. As luck would have it free property by way of family inabled me to move from the downtown city out to the country.

It all started with watching some videos on YouTube of Dr. Kruse on various podcasts and watching his lectures as well. Fortunately, I had just finished a molecular biology course and could follow his science well enough to not be completely lost and even appreciate his points and reasoning.

After that I decided to change my life because everything he said just made sense to be, both intrinsically but also scientifically. My lease was up on the house I was renting close to the metro city center so I down sized and moved out to the country. Most of my day is spent out side barefoot and shirtless, with me spending more money than I'd like on seafood lol.

The electricity is pretty much permanently shut off to the majority of the house 99% of the time. I keep the air and the ethernet getting juice but that's about it. I mostly cook outside except whens it raining, in which case the stove is gas. On a day to day bases the only artificial light I really get is if I go to a store. I do work on my computer but its running Iris the entire time except when I need to check site or logo colors.

Wish I could get more morning pre-UV direct sun but the tree block it.

Anyway, glad to be here!

### JanSz — 2017-08-08T21:12:15+0300

Great.
Now you may want to do 23andme test to figure out your haplotype and latitude of your 10000 years ago grandmother.
Compare that latitude to your current one.

About seafood.
before I eat seafood I check my fatty acids.

Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4)

It turns out that many of us already have excessive O3 stores, specially EPA, which in turn mess up or outright suppress the O6 and AA in particular.

Also, when thinking of latitude,
residences closer or at equator correspond with need of less seafood
and closer to poles with more.

...

# Thread: Hello from Colorado Springs!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-colorado-springs.20003/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 35
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Outlier — 2017-08-13T06:24:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-colorado-springs.20003/post-221575>

While I have been following Jack for over 3 years, I have only just joined the forum as well as becoming a klub member. While I have learned so much and experienced success, the fact I am now dealing with sleep apnea and weight gain demonstrates without words that I don't understand enough.

I joined as a member because I was listening to a radio cast of Jack mentioning that in one of his bio hacks, he had given himself sleep apnea and it seemed it wasn't easy to get rid of. I am hoping to find more about that whole situation. In the mean time, I am trying to get my D3 between 60 and 80 (per Stasha Gominak) as well as raise B5. There's only one desire with this - I must have uninterrupted, non hypoxic, healing sleep.

I think my next step is cold thermogenesis. I had done this before and believe it to be the major contributor to turning around my health. However, when I tried it a few weeks or so back, the cold felt like burning. The water temp was only 55 degrees so I suspect I'm far more warm adapted than I was the first time I tried it. I used to easily sink into a tub of 45 to 50 degrees with ice and think it was comfy!!

So with a different set of problems to tackle, I am here to learn, draw the connections I've missed in the past, connect, share experiences and get my life back!!
\----------------------------------------------------
**Age:** 53
**Sex:** F
**Joined JackKruse.com:** 12 Aug 17
**Location:** Colorado Springs, CO USA
**Dx:** Complex Sleep Apnea; AHI 98
**Surgeries:** Gall Bladder removal and Tubal 1987; Tummy Tuck/Inner Thigh Lift 2009; Uterus removal 2013 (still have ovaries and cervix)
**Current Rx:** Lots of supplements (I obviously don't understand water, magnetism and mitochondria)
No prescription meds
Resmed Aircurve ASV with 2 lpm oxygen
**Current Therapies/Protocols:** Leptin Rx | Magnetico 20 gs |Red lights at night/blue light filters | Avoiding sensitive foods **Health WINS:** Previously lost 45lbs on Leptin Rx with cold thermogenesis
**Health Goals:** This sleep apnea MUST go. Sciatica pain must go. The fat must go. I want to practice kung fu again. I want my life back.
**My Optimal Journal:** No link yet; not started

### caroline — 2017-08-13T09:48:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-colorado-springs.20003/post-221580>

Hi and welcome. Please start a journal in the optimal journal section and give Dr. K. your Mom's and Gran's health history too.

Have you done a 23&me test?

Have you read the C/T protocol? Dr. K. tells us to start with Face dunks.

Dr. Kruse talks about red lights ..... are you doing this before bed?

How much outdoor time? grounding?

how much nnEMF is in your environment? work? home?

I would like to call you something more positive.....?

### Outlier — 2017-08-13T23:07:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-colorado-springs.20003/post-221607>

Thanks for the welcome, Caroline! I will be getting my journal going here soon - hopefully today.

I have not done a 23&me test. I wasn't sure what that was and just looked it up. If it'something I absolutely need, I can get it done. However, I am one of those people that refuse to participate in offerings of the insurance companies to provide metrics for discounts because I don't trust what happens to the information. While I know my personal information can be taken without my knowledge, I just prefer not to give it to anyone. My DNA info in particular is part of that information. Having said that though, I just gave away 26 vials of blood for a number of health tests. I will report the results back on my journal when I get them. I have a baseline from around 3 years ago when I first did the Leptin Rx with CT. I have another from June of 2016 and now the tests ordered 3 days ago. I should have results in the next week or two. It does not include salivary cortisol or DHEA, I'll have to do those separate.

I have read the CT protocol and admit by-passing the face dunking "because I used to do this and it shouldn't be that hard to get back to it." Yeah.  I was wrong!! I need to follow the protocol.

Yes, I'm doing the red lights in the evening after sunset when I would normally have regular lights on in the house. I have been photo-sensitive since my teen years and this certainly helps. I was already using lights dim enough that friends complained about me living in the dark and that they couldn't see to move around the house. The red lights are so much less invasive to my eyes. I use Twilight on my cell phone which is a 'follow the sun' type app that increases blue light filter depending on the time of day for your specific location. I have blu-blocker lenses in my glasses for when I use them. I've had lasik and have 20/15 at distance, but need one pair of glasses for intermediate distance (around 2 feet or so) and one pair for close up and reading. I can't do bifocals.

Outdoor time . . . certainly not enough due to sciatic pain. Without pain, I used to walk for at least 1 to 2 hours a day, and then hit the trail on the weekends for 4 hours each day. I can't even walk (or stand) 20 minutes without pain. When I get up in the mornings and take the dog out, we also walk to the OTHER side of the house where I can look into the sun. I've been doing this about 3 weeks or so. Also with that, I have been sitting in the sun for 20 to 30 minutes a day with my bare back exposed to the sun (usually around 11 am, but always between 10 am and 2 pm). Other than running the dog outside for potting breaks and letting him run and play, I don't get outside much. Being a fair-haired blonde, with very fair skin, being photo sensitive and having sciatic pain make for a difficult time for me being outside!! Also, my house is at about 7000 feet so I'm over a mile closer to the sun than my sea level friends. Bright light indeed!

Grounding . . . I could use some tips here. I sleep on a Magnetico. Not sure if that counts for grounding. I hate shoes (I grew up in a very rural area where we only wore shoes during school or going to town. We basically kicked off the shoes at the start of summer vacation and didn't put them back on til fall when school started.) Even now, I don't wear shoes - I'm barefoot all the time. When I'm outside in the back, 90% of the time I'm barefoot on concrete. The other 10%, I'm barefoot on rocks. I have natural prairie grass in my back yard which translates to others as 'lots of weeds'. In particular, many have burrs or other sharp edges so I don't go barefoot in that! There's nothing else I do that I can think of that may count towards grounding so we may have some wide opportunity for change here.

I'm not sure about nnEMF around me. I know there are 34 tower structures within a 4 mile radius and 144 antenna. I do plan on getting a trimeter and RF meter for Wifi and such here soon. I recognize that Jack recommends a professional evaluation. I will have to look into that when I can better afford it. I work from home so I do need to be aware of what is around me because it's constant. I have lived in this home for 17 years. However, I absolutely suspect something environmental has changed and is exerting a negative influence over my health. It may be some apartments built nearby. I have no baseline for comparison, so I will have to see what I can find. This is certainly a major area of concern and motivation behind becoming a Gold member. I AM considering moving, but then, I have no idea how to ensure that any different place is better.

You are welcome to call me Lisa as that is my name, but please don't think the nickname Outlier is a negative thing!! Of all the archetypes that I identify with, I am a Seeker at heart. I want to know the truth of a thing more than I want words of comfort or security. This set me apart from others at an early age and was my first hint of being an outlier. I have found through my life that I fit very few bell curves. Insurance companies provide medical treatment based on bell curves. I recall going to the doctor with a slight fever - about 99.5 degrees and was told statistically, I did NOT in fact have a fever nor could he document that in my record. I pointed out that my basal body temp is 97 degrees and this represents a 2.5 degree rise in temperature so FOR ME, this was a fever. While he acknowledged it, he said he still couldn't write it as such. Medical advice based on averages and bell curves has not helped me. I am on this site because I AM an outlier. I need help beyond the ordinary advice. I associate 'outlier' with thinking outside the box and having the courage and willingness to discover the truths that are there. I celebrate being an outlier because it represents freedom from everything average (or expected). Jack himself is an outlier and his teachings are outside the mainstream and backed by science that others cannot explain away no matter how much they don't want to know the truth. I'm proud to be an outlier and I think everyone that has joined this community has a measure of it them!!

### Outlier — 2017-08-14T07:36:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-colorado-springs.20003/post-221630>

It is possible for me to work outside. Having said that, Colorado Springs IS the land of crazy, severe weather. I am a project manager and spend most of my days on conference calls and on the computer. In planning to work outdoors, what are the goals? Do I want to spend more time in the sun? Is the goal to be grounded to the Earth? Being in the direct sun can make seeing a monitor difficult. However, I \*\* can \*\* pace around the yard like a caged lion while being on my calls. This is a habit I formed from my days of being in a little cube. What kind of footwear would be appropriate? Or, if I were to make a footpath, what could I use for it? Mulch? It's interesting that you asked me about this - as soon as my back eas healthy enough to support the activity, I was planning to practice my kung fu forms outside while taking my conference calls. Many of the forms, and anything chi kung in particular, teach grounding methods. While these days people think of that type of grounding as a person being hard to move, I'm certain its origins were to literally ground with and to the Earth.

I am generally outside in the mornings for just a short time for the specific purpose of looking towards the sun. But not grounded and not naked! Also, not exactly at sunrise. A question if you can assist? For at least the past year and maybe 3 years in total, I have not gotten healing sleep due to my apnea. I have only been on bipap then asv treatment a total of 3 months. I usually try to sleep as long as possible because for the past few years, that sleep has been deeply hypoxic. In one of my sleep studies, my oxygen sats were documented in the 60s. I personally have observed it in the 50s. Hours of nighttime hypoxia at these levels kill brain, heart, lung, kidney, liver cells, etc. Of my 3 months of treatment, I've only had the supplemental oxygen for the past 6 weeks. So my question is, where should my current priority lie? Should I short sleep to be up at sunrise because light is simply THAT important? Or should I recover more healing sleep and look at the sun when I get up? And just so you know, I usually get up between 5:30 and 6:30am on weekdays and sleep til 8, 9 or 10am on weekends.

I do not not have a kiniki swimsuit, but I think I need that adorable wrap!! I will be getting one soon!!

It's funny you asked about the Vermont talk. Earlier today, I was wondering what to listen to and settled for the current Aug 2017 webinar. The first thing I heard Jack say was now that people have had time to digest the Vermont discussion . . . so I stopped and looked up the Vermont talk. Yes, I listened to it in its entirety tonight but will have to listen several times to get everything.

I did walk away with the importance of light. However, I don't have enough understanding to answer my question above. Is light my priority or is healing sleep. I know I need both and at some point the answer will be that I need sunrise light more than sleep. But is that time now? What do I base that kind of decision on?

I also heard that if I get the 23&me testing done, it's possible Dr. K can advise me if I can heal in my current zipcode. If I get that testing done, do I need the $99 test or the $199 test?

Lastly, I've only read parts of the thread on why UV feels different in the morning - I will go back and read more.

In the Vermont talk, one of the points made was that we are made to be addicted to the sun. From my earliest recollections, the sun was never my favorite place to be. My mother always said I was 'allergic' to the sun. No doubt by my teen years, I avidly avoided the sun. I also begin to sleep excessively. Even now when I sit in the sun for 20 minutes, I can't wait to get out of it. Exercise has never brought any pleasure either. Suggests a dopamine issue, yeah? But why hasn't the light changed it?

### Outlier — 2017-08-14T22:25:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-colorado-springs.20003/post-221657>

I think eventually I will need to do a consult with Jack. Given how crazy busy he is, I was hoping to anticipate as many of his questions as possible and have information he needs at hand. At minimum, I'd like to get my labs back that I just had taken on Aug 10th. My guess is some things will be in this week but the whole set won't be until next week.

I had started the leptin reset, but given odd cravings, I can't say I'm doing a good job managing carbs. To be specific, I crave salt. I have coarse ground celtic sea salt and will eat the crystals straight. My favorite thing to eat is pecans, dried cranberries, 90% cocoa dark chocolate and lots of salt!! I often make a meal out of it. It suggests to me I have water balance issues. I don't know if it is outright salt, potassium or magnesium. This is concerning to me because it's a hugh part of the water/light/magnetism picture. I clearly see the symptom but don't know the appropriate path to follow.

So what changed? Wow, Caroline!! That's what I have been asking myself over and over. I have been a project manager or working in IT for aome 25 years now. Life on the phone and computer has been part of that picture for the whole time. I tend to point to 2 baselines. In 2009, I had cosmetic surgery. They told me that due to the anesthesia, they would be able to tell as I was coming out of it whether I had sleep apnea. At the time, I did not. In July 2013, I did the leptin reset with CT and lost 45 lbs with zero effort. I looked good and felt good. In fact, my avatar pic is from that time - so you can judge for yourself! I was still overweight but very pleased nonetheless. My HSCRP was 0.4 and I was also pleased with that. Because I felt so good, I started taking kung fu classes that Nov. In Feb, my sleep hygiene failed entirely. I met a friend and started hanging out. Their environment was not good for me. First, he lived in an apartment building. Second, he left his TV on 24x7. I was having a great time playing video games with him and next thing I knew, it'd be 2am and I had to get up at 5:30. So I would sleep at his place but he only had a one bedroom apartment with no couch. So I slept in his room. I could not sleep with the noise and light of the TV. Not to mention he DID have sleep apnea and his breathing and waking kept me up as well. This persisted from Feb 2014 til Jun 2014 when he moved in my house as my roommate. At least then I was able to sleep in my room and he in his. However, he continually woke me before I completed restful sleep and he kept my CNS stimulated CONSTANTLY. (Keep in mind he was my kung fu buddy so think Pink Panther and Kato. We were always sparring and surprising each other with attacks.) This guy LIVED for TV and even though I don't have a TV on my main level, he watched constantly (into the evening) on his laptop. I ended up watching it too. In Feb of 2014, the sciatic pain started and it's never stopped. I had to stop practicing kung fu by Aug 2014 because of intense pain. At that point, I was not able to go for walks and pretty much was never outside because I felt so miserable. By March 2016, I had over 6 months of excessive daytime sleepiness. In May 2016, my doctor tried thyroid supplements. I gained 10lbs in the first week and 50 over the course of the summer. He sent me to another doctor who suspected narcolepsy (because of presence of cataplexy). He sent me to a sleep study. This is where I got the news of complex sleep apnea with an AHI of 98. I never made it to the daytime MSLT because of that.

So when you ask about what changed, I suspect I gave myself sleep apnea when I started hanging out with my friend and disrupted all of my sleep. My guess is that the sciatic pain came on as I developed night time hypoxia since lack of oxygen causes issues in nerve signaling.

My friend moved out at the end of April (2017) and I began sleep apnea treatment in May.

Does that give a good summary of what's different? I need to add that as part of my journal - I think all those 'lifestyle' changes had a huge impact. We can absolutely quantify a disconnect from light over the past 3 years. If I understood enough of the Vermont discussion, this alone could be the issue. If I truly have narcolepsy, then I additionally have an orexin issue.

Hey welcome aboard, I am kinda new here myself. I believe all you need is the $99 test. What's great about that is you can download all the raw data and run it through other websites such as geneticgenie.com or promethease.com for basically a small donation of $5 and youll get extensive info on your DNA, genes, and genetic mutations.

It's funny that you say that being in the sun was never your favorite place and that you avoided the sun, and you began to sleep excessively. I have had that same issue majority of my lifetime. The sun takes a lot out of me. When I would be up early morning and stay inside, I am Ok, but if I'd walk out to see the sunrise, I'd get nauseous most of the time and I wouldn't feel well and I'd have to go back to bed. I never could figure that out. That's why I never like mornings, even as a kid. I never felt good in the morning sun, that's why I would sleep as late as I could, and I would stay up late night as well. People accused me of being a vampire at times.

Only recently, I discovered through evaluating my patients through Autonomic Response Testing, that many of them had a stress reaction so severe to exposure of the Sun, Moon, Magnetism (component parts of the photoelectric effect), that it caused their Autonomic Nervous System to push them into a Sympathetic (fight or flight) stress mode, which is not a good thing. Just to be certain, I had two other ART practitioners, including one top practitioner / instructor in Philly and another from Atlanta test their patients and they received the same results as well.

I am estimating that because of the patients sympathetic stress response that they are only receiving approx. 20% of the benefits of the photoelectric effect. Remember, 2/3 of your net negative electron charge should come from the Photoelectric Effect. I tested myself and of course I had the same issue. I have a feeling that this problem is very widespread and maybe a big contributor to a lot of health issues that people are having. Fortunately there is a way to clear this sensitivity.

Remember, 2/3 of your net negative electron charge should come from the Photoelectric Effect. I tested myself and of course I had the same issue. I have a feeling that this problem is very widespread and maybe a big contributor to a lot of health issues that people are having. Fortunately there is a way to clear this sensitivity.

Robert, thanks for the great information and sharing your experiences in the sun. Certainly in my baby and preschool years when I had zero self control, if I got too hot from the sun, I turned into a little monster. No feel good hormones for baby Me at all!! It was like someone flipped a switch and I became quite hostile and down right mean and nasty. Fortunately, self control tempers that, but I honestly am still subject to that today. I grew up in Virginia where it is both hot and humid and that combination was bad for me. When my sister hit her teens, she suddenly started sleeping until 1 or 2 in the afternoon during the summers. It was hard because we used to go to the local swimming pool all day (I may not be much for the sun, but I've always been a Water Baby!) However, when I hit my teens, the same thing happened. Suddenly, normalcy was staying up until 2am and sleeping til noon or later. Even today, if work was not a consideration, I will slowly adjust back to going to bed at 2am and getting up at 10am. The only place I ever fit societal norms was in Hawaii. That's because 10pm there is 2am here and that's my 'real' bed time. And when it's my wake up time of 10am?? It's 6am there. So I went to bed and got up normally while there.

Being on Leptin Rx feels foreign. I try to get up at sunrise but I'm not rested. I look at the sun and it seemingly does nothing. I sit in the mid day sun and I burn and can't wait to get back inside.

So at a high level, I get the ART that you're doing. You said the issue is easy to clear but didnt say what it was. I'm assuming you wanted me to ask outright or Jack's protocols address it and it will 'self resolve' for lack of a better term. Do you care to elaborate?

It's funny you ask what I was eating during that period!! When I first met my friend, I was Paleo/Leptin Rx. Having said that, I did not understand the difference between dietary ketosis and Jack's definition of ketosis. But generally a ton of salmon and cod with lots of coconut oil. However, my friend eats nothing but garbage. I'm talking worse than SAD here. The boy simply will not eat fresh food. It MUST be processed. Having him in my house certainly caused me to shift back towards SAD like eating. For example, he wouldn't eat the BEAUTIFUL, wild caught salmon I bought - it had icky skin and soft bones. So I then went to a wild caught salmon with no skin or bone - read 'processed' and he would eat it. However once my weight went insane on the thyroid meds, nothing in my body worked right. Id eat a quart of ice cream and LOSE 5 lbs. Only to eat a salad and gain 5 back. It made no sense and no matter what, I was on an upward trajectory. So I decided if I had to be fat and ugly, I was going to enjoy it!!! So I ate mostly sugar in the form of ice cream.

So let's take a quick look at what I think was happening. At the time, I was severely hypoxic at night. As in 50 to 60% saturation. This of course, killed my VO2 Max (and I haven't recovered that). So night times meant extended hypoxia with zero healing sleep. Daytime meant inefficient oxygen utilization. What does the biggest enery hog in my body need (meaning my brain)? It needs energy and lots of it. Without oxygen, where was that energy coming from? Glucose. Almost a pure and constant supply. I didn't think about what I was eating. I just reached for sugar every time my brain called for it. And thanks to my friend, after nearly 3 years of gluten free, I added that back too. Like I said, if I had to be fat and ugly, I was gonna enjoy the ride for whatever I could.

The only thing that has caused a partial weight loss was ditching all the foods I'm sensitive to. I lost 20lbs without effort doing that. But right now, I'm in a complete homostasis and one look at me and you don't need labs . . . you will immediately conclude I'm leptin resistant!!

Unless our fine community has suggestions on how to make oysters yummy, I don't know if I can do that. Wild caught salmon and cod are my staples and a preference if given one.

I have to say, eating 50g of protein first thing in the morning is HARD. I'm not hungry and it's the LAST thing I wanna do!! I dont really wake up or want to eat til around 10am. I can't imagine trying to eat 50g of protein worth of oysters first thing in the morning!!

So I'm on track with you in finding and eliminating the nnEMF. I don't have to eat anything icky for that!!! I will reach out to Scompy and Drezy for some advice on RF meters!!

In the meantime, I'm looking towards the sunrise in the mornings, getting 20 min of mid day sun, eating as clean as I can (given weirdo salt cravings) and spring water is literally the only thing I drink. No coffee, tea or anything else.

### Outlier — 2017-08-15T22:57:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-colorado-springs.20003/post-221720>

I share mold allergy/sensitivity with you as well. Virginia is a very wet place and mold abounds. It wasn't until I moved to Colorado that I breathed through my nose for the first time. Here, it gets hot, but humidity is usually very low.

So now you've got me really curious. I have not done ART or muscle testing at all. However, the lastest book I've purchased is about identifing food allergies via muscle testing. The author's premise is that food sensitivities, identified via muscle testing, exist due to nutritional deficiencies. The whole idea was intriguing, so I bought the book.

But, that book was along similar lines of something I was already doing. Specifically, back in 1956, Arthur F. Coca wrote a book declaring he had the solution for obesity, diabetes, high blood pressure etc. His premise is that when you eleminate foods you are sensitive to, you recover health. (He didn't know Jack to get the bigger picture!!) His methodology for measuring sensitivity was pulse rate. I eleminated a lot of foods from my diet in this way. Some foods cause my heart rate to decrease. Some cause an immediate spike ot 20 bpm.

So what you've got me wondering is, if I take my pulse when I go in the sun, will I see a significant jump in heart rate? According to Coca, a jump of 5bpm is OK. Anything more is not. Please realize, I'm telling you the condensed version not his whole protocol.

I will follow up with you tomorrow mid day for me and let you know what I find!! We're just about out of optimal range for the sun and about to get afternoon showers.

### Robert Marking — 2017-08-15T23:44:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-colorado-springs.20003/post-221726>

Regarding muscle testing at home, You could try this,

first thing in the morning, stand tall put your out arm out at a 90° angle in front of you, so its horizontal with a floor, and have someone gently push down on your wrist to check your strength levels in that arm, repeat that for a five times just to get a gauge of how strong your arm is. The person pushing down on your wrist should try to be consistent in his pressure as you are being consistent in your resistance.

Now you should take your free hand and totally cover your ear, pressing the ear inward with the palm of your hand.

Now With one hand over your ear and with one arm out , have the person press on your wrist again while doing this.

1) If your arm goes weak that you are and parasympathetic mode and you are testable at this moment.

2) If your arm stays the same strength as it was before you put your hand over the ear , Then that means that some type of hidden stressor has pushed you into sympathetic mode , and your autonomic nervous system is not testable with muscle testing.

However If your arm goes weak with your hand over your ear, that means you are testable.

So here's what you do next.

Well still indoors, Take your hand away from your ear, and have the person push down in your wrist to check your strength levels again, you should be strong once again.

then walk outside in the direct sunlight and repeat the test. If you're strong muscle goes weak in the sun, that means that the how do I not make nervous system is having a stress response (sensitivity.)

If you're strong muscle stay strong, or possibly even goes stronger, That may mean that you are having a strong sympathetic reaction to the sunlight, the adrenals are kicking in adrenaline, and youve moved into fight or flight mode,

To see if this is the case , While still standing in the sun, , with your arm out to be tested, you put your hand over your ear one more time ,

1) if your arm stays strong , that means your autonomic nervous system is highly stressed and has moved into sympathetic dominance fight or flight mode , Which is more of an allergic reaction than a sensitivity.

2) your arm goes week, and that means that you are in parasympathetic mode and the sun is not a stressor

I know it may be a little confusing but this is a great way to tell if something is a stressor or not, and to what degree it is a stressor whether it's a sensitivity or more of an allergy.

### Karlos Amigos — 2017-08-16T04:19:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-colorado-springs.20003/post-221736>

> [Outlier said:](/goto/post?id=221713)
>
> It's funny you ask what I was eating during that period!! When I first met my friend, I was Paleo/Leptin Rx. Having said that, I did not understand the difference between dietary ketosis and Jack's definition of ketosis. But generally a ton of salmon and cod with lots of coconut oil. However, my friend eats nothing but garbage. I'm talking worse than SAD here. The boy simply will not eat fresh food. It MUST be processed. Having him in my house certainly caused me to shift back towards SAD like eating. For example, he wouldn't eat the BEAUTIFUL, wild caught salmon I bought - it had icky skin and soft bones. So I then went to a wild caught salmon with no skin or bone - read 'processed' and he would eat it. However once my weight went insane on the thyroid meds, nothing in my body worked right. Id eat a quart of ice cream and LOSE 5 lbs. Only to eat a salad and gain 5 back. It made no sense and no matter what, I was on an upward trajectory. So I decided if I had to be fat and ugly, I was going to enjoy it!!! So I ate mostly sugar in the form of ice cream.
>
> So let's take a quick look at what I think was happening. At the time, I was severely hypoxic at night. As in 50 to 60% saturation. This of course, killed my VO2 Max (and I haven't recovered that). So night times meant extended hypoxia with zero healing sleep. Daytime meant inefficient oxygen utilization. What does the biggest enery hog in my body need (meaning my brain)? It needs energy and lots of it. Without oxygen, where was that energy coming from? Glucose. Almost a pure and constant supply. I didn't think about what I was eating. I just reached for sugar every time my brain called for it. And thanks to my friend, after nearly 3 years of gluten free, I added that back too. Like I said, if I had to be fat and ugly, I was gonna enjoy the ride for whatever I could.
>
> The only thing that has caused a partial weight loss was ditching all the foods I'm sensitive to. I lost 20lbs without effort doing that. But right now, I'm in a complete homostasis and one look at me and you don't need labs . . . you will immediately conclude I'm leptin resistant!!
>
> Unless our fine community has suggestions on how to make oysters yummy, I don't know if I can do that. Wild caught salmon and cod are my staples and a preference if given one.
>
> I have to say, eating 50g of protein first thing in the morning is HARD. I'm not hungry and it's the LAST thing I wanna do!! I dont really wake up or want to eat til around 10am. I can't imagine trying to eat 50g of protein worth of oysters first thing in the morning!!
>
> So I'm on track with you in finding and eliminating the nnEMF. I don't have to eat anything icky for that!!! I will reach out to Scompy and Drezy for some advice on RF meters!!
>
> In the meantime, I'm looking towards the sunrise in the mornings, getting 20 min of mid day sun, eating as clean as I can (given weirdo salt cravings) and spring water is literally the only thing I drink. No coffee, tea or anything else.

If you get a buzz from any food, that could be a result of the body having an immune response to that food, The autonomic nervous system sees this stress and it moves into a sympathetic fight or flight mode, Causing an adrenaline rush/buz.

The bigger buzz that you get from a food , means The stronger immune response that you'll have to the food, The more stress and inflammation that food Is causing, and more adrenaline is released while in sympathetic dominance mode to fight that food .

So typically whatever foods you crave the most, or the foods that you have the hardest time getting up, or the food that you love the most, are the foods that are causing the most amount of inflammation and stress in the body.

You could do the Dr. Coca test before and after eating any food to see if you have a sensitivity or allergy to it .

Typically it's difficult for people to consciously tell the difference between a sympathetic state and a para sympathetic "yang"state . That's why measuring HRV , LF, HF, LF/HF. and rMMSD With a heart rate monitor is so valuable.

It's really all about what your immune system and white blood cells have to say about the food you eating. There could be an immune response of some type on a very small scale causing inflammation and stress, but consciuosly you wouldn't feel much difference. But that doesn't mean the inflammation is it leading to some type of chronic illness.

Usually when people say "wow that food makes me feel good " or " I like the way I feel when I have that food" or "that's my comfort food" many times it's because of the slight adrenaline release from the ANS kicking into the fight or flight sympathetic mode.

The hardest food for people to give up is typically the food thats causing the most amount of inflammation, because of this euphoric adrenaline release, much like when you get off a roller coaster ride at a theme park.

Here's the link for an overview of Coca's Pulse Test for detecting hidden food allergies and sensitivities.

Allergic Tension is the body's reaction to food allergy. Headache , fatigue , bloating, and also a rise in heart rate from siting to standing. (It should be mentioned that a drop in blood pressure from sitting to standing is a sign of adrenal fatigue. )

The reason why a person cannot be muscle tested accurately in sympathetic mode is because in this state , the stressor (in this case food sensitivities, ) is causing The cells to emit incoherent light signaling , causing chaos throughout the biophoton feild, , which is kind of like your computer freezing up and you're not able to load and unload programs.

If a patient is tested while in sympathetic mode, the feedback from the autonomic nervous system is not going to be accurate and you'll receive a lot of false positive and false negatives.

This is why applied kinesiology has a lot of accuracy issues and problems in comparison with autonomic response testing that takes this into account.

# Thread: Hello from Belgium

### Stijn — 2017-08-15T16:13:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-belgium.20012/post-221696>

Hi all,

Pleased to be here and meet you all.

First up, how do I get my profile to show Gold Member, as I've seen here for others. And how do I get in the Gold Member facebook group?

About me, here's a short list:

**Age:** 30 **Sex:** M **Joined JackKruse.com:** June 2017

**Location:** Ghent, Belgium (= around latitude 50N)
**Dx:** Myopia, astigmatism

**Surgeries:** None

**Current Rx:** None

**Current Therapies / Protocols:** Uvex blue blocking glasses, wearing long clothes when under artificial light, exposing as much skin as possible outside, cold showers, sweat lodge with IR & UV-A lamps, keto/epi-paleo/IF combo diet, movement, standing desk, music

**Health WINS:** blue blockers, leaving lights off or unscrewing them, in areas that need more light (like bathroom and kitchen) I have incandescent lights, Faraday cage for bed

**Health Goals:** Testing a Schumann generator, testing EMF at home, be consistent in AM sunlight, be consistent in cold thermogenesis (in either tub or lake), save up for a Magnetico, be consistent in diet and movement choices, live and work away from the dangers

**My Optimal Journal:** [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/stijns-optimal-journal.20013/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/stijns-optimal-journal.20013/)

I am quite fortunate in the health front. Haven't even broken a bone in my body. I have been geeking out on this for 6+ years. Curiousity, uncommon questions and answers, controversial beliefs have kept me in good shape. I work as a technical writer. This means: two computer screens, computers, wifi, a cell tower on the premises, commute on the train... 8.5h at work + 2h commute. For now it's 5/7 days. Should move to 4/7 days later in the year. Bad bad bad. I'm quite serious when it comes to health, abundance and relationships. But that's a story for another day. I'm not big fan of fora but will see how this goes.

Be well.

# Thread: Hello from London, Ontario Canada

**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Steph Roch — 2017-08-19T23:21:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-london-ontario-canada.20034/post-221945>

Hello everyone. I am so grateful to be on this amazing journey of discovery and health. I have learned a lot of very valuable information and I am committing myself to learning and improving each and every day. I take this science very seriously and I am going to be treating this material as if I were studying for my PhD in mitochondrial biophysics. I do not have a university level science background (although I did earn very good marks in biology and chemistry in high school). I feel as though I can wrap my mind around all of this as long as I am diligent and ask many questions.

I am in this 100%. I have made many changes so far that have been very drastic (I pulled my three kids from school and am homeschooling them, I have eliminated the use of lights at night with the exception of candlelight and a UV flashlight, I have taken the wifi out of my home, I hired an EMF specialist to come in to measure nnEMF and I turn the breakers off at night). In addition to these changes, I only take cold showers, I use my Quantlet daily, I wear blue blockers whenever on a computer or looking at my cell, I get outside every day in the early morning sun grounded, as well as the afternoon sun and I quite often watch the sun set. I am working on other changes as well, including purchasing leather soled shoes, eventually getting a Magnetico and changing my eyeglasses to blue blocking lenses (I currently use Spektrum glasses over my regular eyeglasses if looking at blue light).

Diet: I eat quite a high fat diet just but just recently started incorporating oysters at least 3-4 times a week, salmon almost daily, sardines, scallops, lobster, and shrimp. I eat grass fed beef and some local chicken and produce from my garden only, with the exception of avocados. I try to limit fruit but occasionally have a bite of a local peach or some blueberries (in season).

A couple of areas that I am struggling with are where to purchase prescription glasses (in my area) that block out the appropriate spectrum of blue light (my local lab said that they could only provide lenses that block out up to 420nm, their version of a blue blocking lens). I am also looking at the BPI tint and what would be most appropriate.

I am also trying to figure out if I should be including more UV light at night, which may be helpful for seeing once the days get much shorter in the winter or whether I should be including more red light and exactly what bulbs to purchase).

Another area that I am very curious about is whether or not I should try a zero carb diet (meaning only seafood or other meat) and limiting all produce in the winter months.

If anyone else lives at around the 43rd latitude, are there any other hacks that I should be doing?

Any advice would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks,
Stephanie


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Hello from a new member in the U.K.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-a-new-member-in-the-u-k.20115/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matt Wales — 2017-09-08T12:23:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-a-new-member-in-the-u-k.20115/post-223154>

Hello Everybody,
First off, thanks to all of you for sharing your wisdom (especially Jack),
About me.
I'm a 55 year old retired London Policeman.
I now occasionally work as a film 'extra'.

For as long as I can remember I've been involved in activities that I believed would enhance/maintain the health of my body:

-   Calisthnetics, swimming and walking as a child
-   Weight training, boxing, martial arts as a young man
-   Pilates, Yoga, Qi Gong and Tai Chi in the last 10 years

What I find rather ironic is that focusing on these practices were far less effective than I believed because at the time I was unaware of;

-   The need for sunlight and
-   The dangers of Non Native EMF

Luckily I was 'tuned in' to my spiritual side by Yoga and the teachings of Eckhart Tolle, Adyashanti and Byron Katie.
It may be that this started to raise my dopamine levels, but in any case at the beginning of last year I was graced with my 'come to Jesus moment' as Jack calls it.
Unfortunately without this 'awakening' I don't believe a lot of people can see the truth of our predicament. They believe the conditioned beliefs that have become firmly established in them are the only truth.

Health wise I've been lucky. Up until 2003 I was 'on the beat' as we call it in the U.K.
Out on foot patrol and in low tech cars.
When I was promoted to Sergeant in 2003 that all changed:

-   I was in front of the computer a lot.

-   At the same time computers took over policing.

-   New digital radios replaced our more effective analogue radios.

I've had mild, constant headaches since then.
In the last few years I've suffered from insomnia.
I'm now very pro-active about improving my environment:

-   I got rid of WiFi at home
-   I got a body voltage meter. I now turn off all power in the house at night if I'm on my own in the house or I sleep in the conservatory with the power there turned off when my wife or daughters are at home (I've got to get 'sleep adapted' before winter comes!)
-   Taken up open water swimming
-   Wearing Blue light blocking glasses when indoors or looking at computers or TVs (I use [www.reglazeglasses4u.co.uk](http://www.reglazeglasses4u.co.uk/) for my prescription lenses)
-   I'll be ordering a gausmeter and RF meter soon.
-   I'm considering a portable 'Blushield' device (as recommended by EMF Warriors) when I can't control my environment. Anyone got any opinion on these devices?

So I'm going to continue to educate myself on how to remain in good health thanks to all of this wonderful knowledge so generously being made available.
Good health to all of you,
Matt.

### Sue-UK — 2017-09-08T12:45:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-a-new-member-in-the-u-k.20115/post-223156>

Welcome to the forum Matt

### Frederica — 2017-09-09T00:48:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-a-new-member-in-the-u-k.20115/post-223212>

Welcome Matt, I've been wondering about the different shields for emf that you see all over online. Something is stopping me though. Would be interested to hear from others on this.

# Thread: Hi from Eastern Washington
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-eastern-washington.20118/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Frederica — 2017-09-09T00:34:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-eastern-washington.20118/post-223206>

Finally, I became a member!! I have lurked around this site for years. I don't understand most of the science but the bottom line of light, magnetism and water are so common sense you just can't ignore it!

Thank you so much for the emails coming every few days. It's amazing how overwhelmed I can feel with all the information to be absorbed and reabsorbed and then the comments. I think I spent 3 hours one day reading the comments on the Paleo Rx thread and still didn't finish because someone will throw in a link to an article that you need to read and the tabs on my computer are about 1/8 " each, filling the whole screen: ) Anyway, I have loved all the blogs that you linked to in those emails. They are definitely my level.

I am beginning to see what led me to my current poor health. Lack of sunlight and nnEMF. We used to live in Boulder, CO and though it is a higher latitude in has elevation going for it! And, all that lovely snow and sunshine in the winter. You just can't help but get out into it and play. Such a beautiful place for being in nature. Also, while we lived there (15 years ago) it was not so crowded and cell phones were just starting to come on the scene. Then, we moved ... to Eastern Washington, which is nothing like Western Washington. We are high desert with about 3 inches of precipitation yearly. We get intense sun May - September, but December - March you don't ever see the sun. We stayed in a small cottage on property while we built a house and the main breaker box, modem, router and anything else electrical was in the Master Bedroom at the foot of our bed. That's when my insomnia started - 2010. I didn't know any better and we have continued to collect all the tech that Americans go for.

It has only gotten worse in the years ensuing with other complaints coming up. The major one was Triple Negative Breast cancer in 2016. But, I had been reading some of this site by then and started to listen to what my body was telling me. I had a lumpectomy but no chemo or radiation - it made me physically ill to even think about putting my body through that. I received a lot of flack with that decision and had my oncologist tell me He wouldn't continue seeing me. Well, I found a great Oncologist who sees me and does blood tests every six months and 18 months later I show no signs of cancer.

I still have insomnia, which I will probably do just about anything to cure (medicine has no cure for insomnia just drugs). I have begun the nnEMF purge, the blue light blocking, the sun worshipping (never had a problem with that one as long as it was there), and the diet. Even done a few icy face plunges (which my husband calls waterboarding). I would take a loan out on my house to buy what I need, vacation where I should or even move. I just need to know what areas I need to focus on and that last blog by Gretchen Bronson gave me a roadmap!

That was a lot to say but once I got rolling it just kept coming. Bottom line - I am so thankful to find a group of folks that want to heal their bodies according to our Body's Instruction Book and Dr. Kruse for writing it!

PS I am rather new to this forum posting and such so I might make some mistakes on how I do it. Please tell me when I'm wrong!

### fitness@home — 2017-09-09T03:37:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-eastern-washington.20118/post-223218>

> Even done a few icy face plunges (which my husband calls waterboarding).

This made me laugh out loud. My husband said similar things but in the end, he built me a mighty fine CT tub

Good luck on your journey.

### Frederica — 2017-09-09T04:21:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-eastern-washington.20118/post-223219>

Hi Dr. Kruse,

I have been reading your blogs since 2012 and i want to say you have done a fantastic job, very enlightening to my health so far. I am a 56 years old black female who was diagnosed with a Stroke and Lupus at the age of 14. Back in 2012 I did the Leptin Rx and CT (Tub 50 -55%) not for a long time and I felt good, stronger could pick myself up of the floor, bathtub with no help. I have been to the doctors twice a year every year but I never took blood test although I have been written many Rx. Back in 2015 I change my Doctor and decided to test for the Lupus, It came back negative, I was not surprise given how good I feel . My Doctor said to me "I though you said you had Lupus" looking at me as if I was lying to her, but if she really needs to see the prof she can get it from my previous Doctors; she keeps on testing for Lupus though. I am restarting the CT and already started the Leptin Rx + Cold Showers (Florida Tap water 77%) LOL for the last Month now. Now I know what Leptin Rx + CT can do I am adding Sunlight + Blue-blockers to the regimen lets see where that takes me. I have an appointment in a few weeks are there any blood test I can do to help me through this other than telomeres. Thank you again DR. Kruse, MUCH BLESSINGS. Sunshinegirl
