Bigknife7, I had SLE (systemic lupus erythematosus) disease of the skin, heart, lungs, kidneys, joints, and nervous system, not Discoid Lupus (limited to the skin) I ate Organic foods only and when I came upon Dr. Kruse's web site I did his Leptin protocol and CT Brrrrrrrrrrrr. (It made sense to me). I did not test for Lupus for some years and when I tested again in 2014 My Lupus Anticoagulant Panel came up 0.0 Normal. But I have been told by my DR's twice before that I was in remission, Once at the age of 16 and again at 25 but I did not put two and two together, but when I understood what I put into my body is what is hurting me I paid a lot more attention. Hi All,
I am so happy to have found all this wonderful information! My partner and I have worked in natural health in the UK for nearly 30years running a shop full of natural foods and remedies. We are passionate about helping people to take control of their health and have spent so much time researching around the way we live in this world. We discovered Weston A Price in 2002 and have been filling our shop with really good traditional foods ever since. Listening to Dr Kruse has joined so many of the dots for us. Things we heard years ago are all tying together. Why Michael Crawford's work is so important, why we felt so good from sun gazing, but let it slip because we didn't really get its implications, and more recently learning so much about our mitochondria.
We are feeling so inspired. Thank you!

# Thread: Hey all!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-all.20254/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Eric Waldron — 2017-10-09T00:26:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-all.20254/post-225323>

Hello everyone,
I found out about Jack Kruse through Adam Lowrey's podcast about a year or so ago (thank goodness for that), and have been putting some of his protocol into application slowly but surely ever since.
Finding out about inheriting our mitochondria from our Mom's side left me awestruck two months ago when I heard that for the first time. The reason being is because my Mom has suffered from MS since she was 23 (about two years after giving birth to me) and has been declining ever since. My Grandma (Mom's mom) has had her fair share of problematic health issues. I'm currently working in a bank under a lot of LED lighting, and, of course, looking at a laptop screen without any sort of protection/blue blockers what so ever. I get out in the sun on my lunch breaks and earth with my shirt and tie off, which helps, but certainly doesn't feel like enough. I also sun gaze in the morning and have implemented the keto diet to the fullest within the past two or so months. My wife is Peruvian and we already purchased flight tickets to stay in Peru during the holidays through Februrary, before coming back to then move to Arizona for lots of sunshine all year around. This decision was greatly propelled by the work Doc JK has put forth. We don't have all of the money in the world, but we have been saving our pennies for a while now in order to make this move happen. We will be getting lots of sun in Peru, even heading up to the Amazon during our two month stint. I was born and raised in the suburbs of Philadelphia and I'm currently here. I lived over four years in South America with sun everyday of the year for the most part. Last winter was my first one back in Philly, meaning a season without sun. And boy did I feel the effects of it, but didn't know why until really digging further into Jack's work.

I'm not sure as to what else to share here. I get lots of water in throughout the day, lots of sleep, blue blockers at night, trying to limit cpu time, keto diet with lots of seafood (more in a week than I would have eaten before in months), lots of sun and earthing on my off days and in the mornings/lunch breaks, etc. My next year plans are to move to AZ where it's nice and sunny and look for work that aligns with getting away from artificial lighting for the most part. Getting out of the city/suburban living after that.

### caroline — 2017-10-09T02:05:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-all.20254/post-225324>

Hi Eric and welcome.......please start a journal in the optimal journal section. It is great for keeping track of your progress.

Do you get morning sun? watch the sunrise?

C/T?

### Eric Waldron — 2017-10-11T17:44:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-all.20254/post-225572>

Hi Caroline,
Thank you! I will look into the journal. I know I keep one in my notebook via pen and paper here at home.
I have been starting to watch the sunrise for about the past two weeks. I also have been getting on all fours like the "sphinx", which really seems to help me so far. With regards to C/T, I take cold showers and have been for three years or so. I haven't officially measured the temperature of the water, but would estimate it being well within the 50-55 range that JK talks about.

I'm super interested in working with red lighting during the evening. Any good bulbs/devices at an economical price?

# Thread: Sup! New member saying hello from Minneapolis
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sup-new-member-saying-hello-from-minneapolis.20268/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Phil — 2017-10-10T22:01:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sup-new-member-saying-hello-from-minneapolis.20268/post-225501>

Hey,

I've been fallowing Dr Jack Kruse for a while now and finally pulled the trigger on becoming a member. I've been obsessed with health and wellness my entire life. I've mostly been obsessed with nutrition, but recently pulled my head out of my ass and I switched my obsession to light. I grew up in Colorado with the altitude and a lot more sun. Currently living in Minneapolis, luckily I have always enjoyed taking ice baths.

Little about me: Was football kicker for the University of Wisconsin. I was given two rounds fluoroquinolone for a sinus infection in college. I was never really the same athlete after taking this. I had a quad injury that plagued my senior year, pretty sure it was a result of taking the antibiotics. I never made it in the NFL, because I couldn't stay injury free. It sent me down a different path in life of questioning everything and becoming obsessed with mitochondrial health.

I've been eating bulletproof ketogenic diet for the last four years with bulletproof coffee every morning. I'm now a week sober from drinking butter coffee and I plan on never drinking it again. I used to be obsessed with supplements, but recently threw them all out beside turmeric.

I'm currently stuck working on the 21st floor, in a cubicle, under bright LED lights, staring at a computer all day. I am working on an exist strategy. For now I am the weird dude wearing dark amber glasses everywhere I go. Rocking an umbrella at my desk to block the LED lighting, comboed with red lights and a black light at various times though out the day. Also, I have been using my Quantlet for the last few months and I am loving it.

My goal is to keep learning as much as possible. I don't know what I want to do when I grow up, but I tell people it will be something that doesn't exist and that's why I don't know. As of now I want to start some sort of business using light to help people with maybe a focus on athletes.

Short term I am very concerned about 5G. My Mother and Sister both have autoimmune diseases. I'm concerned 5G is really going to hit them hard. I'm learning and experimenting as much as I can so I can maybe help them. Knowing I have the same mitochondrial genetics as them, I am very aware I need to do everything I can for my own health, which means getting out of my current job.

### caroline — 2017-10-11T02:04:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sup-new-member-saying-hello-from-minneapolis.20268/post-225516>

Hi Phil and welcome. Please start a journal in the optimal journal section. Give Dr. K. some health history - and your Mom's and Gran's.

Have you had a 23&me test done?

### Jess on the Brink — 2017-10-30T04:01:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sup-new-member-saying-hello-from-minneapolis.20268/post-226920>

I'm currently stuck working on the 21st floor, in a cubicle, under bright LED lights, staring at a computer all day. I am working on an exist strategy. For now I am the weird dude wearing dark amber glasses everywhere I go. Rocking an umbrella at my desk to block the LED lighting

I feel your pain! I'm right there with ya as the weird gal with the amber glasses. And I'm totally stealing the umbrella idea, my superstitious colleagues are gonna freak!

### Phil — 2017-11-02T20:06:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sup-new-member-saying-hello-from-minneapolis.20268/post-227213>

# Thread: Starfishing in NJ
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starfishing-in-nj.20364/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jess on the Brink — 2017-10-30T02:29:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starfishing-in-nj.20364/post-226916>

Hi All,

I’m so grateful this community exists and is available to me! Your knowledge and stories are an inspiration – thank you! So, last week I fired my closed-minded, concierge doctor that was charging me $40/month and getting no where. This week I’m transferring that $40 to a Silver membership and starting my journey towards Starfish Status.

A bit about me – I’m Jessica from Jersey and I’m a podcast junkie. The first time I heard an interview with Jack Kruse I thought, “Wow, this guy is intense and rather confident about his unconventional theories.” I was intrigued and skeptical so I searched for more podcasts and You Tube videos. He did an interview with John Marchegiani about the mistakes of Functional Medicine. My mind was blown. I literally listened to Jack for the next 72 hours straight. Everything I could get my hands on. That was about 6 months ago. Since then I’ve made some changes in my life and my environment. I’m here to share my story, make connections, get inspired, be inspiring and earn my starfish status.

My story –

38 y.o. living at the 40th latitude

126 lbs, 5’9”

Maternal haplogroup: T2b1

Loved growing up in Jersey in the 80’s – wood paneled station wagon, Carnation instant breakfasts, Fun dip, Goonies, and Sabrett Hot Dogs – oh yes!!

Joined the Coast Guard in the late 90’s and moved to Louisiana (I should have stayed there).

Separated from the CG, moved back to Jersey post Katrina and went to Nursing School.

Got a hospital job, got married, got stressed out, health issues started, got divorced.

Frustrated by conventional medicine and standard of care, left the bedside and went to the dark side – the insurance company (at least I wasn’t working nights, weekends and holidays).

Fatigue, brain fog, anxiety, belly pain, gums bleeding, metallic taste in my mouth, hair thinning / changing texture, weight loss, dry skin, cold extremities, anxiety, poor appetite, chemical sensitives, low libido, acne, anxiety…

I’m inquisitive and I consider my body and environment an experiment of sorts. I’ve tried all kinds of stuff – conventional docs, functional docs, chiro, acupuncture, meditation, reiki, yoga, supplements, ionic foot baths, Korean saunas, a trip to India that included pills that smelled like cow dung… You get the idea.

Moral of the story, I’ve been searching for something that makes sense to my scientific mind, but at the same time, appeases my ‘spiritual’ side. When I found Dr. Kruse and started learning more about light, water, and magnetism something clicked.

Since my starfish journey began I’ve:

\- Watched the sunrise, barefoot on the beach nearly every day.

\- Read the Epi-Paleo prescription and started choking down oysters – it’s much easier now J

\- Currently reading 4th Phase and Water and have The Body Electric lined up after that.

\- Purchased blue blocking glasses, a trifield meter, incandescent and red light bulbs.

\- Ditched my wifi and hard-wired my computer, shield my thyroid, f.lux on my computer, night shift on my phone, airplane mode more than anything, cut the breakers at night.

\- Hired an EMF engineer to assess my apartment – I’ve got some work to do and moving is not out of the question.

\- Found a spring on Find a Spring.com and started sourcing my water there, although the spring is in Jersey, so I’m still a bit unsure about this decision.

\- When working from home (3 days a week) windows are wide open, I eat my lunch outside, grounded and take at least 3 sunshine breaks a day.

\- Downloaded the dMinder App.

\- When I’m in the office on the 14th floor, a stone’s throw from Manhattan, I still take sunshine breaks, but not as often.

\- Became a certified Nurse Patient Advocate, so that I can begin my transition from the dark side to a career that fulfills me and truly helps people find wellness.

\- Started posting the “Jessica” version of what I’m learning on my FB page so my friends and get on board – so far, I’ve created some Dr. Kruse groupies J

\- Become more conscious of my thoughts and my environment (walking around in the dark will do that) and making a concerted effort to give thanks every day, especially to the sun.

\- Health habits that existed B.D.K. (before Dr. Kruse): clean / mostly organic paleo eating, all natural personal care products / cleaning products, chlorine shower filters, Epsom salt baths, dry skin brushing, daily rebounding, standing desk.

My observations since starting my starfish journey:

\- I sleepy by 9 pm and fall asleep very easily.

\- I wake up without an alarm clock about 30 minutes before sunrise (I used to snooze 4-5x’s)

\- I wake up hungry.

\- Having a routine brings order to my life and reduces stress.

\- I’m craving more fats than carbs/sugars.

\- Tingling sensation across my chest while working on my computer has diminished.

\- My period has regulated in cycle with the moon (sorry, TMI?)

\- I’m more observant of my environment – the good: where the sun/moon rises and sets, the wind and the tide, the way the sand swallows your feet as if Mother Earth insists that you get grounded, the way the seagulls react when the sun breaks the horizon. The anxiety inducing: all the cell towers in my area, the magnetic field of my car, the hum of my refrigerator…

My concerns:

\- Myopia since 10 yrs old. Lasik at age 23.

\- MTHFR, homozygous for C677T

\- Exposures during my time in the Coast Guard – vaccinations, TB, ammunitions, hazmat.

\- Worked nights in the hospital for 6 years.

\- At the age of 32 I was told that I have ‘stale eggs’ by an infertility doc (elevated AMH)

o Had my eggs frozen for safe keeping

\- I feel as though my vitality took a major dip about 2 yrs ago. I’ve investigated Lyme, Thyroid, Gut dybiosis, but something is still nagging.

\- Thyroid nodules on u/s 8 months ago.

My questions:

\- Do any of my fellow Jersey folks have recommendations for a doc that is open to the ways of a mitochondriac and will order the suggested labs? (we can’t self-order in this lovely state)

\- For those of you that have been walking the walk for a while now, how do you explain nnEMF, alien sun, etc. without making people’s head spin? I’ve been sounding a bit like an evangelist. I may need to soften my approach

\- I’m seriously considering a Magnetico. It’s a big investment. Is there something else I should prioritize ahead of that purchase? I sleep on the third floor and my bedroom has a mG reading of around 1. The far corner of my apt where the power lines are fixed (which is now forbidden territory) has a reading of 4 mG.

\- If our goal is to optimize our mitochondria and cancer cells also have mitochondria, do those whose bodies have cancer run the risk of optimizing their cancer cells?

\- There are a million more, but we’ve both spent too long in front of this computer.

Any thoughts / advice / criticisms / comments are welcome. Thanks in advance.

My last round of labs April 2017:

B12 – 689 range 211-946 pg/ml

_DHEA S – 295.8 H range 57.3 – 279.2 ug/dL chronically elevated for the last 10+ yrs_

AM Cortisol – 9.6 range 6.2-19.4

Serum Ferritin – 46 range 15-150 ng/ml

TSH – 1.580 range 0.450-4.500 uIU/ml

Free T4, direct – 1.34 range 0.82 – 1.77 ng/dL

TPO Ab – 9 range 0-34 IU/mL

Thyroglobulin Antibody <1.0 range 0.0 – 0.9

Free T3 – 2.5 range 2.0-4.4 pg/ml

Reverse T3 – 22.8 range 9.2-24.1 ng/dL

Mag, RBC – 5.2 range 4.2 – 6.8 mg/dL

Total Testosterone – 26.5 range 10.0 – 55.0 ng/dL

Test, F+W Bound – 1.5 range 0.0 – 9.5ng/dL

Test, %F+W bound – 5.7 range 3.0-18.0%

Calcitriol – 72.5 range 19.9-79.3 pg/ml

Vit D, 25-Hyd – 43.5 range 30. – 100 ng/ml

Mercury – 1.8 range 0.0 – 14.9 ug/L

Fibrinogen Antigen – 289 range 189 – 350 mg/dL

Cholesterol, total – 202 range 100 – 199 mg/dL

Triglycerides – 47 range 0-149 mg/dL

HDL – 90 range >39 mg/dL

LDL/HDL ratio – 1.1 range 0.0 – 3.2

VLDL – 9 range 5-40 mg/dL

LDL – 103 range 0-99 mg/dl

Homocystine, Plasma – 8.2 range 8.7-10.2 mg/dL

Glucose, Serum – 86 range 65-99 mg/dL

BUN – 13 range 6-20 mg/dL

Creatinine – 0.73 range 0.57 – 1.00 mg/dL

BUN/Crea ration – 18 range 9-23

Protein, total serum – 6.5 range 6.0-8.5 g/dL

Albumin – 4.7 range 3.5-5.5 g/dL

Bilirubin – 0.4 range 0.0-1.2 mg/dL

Alk Phos – 48 range 39 – 117 IU/L

# Thread: Hello From Portland, Oregon!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-portland-oregon.20450/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rebecca Hicks — 2017-11-16T02:26:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-portland-oregon.20450/post-228131>

Hello Everyone!

I am 38 years old and have had debilitating fibromyalgia for the past 8 years to the point of not being able to work for the past two. I also discovered a couple years ago that I have EHS (electromagnetic hypersensitivity). I met a biologist this past year who referred me to Jack Kruse. Since then I've been reading a lot and working on all kinds of bio-hacks (sun, grounding, blue light/EMF/DE mitigation, etc) and I started the epi-paleo diet a little over a month ago. I never thought I'd like raw oysters but the way they make my gut feel is so good I think I am addicted now!

With all these changes, I've seen some minor improvements. For one - the rain isn't bothering my barometric pressure sensitivities as much this fall! I also feel lighter and my gut feels good. I do, however, have one annoying symptom that seems to have gotten worse. I have these little bumps on my chin and lower cheeks around my mouth. Really they are all over my face but worse in those places. I've always attributed this to an overgrowth of candida but I could be wrong. I've just noticed over the years that if I eat sugar, these bumps sprout up quite a bit. And now they are really bad. I thought this would get better on paleo but I just read that candida feed on ketones which is exactly what my new diet is promoting.

Does anyone have a solution for this? Maybe carefully selected probiotics?

Thank you!
Rebecca

### drezy — 2017-11-16T02:54:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-portland-oregon.20450/post-228136>

Where do you live Ms Hicks?

### Rebecca Hicks — 2017-11-16T03:15:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-portland-oregon.20450/post-228137>

Tigard, Oregon. Just outside of Portland.

### Rebecca Hicks — 2017-11-16T03:18:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-portland-oregon.20450/post-228138>

I know... not enough sun here! But I'm hoping I banked a bunch of vitamin D over the summer with my daily sun bathing. I'm also using a UVB only tanning bed twice a week to help with vitamin D. I only use it for three minutes, not to get tan. I'm hoping this helps me this winter because last winter was so terrible for me. I could barely do anything.

### JanSz — 2017-11-16T03:35:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-portland-oregon.20450/post-228139>

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...re-to-move-to-thread.19529/page-6#post-222624](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/we-need-a-where-to-move-to-thread.19529/page-6#post-222624)

### Mito1 — 2017-11-16T05:34:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-portland-oregon.20450/post-228142>

> [Rebecca Hicks said:](/goto/post?id=228138)
>
> I know... not enough sun here! But I'm hoping I banked a bunch of vitamin D over the summer with my daily sun bathing. I'm also using a UVB only tanning bed twice a week to help with vitamin D. I only use it for three minutes, not to get tan. I'm hoping this helps me this winter because last winter was so terrible for me. I could barely do anything.

bumps on your face - that's easy to answer. Tramadol causes an out break of zit like bumps on your face. Either a bad reaction or an infection. Also saw you mentioned hypoglycemia - that's another side effect of tramadol. It's a pretty ugly drug I personally would steer clear of.

I'm in Portland for a few months if you need help or questions.

.

### Rebecca Hicks — 2017-12-23T20:50:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-portland-oregon.20450/post-230957>

Thanks for your input! Yes, I am aware of tramedol lowering blood sugar as I ended up in the ER from taking it all day without food because I just wasn't hungry. It was a severe low blood sugar attack and the doctors didn't even know it. They drug tested me and had no Dx. I figured it out myself after a few months in support groups. That woke me up and I decided to wean off.

I am going off of tramedol now but very slowly. I am down from 600 mgs a day to 50. I have to go down super slow because my body has a very intense reaction to any changes. I should be off in a couple months. that's interesting about tramedol causing bumps. Not sure that's it in my case as I've been taking tramedol for seven years and have only had this problem for a couple years. I notice the bumps after I eat any amount of sugar so that's why I thought it was a candida overgrowth problem. They've gotten much worse since I went paleo which at first I thought was odd but now have read that candida feeds on ketones too! But if it is tramedol related, that gives me more to look forward to once I get off. Thank you, Mito!

### Mito1 — 2017-12-23T23:59:13+0200

>
> Thanks for your input! Yes, I am aware of tramedol lowering blood sugar as I ended up in the ER from taking it all day without food because I just wasn't hungry. That woke me up and I decided to wean off.
>
> I am going off of tramedol now but very slowly. I am down from 600 mgs a day to 50. I have to go down super slow because my body has a very intense reaction to any changes. I should be off in a couple months. that's interesting about tramedol causing bumps. Not sure that's it in my case as I've been taking tramedol for seven years and have only had this problem for a couple years. I notice the bumps after I eat any amount of sugar so that's why I thought it was a candida overgrowth problem. They've gotten much worse since I went paleo which at first I thought was odd but now have read that candida feeds on ketones too! But if it is tramedol related, that gives me more to look forward to once I get off. Thank you, Mito!

# Thread: Hola from Oregon... for now :)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hola-from-oregon-for-now.20534/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Venessa — 2017-12-02T06:22:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hola-from-oregon-for-now.20534/post-229329>

Hello, My name's Venessa. Geez where do I start? I'll try to keep it concise...

I remember briefly coming across Dr. Kruse's site like maybe 6-7 years ago (?) but was obviously not ready for it or understood anything, as I am just now introducing myself today. Lately, I've been diving into Kruse blogs, podcasts, webinar recordings, and will take time but everything is now coming together for me - it's finally the right time (as she writes with 7 browser tabs open relating to mitochondrial health).

Background: I'm a functional nutritionist and what I've been calling "intuitive nourishment coach"... but interestingly I'm less interested in the "food" that you put in your mouth and more interested in connecting to nature and other "food" we feed our body, mind, soul the deeper I get into this work.
My family is from Puerto Rico, I was born and raised in Chicago - just moved to southern Oregon in Jan of 2017.

I got into the "alternative" health world after my mom was diagnosed with breast cancer in 2004. Knowing what I now know... she was clearly also dealing with insulin resistance, adrenal issues, hormonal imbalance, SIBO, etc etc prior to her diagnosis. Went the conventional route, did nothing for her - gave her a few months, we then did alternative route \*in sunny Arizona\* and she actually GOT BETTER. But then we had to come back to Chi and it was downhill from there. My mom passed in 2010 - a year longer than they gave her. Throughout her journey I was reading things like Becker's Body Electric and pissed that doctor's didn't seem to know anything.

I overcame insomnia, chronic acid reflux, asthma, thyroid issues with lifestyle/diet changes during her journey which prompted me to go back to school and start helping people so they didn't have to suffer same fate. I find that I learn more by experimenting with myself (like most of us here it seems! N=1) and working with people than any school program can teach.

Genetics: Haplotype L1b1a7a.
Mom always wanted to move somewhere sunny and warm, and eat Mangos and fish lol Gratitude that I know live in the countryside very near vast nature, woods, etc (coming from innercity Chicago) and my partner and I have plans to move to Mexico some day (my partner's family is from there).

Would anyone be able to point out where I could get more info on haplotypes (i.e. optimal environment and inputs for various haplotypes)? Appreciate any help on that front!

Last thing... As I started going deeper into nourishment work, and "re-wilding" myself as in connecting with nature in a deeper way, I started to get messages in dreams and Akashic Records (I'm an Akashic Record reader and intuitive in this way - btw not interested in discussions around validity of that or the woo-factor, etc). Interestingly, I started to get messages about mitochondria a while back and that I needed to look more into this. That there was an entire story that is not being "told" as it relates to the energetics that is being passed down in this way as it relates to disease. There's also something about viruses and how they play a role - I see that Dr. Kruse writes about this as well, so going to look into that more. The info that Dr. Kruse and Dr. Wallace share about mitochondria has been the most profound and in line with so many ancient "ways of knowing" that I have found so far.

The latest dream I had was of a plant that absorbed and utilized external nutrients in environment more efficiently and effectively when done in sunlight, and that we (humans) are the same way - that eating out in the sun, alfresco, has many benefits for absorption, assimilation, utilization, and function. I'm curious if this is true... if anyone knows of actual science or data in literature (or a possible explanation of mechanism) that might support this? While I'm driven by intuition, a girl's gotta have some sound evidence to backup!

Thanks for reading if you've stuck around and looking forward to connecting more with everyone

And thank you Dr. Kruse for your work and community.

### caroline — 2017-12-02T06:41:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hola-from-oregon-for-now.20534/post-229330>

welcome Vanessa....

How about starting a journal in the optimal journal section ....clearly you have a lot to say . and a lot of questions!

If you can manage it - become a gold member and you will have access to Jack's monthly webinars and the follow up live Q&A when you can ask Jack anything.

sign up for the live event on Dec 17th....

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hola-from-oregon-for-now.20534/post-229431>

# Thread: Hello From East Tennessee
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-east-tennessee.20539/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Angie Medler — 2017-12-03T19:58:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-east-tennessee.20539/post-229429>

I started reading Dr. Jack Kruse a few months ago and was totally overwhelmed but know that he speaks the truth. Little by little I am putting the pieces together.

I wish I had found Dr. Jack Kruse! earlier! We moved to E. Tennessee from Washington St. in 2015. I had many must haves for the place we moved and sunrise and sunset was one of the must haves I didn't get  We live in a valley but it runs in a way the sun hits our place for a very good part of the day. I don't have big power lines, I don't have traffic, I don't have cell towers, I don't have a lot of neighbors and it is a beautiful location with ponds and trees etc... We remodeled the house and oh how I wish I had known not to put in LED lights. And if I had known better, we would have looked a little further south for our place. But here we are and I need to learn how to make the most of it for now.

My husband had a heart valve replacement in June of last year. It was defective, very bad, the surgeon said it was about the worst he had seen. This was congenital and yet the drs and dietitians wanted him doing no salt low fat yada yada even though they all admitted his arteries looked good and his numbers were excellent. (Dean had cancer in 2000 before we met. Testicular that spread to kidney and he went through chemo. He has been clear of any cancer since.) The only thing he is on is Warfarin and it would be awesome to get him off of that.

Me. I'm tired of being fat. I'm tired of trying so hard and having what works for everyone else fail miserably. I did HCG for 7 months, lost 20 lbs in the first month and nothing else. 500 freakin calories a day and I could not lose weight. I can't eat wheat or grains or dairy and it is getting to where I can't eat anything without feeling bad.

I watch the Vermont 2017 talk. Wow. Even though I am outside every morning at dawn to let chickens out and take care of my horse, I have my glasses on. I don't get skin exposed because all my life with horses and animals and farm work, I don't wear shorts.

But now! Deuterium....wow. I deuterium bomb myself most days with things that are "good for me". I feel like this might be the biggest key I have been looking for. I know that I'm missing something.

Haplogroup is U2e2
I will attach my Methylation Profile

Dean is Haplogroup H5

That's me. For the time being I will have to be a lion or a hippo but I am learning!

Angie and Dean

### caroline — 2017-12-04T10:40:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-east-tennessee.20539/post-229456>

Hi Angie and welcome.......

Have you read the leptin Reset? the C/T protocol?

You need to get sun on more of your skin/body. Why not throw on shorts and a tank top and get outside for sunrise? Even better - can you get outside naked? You must get sun on your body.

Do you have a kiniki swimsuit?

Give Dr. K. your Mom's and Gran's health history.

What are you eating? drinking?

### Lahelada — 2017-12-04T11:43:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-east-tennessee.20539/post-229464>

Hi Angie,welcome. I might add another missing piece to the puzzle. Have a read of CT 4 . It explains about HCG ,why it works and why it fails. If you can see the sun from your house as it rises with your bare feet planted on a patch of grass you are not so badly off.Yu probably would have been better further south but the rest of your living conditions do not sound bad at all. However, exposing flesh would be great,especially the ladies. As you are up with the chickens anyway it would be a shame to miss that opportunity.

The LED lights have remedy. Use them only when you NEED to see. You may even be able to change them out for yellow ones or even red ones. Red would be better but I am not sure if one can get them. For evenings candlelight is best. Failing that you could use floorlamps and table lamps with red bulbs. Low level of light though. Do not have them at eye level though or in line of sight where you spend most of your time.

Do you use IRIS on your tech gear screens? It is a free app in its basic form iristech.co

Seconding what Caroline said. Give a history of your Gran and your Mum and your history paying special attention to your first 6 years of life.

### Angie Medler — 2017-12-05T17:01:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-east-tennessee.20539/post-229542>

Thank you ladies for the great info and advice!

I did read the Leptin Reset, I purchased the EpiPaleo book a few months ago. I'm overwhelmed with where to even begin.

I cannot see the sunrise from the house, or the sunset, we are in the hills (mountains). We just built a chicken coop that may have given us our naked sunny place where the neighbors can't see. Its crazy, but it is illegal to be exposed in TN  I will look in to the kiniki swimsuit for sure and work on exposing more skin.

We eat organic, grass fed, pasture raised. I know who is raising my food and how they were processed. I feed my birds (and horse) organic feed soy and wheat free so that my eggs don't have wheat. Unfortunate that we don't like seafood, so we are going to have to find a way to get good quality seafood in to us. I don't eat dairy (even though we get raw grass fed cow milk here, I do use the butter though), no soy, no wheat or grains, no nightshades and sugars are rare. Potatoes make my ankles hurt and weak immediately.

Well water through a water softener and filtration system with UV light. The water here has a lot of iron and sulfur.

Mom is in good health, thyroid issue is the only think I know of for her. I don't remember much about my first 6 years, I was the first born of 4 in WI, we moved a lot, lived in Montana, Minnesota, Illinois and Wisconsin during that time. Mom's mom died when she was very young, we knew nothing about her until recently when I was able to track down the family that she came from via my DNA and people that are related and looking for their relatives. She was born near Kingsport TN (near me now), she was married when she was 13 and had 3 kids. Her 2 boys died within a year of each other from VEO-Colitis. She ran off and married my grandfather near Chicago and then another son die in a stock tank on the farm. She had 6 more kids and died from exhaustion or bad blood transfusion.

I've put some red bulbs in several rooms, it helps when we are up 2 hours before the sun comes up.

### Inger — 2017-12-05T19:34:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-east-tennessee.20539/post-229560>

Welcome Angie
You will still get light even if you cant see the sun.. which helps for sure. But maybe cou could rive a bit or bike to a spot where you can see the sunrises? At least a few times / week?
Great to have a place no one can see for nude tanning! It helps huge   I do not think it is bothering anyone when you build a space where no neighbors can see you

# Thread: I was attracted by the light

### Claudia Chadwick — 2017-12-25T03:39:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-attracted-by-the-light.20615/post-231041>

Hello,
This is my first post. I happened upon Dr. Kruse as I was listening to ketogenic diet videos on YouTube. When I heard him speak of the importance of sunlight I knew I had happened upon someone who could explain the science of what I had intuitively known all along.
I have always been very particular about light, attracted to sitting in morning sunshine, & loathe fluorescent light. When we were selling my elderly mother's house I took every old fashioned incandescent light bulb out of the house and transferred them to the fixtures in my house.
I often dream of living in a house with floor to ceiling windows to allow in as much natural light as possible. When I try to focus to get tasks done, I have known for years that if I don't get the tedious stuff finished before 2 pm, it just won't get done that day.
I am a morning person and wake up cheerful most days. Being outside in the sunshine with birds & flowers is about the best thing there is.
I live at 41 degrees latitude.
Looking forward to learning more.

### Mariangela — 2017-12-26T23:46:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-and-introducing-self.20617/post-231117>

> [Susie Cahill said:](/goto/post?id=231080)
>
> Welcome Mariangela,
> Gosh, you’ve been through the ringer. Thrilled for you you’ve had such rapid improvement. Can you say what the game changer may have been? Or did you do all the protocols together? Are you doing CT?
> Cheers.
> .

After I stalled at 85% better and not budging, I listened to one of JKs podcasts. In it he said that when he is doing cranial surgery he uses Ice to save the brain. That was my AHA moment. I said give me a bucket of ice water now! My encephalitis was coming back. I had been doing cold therapy 2 years following Wim Hoffman but only did it out doors in winter here in Pennsylvania. So the next day I read a lot of Jacks blogs and watched more podcasts. The next day I had a plan. I went out and got the sunrise in my bare feet with no contacts in. I had the strangest energy exchange feeling happen between me and the earth. I did about 3 minutes. After that I had more energy than I had for many yeas. All my coordination was back. It was incredible. I unplugged the wifi next that day and went back out for 11Am sun and all four limbs on the ground. I did 15 minutes. After that i knew I was healed. I could just feel it! And I knew my gut was healed and I could now eat anything . After that I went to the grocery and bought food - real food. And Ate. I did not get sick. The following day I started CT with a cold tub soak. I went right to the full immersion and stuck my head in. what a relief! It was like putting a fire out in my brain. That was it. I continue with Ct and do Epi-Paleo and all the other protocols I mentioned. For me it was the combination of doing them all as I described. Hope that gives some insight.

### Bryn — 2018-01-07T13:53:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-new-zealand.20661/post-231869>

I’m a newbie from NZ. I have t2d & hypertension and have been working very hard on curing myself of both for the past 2 years. The main method has been by LCHF & some resistance exercise. I came across Dr Kruse this past year and have been reading as much as possible. Have the Leptin Rx, about halfway through (on my iPad!). Have filled out the signature with my main details. Stopped metformin last year, bgl’s run a bit higher than I’d like. Stopped inhibace 3 months ago and reduced my atenalol by half just before Christmas. BP is ok but higher than before, maybe trending down.

Thank you Jack & Caroline for your welcomes! I’ve been prowling around today looking for the optimal journal section, but my dopamine must be a bit low, I can’t find it. Can someone point me in the right direction please.

To answer your other questions, I have a plan to do 23&me but need to save up for that. I have retired and have a limited income currently. I have worked as a midwife for most of my working life.

My mum and some of her siblings have t2d as well as her mum also. My mum is 91, on insulin. Has been well but slowly fading at the mo.

I live in New Zealand on a small peninsula into the Tauranga harbour, outside of the main city but sadly now getting very built up. I’ll add a map. Good fishing but I can’t handle the boat alone.

# Thread: Hello from Utah
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-utah.20718/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### 1021km — 2018-01-18T10:24:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-utah.20718/post-232811>

Hi everyone!

I'm just here to soak up as much knowledge as I can in hopes that one day I can use it to help our species thrive.

Just figured I would introduce myself instead of continuing to lurk in the shadows.

My name is Ken. I have lived in Utah for most of my life, though I have done my fair share of traveling abroad. I value the pursuit of knowledge, simplistic efficiency in systems, purpose of being, and human connection.

Jack, big fan... Keep up the good work, it is appreciated by many, and many more to come. I came to this site as a result of stumbling across a podcast that you did a while back. Though I quickly found myself out of my depth, somehow your message still strongly resonated with me. I believe we know when we hear the truth, even if we don't fully comprehend it yet. Call it a remnant of our tie to the underlying matrix we affectionately deem the universe. That was all it took, I quickly searched out every podcast I could find you in, and set about learning your message. Along the way, I got the impression that there were much more intricate details that you weren't overwhelming the public with, and so I ended up here.

Because I have no specific malady that has yet manifested, I find myself without an ultimate goal to anchor and organize my efforts to learn. As such I tend to bounce around from one interesting topic to the next trying to pickup the language and syntax as I go. I find that is my biggest stumbling block. I am neither well versed in medical academia nor the realm of physics. Yet, I am inexplicably drawn to learn what is taught here. Despite its intricate complexities, the message seems simple enough in application: live like a polar bear, eat like a shark, make like the sphinx. Then, live where your DNA(s) are programmed for while mitigating nnEMF and optimizing your water quality. And as always N=1. Please let me know if I have missed, or misunderstood any of the basics. I am certainly not one who is afraid of correction. If anyone has any suggestions on how to begin eating the elephant, I am all ears. I suppose if I was forced to provide a sub-interest, it would be either longevity or optimization of my home environment.

I look forward to conversing with this great bunch of truth seekers!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Hello from Hal Weiss (Chappaqua, NY  - soon maybe Fl)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hal-weiss-chappaqua-ny-soon-maybe-fl.20743/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### halvibe — 2018-01-22T23:50:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hal-weiss-chappaqua-ny-soon-maybe-fl.20743/post-233196>

Don't believe I've posted before, saying hello, and Dr. Brett Jacques suggested I share some DDW info.
I got the following feedback from Gerolsteiner saying their water has about 58-61 ppm deuterium:

"Thanks a lot for your email. To evaluate the quality of our mineral water as well as the deep waters in our sources we use very thorough analysis. This includes isotopic examination to evaluate the age structure of our deep waters. Tritium is the leading parameter und is examined regularly. Deuterium play a less important role and is determined with special analyses.
In the analyzed deep waters, the stable isotope had an amount of **\-58.0 to 60.9 per mil for Deuterium.** There are no significant differences found in the various sources.
We hope we could answer your question. If you have any further queries please do not hesitate to contact us.
Best regards from Germany"
I sure hope they have that low an amount of D. I intend to send a sample for analysis (maybe about a month down the road). I'll post results if-n-when I get it analyzed.

Love, Light and Warmth to all
Hal

Update:

Thanks WalterNL. I guess it was too good to be true. One person I spoke with said that Perrier and Pellegrino are at about 140ppm, so I should have know Gerolst was around that also. Sorry to give anybody high hopes. I'll keep on looking.
Regards,
Hal​

### WalterNL — 2018-01-23T20:50:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hal-weiss-chappaqua-ny-soon-maybe-fl.20743/post-233267>

Welcome. -60 promille from VSMOW is not 60 ppm. I made the same mistake when I first looked up mineral waters.

\-60 promille = -6 percent. VSMOW = 155ppm deuterium, 6 percent of that ~ 9ppm, so Gerolsteiner has about 147 ppm deuterium. Which is the number you can find in Germany, confirmed by other sources.
The poles have around 80ppm deuterium, so it's very unlikely somewhere in nature there is less. Maybe snowmelt on top of mount Everest?

Hal

# Thread: Posting from Under the Sun
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/posting-from-under-the-sun.20858/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### aguzman — 2018-02-10T22:48:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/posting-from-under-the-sun.20858/post-234513>

Hello, my name is Andrea and live in the SF Bay Area. I was introduced to Jack Kruse and his strong beliefs around the science and healing potential of light, water, magnetism via a podcast on photobiomodulation. Despite many years of working with practitioners in naturopathic medicine, functional medicine, infectious disease, acupuncture, homeopathy, etc. this really is the only theory around wellness that resonates with me based on it's connection back to nature and the more primal self. Intuitively, I have felt for the past 15 years that my life has strayed from the fundamentals through increased professional and family responsibilities that have drained my life force. Though I have experienced profound fatigue and related issues over the last 20 years since my first child, I have remained functional. However, 2 years ago I had an allergic reaction to a bug bite which caused burning, scraping and itching pain all over my skin with no rash. Despite the healing of the bite, my pain has never subsided. I have what is called allodynia and hyperaesthesia triggered by mechanical friction, heat and pressure on my skin. It feels like a permanent sunburn. Anything that touches my skin hurts and the pain does not subside after the stimuli is removed. My CNS has been centrally sensitized, my body remembers the pain. In essence my immune system has gone haywire and is triggering massive activation of my nervous system. This has now set off a cascade of other symptoms, the most serious of which is pelvic pain for 6 months after an infection (first ever UTI). The symptoms are debilitating and have caused great existential angst. I continue to search for solutions however. I just started low dose immunotherapy to desensitize my immune system to the many chronic infections I have been diagnosed with. Four weeks ago, I started red light and near infrared light therapy, major doses of UV from the sun, cold thermogenesis, leptin prescription and EMF and blue light blocking. The challenge for me is that my skin is thermally sensitive and so the cold and heat can exacerbate the skin symptoms. I figure that there will be a tipping point however and once my biology starts to shift from these therapies, I will be able to better tolerate and start reaping major benefits. I seems like I have had almost every test in the world but not any of the ones that jack recommends to evaluate leptin sensitivity. All of my tests correspond to auto-immunity and have had chronically low Vit. D as long as I can remember. Mitochondria Profile: Maternal side of family was relatively healthy and hearty despite my grandmother being a chronic smoker for 60 years and she didn't even die from cancer or a lung related condition. Type 2 diabetes runs on this side. My Mother is 71 and very vibrant and healthy from a functional perspective but does struggle with high blood sugar and some neuropathy in her legs.

My objective is to completely heal and live a joyful life without chronic pain. I waver between being tenacious and utter despair at times. I feel like I just need some signs of progress to keep me moving forward. I am glad to be a part of this group and am thankful for your time to read about my background and to Jack for this movement.

### RebbalforLight — 2018-02-11T00:44:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/posting-from-under-the-sun.20858/post-234522>

Hi, Andrea.

_I have felt for the past 15 years that my life has strayed from the fundamentals through increased professional and family responsibilities that have drained my life force._

Me, too. I hope you find health.

_I have found much pain relief from doing CT in the cold pool and then getting morning sun._ _I stand in my bare feet in the grass and look at/toward the sun for 10-15 minutes within 30 minutes of sunrise without glasses or contacts. This is the #1 thing I need to do every morning. Welcome!_

Welcome Andrea
Jacks protocols have helped me massively. Are you also following the Leptin RX? In my experience that along with morning sun, CT and grounding is very effective. I know JK doesnt usually recommend short cuts with supplements but speaking personally when I have severer skin issues (red face rash in particular) I get great results from high dose Sun Chlorella along with activated charcoal. Leptin RX is very good because you will eventually find that your sweat patterns change and toxins get easily removed from you skin via sweating. Magnesium theronate helps me big time as well.
All the best with your recovery
Chris

# Thread: Hello from England
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-england.20960/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cisq — 2018-02-26T18:45:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-england.20960/post-235780>

Hi, I’m in the process of learning the massive amount of amazing information coming from the general direction of Dr. Jack Kruse.

I’ve just bought a couple of pairs of blue blocking glasses for computer use which already seem to be helping with getting off to sleep quicker and improved sleep quality and more dreams I reckon too!

I’m also grounding daily and getting out in the sunshine whenever possible. It’s pretty dull here in the North of England.

I have an initial question which I’d like help with. I’m currently working under 24 flourescent tubes which I know aren’t good at all for me even before coming across Dr. Kruse’s page. I have to work indoors at the moment but I do have the option of choosing the type of lighting with which to replace the flourescents.

Do I go with full spectrum or daylight flourescents, LED or incandescents if they’re still available or something else? I’m wearing blue blockers at the moment at work.

I’d appreciate input and advice from anyone with knowledge on this please, many thanks, Cisq.

### caroline — 2018-02-27T01:58:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-england.20960/post-235813>

Hi Cisq and welcome......

Try and stay sane as you embark on this journey. Living in the UK, knowing you do not get enough sun can be very frustrating. I would buy an EMF meter, Acoustimeter and test your surrounding area and house.

# Thread: I'm back, and making progress.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-back-and-making-progress.20971/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### WereBear — 2018-02-28T16:50:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-back-and-making-progress.20971/post-235939>

To be clear, I never stopped "Krusing"

Dr. Kruse's principles were the bedrock of my attempts to climb out of the pit of extreme fatigue, crushing insomnia, auto-immune issues, and my continuing loss of Executive Function that no doctor would even try to understand, except my gem of a GP. After so many tests which came back "normal," he told me these were "stress symptoms" and I had to find the stress and get rid of it.

Turns out, I was born with it. First I thought it was Hellish Menopause, then I figured it was Adrenal Fatigue, and then I discovered Cortisol Resistance, Dr. Kruse, and part of the answer. I improved and then I got stuck.

I think I've got the final part of the puzzle now. I have Asperger's Syndrome.

I'm not unhappy about it  My first reaction to following my hunch to an online test was, "This explains so much." Obviously I am high functioning, but also I couldn't have possibly been diagnosed as a child, because the condition didn't exist then. I was seen as simply "weird and gifted;" as I still am.

However, this was also a nightmare when it comes to medical help; as in, _there is none_. As little understood as autism still is, how it manifests in women is even more obscure. As I like to say now:

> Those bacteria who live in vents on the ocean floor, under tremendous pressure, and existing on geothermal energy? Science knows more about _them_ than they do about women with Asperger's Syndrome.

So even though I have been thrown back on my own devices, I'm better at it than most "experts." I am embarking on a way of "fixing" what I think is a key element: I have been working my brain so hard in the absence of needed hormones (via an idiot doctor who threw me into surgical menopause) I have essentially given myself a Traumatic Brain Injury. From the inside.

In an ideal world, I could rest up and otherwise follow the protocols for people who are recovering from such injuries. Of course, we don't live there. I've got a full time job, a husband with a chronic illness, and a fledgling business that have all been continuously juggled out of sheer necessity.

After trying artificial estrogen and progesterone, tentative experience with steroids from serious crises, and some amino acid supplementation, I have decided that I should let my body manage this crisis. I am taking increasing amounts of **pregnenolone**. All with good results.

The only cautions I have run across regarding this hormone/neurotransmitter precursor is that when people take exogenous hormone supplements at the same time as pregnenolone, this interference with body regulation leads to a imbalance. So I'm not taking any other hormone or neurotransmitter supplements with it.

I'm essentially sending a series of cement trucks to the job site, and let the Supervisor decide to build a wall, or fire stairs, or a chimney, as needed.

This turns out to be the missing element. I was faithfully wearing my amber lensed sunglasses and working natural light and avoiding fluoride and eating as well as I could, like lots of mackerel. (Fortunately, I love it!) Now, things are finally improving again.

There really is light at the end of this tunnel. Sunlight

### JanSz — 2018-02-28T18:42:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-back-and-making-progress.20971/post-235963>

Which one are you using?

# Thread: New here..from Melbourne AUS.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here-from-melbourne-aus.20980/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Trev Douglas — 2018-03-02T02:34:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here-from-melbourne-aus.20980/post-236116>

Thought I should introduce myself...

I'm Trev, from Mordialloc, a bayside suburb of Melbourne, Australia.

Been taking in as much as I can from Jack via FB, Insta, here and some podcasts etc the last couple of months.

I must say I'm blown away! This has cracked open a whole new thing for me...

As a former Arborist, hearing Jack talk about light and Mitochondria using Plants and Photosynthesis as an analogy helped make it quickly make sense to me that there is something in this...now hooked on learning more!

I'm now getting up for sunrise, watching sunsets, drinking better water, dodging blue light, conscious of nnEMF exposure, and have added Seafood and some meat after almost 5 years vegetarian.

I have been using blue blocking glasses for a few years, but more because it helped my eyes on being on computers so much. I have a new understanding of them now.

I did have a recent health episode (pneumonia) that was a wake up to get serious about all this.

Thanks!

# Thread: Hello, from Melbourne, Australia
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-melbourne-australia.21009/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### dbaverstock — 2018-03-07T18:50:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-melbourne-australia.21009/post-236592>

Hi folks,

Been lurking for a long time.
Got into 'bio-hacking' two years ago after my chronic fatigue flared up again and got sick of being sick.
Got really good at reducing the time it takes to 'bounce' back from the symptoms, although never really feeling like I've ever totally 'cured' myself.
Got well within 6 months which is a new record for me.
All thanks to the advice from Dr. Jack Kruse's blogs, Joseph Cohen's blogs and Dave Asprey's blogs.
Plus some extra help from friends I met locally in the last few years who are also into this.

Didn't really feel like introducing myself until I put some skin into the game, so to speak.
Now I'm invested I figure I should say hello.
May as well share my story and how I ended up here. Its a bumpy ride, feel free to skip it.

Ever since I can remember my life has been a living hell. Potentially before that too but I can't remember.
Suffered constant psychological abuse since I was three.
Sexual abuse at five, seven, nine, eleven, fourteen and twenty one.
Sick with severe chronic fatigue from six until thirteen. Seventeen until twenty two.
I suspect the traumatic home life I had combined with multiple vaccinations around six is what tipped the scales off balance permanently.
My mother was and is useless at mothering. Couldn't take care of the house, let alone her three sons, never knew what she wanted out of life, the point being she was inattentive and ineffective. Later in life I learned she has sporadic bouts of depression and epileptic seizures. Explains a lot.
My father was a workaholic, and alcoholic, combined with being bipolar and depressed, worked painting houses. Probably the paint fumes didn't help.
Constant fights and disputes at home, oldest brother defended us two younger ones.
Middle child was bullied at school, he was born premature and has cognitive and social deficits because of it, even at nearly 33 years old he acts more like a young teen than I do. Quick to anger and always on edge.
He sexually abused me a few times when I was still very young. Not so great.
Being sick all the time since I was six I was rarely at school and so didn't have the influences to give me the right social skills at the right times.
The lack of energy and staying at home meant my mother tried to teach me (poorly).
My only real hobby was using the computer I had to play video games and then later on browse the internet so I could teach myself.
I remember having few friends at school and the ones I did have were poor influences on me, having poor home lives themselves. I often remember being put up and left out at their houses by my mother, presumably due to a bad home situation that I wasn't made aware of at the times.
My mother and my older brother moved out of the family home when I turned 12. We never really had stable housing or living until I was 14-15. Then dealing with the throes of my late onset of puberty didn't help. Yeah I didn't really start growing up until everyone else around me was halfway through finishing.
Always was the shortest and thinnest.
Had to get testosterone shots from 12 until 14, probably should have kept on with them perpetually, but family couldn't afford it. Suspect that I've never properly finished developing. Can't grow a full beard!
I suspect this lovely combo fused to create the environment to help me fail to thrive.

I didn't really end up shaking my chronic fatigue until around 9-10 where my mother took me to a few naturopaths and treated me right. Conventional medicine always seemed to fail me.
Got sick again after a bad young love went all sour around 15 and got glandular fever (epstein bar virus).
They say it never truly leaves you. Slowly recovered by 21.

My life never really got on track until I ended up back with my oldest brother taking care of me.
He was the only one who really cared properly.
My life never really properly started until I turned 24. Got a real job and by 25 lived out of home on my own.
The healing truly started from then on.
Well, I thought so anyway, I've learned that my job in IT is killing me.

I still suffer from chronic fatigue, albeit in a lesser form, I can still function in a day job but can't do anything much beyond that, I have anhedonic style depression, I'm not ever really 'sad' but I never truly enjoying the fruits of life, I can fake it quite well though. I don't feel things, all the trauma as a child turned that off.
I obsess over topics, have a few pet ones, biohacking, computers and now oddly gardening. I'm told that's an autistic/aspergers thing. Especially since I don't enjoy socialising much unless its to talk about those specific things, so I have very small friend groups for just that. Talking to people just doesn't trigger any pleasure.
I often have intrusive thoughts and my thoughts race, have a hard time focusing in on organising things as you might tell by how this whole post has been structured.
I can be quite intelligent though, quite good at matching together data across different disciplines, which is probably why I enjoy biohacking and Jack Kruse's approach to health.

I've taken my health into my own hands. If life up until now taught me anything its that literally no one gives a shit about you, life isn't fair, and I've been lied to my entire life by everyone around me.
You could say I have some trust issues...

So what got me healthier?
So far, lots of things that alter or reduce anxiety/cortisol, boost NAD+ or mitochondrial function and repair cognitive function.
PQQ, CoQ10, Bacopa, Piracetam, Lion's Mane, Memantine, Selegiline, Shilajit, Moclobemide, Tianeptine.
My favourites for helping me 'function'.
I've tried CBT with a psychologist but could never really stick at it long enough. I suspect I have some PTSD lingering untreated.

Sorry for the long rant style and gaps.
Its painful to me for trying to remember those times which is probably why I struggle to do so coherently.

Anyway.

What lead me to these parts of the internets?
Well, it seems the only place where everyone is on point with their health and correct.
If anyone can help me onto the road to optimal its you guys and gals.

All I want to do is thrive, succeed, and use my life experience to help treat others much younger than I am now so they don't have to go through everything I did, wasting time, not living.

I want to be the best damn medical doctor there is here and incorporate the teachings we have here so people can truly get better.

Err.. yeah. So, Hi!

I do wonder if my story resonates with anyone else?

Peace.

### Jenny S — 2018-03-08T05:08:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-melbourne-australia.21009/post-236653>

Hi & welcome. I'm living at Bridgewater, just north of Bendigo at them moment but not planning on staying here. Moving north again.
I had chronic fatigue & at Paleo Josh suggestion went up to Cairns, got as much sun & beach as I could. Had blood tests before & after 2 weeks up there & showed a remarkable improvement in that short time. He is really helpful & has an osteopath business in Bendigo & Torquay - if he's still at both if you wanted to see someone. Good luck.

### Trev Douglas — 2018-03-09T08:31:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-melbourne-australia.21009/post-236743>

Welcome!

Sounds like you've had quite the start in life...thank you for having the courage to share.

In the area of past trauma and processing it mentally in a way that may help you to move forward, I highly recommend looking into the work of Dr John Demartini.
He applies Quantum principles to psychology in a way that makes a lot of sense, to me anyway. He holds a 2 day workshop called The Breakthrough Experience that is well worth doing.
You can read a lot of his content online and in books, but the "Experience" part is key and you can only really get that by "doing" it.

Also from Melbourne here.

### caroline — 2018-03-09T08:43:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-melbourne-australia.21009/post-236744>

Please give Dr. K. your Mom's and Gran's health history.

Have you read Dr. K's book? The leptin reset. It is the first couple of years of his blog.

Do you listen to Jacks podcasts? His two Vermont talks? These are all extremely important to get handle on all this.

What are you eating? drinking?

what is your nnEMF situation ...at home? work?

How is your sleep? your libido?

### dbaverstock — 2018-03-09T13:15:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-melbourne-australia.21009/post-236750>

> [Jenny S said:](/goto/post?id=236653)
>
> Hi & welcome. I'm living at Bridgewater, just north of Bendigo at them moment but not planning on staying here. Moving north again.
> I had chronic fatigue & at Paleo Josh suggestion went up to Cairns, got as much sun & beach as I could. Had blood tests before & after 2 weeks up there & showed a remarkable improvement in that short time. He is really helpful & has an osteopath business in Bendigo & Torquay - if he's still at both if you wanted to see someone. Good luck.

Hi Jenny, nice to meet you. Yes I am aware of Josh and have been trying to arrange an appointment with him though so far unsuccessful.
I agree the sunshine is best for curing it, I had some good experience with that the last time I was able to take leave from work for three weeks and spent the whole time outside in the park in the sun during Autumn. It was enough to let me pull through the rest of the year until just recently. I feel like I'm slipping back down into the depths of my condition.

> [Trev Douglas said:](/goto/post?id=236743)
>
> Welcome!
>
> Sounds like you've had quite the start in life...thank you for having the courage to share.
>
> In the area of past trauma and processing it mentally in a way that may help you to move forward, I highly recommend looking into the work of Dr John Demartini.
> He applies Quantum principles to psychology in a way that makes a lot of sense, to me anyway. He holds a 2 day workshop called The Breakthrough Experience that is well worth doing.
> You can read a lot of his content online and in books, but the "Experience" part is key and you can only really get that by "doing" it.
>
> Also from Melbourne here.

Hi Trev, nice to meet you too. Thank you for sharing your advice I think it may really help if this person is of a similar way of looking at things to Jack Kruse.
Yes its been one hell of a ride but now finally on the mend and getting on with things. At least I'm still alive, well, I tell myself that anyway, a living shell is still living right?

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=236744)
>
> Please give Dr. K. your Mom's and Gran's health history.
>
> Have you read Dr. K's book? The leptin reset. It is the first couple of years of his blog.
>
> Do you listen to Jacks podcasts? His two Vermont talks? These are all extremely important to get handle on all this.
>
> What are you eating? drinking?
>
> what is your nnEMF situation ...at home? work?
>
> How is your sleep? your libido?

Hello Caroline, pleasure to meet you.
My Mom has history of cardiovascular issues, same for her dad and mom, some mild epilepsy issues too, definite MTHFR mutation and passed onto me (i've got hers and mine 23andme data). No idea of more than that I should ask my Mom more about it. never knew my gran tbh. My mom had me when she was relatively old which probably didn't help the situation.

I haven't read it yet. Only really started getting serious about Jack Kruse's work beyond Facebook recently.
Slowly getting through a few old blogs.
Same for the podcasts, talks, etc.

Diet is a funny thing. I definitely think I'm leptin resistant based off my short readings of a few blogs. I'm perpetually skinny, never seem to have a great interest in food though its not from an aversion to it. The appetite just isn't there most of the time.
When I do eat I seem to be drawn mostly to proteins, chicken, salmon, sardines, eggs, those are my staples. Usually with loads of butter. I rarely eat carbs these days.
Meals tend to be only once a day, sometimes twice. Breakfast if I fit it in and dinner.

At home I'm nnEMF conscious, well as far as only lowering the output power on my wi-fi router to "low" so it doesn't reach my bedroom, turn it off at night.
I live by myself so the only nnEMF I am exposed to is the surrounds of wi-fi by neighbours, which is thankfully not too strong and only 5 networks.
A far cry from my last living situation a year ago in an apartment block three stories up and 20 wifi networks blaring at me 24/7.
I wear blue blockers at home and at work whenever artificial light is in play. Usually sit in the dark at home with only the laptop screen lighting things up.
Work is all CFL tube lighting and no natural sunlight. Evaporative cooling.
I work in IT fixing computers so typically the day involves being surrounded by 40+ nnEMF emitting things at any given time.
It is literally an nnEMF hell but its all I know how to do right now and so I keep on bearing it until I can change my skillset.

My sleep had been mostly good up until this week where I've slept only 2 nights for about 5-6 hours, the rest about 2-3.
I'm often extremely restless when trying to get to sleep and toss and turn manically until I'm physically exhausted enough to sleep. Its not ideal.
I've been taking Baclofen to help me relax for the last month, it was successful and it seems now I've built a tolerance to it as it no longer works, even after doubling the dose, so I've stopped trying with it.

Libido is sparse. I might feel some urges every week and a half to two weeks but it subsides quickly. I have no good outlet for that so I try to ignore it.

### caroline — 2018-03-10T04:57:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-melbourne-australia.21009/post-236808>

Can/do you stay off electronics in the evening? It will improve your sleep dramatically.

Have you thought of using candles?

are there smart meters where you live?

Please listen to Jack's Vermont talks ASAP .......

It is all about the three legged stool.

Can you get away? go camping? place yourself near a river, stream, lake and get in some nice cold water

Have you read the C/T easy street guide? C/T is massively important.

Yes I can go electronics free.
I won't like it because I like to listen to podcasts and facebook after work but it's one of those things I must do I suppose.
Back to gardening then.

No I haven't thought not using candles.
Should I do that instead of sitting in the dark?

Yes. Every house has one.
My block has two because it's subdivided with two units.

The meters are about five feet away from my lounge. Thankfully the bedroom is far away from everything.

Yes I'll give them a listen.

There's nothing convenient for me so I can do that during the week but weekends I can get away sure.

I haven't read that guide no.
Thanks for pointing me in a few key directions.

I've been trying CT but haven't kept up with it. I did notice after three days if doing it at same times I started feeling better but mental issues kicked in and bad habits stop me going on.
I'll keep fighting myself to continue.

### JanSz — 2018-03-13T17:14:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-arctic-tundra-of-mpls-mn.21022/post-237117>

> [Annemarie Heise said:](/goto/post?id=237114)
>
> Can you please comment on why you advised not to drink Preventa straight? Thank you

**My own guesses.**
After reading @[WalterNL](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19189/) complaints (when drinking Qlarivia-25 straight), and reading that DDW among other helps in diabetes.
I think starting with highly depleted water may lower blood sugar excessively.
But
getting there gradually should be acceptable (tolerable, or lesser of two evils), so it would be good idea to monitor
fasting glucose and insulin while using highly deuterium depleted water.

Drinking (successfully) DDW may cause other discomforts, that may have to be dealt with.
Say having brain tumor, now dead tissue, how do one lives with that? Surgery is/may be need, to extract dead tissue, discomfort in mean time.

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)
As we speak, I am having discussions with my friend in Poland.
Prostate cancer metastasized to sacral bone. Prostate removed over 10 years ago.
Numerous radiations on sacral bone including very recent one.
He drinks DDW.
Started having pain, dilutes DDW only as much as he is able to tolerate his pain.
Is that good solution?
**Possibly he should get (major class) pain killers and go for stronger DDW?**
FENTANYL, morphine
I also suggested **that he assumes** that his pain is due to his (dying) cancer.

The plan is to follow this:

### drezy — 2018-03-13T17:49:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-arctic-tundra-of-mpls-mn.21022/post-237119>

>
> Started having pain, dilutes DDW only as much as he is able to tolerate his pain.
> Is that good solution?
> **Possibly he should get (major class) pain killers and go for stronger DDW?**

That's a good question.

I know you know this but for reference Pg. 36 of that book Notes that "Temporary increase of pain" is a side effect and does suggest to "bridge this period with the help of prescribed painkillers".

Knowing full well my opinion has the value of a filled spittoon I'll share it anyways. I've avoided surgeries, anesthetization, and pain killers(not counting a couple advil) completely for my whole life and intend to continue to do so. Cancer scares the shit out of me though. By default, even having a strong aversion to any drugs, I think I'd be telling my family "Looks like I'll be high as a kite for the next month or two. Strap me down in the sun daily and I'll see you in a couple months."

# Thread: Greetings from California
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-california.21104/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### RobertR — 2018-03-29T02:35:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-california.21104/post-238178>

I have been a reading, you tubing, explorer of mitochondrianism for about 8 months. I am extremely grateful for these resources and can see there is an endless amount of ever increasing information. I am introducing myself to the forum so that I can more effectively participate and access the information.

I am 68 yo and living on the central California coast. I have had a large garden for about 45 years, which has taught me a lot. I have always greeted medically/socially recommended behaviors with more than a bit of skepticism hence: I have never worn sunscreen or sunglasses, never gave up butter, meat, eggs or fat. Never owned a microwave No pharmaceuticals beyond a very occasional antibiotic. Tried vitamins a few times...tasted bad and did not digest. If I am looking for a super food it is always underfoot right where I live. I eat seasonal and locally. Pretty easy to do on the central coast. As I gardener I have always had a great connection with the sun. I am barefoot as much as possible. I live in the mountains in a tiny house in the trees with minimal electric, amber lights there is wifi from a neighbor and 3 bar cell signal. No light pollution. My cabin has a wood fire radiant floor so early evening til 10 pm it is delicious radiant heat. At night and early morning it gets down to 40-50 degrees and gives me some CT. I am also doing cold showers and the pond which is quite cold. Since leaving an emergency room job 12 years ago my vision returned to 20/20. I know first hand how emf toxic hospitals are. The morning after particularly bad ER shifts I would find myself watering bare foot in the garden it always cleared my head. My lighting is 2 low watt amber incandescents and candles. The electric is on about 1/3 the outside perimeter with a kill switch. I wear blue blockers when the lights or computer is on.

Things that I have added since becoming mitochondriac are the blue blockers, more seafood, timing of meals, sunrises ( I have seen way more sunsets than sunrises) now I am catching about 3 out of 4 sunrises, much more seafood. Searching for better water and Malbec. Most importantly I am better understanding the gravity of the current and future environmental light degradation.

The Entanglement webinar and the Clear Impact podcast have inspired me to be more vocal and involved. I have lived a lot of my life through intuition and observation ("really. so all of a sudden" the sun and UV light are poison and I need to slather your expensive chemicals on my skin hmmmm). I need to be more diligent about learning the science so I can talk to anyone. I have grandchildren I want them and all children to live their lives in a healthy environment.

So I am going to get my DNA analyzed and start some lab work. I am very interested in blocking emfs in the design of my tiny house (and every where and anywhere) I know due to my stubborness and frugality I am going to try deuterium deplete water. Leather soled shows / moccasins. Much more.

My 98 year old mother is still living her mother and all her sisters live to mid ninety.
I am meandering so I will stop.

After I give thanks to Jack for his vision, intelligence and passion. And to everyone I have and will interact with on the forum for their courage knowledge and kindness.

Robert

### mrc — 2019-05-23T11:28:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-california.21104/post-271091>

G'day from the west coast.
I was up in central California for a day of work this week & its insane how Sacramento is being geoengineered like crazy on a Monday day/night.

### caroline — 2019-05-24T06:31:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-california.21104/post-271172>

welcome Robert....

Please start a journal in the optimal journal section and tell us more.

You are building a tiny House??? I love them....
