# Thread: Hello and Greetings from Marina del Rey, California
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-and-greetings-from-marina-del-rey-california.21150/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### neurotms — 2018-04-05T23:02:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-and-greetings-from-marina-del-rey-california.21150/post-238787>

As many have declared, I'm also a longtime lurker but first time poster on this forum. In the past few years, Ive been able to take a deep dive into the vast amount of information that Dr. Kruse has so graciously bestowed upon us. As someone who has been brought up in the medical/surgical field, I wanted to take a different approach and do a Retrospective Analysis of my N= 1 since I started following this blog in 2015. Boy have a learned so many things from @[Scompy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17718/) to @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) to @[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) to @[nonchalant](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/191/). My gratitude for your contribution is extremely out of this world.

I look forward to participating and letting curiosity be the best of me while continuing my N=1 protocols for optimal health. I've worked with Neurological Disorders for the past several years utilizing Transcranial Magnetic Therapy. Full Disclosure that I am in Sales, but this technology speaks much further to me as Ive lost a very close family member to suicide at the young age of 20. Miraculously, TMS took me out of the Operating Room which always always felt like a hell hole. I love what I do and absolutely love speaking to people about light, water, and magnetism.

PS: I've read epi paleo 5x's (no more room to highlight), printed and read almost every JK post, listened to every podcast multiple times, when I was a Gold member for a while I watched every video that I could.

I say this not as a to seem great in anyway, but I absolutely eat, sleep, and breathe this information. It's a true passion.

### Dean6789 — 2018-04-06T19:39:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-and-greetings-from-marina-del-rey-california.21150/post-238848>

There are a bunch of raw meat eaters meeting up for a potluck tomorrow Westchester, CA. Here's a copy of the invitation:

"POTLUCK this Saturday, April 7, at noon at 6700 W 83rd St., Westchester. We are playing the first hour of the Primal Workshop. See Aajonus on the screen, get valuable information to revitalize your health goals!

Please bring a raw food dish, preferably a meat dish enough for two to four people, so everyone can have some. A few desserts are good, but the point is to share food, ideas, and to contribute to each other, share knowledge. Even people who are bringing the video, or contributing in other ways are expected to bring a dish.

There are lots of ideas in the Recipe book, or the recipe videos. Check out Melissa’s Raw Gourmet website, or google a raw meat recipe!

We’d love to see your friends who just want to know what we are about, too!

See you Saturday!

The Folks at RA"

Feel free to come by if you're interested. I'll PM you with my phone number in case you have any questions.

### Phill the PT — 2018-04-26T06:38:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-australia.21224/post-239946>

Hey everyone my name's Phill and I'm a Personal Trainer living in Queensland Australia. Been following Jacks work for almost a year and really enjoy the subject although a lot of the time everything is still over my head.

The overall goal is to assist clients with practical applications of achieving optimal health and going much further than any personal trainer would in understanding the human body and how it operates on the much smaller scale.

I also want to help my partner who has JOBS syndrome and hyper iGe and know there will be a whole heap of expertise on this within the forum.

Looking forward to learning with everyone and hope you're all well.

Phill

# Thread: Mother, father, 3 children: our journey toward optimal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mother-father-3-children-our-journey-toward-optimal.21281/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MKelly — 2018-05-04T15:49:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mother-father-3-children-our-journey-toward-optimal.21281/post-240488>

Hello everyone -

Thank so much for this forum. It's incredibly helpful.

We joined the forum a few months ago and I've been re-arranging life to make being a part of this community a priority.

Why I'm posting here:
\- to connect with you
\- give myself accountability and track my progress
\- be a source of information for others

Why we joined:
Chronic Fatigue - mom
I have been chronically fatigued at various levels for the last 5 years. It is much better now that we moved out of a high-rise and into a house and started to implement the protocols suggested by Dr Kruse's research.
Weight gain and food intolerances - daughter
My 11 year old has sever gluten intolerance, dairy intolerance and a host of other more minor intolerances. She gains weigh, mostly in her stomach, very easily and is currently very over weight, most of it around her middle. She eats well and is active, she eats a million times better than all her friends and is heavier than all of them.
General health - dad, other children
My husband is an ox and is usually fine but has recently suffered from tinnitus and hearing loss. He has found since adopting the protocols his sleep is massively improved.
We're all N. European ancestry. Uncoupled all the way! Live at 48th

What we're doing:
Bought a JOOVV light (using less now that the sun has returned but Paris winters are very long and rainy)
cold baths - any suggestions on how to entice children to do this?
blue blocking glasses - mandatory after sundown but we should probably use them all the time we're on the laptop
restricted screen time in general

Next steps: (suggestions welcome)
figure out what labs to do for my daughter (and for me probably)
find a fun way to do CT outside now that it's summer

Slowly learning the science, 4th Phase of Water and the one by the scientist who went to law school, paradigm shifting books! The first made me realise I don't know anything, the second one made me understand how flawed mainstream science is, and how I gave it much more power over my decisions than it warrants. Lots of Dr Kruse podcasts and the Vermont talks. Still finding the blogs impenetrable. I'll get there

Thanks to you all for being here, for being pioneers in this field. I hang out with very smart, successful, progressive people and they ALL look at me like I'm crazy when I mention blue light toxicity. So I very much appreciate this community

### drezy — 2018-05-04T23:08:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mother-father-3-children-our-journey-toward-optimal.21281/post-240512>

> [MKelly said:](/goto/post?id=240488)
>
> cold baths - any suggestions on how to entice children to do this?
> ...
> find a fun way to do CT outside now that it's summer

The intolerances read like environment + deuterium issues to me.

Cold enticement probably depends on the kid's personality.

My son loved that one of his grandparents thought that cold water immersion = instant heart attack.

He likes to create things, so if I ever wanted him to eat a dish I'd have him make it. Once his time and energy were spent making a dish it was almost a 100% guarantee that he'd eat it. So I put him to work on our cold tub. He did like the fact that the spray foam chemical pack the we used urged "KEEP ALL SPRAY FOAM OUT OF REACH OF CHILDREN". He couldn't wait to operate the sprayer after seeing that:

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/ct-chamber-experiment-interested.19445/#post-214425](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/ct-chamber-experiment-interested.19445/#post-214425)

Way before mobile devices I remember spending time as a kid swimming. My cousins and I had a game. We'd hop out of the pool and pour cold cold well water on us to get really cold then jump back in the pool to feel how much warmer it felt. It became a game of who could cold hose themselves the longest before needing to run back to the pool. Then there was the Slip and Slide!

^^^ Wow! Those things are still selling out there. I might have to buy one...

Do you know the water temperature of your water supply?

48th latitude sounds rough and big old city life sounds rougher. Cold may help but I'm not sure Paris is going to be a very workable environment for your girl. I grew up at the 41st latitude. Between 10 cousins and 3 childhood friends that still live there (ages 30-55), about 70+% are dealing with a health condition. The younger ones are all dealing with either obesity, or some weird condition (like Urticaria), or both.

### shiran — 2018-05-05T08:03:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mother-father-3-children-our-journey-toward-optimal.21281/post-240543>

> [MKelly said:](/goto/post?id=240488)
>
> Hello everyone -
>

She eats well and is active, she eats a million times better than all her friends and is heavier than all of them.
> General health - dad, other children

> We're all N. European ancestry. Uncoupled all the way! Live at 48th
>
> What we're doing:
> Bought a JOOVV light (using less now that the sun has returned but Paris winters are very long and rainy)
> cold baths - any suggestions on how to entice children to do this?
> blue blocking glasses - mandatory after sundown but we should probably use them all the time we're on the laptop

> find a fun way to do CT outside now that it's summer

The first made me realise I don't know anything, the second one made me understand how flawed mainstream science is, and how I gave it much more power over my decisions than it warrants. Lots of Dr Kruse podcasts and the Vermont talks. Still finding the blogs impenetrable. I'll get there
>
> Thanks to you all for being here, for being pioneers in this field. I hang out with very smart, successful, progressive people and they ALL look at me like I'm crazy when I mention blue light toxicity. So I very much appreciate this community

Welcome!
I found that with children it is easier to put a screen protector on the computer and use IRIS and not use after sunset if possible.
Letting the kids dip their faces in a bowl with cold water with ice is a good start and a game they will really like.
Playing with water in the garden or fill A large water vessel or any activity that causes them to take the clothes off to be in the water and sun
Believe me, I took it as a project It's a challenge and not easy today with screens . i tried to think about how to get the children out of the house in any way possible, a vegetable garden ropes on the trees and wooden houses Any activity outside the house is a blessing

### MKelly — 2018-05-07T18:56:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mother-father-3-children-our-journey-toward-optimal.21281/post-240719>

Thank you Shiran and Drezy, I really appreciate the encouragement.
We just spent 9 days in Morocco, lots of sun and some days 3 hrs in the pool. I can already notice a difference in my daughter after months of rain at the 48th latitude...
We will start some outdoor family projects, we live outside the city so have some outdoor space to work with.
Thank you!

### MKelly — 2019-12-19T19:55:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mother-father-3-children-our-journey-toward-optimal.21281/post-283808>

I have not been updating my mitochondrial journey on here since I began. I thought my journey was not significant, that I wasn’t doing enough, that it wouldn’t add much to the community.

I now realize that is not true.

Looking back, I took my first deep dive into the mito world when I scheduled a skype consult with Dr Kruse in late 2016 (not sure he does those anymore, glad I got in there)

It was early morning in New Orleans and night time in France, where we were living. He insisted my husband join, which was extremely wise. He gave us a talking to that we will never forget, and got my husband on board in a way that I never could have. (It’s interesting how we often have the least amount of influence on the people closest to us… but that’s another post)

Dt Kruse’s first question to me was to ask what time it was. About 8pm. His next question was “where are your glasses?” YES. I got on a computer call with Dr Mitochondria at night with the LED overheads on, the screen on and no eye protection! I have learned since then…!!

The last few years have been slow but steady progress toward a healthier life. We are not black swan poster children. We have made many small changes that have added up over time. When a new world opens up I tend to dive in, become obsessive and live with a level of intensity that is not sustainable long term. As the intensity wears off, a core of new behaviors remain and those become part of daily life, without having to think about it too much.

I start by consuming information, then making changes at home but the real test of whether it will translate into

When I downloaded the Dminder app in October in France and it said “148 days until your next Vitamin D exposure”, I suddenly got it. “Honey, we are flying to Mexico for Christmas! There will be people from the internet on the beach. We will know them by their kiniki bathing suits.” And we did. We needed the Vitamin D but we also needed to meet some of this community face to face. It’s much easier to make big changes when you have met others working to do the same.

We should not underestimate the courage it takes to make such a trip, whether it’s in real life or virtual. For all of you who show up; on this forum, on the calls, on the beach, good for you!

Major change is a mind game, doing it alone usually ends in defeat.

At the end of this month I’m going to Florida to the VIP event. Another act of courage. It’s scary to look. It’s tempting to just accept my primary care doctor’s opinion, which is that I’m fine. I’m not sick, but I am not on the road to wellness and I can feel it. I’ll bring my labs and find out how many more changes we need to make, and how quickly.

I have friends around me, dying of cancer and lung disease. Women in the prime of their lives. It breaks my heart. And it motivates me to take radical responsibility.

### caroline — 2019-12-20T06:16:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mother-father-3-children-our-journey-toward-optimal.21281/post-283819>

Thanks for the great update.....it really is all about baby steps!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mother-father-3-children-our-journey-toward-optimal.21281/post-283821>

And it motivates me to take radical responsibility.\[/QUOTE\]

### Floyd Aldrich — 2018-05-06T21:20:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-maine.21292/post-240652>

I'm new  I'm also a wildlife photographer and most mornings I go out for 2-3 hrs and photograph birds and other wildlife.Now I understand more of the benefit of early exposure to the sun,I know it always feels good to be out then. Also I may be one of the few people without a cell phone.
Floyd

### Sangye Lhamo — 2018-05-07T04:17:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-maine.21292/post-240669>

Hi Im new in the last month. I guess my brain levels are completely off since I have no idea how my photo ended up sideways.
I work in a very stressful job due to the environment, not the work itself. Hoping to retire very soon.
Have been getting more morning sun and going outside as much as possible as work permits. Has helped my sleep tremendously especially after I purchased a pair of good quality blue blocker glasses. The cheaper pair I had for a year didn't help very much.
Still have so much to learn.
Have been getting a new symptom since 5 months starting with a clicking in my thumb. Causes a lot of pain when I use the muscle to bend the thumb but none if I manually bend it myself. I beginning to think it may be nerve related or muscle related more then calcification or osteophyte at the joint. Waiting for an X-ray. Also starting to get pain at the wrist on the nerve on thumb side of wrist. That started since last 2-3 days. Don't know what to do to stop this process. I seems to have so much inflammation in the boy but what I try doesn't help.
Anyways, Hi everyone.
Sangye ( pronounced Song-gay)

### caroline — 2018-05-07T05:13:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-maine.21292/post-240672>

Hi Floyd and welcome....

### caroline — 2018-05-07T05:20:26+0300

Hi Sangye and welcome ....what a lovely name.

Please start a journal in the optimal journal section and give Dr. K. some health history - also your Mom's and gran's.

Where do you live? work?

nnEMF???? at work and home

Have you watched Jack's 2 Vermont talks? Jack's podcasts? the live event with Jack and Jeremy? these are all extremely important and will give you a great head start on all the info here.

If you can manage it ....become a gold member and you will have access to all the blogs and webinars and the monthly live Q&A when you can ask Jack anything.

Stress is seriously debilitating ......when will you retire? Can you spend some time in the Yucatan and decompress? Your symptoms may all disappear .......

# Thread: Good to be apart of the tribe.  wx D
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-to-be-apart-of-the-tribe-wx-d.21433/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mike David — 2018-06-06T05:25:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-to-be-apart-of-the-tribe-wx-d.21433/post-242818>

Hello everyone I’m a stay at home dad from Perkasie PA. Spent my last ten years as an electrician and it felt like it was slowly killing me. Daily aches and pains were getting old and my teeth were rotting out of my head. That old picture of me standing inside a live 2000 amp distribution center for a hotel makes me cringe after reading Body Electric. I’ve been studying Jack’s work for 9 months, reading the books and listened to all the podcasts can’t get enough of quantum biology. I’m happy to report I no longer have aches and pains, got my teeth fixed and a kick ass tan. Thanks Jack for shining a light on the situation. I’ll be on the lookout for you when I’m in New Orleans for crime con next year hope to catch some rays with the man the myth and the legend.

### caroline — 2018-06-07T10:11:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-to-be-apart-of-the-tribe-wx-d.21433/post-242899>

welcome Mike ..... sounds like you are nailing all this!

You are doing great things for yourself with a spill over effect for your family.

How is it going with the family?

### Mike David — 2018-06-07T14:20:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-to-be-apart-of-the-tribe-wx-d.21433/post-242919>

Thanks learn something new every day. Or some cases every minute. I love this stuff eating it up. Friend said I need more biologist friends. Lol

# Thread: Hello
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.21456/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### KrusinWitchie — 2018-06-10T13:45:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.21456/post-243135>

Hi, again. I created a new account to change my name. I had only been on the forums for about a month or two before this.

I’ve listened to the two Vermont talks and had the pleasure of seeing Jack at Vermont 2018.

After skipping around a bunch on the site, I’ve now printed out the first 50 or so blogs as well as the quilt. I am also reading The Body Electric.

I plan to start an optimal journal to track my progression as well as my fiancé’s.

For starters within the past few weeks:
\-fiancé’s snoring has drastically reduced. I used to punch him multiple times/night and I haven’t in a while now. He’s happy about that.
\-we’ve both started dreaming again. I remember reading somewhere that was a positive sign in terms of redox ?
\-we feel like we’re missing something throughout the day if we haven’t had shellfish for breakfast
\-we both seemed to have leaned out our midsections
\-fiancé’s knee pain is gone
\-fiancé’s eye strain is gone thanks to Ra Optics' glasses
\-my shitty vision is improving the more im in the sun and the less I’m inside
\-it seems like we have a higher capacity for BS, less bickering between us and less angst towards frustrating situations in general

Something we are both very aware of and would like to improve is our ability to connect with others. I think I read somewhere here or in the blogs about a shift happening where an individual noticed their nervousness before speaking publicly had greatly reduced. I’m looking forward to reading that again as I read through the blogs! My fiancé and I were both deeply affected by your candor and love of knowledge. Thank you Dr. Kruse! We look forward to connecting with you and your peeps.

I will update my signature to reflect the input wanted there and begin My Optimal Journal.

She certainly has some vibrant paintings but I’m not sure the colors are all that different than other artists who use strong, unusual colors? Kind of like that artist who somehow got crazy popular with the American masses, who was it—Kinkaide? I’m probably missing the point. Or maybe I can see some UV too so this looks normal to me?

The phosphenes are fascinating. I used to see them only in a dark room, but now I see the colors and patterns just closing my eyes anywhere.

I wonder if she ever used hallucinogens? Some people see colors in whole new ways afterwards. I haven’t gone there yet...

My brain is going around in enough circles already as it is.

Hi Phosphene,
Articles about her say that she sees 99 million more colors than the average human because of a 4th type of cone cell that extends color perception into the UV range. And people say humans can't see UV!!??
Here's an interesting article about her with pictures: [http://munsell.com/color-blog/tetrachromat-artist-concetta-antico/](http://munsell.com/color-blog/tetrachromat-artist-concetta-antico/)
It got me thinking that wouldn't it be interesting if you were a Tetrachromat like Concetta Antico, also.  The article said that female offspring of a Tetrachromat have color blind daughters.  Do you have any daughters?
I googled tetrachromat and ability to see phosphenes, but came up empty. However, absence of evidence does not indicate evidence of absence. It would be nice if a simple test existed to detect this ability.
I need to google Erwan Lecorre now as Caroline seems pretty excited about webinars with him.

### Sue-UK — 2018-06-27T12:10:33+0300

> Here's an interesting article about her with pictures: [http://munsell.com/color-blog/tetrachromat-artist-concetta-antico/](http://munsell.com/color-blog/tetrachromat-artist-concetta-antico/)
> It got me thinking that wouldn't it be interesting if you were a Tetrachromat like Concetta Antico, also.  The article said that female offspring of a Tetrachromat have color blind daughters.  Do you have any daughters?

A musing ….

I've only ever seen an aura once, it was the speaker at a conference of healers, meditators and dowsers. I think that my own brain waves became more synchronised, more coherent, because of who I was surrounded by. I've never managed to repeat the experience, but I see it as a moment of grace. It sometimes happens unexpectedly, but I can also change my colour perception through a meditation in the sun. The sun is strong at the moment, so when the sun gets above the tree line I know UV is in. If I lean back in the chair, with my feet grounded on the grass, keep my eyes closed and expose my eyelids face and neck to the sun for a few minutes, perhaps massaging my face, particularly round the forehead and facial bones (better with an ice cube...), then stand up, turn my back to the sun, and perhaps give my scalp above my visual cortex region a massage, then stop and slowly open my eyes. The sun is behind me and I can look at the trees and sky in front, and there's almost an ethereal glow. If I look down at the black cat, his coat seems to shimmer differently.

Perhaps her art doesn't necessarily reflect tetrachromacy or be something unique. Perhaps she's not portraying the world as it is, but as she is …

If you want to see your own aura, be on your grounding mat in the early morning when the cones are still "sleeping", but the rods can detect the low light. Just put your hand about one foot away from your face. Be ready for a light show. You will see little spikey lights that bristles around your fingers maybe .5 cm long. It will look like a cactus. You might even see tiny lights fly off. This is the physical level of auras. I knew my earthing pad was delivering electrons as I had not seen my hand look so alive before using it about 7 years ago. Unfortunately, people in the United States may have a problem plugging their grounding mat into the grounding hole as it most likely leads to dirty electricity off the grid.

Ugg! that's right. Condemning our daughter to color blindness does not seem like good evolvement.
O, yes, the pictures of Alex Grey are beautiful and put me in chills. I have seen this before, but did not know who the artist was. I once heard that if people had UV vision, they could see through walls. I have also heard that there are people who actually see the inside of the body and see the problems. When I was learning to take x-rays, I wished I could see inside to get the equipment in the right place. Real anatomy of people does not match the drawings inside the books. Parts can be all over the place and hearts on the right side instead of the left.
I had to disable the f.lux to see the colors of his paintings. It is set at 2100K now, nice and orangey.

[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/alex-grey-art-tattoo-ely-tattoo-alex-grey-art-modern-tattooing-tools-jpg.8113/) This is going to be a big ouchy in 5G.

### Sue-UK — 2018-06-28T11:24:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shalom-from-a-grandmother-in-israel-happy-to-be-here-and-forum.21475/post-244486>

> [Joyfun said:](/goto/post?id=244482)
>
> [@Sue-UK](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9658/)
>
> I have also heard that there are people who actually see the inside of the body and see the problems.

Woo territory alert  I won't have a rant about my experiences of people in action who think they can see inside me.

I have my doubts that anyone can actually see inside the body, but perhaps its more that if two bioenergetic fields are interacting, and by the viewer applying intention, the bioenergetic field brings back information that his or her brain interprets as a picture. Perhaps "imagine" becomes more of an "image in" (an internally generated simulation). A bit like a dowser's intuition progressing to not needing a pendulum. They might get an image or just "know." At the conference I had the experience of "knowing" that someone behind me was scanning my energy field, I turned round and saw a woman looking at me, and using a pendulum …It was more of a 2 bioenergetic systems interacting phenomena.

If she'd wanted to know something about me, she should have come up and asked me, and I had a choice as to whether to answer the question or not. But giving permission is still a risk. I think its so easy for someone to assume that the viewer/dowser (or however they get their information) is somehow "gifted" and take it for granted they know what they are talking about. Sometimes they can see what they expect to see, and their "advice" is based on what they know, when what they don't know might be more important …. And if appearances are deceptive and their bioenergetic field is worse than mine, I'm the one giving them an electron and biophoton free lunch.

> [Joyfun said:](/goto/post?id=244482)
>
> [@Sue-UK](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9658/)
>
> I once heard that if people had UV vision, they could see through walls.

I've wondered if before the electric light bulb everyone could use their own UV photons emitted through the eyes to see more in dim light. I can see how it would make survival sense. It reminds me of "Use it or lose it, but don't wear it out. " Tetrachomacy with effects on a daughter could be a case of wearing it out … ?

### Joyfun — 2018-06-28T14:47:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shalom-from-a-grandmother-in-israel-happy-to-be-here-and-forum.21475/post-244494>

[@Sue-UK](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9658/)

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=244486)
>
> Woo territory alert  I won't have a rant about my experiences of people in action who think they can see inside me.
>
> I have my doubts that anyone can actually see inside the body, but perhaps its more that if two bioenergetic fields are interacting, and by the viewer applying intention, the bioenergetic field brings back information that his or her brain interprets as a picture. Perhaps "imagine" becomes more of an "image in" (an internally generated simulation). A bit like a dowser's intuition progressing to not needing a pendulum. They might get an image or just "know." At the conference I had the experience of "knowing" that someone behind me was scanning my energy field, I turned round and saw a woman looking at me, and using a pendulum …It was more of a 2 bioenergetic systems interacting phenomena.
>
> If she'd wanted to know something about me, she should have come up and asked me, and I had a choice as to whether to answer the question or not. But giving permission is still a risk. I think its so easy for someone to assume that the viewer/dowser (or however they get their information) is somehow "gifted" and take it for granted they know what they are talking about. Sometimes they can see what they expect to see, and their "advice" is based on what they know, when what they don't know might be more important …. And if appearances are deceptive and their bioenergetic field is worse than mine, I'm the one giving them an electron and biophoton free lunch.
>
>
>
> I've wondered if before the electric light bulb everyone could use their own UV photons emitted through the eyes to see more in dim light. I can see how it would make survival sense. It reminds me of "Use it or lose it, but don't wear it out. " Tetrachomacy with effects on a daughter could be a case of wearing it out … ?

This is a hard one. The mother gets all the eye candy and the daughter gets the wrappers.

Hi Sue-UK,
I like your thoughts on how a person might "see" inside another's body. There is lots to chew on here. I like the quote of Einstein:

Who's to say that a person with the ability of seeing into another's body is perceiving at that vibrational level of the physical. We are such powerful beings who have forgotten what we are. Some things are not of the level of proof, but are confirmable and experiential. Just like you knew someone was behind you scanning your energy field and to turn around and see her with the "evidence" in her hand. That knowing is not necessarily "provable" but it sure was experiential.

# Thread: I Didn't Know Jack When I Didn't Know Jack
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-didnt-know-jack-when-i-didnt-know-jack.21491/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sara — 2018-06-19T22:41:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-didnt-know-jack-when-i-didnt-know-jack.21491/post-243816>

Hola, Good People …

I'm a 70-year-old SaddleTramp living in a wilderness area. I reside in a vintage Spartan trailer made of metal (thank my intuitive stars) and am on the verge of grounding it. The population here is around 3500 people across 6928 miles of territory known as Catron County in New Mexico. My immediate location consists of around 500 people. We have 2 or 3 cell towers many miles away and I understand that the Forest Service would not approve the installation of 5G. I'm surrounded by pine forests, which are one of the most healing of Natures' growths. I take no meds, do not fall ill and the cancer I was diagnosed with 42 years ago is non-existent. I did not seek medical help, btw. I'm a proponent for proper hydration above all things that we put into our bodies and I love the sun. Almost everyone else here is sick.

I'm grateful to the people who led me here.

Thank you.

### caroline — 2018-06-20T03:03:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-didnt-know-jack-when-i-didnt-know-jack.21491/post-243837>

Hi Sara ....I love your storey! but - why is everyone around you sick?????? Did I take that too literally?

Please start a journal in the journal section and tell us lots more.....

### Sara — 2018-06-20T16:17:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-didnt-know-jack-when-i-didnt-know-jack.21491/post-243873>

Journal? Maybe I can't find the journal section until I become a paid member, which I can't do until next month? But to expound on the story of here:

Oh, I do mean literally sick: COPD, rheumatoid arthritis, auto-immune def, thyroid conditions, heart disease, diabetes, misshaped bodies on a par with walmart in between overweight and obesity; the meth and alcohol community that won’t quit with some psychopathic results. People here hold on to their diseases; it defines them, gives them something to talk about and, of course, it’s all genetic. There is no talking them through it; no alternative method toward health except medication. Food allergies are rampant here. I used to try to talk to people about hydration and was told by all that they drink a lot of water, although I never, not ever, see them drink water; I see them drink soda, Arizona and other teas, juices, energy drinks, coffee, but not water. In my little neck of the woods, we have an 89-year-old geezer, a friend, who has prostate cancer and has been self-medicating with milk of magnesia for years, evacuating every 3-5 days. He has eaten a SAD diet all his life. One day, he came in to say goodbye; he would be dead in a matter of days because he could no longer poop. A friend and I rehabilitated his diet, put him on pro-biotics, digestive enzymes and psyllium awash with proper water and he’s mostly a happy camper now. At 89, he has his complaints, but he’s pooping away and no longer making declarations of impending death. And, astonishingly, in this outdoor environment, most people live indoors. I am completely perplexed by this. We have some people who go for constitutional walks every day. They walk on the road and never think of walking in the forest, which is right behind us and is, basically, pine with high healing qualities. There are 2-track roads out there; easy pathways on which to tread.

### Inger — 2018-06-20T17:30:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-didnt-know-jack-when-i-didnt-know-jack.21491/post-243880>

Welcome Sara!

Well everyone can start a journal, I guess, no need to be a paid member for that?

Sounds interesting, your observations! Yes, what did it help to live in the wilderness, if you are not outside?
Is it some kind of hippie community or what is it where you live  Why are people living there? Do they have some common goal?

Did you tell that 89 year old gentleman to tan nude too?

Please keep on sharing your observations and your experiences, so glad you found us

### Sara — 2018-06-20T23:32:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-didnt-know-jack-when-i-didnt-know-jack.21491/post-243912>

The “old gentleman” is an outdoorsman. He’s out chopping in his wood pile, gardening, puttering and generally finding stuff to do. If I suggested he do it naked, he’d have a cow. Sometimes, when I bring him his Holy Tea in the evening, he’s already abed in his pajamas and asks me to wait to enter until he’s back in his bedroom. Lol! This is outlaw territory, one of the last bastions of the wild west, if not the last, where a law was passed that all male residents of legal age must own a gun; pretty sexist if you ask me. The southern tip of The Hole In The Wall Trail begins just down the road from me and Geronimo was born on the west fork of the Gila River, about 4 miles from here as the crow flies. There’s also the old Pony Express Mail Trail. We’re a wild bunch, leftovers from old ranching families that don’t ranch anymore. We have a large elderly community with a lot of die-offs and many residents who are on disability; a lot of opioid use. It’s the outsiders, such as myself, who come in that give this place life. And why do we come? We are drawn here for different reasons: health reasons; anti-societal reasons; anti-government reasons. Like that. I was drawn here as the last refuge of the end of the world as we’ve known it. The stage of the entire world seems to reside on the other side of these southern Rockies. It’s like a great wall of sorrows. In short, we are outcasts by choice. We outfit and guide dudes into the wilderness for hunts and for pleasure. We have our loggers who seem to have begun to poach wood to sell to wood-burning stove users and we have our elk antler gatherers. We’re a tourist mecca like a ghost town, consisting of Anglos and Mexicans, not a Native person in sight; they’ve vacated the territory, through force, for the Mescalero Rez across the state to the Southeast. It’s quite a beautiful rez.

There is a very small, almost non-existent group of like-minded people who are aware of modern day realities; geo-engineering, emf pollution; gmo seeding; 5G; etc. Most others cannot comprehend what the hell is going on. Try as one might, the battle for awareness is already lost.

Where do I find the journal section?

### Jenelle — 2018-06-21T00:13:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-didnt-know-jack-when-i-didnt-know-jack.21491/post-243915>

Click on "forums" at top left ~ click on "my optimal journal" in the meet & greet section. Welcome!

# Thread: My personal introduction
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-personal-introduction.21494/>

### Corey Nelson — 2018-06-20T02:26:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-personal-introduction.21494/post-243832>

Hello,

I've known of Jack for about 7-8 years and had always thought his protocols seemed valid and very interesting (it seems so hard to deny that light and cold will alter physiology and gene expression - and little did I know there was so much more to it) but for most of that time, my youthful arrogance kept me from diving in.

As you will see in my signature a lot of that arrogance caught up with me, in 2014-16 or so. A couple of years of putting my life back on track, and I began to investigate and apply the protocols, somewhat slowly.

Late last year I said "why not" and went into deep dive mode; I've been all in since then, and everything I've learned and applied has worked phenomenally well for me (also see signature) and always leads me to the next step. Not to mention the profound truths that really undercut a lot of what I thought I knew.

That's fine; being vulnerable and brand new is a great place to grow.

One of my next steps is to become a gold or platinum member, and I'm pretty sure eventually I'll go for the Quantum Health Practitioner listing with whatever that entails. Currently able to afford the Patreon only but grateful for everything.

At the moment I'm just honored to be here and interact with a community built around this remarkable, abrasive, and kind individual's ideas and wisdom

Corey

PS: Does anyone know of a way to see posts \*I\* have "Liked" or another convenient way to sort/create a favorites list of individual posts, within the forum? Still exploring the mechanics. Thanks.

The three legged stool is all encompassing and is all we need to get started and keep going.....

I love your story of acceptance and denial and then arriving at vulnerable and brand new and ready to have skin in the game!

Being brand new and vulnerable and open is a great place to be - I remember the day very well!

looking forward to your journal....

sorry I don't know the answer to your tech question - but someone else will I am sure.

### Corey Nelson — 2018-06-20T19:21:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-personal-introduction.21494/post-243894>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=243884)
>
> Welcome Corey
> Excited to hear more about your deep dive (awesome  ), details and what you changed - what the results where

Thank you.

Deep is kind of a relative term I suppose, I'm as deep as I can cognitively get for now but not pushing myself in an unkind way. I haven't gone as deep as I plan to in terms of understanding - definitely getting there slowly and steadily though, and using many practical recommendations every single day (mostly all in my signature).

Moving on to results - I think it's hard to really pinpoint exactly what it feels like having better "quality of life" but that seems to be the most noticeable overall effect. For sure I feel more patient, accepting, and happy with less (I think I've heard Jack K. talk about this). There's really a great sense of belonging and being pretty happy with my situation even though it isn't "perfect." Less obsession. This is both a great effect of healing and a route to further healing.

I really appreciate Buddhist philosophy and to some extent practice (though, I have been practicing less meditation recently, just feels like the correct thing at the moment - following my intuition here) and so to me being less obsessed and more content are some of the real keys in life.

More objectively speaking I am noticing a massive improvement in mood, better sleep, sex drive is way way up (can easily be with my wife every single day as she prefers, which was never the case before in our 2.5 years of marriage - speaking as a guy that's a major change!), slow and steady improvements in energy, and symptom relief from erratically occurring heart palpitations and angioedema and urticaria. Previously I'd get swelling episodes where my lip would blow up about triple size and my body would get large, itchy, angry hives. Swelling now happens, albeit to a lesser degree, if and only if I end up sitting near a router for more than an hour or so, or heavily use a cell phone.

After listening to Matt Maruca some I also quit exercising (had been lifting with kettlebells once per week, running once a week, hiking once a week, and doing a mobility type day) because I realized I was way too fatigued and foggy to be in the right place to do that. That's been another great thing for me, just parting with that habit as an absolutely necessary part of life and knowing it's there to enjoy when I'm well.

Quit caffeine for the most part (I've had it maybe once every 2-4 weeks this year, usually in tea form) and that really improved sleep but also increased brain fog. I still feel like I'm usually at about 50-60% of my former peak cognitive capacity but I can find other things to enjoy and appreciate in life. I just feel happier and I can be happy at this level. That's currently, along with my fatigue, my most noticeable symptom, though. I just don't have the depth I used to intellectually or it takes a lot longer to get there, and I can't really focus on things for more than an hour or two at a time before I get fatigued mentally and need to do something restful.

Actually, I should clarify - 50-60% of former absolute peak is enough to live a good and productive life, and it may be ideal to be here most of the time with occasional bursts of insight and productivity, definitely better than trying to always force the ceiling higher and stay at peak all the time. Also, I think I am currently more likely to listen to myself and my body when I need a rest or a break - after years of working through pain and discomfort and basically creating a neoliberal inhuman existence for myself (partly out of motivation to succeed and partly just having something to prove). Very unhealthy habits of mind that I think are absolutely socially conditioned. Huge paradigm shift, not having much to prove. SO despite sounding a bit burnt out still I don't really FEEL that way, I just have a feeling of being realistic and it is often the case that I back away from something requiring intense focus after not so long - if it changes, great, but that's where I am.

Based on what had happened and the foolishness I was engaged in going back to 2011-2014 - use of modafinil and an Rx stimulant, absolute caffeine abuse, blue light way too often (I knew better but got complacent and stopped wearing my Uvexes I had at the time), EMFs 24/7, some really serious life changing trauma to cap it all off - I'm lucky I only got out with what I did.

If it takes years to level off and be "healed" that's okay!

Oh, I also restarted cannabis in 2015 after a 10 year period of having quit it, and it wasn't a bad crutch - helped with the trauma and (I now know) regulating mitochondrial function despite blue light and EMF damage - but I got a big wakeup call in the form of some near fatal cannabinoid hyperemesis episodes (body temp dropped to 95f at one point, dehydration making me so weak I could barely get help to go to ER) - and now that I'm entirely off the weed again (and glad to be) that's a new healing chapter, too.

I noticed that most of my issues were extremely "nonlinear" in their onset and wow, was that a wakeup call. I really am fascinated and horrified to see what begins happening individually and societally as these devastating and rapid effects begin to leap down to the next lower age groups of young "millennials" and "generation Y."

My old neighbor who's a year older than me, when I was living in an EMF hell (landlord in attached premise had dozens of wifi network devices and there were 3 smart meters, the suites were partially below ground), had the following all within the space of a year: hysterectomy, stroke with loss of function in one eye (now regained), and a mental breakdown where she got the police called and was evicted for attacking her boyfriend. Really sad. She is/was a gamer so there's your case study in 24/7 EMF + PM blue light.

I think I covered the rest in my signature but happy to share whatever I can, for curiosity sake or in case it helps anyone.

# Thread: 21 y/o newbie from Canada
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/21-y-o-newbie-from-canada.21527/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ossa — 2018-06-27T23:48:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/21-y-o-newbie-from-canada.21527/post-244466>

Hows it going everyone, Im Alex. Just wanted to introduce myself and tell my story.

The path that lead me here all started back in highschool when i started to get into health and fitness. I grew up an overweight kid and had a friend that was always in amazing shape. So one day when i was around 15 he started taking me on long distance runs. 3km to start which eventually turned into 10km. This lasted for a few months over that summer and i lost a little bit of weight. Eventually i started getting into weight lifting and this is where the problems started happening. I got into it seriously and not knowing really what i was doing decided to push myself as hard as i could because i was told and thought this was how you got results. I would take excersized sush as pushups, pullups, squats, all the way to complete failure for multiple sets and i did this for about a year until i completely collapsed from exhaustion. What started happening over time was that i became more and more exhausted. Started having to take multiple hour naps everysingle day. My libido completely vanished and i became really depressed. I stopped when i was about 18 and recovered a little bit.

Eventually i started learning about calories and the diet side to fitness and how to workout properly but unfortunately still had to take it easy because of how exhausted i was. I was able to squeeze through highschool barely. Ive always been a very curious person so eventually started learning about intermittent fasting and circadian rhythms. Started listening to scientists such as Dr. Satchin Panda which eventually led me here. Dr . Kruse’s work makes more sense to me by far than anything else ive ever stumbled upon.

One thing i havent mentioned yet is that my whole life i have had the terrible habit of staying up on computers/ tvs playing video games up until 12:30-1am for my whole life up until recently. I think this explains the reason i have had mental health issues my whole life, most came in the form of mainly severe anxiety and suicidal thoughts. Ive been on three different medications over the years and had huge struggle getting off all of them. Ive struggled with lots of daytime sleepiness and fatigue for as long as i can remember. Since discovering Dr. Kruse i have made huge changes to my lifestyle such as; lights out at sunset, wearing blue blockers at night and while looking at electronics, started the leptin rx (as i have suffered from binge eating disorders), and getting sun everymorning as well as grounding. Ive always had the problem of not feeling sleepy at all at night and ive started to notice small improvements. I understand it will take a while to get well again.

Ive went to my family doctor a few times in the past to try and get help for my fatigue problmes and all my lab results came back fine except for the fact that i was on the very low end for normal testosterone levels.

Im hoping by applying all that i learn here and from Dr. Kruse i will finally be able to feel and be healhy once and for all. Thanks for reading. ~ Alex

I was recently diagnosed with systemic scleroderma which in turn sent me scouring through everything I could to figure out what could help my quality of life. My searches brought me here. I have been in awe of everything I'm reading. I also have a daughter with williams syndrome and mid-aortic syndrome and we have medical bills out our ears with actually nothing to show for it (her quality of life is not improving with all the medical specialists we see). My eyes have been opened and I cannot wait to dive in and change both of our lives for the better. I can't thank Dr. Kruse and all of you enough for sharing all of this information.

### caroline — 2018-06-30T03:16:23+0300

Hi Tara and welcome ....to you and your daughter.

Do you have Jack's book? The leptin RX ...it is the first couple of years of the blog to get you going!

Is that you in the picture? Horses are amazing - Jack's wife Sandy loves horses.

You may want to learn more about Jack's new venture - The farm ....click on "contact us" at the bottom right hand of this page.

If you can swing it at all - become a "Gold" member. There is a live Q&A when you can ask Jack anything. The last one went for 4 hours and 17 minutes. We have a thread about the June Q&A - have a look. \[in the cave\]

In my opinion you can't afford NOT to be a gold member .......you will also be able to get a consult with Jack. You will save tons of money on all the specialists you are seeing - with no improvement.

Good luck to both of you ......baby steps!

### Katie Durham — 2019-08-30T15:26:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rf-meter.21546/post-276859>

Coming up to my 1 year anniversary here and I \*just\* discovered how much radiation a wireless keyboard and mouse can generate. "Just" because I let myself be blinded by the convenience and never checked.

I brought my ENV RD-10 to work. (I have a few meters but this is the one I tend to reach for first for spot checks.) I have tested other areas of my office before. This time I put it between me and the keyboard and mouse. Holy moly! I was really surprised by the mouse. Any movement sets it off. I put in a request for wired. If my company won't pay for it I'll do it myself.

Retirement in 4 years. Sooner if I don't like what I'm experiencing.

I don't know how accurate the Cornet 88T is with LF and ELF. i want to get a separate meter for measuring Low frequencies. Do you know how I'd ground my keyboard and mouse?

Anyone know anything about having a car bluetooth removed. Should I just bring it to a shop? or is it something pretty simpl I can do. I've looked up online about having it removed, and everything just shows how to get Bluetooth in your car lol. I drive a lot, and it reads pretty high with my cornet.

### Katie Durham — 2019-08-30T15:46:29+0300

Wow! 30 minutes later, I'm now wired! The mouse/keyboard aren't setting off my meter at all (RF range). Kind of junky equipment but I'll get used to it.

The IT guy who brought it up had some familiarity because a friend of his is a 5g tower installer and is getting zapped. I demonstrated my meter for the IT guy, who I hope appreciates better what we're expected to endure here at work.

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-08-30T19:04:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rf-meter.21546/post-276881>

> [Nrelax11 said:](/goto/post?id=276860)

> My keyboard and mouse give off a lot of low frequency in guass mode. They are both wired, so I need to do something about that. I don't know how accurate the Cornet 88T is with LF and ELF. i want to get a separate meter for measuring Low frequencies. Do you know how I'd ground my keyboard and mouse?
>
> Anyone know anything about having a car bluetooth removed. Should I just bring it to a shop? or is it something pretty simpl I can do. I've looked up online about having it removed, and everything just shows how to get Bluetooth in your car lol. I drive a lot, and it reads pretty high with my cornet.

I am somewhat skeptical of products that claim to measure RF and ELF in a combo meter. I use an RF meter and Gaussmeter separately to measure these fields.

Assuming your computer is a laptop, you could try shielding it with ELF shielding materials like Harapad and Wraptop to reduce EMF exposure (similar shielding products are available for desktop computers too).

[http://www.emfwise.com](http://www.emfwise.com) has helpful info on grounding computers and other electrical devices (but if you have dirty electricity in house you may inadvertently bring in stray voltage this way). Also, even if you have a wired (ethernet) connection to computer (using no Wi-Fry router) it is possible your computer has original factory computer settings set to enable Wi-Fry (i.e., your computer has its own internal Wi-Fry antenna and unless it is disabled it is contantly barking out RF microwaves vainly to try to connect to an external Wi-Fry source).

It actually my work computer, but I believe its connected via Ethernet cable because the RF readings aren't high in my office. The RF readings go up a tiny bit near my computer, but not much compared to my phone or a laptop connected via WIFI. Its a desktop, so I'll look at that site for shielding. Thank you!

### Alex97232 — 2019-09-01T20:08:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rf-meter.21546/post-276966>

> [Nrelax11 said:](/goto/post?id=276897)
>
> The usually peaks at 0.45 mW/m2 and gauss 0.6mG

Hi. Consider that .45 milliWatts converts to 450 microWatts to compare your measurements
with various studies that have been done:
\_The Bioinitiative 2012 recommendation for power density limit is 3-6 microWatts/m2.
\_SBM 2008 notes "Severe Concern" for sleeping areas measuring 10-1,000 microWatts/m2.
\_The Oberfranken study evaluated medical complaints of 356 people with long term radiation
exposure in their homes. Above 100 microWatts/m2, only 5-6% of the people did NOT have
adverse health effects.
Quoting Drezy: "Once you see, you can't unsee".

# Thread: Hello!  SO happy to have found this!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-so-happy-to-have-found-this.21612/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kellyjwarren — 2018-07-10T23:59:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-so-happy-to-have-found-this.21612/post-245570>

I am in the process of receiving a 20-year-overdue Biotoxin Illness diagnosis, following Lyme disease in 1997 (not properly diagnosed or treated until 2001 when I was bedridden). After trying just about everything to restore my health and relieve pain/dysfunction with extremely limited success, I am hopeful that I finally will have a correct diagnosis and a path forward!

I would like to dive headfirst into a restorative eating plan, and have a few questions about the basics I have read about the Epi-Paleo plan:

1\. Do oysters have to be raw? Cooked? Canned?
2\. I have my own flock of free-range chickens...How do I know if I can/should consume their eggs on this?
3\. I am hoping to receive a copy of the book soon, but in the meantime is there a summary post of all the recommended foods? Is coconut milk and coconut meat part of the diet or just coconut oil?
4\. Other than cilantro, are there suggested or banned greens to consume? Does high-quality cilantro essential oil work as well?

In addition, what are Dr. Kruse's thoughts on the Shoemaker protocol? I have a TGF-beta > 12,000, low CSF glucose, and central diabetes insipidus. Still waiting on other labs (C3, C4, CD profiles, and others...). While I have read that DI can be caused by biotoxins, I have had multiple brain MRIs since 2013 showing a dangling fragment of pituitary. My Cleveland Clinic MD thinks my severe postpartum hemorrhages incited an autoimmune lymphocytic hypophysitis that continues to attack what remains. (No intracranial HTN causing PES...remaining gland is not flattened.)

Any other quick-start suggestions you may have are greatly appreciated! I will be reviewing the Leptin and CT information as recommended in the welcome video.

Fingers crossed and mind open,

Kelly

### caroline — 2018-07-11T02:58:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-so-happy-to-have-found-this.21612/post-245575>

Hi Kelly and welcome....
Please start a journal in the optimal journal section and give Dr. K. your Mom's and Gran's health history as well.

Where do you live? work? your post code is important.

Until you get Jack's book which is the first couple of years of the blog....listen to all his podcasts, especially the nourish Vermont ones - there are three now. These are extremely important..... as well as all Jack's other podcasts. They are a source of fast and easier to understand information.

Might I suggest that you become a "gold" member. You will have access to the monthly webinars and the follow up live Q&A when you can ask Jack anything. The June Q&A lasted for 4 hours and 17 minutes! We have a thread about that one - please have a look. The Q&A's alone are worth the price of membership.

Lots of people say they can't afford it ...but ....you have been sick for a long time - and now you have found the right place for healing. It won't be easy or fast .......you will be on a tremendous healing/learning curve.

Jackis all about the three legged stool ......light, water and magnetism. This won't cost you a thing.

Get outside every morning for the sunrise, with as little clothes on as possible, with barefoot on the ground. Make sure to see every sunset too. This is all about resetting your circadian rhythm.

On the home page there are easy start guides. Have a look at the leptin reset. Also read about C/T - it is a game changer.

There is a welcome from Jack on the home page and how to get started.

Dr. Kruse has also started a thread about the importance of newbies - please read.

Don't be afraid to ask questions if you don't know something ...... There are lots of great people here to help ....and Dr. K. reads journals.

Also - there is a little book by John Ott .....Health and light - please read

### WalterNL — 2018-07-11T12:41:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-so-happy-to-have-found-this.21612/post-245610>

Welcome. Check out all the Rx's in my signature.
You'll probably get overloaded with information. It will take many years to understand a fraction of all this, but you can get practical today.

### kellyjwarren — 2018-08-12T02:26:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-so-happy-to-have-found-this.21612/post-248276>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=245575)
>
> Hi Kelly and welcome....
> Please start a journal in the optimal journal section and give Dr. K. your Mom's and Gran's health history as well.
>
> Where do you live? work? your post code is important.
>
> Until you get Jack's book which is the first couple of years of the blog....listen to all his podcasts, especially the nourish Vermont ones - there are three now. These are extremely important..... as well as all Jack's other podcasts. They are a source of fast and easier to understand information.
>
> Might I suggest that you become a "gold" member. You will have access to the monthly webinars and the follow up live Q&A when you can ask Jack anything. The June Q&A lasted for 4 hours and 17 minutes! We have a thread about that one - please have a look. The Q&A's alone are worth the price of membership.
>
> Lots of people say they can't afford it ...but ....you have been sick for a long time - and now you have found the right place for healing. It won't be easy or fast .......you will be on a tremendous healing/learning curve.
>
> Also - there is a little book by John Ott .....Health and light - please read

Thank you, Caroline!

I received the Epi-Paelo Rx book from the library, and am currently in my second reading while taking notes/creating questions. Will try and reserve Ott's book next.

I will look for the optimal journal section online to get my personal one started. I plan on becoming a gold member in the future, but am currently an unemployed single parent trying desperately to make ends meet until I am well enough to return to work.

More lab results are in... elevated MMP-9, lymphocytes, and C4a... and an entire host of parasites, yeast, mold, fungi, and bacteria in my "sterile" spinal fluid, which helps to explain the low CSF glucose result. My best guess is this is secondary to leaky gut caused by CIRS/Biotoxin Illness. My follow-up appt is on Tuesday for the treatment plan.

In the meantime, I am doing the Leptin Rx, have been watching sunrises and sunsets, walking barefoot outdoors (which I often do anyway), and reading all I can about the best daily activities/nutrition for healing. I was pleased to see mention of Shoemaker's protocol in Epi-Paleo Rx. After 20 years of debilitating chronic illness following Lyme disease, I think I need some major detoxing for the lifestyle changes to work best for me. Luckily, progress is being made in the treatment of CIRS based on a person's specific genetics that create the biotoxin accumulation susceptibility. I'm curious if Dr. Kruse is aware of this development and what his thoughts are.

# Thread: Losing weight with nutrition
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/losing-weight-with-nutrition.21644/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shely Propst — 2018-07-16T01:20:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/losing-weight-with-nutrition.21644/post-245944>

Hey Everyone,

New to the thread and Jack Kruse and I’m hooked. I may not understand all he says but it makes sense!

I’ve listened to all Vermont talks—twice and every podcast I can find. What brought me to this was that I recently came off my antidepressant —and two drugs that I went on because of some of my side effects from my antidepressant (high blood pressure and reflux). My sleep has been even more screwed up and my stomach issue (IBS) is back.

I’m getting my AM and PM sun—and all my Vitamin D from the sun using the dminder app. I live in KS.

My question is about calories in vs calories out and I’m a total believer that it’s more than calories in vs calories out. I’ve been a personal trainer and “nutritionist” for 12 years. I have a nutrition coach as well. I’ve seen many lose weight and many not lose weight. I’ve increased people’s calories and they lose weight. Etc. I’ve lost weight on cuts and trying to lower my body fat for competitions.

My question: (not sure how to phrase it). What is going on in the mitochondria and respiratory protons when someone loses weight—-without altering their environment?

I have people who’ve lost 70lb+ and I’ve watched others plateau—more often than lose weight. So what is going on inside—hormonalky—or whatever else when nutrition is altered?

Again, I do believe nutrition is only 1/3. For many reasons. I also know how to manipulate my own body to lose weight too.

### caroline — 2018-07-16T03:07:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/losing-weight-with-nutrition.21644/post-245959>

Hi Shely and welcome.

Please start a journal in the optimal journal section and give Dr. K. health history and your Mom's and Gran's too.

where do you live? work?

The Vermont talks answer your questions.

Do you have Jack's book?

### Sue-UK — 2018-07-16T17:08:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/losing-weight-with-nutrition.21644/post-245983>

> [Shely Propst said:](/goto/post?id=245944)
>
> I have people who’ve lost 70lb+ and I’ve watched others plateau—more often than lose weight. So what is going on inside—hormonalky—or whatever else when nutrition is altered?
>
> Again, I do believe nutrition is only 1/3. For many reasons. I also know how to manipulate my own body to lose weight too.

Hi and welcome to the forum

A good thread to read is this one [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/your-first-post-to-read-here-at-jackkruse-com.17829/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/your-first-post-to-read-here-at-jackkruse-com.17829/) Notice that the only mention of food in the list is seafood at number 4. and "the order of the recipe is critical to the success."

A tool that you may find useful too is the quiz at [https://jackkruse.com/reality-5-scompys-quantum-quiz-mito-hacking-life/](https://jackkruse.com/reality-5-scompys-quantum-quiz-mito-hacking-life/). It will give you an idea of the types of technology to avoid or mitigate. For 70 lb overweight clients the intermittent fasting wouldn't come before they've regained leptin sensitivity (lots of info on leptin resistance on the public blogs - just do a search), but it gives an overview of the issues. Any 5G rollout is a whole new ballgame, but it could help with the basics.

I'd suggest investing in a gold membership. The webinars and Q and A's transformed my understanding of the mitochondrial dynamics, sleep etc and may also help you "fine tune" your own programme and individual advice to clients based on their own n=1 context.

### kris90 — 2018-07-19T22:33:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/losing-weight-with-nutrition.21644/post-246232>

It's pretty simple. People who lose weight from a calories/nutrition perspective: calorie restriction = more body fat being tapped into. It will always work short term, but long term depends on leptin sensitivity/resistance. It activates part of a metabolic pathway, but needs to be yoked to the environment. Intermittent Fasting WITHOUT calorie restriction activates the same pathway.

Your best blog to read: [https://jackkruse.com/the-holy-trinity-ct-4/](https://jackkruse.com/the-holy-trinity-ct-4/)

The environment is the only way to make calorie restriction occur naturally: Cold and UV/IR light do it. Can you still lose weight by restricting calories in a blue light/nnEMF world? Of course. This is called stem cell depletion, and most will end up binging and gaining the weight back as a protective mechanism, but those who have the "willpower" to keep going will end up looking like a superhero on the outside, while they drop dead of cancer or a heart attack in their 4th decade of life.

### JanSz — 2018-07-21T20:16:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/losing-weight-with-nutrition.21644/post-246363>

(high blood pressure and reflux). My sleep has been even more screwed up and my stomach issue (IBS) is back.

I’m getting my AM and PM sun—and all my Vitamin D from the sun using the dminder app. I live in KS.
\============================================================
Sun harden your skin first.

In my view there is no need to talk about dminder except possibly when in tropics.
Every where else you will newer get too much sun.

/
///////////////
Treat high blood pressure with more sunlight exposure.
Treat sleep problems with additional sunlight exposure.
.....
It helps to know one's homocysteine.
Keep it at about 6.
Treat it diligently when 8 or more.

/////

stomach issue (IBS)

Gut flora is correlated with your sun exposure (ie, not with the food you eat).

Eat what your gut flora expect to be fed with.

...........

# Thread: I love this group!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-love-this-group.21873/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dr. Jon Motley — 2018-08-19T07:03:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-love-this-group.21873/post-248780>

Hello, it is really great to Dr. Kruse and this group!

I became interested in light and energy when I started Chiropractic college (1995), I read The Body Electric, and gave a presentation to my class in my first year on Holograms, Homeopathy, and Acupuncture on how they worked energetically. I thought that the Acupuncture Meridians were step down transformers for cosmic energy (light), turning it into a dc current in the body. The presentation went over everyone's head, including the teacher, lol.

Later I learned of **Dr. Christopher Hills** who wrote several books on the nature of light color and consciousness, which I am still trying to process, because his views on color and light are so different from what we are taught. I was wondering if Jack has heard of his work, **Nuclear Evolution: The discovery of the Rainbow Body**, I think it is something he would understand. Out of Dr. Hills work came the Heartmath group, which I use in practice.

In 1996 I discovered Applied Kinesiology and Dr. George J. Goodheart Jr., where I learned about muscle testing. Since then, I have been practicing in Manilla, Philippines.

From Applied Kinesiology my perspective on the diet was to correct abnormal function of the digestive system through proper diet, once that was working I had patients follow a Keto diet. After listening to Jack I realize that a lot of what I have been doing is a temporary thing, which is why many people tend to get better for a while, then they regress into their old patters and problems. I am in the process of education my patients on the Leptin Rx and CT now.

A bit about me: I am 51, born in Denver, Co., lived all over the midwest to California. Father was Italian and Cherokee Indian, Mother was a big mix of Western European (traced her family to coming off the mayflower). Do not know my Haplogroup.

Was always skinny, even when I moved to the Philippines, never wore shoes, swam everyday, and always ate higher fats and proteins, although I snacked a lot. Then I married and after the first year I blew up like a balloon. Since then I have gone up and down with the weight and currently sit at 210, at a height of 5'9".

I was an avid video game player for years, with many hours glued to the computer screen, which has dropped off dramatically with the finding of this group.

I have always sweat profusely with mild exertion (same as my father), which gets worse if i drink coffee, was foggy in my thinking and often had to grasp for words, even though I knew what I was trying to say. Had a low energy level, which I would try to bring up with coffee in the morning, I smoke golden virginia tobacco, use hemp papers, no loss of sex drive with the wife which has always been high, I do not exercise, but my work on patients keeps me fairly fit (I have a website with some videos of patient treatment [www.chiropracticphilippines.net](http://www.chiropracticphilippines.net)).

I have always been a night owl, often staying up to 2am in the morning, when I would go to bed my mind would often stay active and not allow sleep. sleep has never been restful, and always woke up early, 7-9am, sometimes crashing at around 3-5pm and napping 3-4 hours. I have always practiced mindfulness and introspection, or have tried too.

After discovering Jack and this site I have been studying like mad to catch up on what i have been missing. Printing out tons of information because I just hate reading off the pc.

I get out into morning sun at 8am for 15-30 min while grounding.
Eat breakfast after that.
Been eating seafood, Tuna, Salmon, shrimp for lunch and dinner, fresh eggs from our chickens in the morning.
Drinking ice cold water after meal.
always take cold showers as it is usually hot here, swim almost daily in cold pools, beginning ice baths in the next few days.
Bought BlueBlockers glasses and wear them religiously when indoors around lights, tv's, pc's, cellphones.
When not posting or treating patients, I am outside reading and getting the sun.

Any thoughts on how I am doing would be much appreciated, wife thinks I am crazy... again, lol.

Again, thank you Jack for this wonderful site!

### caroline — 2018-08-19T08:26:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-love-this-group.21873/post-248781>

### Nick004662 — 2018-08-21T04:14:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-in-brisbane-australia-daily-habits.21888/post-248961>

Hello fellow Mitochondriac's!! A bit about me, SInce my teenage years I've really struggled with ADHD and was medicated with Dexamphetamine for a number of years. It somewhat helped, but it made me feel like a robot. I lost the ability to connect with people, i never joked around anymore!!. Once that medication stopped working, I swapped it for cannibus which worked great as long as I was always stoned. If not then I'd be depressed. I also had my health food kick around now which certainly helped. Still, I partied a lot and once I had to get a real job, I sobered up and severe despair and depression took hold and I couldn't function. I'd never felt such hopelessness before.

Around 3 Months ago I actually committed to daily morning meditation. Each day I felt a little better. Until 2 months ago after listening to abraham hicks on youtube, the next video started playing and it was Dr Jack Kruse. I resonated with what he was saying immediately! Since that day I have been reading/listening to this magnificent light, water and magnetism story as much as I can around work. I've been implementing what I learned and I feel AMAZING, I'm more social, confident, clear headed, energised and most importantly HAPPY!

Seeing as there's so much to learn about I'd like some advice on what I should be doing

This is my routine so far

Cold shower then Morning sun 8-9 (grounded ofc)
Water is from italy because our water wont hydrate us because of the levels of dueterium??
Seafood daily
Ice bath twice a week (5min)
Iris blueblocking software and blue blockers for when I'm working at the hospital
A have a red light, but it's not LED, does that matter?

Also
I read somewhere that Aussies really need a magnetico? why is that?

SO GRATEFUL FOR THIS COMMUNITY

Nick

# Thread: From Paleo to Sunlight
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-paleo-to-sunlight.21936/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nina91 — 2018-08-26T22:48:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-paleo-to-sunlight.21936/post-249466>

Hello fellow mitochondriacs!

Let me share a bit of my thought process that led me to this place.
Through our cultural heritage we still get bits and pieces of how important being connected to the sun is through mythology, religion and sometimes even Disney movies
Think of The lion king when the monkey shaman Rafiki lifts up the newborn Simba towards the sun,
or the fact that most of Christian churches, especially the older ones, are built facing east.
The earliest cathedrals were built so that when the sun rose at the equinox March 21 it shone upon the altar.
Anecdotally I also remember my mother telling me a family story of her great aunt’s infertility problems and her doctor at the time prescribing her - more sunshine!
I also know for a fact that my grandparents growing up ate only one meal per day- today it is considered child abuse if 3 meals per day + snacks in between are not offered. Sometimes I have random discussion about that and it seems like that particular question- why are we eating so much more than people 2-3 generations before- doesn't even cross anyones mind.
Over the last few years I’ve been experimenting with variations of Paleo diet as that one made most sense to me - eating like hunter gatherers that we used to be.
But even that premise- which seems logical- didnt address the fact that hunter gatherers around the planet hunted for and gathererd completely different foods, depending what was available locally. I even have a friend who is half African half Europen, and she said she just couldn't do Paleo- she loves her carbohydrates. Is it possible she is more genetically adapted and inclined to eat carbohydrates and foods like Alaskan salmon is less suites for her?

Following Paleo increased my energy levels a little bit, but not as much as I hoped given the effort I was putting in (and money I was spending on all organic foods).
I have to say, to be fair, that consuming bone broth in particular did noticeably improved my digestion. But my minor health problems - mild eczema and PCOS - did not improve.
On the flipside after only three, yes three! days of morning grounding and sunrise gazing, my need for food significantly dropped - no more craving for snacking in between meals - but even more impressive - my period started a week earlier than it usually does (PCOS causes prolonged menstrual cycles).
That was a clear sign of my body being more balanced and responding to the newly found natures signals.

My routine currently “only” consists of morning grounding and wearing a blue light blocking glasses at night.
I also started taking cold showers a few years ago- didn't hear about it- it just instinctively felt good- and now I'm addicted to it to the point that no water feels cold enough- does that happen to anyone?

I am excited to learn about more complex practices through this community and make new like-minded friends!

Nina

Ive been thinking a lot about this lately too -- interesting they grew up with only one meal a day. I have a 3 year old who wants to smack all day too and its so hard to break the habit. I wonder if there is a reason we are eating so much more.
