### Brent Patrick — 2018-09-02T07:15:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-sydney-australia.21964/post-249931>

I too hated seafood and would never consider eating raw oysters, knowing what I know now Oysters are a staple in my diet.
Best to eat Epi Paleo diet that is seasonal.

I eat four dozen raw oysters with cracked pepper lemon squeezed and the rest of the lemon in a shot glass.I always consume turmeric with oysters.This is standard every week.
I eat sockeye salmon in a tin 5 times a week.
I could cut back if I choose but for my N=1
this works for me.
Raw is always best but cooked seafood is always going to be better than the best lamb or steak.
Oysters are a complete biological package, so if your able to consume oysters raw if not Kilpatrick is the way to go.
If your environment is blue light toxic and highly populated you need to clean that up and increase seafood and get out for sunrise and the transition to UVA whilst grounded so your mitochondria can make sense of the information coming in.
Stay away from fish oil capsules and Algae oil .DHA has to be in the SN-2 position to be used, only seafood can provide this

### Emma Ruttyn — 2018-09-02T08:35:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-sydney-australia.21964/post-249936>

Hi Catherine!
I am from Sydney too. I am not on here often but have been following JK's work now for a couple of years. He has changed my life as well as my kids! Not sure what part of Sydney you are from, but we get all our seafood from Frenchs Forest Markets every Sunday from Karl at 'Peyton Blu Royale Seafood'. He has an awesome selection of fish that is really well priced. He has a stall at many other sydney markets too each week.
We have hacked our water by buying Icelandic - I try to stock up from Woolies when they have a deal.
We are so fortunate to live near the beach, so each morning we are down there barefoot looking at the sun. We've been watching sunrises for a bit over a year now, and still people look at us like we're crazy or think we're tourists!
I feel lonely sometimes because I don't know anyone locally who is a mitochondriac - the food movement is HUGE on the northern beaches, but they look at you like a weirdo if you suggest pork belly over kale smoothies!
The magnetico from USA is too expensive for us ATM, so we have purchased a magnetic mattress from an Aussie lady called Jannese up in QLD - she is very helpful if you needed to chat to her. Website: [https://www.magneticexperience.com.au/index.php?route=product/category&path=1\_138](https://www.magneticexperience.com.au/index.php?route=product/category&path=1_138)
Stay covered up when indoors and get nude when outdoors, hehehehe!
Welcome to this forum!
Emma.

I make mistakes just like everyone else and have certainly made my fair share the most important thing is to not fold like a cheap suitcase (skin in the game) and keep putting one foot forward. Reflecting on my mistakes and correcting them moving forward I feel has helped me focus on whats important.

I eat local oysters and purchase sockeye salmon from overseas.
I have testing done but to be honest I go on how I actually feel especially in terms of sleep I sleep very well,this was not always the case before I joined Jack's forum.
The biggest thing I recommend is to read the blogs and do not make the mistake I did, not reading in numerical order as I missed something fundamental several months ago as connecting dots is far easier.

I have made good gains only because I feel discipline and consistency is key and doing the following everyday and not being concerned of what others think or say.

Morning sunrise grounded
transition to UVA
seasonal eating based on Epi Paleo Diet
Consuming DDW
CT daily in some form either cold showers tub CT or of recent this winter Sleep CT
Watching my light environment
Using UVA Bulbs when unable to be outside for required time

I think the main thing Catherine is to just start and take it from there many Ozzies on the Forum and plenty of good advice from the members on this forum.

# Thread: New from Atlanta
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-from-atlanta.22012/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JVB — 2018-09-05T17:11:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-from-atlanta.22012/post-250240>

40 year old mom of three in Atlanta (I know - Atlanta, we're getting our house ready to sell and my husband is contacting recruiters/working his contacts to get out). Until we can leave, we've got vacations/weekends away planned every two or three weeks. Small cells have been going up, seemingly over night, around us and no one else seems to care or notice. We live in the city and have 190 cell phone towers within 4 miles of our house, but we've never gotten cell reception at our house because we're at the bottom of a hill with huge trees all around us.

Generally healthy (although I think I still have low progesterone) and at a healthy weight (have gotten healthier since quitting my job two years ago and being home and outside a lot more). Once my husband and I instituted cold showers last year we both now remember our dreams almost every night.

The kids - got pregnant with my oldest easily. She was slightly jaundiced but not enough to need therapy and later myopic (although her dad and I have very good eye sight) She had her appendix out last summer (a lot of stomach bugs in the years preceding this). She was rarely sick before wi-fi was put in the school. Two miscarriages (low progesterone) in between my oldest and my second. My younger two are rarely sick and outside all of the time. All three were born in July or August.

I took the kids to the drs. for their annual physical last week and their pediatrician tested my 11 year old's cholesterol which I thought was strange. I asked him about it and he said he sees an unbelievable amount of kids with high cholesterol and has had to start testing at age 10. This is in a very affluent area but also very high EMF.

Just got labs done -

Vitamin D - 55 (I'm guessing this should be higher)
Glucose - 81
BUN -16
Creatine - 1.01
Total Cholesterol - 179
HDL - 79
LDL - 87
TSH - 1.35

Haplogroup - V. 70% British/Irish/Scottish. Originally from Cape Cod and we spend half the summer up there now to get the kids out of Atlanta and on the beach. We ditched the sun screen last summer and none of us burned at all. I'd like to move back to small town New England and get a large plot of land. My maternal grandmother died in her mid-70s (complications of diabetes/heart disease). She and my grandfather lived on the water but I never remember her being outside, she was always inside sewing, reading and knitting. My mother has Type 2 diabetes (again probably diagnosed around the time she got a smart phone) and has been myopic from a young age (she grew up sailing competitively so I get the sense that she did spend a lot of time outside). My mother had me when she was 24 but was not breastfed long (soy formula). I was a healthy kid and rarely sick and spent most of my time outside. Dad is one of nine siblings and they all live on Cape Cod, and are all pretty healthy (oldest sibling is 78). My dad has worked outside his whole life and golfs every weekend, still very healthy at 70. He's always spent a ton of time outdoors even though his diet has never been great and he has always slept with the tv on.

I found Jack Kruse because my husband wasn't sleeping well and about ten years ago had a sudden loss of hearing in one ear and tinnitus (probably got this around the time he got his first iPhone now that I think about it). His hearing came back about 50% (they inserted a sponge in his ear drum and did steroid drops). He's an attorney so works late nights a lot and has periods of quite a bit of work stress. After hard wiring our house and instituting blue blockers at night his sleep has improved but I know we all feel better out of Atlanta. We spent last weekend at a lake house in the mountains and both slept great, felt calm and relaxed.

### JVB — 2018-09-07T17:10:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-from-atlanta.22012/post-250444>

Thank you. Excited to learn more. I feel so much better than I did two years ago since I started spending more time outside, eating more seafood and taking cold showers.

So Atlanta - no more grounding and no more coffee (I don't drink it every day but should give up my one cup)? We have small cells in my neighborhood now - one on each street (3 total) and they say LTE with the name of the fire department on them. My backyard is surrounded by huge trees and a creek. My kids are fighting me on keeping their shoes on. I know in June a neighbor mentioned she was feeling buzzing in her body and I've now realized she lives across from one of these small cells (I made the connection that it was some sort of symptom but didn't know these things were in my neighborhood).

### JanSz — 2018-09-07T18:54:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-from-atlanta.22012/post-250460>

Wonder what to do (naturally) to raise progesterone?

\------------------

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/australian-mitochondriac.22092/post-251172>

Hello all! Am using the mitochondriac label a little prematurely but am excited for the journey ahead. After working with clients for nearly 10 years now as a personal trainer the steady decline in wellness and vitality across the board has become so apparent, which has led me on this journey of discovery. The truth never ceases to amaze me, nor the extent of the deception. I'm incredibly excited to tap into the body of knowledge that sheds light (pardon the pun) on this foundational level of human health. Thanks so much for your work Jack. And thank you in advance to everyone else for your love and support along the way. Any suggestions on where to get started is always welcome. Blessings!

### caroline — 2018-09-17T09:16:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/australian-mitochondriac.22092/post-251177>

Hi JKay and welcome......

Please start a journal in the optimal journal section and give Dr. K. some health history - also your Mom's and Gran's. Journals are great to keep track of your progress.

Where do you live? work?

Have you watched Jack's 3 Vermont talks? these are incredibly important. Also all his podcasts are a fast and easier way to get info.

Jack's Book is the first couple of years of his blog. EPI PALEO RX

John Ott's book ...Health and light

If you can manage it ....become a Gold member and you will get all Jack's monthly webinars and the follow up live Q&A when you can ask Jack anything.

There are a ton of Aussies here! There is a great Aussie thread where Jack gave us lots of help.

There is a member's optimal event in Mexico - PDC - at the end of December.

# Thread: Hi
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.22145/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rebecca Pascal — 2018-09-26T07:38:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.22145/post-251694>

Hi - that's me on the right in my profile pic. We played outside all day, every day when we were kids - no sunscreen.  Yes, I received the same haircut as my 3 brothers.  Anyway, I'm now almost 50 and smack dab in perimenopause/menopause. My adrenals are shot, and my progesterone, estrogen, testosterone, and DHEA are all low. I'm also hypothyroid. I have also had allergies since I was a child. I was always skinny, and in the past 3-4 years, I have begun to gain weight despite the fact that I do kickboxing 3x a week, and tough, hilly bike rides. I know wifi is killing me. I have been telling ppl this for years and most look at me like I'm mental. I always hold the cell phone away from head and use the speaker bc holding it up to my head makes me sick. I've learned in the past couple of years that it's imperative to ground myself to Mother Earth, but I'm not spending enough time outside. I LOVE to learn. I already own several of the books Dr. Kruse suggested to read (though, admittedly, haven't read them all.) I watched about 10 hours of Dr. Kruse's Youtube vids, and decided to look up his website. I really like his spirit. I really admire his tenacity and his wisdom. I've been threatening for years to start a "Super Group" of smart ppl from all branches of science and math to discuss various topics, mostly to explore Metaphysical concepts, which fascinate me. I wish I had the capacity to learn (IQ) these complex concepts without rereading the materials 5 times, but that won't dissuade me. I will just have to shift the 40+ hours a week I devote to metaphysical reading/watch vids to reading Dr. Kruse's website, and possibly cracking open that Biology textbook I kept from college. I haven't trusted my health to the traditional healthcare system for years bc I know that all the research is being funded by Big Pharma. I do my own homework, which is how I stumbled upon a video with Dr. Kruse while researching the harmful effects of fluoride. Bottom line is that I became a member so that I could learn how to restore my health. I hope that I possess the intellectual capacity to grasp the material. I wish you all love and light on your healing journey. Thanks so much for the work you are doing, Dr. Kruse.

### caroline — 2018-09-26T07:58:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.22145/post-251697>

Hi Rebecca and welcome....

First of all - Dr. K. will need a current pic of you ....He needs to know who he is talking too.

Please start a journal in the optimal journal section and give Dr. K. your health history as well as your Mom's and Gran's.

Where do you live? work? your postcode matters.

How is your sleep? libido? What do you eat? drink?

Do you have Jack's book? it is the first couple of years of his blog.

If you can manage it - become a gold member and you will have access to all Jack's webinars plus the monthly live Q&A when you can ask Jack anything

# Thread: New Platinum (meant to sign up for gold) member from Atlanta

### Scott Hardin — 2018-09-27T01:33:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-platinum-meant-to-sign-up-for-gold-member-from-atlanta.22152/post-251774>

Hey y'all,

This is Scott,

I'm 42 years old and live in Atlanta. I've been on the site for 2 weeks but I've been following some of Jack's recommendations for a little longer. I have a 40 day streak of watching the sunrise on the parking deck of my building.

For most of my adult life I've struggled with 1) frequent respiratory infections 2) Inconsistent energy levels 3) ADHD and 4) episodic depression.

Since getting up for the sunrise every morning my focus, energy and mood have been significantly better, but I did have to fight off a sinus infection recently (first one I have done without antibiotics) but it took 2 weeks!

Mitochondrial Haplotype: K1a

Potentially relevant SNPs
[rs1800497](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs1800497)[(T;T)](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs1800497\(T;T\)) - Dopamine receptor related - associated with ADHD

Wet and Dry Age related macular degeneration
[rs11200638](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs11200638)[(A;A)](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs11200638\(A;A\))
[rs10490924](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs10490924)[(T;T)](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs10490924\(T;T\))

homozygous for C677T
[rs1801133](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs1801133)[(T;T)](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs1801133\(T;T\))

Warrior
[rs4680](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs4680)[(G;G)](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs4680\(G;G\))

BSML Vitamin D recepter polymorphism
[rs1544410](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs1544410)[(A;A)](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs1544410\(A;A\))

[rs3135391](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs3135391)[(C;T)](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs3135391\(C;T\))
HLA-DRB1\*1501 carrier; higher multiple sclerosis risk

I'm not quite sure where to start the Journal - I would appreciate any feedback from the group and also from Jack!

### caroline — 2018-09-27T04:34:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-platinum-meant-to-sign-up-for-gold-member-from-atlanta.22152/post-251787>

welcome!

### Shane Saunders — 2018-10-03T08:43:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-started.22219/post-252229>

Hi, my name is Shane Saunders, I'm a co-owner of Breathe Me, and I'm also co-host of The Breathing Edge Podcast.

I served in the Australian Defence force for 13 years, two tours in the Middle East and I had the privilege of training our nation’s Navy divers, sailors, soldiers and Elite Special Forces. It was here I developed a desire to understand how people can reach their peak and maintain that level consistently.

From an early age, I've always been a sungazer especially in the morning, and I love Sunset.

I’ve invested thousands of hours (and many thousands of dollars) studying and trialling multiple disciplines, and I thought I was on the right track with the breathing and cold immersion, and now I came across Dr Jack's work now the pieces are falling into place.

I've joined the group on here to share ideas and learn what it is that I don't know.

Much Appreciation.

### nancy peden — 2018-10-08T02:15:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-me-and-mthfrsupport.22254/post-252591>

Hi, my doctoral work is in Transformative Learning and Change and have long known of Jack via Asea esp. I have tons of evidence and work to post for 45k followers on MTHFRSUPPORT (limited to 2 hours a day is not easy) in a way that is both attractive to the academics and trolls we get as well as what is occurring since I took over: the absolutely uneducated into self-healing. I have the privilege of working with a lot of what Sterling Hill does and I feel my own knowing of learning at the quantum level has had an impact. I wish I could afford to do more here and I am excited about the farm near Sterling's home in LA. BTW I am globally friends with many in our areas of interest especially Eileen Day McKusick whose dissertation was "Tuning the Biofield." The book is just ok but her global reputation is growing and she does distance healing and has been tested in some place near Stanford using Faraday cages and her energy still went through. I am also a mystic and I like the mix of science, spirituality and earthed experience.

So to create entertaining posts in two hours a day and really allow spirit to guide is a bit of a challenge and I am glad to have you all to help me learn and share. All knowing is in relationship. In healing ourselves we END the transference of power in authoritarian collapsing paradigms. Learning, together with peer respect is key.

### Mark Osinski — 2018-10-19T15:53:47+0300

Ha. I rarely wear those sunglasses but, the polarizing is great for taking the glare off the water when reeling big fish.  (and I am wearing blue blocking glasses as I type this!)

The good news is, from what I understand, they are putting in the 5G downtown/back bay first so I should be far enough away from it for a few years to observe how the death and destruction goes and then can take steps to move farther away as needed. It's something I am monitoring for sure.

### Mark Osinski — 2018-10-25T15:44:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-morning-from-boston.22332/post-254116>

So I might have to retract my previous statement. I "think" I now know what a 5G tower looks like and they seem to be popping up along some of the highways;(

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-10-25T17:57:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-morning-from-boston.22332/post-254126>

> [Mark Osinski said:](/goto/post?id=254116)
>
> So I might have to retract my previous statement. I "think" I now know what a 5G tower looks like and they seem to be popping up along some of the highways;(

So Mark, what does a 5G tower look like? I know 5G's tell-tale appearance as DAS mini-antennas (you know, those smallish gray boxes or cylinders on street and utility poles as well as sides of buildings).

I always thought they would add 5G broadcast antennas on existing major base stations (cell towers).

A typical cell tower (4G etc) can be on a lattice tower or monopole, with stacked layers of rectangular antennas arrayed in groups of 3, with each group usually covering 120 degrees of radius broadcast coverage. Wi-fi antennas can be smaller and different than standard rectangular gray box. Backhaul antennas can be attached to a tower-- they look like satellite dishes or drums.

If it is a busy communications tower, different wireless carriers (the facility's 'tenants') will have their own layer on the stack. Many cohabiting carriers on a tower makes for a bristling, fearsome looking evil creature. I know you have seen them and felt a silent shiver.

If you have a RF meter you are best advised to take radiation readings frequently to see what is coming your way. Today's meters can't measure 5G mm wave forms so be forewarned.

Good luck, and if it is time to get outta Dodge get out while the gettin' is good.

### Phosphene — 2018-10-25T18:17:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-morning-from-boston.22332/post-254129>

> [ElectricUniverse said:](/goto/post?id=254126)
>
> Many cohabiting carriers on a tower makes for a bristling, fearsome looking evil creature. I know you have seen them and felt a silent shiver.

Absolutely.

### Mark Osinski — 2018-10-27T21:00:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-morning-from-boston.22332/post-254368>

This is what the towers I see more often look like... If this is not 5G then great. I might have a bit more time. Thoughts?

I am taking supplements (vitamins E, C, Magneisum and Zinc and adrenal cortex extract) to support my body through the process. It's going smoothly.

# Thread: Greeting from the Midwest....on my way back to the South
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greeting-from-the-midwest-on-my-way-back-to-the-south.22345/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SunnyGale — 2018-10-20T20:34:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greeting-from-the-midwest-on-my-way-back-to-the-south.22345/post-253823>

Diving In! My name is “Sunny Gale”, no pun, and yes an oxymoron which seems to characterize my personality.
I discovered Dr. Kruse a few months ago, and now listen to him 1-2 hours a day. I would absolutely love to spend all my time, working to help others understand Docs work and how to apply it to their lives.

I have a degree in Nutrition and Dietetics, and worked in the Fitness industry for 15 years. When I was in the midst of clinical rotations, I was sickened by what I saw as “healing modalities” for sick patients. I knew I did not want to go on to become a R.D., and that Diet alone.... was not the answer to healing the physical body. Fast forward 20+ years, and I am now working as a commercial trucking/transportation liability insurance adjuster. (Long story) Since staring this career, my health has taken a nose-dive. I was diagnosed with Fibromyalgia and low thyroid, and am currently take prescription meds. 5 years ago I was a runner, and in natural light at day. Now, 70-80 hour work weeks, keep my enslaved in artificial light. Seriously looking for to get out of this profession, and to move back to the South by next April 2019.

After finding Dr. Kruse, I recently started my current protocol:
Sun gazing; sunrise, sunset
Mediation (following Dr. Joe Dispenza)
“Light” break at work every hour, with my lunch break in the sun, barefoot, sitting on the grass, when I can.
Switched out light bulbs at home to a red bulbs, now installing full spectrum, but try to keep them off as much as possible. I have natural light coming in the sliding glass door, and large double pane windows.
\- - (Would love to hear the light that Dr. Kruse says he keeps on over his shoulder in his podcasts)
Blue-blocker glasses while at work (although since reading, I’m not sure my brand is really helping me)
\- - purchasing yellow/amber glasses this weekend
Iris is installed on all my computers
Grounding; use a grounding mat at computer, and sleep on a grounding sheet (in an apt. Plugged into grounded outlet)
Diet: Was Vegan, transitioned to vegetarian, and now have added back in seafood
Supplements: Have begun weeding them out, per Docs suggestions

I’ve read many posts, and I thank you all for the wisdom and love that you share! Looking forward to living and sharing these truths to all who will listen. Love and Light!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: First time caller long time listener...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-time-caller-long-time-listener.22415/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Karlosis — 2018-10-31T18:02:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-time-caller-long-time-listener.22415/post-254636>

So a couple years ago I started my own company and I vowed to myself that I would spend at least 1 hour a day researching stuff that was outside of work that would ultimately make me a better person.

So I started listening and reading the works of Alan Watts. Which lead me to be interested in meditation and begin a daily practice. So I started reading books on that. Then I was turned on to Joe Dipenza, Gregg Braden and Dawson Church. I was blown away by some of their ideas and research. Then all of a sudden a buddy of mine who is an ER dr sent me a link to Wim Hof. I was beyond blown away. I immediately started learning about what he was doing. I signed up for the Wim Hof Method and was breathing like crazy and taking cold showers. I noticed that something in me was changing but could pinpoint what it was. He is a great leader but not great at explaining the science behind it.

Then while reading a facebook post on Wims page someone asked a technical question about cold exposure and another person recommended googling the name Jack Kruse. Now almost 1 year later here I am. My thirst for information is currently being quenched by reading all the blog posts about everything from cold exposure to vit D3, to grounding and the powers of the sun. All of this research has made a major impact on my life and my wife and 2 kids. I have a HUGE list of questions that I would like to ask the fine folks on this forum but didnt want my first post to be for selfish reasons.

So I figured I would just introduce myself and in doing so say thank you! The information that I have learned so far and have applied to my daily life has done 2 major things to my life and ultimately my family's life. 1. it has provided me a connection to this earth that I didn't expect or know was possible. I would assume because I have seen the sun rise almost everyday and I am outside barefoot all the time. 2. It has created a sense of love in me with everything around me including the people, the seasons, the sun and the food.

With that said, hello, my name is kyle!

### Karlosis — 2018-10-31T22:52:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-time-caller-long-time-listener.22415/post-254663>

> [Dylan Petkus said:](/goto/post?id=254660)
>
> Yo Kyle, good to have you!
>
> Great picture at the beach!
>
> I cannot recommend going GOLD from the get-go. It's like trading in a razor scooter with one good wheel for a Ferrari in terms of accelerating your knowledge of the material and integrating it into your life.

Thanks Dylan!

That picture was the first time my son was introduced to the ocean. I will never forget that trip to Destin!

Excellent analogy. Looking forward to learning to drive a Ferrari in the near future

### Karlosis — 2018-11-02T19:39:13+0200

# Thread: Hello and Thank You!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-and-thank-you.22471/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Soleil — 2018-11-06T19:21:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-and-thank-you.22471/post-255133>

Hi Everyone,

I wanted to say Hi and Thank you to the members here who share and contribute to this great forum. I’ve been a member for a few months now, but have been immersed in reading, listening and learning from Dr. Kruse for about 8 months now.

A bit about me… I spent many years in healthcare, working in a toxic nnEMF and blue light environment. Contact lenses for over 40 years. Very fair skin so would never go in the sun. My bare skin never touched the earth. I was increasingly tired, and my sleep quality was worsening every year. Tinnitus for the last 5 yrs (can trace back to smartmeter install). Body aches and pains, especially in the morning. Increasing sensitivity to EMF’s (fingers burn when touching cellphone and laptop touchpad, feeling unwell in malls/stores but fine after leaving). I’m grateful and fortunate for not having had any diagnosed disease up to this point in my life.

Changes I’ve made so far: up and out with the morning sun, grounded, regardless of weather or temp. Without fail. I’m in northern IN so will see about winter with no clothes on, but nothing deters me from being outside and grounding when the sun rises, and as often as I can during the day. Also getting outside for sunset. Protein breakfast. RO or MV spring water. No lights on in my house, not during the day, esp not at night. I get up with the sun and go to bed shortly (1-2 hrs) after dark. Easy in summer, not so much now, but I’ve adjusted my work schedule to fit. Iris for my computer. Orange glasses for nighttime, only when needed. Turned bedroom power breakers off permanently. Wifi disconnected except for minimal daytime hours. Got rid of cordless phones. Got rid of TV 6 yrs ago. Faraday cage for the Smartmeter that was installed 5 yrs ago without my knowledge or permission. No more contact lenses (over 2 yrs ago). Eating lots of seafood and foods local to my location as much as possible. No CT yet, that's next, although I’m outside, grounded, in current morning temps of 30-45 degrees (in moose hide moccasins). No fluoride for over 20 years. No sunglasses, never wore sunscreen or face makeup. No OTC or prescription meds ever.

Huge for me was understanding that it’s not a food story, but a light story. That alone has completely transformed my life. I’ve lost 18 lbs. without trying. Desire for food has completely changed/lessened. Sleep is amazing, with vivid dreams. Steady energy, brain fog lifted. No body aches or pains. NONE. The tinnitus remains. There’s much more, but these are highlights and it’s just the beginning.

Little about a Mitochondriac’s journey is initially easy or convenient. The unavoidable 5G reality dictates that I’ll need to move. Not at all what I want.. I love where I live and I love my home, but making decisions based on facts and realities, rather than what or how you’d like things to be, is required. I’m grateful to Jack for teaching N=1. Again, not easy, but it has enabled me to see life and what I need to do in a much more realistic way, the only way, really. You’re either all in, or you’re out. Thank you so much Dr. Kruse.

### caroline — 2018-11-07T05:37:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-and-thank-you.22471/post-255149>

I love your journey to wellness Soleil.

Please start a journal in the optimal journal section so we can all follow your journey.

Where do you live?

# Thread: Camper Trailers
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/camper-trailers.22492/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Eric Waldron — 2018-11-11T06:30:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/camper-trailers.22492/post-255431>

Does anyone have any mitochondriac-esk camper trailer nnemf hacks? I'm super interested in the idea of living minimally in an off the grid trailer, but JK says solar panels aren't the route to go, so I'd love to hear what some of you guys are doing.

### Huck — 2018-11-11T17:30:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/camper-trailers.22492/post-255437>

I put solar panels on my van which I use to tow my trailer. Solar panels, charge controller, inverter, and batteries are all in van. I'm also thinking about putting a 12v refrigerator in van.

Check and see where shore power comes into trailer. On many, it is right under the bed. If I had some electrician skills, I would relocate the electrical panel as far from the bed as possible.

In many trailers, the refrigerator is 3 way - propane, 12v, 110v, so you could run it on propane. Heat is usually propane. but with an electric fan. It doesn't draw many amps, so I don't know if it is much of a problem. Stove is also propane, as is hot water heater.

You could also use portable solar panels so they could be a distance from trailer, but the charge controller needs to be close to the battery. I find a 100w solar panel is enough to run lights, recharge phone, laptop, water pump, etc. A 200w panel is probably enough to compensate for cloudy weather.

My thinking is I can park the van in the sun and the trailer in the shade. I can run an extension cord from inverter in van if I need power for my ipot or air fryer (probably outside), recharging trailer batteries, etc. If in cold weather, I plan to warm up trailer and then turn off heat for night and use plenty of blankets.

### Phosphene — 2018-11-11T17:42:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/camper-trailers.22492/post-255438>

Interested in this as well.

[@caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) - How do you like your custom camper? Anything you would change?

### Sun Disciple — 2018-11-11T19:33:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/camper-trailers.22492/post-255450>

> [Eric Waldron said:](/goto/post?id=255431)
>
> Does anyone have any mitochondriac-esk camper trailer nnemf hacks? I'm super interested in the idea of living minimally in an off the grid trailer, but JK says solar panels aren't the route to go, so I'd love to hear what some of you guys are doing.

Solar gives off a ton of DE in homes, do to the DC to AC conversion going back onto the grid. Not sure what a portable set up would be like. Whatever you do make sure you get some meters and test because your going to be in close proximity to every appliance i would imagine.

### caroline — 2018-11-12T11:12:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/camper-trailers.22492/post-255485>

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=255438)
>
> Interested in this as well.
>
> [@caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) - How do you like your custom camper? Anything you would change?

That is such a loaded question for me! I loved our camper trailer because you sleep under canvas and the kitchen is outside - so you spend all your time outside.

Our new slide on camper is more like glamping....and we haven't had it long enuf or been away long enuf to get a better routine going.

So far - I tend to cook inside because it is faster than dragging out the BBQ and all the accoutrements....and I have a great fridge and sink and a ton of storage inside.

We are going away tomorrow for 6 days to a music festival so we are going to set up everything outside and I will get in some kind of a routine if it kills me! The trouble is when you love good food and prepare everything from scratch - it takes a lot of thought .

It is going to be BBQ steak and salad and a roast leg of lamb this week. We have a huge thick steak and a big leg of lamb so lots of leftovers.

But .....sleeping under canvas rocks!

We have talked to loads of campers ....and everyone agrees - there is no perfect camper.

We are outside every morning for sunrise and as much of the day as humanly possible.

We meet tons of people who are permanently on the road and loving it. It is a very appealing lifestyle ...for sure in this country.

We spent 6 weeks in North Queensland during our winter ........and that was fantastic. Except for the crazy price of diesel in this country. In our town it is presently $1.71 a litre. When we are travelling it is costing us almost a $1000.00 a week.

Everything is a trade off........


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Pursuit Of Happiness
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pursuit-of-happiness.22507/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Safeby2 — 2018-11-15T04:23:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pursuit-of-happiness.22507/post-255648>

Hello there fellow mitrochondriacs! My name is Samuel. Im 25 years old residing in Gainesville, Florida. A beautiful place to get sun!

Ive been lurking the forums anon for a while as I am not much of a poster on forums. However this may be a place that I might be sticking around. Regardless I will forever be following Jack's guidelines. Let me get you up to speed with me.

Since I went on lowfat diet at 18 to lose weight I have never felt right. Well I never felt right ever since I had 4 amalgams in my baby teeth and grew up on a SAD diet. I always had OCD, intrusive thoughts, depression, and anxiety. At 18 I was bed ridden due to my weight loss plan I subscribed to. Ever since Ive always had issues with my mental health in regards to feeling alert and alive. I have always been hiding behind a curtain of brain fog. Until I read up on Jack Kruse.

Now I still have fog, but its getting better. I am eating A LOT of seafood, about 5 oysters a day, .25-.5 lbs of fish a day(usually steelhead trout), and around 1.5 lbs of beef. Im mainly zero carb, but I do eat fruits every few days. Pineapple or melons are my go to.

I've noticed I dont get much brain fog from seafood, but after eating beef it happens. I want to make a journal and report back how I am doing in the future. I'm really on a quest to be 100% mindfully content and happy with life. I strive to feel the same clarity and wellbeing I used to feel as a pot smoker. I have since stopped over the past month, I do still consume coffee but thats due to me working 3 jobs. I rise and rest with the sun as much as possible and get to check out the sets. My brain is feeling better as days go on, but it still gets dull sometimes.

Sorry if this is all over the place, I have my laptop hardwired to my internet and I am trying to make this post as I get my cold bath ready for some CT! Today I learned how to disable the wifi on my router so it is currently off. I noticed a difference almost immediatly. As I did when I first put my phone on airplane mode.

I want to add that oysters are dangerous! Last night with an oyster shucker I stabbed the knife all the way through my thumb. Was more shocked the fact that I did than the fact that there was fatty tissue now hanging out of my thumb.

Carry on my fellow Mitoluvers, I'll talk to you all soon hopefully!

Take care

Samuel

### drezy — 2018-11-15T15:15:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pursuit-of-happiness.22507/post-255661>

> [Safeby2 said:](/goto/post?id=255648)
>
> Today I learned how to disable the wifi on my router so it is currently off. I noticed a difference almost immediatly. As I did when I first put my phone on airplane mode.

Nice progress. It's tuff like this learning and implementing that will give your fellow optimizers here the signal that you crave enough to learn and act on what you learn. It's way more enjoyable to share with people who act in this way. Keep it up.

> I want to add that oysters are dangerous! Last night with an oyster shucker I stabbed the knife all the way through my thumb. Was more shocked the fact that I did than the fact that there was fatty tissue now hanging out of my thumb.

A little horizontal gene transfer now and then is alright. Just walk it off. At 25 you'll be healed before the next unfunny meme gets created.

# Thread: Facinated and looking for "mito" community
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/facinated-and-looking-for-mito-community.22512/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7

### ALEXIS TUDOR — 2018-11-16T01:08:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/facinated-and-looking-for-mito-community.22512/post-255718>

I am always investigating renegade scientist types because I know what is out there in mainstream is generally ineffective by the number of people on $$$pills, and myself being so often found three standard deviations from the center of the bell curve of human health. Despite having decades of moderate disease, I have never been on any western meds. I have had a lifetime of illness which includes MS like symptoms that come and go with stress, as well as weak joints. I have tried many different lifestyle changes over the years living on 5 different continents in different settings being fully in the grid and at time way off the grid with moderate but conditional results. I have been with teachers who have exhibited a lot of the things Jack has talked of(often using a unintentional CT) but it seemed like, in hindsight, that did not have the full perspective from a quantum physiological perspective. Imitating those teachers methods have been helpful but the outcome has been limited for me due to my already compromised matrix. I look forward to learning more even though there seems to be a steep learning curve, but what I have encountered sounds right to the soul and gut.

I have had one problem or another since a young age. The worst of it began almost 28 years ago when I grinded my knees down to the bone from trying to cycle across America that lead to a series of diseases which rendered a muscular 175 guy down to 120 in a lot of pain. Having no insurance(in this dumbass country afraid of national health care) I was forced to seek alternative healing methods which in hindsight may be a blessing. It didn't heal for years and morphed into chronic fatigue/MS like stuff. Eventually my digestion collapsed on top of that. I was able to get out of the worst through some CHI/prana related yogic practices eventually and got back up to 134 pounds. I have explored many other methodologies including addressed stored emotions within traumatized cells which allowed me to run for the first time in 24 years. Things are better but I call myself a 50% person because I can not express my soul nature at a 100% due to limited energy.

Perhaps the silver lining is it had forced me to become a citizen health scientist as I am naturally fascinated and not shy at all to the the "strange" if it will yield results. I know Jack has mentioned take no prisoners when you are trying things that can help you despite losing fame, fortune, family or friends, and I have lived that way for the most part. Had I not, I think I would have been dead long ago or at least my soul would have living in what I would call a pathetic way. I see life as a exploration and not as a specific goal. I don't know why so many people at my age 50 want to stop learning. Reminds me of a Chinese saying that people often die at thirty and their bodies die at 65. I look forward to learning and meeting others experimenting.

THanks

inside cell
outside cell

**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### carlos — 2018-11-26T08:56:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yellow-skin-in-hands-and-feet.22558/post-256601>

So much love and respect for Dr Jack. I have yellow hands and feet, my bilirubin levels are normal. I did a keto diet for 9 months eating 2 to 4 avocados a day plus omega supplements. To much poly unsaturated fat? Could it be yellow fat disease? Fatty liver? My skin is really dry. Live in Minnesota. I know vit D is so important since I'm originally from Ecuador. Any idea what could be happening. I work in a Restaurant under blue light and heat lamps to keep the food warm. I could eat carbs until I started wearing blue blocker glasses and CT. Those two changes are saving my life. Thanks a lot for any information

### carlos — 2018-12-04T05:52:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yellow-skin-in-hands-and-feet.22558/post-257226>

I've seen a lot of them but they can't figure out what's wrong. I'm anemic and they only prescribed me iron tablets, I know that's not the solution. Since I started protecting my self from blue light I'm doing much better, as i said for almost 2 years I suffered from chronic fatigue and couldn't eat carbs because if i did i would crush and feel horrible. I used to wake up so tired every single day. Thanks so much for all you do. I've tried every single diet out there and nothing worked. Eating only organic and grassed fed and nothing worked. I knew food wasn't my problem. When I saw a you tube video from you I was so happy because i Linked all my health issues to what you explain. Now I wear blue blockers, wake up every single day refreshed, as soon as I wake up I do grounding and watch in direction of the sun for 5 minutes. Take a cold shower and have breakfast. My bones don't hurt anymore but I'm constipated most of the time and I don't eat any meat because if I do i get uric acid accumulation in my feeth finger tips and it's like having needless. What could I do in order to star eating oysters and don't get this issue. I make sure I'm well hydrated. Thanks a lot.

### Cheryl. — 2018-12-04T06:46:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yellow-skin-in-hands-and-feet.22558/post-257227>

Did they check your liver, kidneys, diabetes? [https://www.google.com/search?q=yel...d-acer&sourceid=chrome-mobile&ie=UTF-8&inm=vs](https://www.google.com/search?q=yellow+hands+and+yellow+feet&client=tablet-android-acer&sourceid=chrome-mobile&ie=UTF-8&inm=vs)

# Thread: Militant Food Groups Online- My Mistake
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/militant-food-groups-online-my-mistake.22731/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mark Osinski — 2018-12-20T17:18:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/militant-food-groups-online-my-mistake.22731/post-258542>

If it helps anybody on here avoid wasting energy and resources online. I broke my own rule and I paid for it...

I did not know about this forum/Kruse until a few months ago when I stumbled on the VT 2016 video. Before that I was deeply engaged in following the LCHF, Keto, Insulin, Salt, Lipids, Carnivore related groups. I read a lot of their books and followed a lot of their online groups etc. I started to sour on much of that about a year ago for a number of reasons and once I finally got here I instantly realized how short-sighted the food groups are regardless of their motivation.

I made the mistake of commenting on a post in one of these groups yesterday. I suggested that when the environment in which somebody lives changes -and- their diet changes you can't just say it's the food and ignore those other factors. Apparently, you cannot suggest this in a food group that is based on the "science." I was immediately doxxed and was accused of being a "paymaster" of the big agra/pharma industry. After I said, I was not that person they accused me of using a fake profile picture to hide my real identity and intent to spread lies about food. But, ultimately they accused me of being part of that "electromagnetic" group which I can only assume is this group here.

Today, I am unfollowing all of that noise. Group think and confirmation bias comes up a lot in those places as reasons to question the "science" but, they are entrenched and it's really a shame. Its my fault, I own it. I broke my own rule of never commenting. Just thought I would share.

MarkO

### drezy — 2018-12-20T17:49:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/militant-food-groups-online-my-mistake.22731/post-258545>

Just a thought:
If you ever feel the urge to waste some time with dopaminergically challenged non-critical thinking folks you could always just hang out at the DMV for an hour.

### Mark Osinski — 2018-12-22T17:27:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/militant-food-groups-online-my-mistake.22731/post-258731>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=258545)
>
> Just a thought:
> If you ever feel the urge to waste some time with dopaminergically challenged non-critical thinking folks you could always just hang out at the DMV for an hour.

All unfollowed.

### TabulaRasa — 2018-12-29T13:53:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/militant-food-groups-online-my-mistake.22731/post-259161>

I have spent most of my life studying diets and have tried so many different ones. I think that one major determinant of the correct diet for the body is the environment you live in; ie. you can get away with raw vegan in hot dry or tropical climate, but the diet basically does not work in cold damp places and ideally what you eat should vary with the seasons. A lot of that specific idea is basically explained in Chinese medicine. I found some people I brought it up to would agree but a lot of other people would be diet fanatics and that's just human nature.

### WalterNL — 2018-12-29T16:50:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/militant-food-groups-online-my-mistake.22731/post-259169>

I would call this a 'sun group' above anything else. Sounds much better.

### Sheddie — 2018-12-29T17:22:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/militant-food-groups-online-my-mistake.22731/post-259174>

Whatever else, when you change your diet you change your biochemistry. This can be good, good for the short-term/bad for the long-term, or 'bad' for your n=1. Monitoring your n=1, and the goals and experiments you undertake, takes diligence and being 'educated' -- an ongoing exercise to keep up with research and theorizing in such a field of interest 'guided' by opinion from many factions -- real health care pros, robotic health care pros, producers, suppliers, vendors, research designers and funders, test developers... as well as online interest 'groups' vying for your singular attention, or your disinterest. Discernment and 'know thyself' are critical. Being 'open-minded' is usually advantageous for learning. However, careful assessment of what flows in an open mind is a must. Your n=1 is a beautiful thing when shared with others who only need two habits , two criteria first assessed -- to tell the truth and take responsibility. Mostly, we do get excited and curious about novelty; new material to think about and integrate with older concepts, or analyze for what furthers. Usually, "fanatics" are self-deceptive to some stagnation point. This is very very different from engaging with, exploring, and allowing for paradigmatic change.

Hi! Thank you all!!!!
I love all what I read here, with great excitation!
I'm French, MD, ophthalmologist. I left the University Hospital in May this year, I have now my own office and patients. Now I can explain them why not to wear their glasses in the morning outside . And why it could be beneficial to give up statin. etc. I read the blog every minute I can, to learn further...
I eat much more fat than before, but my liver has difficulty to digest all this fat (sometimes nausea...) I suspect my redox is not optimal, so I'm on the road !
Cheers to all,
Melanie

# Thread: Greetings from Australia
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-australia.22785/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JPB — 2018-12-31T04:08:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-australia.22785/post-259242>

Hi all,

I've been a silent observer of this forum for a while, I thought i'd post and share my story now since I've applied some of the things Jack Kruse recommends and have gotten some good results.

Ever since my mid teens I've suffered from depression, anxiety and mood swings. I most definitely have inherited these issues from my parents. My mum is an obsessive, anxious, highly strung, angry, high acheiving type. And my Dad was a horrendous Alcoholic, he had a couple of strokes from his drinking and chain smoking, and is now in a nursing home at a relatively young age. I was put on a host of anti-depressant medication, and have seen a few psychologists. Non of which
helped very much.
After a severe breakdown at 22 while working/studying I moved homed and decided to look for alternative treatments for my health problems. At this point they went beyond depression/anxiety, I was now experiencing extreme fatigue, frequent infections, weakness of my limbs, I had also become very suicidal.
I saw a Dr who diagnosed me with Pyrrole Disorder. The treatment consists of taking large doses of B6 and Zinc, along with a few antioxidants. The treatment worked miraculously well and within a few months I was in the best health of my life. My depression, anxiety, and suicidality completely resolved. I went back to working and studying.
Then about three years ago I started to lose my hair, a Dr prescribed propecia, and this is where the fun really began.
I took it and within three days I started experiencing extreme anhedonia and sexual dysfunction. I stopped the drug, but the damage had been done, and i now have the imfamous Post Finasteride Syndrome. Since that time I've been put on the disability pension and havehave in and out of psychiatric hospitals.

For the last three years I've been trying to figure out what is wrong in Post Finasteride Syndrome, and I'm pretty sure I know what is wrong, as well as quite a few guys in the Post Finasteride community. However knowing what is wrong, and knowing how to fix it are two very different things. So that's my story in a nutshell!

Cold I have found to be very helpful. As well as grounding, early morning and late evening light (although too much UV light can make me quite worse). l religiously watch the sunrise and set every day. Anyway look forward to meeting you all, and learning more about this approach to illness!

Kind regards,

Josh

### Cheryl. — 2018-12-31T07:14:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-australia.22785/post-259245>

Welcome, sounds like you are on the right path, congrats.

I live in Dulwich Hill of Sydney, at the moment I'm not working, I'm on the Disability Pension. I eat pretty well, although I've eaten things I shouldn't of over Christmas. I do eat seafood, I love Japanese food, Sashimi especially. I especially like Salmon, prawns, oysters, I'll have a tin of sardines every once in awhile for breakfast. Water, mostly San Pellegrino, San Giorgio, Icelandic, and another brand I can't recall at the moment.
I haven't had 23andme, but I have had gene testing. I'm compound heterozygous for mthr, homozygous for mtrr, compound homozygous for VDR. It's on my to-do list 23andme gene testing. Will write in the optimal journal section for sure.

### Mitchell97 — 2019-01-21T11:35:03+0200

### Pablo — 2019-01-21T15:01:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-australia.22785/post-260908>

Welcome!! Damm finasteride... Too many of us suffering that shit, the drug should me out of the market ASAP, at least for cosmetic reasons. Yeah I also feel JK´s protocols are great, it just seems to me that we must implement them for much longer than other people to get back to homeostasis. Patience and consistency seem key, many times I get discouraged for not seen the changes I seek... But at the same time feel total recovery is very near and something super achievable, so keep it up!!

I also feel CT and grounding are great. In grounding for example, I´ve done it sporadically in the park or beach for 1-2 hours at a time, and it kind of feels good. But more recently I´ve done it for, lets say 4-5 hours in the garden of my village, and 2 out of those 3 days I´ve experienced some subtle but noticeable sensations. Like some tingling sensations in the body and more specifically in the penis, anus ang genital area in general... like if things were getting back to life hehe. Was it the flow of electrons or just my mind playing playing tricks? Whatever, I´ll keep doing it. I just feel we need a looot of these things to get to homeostasis, but once things get back to normal, it will be far easier to stay there. I´ll be experiencing with this next weeks/months.

Anyways, this is just my intuition. I know, from your posts in my journal that you are into something regarding the mechanisms of PFS. I´ve just focused on the practical side and have almost no idea about the mechanics involved, but I will in the next months... I´ll ask you for direction for sure, maybe by getting to know the underlying issues we are able to get a more specific recovery protocol, who knows... Until then, I´ll focus on sun, CT, grounding, nature, low nnEMF´s, outdoor exercise, etc. All that makes me feel quite good.

Take care

# Thread: Hi from France!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-france.22819/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Caroline Vigneron — 2019-01-06T11:10:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-france.22819/post-259558>

Hello, newbie from France here!
I started to pay attention to Dr Kruse's publications on my facebook feed only a few weeks ago. Can't even remember when I found his page exactly, nor how long ago but I'm glad I did!
I wish I had found it earlier though, because I just bought a house a bit further north from where I live. Had I known better a few months ago, I'd have try harder to go south instead (it was my first wish but budget came in the way!).
I'm 42, in premenopause (I get cognitive symptoms, so it doesn't help understanding/memorizing everything Dr Kruse says!!! I have to take notes!). A few years ago I took a training in naturopathy but I quit trying to be a naturopath recently, for many reasons, one of those reasons being that I was more and more aware that the training kind of sucks (the institute where I studied had wifi in the building, for exemple!)! And I'm autistic (Asperger syndrome), so trying to be your own boss and selling your own "product"/services when you suck at communicating, that's quite difficult!
Anyway, I'm also electrohypersensitive and when I became aware of that and of how far it goes (sensitivity to/nocivity of synthetic materials), that was in spring 2017, it allowed me to make great changes in my life, and it allowed me to get way better really quick: I started to sleep grounded on fully natural materials and rather away from EMFs, I started wearing only natural materials and full leather shoes to allow natural grounding when I'm outside on natural ground, I started avoiding synthetic grounds as much as possible, not only avoiding artificial EMFs but doing my best to get in touch as much as possible with "raw" natural EMFs (the natural EMFs are distorted/perverted by modern/synthetic building materials)... All of that helps regenerate, loads! But I totlally missed the artificial light issue and I went on spending much time on my computer (properly grounded and connected to the web through a wired connection but with no bluelight filter!).
So I'm working my way towards less artificial lights and more natural ones. I started knitting (during daytime)! Lol... I downloaded f.lux and use it always in "ember" mode. I guess I'll have to invest in good quality yellow glasses but budget is tight...
I'm looking forward to a more natural lifestyle in general, I bought an old farm in the countryside for that, in a quiet area (near Autun, in case some French here would wonder!). Thick stone walls, mainly natural building materials, more than an acre of land, a mile from cell phone towers (far enough to be rather safe, and close enough so I have little risks to see a new one popping out just in front of my window someday!)... Big stressful adventure (for a single autistic woman with a long history of chronic fatigue), but I'm looking forward to finding out how my ancestors did it in Europe, how they survived (and more or less thrived?!) through the long and dark winter..., eating plenyty of deuterium depleted foods, I guess?!
Looking forward to learning plenty of new things here!

### Inger — 2019-01-06T16:04:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-france.22819/post-259569>

Welcome Caroline!

I am not too far from you, I live north Germany
I love those old French farm houses made of stone... You are lucky to get to live in one!

### caroline — 2019-01-07T03:21:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-france.22819/post-259602>

welcome Caroline.....

### Alex V — 2019-01-07T07:37:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-france.22819/post-259609>

Salut Caroline et bienvenue! I am also EHS. I thought it was interesting to hear about your stone farm house!

### Caroline Vigneron — 2019-01-24T21:55:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-france.22819/post-261264>

Thanks y'all!
And yep, old farms or houses built with strong natural materials are quite a treasure. They offer good shielding from modern nuisances while distrurbing the native EMFs of the place/ground as little as possible. They were built using no mechanical machines, so they're usually much more in harmony with the "spirits" of the place than the modern houses built with rather "dirty"/perverted kinds of materials (sometimes litteraly filled with industrial rubbish) and on bascially raped grounds. Walls have ears, as the saying goes, and grounds have memory!
I wonder if geobiology is talked about here?

### Caroline Vigneron — 2019-02-06T09:11:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-france.22819/post-262388>

"Test"?
Gee, I feel definitely old. I used to know how to use forums, 20 years ago, lol! Sorry!

### Inger — 2019-02-06T10:24:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-france.22819/post-262390>

Caroline, i guess Jack means.. test, with meters, or other test that are scientifically approved.
For nnEMF, or for other poisons like radon, mold etc. in the environment.

Do you have an EMF meter already?

### Caroline Vigneron — 2019-02-09T10:24:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-france.22819/post-262661>

Ok, so yes and no!
I have a little meter that evaluates parts of the electrosmog (an esi 23). Not perfect, it can't measure all that I feel and it can measure some things I don't feel at all. I can't afford more. I've heard of radon too.
Then I used to consider all that is called feng shui/geobiology as a big bag of horseshit, until I became aware of my electrohypersensitivity, until I started to test certain things (such as body voltage), only to discover that some of what I perceive/feel doesn't seem to come from measurable pollutions/"scientifically proven" stuff (but what is "scientifically proven" stuff? you may still measure electromagnetic fields in a place with extreme scientific precision and "prove" it's a very dense electrosmog and then sceptics will ask you "So what?!"!).
To fully spit it out, I've had some experiences with some substances that enhance "subtle"/electromagnetic perceptions (psilocybin truffles), not necessarily high doses (it can be microdosed); some of those "subtle"/6th sense kind of perceptions are clearer on psilocybin, to me it's quite obviously why some shamans use "psychedelics" (psilocybin or ayahuasca)! These substances are well known to enhance light and noise sensitivity. Psilocybin/psilocyn seems to be an aromatic amine, from what I gathered here and there.
It' my personal bet that "hippies" ended up naked so often when "high", and also often ended up wearing leather skin and pelts (such as Afghan coats) instead of regular modern clothes, because of those psychedelics electromagnetic enhancing properties, because when you're on serotonergic psychedelics, you may suddenly discover how disturbing syntetic clothings are (synthetic = electrically insulating).
So, before saying a metal chair is fine (or a plastic one), someone should sit on it when on a medium dose of shrooms and then compare with how it feels to sit on a chair made of 100% chemical free wood!! ;-) Or maybe asking electrohypersensitive people how they feel on metal compared to wood could be enough? (but often, people, even electrohypersensitives, don't think about paying attention to that sort of detail because they don't think it can make a difference, and so they may feel things without being able to tell where it comes from, just because they're not directing their attention in the right way).
Or maybe shamans could have interesting things to say about that too. They address part of those feng shui/geobiology/"subtle" energetic problems when they bless a place, when they smudge a place with eagle feathers and prayers to get it free of malevolent spirits/energies (there may be publications about the biochemical composition of sage and other encens smokes: antibacterial chemicals, ions and so on)... They can harmonize a place in ways that no scientific will be able to explain yet, and yet to these shamans it will be regular and very "real" business. Shamans are getting more and more credits these days even from researchers. I wish electrohypersensitives could start getting some of the same credit too (not only when they talk about wifi or 5G!)!
Other exemple: being grounded on a modern concrete screed, rich in cement rather than in gravels (therefore quite impermeable), and containing petrochemical additives won't feel as good or as biocompatible as being grounded on a rather "porous" floor made of terra cotta tiles put on a good old lime screed. Some people competent in ecologic/healthy building materials know about how important the "breathability" of building materials is (they allow water, ions, air... to flow both ways up and down), but of course that's no proof of how it can affect mammals!
There's a guy here in France called Eric Petiot who wrote a book titled "Agriculture énergétique", he talks about various things such as the redox potential of the ground, and how the elctromagnetic properties of a ground can be disturbed by modern agricultural technics (chemicals and plowing).
Here in France we have people who work on those problems they're called "géobiologistes". I used to mock them, honnestly. Now I'm stuck in their position!
Maybe you could invite some native american healers at your farm someday and have them give a look around?! They have powerful tools that could be of good use, eventhough totally non-scientific!

# Thread: New here. Greetings From northern Utah!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here-greetings-from-northern-utah.22839/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nick84032 — 2019-01-08T16:37:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here-greetings-from-northern-utah.22839/post-259746>

Hi all.
Been a devout follower of jack on fb for a year and a half now. Figured id step it up a notch and actually join in to the thick of it here. Since a lot of the fb posts are just teasers. Silver member as of last week, going to cut back on some other expenses and move up when I can.

No lights in my House concentrate the blue spectrum. Either all red lizard lights or 40-60w incandescents. After sun is down candles or one 50w red light are on. Of course morning sunrise every morning. Cold showers, the works. I even use my fireplace instead of central heating when I can. Idk if that makes a difference but doesn’t hurt. Got a flicker free LED PBM device on the way too.

Currently eating only meat till spring comes around, I try and not eat after 3-4pm. All nnemf that is in my control is off except when I need it. I’m single so that makes all of this really easy lol.

What led me here:
I had the damndest time falling asleep for years and was fairly obese and had been my whole life. Randomly a friend showed me jacks fb page and it all started there. I began with the leptin rx (last winter), doing “the winter protocol” as i call it, and despite eating carbs in summer, I’m now a healthy 175 lbs (down from 245) and work as a furniture mover, and I fall asleep really easily. Very active. Lots of sunlight everyday. I basically work out in the sun. It’s been a blessing. Since I started using jacks wisdom I have gotten two raises and was able to become a crew leader.

But I live at 6000ft... Trying to find a way to move south and to lower elevation. I moved up here from sea level 3 years ago, today actually, and could not climb stairs easily at all for 5 months. I also came addicted to IV heroin, I came for rehab. I watched the sunrise every morning I could because “sun gazing” was something I learned about in my late twenties and tried it for 30 days and it helped then with my anxiety and other mental problems. But was told that after 30 days I could no longer do it lol. What a joke. After doing it again I was the happiest most energetic patient in my rehab. I was the one spreading hope that the pain of withdrawal would end. But I was still 240 pounds and gaining more each day. (Trading alcohol and drugs for carbs is a common practice... and now I was at 6000ft in the dead of winter) interestingly, as noted above, today is my 3 year anniversary of my last day of shooting up drugs. 1/8/16 was the last day I used.

Reading more I’ve realized that my years in front of a computer from a VERY young age was what did it. And explains why I was a MASSIVE 235 pounds at 12 years old.....I see my life in retrospect wishing to have been able to make one change that would have prevented All the misery... cut out the computer... my myopic vision would be better too. Only one in my family with glasses....

Anyways I hope to grasp this wisdom at a higher level here and begin attending member events. I’ve begun studying biochem in my down time, currently starting with metabolism/AMPk, UCP1, lipolysis/glycolysis etc. The basics. As well as their upstream signaling pathways. Whenever a new term or pathway is mentioned I briefly look it up and take notes. I have a thick notebook that is filling up quick. Complete with diagrams and details. It’s addicting and I hope to maybe enter the world of biochem professionally someday, albeit armed with mitochondriac wisdom.

### Christine_L — 2019-01-09T00:29:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here-greetings-from-northern-utah.22839/post-259806>

Congrats on being clean for 1 year! Keep up watching the sunrise everyday and eating seafood. There's a ton of info on the forum and in the blogs and webinars. The podcasts Jack has been on are likewise really great starting points.

I am a Brit who now lives at the beach in the pacific nw. I recently made some very impactful lifestyle changes and since listening to a number of the podcasts this week, have already added a few more. I am a thriving work in progress willing to do the work to reverse bad habits, seek knowledge and adventure, and help educate when the woo can be explained.

One of the many things i’d like to explore is how to design a great house that will work with me, not against me. I trained as an architect and have bought a beautiful plot of land on the bay by the ocean which has healthy trees, a well, and only one neighbor who has a vacation home with no WiFi. For example, I’m thinking a grounded east facing outdoor shower could be a nice way to start the day bathed in light but would wet skin affect the light I absorb? Does all glass block uv? So many questions..... so much fun research to explore, do, and share

### laea10 — 2019-02-21T21:48:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-everyone.23179/post-263731>

Hello,

My name is Rafal, I come from Poland and I'm really glad that I found a way to this page, to this forum. My journey with health started when I was having heartburn. Doctors couldn't help me, food gurus couldn't help me so I started to look for information by myself. After months of reading paleo and keto books and listening to podcasts i realized something is missing, because my health wasn't significantly improving. So i started looking for "biohacking" then I found podcast with Mr. Jack and i got hooked. I didn't understand most of things that I heard, but it got me thinking "this is something new and it kinda makes sense." So here am I, ready to learn something that will help me to deal with my condition, let my journey begin. Cheers !

Hi and welcome....

### laea10 — 2019-04-18T20:37:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-everyone.23179/post-268372>

Hi,

basically I'm biohacker now, I use blue blocking glasses, i expose to the sun every morning, I sleep 8 hours every day from 10 PM to 6 AM, I eat primally ketogenic diet, more towards carnivore, but still, my health didn't improve, I mean my main problem. My mood generally improved, and i wake up everyday energized, my energy levels throughout a day are high and stable, generally I feel a lot better so tips of Mr. Jack helped significantly. My main problem is low stomach acid levels. I take Betaine HCL for 6 months, now like 12-15 capsules per meal (I eat 2 or one meal a day) to feel like my stomach is actually digesting. When i don't supplement with betaine it's just painful, I feel full really fast after eating protein, then I never get hungry again, it feels like food isn't digesting at all and stuck in my stomach. When I take Betaine, I feel better, my stomach is working better, but nothing changed since 6 months. I made some labs, and my vitamin D levels are low, thyroid labs are fine, my blood cells are fine, but slowly getting worse due to lack of absorbstion of Iron and B12. I try to prevent that eating like 500g chicken liver per week to not run of my vitamins and minerals. I don't know what to do really, i can't really find a causation. I don't have H.Pylori overgrowth, my gastroscopy didn't show anything abnormal, I hope someone could help me on this forum

Answering to your questions:

Have you listened to Jack's 3 Nourish Vermont talks? Yes

Have you had a 23&me test done? no

Where in Poland are you? big city? what do you do for a living? Warsaw, biggest city in my country, I work in office, I sit 8 hour a day in front of computer screen, but i wear blue blocking glasses

How is your sleep? your libido? what are you eating? drinking? Sleep is good, libido too, I eat keto diet, not a lot of vegetables, i use app to track my micronutrients, I drink high mineralized sparkling water from grocery shop.

# Thread: Hello, Black Swan in training :)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-black-swan-in-training.23181/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kim Donner — 2019-02-22T01:03:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-black-swan-in-training.23181/post-263753>

Hello, my name is Kim Donner, I thought I would start a thread to introduce myself and get started.

I'm like a sponge absorbing all this wonderful info, and look forward to meeting some of you in person at some point.

I live in Texas, outside Dallas, and work at the airport, so I have some definite negatives going for me. We moved here from Hawaii 3.5 years ago for a job transfer for my husband (Yes, I know... why!?) But the islands get small and very expensive after a while (although in hindsight I would have made it work). But we are here now, and with everything I have learned recently we are looking at relocating to the panhandle, hopefully within the next 6 months or less.

I have a pool that has very cold temperatures right now and have been sticking my feet in it. I did start CT (facial ice baths) and had a strange reaction, my face turned whitish with red patches and I broke out in a rash on my neck, chest and tops of hands/wrists. From the research I found through Dr. Jack I believe this was because I had too much Omega 6 and not enough Omega 3, so I stopped the CT and have been eating a LOT of seafood (and pork), about 20 oysters per week. I have not yet tried the facial ice bath again yet (am am a little hesitant to still, not sure how long to seafood load).

Sun has been behind some thick clouds here for the last 2 weeks, cold and rainy, and where I live there are tons of trees but a mile away is a freeway (yes, another negative) so it's difficult to find a sunrise, but on the couple days the sun has been out, I have been in it as much as possible from sunrise to 10:00am. which brings me to another issue... I am fair skinned with red hair and have always wore sunglasses and a hat when at the beach (now I know a mistake) but mostly have been in the midday sun, where I will get a rash if I spend too much time. Once I learned of Solar Callous it was like a lightbulb went off, but there hasn't been enough sun to get a callous yet for me, but this is the first thing I will be working on when we get some rays around here.

So that's a little about me, I will certainly be adding more, any feedback is encouraged and or appreciated.

Have a great day!!! SO glad to be here! Kim

### Mike David — 2019-02-22T01:53:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-black-swan-in-training.23181/post-263762>

Hello there. Remember cloudy days are red light days and red light in the am builds the solar callous too. Where I’m from it doesn’t feel much like red light day but it def. is. It’s more like you need to move south day.

Have you had a 23&me test done? it may explain your C/T situation.........

### caroline — 2019-02-23T03:33:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-black-swan-in-training.23181/post-263844>

Hi Kim .....

Click on forum at the top left hand side of this page ....pale blue bar.
Count down five sections and you will come to Optimal journals.
Please let me know if I haven't explained that properly...

Just get outside at sunrise ....that is how you develop a skin callous ....wether it is sunny or not. Cloudy is still good!

yes the 23&me test is very important., especially regarding sun and C/T

# Thread: Hello from Austin
