### SusanR — 2019-03-08T01:07:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-austin.23256/post-264740>

Hi all! I discovered Dr. Kruse’s blog & FB page several months ago. then joined Patreon. Reading his book now. I’ve made several changes and feel better already. Decided to become a member a couple of months ago, even tho my work schedule seriously limits the amount of time I can spend here. There's so much to take in!
Thank you Dr Jack for all the knowledge you put out there and the time you give.

If all goes as planned, I’ll be moving away from Austin within the year (and will be so happy to get out of here). Heading east. Not sure where to settle. Would like to be in the middle of the woods somewhere, or near the ocean. I have family near Asheville North Carolina, and Hilton Head South Carolina. Wherever I end up, I want to be closer to them.

### nonchalant — 2019-03-08T01:26:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-austin.23256/post-264741>

# Thread: Hello from Berlin, Germany - Biohacking my zip-code
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-berlin-germany-biohacking-my-zip-code.23294/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### OliverGruener2 — 2019-03-13T11:22:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-berlin-germany-biohacking-my-zip-code.23294/post-265332>

Hi everyone,

I want to introduce myself to you: I'm Oliver, 34 years and a passionate Biohacker from Germany.

Biohacking to me has nothing to do with drinking Bulletproof Coffee or tracking sleep or any of that stuff. This is superficial at best. I want to go the path you guys are going here: Biohacking my zip-code. Live as close to nature as practical. Enjoy the sun with all senses and get away from nnEMFs.

I am currently living in the biggest city in Germany. With a lot of wireless networks and blue lights around me. I am quite convinced that the my dry skin, strange headaches as well as all the symptoms my wife has (skin, gut, endometriosis) come from nnEMFs / too little sunlight.

We love being in nature and last year we decided to look a house outside the big city. Little did we know that now it looks like we might be moving 130km away from the city near the border of Poland. We are about to make a huge biohack with changing the zip-code. I can't wait for this to happen.

The house has a huge property where we can enjoy AM and PM sunlight as much we like to. Ground ourselves at all times. Grow organic food and do a lot of other healthy biohacks. The house is located in a tiny village where technology has no priority meaning big communications company are not interested in this area. So, 4G is limited and 5G probably far, far away.

I won't allow any wireless connections to be established in the house. Wifi and BT are off. I'll install kill-switches around the house to kill the power at night. Ground as many wirings as possible. I wire every computer (2) and mobile device we have.

I hope all this will improve my dry skin, dopamine levels and headaches, as well as the skin and gut problems of my wife. It will be also interesting to see whether the dog will change his behavior.

Professionally, I am currently switching careers from Marketing to Health Coach. I am a certified nutritionist and mental coach but I need more experience to be able to really help people.

Wow! Really exciting things ahead for you! It sounds like you have a great plan and the motivation to make it happen. I take it you've been here learning from Dr Kruse for a while. It's really hard for those of us in the US to find land similar to what you've found. I am hoping to move out of the US once my youngest is finished with school - 4 more years. I hope you will continue to track your journey here. Continued success!

### drezy — 2019-03-13T16:08:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-berlin-germany-biohacking-my-zip-code.23294/post-265342>

Cool.

I'm a big fan of lower population density.

### OliverGruener2 — 2019-03-13T17:22:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-berlin-germany-biohacking-my-zip-code.23294/post-265353>

It is funny isnt it... as a teenager I wantef to move to the big city. There was nothing for me in my small hometown.
Now, I could be moving from a 4 million city to a place with 150 people in it and it feels awesome.

I have to say, after all I have learned from Kruse there hasnt been much if any that I want more than this move!

### drezy — 2019-03-13T17:39:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-berlin-germany-biohacking-my-zip-code.23294/post-265357>

> [OliverGruener2 said:](/goto/post?id=265353)
>
> It is funny isnt it... as a teenager I wantef to move to the big city. There was nothing for me in my small hometown.
> Now, I could be moving from a 4 million city to a place with 150 people in it and it feels awesome.
>
> I have to say, after all I have learned from Kruse there hasnt been much if any that I want more than this move!

Same for me.

I think it's pretty easy math when you step back. I just heard a podcast about re-wilding a section of war torn Africa with lions. The host was shocked when he heard that once the artificially re-established lion pride produced lionesses near estrus, male lions from up to 80 mile away started treking toward the new pride. Males will travel the world for some uhhhhhh reproductive options.

In a big city you, like me, likely had more choices and thus better chances.

The wife I plundered from the city disliked the idea of living on acreage the first 2 years. Now, 15 years later she insists we plan to get 25 - 50 acres.

Enjoy the move and connect regularly with your new soil.

Yes, opportunities are better when you pursuit a career. But once you got your mind right, talked to the right people, and realize that fortune and fame are pretty worthless in comparison to health and peace of mind, well, then you need to move back.

I think there is something in each us that draws us back to land where everything is (more) quiet. Maybe it is because of the world we built through technology or maybe it is innately within us.

# Thread: Hello from Norman, Oklahoma
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-norman-oklahoma.23300/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Charmane — 2019-03-14T00:45:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-norman-oklahoma.23300/post-265420>

New to the site. Took me two months to finally get around to joining it. Recovering from brain cancer (no sign of recurrence since 2016, so far). I lived directly across the street from a huge (and rather low) cell tower (plus another huge antenna right beside it) for 12 years. When I moved there in 2002, the tower was just a pole with 3 little panels on it, but it just kept getting more "busy" and "thick" as the years passed. After 12 years (which included cell phones, lap tops, wi-fi, and a "smart meter" added to my home), I was diagnosed as having a brain tumor. Life's been rather crazy ever since. My son took care of my house and cats while I went to California for two years to try and "get healthier" after having had brain surgery. Now, I've returned "home," but I've decided upon getting here, to work with a realtor to sell this "tower-side property." NO idea where I'm going from here, but I want to learn about optimizing my life/health from this point forward. Thanks for welcoming me into the forum.

### caroline — 2019-03-14T02:22:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-norman-oklahoma.23300/post-265443>

Hi Charmane and welcome....

Please start a journal in the optimal journal section and give Dr. K. your health history and your Mom's and Gran's.

where do you live? work? Population density?

Sounds like you could spend a month or two in the Yucatan and see how you feel?

Have you done a 23&me test?

Jack is offering a new VIP event. Four people get to spend exclusive time with Jack and Jeremy in an unpublicized location so there are no interruptions.

Read Jason's new journal .....he just came back from such an event.

are you able to become a silver or gold member? You will have access to Jack's months webinars and the followup live Q&A when you can ask Jack anything.

### Charmane — 2019-05-02T22:12:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-norman-oklahoma.23300/post-269530>

Thank you, Caroline. I've been so busy trying to get my house sold, and get my 20-year-old out of it and more on his own (and finding a place where my cat can stay during all this crazy). Just now, after a month and a half, came back in here to see whether anyone answered me. My house sold this morning. A month or two in the Yucatan sounds awfully nice! Just no way to get there, I'm afraid! I think I'll take your bullets one at a time here, starting with starting on the journal and health history. Thanks again!

### caroline — 2019-05-03T01:43:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-norman-oklahoma.23300/post-269542>

baby steps Charmane. Congrats on selling your house. Take it slow and be methodical about your next move or moves. There will be lots of things to consider - maybe lots more than one move in our future.

think about renting or buying an RV and travelling around...... don't overlook any opportunities ....follow your instincts!

### Charmane — 2019-05-04T22:26:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-norman-oklahoma.23300/post-269688>

At present, my instincts tell me to stay out of RVs. I can rarely enter them, the air smells so toxic. I've had chemical sensitivities starting with being sensitive to gas stations when I was a child. Right now, I just want to get my stuff out of storage and into an apartment where my cat and I can settle in and I can plan "next moves." One day at a time... :/

I live in Norman, Oklahoma, and I haven't worked since 2015. My father has been 100% supporting me since then, with some exception for help from friends and my son helping me out and some part time help working in a friend's gift shop. I'd been a stay-at-home homemaker and parent during a marriage that began before I'd graduated from SJSU. So, after the divorce, the fact that I'd homeschooled my son from 1st through 3rd grade got me work as a reading tutor and a substitute teacher. From there I got a job as an assistant kindergarten teacher at a Waldorf/Lifeways school. But I only worked there a semester before my health required I work fewer hours... and that shortened schedule only lasted the spring semester, when I quit and "took the summer off to rest," and lived on credit cards. I have no idea how things would have turned out if my dad hadn't funded the huge things -- living expenses, a trip to Mexico, taking me in for 22 months and keeping my paid-for house's bills paid while my son finished out his 12th grade year and we kept putting pressure on him to work and/or attend school (more than he was doing). But I still blame that cell tower for troubles. My son lived in that house from age 3 to 20. He's been diagnosed with depression and anxiety and now the therapist is toying with the bi-polar label. I am glad that selling my house forced him out of there. He's now living with his father outside of town, up a dirt road that washes out in the rain, and gets lousy cell reception even though a tower is in distant view.

Norman, Oklahoma is the home of OU (University of Oklahoma), population's gone from 95,000 when I moved here in 1999 to 121,000 now. Can hardly believe an entire new city of people have come here in 20 years. It is getting more urban by the day, and being "anti-5g" is NOT a popular stance. The most interest I've found is people telling me, "let me know what you find out." Like I can actually report all findings to a list of people! Frustrating to be in a sea of people who seem oblivious...

I've never had a 23&me test. Leery of such things. Not sure I want my genetic information documented/owned by others. Do you think the test is that worthwhile?

### caroline — 2019-05-05T05:07:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-norman-oklahoma.23300/post-269701>

that would be a question for Jack.....about 23&me test

### Charmane — 2019-08-13T00:11:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-norman-oklahoma.23300/post-275863>

Hello again! Finally found myself an apartment on the outskirts of Norman and the radio field readings are much much much lower than where I was. No wi-fi and only a 3g phone from 2010 so far. Still have a ton of stuff in storage and my cat is still being cared for by my son. This has been such a long journey... 7 months since I decided to sell my house and see "where to go from here." I'll only be online occasionally, when I visit the public library branch near my "new" place.

I'm still curious about the 23&me test. But I'm not "qualified" to go into the "ask Jack" section.

### Saichi — 2019-08-13T01:12:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-norman-oklahoma.23300/post-275867>

Hi what are your new indoor RF readings?

# Thread: Hello from Vancouver, BC Canada
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-vancouver-bc-canada.23373/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kana — 2019-03-23T20:24:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-vancouver-bc-canada.23373/post-266279>

Hi Black Swans! I’m Kana from Vancouver. Originally born in Japan but immigrated here to Canada in 1968. First moved to a farm in Alberta then moved to Vancouver. I was pretty much diagnosed with many conditions at age of 6. Neprosis kept me in and out of hospitals during my elementary years. Doctors prescribed many type of meds which saved my kidneys but had profound effects after I was weened off. I lost majority of my hearing and developed eczema and seizures as a teenager growing up. I lost my little brother (age 15) whom I’ve had a very close relationship with to an automobile accident (drunk driver). I think that had put an enormous amount of stress on my health since my parents were also going through rough times as well. However, thanks to my parents healthy attitude towards life and eating habits I began to heal quickly and during my 20s to 30s my health was back... for awhile. Until recently 5 years ago I noticed some of inflammation from my childhood allergies was beginning to return. I’m 51 yrs old woman who has been on keto for 10 yrs so I figured I was going through menopausal stages. I knew that going to my doctors was no use cuz I always end up arguing with my doc about my prescriptions. I have always been so curious about everything that I ask. I began my own research on everything about nutrition and health. I found a doctor in Japan, forgot his name, who prescribed his patients to go swim in the ocean. All of his patients did heal without taking any meds or supplements. I had forgotten about that research for awhile I was seeing a naturopath who helped me balance my hormones. I am appreciative towards her but somehow I didn’t feel quite 100%. Recently started to watch YouTubes and listen to podcasts on biohackers on keto and light therapy where I ran into Jack Kruse’s podcast. I recalled I’ve had a small introduction of infrared sauna in the past, connecting with the info from the Japanese doctor and Jack Kruse that light therapy was missing from our health and wellness. I have a red light bulb I use during evenings and use red light therapy twice a day. I found it helps much more than taking supplements. I also live right next to a ocean so I get outside for my walk every mornings, afternoons and evenings. My hormones returned and insomnia fixed. My hearing has also greatly improved which doctors have told me I’ll never get my hearing back as I age. No more tinnitus either. I do believe that our generations’ health has greatly declined because of the radiation from our computers and staying indoors 24 hrs a day. Let’s all spread the word and thank you for reading.

### caroline — 2019-03-24T09:05:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-vancouver-bc-canada.23373/post-266321>

Hi Kana and welcome. I love your story of perseverance and discovery.

Please start a journal in the optimal journal section and give Dr. K. some health history and your Mom's and Gran's too.

We would love to follow your optimal journey to wellness......

well done for tying things together!

The three legged stool!!

### Kana — 2019-03-31T00:41:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-vancouver-bc-canada.23373/post-266867>

Thanks Caroline! I was having a hard time logging in this website. Bit slow to load and didn’t have the time to wait around till this weekend, my apologies.

# Thread: Exodus from Seattle Area
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exodus-from-seattle-area.23384/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jeffrey Sloat — 2019-03-25T19:00:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exodus-from-seattle-area.23384/post-266408>

Hello Dr. Kruse and All,

I live on the Seattle Eastside and have been experiencing health issues in terms of fatigue, sleep disturbance and numbness in extremities which started becoming more apparent 2.5 years ago. When I learned about Jack Kruse and explored his position in terms of light-water-magnetism I couldn't agree more with the general insight: nature healing, EMF technology damaging.

Shortly after my wedding last summer I began seeing the rapid spread of 5G towers throughout the Greater Seattle Met Area. Now what was a plan to leave the area in 5-10 years has become (for me) a much more pressing matter as I cannot maintain my dwindling health despite my age and honest efforts including visiting the beach and forest regularly (you cannot get well in the place you got sick in). Blood is not effectively reaching my extremity and my hands are most-always cold even inside even with OK core temperature. What I felt was an adequate quantity of radiation sticks is being added to at an alarming rate: more panels and more towers with some having 5 TIERS of radiators in an attempt to blast through the thick forests to provide faster internet? - I am repulsed, sick and highly concerned - the degree of spinal mis-allignment and depression I am experiencing is totally unacceptable, and despite rarely being sick I 'felt a cold coming on' for about 1/2 of the days this winter.

Unfortunately, my wife does not share my concerns, and both our family and jobs are here. For these reasons she is not interested in re-locating, while I would live in Chile, India or wherever if it meant not succumbing to disease - my quality of life is everything! Anyway, I finally joined this forum as I am seriously considering a major relocation and basically starting a new life at age 27. I know my health should be better- I know how many hours I spend in front of fires or grounding naked in the forest but all signs point to leaving my Home so that my life doesn't end in tragedy.

Do you have any suggestions for re-locating? I know low population density and a less developed country would help, and building a shielded house is in my future, but I prefer an outdoor lifestyle so I can't just build a bunker in Seattle. Is there a 1-3 year fix to where we can re-locate to rural Washington State with a goal/intent to move further? My wife is very concerned, I don't want to lose her but I will not lose my health to live out a crummy existence in Radiationville.

Due to this marital conflict I have been staying with my Parents in Seattle proper for 12 days and every night I have highly disrupted sleep - I literally woke up, wide awake at 0-dark-30 last night, no nightmare, no bump in the night but my body was completely awake - magnetism.

I don't want to dive before I know which end of the pool I'm at - would the Washington coast (with Olympic Mountain Range between me and Seattle) be a real change? Someone else mentioned Utah, is there any place in the USA where 5G is being resisted at therefor a potential relocation site?

Thank you all,
Jeffrey

### Jeffrey Sloat — 2019-04-05T19:34:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exodus-from-seattle-area.23384/post-267414>

Dan 2,

Thank you for your thoughts and the resources you provided. I was surprised to see such a lengthy response so quickly, (albeit a little ramble-y

I appreciate both the time spent and that you stuck your neck out to communicate these points.

Your message left me thinking and I have left you hanging, my apologies!

Being a new member, my perspective on Jack and his starfish has mainly been gleaned from podcasts, interviews, speeches. I have the Epi-Paleo RX but to be honest a lot of the information was over my head and I didn't see clear recommendations provided, more so the proper testing to diagnose. I agree he is a bit forceful with his approach and unforgiving of people who have differing opinions, but the information (to me) appears well considered yet flexible/evolving with regard to new information. I have not found another individual who has credit as a doctor and is aware of so many environmental/personal factors which contribute to health/sickness. Not that they aren't out there, but Jack has an inherent value for that reason. For example, with regard to sunlight/light therapy: I have heard enough physicists talking about the importance of appropriate sun exposure and effects of chronic lack of vitamin D to know Jackie is on to something, and that he can take it further to the benefit of his followers makes him valuable.

At some point that value needs to convert into money in our society, otherwise he will exhaust his own wealth spreading information that a lot of people are closed to or actively trying to suppress (ie telecom or supplement makers).

I've heard him say several times (effectively/reworded) that he is selling lifestyle changes which don't offer financial incentives for him to better ourselves other than altruism, so as a doctor there is a lack of incentive to pursue this course of action despite the value of his perspective. Although I wish I could 'have access' to Jackie without paying damn $80 a month, I have spent far more on far less valuable tools, and being compensated for his time and effort in directly assisting someone is valid, I still charge my gardening clients for time when I am educating them in their garden, there is value in that act, and our society assigns value in terms of money (most generally). He sells his time, content and gadgets like the Quantlet, but his actual advice (to my perception) is basically: 'if you are sick: move, get grounded, drink and eat well, consider where you sleep, see the sunrise, never stop learning/growing/thinking'. Added to that is 'if you want to learn more, we can talk online more concrete about your personal situation or you can come to a retreat of mine, or if you fly a lot consider the quantlet to help mitigate biologic stress' all feel valid reasons to be monetarily compensated but at the same time optional. To your comment about his compound/refuge and his financial incentive: there is a huge value/incentive for a sick person to seek that service/living accommodation to address their ailments, and without $ such a place would not exist or persist, so there is a further incentive for Jackie to charge for a compound that represents the culmination of his intellectual pursuit and which will provide value in the form of quality of life. To me this feels like a simple exchange of goods and services which again: our current society lives by.

Given the knowledge base Dr Kruse claims, and the ability for him to produce a plausible 'why' are enticing: it makes me want to pursue his advice directly to the point that I feel I would be better served to show up on his door step with cash, because every naturopath (3) I've seen have all either validated Jacks position or admitted a lack of knowledge regarding each area of concern. I can't seem to find someone who has a holistic approach with a profound understanding of root causes like Jack. The Naturopath yesterday recommended taking tumeric (I told her I would take the fresh plant) to address my low bloodflow to extremities. Now; is my body experiencing a lack of bloodflow due to a tumeric deficiency? They also recommended dedicating more hours to sleep since I told her my sleep has become more disrupted with 2 consecutive moves closer to Seattle proper. Is my body experiencing lack of sleep due to time committed to sleep or due to low quality sleep, and **WHY**? To the sleep question: Jackie points out magnetic influences, light exposure and hormones which he proclaims can be addressed by limiting blue light, allowing the sun to signal hormone production and either sleeping on the ground, grounded or on a Magnetico- preferably away from EMFs. At what point in the last sentence does Jackie profit, and at what point would the Naturopath have profited from tumeric pills?

Jackie aside, as I type this message I am experiencing negative biologic feedback (cold hands, aching knuckles, tingling fingers, body shivers, pitting out my shirt despite a comfortable core body temperature) which occur with any type of activity I pursue on my computer or cell phone: How I feel about the current activity (writing you) is positive, but the cold and aching continues, so I feel disinclined to believe that the problem I have is mental, or just mental.

In summary:
I don't think Jack is out of line in seeking financial compensation in the ways he does. That his free advise would mean relocation and a change of lifestyle for many/most modern humans does not make me resent him. If I leave my wife, and move away from my family of course that would be painful, but I could never blame Jack Kruse for such an occurrence. His science-y, academic approach to conveying information elicits fear, but the reasons why we are experiencing widespread neolithic disease is concerning, and should be feared. I don't think he is fear mongering because the fear is our reaction to understanding the dangers inherent in something like cell phone radiation: it isn't baseless and it isn't needless. That his talks trigger cortisol release is partly in his delivery and party in the shattering/juxtaposition of his scientific knowledge against conventional belief so I don't think fearmongering fits, but I totally agree (if this is what you are saying) that he instills fear by communicating his hard-line beliefs apart from the validity of the information.

Finally:
I am sorry, I don't think we are any further after all I have typed but I have enjoyed the correspondence. Hope to hear from you again, Dan2

Jeffrey

Edit: to quickly address Elon, whatever beliefs he has/shares he is deeply involved/intertwined/incentivized to continue and advance the tech industry and telecom as it works with his products- he has some huge skin in the game which makes me think 'bias'

> [Dan2 said:](/goto/post?id=266454)
>
> I look on this site for info including perspectives about how to handle EMF exposure, but there are other methods that have worked for some people that don't require you to literally move to a new environment. I'm not trying to cause problems with the members of this site or Jack, but since you're saying a possible move is causing problems for your marriage I'll add this. Some people have been able to change their neurological response to electromagnetism by changing their beliefs and feelings about it, basically. This is the main one I've read about:
> [https://retrainingthebrain.com/](https://retrainingthebrain.com/)
> I thnk for a while they only offered for people to attend seminars or buy DVDs, but I see now there's a book about it. Since I didn't know the book was available until just now and didn't want to attend a seminar or buy a DVD, I tried thinking some about the theory of how it could work.
> There's also a lot of information about neuroplasticity without info about the application specifically to electromagnetic sensitivity. Norman Doidge wrote a couple famous books about neuroplasticity and mentions others in those who also have books written.
>
> My thinking about it is that your nervous system uses electromagnetism to function, the electromagnetism in the environment affects the electromagnetism in your nervous system, and your brain also affects the electromagnetism in your nervous system. Since you have more control over your brain than what other people do around you to change the physics of the environment literally, by using your choices in how you think and feel to change the patterned responses in your neuronal networks of your brain, you can gradually shape and strengthen patterns of the neuronal responses in your brain to your perception of the environment which affects the physics of the electromagnetism in other parts of your nervous system which interacts with the physics of the environment you have less literal control over. I say less literal control because by changing the neuronal patterns of your perception with consistent deliberate choices in thoughts and feelings in response to the environment, you effectively "control" the environment in the sense of what that environment is to you personally as you choose to perceive it.
>
> It seems to me there's some tricky catches about it, though. When I say "control" your reaction to it, there's the question of extent of influence in the interaction between your chosen perceptions and the physics of the environment, meaning for example if you stand directly in front of a 5G antenna all day you should be able to, in theory, control your nervous system's reaction to the electromagnetism it puts out to some extent, but to what extent you can influence that reaction in comparison to what extent the electromagnetism it puts out can influence your nervous system will make the interaction a matter of influence between the antenna and your perceptions rather than "control". I guess you could still call it control, but since the control is by your perceptions, to have total control you'd have to have very extensive insight psychologically or maybe you'd say spiritually so that the extent of your control over the physics of the environment is almost total. Of course, most people don't have very close to that extent of psychological or spiritual insight, so the ability to control the physics of the environment in the way demonstrated in stories of some intensely trained yogis, for example, is going to be impractical for most people, but most people can learn to control their nervous system's reponses to the environment's physics enough that they can change the extent of influence of the environment's physics on their nervous system even if they can't use their control of their nervous system to change the physics of the environment around them very far beyond their skin. I guess you could think of the phsyics of a person's nervous system and the physics of the environment as being connected, and an extent of insight could extend the influence of that person's nervous system into the physics of the space around them but that extent of insight is uncommon and impractical or undesireable for a lot of people, but an extent of insight that doesn't extend the influence of the nervous system far beyond the skin but does influence the effect of the environment's physics in toward the person is more practically attainable for more people. Maybe when I say "nervous system" and the influence of the nervous system on the environment it'd be more accurate for me to use a term from a spiritual tradition like light body or aura or something like that; maybe what I'm calling nervous system would be more accurately included in a different idea about the body. For example, there are some yoga exercises that can increase the magnetic field of the body, or something like that, something about increasing the magnetism of the body's electromagnetic fields, and when Jack says people should move to Mexico or buy a magnetico pad to improve their mitochondrial health with increased magnetism of the right kind, I'd be surprised if the supposed increase in the type of magnetism from those yoga exercises isn't the right kind of magnetism, and so my point related to that is, there are old systems of thought about the body that acknowledge the light, water, magnetism aspects of health but use terminology that might seem unscientific or new agey or vague but that could be a more useful context of terms than saying "nervous system" when talking about the the dynamics, the physics of the interaction between your mind and nervous system and the environmental electromagnetism. So anyway, there's the catch of your practical ability to understand how specifically to influence your nervous system's reactions to the environmental physics with consistent gradual repatterning of your neuronal networks, in other words just because it's possible to do repattern the neural responses through conscious decisions in theory you still have to figure out specifically what an effective method to do that in relation to perceptions about electromagnetism is. Another catch is that it seems to me that one of the main changes in perception about electromagntism that helps people's nervous systems respond in a more healthy way is to not think, feel, believe that electromagnetism in the form of wireless telecommunications signals are bad or harmful to your body. But that's vague with the clumping together of think, feel, believe. So to be more specific - and I'm just considering these ideas, I don't really know what I"m talking about - maybe you could think that they are harmful but feel happy about it anyway and so align your belief with your feeling, like you don't know that they aren't harmful but you choose to believe they aren't so you can feel better about your interaction with them and as result pattern the neuronal networks to make that choice of believing it's better to feel good about it than know that they can be harmful a more automatic reaction of your neuronal networks and consequently nervous system when you think about whether the electromagnetism in the environment is harming you. Like your thought process is diverted by your belief in the priority of your feeling reaction to the electromagnetism, and if the neuronal networks affect and are affected by thoughts and feelings both then there's room in the theory of the neuronal network patterning for feelings to be prioritized over the thinking about the telecommunications signals, and the belief would be the go-between in the way the neuronal networks affect and are affected by the prioritization of various thoughts and feelings. And so another catch would that if believing the telecommunications signals are harmful can make them more harmful to the person's nervous system, then telling people they're harmful and that exposure to them should be avoided would make them more harmful for that person if they believe you, and so trying to change public perception about wireless signals and EMFs toward disfavor of them would be effectively hurting the people who believe that the inverse square law idea of avoiding them as the only solution other than legal measures should be their priority, and some people don't want to feel like they're on the side of the telecommunications companies by not warning people about the signals effect on health, they have an ethical issue with that, but I guess the neutral ethical position would be to not be in active assistance with more antennas being put up, not be active legally to remove antennas, not move to a place further from the signals where the inverse square law would be helpful, not move closer and increase your exposure to nnEMF, but to prioritize widening your perception of what "non native emf" is and who you are by affecting your interaction with nnEMF by making the first move in the interaction with your perception of your identity as a native of the universe instead of just earth and so identifying as a universal Mind instead of earthly body and so consequently effectively making nnEMF native aspects of your mind that can imagine beyond earthly limitations of evolutionary biology, or something like that, I don't know, to affect the physics of your nervous system in the present to adapt to environments imaginatively that people haven't experienced before, and that would be like a neutral ethical position toward the issue of telecom signals affecting health, or maybe toward almost any issue. It's kind of a cop out, I guess (as John Lennon said, "nothing to get hung about"), it's easier said than the ability to practically maintain that is done (and as Biggie Smalls said, "but can you maintain this shit, that's the question"), but it seems like when the issue is your nervous system's and body's response to new electromagnetic signals, with consistent efforts the neural networks can be patterned to make the body's responses physically healthier and as the new neural patterns become more habitual you don't need to make as much effort as consistently to handle those new electromagnetic stimuli. Where Biggie's "but can you maintain this shit, that's the question" applies in context of neural patterns becoming habitual in response to repeated electromagnetic stimuli is that there'll be variations in the electromagnetic stimuli as you move around a city and there'll be gradually more frequencies used for telecommunications that you would want to maintain the deliberate neural responses to.

### Novah — 2019-04-07T00:59:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exodus-from-seattle-area.23384/post-267499>

> [Jeffrey Sloat said:](/goto/post?id=266408)
>
> Hello Dr. Kruse and All,
>
> I live on the Seattle Eastside and have been experiencing health issues in terms of fatigue, sleep disturbance and numbness in extremities which started becoming more apparent 2.5 years ago. When I learned about Jack Kruse and explored his position in terms of light-water-magnetism I couldn't agree more with the general insight: nature healing, EMF technology damaging.
>

Now what was a plan to leave the area in 5-10 years has become (for me) a much more pressing matter as I cannot maintain my dwindling health despite my age and honest efforts including visiting the beach and forest regularly (you cannot get well in the place you got sick in). Blood is not effectively reaching my extremity and my hands are most-always cold even inside even with OK core temperature. What I felt was an adequate quantity of radiation sticks is being added to at an alarming rate: more panels and more towers with some having 5 TIERS of radiators in an attempt to blast through the thick forests to provide faster internet? - I am repulsed, sick and highly concerned - the degree of spinal mis-allignment and depression I am experiencing is totally unacceptable, and despite rarely being sick I 'felt a cold coming on' for about 1/2 of the days this winter.
>

For these reasons she is not interested in re-locating, while I would live in Chile, India or wherever if it meant not succumbing to disease - my quality of life is everything! Anyway, I finally joined this forum as I am seriously considering a major relocation and basically starting a new life at age 27. I know my health should be better- I know how many hours I spend in front of fires or grounding naked in the forest but all signs point to leaving my Home so that my life doesn't end in tragedy.
>
> Do you have any suggestions for re-locating? I know low population density and a less developed country would help, and building a shielded house is in my future, but I prefer an outdoor lifestyle so I can't just build a bunker in Seattle. Is there a 1-3 year fix to where we can re-locate to rural Washington State with a goal/intent to move further? My wife is very concerned, I don't want to lose her but I will not lose my health to live out a crummy existence in Radiationville.
>
> Due to this marital conflict I have been staying with my Parents in Seattle proper for 12 days and every night I have highly disrupted sleep - I literally woke up, wide awake at 0-dark-30 last night, no nightmare, no bump in the night but my body was completely awake - magnetism.
>
> I don't want to dive before I know which end of the pool I'm at - would the Washington coast (with Olympic Mountain Range between me and Seattle) be a real change? Someone else mentioned Utah, is there any place in the USA where 5G is being resisted at therefor a potential relocation site?
>
> Thank you all,
> Jeffrey

If you’re getting fatigue, depression, sleep issues etc it’s likely in part due to Seattle’s crappy, rainy weather. It sounds like you need trips south to get some sun.

What’s your vitamin D level? And is that from real sun or supplements?

Has your doc checked your b12/folate, thyroid/thyroid antibodies and ferritin levels? What’s your bun/creatinine ratio?

How much fish do you eat?

Do you eat dairy and gluten?

How much time do you spend outside?
How much time do you spend in front of screens?

Do you have D minder on your phone?

Have you checked your house for EMFs and geopathic stress, especially your bedroom?

What’s the lighting like inside your house? (Ie incandescent, CFL, LEDs etc)

Have you tested your house for mold?

What about starting with baby steps?

\- I’m not a fan of chronic use of tanning beds, but what about doing an experiment— what about trying a few sessions and see if it has any effect on your symptoms - It’s something you can do immediately to see if you notice a change.

\- Sunshine trips — why not start small with vacations south for you and your wife. Or go by yourself if she’s not interested in joining.

\- Weekend getaways — if you feel Seattle is not working for you, what about driving a couple hours on the weekends to explore/stay in different areas to see how you feel and if you guys like the area.

\- If you haven’t already, get yourself to a good Chiro - they can work wonders.

Other countries aren’t necessarily a blanket solution - everywhere has good and bad. India has a high rate of type 2 diabetics I believe, not to mention I know quite a few North Americans who have gone to India and ended up with some serious and long term tummy issues/complications.

The benefits of the west coast is great fish, great water, clean air, tons of trees, easy access to nature, easy CT in nature, the ocean/mountains and lifestyle/healthy minded people. So there is a lot going for it, it’s just a matter of, can it work for you with some tweaks or do you need to make bigger changes.

As for your relationship, here’s a stranger’s 2 cents... true love can be elusive so if this is someone you genuinely care about, I’d think carefully before I did anything drastic (while also knowing you need to take care of yourself).

You also can’t force your spouse to do anything and the more you push, likely the more they’ll resist. All you can do is lead by example and if you’re having a good time/feeling good and looking good, she’ll likely start to join in.

Chucking your life may work or may result in a huge depression. Personally, I like baby steps but everyone’s different.

All the best

### Jeffrey Sloat — 2019-04-09T18:53:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exodus-from-seattle-area.23384/post-267701>

> [Novah said:](/goto/post?id=267499)
>
> If you’re getting fatigue, depression, sleep issues etc it’s likely in part due to Seattle’s crappy, rainy weather. It sounds like you need trips south to get some sun.
>
> What’s your vitamin D level? And is that from real sun or supplements?
>
> Has your doc checked your b12/folate, thyroid/thyroid antibodies and ferritin levels? What’s your bun/creatinine ratio?
>
> How much fish do you eat?
>
> Do you eat dairy and gluten?
>
> How much time do you spend outside?
> How much time do you spend in front of screens?
>
> Do you have D minder on your phone?
>
> Have you checked your house for EMFs and geopathic stress, especially your bedroom?
>
> What’s the lighting like inside your house? (Ie incandescent, CFL, LEDs etc)
>
> Have you tested your house for mold?
>
>
> What about starting with baby steps?
>
> \- I’m not a fan of chronic use of tanning beds, but what about doing an experiment— what about trying a few sessions and see if it has any effect on your symptoms - It’s something you can do immediately to see if you notice a change.
>
> \- Sunshine trips — why not start small with vacations south for you and your wife. Or go by yourself if she’s not interested in joining.
>
> \- Weekend getaways — if you feel Seattle is not working for you, what about driving a couple hours on the weekends to explore/stay in different areas to see how you feel and if you guys like the area.
>
> \- If you haven’t already, get yourself to a good Chiro - they can work wonders.
>
> Other countries aren’t necessarily a blanket solution - everywhere has good and bad. India has a high rate of type 2 diabetics I believe, not to mention I know quite a few North Americans who have gone to India and ended up with some serious and long term tummy issues/complications.
>
> The benefits of the west coast is great fish, great water, clean air, tons of trees, easy access to nature, easy CT in nature, the ocean/mountains and lifestyle/healthy minded people. So there is a lot going for it, it’s just a matter of, can it work for you with some tweaks or do you need to make bigger changes.
>
> As for your relationship, here’s a stranger’s 2 cents... true love can be elusive so if this is someone you genuinely care about, I’d think carefully before I did anything drastic (while also knowing you need to take care of yourself).
>
> You also can’t force your spouse to do anything and the more you push, likely the more they’ll resist. All you can do is lead by example and if you’re having a good time/feeling good and looking good, she’ll likely start to join in.
>
> Chucking your life may work or may result in a huge depression. Personally, I like baby steps but everyone’s different.
>
> All the best

Novah,

Thank you very much for your response. I got some labs done last week, I will post the results and a more detailed response at that point - but I wanted you to know I have seen your post and appreciated all the thoughts

Jeffrey


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Hello fellow Black Swans...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-fellow-black-swans.23398/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kiersten Jarvis Webert — 2019-03-27T01:31:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-fellow-black-swans.23398/post-266537>

I have been on a healing journey for the last 19 years of my life for my children. Elise (21) and Ben (19) were both diagnosed with autism at the ages of 2 and 16 months (we were watching Ben closely and saw him beginning to decline). I have run a complete chromosome array and FULL genome panel at the Mayo Clinic and so far there is not a genetic reason found for their autism. I'm told by some researchers that it may be because I was sick a number of times while pregnant (working as a speech-language pathologist with preschoolers with disabilities), so most likely it has to do with my immune system while pregnant...ben and Elise's developing (broken) immune systems, many rounds of antibiotics between all of us, a lack of sunshine and vit D, and our mitochondria.

I grew up in Minnesota and we currently reside here... but I found out yesterday that according to my haplotype our first earth mama most likely originated in ECUADOR or a few other surrounding countries (B2b)! (scream face inserted here) Although I have always considered myself mostly Irish, I am now rethinking the amount of sun we need!!

Since my children were very young, I have searched for answers and pursued finding a healing path for them with all my heart. I lived in FL for a year and 1/2 to be close to a Dr. we were working with while trying hyperbaric O2 therapy, but unfortunately at the time I didn't know about the sunrise, sunset or the fact that I shouldn't have been slathering all of us with spf 80 sunscreen!! I've also had the children at the NIH and Johns Hopkins. When they were younger we tried infrared saunas 4x/day and I got rid of all the plastic in our home...(that was fun :/)...They have been GF/CF and low sugar forever....They also had between 20-35 hours of ABA therapy when they were from 2 to about 8?? My memory gets fuzzy!! Recently we visited Dr. Nemecheck in AZ and have the kids on his protocol (DHA, Olive Oil, rifaximin as needed, vagal nerve stim).

I am blessed beyond measure to be married to Mike Webert... and with our union I "adopted" another son with autism (Jack - 20) as well as our neurotypical Thomas (17) who is a goalie and an amazing brother. Jack has a genetic variant (CHD2) and a rare circadian rhythm syndrome called Klein Levin Syndrome. These are the the loves of my life!

So... I am here for my children, and in the meantime I know my husband and I will find fuller health and wellness in the process. Hopefully longevity because at this point we can't die before our 3 special needs kids!! lol - those of you who are in this same boat know what I am saying.

I have been wading into Dr. K's blogs, webinars and podcasts and have recently decided to dive deep and figure this out for good. We are willing and able to move - our children will come before any physical barrier. I would love to bring them to the Farm (I don't know if we can still be a part of that?), and we plan to come to the next member gathering with them. I look forward to meeting many of you in person and becoming connected beyond our computers. One of my dreams is to relocate within a community of Black Swans... somewhere in MX or Costa Rica... or Louisiana??

I look forward to learning and sharing with you all! I hope to help others along our path to wholeness. In the end, my goal is to turn around after finding true health and shine a big, bright red light for others to follow.

Namaste
Kiki (my close friends and family call me Kiki - I can't figure out how to change my name on this!! lol)

# Thread: Mingle Here With Other Single Mitos!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mingle-here-with-other-single-mitos.23413/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 20

### Gina R — 2019-03-29T18:37:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mingle-here-with-other-single-mitos.23413/post-266762>

Hello! I'm guessing that this title caught your eye Good and Welcome!

I started this thread so there's a place here in the forum for fellow single mitos and who are also seeking "a partner in crime"  in this journey for optimal health with our mito/quantum ways!

Along my journey, again and again, I continue to find it extremely challenging to really connect with potential partners for a "romantic" relationship, or however you want to phrase it! One of the biggest issues (for me) is when there isn't any shared foundations/pillars relating to how I want to live for optimal health. I've also found that it doesn't work for me, to start a relationship in hopes that they will change their ways or morph & join me in the adventure. For me, that just doesn't go so well! Perhaps, you can relate to how difficult it is to come across like-minded people, on top of that someone that is single, and on top of that someone that is "available" for a relationship!

So, I started this thread for us single (and available) Mitos to connect! I wanted to start the conversations and see what stems from us all connecting.

Please pop in and say hello. I'm open to this taking other forms, other ways to connect as well!

Looking forward to mingling with fellow single mitos

\- Gina

Hi, Michael here, I reside in Minneapolis Minnesota, but am moving out away from the city in a month, yay! 53 years old, mostly swedish, german, and bohemian, just a touch of irish. anyways, I am here to learn more about reconnecting with nature using the powers of light, water, and magnetism! I think I found the right place

Especially with the onset of 5G.

# Thread: Hi From NY!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-ny.23488/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Tim C — 2019-04-11T02:35:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-ny.23488/post-267818>

My name is Tim Conroy.
I'm a 49-year-old firefighter, originally from Queens, and now living in Orange County NY for the last 15 years. My wife and our two sons (ages 17 and 19) struggle with a variety of different health and medical challenges. Our 9-year-old daughter is fairly healthy but given everything I have been learning, I am concerned for the future of her health.
Working in the FDNY, I am constantly learning of friends and colleagues who are falling ill and dying of WTC-related deaths or other rare illnesses. It has led me to make health my number one priority, and over the last few years, I have been learning as much as I can through books, videos and podcasts. The more I learn, the more I want to share with as many people as possible. I feel so fortunate to have found the teachings of Dr. Jack Kruse and I'm looking forward to learning even more as a member of this community.

### caroline — 2019-04-11T03:53:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-ny.23488/post-267823>

Hi Tim and welcome.

Please start a journal in the optimal journal section and give Dr. K. some health history and also your Mom's and Gran's.

Have you done a 23&me test?

We look forward to reading as you discover and implement new strategies to save you and your family.

### Solidsilverteeth — 2019-04-18T10:16:47+0300

hi everybody, I'm so excited to learn these things. I've been reading a lot on here and listen to many of Jacks interviews.

I have been into health and always knew about the importance of light and magnetism from listening to Andrew Norton Webber ten years ago.

He taught mainly about water, but always he would say about the sun, and light is life.

I'm also wanting to learn all about DDW. I'm drinking distilled water, and my own orin (urine therapist) and would love to know Jack Kruse opinion about urine therapy.

I eat OMAD (one meal a day) which increases HGH by about 2000% in your urine which you then loop again and again everytime you dump any toxins at the liver and it gets cleaner and cleaner. I also practice semen retention which increases testosterone in your urine. And I do many other things which increase beneficial substances in the ultra filtered blood plasma (urine).

Anyway looking forward to continuing to learn together, what a great forum.

This is why all babies drink it for nine months before we are all born..

And actually 90% plus of the urine that the kidneys make goes back into the bloodstream anyway. If you loop that around 10% that goes to the bladder your body can reuse all the good things again.

### Sue-UK — 2019-04-19T16:57:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-post.23533/post-268429>

> [Solidsilverteeth said:](/goto/post?id=268402)
>
> Thanks Caroline, can you tell me, has the subject of urine therapy ever been seriously researched by anybody on here?
>
> It fits in so well with all of Jack teachings.
>
> If you get yourself optimal as possible, then your urine will be as optimal as possible.
>
> All those great stem cells with mitochondria Can be consumed again and again.....

Thought about .... some, so far enough to make me cautious.

I haven't delved into the stem cell aspect, but _harvesting_ stem cells from urine and perhaps using IV to put them into the bloodstream might be one idea, but before drinking urine I'd like to know how the stem cells in urine are dealt with going back into the body, especially after looping again and again. I'd also want to know if constantly chucking out stem cells via the urine was normal, or pathological.

What if we are looping the bad along with the good....If the urine is an excretion route, then apart from any stem cells, by drinking it wouldn't we also be recycling toxins, some of which, like organophosphates, are toxic to mitochondria and might be getting another chance with each loop to damage mitochondria again or be incorporated into our proteins, which then don't function properly?

Someone with a good redox might get away with drinking healthy urine, my 21 year old self might have handled it better than my now 61 year old self. If someone is sick, would that not mean that their urine would reflect that? Not just on a content basis, but on an informational level?

Those questions are just for starters ... Drinking urine to avoid dying of thirst might be one thing, but with my current heteroplasmy, mitochondrial inheritance and today's environment, its too risky a hack for me.

### Solidsilverteeth — 2019-04-19T17:04:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-post.23533/post-268432>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=268429)
>
> Thought about .... some, so far enough to make me cautious.
>
> I haven't delved into the stem cell aspect, but _harvesting_ stem cells from urine and perhaps using IV to put them into the bloodstream might be one idea, but before drinking urine I'd like to know how the stem cells in urine are dealt with going back into the body, especially after looping again and again. I'd also want to know if constantly chucking out stem cells via the urine was normal, or pathological.
>
> What if we are looping the bad along with the good....If the urine is an excretion route, then apart from any stem cells, by drinking it wouldn't we also be recycling toxins, some of which, like organophosphates, are toxic to mitochondria and might be getting another chance with each loop to damage mitochondria again or be incorporated into our proteins, which then don't function properly?
>
> Someone with a good redox might get away with drinking healthy urine, my 21 year old self might have handled it better than my now 61 year old self. If someone is sick, would that not mean that their urine would reflect that? Not just on a content basis, but on an informational level?
>
> Those questions are just for starters ... Drinking urine to avoid dying of thirst might be one thing, but with my current heteroplasmy, mitochondrial inheritance and today's environment, its too risky a hack for me.

All very good questions
Sue.

One thing to keep in mind is that all humans drink their urine for nine months in the womb.

There is no cap on the genitals both male and female pee into the space they are floating in. The cap is on the colon, that is the waste that goes out the umbilical cord.

So the fact the babies loop it all before they are born is a clue that it gets cleaner and cleaner the more times you loop it.

I know from experience that looping it again and again it gets cleaner and cleaner, you dump any toxins at the liver everytime you loop it.

Also keep in mind that of all the urine that the kidneys make, over 90% goes back into the blood stream. This is all the good stem cells and nutrients exactly the same as the roughly ten percent that goes to the bladder.

As for toxins, the liver is constantly cleaning the blood, the waste goes out one of the excreated ion channels your poop, sweat and out breath. Then the good cleaned blood goes to the kidneys to be regulated. The excess good stuff gets taken out of the blood and into urine which then goes slowly back into the blood stream, and a little of the excess goes to the bladder.

There are really only very very small amounts of any remaining toxins left in the urine, just the right amount to teach the body how to get rid of them and build antibodies to fight them.

### Sue-UK — 2019-04-19T18:38:29+0300

### Solidsilverteeth — 2019-04-20T17:26:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-post.23533/post-268499>

Thanks for this link Sue.

What is in urine? [https://www.livescience.com/39453-urine-chemical-composition.html](https://www.livescience.com/39453-urine-chemical-composition.html)

3000 identified substances and counting. My guess is there are many more thousands of as yet unidentifiable unknown wonderful substances that are made by the body for the body. We don't even need to know, just trust nature.

This is from my book OMAD Orin Looping.......

Why does the body make urine and excrete it, if it’s so good for us?
The production of urine involves highly complex steps of distillation and re-absorption. Most of the finished product called urine is reabsorbed back into the blood, only about between 2-10% goes to the bladder. This process is necessary to maintain a stable balance of body chemicals.
The kidneys also actually manufacture and produce hormones that affect the function of other organs. For example, a hormone produced by the kidneys stimulates red blood cell production. Other hormones produced by the kidneys help regulate blood pressure and control calcium metabolism.
The kidneys are powerful chemical factories that perform the most amazing function of regulating the blood. Remember the liver takes out anything bad, and the clean good blood is sent to the kidneys to be regulated. The liver is the primary workforce it produces yes actually creates lots of good things to go into the blood, this mega factory in your body even has storehouses and warehouses which can be tapped into and put into the ultrafiltered blood plasma (urine) when needed. This is also true about the kidneys but to a lesser extent. Your two kidneys as well as your liver together actually produce a plethora of good substances balancing the body's bloodstream. Sometimes taking out the excess of some things, and other times along with the liver actually producing good things to put into the urine, which 90% goes back unto the blood and upto 10% goes to your bladder. The other way to say taking out, or putting in is regulating. The kidneys regulate your now cleaned by the liver bloodstream.
For example, somebody who doesn't get outside in the sun much the kidneys themselves manufacture and produce an active form of vitamin D that promotes strong, healthy bones. This is just one example of countless good substances that if you have deficiencies then your body does it’s best to regulate. Same with too much of anything, good or bad. The bad stuff gets taken out by the liver, and the good stuff gets sent to the kidneys to be regulated. Once again because this is so important the kidneys regulate the cleaned blood that the liver has cleaned and decided is good for you, many substances, chemicals and water are either added to or removed from this filtered fluid according to the body's needs. If you loop it then it just goes round and round so your body can use it again and again.
You are the device. You are all that you need. Supplement manufactures want you to believe that you are so broken that you need all these extra supplements. You need to buy fancy liver and kidney flush packs and products. When the truth is all you need to do is go on omad orin looping and your body will soon sort out any toxicity or deficiencies. The Omad orin looping programme will clean the body right the way through. This is the best ever kidney and liver flush.

### Sue-UK — 2019-04-21T18:07:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-post.23533/post-268560>

> [Solidsilverteeth said:](/goto/post?id=268498)
>
> Imagine a supplement that can change throughout the day and give your body exactly what it needs at the right time, exactly what the body needs at that time and is bio available recognised to be used straight away. You just imagined your own orin.

If I am out in the sun, the wavelengths of light can initiate different hormones, and turn them on and off at the right time chronobiologically. Frequencies of light can also transmute one element into another. Real food grown at the latitude I live at gives information from the photosynthetic food web, as well as measurable nutrients. I can raise my dopamine by seeing a sunrise, or getting into ice cold water when temperature trumps light in the winter. Or I can raise my dopamine by meeting up with one of my friends for lunch, even anticipating that meet up can raise dopamine. Comparing any endogenously triggered hormone, or a vitamin or mineral in a locally grown food to one that has been made in a dish in the lab, biochemically they may be identical, but on an information level, I don’t see them as the same. We may re-use compounds, but at some point, if the i_nformation_ has been extracted from the endogenously produced compound, vitamin or mineral etc, then it’s whether it’s now the informational equivalent of a compound made in a lab. And whether relooping compounds that have already given up their information or gone beyond their best before date would signal to stop making any more, implying the potential loss of gaining new information. So for me it’s also about the biophysics, not just the biochemistry.

Bears don’t make urine or faeces during hibernation. If humans have a shortened hibernation, say 2 hours during the night, and a circadian rhythm as to when ADH is at its peak, then that implies to me that there is a either a chronobiological time to make the least amount of urine and so recycle what the body wants, or it’s a function of one of the phases of sleep (possibly REM). I’d also be concerned that compounds dumped into the early morning urine would reflect compounds usually only produced during the night and eliminated in the morning urine, and I’d want to know what drinking them outside of their normal circadian rhythm might do. I don’t think mother nature made a mistake not stopping us making urine at all so that we recycle everything, or not giving us an anatomy that hooks up the bladder to the mouth. Apart from things like exclusion zone, good quality drinking water brings in new information from the environment, urine might be just yesterday’s news.

I almost see it as the liver is the solar organ, and the kidneys are the lunar organs. As you can see, I imagine orin a little differently.

I really like talking to somebody educated and open minded. I've debated so many people over the years about orin therapy, but useually it's the same simple questions.

Absolutely correct about the light creating different hormones and substances, and these are all in your water of life. Yes the body does do transmutation turning one element into another, or even creating new things inside the body and putting them into the water of life.

Yes real food, locally grown is best. And the excess good things that the liver decides are good for you and decided not to be excreted from the body get sent to your water of life. Of which 90%+ slowly goes back into the bloodstream.

Did you know that when you raise your dopamine levels from good genuine feelings like meeting real friends then that dopamine will be in your water of life, and you get another hit of it next time you loop. You get more great feelings again and again.

We may reuse so many good things in the body or made in the body. You make a good point about the information extracted. This is a new thought to me that nobody has ever brought up in my multi decade debating urine therapy with some of the best doctors in the world.

This is the water that is made in the matrix by the mitochondria and changes throughout the day giving you just what you need when you need it.

### Sue-UK — 2019-04-22T13:45:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-post.23533/post-268602>

> [Solidsilverteeth said:](/goto/post?id=268578)
>
> Did you know that when you raise your dopamine levels from good genuine feelings like meeting real friends then that dopamine will be in your water of life, and you get another hit of it next time you loop. You get more great feelings again and again.

I don't think I want another hit of it next time I loop. Every day is a new sunrise, a new beginning, new experiences with the potential to raise dopamine. Acutely an animal might find therapeutic value in drinking their own urine, but chronically could be a different matter. An animal that is truly connected to the environment might find their dopamine levels dropping, and over time that could be the signal to change their environment or their conditions of existence ... move, migrate for the winter, hibernate, whatever.

I'd be wondering if looping dopamine through my urine has the potential to stop me seeking new experiences, keep me repeating old familiar patterns that no longer serve me, or stop me letting go of relationships I might recognise as unhealthy etc if I wasn't self medicating with urine.

If you had an identical twin, and they were healthy and you weren't, would you self medicate with their urine ...?

### Chris Topher — 2019-04-29T09:56:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-sexy-humans-from-melbourne-australia.23586/post-269213>

Hello all... I'm giving all the folk who post here with their info and help a big thankyou to start with...

I'm Chris from Melbourne, Australia wondering if anyone in the area has printed out all the blog posts that i could borrow perhaps... or buy... to save printing more paper or me from staring at more screens...

Thanks to all... stay sexy...

### Dave Key — 2019-05-02T02:12:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meet-for-coffee-tea-in-playa-del-carmen.23587/post-269461>

> [Peter Hawley said:](/goto/post?id=269447)
>
> I too along with wifey plan on a PDC visit 5/31 - 6/3. Is there a board or general meeting place here on the site where I may converse with those who are there or have experience traveling in that area. Would so like to meet more experienced hackers in fact that is the purpose of planned trip.???????

Be ready to do some hacking.
They only have smart meters here, every single place I've been in has wifi, and if you're staying by the beach there are cell towers.
I brought a body voltage meter, outlet detector and trimeter. After a crappy night's sleep I surveyed my room. None of the power outlets are grounded, which isn't as bad as a neutral to ground... RF is high by the windows.

The good news is, I have cement walls to block most of the radiation. So, tonight I will be on the floor so the walls can block radiation.

Other tips (you may already know): do not use ATMs along 5th. It's the best place to hack cards.
Take the ADO bus from the airport. Buy the ticket at the ADO counter before you walk out the airport. It's only $14 to Playa. $45 for taxis.
Get an RFID-proof wallet to carry credit cards in.

I’ve been following Dr Kruse for a bit over a year now— big learning curve even with a science degree! My then 15 yo daughter was Dx’d with MS although I’m not convinced. I think her problems stem from 2 sport concussions, a tick bite, a CPn infection, definite (now known) gluten intolerance (lots of celiac in the family), a booster shot given months after her second concussion and low redox! I’ve got her diet turned around, eating fish/seafood 3-5 x a week, trying to block EMFs, wearing blue blockers at night. She’s been symptom free for a year and 4 months now. Now I’ve got to focus on me- ringing in my ears, feeling my heart beating in the back of my skull, right side random numbness at night. I’m convinced it’s EMFs and need to step up my mitigation. I found an awesome oyster guy here- Oyster Tribe! Trying to up my intake and forcing my kids to have at least 2 as well. Thank you Dr Kruse for all you do. I honestly think you help more people than you know. My daughter is one of them. I look forward to learning more on this forum.

### Kara Nova — 2019-05-10T22:24:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/marin-county-artist.23652/post-270148>

Hello from Northern California, currently straddling a space between the SF techpocalyse and Mill Valley (who has protested 5g). The Bay Area was my home sweet home for a decade but my health declined about 5 years ago (along with the soul and spirit of San Francisco.) First mold allergies then adrenal fatigue then EMF sensitivity. I kinda lost my shit in 2015 with the daily chemtrails and “feeling wifi and phones” X-Men curse, so I tripped off to Peru for a year as a nomad. Been back since late 2016, trying to join the Bay Area exodus but not sure where to land... My career has been professional performing, dance, and circus arts. They say many dancers have a heightened mind-body connection (through training, yes, but perhaps we have it first and for that reason gravitate to the training?). I physically feel subtle sensations, let’s call them energies, and the tech/EMF literally blocks my chi, the energy flow. It makes me sick too but the fact that it kills my craft just gets to me. It’s been a struggle to try and do what I do amongst the changing landscapes. I spent a recent year living in Grass Valley outside of Tahoe, which is wonderful. Still gotta travel for $, though. Dreaming of an off-grid creative yurt village! Anywho, no one in real life really understands the strangeness I have been through and endure, so maybe some of the black swans will get it.

I first spent a month in Costa Rica, then about 12 months in Peru, and to my surprise at the time (2015) \*both\* countries had geoengineering. They were not as frequent or intense as the US, but still. In Peru they were more concentrated in Lima compared to rural areas, which seems to be a general observation of geoengineering overall.

Recently listened to a podcast where Jack suggests the chemtrails are put into place to create an artificial atmosphere to a) protect us from the third Van Allen belt of nnEMF radiation and b) to experiment creating an artificial atmosphere in preparation for terraforming Mars.

Had never heard those ideas before. Maybe in regards to the first, with the Earth’s magnetosphere weakening and leaving us more vulnerable to all sorts of space radiation, solar storms, sun flares, they’re protecting us from a future Carrington Event? Worried about the grid going down?

Other times it just seems like the Matrix, terraforming Earth to create ideal environments for super connectivity and 5G and smart dust surveillance and IoT.

That’s my long answer to yes, there were chemtrails and geoengineering in Peru. ‍

**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### KrystleSky — 2019-05-19T11:18:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-australia.23704/post-270820>

Hi all,

I've been lurking here for a few weeks after hearing the interview Jack did with the Up for a Chat girls. I'm half way through the book and trying to implement changes. I've just returned to Australia after spending the last 8 months travelling around SE Asia. I'm so glad I discovered the podcast as I was able to spend lots of time in the sun without the usual guilt.

Anyway, just thought I'd say a quick hello

### drezy — 2019-05-19T16:27:21+0300

### caroline — 2019-05-20T07:48:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-australia.23704/post-270892>

Hi Krystie and welcome.......

There is a huge aussie donut hole thread here ....have you come across it yet?

where are you living in Oz?

### KrystleSky — 2019-05-20T08:13:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-australia.23704/post-270897>

Hi Caroline,

I haven't come across it yet but I'll go seek it out! Thanks

Currently living in NW NSW, about 5 hours inland from the coast. The good news is I don't have to worry too much about 5G here, can barely get a mobile signal. Trying to work out how bad satellite internet is. We're satellite NBN...

Many questions have been asked over the years here. I've read many posts and tend to remember a bunch of them as well as search them up OK.

### caroline — 2019-05-29T06:50:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-pierson-fl.23731/post-271494>

Hi Jackie and welcome..... when you met Dan - is he as handsome as he claims he is???

I haven't had the pleasure yet!

I hate FB .....I now only use messenger for a few friends. It eats up way too much time ...if I miss something - too darn bad I guess.

### Jackie Beatty — 2019-05-29T12:47:09+0300

### Jackie Beatty — 2019-05-29T12:51:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-pierson-fl.23731/post-271503>

>
> I haven't had the pleasure yet!
>
> I hate FB .....I now only use messenger for a few friends. It eats up way too much time ...if I miss something - too darn bad I guess.

Hi Caroline, yes Dan is very handsome! And kind, to come talk with me when I was standing alone. Yes I am breaking up with FB, it is very time consuming/wasting and even though I know I'm not supposed to compare myself to others I always feel inadequate after getting off of FB. I think I will be happier without it.

### drezy — 2019-05-29T15:16:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-pierson-fl.23731/post-271512>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=271494)
>
> Hi Jackie and welcome..... when you met Dan - is he as handsome as he claims he is???

LOL!

> [Jackie Beatty said:](/goto/post?id=271503)
>
> I know I'm not supposed to compare myself to others I always feel inadequate after getting off of FB. I think I will be happier without it.

I think it's worth ditching it for a while.

I set up facebook in 2015 or so. I was applying to a hardware job within FB so I quickly put up one picture of kimchi I made on my timeline. Apparently the FB managers were not impressed...

Occasionally Doc will link to something he posts on FB.

Infi-sccrolling of people's saccharine portrayals of their life and getting caught in opinion echo chambers just isn't for me.

My own policy anymore is that FB is as conducive to a good life as 3AM at The Waffle House is conducive to finding a really good mate.

### Jackie Beatty — 2019-05-29T21:50:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-pierson-fl.23731/post-271552>
