> LOL!
>
>
> My own policy anymore is that FB is as conducive to a good life as 3AM at The Waffle House is conducive to finding a really good mate.

That's hilarious! Okay well definitely feeling better now. And it does look saccharine and unrealistic. It's like a fashion magazine...just now realized that. Thank you!

### drezy — 2019-05-30T02:50:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-pierson-fl.23731/post-271566>

When you back away from that over stimulus maybe spend a little extra time outside smelling the air at different times of the day and feeling the soil under your feet.

### caroline — 2019-05-30T05:43:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-pierson-fl.23731/post-271569>

really great advise Dan ^^^^^^ you rock - a lot!

### Jackie Beatty — 2019-05-30T14:43:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-pierson-fl.23731/post-271580>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=271566)
>
> When you back away from that over stimulus maybe spend a little extra time outside smelling the air at different times of the day and feeling the soil under your feet.

I will be doing that. I'm pretty ecstatic about this decision. Thank you so much for your advice and support!

### drezy — 2019-05-30T16:46:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-pierson-fl.23731/post-271595>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=271569)
>
> really great advise Dan ^^^^^^ you rock - a lot!

Just sharing what I've learned from Doc and so many people here.

I think the phrase "reconnect with nature" is amazingly layered and that whatever layer you can start at will be rewarded. There is zero need to feel inadequate. We all started from such different spots. Some of us have great intuition about all this and others are working on it. Any given day the most important choice is always your next one regardless of whether all the previous one's were successes or failures.

I started really really far back. I might have only mentioned this once before. For 9 years I sat with my cranium 4' away from 200-400Amp "instantaneous" power supplies 4 days a week 4 hours a day on average. They pulsed DC on and off like crazy. That's arc welder (those ridiculously bright welds done in construction) power level. Surprise surprise, that side of my head eventually developed quick growing some precancerous moles. Not cool.

### Jackie Beatty — 2019-05-31T00:13:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-pierson-fl.23731/post-271650>

> [Michael CULLEN said:](/goto/post?id=271581)
>
> Ola

HI!

### Jackie Beatty — 2019-05-31T00:22:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-pierson-fl.23731/post-271651>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=271595)
>
> Just sharing what I've learned from Doc and so many people here.
>
> I think the phrase "reconnect with nature" is amazingly layered and that whatever layer you can start at will be rewarded. There is zero need to feel inadequate. We all started from such different spots. Some of us have great intuition about all this and others are working on it. Any given day the most important choice is always your next one regardless of whether all the previous one's were successes or failures.

I love that word "choice". I have been working on my connection with nature and still amazed I got so far away. I got really sucked into tech and am now trying to limit it to work only. I'm simplifying and channeling "less is more".

I'm glad you got your cranium away from that environment. I just saw that you drink rain water, that is pretty awesome!

### drezy — 2019-05-31T01:00:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-pierson-fl.23731/post-271655>

> [Jackie Beatty said:](/goto/post?id=271651)

Fluoride is not an issue, but I'm spending time learning here how to improve it.

### DockMD — 2019-06-01T04:08:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-missouri.23766/post-271737>

I am a long-time follower of Jack Kruse. I have read much of his blog and listened to all of his podcasts. I have also lurked these forums for a very long time. I am a second-going-on-third year medicine resident (M.D.) in Missouri who will be doing private general medicine practice (as both an outpatient generalist and inpatient hospitalist) in very rural South Dakota starting in July 2020. I'm writing this introduction because I bought a Platinum Klub membership today.

# Thread: Hi from The Central Coast Australia
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-central-coast-australia.23827/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### D’Nyelle — 2019-06-15T11:48:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-central-coast-australia.23827/post-272685>

Hi
My name is D’Nyelle and I live on the Central Coast NSW Australia. I found Jack when I went my optometrist and was talking to her about my husband and his myriad of health problems. That was 3 months ago and I haven’t missed a Sunrise since. My husband was skeptical at first as he has been through the medical system for 32 years having rheumatoid arthritis since he was 19 leading to all the usual outcomes when you take all those drugs. He is seeing the change in me so it’s sparked a bit of hope in him which I thought was long gone I had to pull him off the window sill and Jack came along thank you universe Thank you Jack. So much to learn it’s so exciting.
Thank you for sharing your knowledge Jack I love going down this rabbit hole. Thanks again for the wake up call.

### Jenny S — 2019-06-16T09:51:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-central-coast-australia.23827/post-272721>

Welcome D'Nyelle, I'm living in Victoria at present, but eventually going to build at Clybucca. My husband & I have bought 100 acres there, so will be above you.
Where are you on the Central Coast?
Heaps of great info. here.

### caroline — 2019-06-17T09:03:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-central-coast-australia.23827/post-272760>

Hi D'Nyelle and welcome. We are on the north coast.

### KrystleSky — 2019-06-17T13:25:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-central-coast-australia.23827/post-272768>

Nice to see fellow Aussies. I grew up in Newcastle, but now in NW NSW. It's nice to read of someone finding alternate health info from a healthcare provider!

# Thread: Hello from Northern Ireland
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-northern-ireland.23929/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Alan Hall — 2019-07-14T14:06:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-northern-ireland.23929/post-274276>

Hello everyone,
I’m 46 and here because I’ve been looking for reasons why I had detached retina at the age of 41.
My surgeon saying “it’s just one of those things” wasn’t good enough for me.
I had a previous trauma to the eye and noticed my vision gradually deteriorating. I thought the detachment was because of the trauma and aging.
I have a cataract in the damaged eye and don’t want to go through the procedure to have it removed and the lens replaced.

I now believe the reason was poor diet -thinking I needed carbs for my sport, blue light toxicity - from working indoors at a PC and a lot of iPhone screen time; and pretty much constant wearing of sunglasses - I know, in a country that gets very little sun... crazy!
I now get natural light at every opportunity- some people accuse me of using sunbeds and am finding my vision is improving!
I’m so grateful for the help and advice given freely by Dr Jack Kruse.
I listen to him at every opportunity and have read a lot of the books he recommends.
He deserves a lot more recognition than he receives.
Thanks Dr Kruse.

### caroline — 2019-07-15T02:08:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-northern-ireland.23929/post-274306>

Hi Alan and welcome.

Please start a journal in the optimal journal section so we can follow your journey.

Great progress!

# Thread: Howdy from Central Alberta
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-from-central-alberta.23931/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Duane Baumgartner — 2019-07-14T19:56:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-from-central-alberta.23931/post-274287>

I'm a 69 year old male that has spent most of his life in rural,sunny Alberta. I love all the trees we have and can see the Rocky mountains off to the west when I travel to the west country, a short 30 minute drive. I love where I live. My grandpa homesteaded here in 1898 coming from Ireland. I live only 5 miles from the old homestead that my cousin still farms. My wife, now deceased, always said my roots went to the earth's core.

My whole life I have tried to be aware of what was healthy and what was not. You would never know that by looking at me, 5 foot 8 inches and 240 pounds! I know what to eat and what not to but at times I let my cravings trump my knowledge. I have been involved with many modalities and believe in the powers within.

I worked in construction my whole life and always got lots of sun. I'm an avid fisherman and when I wasn't working you could always find me camping and fishing. Then 2 years ago the bottom fell out of my world. I was diagnosed with Parkinson's disease to go along with my sleep apnea (12 years) and cataracts, which I had surgery on in January and April. Up until that point in my life I had rarely been sick.

Luckily I found a link to one of Jack's podcasts a week ago and things have really started to change. I had heard of **circadian rhythm** but had never paid much attention to it. I know I need to drink healthy water and lots of it, which I do. So for the last 3 days I have been getting up a bit after sunrise and grounding and looking towards the east, the sun. WOW!! Things changed so fast I can hardly believe it. Better frame of mind, way more energy and sleeping much better, just by catching a few rays!! Who would have thunk it!? LOL

Now I'm really getting into this with blue blockers, red light, healthy eating, more seafood and more Jack and his informative videos on Youtube. I have a real thirst now for more knowledge.

Thank you, Dr. Kruse

### Marko Pollo — 2019-07-14T21:24:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-from-central-alberta.23931/post-274288>

Hey Duane. Welcome. You are in the right place. Im no scientist, but absolutely believe that sunlight and circadian rhythm are non negotiable, critical ingredients of human health. I have great childhood memories of living in Calgary during the early 70s but we were afraid of Ponoka! Now id love to go there. Good luck.

### Duane Baumgartner — 2019-07-14T22:25:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-from-central-alberta.23931/post-274289>

Hey thanks Marko. I have heard all my life of people being scared of Ponoka because of the provincial mental hospital. There is nothing to fear from most of the mentally ill as they are just sick souls, that have a disease most don't understand, looking for help and compassion. Very sad stories in most cases though there are also violent ones too. They are very well screened before they get "town privileges."

You still in Calgary? If so are you tangled up with the stampede this week? Always a fun time.

### Marko Pollo — 2019-07-14T23:00:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-from-central-alberta.23931/post-274290>

Duane. I agree with you about mental illness. We were just kids. I moved away after grade 6. Moved alot as a child but Alberta was my favorite place by far. As i think back, im grateful to have lived there. I would love to go to the Stampede again. We went to the pendleton roundup last year and loved it.

### caroline — 2019-07-15T01:53:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-from-central-alberta.23931/post-274305>

Hi Duane and welcome....

Please start an optimal journal in the journal section ..... so we can follow along with you on your journey to wellness.

# Thread: Hi from League City, Texas, USA
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-league-city-texas-usa.23950/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matthew Smythe — 2019-07-18T05:57:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-league-city-texas-usa.23950/post-274506>

Hello Jack/Optimal Health Forum,

My name is Matthew Smythe and I am very happy to be here. My health story has started from when I can remember... basically my whole life (I'm 26) I have had issues with waking feeling unrested, memory, energy, back pain (2nd half of life), paying attention, headaches (which have recently gone away), and within the last 3 years urgent/frequent urination. I also have pretty bad bags under my eyes which I believe is a sure-sign that I am sick. My most upsetting issue is my memory and low energy levels. Unfortunately I feel very separated from the world because my memory is so bad and my energy so low. I have the passion and drive unlike any other and I couldn't feel more in place when Jack talks about having "skin in the game". But my inability to retain and articulate information has made pretty much everything in life 1,000X's more difficult I feel - especially than what others experience. I just want more than anything to be able to be the best I can be and in return help others. I have so many desires in this life with things like learning new instruments or learn what all is being explained in this forum but my brain and body just aren't up for the task. I'm very excited to be part of this community and I KNOW I will get better. Below is my past in summary with what I've done in chronological order.

Sleep Study (Narcolepsy)
Diagnosis for ADHD
Allergy Exam (Dairy)
Full Blood Panel (Show signs of low T but all else looked fine).
Many different holistic doctors with thousands of supplements and detoxes.
Doctor that removes toxins and infections from body with water through certain frequencies.

If there's anything I should follow up on just let me know. Thanks everybody!!

# Thread: Sincerest greetings.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sincerest-greetings.23953/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 28
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### wildperoxin — 2019-07-18T19:43:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sincerest-greetings.23953/post-274544>

Hi all,

First off, I could be better at introductions, and I’m nervous as all get-out. There’s too much I want to put down here.

I have no known severe or chronic health condition per se, unless we’re counting anaemia and allergens. I have observable sensitivity to environmental factors. Perhaps more sensitive than I can fully be conscious of and I lie to myself/justify that I’m stepping into hypochondriac extremes. By and large, I’m more upset or concerned when seeing others suffer, and by lies/lack of clarity and transparency i.e. their health deteriorates, psychological and other forms of abuse, etc.

Mostly, I want to network. I need help, to understand things better, and to get things done, as well as improve production capabilities.

Living in the UK with a single parent and three younger adolescent siblings is like fighting a maelstrom while climbing a mountain sometimes. We are first generation. Part of the reason I remained with my family or in the country was in the hope that I was able to set up a better living space and support while here, either provide information when they ask re: light, water, magnetism i.e. setup Ethernet/internet of things, lighting, water source, and so on. If I were to move. Say, yes, able to acquire some of these things e.g. a water distiller and alternative lighting/blackout options, yet still ways to go.

The city we live in has recently rolled out 5G. Preparations to pack and go, at least temporarily, for me have come to mind. I receive some support from my family, and I seem to have this belief that nowhere else will provide that. I know logically and feel intuitively that there’s better support. Blood is hard to leave.

Back to the point of first generation: we are originally from East Africa, not far from the equator. I was born there. I have more family there, and I am and have been training, studying and working so that I can practice and work back home.

When I was younger, I made a solo trip back to our home country. Holy cow – talk about a world of difference in terms of all areas of health! Mood, energy levels and so on improved. There’s a link with being closer to the equator, or maybe I feel better in my country of origin.

There’s much empirical evidence of people travelling from higher latitudes down closer to the equator and feeling better, typically after a period of acclimatization.

So, I’d like to ask for help. What actions or suggestions with regards to family?

I’m really looking forward to exploring these forums more and engaging with people. Due to shyness, or uncertainties and anxieties, I realised how important it was to join y’all here, and did not before. That’s changing. Thank you all for sharing your wonderful experiences over the years! And thank you Dr. Kruse et. al.

‘till next time,

Best!

### JanSz — 2019-07-18T20:15:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sincerest-greetings.23953/post-274546>

Hi, wildperoxin.
You have a number of choices to make.
You are definitely not hypochondriac.
Especially with a list like you posted.
anemia and allergens
observable sensitivity to environmental factors
If you want to follow [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) footsteps your goal is to become (Jack's type) (nomadic) narcissist.
You have come from about ideal place (per Jack's standards) so any other place would be not as good.
Technology makes people (temporarily) more comfortable, but in the long run, it leads to destruction.
\-------
I am interested in how have you decided on having your nickname.

welcome
do not be nervous, no need
.....

### wildperoxin — 2019-07-22T16:30:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sincerest-greetings.23953/post-274756>

Thanks everyone! And yes to the Optimal Journal!

[@caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) I'm working on becoming a practitioner, and I had hoped to do clinical research also. I studied Physics at university, opted for Mathematical Physics, took some time off to work and study in Health and Social Care and Health and Social Sciences; I started doing Complementary Healthcare, and now I don't know if I want to stay in the UK to finish. I'm a qualified practitioner in some \[CAM\] therapies, still training in others. I've thought that it might be a better approach to improve my Spanish and go continue to study somewhere else, or stick solely to distant learning course structures?

Education's been a rough ride. One thing I found tricky was being in rooms with artificial lights and nnEMFs for extended periods, and living in university halls of residence for the first 2 years - that was a bad choice. I work with patients who have Alzheimer's and dementia, among other conditions. It is so extremely upsetting and infuriating at times seeing how mainstream healthcare can push their backwardly, upside down standards to and for insane ends and their effects on a general populace.

I have blue-light blocking glasses which are obviously orange/red tinted. Funnily: one other Physics student at our university used to wear these orange-tinted glasses all the time - indoors or outdoors. I want to get some clear ones that I can wear while working, because sometimes you need to see what you're doing clearly

I'm in a situation of financial uncertainty/instability, otherwise, I'd pack up and move right away! Or at least travel to a place with lower population density, nnEMFs and artifical lights and where I can increase my solar callus.

Cold thermogenesis help me quit coffee/caffeine, of which I'm super grateful for. We live in an area near a river with a lot of woodland, another thing to appreciate. The amount of UVA and UVB here at a relatively lower altitude of ~39ft is not optimal. UVA lamps help; albeit bulbs need replacing. In the evenings, red LED lamp indoor, or/then straight up candles. I have an IR lamp with visible red light that needs its bulb replaced. Just for some context, as there are lots of other details. When 5G rolled out a few months back, I was like, "Well, ya knew it was coming! You didn't know when but here it is!"

I'm going to Spain for a week soon, 40° latitude, and hoping the seafood will be good!

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=274599)
>
> [View attachment 12648](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/12648/)

100%.

The coldest our running water gets. I'll see if a metre and/or thermometer can give me readings. It's around or below 5° Celsius by estimation.

I aim for a minimum of 160 minutes a week, 10 minutes head immersion weekly, which breaks down to roughly 40×4. Even if I don't have time to time it, I take a quick cold bath/shower, usually morning after being out in the sun or at night before bed.

### JanSz — 2019-07-27T15:28:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sincerest-greetings.23953/post-275054>

[@WalterNL](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19189/)
[@drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/)
or anybody else
The average water contains 150 deuterium atoms per million hydrogens.
150/1000000=0.015 %

on post #25 or #26 above
dr Boros uses 0.015 % as an approximation of the volume of heavy water within all water.
So 0.015% of 7.2 cubic meters is
0.00015\*7200=1.08 liters
Is that correct?
It would be correct if all deuterium containing water was DHO.
But there is D2O.
If all deuterium containing water was D2O the 1.08 would be halved.

**What is the ratio of D2O, HDO, and H2O ?**
\---------------
a(16)+b(17)+c(18) = weight
a+b+c = 1L
\---------------
Semiheavy water
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Semiheavy\_water](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Semiheavy_water)

Heavy water
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heavy\_water](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heavy_water)

My wife and I just sold our home (this process started pre-mitochondriac) but it sure is serendipitous. We are moving from Chicago to the East Mountains outside of Albuquerque. **Climate in Zip 87059 (Tijeras, NM)**
**Tijeras (zip 87059), New Mexico gets 18 inches of rain, on average, per year.** The US average is 38 inches of rain per year.

**Tijeras (zip 87059) averages 44 inches of snow per year.** The US average is 28 inches of snow per year.

**On average, there are 278 sunny days per year in Tijeras (zip 87059).** The US average is 205 sunny days.

### Clarissa5 — 2019-07-23T14:53:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-australia.23979/post-274852>

Hi from Sydney Australia! I thought I would introduce myself as it seems to be the function of this forum thread.
I have done a little bit of research on Mito issues as my 19 year old son has chronic fatigue, which I now know is due to biotoxin illness. I have tried deuterium free water and use a mitogen red light panel, still waiting to see results but its only been a couple of weeks but I do feel more energy. And of course I get out in the sun - its been a very mild winter here in Sydney!

### caroline — 2019-07-24T05:08:14+0300

# Thread: Hello from Generation Z
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-generation-z.24111/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jorge Roman Lopez — 2019-08-20T21:47:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-generation-z.24111/post-276315>

My health journey in nutrition took off last year with the keto, paleo, and carnivore people––the ones that insist food is everything. I spent too much time(what I consider to be too much time––I'm 19) tracking my food at every meal, and getting my food ratios just perfect. I tried keto for a few months and felt worse––much worse. I was tired all the time and I felt terribly weak when I went to go lift at the gym(indoor, artificial lights). During these dark days, I was learning that food is the absolute cure––you can spend your whole life in what Dr. Kruse calls an "EMF hellhole", but as long as your diet and exercise is right, you'll be as healthy as you can ever hope to become. Being a crazy idealist, I rejected the diet plus exercise = health dogma. There had to be more...

And indeed there was. It wasn't until I listened to a podcast by Neurohacker Collective that I stumbled across Dr. Kruse's work. I listened to that episode twice and shared it with everyone who I knew would be able to break down their own limiting and incorrect beliefs, as I did. Then I got to work. I listened to every podcast I could find that had Dr. Kruse in it and started to read the blogs.

This was the first time researching nutrition where I truly sensed the truth, and it didn't come in a "superfood" or a supplement, but rather from being aligned with nature. Of course I think that deep down everyone knows that being in nature is good for you but I find that it is often swept aside, because _nobody_ I have come across has explained it using quantum biology.

I should add that all of this independent research occurred during the summer. I found it very revealing that I learned more in these past three months of my summer through Youtube, Spotify podcasts, and blogs on the internet than I have in college so far!

I am still only at the beginning of the journey, and I'm eager and ready to learn much more. It's nice to find a group of people so dedicated to learning about health

### caroline — 2019-08-21T06:24:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-generation-z.24111/post-276346>

Hi and welcome Jorge ......


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Hello from UK
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-uk.24115/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tracie74 — 2019-08-21T09:52:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-uk.24115/post-276350>

Hi.
I'm new here and although feeling overwhelmed, I am reading through articles that I feel would benefit me, which is a lot of information. What brought me here was a very bizarre diagnosis of being photosensitive. Trust me, it was the most upside down thing that anyone could have told me, that I am having an allergic reaction to the sun. I have not always been this way and the problem began around 6/7yrs ago, I was never the same after the great big chemical spill in the sky. Every summer, my skin began blistering in the sun, sometimes the reactions I would get would be highly concerning. My eyes and face could swell out of shock, but worst of all was how my brain would feel, like it was being fried and experiencing mini strokes. I knew I wasn't having an allergic reaction to the sun, but what is in the atmosphere. I am allergic to a modern way of living.
I have just moved from a city that is preparing to be switched on to 5G, they chopped down thousands of street trees for such preparations and even though they have stopped doing so, their canopy was lost and feeling chocked was only getting worse. This year, it may have well saved my life to move from a city that never switches down and always saturated in artificial light. Here I can get all the morning sunshine to begin healing myself back to Optimal health. I am getting back to basics with my life, because a modern way of living was literally frying my skin, my brain under the sun.
I am sensitive to light, but the sun is NOT my enemy. I am feeling the consequences of technology and how artificial the light is. I need things real, and being among nature, a part of it is a part of the healing. I am here to understand all that I can that will best help me to navigate my way back to good health again.

### Johan Lindøm — 2019-09-01T08:31:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-uk.24115/post-276950>

Welcome to the club!

### caroline — 2019-09-01T10:42:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-uk.24115/post-276951>

Hi Tracie and welcome.....

### CarolandNick — 2019-09-02T21:24:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-uk.24115/post-277011>

Hi Tracie, we are also from the UK. Welcome.

### tracie74 — 2019-09-14T11:56:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-uk.24115/post-277822>

Thank you all.
We moved from the city in June (I feel this may have well saved my life) and I have been enjoying the morning sunrise just about every morning. This has been helping me considerably with my skin. The atmosphere is different here also, everything switches off at night and there are no bright lights like there are in the city. I no longer feel like I am being chocked out and my environment is now aiding my healing process. I worked mainly indoors, within concrete buildings under bright artificial lighting. This also is changing and now taking my work outside as much as I can. I have this very simple goal, it's just to be back to normal again. To be able to spend all day outside on a warm summers day, to go for long walks on the beaches again, or hiking without the worry.
I am grateful for the diagnosis, also grateful for my gp's honesty in saying there is nothing he can do to help me. It means I haven't the extra worry about any medications they could be giving me and also the healing is 110% down to me now.
The articles that I have been reading have been a great aid in getting to grips with these strange symptoms. Thank you Cuffy for the link.

### wildperoxin — 2019-09-14T16:04:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-uk.24115/post-277826>

Welcome, Tracie! In the UK also. Hope your photosensitivity continues to improve.

We all do that.

### tracie74 — 2019-09-20T14:03:14+0300

### MITpowered26 — 2019-09-21T20:49:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-uk.24115/post-278494>

> [tracie74 said:](/goto/post?id=277822)
>
> Thank you all.
> We moved from the city in June (I feel this may have well saved my life) and I have been enjoying the morning sunrise just about every morning. This has been helping me considerably with my skin. The atmosphere is different here also, everything switches off at night and there are no bright lights like there are in the city. I no longer feel like I am being chocked out and my environment is now aiding my healing process. I worked mainly indoors, within concrete buildings under bright artificial lighting. This also is changing and now taking my work outside as much as I can. I have this very simple goal, it's just to be back to normal again. To be able to spend all day outside on a warm summers day, to go for long walks on the beaches again, or hiking without the worry.
> I am grateful for the diagnosis, also grateful for my gp's honesty in saying there is nothing he can do to help me. It means I haven't the extra worry about any medications they could be giving me and also the healing is 110% down to me now.
> The articles that I have been reading have been a great aid in getting to grips with these strange symptoms. Thank you Cuffy for the link.

HI [@tracie74](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/23916/)

The one caveat I would consider, as far as drugs.. is if it is still a concern/if you are still finding a right amount of sunshine without a negative allergic reaction, is pre-dosing an hour before sun exposure with and chewing 10 to 20mg of ceterizine (antihistamine, reactine is the trade name in canada) (use 20mg if you can tolerate it, it may cause drowsiness at that dose for some people), AND also chew 40mg of famotidine (brand name PEPCID in canada.. its a heart burn med, but actually dual's as another antihistamine) - AVOID ranitidine/zantac ( the other popular heart burn med). That may help mitigate at least some of an allergic reaction., along with introducing your dose of sun exposure slowly in a step wise fashion (Sunrise and or sunset first, later on afternoon sun if its tolerated. maybe depending on your comfort level, severity right now, track and time exposure.. EG. 15 seconds at sunrise for a week ONLY. then 30 second next week. tweak the length and aggression with how long you wait til next dose increase, at your discretion. if you react in the slighest, go BACK to the previous dose and continue from there. GO LOW AND GO SLOW... I think that's the key in this situation.

If you do get a reaction, chew right away another round of the two pills mentioned above.

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)

J

### MITpowered26 — 2019-09-21T20:59:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-uk.24115/post-278496>

That may help mitigate at least some of an allergic reaction., along with introducing your dose of sun exposure slowly in a step wise fashion (Sunrise and or sunset first, later on afternoon sun if its tolerated. maybe depending on your comfort level, severity right now, track and time exposure.. EG. 15 seconds at sunrise for a week ONLY. then 30 second next week. tweak the length and aggression with how long you wait til next dose increase, at your discretion. if you react in the slighest, go BACK to the previous dose and continue from there. GO LOW AND GO SLOW... I think that's the key in this situation.
>
> If you do get a reaction, chew right away another round of the two pills mentioned above.

[@tracie74](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/23916/) I'm not sure if that was clear... but I'll illuminated a dosing regim further as an example:

15 second ONLY, once weekly. Sounds extremely slow, but no harm being safe. Again, you can adjust as your body informs you.
Another factor could be the amount of skin that is exposed to the actual sun at any given time. More skin exposure, more dose you are getting at one time. This should be controlled and tracked.. increase the surface area step wise as well.

Not sure if I am out to lunch here based on the history, so take it with a grain of salt!

# Thread: Hello from San Antonio!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-san-antonio.24134/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Seth Nelson — 2019-08-23T01:12:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-san-antonio.24134/post-276465>

I have been loving these resources recently, so I’m grateful to be here in the Forums now. A friend was encouraging me to get more sunlight, and pointed me in the direction of Dr. Kruse. I’ve read his book, and am only beginning to dig into the website here.

My interest in wellness started in late 2016. I was a habitual over-exerciser and junkfood-eater. I workout hard, so I deserve this high-calorie fluff, right? I now see I was causing inflammation from (at least) two directions there. Some odd symptoms (intermittent light-headedness) started to affect my work (musician), and so I found my way to a neurologist who diagnosed me with MS (at age 36). She put me on a steroid for a few days, and prescribed Copaxone and Kepra. By January 2017, my symptoms had gone away, and I have been symptom-free since, for which I am grateful. But it shook me up, and turned me on to this path of learning what I can about health and wellness.

Since then I’ve been doing a lot of amateur biohacking as my new hobby. My wife and I were familiar with Whole30, so went back on that first. My neurologist directed me to get more healthy fats in my diet, and steered me in the direction of Dave Asprey and Dr. Wahls. My diet has become generally anti-inflammatory (no gluten, added sugar, dairy, nightshades), organic, high-fat, lots of local seasonal veggies, and time-restricted (8-10 hour window of eating). Exercise has moderated. I stopped the distance running, and settled into a routine of superslow heavy weights, HIIT, and Aikido. After making these and so many other changes (I’ll elaborate more in my journal), my neurologist approved me to stop taking Copaxone, so I tapered off and stopped that back in October 2018.

Since discovering Dr. Kruse, I have added the sunrise and UVB (which we get year round here on the 29th Latitude) into my daily routine, and when possible I include the sunset (I often work during that part of the day), and moved my eating window earlier (about 7am-5pm).

I’m particularly interested to be learning more about sunlight. My mother passed away of melanoma back in 2003. Also interested to learn more about Vitamin D. My levels were below 30 in 2016. And I am a little overwhelmed, since the more I learn the more I realize how little I know....especially on Dr. Kruse’s blog. I’ll post a journal for more details. I’m grateful to be here.

### caroline — 2019-08-23T09:13:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-san-antonio.24134/post-276479>

Hi Seth and welcome.....

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-portsmouth-uk.24221/post-277906>

Hello Everyone!

My name is Elliot. I'm 26 and I'm from Portsmouth on the south coast of England. I've been following Dr Jack Kruse for about three year now. It all started when I discovered by accident the video of Vermont 2016 on Youtube. Jack was talking about photosynthesis and light. I was gobsmacked watching it as I was very into training and diet at the time. I started to watch video after video and just couldn't get enough. The thing is, I didn't fully commit to signing up to the forum and patron and fully diving in to the world of quantum biology until recently. I feel like it's time to get my skin in the game and stop being on the sidelines in my own life and take the first step forward.

I want to be the strongest version of myself and I believe I've found the right place to do that. I'm looking forward to joining you on your journeys to optimal health and to talking with you.

Peace,

Elliot

# Thread: Hello from Washington
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-washington.24279/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BartlebytheBlessing — 2019-09-24T01:04:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-washington.24279/post-278715>

Hello all!

I came across Jack's work via the one of the Luke Story podcasts and have been listening to a bunch more and checking out the forum over the last couple of months. About to dive into the blogs - just plan on working through them chronologically.

Excited to be here and soak in the information. Biggest challenge is to stay positive and not get overwhelmed by fear associated with all the toxicity that is in my environment and out of my control. Second biggest challenge is not getting too stuck in information gathering mode and letting that hinder me from taking action to maintain and improve my current health and the health of my family (wife and two little boys).

I don't have any major health issues currently that I am aware of, aside from feeling the effects of aging as I get further into my fourth decade on the planet. Haven't done blood tests yet - I know Jack has a list of recommended ones in the book, but haven't got that far yet. All that said, I have been taking some basic measures (turning off wifi, getting barefoot sunshine, much more considered cell phone use, improving sleep, etc).

### BartlebytheBlessing — 2019-09-24T02:48:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-washington.24279/post-278721>

Mr.angry. Remember these childhood books?
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### MITpowered26 — 2019-10-13T14:07:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-funny-faces-of-the-forum.24324/post-280303>

MR. SAD...

The intention behind this photo I took this morning may very well be the most important photo of me from the last 34 years..

It's a FUCK YOU photo.

I remember this especially on the commutes home throughout the city through the years. Tired. Unable to put on a brave face, a mask of normalcy.

I remember so badly to just wanting to let in the rush of sadness, being "not okay," just doing something that I knew could, would, attract attention from cars to my left and right.. "Wow, he looks really sad".. or even more fucked up "He doesn't look normal for this situation of driving a car" Oh, how anti-social driving is.

Ironically, this photo I am not happy with because it doesn't do the justice to the sadness I felt on those drives... and the fight I put up with myself, to look normal.

So this is my coming out party... my fuck you to normalcy. My self-expression that its okay to not be okay, yknow?

Every winter here, in the dark and cold, I get the urge to fly south. So now, since listening to Dr. Jack Kruse only 6 months, I know why. I would like to get near my family in the Texas Hill County. Hi Saichi,

well usually RF is not too bad, I don't have specific data, but we have no WIFI on in the house, and I'm here mostly, or I'm at the pool or outside in nature, and I try and minimize my exposure to "data" being turned on on my phone (4G). Just recently last few weeks the neighbours installed a smart meter which sits about 8 meters from my bedroom, but from testing with a cornet EMF meter the levels are not that high in my bedroom (still around 0.02 V/M) which is apparently pretty low and shouldn't cause issues except for extremely hyper sensitive people.

Thanks Simon, I will have a look!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-been-a-while-question-re-jack-being-a-neurosurgeon.24483/post-282863>

Might want to educate yourself on electricity and ways to mitigate it while you sleep.

# Thread: Hello from Finland!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-finland.24527/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Steve Ericsson — 2019-12-17T09:11:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-finland.24527/post-283690>

How wonderful to be here!

I have been following Dr. Kruse for quite some time now, and finally
took the step to join.

Me and my family have already experienced the benefits from understanding light (not that we are far on our journey, but we are taking the first steps right now) - and let me tell you - Finland is really dark this time of year.

I am from Sweden - my wife from Finland - and we decided to move from Stockholm (Sweden) to the countryside in Finland. Or actually a small town with 6.200 people in the area.

But even here we now have the first 5G right among the few shops and restaurants there is. In other word - right where most people will be forced to go if they want food, clothes, tools, or go to the library.

I am looking forward to be guided (as Dr. Kruse told me before joining) to how to protect my children and myself - and how to create optimal health in the future.

Thank you for taking the time to read this message!

Steve Ericsson

### Inger — 2019-12-18T18:16:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-finland.24527/post-283771>

Welcome Steve!
Great to have someone from Finland around   I was born and grew up there, so feels like home to me
Too bad Finland is crazy about 5G. Really sad. Otherwise I might have moved back there - I love the culture, nature, and the people! - but I do not want because of their tech use.
Lots of my family lives there and my mom and dad.
My brother told me today that their new prime minister, a quite young lady(I guess 35 yo?), want to push veganism, and make meat illegal?? How can someone do such a thing.. or even try. It is crazy. For Finnish people veganism is deadly. It is so far north and so cold and long winter. They need animal foods, and lots of it!
But my brother told me there is lots of push for veganism right now.. also from the government.

### caroline — 2019-12-20T06:20:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-finland.24527/post-283820>

Hi Steve and welcome......

# Thread: Physical Therapist from Vancouver, Washington
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physical-therapist-from-vancouver-washington.24574/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Trent Corey — 2020-01-09T07:37:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physical-therapist-from-vancouver-washington.24574/post-284359>

Hello from Vancouver, Washington. I've been following videos and podcasts of Jack and found myself drawn deeper and deeper, knowing that the truth was behind his noble pursuits of light, water and magnetism--not the BS about food. It makes so much sense that it's about the environment of the cell!

I'm a physical therapist and really want to help my patients get better with their pain issues, but also some of my patients have neuro-degenerative issues, mostly Parkinson's. I've suggested Jack's teachings on YouTube to them but it's a big leap for them. My intent in being on this website is to optimize my life as best I can, then watch the unfolding of events as entanglement occurs with my patients!

I'm mostly healthy, but have declined a lot in the past 5 years--working indoors, less time off, more stress with 2 small children, aches and pains, stiffness. I'm a runner who used to go 70-80 miles per week in college, then 30-40 miles per week while working before kids. Now I barely run at all due to an ankle/achilles injury that seems to keep lingering even with the PT exercises and methods I know that can help. A lot of this I know comes from living in Washington and spending lots of time under artificial light and around nnEMF.

I see the sunrise every morning and take cold showers daily. I'm starting to IR sauna more but at best 1x/week. I like most seafood, more oysters but not raw, sardines I really don't like but I'm open to suggestions to increase my DHA intake without supplements, and taste good!

I'm excited to get to work on things that will really make a difference in the health of all the people I know, starting with myself. I've always felt like a misfit from conventional thoughts on health...on this site I look forward to talking with like minds and carry forward bolt steps into action. Thank you!

### Awainer1 — 2020-01-09T12:14:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physical-therapist-from-vancouver-washington.24574/post-284360>

Doesn’t like raw oysters

### JanSz — 2020-01-09T14:10:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physical-therapist-from-vancouver-washington.24574/post-284361>

> [Trent Corey said:](/goto/post?id=284359)
>
> I'm a physical therapist and really want to help my patients get better with their pain issues, but also some of my patients have neuro-degenerative issues, mostly Parkinson's.
> \--------------
> I like most seafood, more oysters but not raw, sardines I really don't like but I'm open to suggestions to increase my DHA intake without supplements, and taste good!

Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

Ask some of your Parkinson's patients (or with other neuro-degenerative issues), to get above Fatty Acid Profile at LabCorp or Mayo Clinic.

Then look at the report.
Do not be surprised when you find high DHA and EPA on those reports. And low AA.
Also, think if they should eat seafood.

...................................
Post those reports here when you get them, for discussion.

.............

# Thread: Filtering and structuring water at sunrise
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/filtering-and-structuring-water-at-sunrise.24575/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lucy Harvey — 2020-01-09T19:10:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/filtering-and-structuring-water-at-sunrise.24575/post-284366>

Hello All

I´m hoping you guys will be able to provide a little insight into solving the issue of creating the best drinking water, I´m getting a little bit lost with online research.

I have been following this forum for a few years. I´ve changed my life because of it. I have left 17 years of living in central London and I now live in a marina by a quite beach north of Barcelona. Hurrah!

Based on my understanding, I´m hoping to piece together the best way to purify and structure the water I´m drinking. I have been drinking bottled San P. for years but i´m looking for a better solution now i´m setting up my own little bio-hacked boat life.

Firstly i need to filter the water from a municipal tap, I´m searching for the most inexpensive and simplest solutions. Shipping the best gadgets from the U.S isn´t really an option for me at the moment.

I´m thinking to use Shungite which has a C60 molecular structure to clean the water, has anyone seen any research on its ability to purify the major harmful contaminants? Can you point me to any studies? Or have any other non gadet recommendations?

Once it´s purified I would like to structure the water using grounding and sunlight. Can you advise the best container to use for this? ( I have purposely moved by a beach to be able to ground at sunrise every day to improve my EZ)

I figured If I put my water in a container and set it on the sand at sunrise it should structure it? Would the glass container absorb the infrared spectrum of light as well as conduct the negative charge from the ground? I heard conflicting info on glass absorbing all infrared frequencies. I think i heard Gerald Pollack say that red colored glass would allow the infrared to pass through it? Is this correct?

If not glass, which material container should I use? I´ve heard leaving the water out in sunlight all day damages the structure, is this possible? Many places suggest keeping living water in rounded crystal glass or clay vessels out of direct sunlight to keep its living structure. Is this necessary?

I plan to consume my water each day, so it won´t really need storing longer than 24hrs. Do you have any advice about this?

Once i´ve got the basics sorted I also plan to vortex the water, put pieces organic green veg like cucumber in it and experiment with using amethysts too. I hear JK uses them in water. Is that directly in the water vessel or next to the vessel? Can they contaminate the water?

I know I have asked a lot of questions. I hope there are a few answers among this group.

I´m tired of cost of buying bottled water to me and the environment, knowing it´s not structured or healthy is draining. Especially when I know I could be fully charged!

I look forward to hearing from you guys . Love and light Lucy

### JanSz — 2020-01-10T17:09:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/filtering-and-structuring-water-at-sunrise.24575/post-284386>

> [Lucy Harvey said:](/goto/post?id=284384)
>
> My interest in drinking water is because of the necessity to drink water. I´m aware of deuterium but i haven´t delved deeply into understanding it.

Living as nature designed us is the goal.
Contradict that and you will pay the price, sooner or later.
Try to think of natural reflexes about water.
Do not you think that in a perfect world we would eat only when hungry and drink only when thirsty?

Do you think that you will ever develop a sense of thirst when you drink just in case, not waiting for a thirst to develop?
\---------------------
We live on water.
We make water in the matrix of our mitochondria.
Making water is really burning hydrogen (hydrogen from fat (good) or sugar we eat (not so good)) using oxygen from the air we breathe.
That is a tremendous amount of energy that we are using to live.
It is thousands of times more energy than usually accounted for in food (2000calories a day), or solar energy (assuming equator and constant noon there), or both of them together. Most of that water stays in our cells and is recirculated.
Only a very small amount of that (7000 liters/day produced water) ever leave the cells and finds its way into urine or sweat.
Think of that 7000liters/day as variable, equal to volume of blood that goes thru heart.
So really variable and dependent on body's effort at given time.
The rate of blood flow (and matrix water production can go up to 3x7000, when at great effort, say when running marathon.
The water you drink, you may want to think of as a lubricant of machinery that really runs on (steam, gas or diesel). It always reminds as extracellular water.
When you would drink only what your (properly trained) thirst would tell you to drink, you would (allow) for slightly more water to be produced.
That extra water would come with the additional energy (that is still more then what you would get from sunlight exposure.
\--------------------------------
Above is not an argument against sunlight.
It is an argument on how to generate more energy for living.

Eventually, all energy on Earth comes from sunlight.
But, when comparing real-time sunlight energy to energy used by eucarytoes it is apparent that eukaryotes use earth energy reserves. Likely one of the reasons why they appeared much latter on Earth.

when eucarytoes appeared on earth

# Thread: Hello
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.24710/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 30
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DReeves — 2020-02-18T20:43:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.24710/post-286368>

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/c9510aa1-9b72-46c7-98b2-a0a1ad4662d1-jpeg.14786/) Hi, Name is David. I’m 47 yrs old. This year my body has gone through major changes. I will try to keep this brief as possible.
This all started about 5 years ago when my wife had back surgery that turned into 3 surgeries. She was given very strong antibiotics and oral steroids. The antibiotics she was given are in a class called fluoroquinolones. She had a very bad reaction to these drugs. Her body seemed to be shutting down. She would have seizure like episodes where her hands would draw up and she said electrical shocks would travel her spine. Her connective tissues were affected, she was in a wheel chair. Her thyroid was severely damaged. Her adrenals were tanked. The doctors wanted to remove her thyroid or irradiate it.

We said enough is enough!!! We told the endocrinologist that we wanted to treat her thyroid naturally, he said it wouldn’t work. We asked if he would simply continue to follow her bloodwork while we try our treatments. He refused. That was over 5 years ago. We haven’t seen a doctor since. We sought the help of an extremely smart TCM healer. She went full Paleo AIP. Iodine Protocol. Got out in the sun. Paid attention to her circadian rhythms. (We hadn’t found Jack yet) Supplemented key minerals and vitamins.() Her thyroid numbers are fine. Her adrenals are working fine. And she is up and walking.

Anyway, you’d think after all that I would have followed my wife’s example and followed a Paleo lifestyle. I knew it worked.

I had been on TRT for over 10 years. Omeprazole daily for over 25 years. High Blood Pressure, weighed about 240- 250lbs. I have dealt with Alcoholism issues off and on my whole life.
In January of 2019 My recurring back problems resurfaced.
I decided it was time to quit Omeprazole and Testosterone. I know it’s not advised to stop “cold turkey” But that’s what I did. That was over a year and a half ago.

I had been following a reasonably clean diet for several years with my wife. Paleo AIP.
But I recommitted and went full liquid diet for about 3 weeks or so. Bone Broth and raw eggs only. I supplemented Betaine HCL, digestive enzymes, lots of ferments. I used aloe Vera juice, slippery elm and marshmallow root as a rescue when it got bad. I had to take a week off work the pain got so bad. I had to lay flat for about 3 days.
I supplemented iodine/Boron/MSM
lots of salt loads and flushing I would have to pulse my doses when things weren’t flowing. Lol.

Anyway, I’ve lost 80lbs. I’m still sober My body is making its own testosterone again. I can eat without heartburn and my blood pressure is perfect.

I’m still having some issues. Back problems persist. Having some Liver issues. Dealing with Reynauds since I relocated from southeast Texas to Iowa July 2018 Been dealing with “cold spells” or something. I have a several bulging discs. But one at T7-T8 is giving me this most trouble. Intercostal nerves were pinched, pain wrapped around ribcage and seemed to ache through my entire midsection. Through my organs to my stomach.

The rib pain is all but gone.

But I still deal with pain in my spine daily and an ache to my core when the spell comes on.

I know I’m missing something. I’m so excited to learn. Been reading obsessively since I discovered Jack. Lol.
I bought his book. “Epi-Paleo RX”

She is dealing with IIH right now.
\-------------------------------------------
**Idiopathic intracranial hypertension**
Idiopathic intracranial hypertension, previously known as pseudotumor cerebri and benign intracranial hypertension, is a condition characterized by increased intracranial pressure without a detectable cause. The main symptoms are headache, vision problems, ringing in the ears with the heartbeat, and shoulder pain.

### DReeves — 2020-02-23T17:25:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.24710/post-286652>

Reynauds:

I’ve been off work the last two mornings. We’ve gotten out to watch the sunrise. Yesterday, we stopped at a roadside cemetery, watched the sunrise while doing some tai-chi. Drove to a state park, found a secluded area in the back where my wife and I both could get some sun on our skin. Lol. I wore gloves so my Reynauds stayed pretty manageable. I also climbed a tree and scaled a hill down to the frozen river. But, I’ve always been a big kid. Lol. Anyway. That was during midday sun.
This morning was more cloudy. We weren’t moving around, just waiting in the cold for the sunrise get past the blockade of clouds. I got really cold. I had on gloves, jacket, boots, benie....fingers went pale as did toes. Toes were really numb by the time I got home.

I have“toe dip” test scheduled for this coming week. HS-CRP, Vit.D, DHEA
I’m thinking now I might as well get full battery. I’ve never been a toe-dip personality. Lol. I’ve been on a strict Paleo-AIP diet for a long time. We try to use food more than supplementation.
I know I haven’t been paying enough attention to light schedules. Luckily I’ve worked outside for most of my life. So I have always been exposed to the elements and the natural light schedules. Although my skin was covered by clothes for the most part. Except for my forearms and neck. lol Later in life the forearms got covered but back of neck stayed red. Hence “Redneck” Another variable as of late, we relocated from Southeast Texas to SouthWest Iowa In July of 2018. I think back. I’ve always dealt with cold hands and feet, especially in the winter months. We just didn’t have much winter to speak of in Southeast Texas. Now, living in Iowa, It has surfaced and become more obvious. Plus, I have lost 80lbs. So my water distributon and maybe my insulation factor has changed. I think I’m just all out of kilter.im looking forward to the tests.

I have been watching webinars obsessively lol. I have an internal clock that wakes me up at 4am. Been that was for 20+ years. My mornings have been (under red light) listening and taking notes. I have filled up several notebooks. Helps me to remember when I write. OMG!! Where have I been?? My TCM healer pointed me toward Jack 4years ago. I guess I wasn’t ready to hear it. She has been steering me toward many of his practices but I’ve been inconsistent at best. My wife on the other hand, is home when I’m at work. She pays strict attention to our TCMhealer and her light/sleep/ food schedules. I’m just a bullheaded old man I suppose that has to hurt to listen. Ugh.

### Chris Herwig — 2020-02-26T04:56:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sweet-home-alabama.24733/post-286769>

I am a seeker of truth in all its forms. I have been a diabetic since 2004 and never lost faith that I could reverse this disease. I believe it and nothing can dissuade me from that faith. I just think that I haven't found the answer yet. My journey has led me to having tried just about every diet out there usually with blood tests every 3 months. Everything has failed and now I have found Dr. Jack Kruse. I have read his Epi Paleo RX book and count myself a mitochondriac since the beginning of February (for about 3 weeks now). In only the past month I have developed acute onset neuropathy and cannot get my blood glucose below 180 (I am not taking any drugs). I am considering it because of the neuropathy but don't want too. I am about a month into the Leptin Reset. I work in a toxic environment. The grounds outside are so toxic the Army will not allow the dirt to be removed in any construction effort. My office is about 200 yards away from an industrial control power sub station which powers a data center which is about 30 feet from my desk in a room with no windows, surrounded by phosphorescent lighting and lots of computers and monitors that I have been working around for about 12 years now. I honestly don't know if the Leptin Reset can make a dent in my toxic life or what it will take to heal me. I am committed to doing what needs to be done. The truth is out there and I hope Jack has the answers I seek.

Drink RO (reverse osmosis) water. Not less than one liter/day, unless heavily exercising, no more than 2.5 liters/day. Wait until you feel actual thirst, then drink, but only a little, then wait again for thirst.

excessive water drinking----->Diabetis insipidus

### Chris Herwig — 2020-04-11T03:29:37+0300

Thank you for the feedback. I have a wonderful wife of more than 27 years and two little boys (6 and 9). Our home is filled with nnEMF and am slowly trying to get the support of my bride and family to make significant changes (not so easy). I have attached my latest blood results below. My HbA1C is 12.3 with an average blood glucose of about 300. I am waiting for my mold toxicity test results (after 2 long term black mold exposures). I have converted to a paleo diet, have started the cold thermogenesis protocol once a week now (I will increase as I build up my tolerance), I automatically wake up every morning to watch the sunrise with my bare feet on the ground and use red glasses at sunset (blue light blocking) nearly every day. I distil all our water then add minerals and cell salts to drink throughout the day (about half my weight in ounces every day). I am 5'9" tall and average 170 pounds with a relatively lean build and do High Intensity Training (HIT) just before sunset 2 or 3 times a week. I average 2 or occasionally 3 meals a day (never after 8 pm and am transitioning to 1 meal a day (in the morning within 30 minutes of waking) but haven't figured out the needed nutritional balances yet. My doctor wants to start me on Peptide therapy (thymosin alpha-1) and potentially testosterone (even though my levels are within normal range) in efforts to reverse my diabetes. My neuropathy pain is getting worse, have been diagnosed with Branch Retinal Vein Occlusion (BRVO) which is basically poor circulation in my eye. All the while I am becoming more desperate for a cure as this pain steadily worsens. I am reading every book Dr. Kruse has recommended (reading 'The Body Electric' right now) but just can't get to the results I greatly need fast enough.

### Inger — 2020-04-11T11:47:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sweet-home-alabama.24733/post-288573>

Welcome Chris!

I am not a lab expert, but I checked your bloodwork and here is what catched my eye.

Your ferritine is too high! You need to get that under 100, better around 40. **Too much iron in the body can cause diabetes!**
If you do not believe me, please buy this book written by a Professor Emeritus of Microbiology and Immunology - it is eye opening, a must read!

[https://www.amazon.de/Exposing-Hidden-Dangers-Iron-Professional/dp/1581823363/ref=sr\_1\_3?\_\_mk\_de\_DE=ÅMÅŽÕÑ&dchild=1&keywords=exposing+the+hidden+dangers+of+iron&qid=1586594456&sr=8-3](https://www.amazon.de/Exposing-Hidden-Dangers-Iron-Professional/dp/1581823363/ref=sr_1_3?__mk_de_DE=%C3%85M%C3%85%C5%BD%C3%95%C3%91&dchild=1&keywords=exposing+the+hidden+dangers+of+iron&qid=1586594456&sr=8-3)

You could get transferrin measured by your doctor too, to see if you have huge iron load. But it can be hidden in your organs..and bloodwork want show that - that might be why the diabetes symptoms. You need to rules this out. If your transferrin saturation is high, you will get phlebotomies at your doctors office, and you will get your iron down faster as they can take blood every week. Blood donation will take longer. The book above will explain all this in detail, please read it.
Anyways what I would do now, is;
Start donating blood ASAP. **Stop eating any red meat**, and eat seafood as much as you can instead. Pastured eggs and raw sheep or goat cheeses are great too.
Lots of green tea with meals.
And you need to get that glucose lower, it will destroy your eyes and organs.
Maybe Metformin(I am no expert in this, you need to research or ask your doctor) can help, until you get the other things sorted out? I am sure there are some less dangerous medicine to help it out there.

And you need to get that bride of yours to understand, limiting exposure to nnEMF, RF and fake light both, is CRUCIAL to help your blood sugar go down!! Does she want you to go blind?
You need to have a serious talk with her. This is not anything to play around with, you need to show her your bloodwork and talk to her seriously.
It is great all that you do, but you need more. No fake lights, and no WIFI and smartphones or other gadgets in your home either.

If you do not have a nnEMNF Meter yet, get one ASAP. You need to measure. This might help her understand better too.

Get naked in the sun all you can
And have lots of sex - it lowers bloodsugar!   (I have tested this  )
You can do this

If any of this does not help, you need to change work! In fact you need to do that anyways, a toxic work like that is a good way killing oneself. But well, if you get your iron down and all the other things right, it might help you heal. No way to know before you try.

PS. **I would NOT do testosterone therapy with those iron numbers**!! Testosterone therapy will increase the iron uptake from food further! Maybe if you do phlebotomies first..you could do it, but I have no idea why testosterone should help diabetes, have not learned about that yet.

### Daulatwant — 2020-04-11T21:39:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sweet-home-alabama.24733/post-288602>

I was never diagnosed, but combining a horrible wheat based diet with some medication made me react badly to carbs. Eating some carbs would leave my whole body aching and an unquenchable thirst. This happened two times over a couple years, both times I started acting quickly and stopped this from progressing further. First time I stopped taking the medication and started eating tons of salads, but that while it seemed to help, it brought some other bad side effects and my metabolism was still bad.

Second time I got myself some electrolytes and made a solution to help with fasting. Did a 48h fast with the electrolyte solution. I was able to continue taking my medication, no longer reacted badly to sugars, carbs after the 48h fast. After which I switched my diet to a whole foods low carbohydrate diet, and only ate once a day, by increasing fat(butter, coconut oil) I stayed satiated for longer and fasting was easier. Haven't had problems with blood sugar/insulin since I did this.

Prediabetes, diabetes the way I experienced it, is that your body goes into a state where you are always hungry and have a strong urge to continue eating all the time, it may seem impossible to do fasting. And it was pretty much impossible for me too stop eating for half a day until I got really scared for my health and included the electrolytes in my water.

I typically drink about 90 ounces of water a day (which is about half my weight in ounces). I think your suggestion offers improvements over my current approach in mineral transport alone.

### JanSz — 2020-04-13T01:56:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sweet-home-alabama.24733/post-288637>

> [Chris Herwig said:](/goto/post?id=288634)
>
> Wow. Excellent and will be much easier than refilling my metal tub in the yard every day. What do you do to keep this system grounded to the earth?

This system runs on electricity and is filled with saltwater.
You really want good, likely 100 amp gfci breaker there.
That breaker would fill better if hooked to 1" dia 8 feet copper rod sunk in the (wet) ground as close as possible.
So get a piece of copper rope, hook one end to that ground and sink the other end in saltwater.

..........

### Marko Pollo — 2020-04-13T16:52:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sweet-home-alabama.24733/post-288647>

Chris,
I believe your biggest threat right now is your toxic work environment and any amount of effort to mitigate toxic effects will only postpone your getting the hell out.

If you have money to squander on a refrigerated cold tub, I recommend you consult an electrician or just do a little research. That 1 hp chiller, which by the way probably also requires a circulation pump, draws 1100watts. A 15amp breaker is probably more appropriate. You want the trip point to be close to operating current or it doesn’t do any good. In my mind, a 1 inch copper ground is also overkill unless your rolling in dough, in which case, you should quit your job! I’m not an engineer or electrician btw.

The leptin protocol is the way to go, no matter what. Probably won’t overcome your health issues alone, but is the place to start and never leave. I’m no doctor but this has helped me turn around my issues.

Extremism in the pursuit of sun and health is no vice. Moderation in the avoidance of non emf and toxic light is no virtue!

Good luck!

I'm still a little ignorant on all this but the podcasts are helpful i just need to read more ... trying to elimante blue light by red light filters and glasses and all that, but im still not sure how much of an effect the wifi router in my room is having on me, kind of wanna throw that in the ocean....

Hello, I came home from our place in Mexico for a family celebration and dental work. I'm not supposed to fly back to Mexico until April 24th which is looking like it won't happen. I'm immune compromised and considering booking a ticket for tomorrow to ensure I'm in a healthier environment while the pandemic plays out. However, I will be there solo as nobody else wants to go with and we don't have a car. I'm torn on what to do as if something goes wrong I will be 100 percent on my own.

# Thread: Hello From Montana
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-montana.24860/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### XCSKIER13 — 2020-04-03T01:37:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-montana.24860/post-288280>

I began in December reading the blog, listening to podcasts with Dr. Jack Kruse, and trying to learn how to improve my mitochondrial function. I have had poor sleep and troubling memory issues for 15+ years. I've tried many avenues to heal my self but have come up empty and I now know why as my redox is super poor (BUN:CREATINE=21:0.74) and potassium is low.

I'm 57 and have been in menopause for 6 years. I am 5'1" and now weigh 88 pounds. When I began the leptin rx I weighed 95 pounds.

For last year I have primarily eaten protein and fat but little seafood. In January I began working on the Leptin Rx. (the timing of meals). In February, I began trying to get more sun particularily sunrise; however, Montana many mornings are too cloudy to see the sun. I'm really working on 30 minutes at sunrise + has much AM sun as I can get as the days lengthen. I'm looking forward to warmer days so I can shed some clothing.

In mid January, I start doing CT with face dunking. I did this for about a month and then began doing CT in the bath tub. I started at 5 minutes and gradually increased to 15 minutes. I had difficult time warming up afterwards and shivered for quite awile. After about 3 weeks, I started to have increased heart palpitations (pounding heart) and my sleep got worse. I then stopped and it has been about 3 weeks since doing CT and my heart palpitations continue and I can feel the cortisol increasing toward late afternoon. I am wondering if my body thinks it is starving. I feel satiated when eating the epi-paleo diet with the circadian timing but I continue to have a coritsol response. I'm hopeful that I can find some answers to improve my health and turn towards a more optimal direction by addressing my mitochondrial weaknesses.

### Duane Baumgartner — 2020-04-05T20:45:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-montana.24860/post-288391>

Hi there. I'm a neighbor to the north of you. In another month we will start to get more heat and sun in the mornings! For me that was the key to get my circadian rhythm set. Make sure you get the sunrise everyday. More melatonin ... more sleep. Are you wearing blue blockers and avoiding artifical light after sunset? Good luck.

### caroline — 2020-04-06T01:33:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-montana.24860/post-288400>

Make sure you get the sunrise everyday. More melatonin ... more sleep. Are you wearing blue blockers and avoiding artifical light after sunset? Good luck.

Are we allowed to go to the ocean if we are observing social isolation?
On the news this morning ...a guy went out jogging and then stopped to buy a take away kebab. He was fined $1000.00. That doesn’t seem right???
We are in Wollongong right now as the Ruby Princess is docking at Port.
