# Thread: Introducing myself and possibly leaving NYC asap
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-and-possibly-leaving-nyc-asap.25019/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kimberly P — 2020-05-30T20:48:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-and-possibly-leaving-nyc-asap.25019/post-289938>

Hello, new friends. I've been following Jack for the past couple months and I'm so grateful for the wisdom. I don't have any pressing health concerns, but of course I'm widely aware of the importance of getting away from toxins, nnEMF's and 5g. Cancer has been a big problem in my family so that is something I'm always conscious of and very passionate about.

Aside from that, I'm in NYC and things are getting ugly here with riots breaking out. I'm trying to decide my next move. I've been trying to get things organized to leave the city and join my family in Michigan - I'm even questioning if it's too late with highways across the country getting shut down by protestors. I don't really want to be in Michigan but I feel it might be better than being alone in NYC. It's hard to find people who share my concerns because most are brainwashed by MSM and don't understand the long-term impacts of what is happening and the overall big picture. I'm confused as to what to do, but my instinct tells me that being with family would be smart. Insights would be welcome. Feeling a bit alone and overwhelmed (but ever hopeful). I hope everyone stays safe and I'm praying for peace.

### Saichi — 2020-05-30T22:26:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-and-possibly-leaving-nyc-asap.25019/post-289942>

I've been in NYC past few days and I'm seeing serious psychological and physical damage being done to almost every one I know. Since you're new to nnemf, unless you're wealthy and can afford to spend big to mitigate you'll want to move out to some countryside sooner than later. You want to avoid all cities.

### Kimberly P — 2020-05-30T23:12:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-and-possibly-leaving-nyc-asap.25019/post-289943>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=289942)
>
> I've been in NYC past few days and I'm seeing serious psychological and physical damage being done to almost every one I know. Since you're new to nnemf, unless you're wealthy and can afford to spend big to mitigate you'll want to move out to some countryside sooner than later. You want to avoid all cities.

Thank you for replying. I'm not exaggerating when I say I feel I could cry just to have someone understand where I'm coming from. My first instinct in March was to jump in a car and head back to Michigan but I didn't want to panic and just leave all my belongings behind. I should have done it. I'm going to try and get out very quickly but I'm afraid of driving alone on the highways with the current environment of people blocking traffic and attacking deadlocked cars. Also, in Michigan I'll be in a higher latitude, but it will be country (really didn't want to go into higher latitude but maybe best option).

Again, thank you for the reply. It really means so much to me. I hope you stay safe in NYC. Yes, people are losing all sense of reason. Praying for peace.

### Saichi — 2020-05-30T23:42:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-and-possibly-leaving-nyc-asap.25019/post-289944>

There's plenty of sun in Michigan. And you want to take advantage of cold adapting when and where available.

You shouldn't be afraid of driving on the interstate right now.

If your family uses wireless inside their home then that's more exposure than on the streets of NYC. Meaning wherever you go you have to eliminate your wireless sources indoors. Keep your phone in a metal tin when not in use.

Safe is easy at the moment, sane is harder. Had an ironic thought today that phone zombies who used to walk into me on the street have turned into mask zombies who won't walk within four feet of me.

### Kimberly P — 2020-05-31T00:47:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-and-possibly-leaving-nyc-asap.25019/post-289945>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=289944)
>
> There's plenty of sun in Michigan. And you want to take advantage of cold adapting when and where available.
>
> You shouldn't be afraid of driving on the interstate right now.
>
> If your family uses wireless inside their home then that's more exposure than on the streets of NYC. Meaning wherever you go you have to eliminate your wireless sources indoors. Keep your phone in a metal tin when not in use.
>
> Safe is easy at the moment, sane is harder. Had an ironic thought today that phone zombies who used to walk into me on the street have turned into mask zombies who won't walk within four feet of me.

Thank you for your insight! The good news is not too much wifi where I'd be going. Plus I bought my ethernet cables thanks to Jack's advice

You're right about the mask zombies - what's worse is combination phone/mask zombies. I've seen it!

### caroline — 2020-05-31T05:51:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-and-possibly-leaving-nyc-asap.25019/post-289946>

Hi Kimberly and welcome...
Please start a journal in the optimal journal section so we can follow you on your optimal journey.

What do you do for work? can you work remotely? If so - the world is your oyster so to speak.

Have you thought of getting yourself a small camper van and heading to your parents place for now and then moving on when you feel able to?

We all need to have a couple of backup plans these days.

Travel light and stay mobile ....you will be able to turn on a dime and follow your heart
and your instincts.

You are pretty lucky ....I am guessing that you aren't tied down with a partner and kids?

Please don't be afraid ...use this as a fantastic opportunity to take control of your life.

Dr. K. would call this "jumping without a parachute and developing wings on the way
down!"

A lot of us here have done just that .....and life keeps getting better and better.

With an open mind and an adventurous spirit You will find opportunities you never
dreamt of.

These are difficult times ....no question - but be optimistic. You are young and lovely and you have found Jack kruse.......we are all very lucky here.

Do what needs to be done and don't look back ....your future is waiting for you.

Please stay safe and connected ....

Have you thought of the Florida panhandle .....The Destin area?

There are families here that bought an old school bus etc. and refurbished it and took the whole family to Florida. Last we heard they are all homeschooling and getting healthy.

### Kimberly P — 2020-05-31T07:05:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-and-possibly-leaving-nyc-asap.25019/post-289947>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=289946)

Hi Caroline, thank you for such a kind and thoughtful welcome note. Your uplifting insight mean a lot to me, especially right now. I'm usually quite positive and optimistic but things definitely feel heavy at the moment. Change doesn't usually scare me but violence is another story.

It's funny you mention a van and Florida, because yes - I've been heavily considering Florida and today I had the thought about buying a used van so I can be mobile. My friend said that I could work at his restaurant near Palm Beach, at least while I figure things out. Destin sounds beautiful - how nice about the family and the refurbished bus!

I'm unemployed at the moment, as are most New Yorkers, but I'm an actress and usually make my living bartending. I'm aware of and agree with Jack's take on shift work though. Luckily I haven't worked nights in a while.

So, for the past month I've been contemplating Florida vs. Michigan. With Florida, I have only one friend and as of now, nowhere to live - and in Michigan I have my entire family with whom I could stay. I really don't want to leave my apartment in NYC as I'm so happy here but my instinct is telling me it's not safe. Anyways, maybe summer in Michigan and then head south for the winter . . .

Oh, and yes I'm single and no kids. 'll start the optimal journal as you suggested!

Thanks again and I hope you're safe and well wherever you are

### caroline — 2020-05-31T09:12:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-and-possibly-leaving-nyc-asap.25019/post-289948>

I am in Australia BTW.

Jack has a beach house in Destin Florida and loves it there in the off season.

Probably no place is going to be even close to perfect now so we all have to do the best we can and be prepared to change course when necessary.

It will be a tough decision to leave NYC ....especially if you are an actor.

You just never know what/who will cross your path ....and change everything.

You are young and there isn't anything you can't do if you put your mind to it.

Treat your life as a blank canvas and just live each day to the fullest.

Follow Inger's Journal ....she has had to make tough decisions in her life and she is stronger and wiser because of them.

I look forward to your journal...

### Kimberly P — 2020-07-04T02:48:32+0300

### Kimberly P — 2021-01-27T22:12:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-and-possibly-leaving-nyc-asap.25019/post-294983>

Hi friends! I wanted to post a quick update. I left NYC in July and have been living in a tiny one-room cabin on 27-acres in Northern Michigan ever since. I’m living semi-off grid with no kitchen, no bathroom and no running water. I do have electricity and the first thing I did was change the lightbulbs to incandescent. Also using oil lamps. I spend a decent amount of time outside but it’s pretty cold and cloudy here - so cold that 20 degrees feels warm. Hoping to make my way to Florida for a vacay but I don’t have any solid plans in place. I hope everyone is doing well. It’s unbelievable how much has changed in these past ten months and I’ll never forget how happy I was to find this group when I was so scared and alone in the city.

### Marko Pollo — 2021-01-28T00:20:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-and-possibly-leaving-nyc-asap.25019/post-294987>

You are way cooler than all the people living in cities! Not that that matters to someone like you.

### caroline — 2021-01-28T04:46:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-and-possibly-leaving-nyc-asap.25019/post-294994>

great update Kimberly! Tell us more when you have time......

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-and-possibly-leaving-nyc-asap.25019/post-295694>

Hey Kimberly, Have you ever thought of starting a youtube channel or IG about your life in the Michigan Arctic? lol! Hang in there, Spring is near! I can't wait to go to the Beach!

I spend a decent amount of time outside but it’s pretty cold and cloudy here - so cold that 20 degrees feels warm. Hoping to make my way to Florida for a vacay but I don’t have any solid plans in place. I hope everyone is doing well. It’s unbelievable how much has changed in these past ten months and I’ll never forget how happy I was to find this group when I was so scared and alone in the city.

Great escape! Hope you can make it to Florida as you desire.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Bryce, Ready For Change,  Ready For Vibrancy
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bryce-ready-for-change-ready-for-vibrancy.25108/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Bryce Boyer — 2020-07-13T06:20:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bryce-ready-for-change-ready-for-vibrancy.25108/post-290902>

Hey All!

My name is Bryce. I own a holistic wellness practice out of a Chinese medicine center in Beverly, MA where I do many things from body work to health coaching, to in home EMF safety analysis (going to do a building biology program when I have the time/money for it), to educating people on the practices of a mito. I've been studying Dr.Kruse for over 2 years now and have implemented a ton of his protocols into my daily life. Haven't missed a night without blue blockers in 3 years now, get outside in unfiltered sun EVERY DAY for sometimes hours a day, constantly grounding, eating local/paleo/keto centric diet. I do CT on days where I'm not feeling overly fatigued or weak, mostly just cold showers until I get stronger and more stable. I live in an EMF minimized home to what I could reasonably do and **have completely changed my lifestyle to try to recover from debilitating fatigue and stress related issues from 5 years ago**. Overal I'm doing significantly better than I once was, thanks to this community and Dr.Kruse, as well as some other biohackers and wise individuals I've had the honor of spending time with as mentors/friends.

Currently my biggest challenges are fatigue radiating from my throat that can also cause anxiety/nausea or sometimes the other way around and waking up in the middle of the night CONSTANTLY. I just got a 23andMe to learn more about my haplotype and phenotype as well as a 10 gauss magnetico sleep pad. The sleep thing is really the biggest frustration and hurdle I want to get through. At this point I'm willing to do **almost** anything I can.

Like many of you, I'm sick of feeling sick and just want to be healthy so I can support others efficiently and enjoy my life. Here to help anybody I can, here to learn, here to be with you all as we grow together. Blessings to you all

PS if you have any insights into the magnetico pad I'd really appreciate hearing them.

### caroline — 2020-07-13T10:12:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bryce-ready-for-change-ready-for-vibrancy.25108/post-290904>

Hi Bryce and welcome.....

Please start a journal in the optimal journal section and give Dr. K. some health history. Also your Mom's and Gran's.

Do you have 5G there?

How do you do when you get away from civilisation for a few weeks?
Do you go camping? swimming in rivers? fishing?
Have you spent iany time in the Yucatan?

What happened five years ago when all your issues started? Where did you live then?

# Thread: Newbie from Idaho!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-from-idaho.25114/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Katrina Morris — 2020-07-18T06:50:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-from-idaho.25114/post-291023>

Hey all!! New to the group and super excited to start my journey to optimal. Just a little background on myself. I am a wife and a mother of two beautiful kids ages 8 and 7 (boy and a girl). I am a RN and work in Interventional Radiology (which I love) but realize there are risks that come with that. I had my thyroid removed 3 years ago due to thyroid cancer. Ever since then, I have been struggling with major weight gain, fatigue, loss of motivation, some depression, hormonal imbalances, etc.
I have been wanting to make a change for a long time and a fellow nurse told me about Dr Kruse and his theories. Needless to say I was VERY intrigued and started making some minor adjustments since then. I got EMF stickers (don’t know if they work) for all the electronics in the house. Blue light blocker glasses for the whole family. Blue light screen protectors for the phones along with EMF blocking phone cases. I’ve been getting out in the sun as much as I can although it is fairly difficult to get out right at sunrise due to my job. I have not changed any lights in my house yet because I would love to get advice on that from y’all . I am so new and trying to find a good place to start. Any and all advice is very much appreciated and THANK YOU!!

### caroline — 2020-07-18T11:00:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-from-idaho.25114/post-291028>

Hi Katrina and welcome.

Please start a journal in the optimal journal section and give Dr. K. some Health history ....and your Mom's and Gran's too.

Where do you live?

Do you do shift work? In a hospital?

What are you eating? what water are you drinking?

Have you read the Leptin RX?

Do you have Jack's book?

Please listen to all Jack's podcasts ...they are an easier and faster way to get up to speed around here.

### Pablo — 2020-10-01T18:55:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-here-living-in-spain-or-the-belearics.25234/post-292539>

Great seafood, most of it comes from Galicia, next to Asturias (my region). Some of it is expensive, but mussels, mackerel and sardines are cheap. Good olive oil and probably cheaper than elsewhere. Butter I generally buy kerrigold from ireland but I think the one made here is decent. Good iberico lard I´m eating too. Yeah masks are mandatory in most places which sucks. Funny enough when I´m in city I go everywhere by bike cause there is no mask needed then lol. Actually I´m going to take it right now to see sunset in park. Take care guys

### Michael CULLEN — 2020-10-06T15:22:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-here-living-in-spain-or-the-belearics.25234/post-292694>

Good olive oil and probably cheaper than elsewhere. Butter I generally buy kerrigold from ireland but I think the one made here is decent. Good iberico lard I´m eating too. Yeah masks are mandatory in most places which sucks. Funny enough when I´m in city I go everywhere by bike cause there is no mask needed then lol. Actually I´m going to take it right now to see sunset in park. Take care guys

Hi Pablo, what was your last winter like?

### Pablo — 2020-10-08T19:40:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-here-living-in-spain-or-the-belearics.25234/post-292751>

> [Michael CULLEN said:](/goto/post?id=292694)
>
> Hi Pablo, what was your last winter like?

hMmm... it was not perfect cause last november and december there was a ton of rain = river was flooded and couldn´t CT there for 3 months or so, and not too much outdoors that time. There is a big difference in weather in different areas of Spain, though, it depends a lot.

This winter I expect much better health/energy levels. I wanna do much more CT, I just ordered a small light panel, exercise outdoors shirtless, etc. I just think it is difficult to feel awesome in winter in my latitude without damm good CT (and sauna and other stuff). All my life I felt awesome in summer and kindda lethargic/sad by end of winter and especially spring. Now I know why.

I guess it´s so much n=1, it depends on your mitochondria. I need to test mine, I guess I´m mixed/mediterranean type. To be honest I´ve been hesitant to do a 23andme cause I read they sell your genetic data and it sounds kindda dark stuff lol. I don´t know

### Emma Sabin — 2021-05-03T18:13:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-here-living-in-spain-or-the-belearics.25234/post-298116>

Hey, just catching up on this thread. I live in Ibiza and have done for 4 years after finding Jack and realising I needed light! The light is Ibiza is amazing, on all levels (artists come here specifically to capture it). There is a great 'health' side to the island although veganism is high rated still, but things are changing. And grass-fed meat is hard to find. Northern Spain is the best place for that apparently. I love the climate but the police, tax system and left over Franco feel is not so good. But I am surrounded by like minded souls that provide the community I need. However I would love to have some Jack peeps over here. Oh an I have a CT group that runs throughout the winter (basically swimming in the sea, which is a great way to meet people. The winters are amazing, big bright blue skies, cold sea and no tourists!

Humidity is high here though so lots of damp in houses and its an expensive island. But it definitely has a magic to it.

Let me know if I can help you in way

### Emma Sabin — 2021-05-05T12:09:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-here-living-in-spain-or-the-belearics.25234/post-298177>

Regarding getting stuff, its not that easy. I used to bring a lot over from the Uk before Brexit screwed that! I make my own ghee and source what they say is grassfed meat, but who knows... # Thread: Could this one picture tell your future?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-this-one-picture-tell-your-future.25440/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mike David — 2021-01-05T17:04:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-this-one-picture-tell-your-future.25440/post-294338>

At this location stands this marker behind thick shrubs. It used to be out by the road like any great attraction marketing piece. Then came the day of the offense. What’s this offense you say? Well people who lived near this worldwide attraction became themselves offended. They ordered the statue into its hedge fund cell. Where it sits just out of sight. For the curious who travel the stone path, around the maze, into the hedge rows. A glimpse of perspective perfection.

I commemorate this misjudging of art by shining the light on the foggy eye. You just stand there in awe. At the right time. From the right angles. In all its intended glorious glory. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Mike David — 2021-01-05T17:09:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-this-one-picture-tell-your-future.25440/post-294339>

Hi, my name is Michael David. I live in Perkasie, PA. I took this photo in St. Augustine Fla. The date was one month before the COVID was sent to strike our starboard side. We spent one glorious week in the sun in Fla. in the dead of winter. Unrestricted co mingling with thousands as we saw as many sights as possible. ONE MONTH before it was too dangerous to go outside. Then air travel and all. No masks. No distance. Just freely flowing and constructing life forces. I know Jack said evolution is Speeding up. I couldn’t imagine this ancient epic saga cycle could be reduced to one month. That’s fast Jack. Almost too fast.

# Thread: Hello from New Zealand
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-new-zealand.25492/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Richard Watson — 2021-01-22T09:51:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-new-zealand.25492/post-294831>

Hi everyone, I live with my partner (Jackie) in the rural eastern South Island on 5 hectares of land, we have three kids in there early 20's who thankfully have chosen to work outdoor in the farming community. Jackie works 5min drive away on a outdoor free range pig farm and I work from home growing vegetable seed while also growing most of our food.

My journey into the awareness of health and well being started when in my 20's I developed a passion for gardening and that the idea of growing organic was a no brainier, this lead to more recently growing seed and plant breeding.
Like millions of others I went though a period of following what doctors and the Heart foundation were recommending at the time - 'The food pyramid' eat mostly high fiber grain and low fat, that diet nearly caused diabetes and the doc wanted me on meds, so, I though &\*$3 that so I cut out 95% of grains and enjoyed eating the natural fats again, my yearly health check blood tests have been great ever since, and that was fifteen years ago.

I discovered the benefits of the sun by chance, about 7 years ago I started to develop a rash on my belly during June, at the time I didn't connect it to lack of sunlight and had visited the doctor a number of times to which he had prescribed useless creams to apply, it was then that I though, 'I wonder if the sunlight could help'?, June being winter time I started sitting in a sunny spot for an hour or so at midday, within a week it had gone. This helped keep it away for two more Junes but the third winter it didn't quite do it anymore so I started using a sunbed hire which has kept me rash free since, plus i stay tanned all winter thus getting a head start for summer.

My other success story came about with the use of a 670nm IR light [https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2020/06/200629120241.htm](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2020/06/200629120241.htm) I have been using this light now for 10 months and now my short sighted myopia has reversed by about 95%, I dont need to wear my glasses any more, its just so wonderful to be able to see clearly a tree on a hill that could be over 10km away. I will be using my light for the rest of my life if this is what it can do.

My gardening allows me to spend most days with nothing on from sunup till mid/late afternoon then again an hour or so before sunset, so I have the suntan from hell atm. I cold shower each morning ( cold bore water ), recently we have increased our seafood diet including 2kgs per week of green lip mussels, aseholed the wifi and smart meter out of the house two years ago.

Having already experienced the power of light, seeing Jack Kruse page on FB was just meant to be, that was it, I wanted to learn more, I'm now a Bronze Klub member, what limited time I do spend in front of a computer I like to read this forum as Ive recently deleted my FB because of the censorship bullshit.

That's a brief about me, still more I could talk about later, I do have one question that I would love to know the answer to though,
Lastly, am I the only kiwi here, see there's a few ozes

### caroline — 2021-01-23T02:32:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-new-zealand.25492/post-294853>

there are more Kiwis here and a ton of aussies - me being one!

welcome. We may have to look you up one of these days since there aren'y many places we can travel to!

What is your weather like now?

We are hoping to go to Tassie - but border crossings are an issue right now - or last we checked. It keeps changing!

### Richard Watson — 2021-01-23T05:02:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-new-zealand.25492/post-294855>

Hi Caroline, good to hear there's a few from both sides of downunderville.

The weather has been bizarre really, we are having a very rare green summer, a sheep farmer not far from me who is getting near 70 and was born and breed in the area reckons this is the first summer he can remember where it hasn't dried at least one point. I hear its been the same over you way too.

Wont be long before all the airlines will insist all travelers show that they have been injected with the EBA, my days of flying anywhere will be over then.

# Thread: Looking for more clues
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-more-clues.25635/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jesilyn — 2021-03-17T18:45:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-more-clues.25635/post-296398>

Hello all,
I have been working steadily on the puzzle of my poor health for more than two years now, and discovered Dr. Kruse's work a little more than a year ago and working to implement more and more of this understanding. I am always poking around on this forum looking for hints, and am finally wanting to reach out and see if I can get some more help putting the pieces together! I'm a 34 year old female, have always been active until I started to really lose musculoskeletal function over the past two years, tend toward underweight rather than overweight. My most unrelenting symptoms are musculoskeletal- structural dysfunction and degeneration. The next worse is probably depression and brain fog/cognitive impairment. All of these are worsened by rather severe insomnia.

I've been in a more severe state for the past two years, but declining significantly for the past 4 or 5 years, and having signs and symptoms of hypothyroidism and poor redox for my whole life really. Of course I've had a poor environment my whole life.

I've been working with a naturopath who has studied with Dr. Kruse and so has that perspective as well as the naturopathic and biochemical. But nothing has really helped my system shift out of this severe dysfunction yet, and I am impatient to find out why I haven't experienced more improvement. After lots of sunlight and reduced EMF (as much as I could reduce it- sleeping outside in the woods in a faraday cage for a whole summer, and living outside barefoot in the sun near Santa Fe, NM) made hardly any impact on the severity of the symptoms, we looked into Chronic Inflammatory Response Syndrome (CIRS) and determined that biotoxins/mold are a significant factor.

I took Cholesytramine until I could pass the Visual Contrast Study, moved to Mexico (2 months ago), and have been implementing a DMSA oral chelation protocol to address the buildup of heavy metals in my system. It has not been going too well... some days I think it's helping and I have improved sleep, but some days I can't sleep at all. Again, I am literally living outdoors barefoot in a planted courtyard in full sun in a bikini all day long. I'm of Irish/Nordic decent and I am very brown at this point. I can feel the sun helping me, but I feel like a weak/leaky battery that can't hold a charge.

The best I can guess right now is that for whatever equation of reasons my body is not really able to use the sun well yet. I am thinking to try more seafood? I am living inland in a mountain town so it has been hard to source, but I have recently found a source for wild salmon. Could that be enough to make the difference? What else could account for a lack of ability to use the sun to improve redox?

Looking at the forum I gather that chelation therapy is not necessarily recommended. I want to talk to more doctor more about this, but until I can thoughts about this are welcome. My instinct is that it's too much given my trashed redox, but I also trust my doctor, and don't know how to address the build up of metals otherwise. I would especially appreciate first hand accounts in this department.

I have been living in Mexico for two months now in the best situation I've had yet, both in terms of the physical environment as well as social/emotional, although both could still improve more. I have an EMF meter, but I haven't dared measure given that I wouldn't be able to make additional modifications at this point, and I've learned that the fear of EMF is worse for my body than the EMF itself. I have some distance from all of them, but there are several wifi routers in the vicinity. I'm living with a dear friend who has had very similar health struggles, albeit for much longer, and although she still has a ways to go to optimal, has largely regained functionality and quality of life. It's very valuable to be with someone who can relate to floundering for years in the depths of hell and wants to help me through it.

I was struggling with food for a long time because I didn't seem to be able to metabolize fat and was chronically constipated. My bowels are moving well here though, and I've been eating more and more coconut oil and avocados, and very few carbs. Also keeping feeding window 8 hrs or less because that seems to help with sleep, although not consistently. My appetite for protein is weakened, but I am still eating a decent amount of mostly beef, some pork and eggs, and fish, which I will try to increase.

I usually fall to sleep no problem, but on the insomniac nights wake soon after and have either broken sleep or no sleep the whole night. If I do get some sleep it's usually in the 1-5 range. I wake up early no matter what, usually about an hour or more before sunrise.

I am feeling very stumped and demoralized by the lack of improvement after doing so much to transform my situation. Any ideas?? Thanks in advance!

### caroline — 2021-03-17T22:30:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-more-clues.25635/post-296409>

What about having a consult with Dr. Kruse?

**Welcome [@Jesilyn](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/24472/)**
Congratulations for making the changes you’ve made so far! <- Your move to Mexico, staying with a friend who can be empathetic alone with you on your journey.

May I suggest you start out with setting up your “Signature” [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/update-your-signature.9865/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/update-your-signature.9865/)
and create your Journal [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?forums/my-optimal-journal.17/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?forums/my-optimal-journal.17/)

Consider constructing a brief Quantum Health history:
Stratify your findings into: urgent, critical and needs improvement

Quantify your health history - Where have you lived, for how long,​

-   Latitude, city, state, country, zip code
-   Did you symptoms progress from location to location?

What were the environment(s) you’ve lived in:​

-   Are you sleeping (at home) or are you working (at the office) in an nnEMF toxic soup? Have you had these environment(s) tested?
-   Is there mold in your “home”, apartment, or wherever you spend most of your time?
-   Have you been “exposed” to viruses, bacteria’s, which maybe currently “dormant”?

What are your “health practices”​

-   Nutrition – what goes in your mouth
-   Exercise – when, where, what type and how much?
-   Recovery – what is the quality of your sleep?

And a bit about family history specially your mother's, her mother, etc.​

May I begin by recommending, please stop your chelation therapy for now until you get your energy back. Your body needs to be strong in order to handle the beating of chelation therapy, which is very hard on your liver.

May I suggest you work on your energy levels first. We know there are three elements for increasing your cellular _"Energy"_ quotient

-   Photoelectric force
-   Biochemical-electrical force / signaling
-   Piezoelectric force

So your sunrise therapy, bare foot on the moist earth, exposing as much as possible -> is vital. Please, make sure you’re getting enough UV noon exposure as well.
If possible, add more seafood and sea vegetables.
Take walks, bare foot on the moist earth, with your friend and enjoy good conversation.

If you find yourself waking up before sunrise, spend that time in red & infrared light. It’s a great time for prayer & meditation.
If you have broken your adrenals / liver function, you may want to consider restoring your Prostaglandin function first with Dihydromyricetin -> [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?posts/293015](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?posts/293015)

Grandpa John

# Thread: Hello from the UK!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-uk.25641/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ravi Sandhu — 2021-03-21T16:25:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-uk.25641/post-296505>

Hello friends,

Great to be here.

I have begun to learn about Dr. Jack's ideas recently and am going through the process of having my mind blown wide open, I'm sure you've been there.

I'm Ravi, I'm 29 from England, work as a Project Manager and enjoy a range of hobbies, guitar, language learning, learning and self improvement.

I have been on this journey of health recovery for many years, my problems began at around 19 with high levels of anxiety and depressive ideation, they reduced a bit during University, and then picked up massively. I have been through the gamut with regards to trying different approaches, and have arrived here after much trial and error, with some improvements coming in some areas, and work to be done in other areas.

At any rate, I am working remotely right now, and am looking to escape the UK for 4/5 months when the UK travel ban ends and national lockdown eases. I am getting myself to Spain for a few months to recharge myself and rebuild myself.

My fundamental goal is to realise my human potential. I know there is still a lot in me and a lot I can do, just to get myself better, bring back my brain function and thinking ability, and start to feel good again!

I'll get there I'm determined.

Best wishes,
Ravi

### caroline — 2021-03-21T23:56:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-uk.25641/post-296511>

Hi Ravi and welcome....

Please start a journal in the optimal journal section and give Dr. K. some health history and also your Mom's and Gran's.

Have you had a 23&me test done?

Are you listening to Jack's podcasts? They are a great and easier way to get up to speed fast.

### Ravi Sandhu — 2021-03-22T10:49:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-uk.25641/post-296525>

> [Richard Watson said:](/goto/post?id=296508)

Thank you Richard I appreciate that.

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=296511)

# Thread: Hi from a new member moving to Florida

### Joel Marquez — 2021-03-24T06:08:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-a-new-member-moving-to-florida.25650/post-296564>

Hi Everyone!

My wife and I currently live in Seattle, and we've made the decision to sell our house in our overpriced neighborhood and move to Florida because we're in desperate need of sunshine.
We're looking at the north part of Florida, likely the panhandle area. We'll be visiting in April to look at some places.
We've heard that Perry, Lake City, and Milton are good options. Anyone have any opinions about that?
Of course, we'd like to be away from densely populated areas and ideally would like to find a few acres.
If anyone else is going through this process, I'd love to share notes.

Thanks!
Joel

### caroline — 2021-03-24T10:11:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-a-new-member-moving-to-florida.25650/post-296566>

Hi Joel and welcome.

There are others here looking to move to Florida as well.

Good luck on your optimal journey to health and wealth!

Thanks Caroline. I'm not sure where to put this, but after the monthly Q&A, I was inspired to start a Facebook group called "Mitochondriacs Moving to Florida."
If anyone on the forum here would like to join, just search for it on Facebook and I'll approve your request.
If any of the moderators think this would be better posted on a different thread, let me know. Thanks!

It's a rural area with horse farms everywhere. I'm going to head down mid May and check it out and take measurements with my devices (I trained with Geovital) so I'll report my findings here afterwards. # Thread: Hi to Everyone
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-to-everyone.25667/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### stuart buchanan — 2021-04-01T19:01:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-to-everyone.25667/post-296811>

Hi my name is Stuart, I'm 60yo, and I live by myself in a town close to Glasgow, Scotland. I was diagnosed with Parkinsons 5 years ago, and since then i have changed my lifestyle quite a lot. I am determined to do my best to overcome this illness. Right from the word go i have been looking into natural remedies. At the start, with the help of my Colombian girlfriend at the time, I came back from Colombia with 15kg of Mucuna Pruriens in my rucksack - this led to a couple of tense moments at airport security - added a bit of spice to life. (unfortunately there was no way to get the MP's tested and I only used so much in the end before being forced down the prescription drug route). However, approx 18 months ago, I started using a Root / Plant called Rhodiola, and have been successful in my attempt not to take any prescription meds.

I have engaged many professionals utilising different methodes to help my condition, and I have underwent many tests.

Going back about 3-4 months I saw an excellent Podcast from a guy called Luke Storey and I subsequently bought his package on EMF - something that was completely new to me. Included in the package as bonus items were two podcasts (I think) involving Dr Jack Kruse. I had never heard of him before. I immediately related to his style and intellect, and so I am here.

Looking forward to catching up with a few of you.

Best Wishes
Stuart

# Thread: HELLO FROM NEW MEMBER
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-new-member.25713/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Freebird — 2021-04-12T20:53:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-new-member.25713/post-297183>

Just getting started on the forum...

A few thoughts at the moment: Ready to move. The desert feels like a parasite. But it's better than a shitty city. The sky is nicer, even when they're chem trailing... way less cars and cell towers. I like places with crappy cell service, green plants and water ways... and sea food swimming nearby. Always loved sea food. And always loved nature! Never understood why some people don't appreciate nature.

When I was a kid, I would go around the neighborhood knocking on people's doors trying to get people together to play baseball, and all the kids would be wanting to stay inside and watch TV, on a beautiful sunny day! Weird.

I don't feel great in the desert though. Bone dry and dusty! You get extra chem trails coming up out of the powder dirt and floating around in the air. And no in-between: a few weeks of spring, if you're lucky, then: blazing hot! I like moisture in the air.

I would love to roam around with some like-minded people for a short while and see more of this beautiful country while we still can. Then possibly get the hell out -- go somewhere with a simpler, more natural and healthier environment and life style.

I've been eating some sea food (want more; I miss it), grass fed beef and fat, eggs, butter -- mostly all raw.

I've been waking up around 3:30 or 4 AM (not on purpose). Go to bed between 9:30 and 10 PM. Would like to sleep a little more each night!

Can't wait to get my feet in the grass and in the water again.

That’s it for now. Onward and upward.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Hello from CA - but considering moving....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-ca-but-considering-moving.25733/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jacks — 2021-04-17T20:04:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-ca-but-considering-moving.25733/post-297371>

Hi everyone

I currently live in Berkeley, CA and have been following Dr. Kruse's work for a year or two, slowly assimilating his teachings about the wisdom of nature. I have always been a big outdoor enthusiast and love gardening, hiking, bicycling, camping and just generally spending time outside. I have been struggling with living in our current location for the past few years. Lots of reasons why we have stayed despite this, but we are starting to think more seriously about moving out now that our son is about to go off to college in the fall. My daughter is in middle school still, so this may be a good time to move before high school starts though I know she will not be happy about it.

We are considering moving somewhere in the Rocky Mountains. Anyone have any good suggestions of cities/towns that would be great place for 4-5 years that are not too costly? I would prefer somewhere in the country myself, but for the sake of my daughters social life I think closer to a larger town may be better till she is done with school. I have lived in Salt Lake City for a few years and in Los Alamos for several months a couple of times for work, so know those areas a bit but that was 20-30 years ago now. I have visited Wyoming and many other parts of Utah and some of New Mexico for recreation.purposes. The only place I thought may be OK in Arizona would be Flagstaff but I don't know much about it.

Thanks!

Jacks

### caroline — 2021-04-18T04:13:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-ca-but-considering-moving.25733/post-297386>

Hi and welcome.....

There is a thread here somewhere about moving to Florida and lots and lots of stuff about Mexico.

### Penny — 2021-04-22T18:51:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-ca-but-considering-moving.25733/post-297598>

So, if I remember correctly, here is what JK says about all of that - Colorado is a fail if you live by any of the 7 military bases that exist there - but mountains at 5000 ft are fantastic because of their solar yield - think the Swiss solariums which had people skiing with skimpy clothes for healing in the last century...

Idaho and Florida - Idaho due to its low population = less 5G - plus you'd be free there Unlike this communist state we are currently living in...

Florida for its sunshine plus the governor is awesome and you are also free there - however, there hurricanes so housing insurance is high - JK said to live somewhere off the 10 north of Miramar Beach - kind of rustic there and few towers - but I think they get hurricanes there -

Flagstaff is beautiful - a small town with a university - plus it is a red state no matter what the vote was... however, Jeff Bezos just bought a bunch of land by the Mesa airport so God knows what he will be doing with that... I grew up on Cape Cod but lived in AZ from high school on through college - great state - Flagstaff is great because it is at altitude and there is a lot of nature - that and Prescott would have been my choices if I wasn't so into sunshine and warmth

I have been looking at land to buy in Florida - it is very affordable but I haven't researched insurance costs yet... When it all goes to shit, you want to be somewhere self sufficient - so homesteading would be a nice option - but if it really goes that bad, they will take what you have...

So, in the end, if we all lose our liberty, I don't think it matters what we do... which is why we must fight like hell for it...

So, a piece of property with natural water that isn't a swamp where you can grow stuff and be somewhat off the beaten path I think would be ideal - Utah is great because the Mormons are very community minded - that is the kind of place that will survive IMHO...

So are you also in CA? Sucks here. Yeah - it is a big puzzle right now figuring out where to go. Thinking of visiting a few cities in the mountain west this summer to check them out, but that is going to take some logistics for our family. Was thinking about New Mexico, but heard it is almost as bad as here right now. Seems like Utah, Idaho and Wyoming are looking like the most health-freedom friendly in the west. All have good light and water (I think), but magnetism varies and probably emfs too. Might have to compromise in the short term. Once my daughter is done with school we will be freer but that is not for 5 years. My husband does not want to move to Florida or Texas so we will have to find a compromise. I personally love mountains and being outdoors in fantastic scenery. I don't mind the cold but my husband has Reynauds so not good for him.

# Thread: Hi, I'm new to all this
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-im-new-to-all-this.25771/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 26

### ShellyFM — 2021-04-25T02:32:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-im-new-to-all-this.25771/post-297725>

Hello! I'm new to all this and am a bit excited and overwhelmed at the same time. I recently caused myself some horrible health concerns by taking on a second job and working so much at a computer, getting on average 3 hours of sleep a night since about February 2020 to November 2020. I realized I needed to pull back in late November/December and heal myself. I've actually been making really good progress considering but am now on an ongoing quest to maximize my family's health and trying to figure out balance while still achieving my career goals if possible. I just found Jack Kruse a couple of weeks ago, and the part about the sun, seeing sunrises, minimizing artificial blue light all makes so much sense.

I'm 39 with 2 young kids and am trying to learn all this, which has been a bit tough considering my day job has me at a computer and I'm trying to minimize computer time outside of that. I would love some kind of easy getting started info if anyone has their top articles or resources to recommend that would let us quickly implement changes without a ton of research necessary. We just made a diet change to plant based in February that we decided to try for 6 months to see how it goes, and I have to say that I haven't felt better in many, many years. So, ideally for now, I would prefer not to change the diet again. I really think our family needs the sunlight changes.

I have so many questions so far from what I have learned but will save some of them until later in hopes I can find the answers in what's already available. I joined the patreon site and have been reading the blogs and listening to youtube talks while I work.

For those of you with young families, any tips on how you incorporate this with the whole family? Does watching the sunrise literally mean getting everyone outside when it's completely dark and waiting until you can actually see the sun in the sky? That seems so early for little ones and such a long time to be outside. I've read/heard on the Kruse resources where it's getting outside first thing for about 15-20 minutes, but that doesn't seem to be the same as watching the sunrise. How do you handle that when you tend to get up early like between 5-6, too?

Ok, for now, that's all I'm going to ask even though I have a ton more questions. Thanks if you've read this and have any advice or encouragement to offer!

### ShellyFM — 2021-04-30T17:32:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-im-new-to-all-this.25771/post-297992>

> [Christine Renner said:](/goto/post?id=297980)
>
> My kids are naturally early risers and when we are getting morning sun and evening sun etc that piece falls into place. We don't always get up at the same time but head out once we do.
> I wonder whether you are open to at least pescatarian? I used to be a member, strayed, and then came back, and there is something magical about dha, sunlight and good water. I am eating lots of shrimp, sardines and salmon and my kids eat salmon, tuna and a several times a week spoonful Nordic naturals cod liver oil (not ideal but they'll do it more consistently than eat the fish at this point. They love good water and being outdoors, so those pieces are easy sells.

I'm open to anything but because we just made a dietary change in mid-February, and it's working for us, that's the only reason I'm hesitant to change that part right now. My husband committed us to doing the plant based for 6 months and re-evaluate how we feel at the end of that. So, we're 2.5 months in. It's so very hard to get him to make a healthy diet plunge. So, I really don't want to sway his resolve. I do have to say I'm very nervous about adding animal protein back into our diets as I feel like we ate mostly high quality, grass fed animal protein before our recent diet change, and it didn't seem to do well for us.

I'm reading Jack's book and don't think I've gotten to water yet, if it's in there, but I've heard him say spring water is best. However, we live in Winter Springs, FL, which is known for its horrendous water. We've actually had EPA get involved and such. So, about a year and a half ago, we installed a whole house system and a 15 stage drinking water purification system from Radiant Life. Not sure how that rates with all this, but I've noticed an improvement since we installed it. It adds minerals to the water and we add more.

We're working hard to get as much sunlight as possible around our day jobs. We go outside in the morning with the kids, eat our breakfast, and let them play a bit before we have to start our working day. Some mornings, it's just 15-20 minutes and others it's over an hour. We try to get out a few minutes each hour, though we're not perfect at that, and take a 30 minute walk in the sun mid-day. Then in the evening after work, we take a family bike ride most days while the sun is getting lower before it sets.

I feel like the small changes we've made have been great but also that we have so much to learn and still implement.

We sucked it up and buy glass bottled spring water for primary drinking water. Take care of yourself first. As you improve others will either come along or not.

### Jacks — 2021-04-30T18:12:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-im-new-to-all-this.25771/post-297998>

> [ShellyFM said:](/goto/post?id=297990)
>
> Thank you! I kept looking for it on patreon.

Yes - I wasn't sure either until recently. I've been subscribing to the Patreon site for a while and looking at Dr. Kruse's Twitter feed, though I am not a subscriber myself to twitter. One of the great things about being an Optimal Klub member is that you have access to all the previous Q&A's which is a great resource.

It is hard for me to get my older kids out as they are in online school all morning with minimal breaks. My son is starting to see some benefits of getting out in the sun both before school and in the afternoon, but I think it will take a while for him to recover completely. He used to ride his bike to and from school, sit out at lunchtime with friends, and be outside briefly between classes, and then with school sports spent a lot more time outside compared to now. At least the sports are starting up again.

### ShellyFM — 2021-04-30T20:57:29+0300

### Marko Pollo — 2021-05-01T02:25:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-im-new-to-all-this.25771/post-298019>

This is just a thought I had. Costco RO water is probably sourced from lots of different water supplies. I'm guessing the Seattle costco has different water than in your Florida costco. The water may also vary throughout the year in ph, dissolved solids ECT. An RO system is imo pretty cheap for controlling your own water supply.

### EWO — 2021-05-01T02:34:25+0300

The water may also vary throughout the year in ph, dissolved solids ECT. An RO system is imo pretty cheap for controlling your own water supply.

We buy RO at .35 per gallon in bulk as a piece of the equation. We were buying a distilled water that was vortexed etc and adding our own minerals. I think that's were we really went astray.

Since adding spring water in glass bottles, and using in conjunction with RO and sometimes DDW, we are all feeling and looking much much better.

**Subforum:** Meet and Greet

### BenJenkins — 2021-04-29T01:52:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-adelaide-australia.25790/post-297918>

Hi All, Just thought I'd introduce myself and say Hi

I've been following Jacks work since 2015 and have been lurking around this site on and off, I have a few questions so thought I'd register.

I'm looking forward to participating

Thanks

Ben

### Richard Watson — 2021-04-29T02:49:39+0300

Any thoughts on Bitcoin and how to manage it here in Australia?????

### BenJenkins — 2021-04-29T03:54:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-adelaide-australia.25790/post-297935>

Hey Chantelle, good to see a local

Hi Caroline, In terms of buying and selling I've either used BTC markets or CoinSpot (the latter a little more expensive but have more coins for trade). I don't currently own a significant amount of BTC but I've had some money parked in ADA (Cardano) for a few years, they seem to have a good platform and solid vision for the future. If you're interested I'd recommend having a look at some of the video's from Charles Hoskinson (Cardano's founder).

My wife has recently got into crypto trading and she has been buying LTC locally on BTC markets and then transferring to her Binance account which has a wide variety of coins to trade.

# Thread: Greetings from Playa del Carmen
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-playa-del-carmen.25797/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### quantumaroo — 2021-05-01T10:35:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-playa-del-carmen.25797/post-298026>

Hello everyone!

I've been lurking this forum for a few years now, and since I actually decided to move to Playa del Carmen based on what I read here (more on that in a little bit) I thought it's time to actually make an account and connect with the kruse tribe

A little about me:

\- 25 yo and from UK

\- suffered with fatigue / brain fog for over a decade. Also I have poor long term memory

\- tried many of the usual things people try before they discover Jack- keto, nootropics, paying extortionate amounts for coffee from Dave Asprey and mixing it with the obligatory butter / mct

\- after moving out of my parents place 4 years ago, and then coming back a couple years later, I noticed my fatigue worsened. I learned there had been a flood in the water pipe next to my bedroom while at university. After subsequently spending the worst month of my life in an apartment with visible black mold all over the pipes and suffering massive joint pain, fatigue and difficulty breathing I realized I do have mold sensitivity

\- 5g of creatine per day turned out to be the most powerful hack Ive discovered (so far), since creatine improves mito function, this gave me reason to believe my mitochondria are out of whack

\- discovered jacks work a couple years ago and have been extremely interested since. Admittedly, I dont understand as much as I should about the quantum biology (maybe a consequence of low dopamine). Nonetheless, what Jack preaches has always struck me as being fundamental and something I will have to one day put to the test on my n=1

\- many things Ive tried over the years have helped, but I never felt optimal. And my cognition has always been sub par.

My current situation

Given the UK still being under lockdown and trying to coerce its entire population to take an experimental gene therapy, I decided its time to make the move to Playa del Carmen and see what this is all about.

After arriving in my air bnb in Colosio in early april, I was greeted with the dreaded 'musty' smell of mold. My first day I felt awful- a combination of jet lag from my 33 hour flight and the slight moldiness in the flat.

In early afternoon on my first day,. I headed out to Oxxo to get some water. When I got home with my 20l jug of Cristal and took a swig, my brain felt lile it came back online. I guess all the sweating and having dysfunctional mitochondria that dont produce much water was effecting me

For the rest of April, as I saw each sunrise and got my 2 hours of midday sun with a healthy dose of ocean CT, I felt better and better. My memory improved, i had great dopamine, I felt more social and extroverted and my overall cognition became more enhance.

I discovered my new favorite dish: ceviche. It gives a mental clarity boost lile little else.

_Things were really looking up, until._.

I moved into a new flat closer to centro

To be honest, Im feeling dreadful here- and Im sure its the mold. Nothing else makes me feel this way.

Im unable to sleep and my brain function is terrible here

I guess there is more mold here than the last air bnb (or a more toxic strain)

I am planning to ventilate the flat with open windows and fans tomorrow to see if it helps

Im hoping that as my redox improves, my mold sensitivity lessens. We'll see.

Ive signed a 6 month lease on this flat which is the worst part about this.

I really just wanted to get this off my chest, thanks for reading of you got this far  Any advice greatly appreciated

But, Im gonna try and stay positive and find a solution to this.

Great to meet everyone- looking forward to joining the discussion!

### Plural — 2024-05-02T21:32:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-playa-del-carmen.25797/post-334225>

> [quantumaroo said:](/goto/post?id=298026)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/extracting-the-comment-section-from-the-blogs.25843/post-298490>

hey everybody, hope everyone is enjoying their day.

i just built a little tool, that let's you extract the comment-section from the blogpost and put it in a handy little text-file that you can print.

as we all know, the gold nuggets are in the blog-post

so if anyone is interested, let me know and i'll get you hooked up

cheers.

### jenslangenbach_germany — 2021-05-16T09:14:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/extracting-the-comment-section-from-the-blogs.25843/post-298619>

My pleasure!

How to:

Extract the file anywhere you want. Open the "JackKruseDotComCommentExtractor.ConsoleRunner.exe"-File and paste all the links to the blogposts you want to export.

Those will then be exported to a subfolder named "Exports".

Feel free with any feedback you have

(I then copy-paste them to word and shift the layout around so it doesnt cost too much wood to print them out  )

PS: Just found out that it throws an exception when using it with a blogpost that doesn't have any comments. just a headsup for everyone.

# Thread: Greetings - Just joined
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-just-joined.25921/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MichaelC — 2021-05-28T04:52:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-just-joined.25921/post-299032>

Hello all,

Been wanting to join this forum for some time. I first learned about Dr. Kruse and his work in late 2018, and been a follower on Facebook since then. I've even met a few of the mitochondriacs IRL including GinaR, Sun Disciple, Lloyd and several others while down in Mexico. This is when I first learned of the term 'biohacking', and was informed that I had in fact been a biohacker all my life without ever knowing there was a term for what I was doing.

Anyway, glad to be here. Don't know how active I'll be, with being as busy as I am, but look forward to participating and hopefully being of service in whatever small ways I can. I currently live in Austin but planning to relocate to somewhere a littler further removed from the city pretty soon.

edit: just to add, my profile avatar is a photo that was taken of me by someone who snuck up behind me and captured it as I was watching the sunrise from a hotel lobby in Acapulco.

# Thread: Another New Yorker heading for the Gulf South next month ;-)

### Lisa Norton — 2021-06-01T20:19:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-new-yorker-heading-for-the-gulf-south-next-month.25947/post-299259>

Greetings Everyone!

I've been living in Brooklyn and working in New York for 5 years, and although I'm very health conscious, this lifestyle has definitely impacted my recovery and wellness. Starting in July, I'll be looking for short-term rentals or Airbnbs in towns from Florida to Arizona, and planning to spend some time exploring the lifestyles and cultures in these areas.

Shoutout to Joel Marquez and Deana Louise Heikkinen for their super helpful Mitochondriacs Moving to Florida Facebbok Group. I look forward to learning from all of you, doubling down on the protocols, and sharing what I learn!

Lisa

# Thread: Hello, new here - SF Bay Area
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-new-here-sf-bay-area.25983/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh Benson-Merron — 2021-06-12T12:02:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-new-here-sf-bay-area.25983/post-299691>

Hello all, I was introduced to Jack Kruse earlier this year. I've been reading his blog articles, reading his book, and listening to his podcast interviews for the last couple months. It's a lot of information to take in but I'm absorbing it all slowly. I'm a 30 year old man living in the SF Bay Area in Santa Rosa (wine country).

I moved to New York City after college and was under extreme stress. I developed a severe skin condition on my lips called Exfoliative Cheilitis. My lip skin would crust and peel, thick layers of skin on a daily basis. They were extremely inflamed, swollen, and burning. It caused me extreme anxiety and depression, to the point where I couldn't leave my apartment for a month because I was too embarrassed to be seen by anyone. I slowly recovered from this condition over many years although still to this day I am not fully recovered. I still have to manage the condition and try to hide it when I am in public. In 2016 I developed psoriasis all over my body. A couple years ago I started getting stomach pains in the morning when I woke up. This past year it got so bad some days I could hardly function. Also a couple years ago I started developing this feeling in my right ear like my ear was blocked. Finally, a few months ago I developed a rash on my face which spread across my face around my mouth area and would not go away with antibiotics. Needless to say, I felt fed up with my health situation and felt like I was up against a brick wall. Conventional medicine had done practically nothing for me in all these years, at best given me bandaid solutions for my symptoms without helping me get to the root of any of my problems.

That being said, I feel like I have hit a major turning point in my health in the last few months. Reading everything Jack Kruse has written has turned on so many lightbulbs in my brain. I have been going outside in the morning sunlight every day for the past month or so. I've been eating a ton of oysters and seafood, grass fed meat, pastured eggs, coconut oil, bone broth, a big breakfast with a ton of protein, no snacking between meals, drinking mountain spring water, I purchased EarthRunners and RA Optics glasses. In the past month the rash on my face has almost completely disappeared. The psoriasis across my body has also almost completely disappeared. The stomach pains have significantly improved, many days there is no pain. The issue with my ear persists, as well as my lip condition. However, I feel more optimistic about my health than I have in years. I feel I'm finally on the right track to get better, and I'm committed to beating this lip condition once and for all. It would be the triumph of my life if I healed from this lip condition. I struggle with sleep, especially going to bed soon after sunset and waking up for the sunrise. I have been MUCH better with my sleep in the past couple months but it's not consistent enough. I have been waking up to see the sunrise some days but not consistent enough. I struggle getting away from technology before bed, especially after a long work day, and turning off all lights after sunset. I know it's simple but I am addicted to my phone and I need to break that habit.

Anyway, thanks for reading. I'm trying my best to absorb everything here but it's a lot to take in, and I welcome any pointers!! Especially given my specific health conditions. Thank you!!

### caroline — 2021-06-13T07:24:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-new-here-sf-bay-area.25983/post-299714>

Hi Josh and welcome.......

It is great to read about your progress!

Have you thought of going camping for a week or more - in an area with no wifi?

Maybe camp by a river or the ocean, fish and relax and hang around naked as much as possible.

You already know you have to break your phone/technology habit ....... That is going to be critical for you to reclaim your health.

Have you thought about doing movnat? Erwin Lecore?

Jack did a blog about him and then did a webinar with him - Erwin is pretty spectacular . ......webinar is

maybe around 2015?

Jack also went to a camp of his in New Mexico.

You are young and motivated now - you need to reclaim your life!

Are you into Bitcoin? Jack is pretty emphatic about wealth = health.

Conventional medicine has let us down ......so Jack is working on decentralised medicine to partner with decentralised crypto.

Hello, from Dripping Springs Texas. I spent most of my life in Sonoma County all of my adult life in Santa Rosa. Miss that place sometimes, used to hang out at Brew coffee, Flying Goat and Acre Coffee. We moved to Austin after Santa Rosa fires and to be at a lower latitude. Where in Santa Rosa are you getting a good sunrise?

It took me many years to get over smart phone addiction, start by deleting all the apps the keep you on it. Leave in the car when out and about. These days I turn it on Airplane mode more often, when I arrive hone in the evening it goes to airplane mode until I leave in the AM. Resist the urge to Google things, be curious...

### Jacks — 2021-06-30T03:07:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-new-here-sf-bay-area.25983/post-300498>

Hi Josh

Welcome. I live in the Bay Area also (Berkeley), but seriously thinking about moving. However, I did spend a week recently at the northern end of the Napa Valley and felt much better. Great sunshine, less WiFi and EMFs in general. Also just went camping in the Sierra which was excellent too, though we were deep in a valley so hard to get that morning or evening sunlight. I've been having a difficult time upping my Vitamin D currently and I'm trying to figure it out. Too many EMFs where I live and wondering about interference from pollution/geoengineering which may be affecting my absorption.

Jacks

### Jacks — 2021-06-30T06:37:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-new-here-sf-bay-area.25983/post-300506>

> [Christine Renner said:](/goto/post?id=300502)
>
> Have you hard wired your personal devices and reddened your screens? I'd always hard wired laptops but only added mobile devices more recently. Not sure about the d but those two changes (with sun rises and sets plus good water) brought my fasting glucose down 20 points. If you haven't yet, try it? Also, have you tried deuterium depleted water? Not currently using it, but have tried and would do it regularly if my health gains stalled.

Yes - all our computers are hard wired and I work as far as possible from the WiFi box. We live with my husbands sister and partner (upstairs) and we share internet/phones/cable (Comcast) and they use WiFi extensively (it is all boxed up in one unit). My BIL stays up till 3 or 4 am working or surfing or watching TV and so we can't turn off WiFi at night as everything is combined in our system including phones. When they go out of town we turn it off at night. But that is fairly infrequent these days. We are trying to move the WiFi box upstairs to their space but it would still affect us but hoping it is less.

### Penny — 2021-06-30T18:13:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-new-here-sf-bay-area.25983/post-300523>

Greetings I think you have a melanopsin problem...  So, basically, retinol (vitamin a) is bound to melanopsin (found not only in your eyes, but also skin and blood arterioles) - so when you get into a lot of blue light (think: phone) or wifi - the very weak covalent bond between melanopsin and retinol is broken leaving free retinol to destroy any molecule that absorbs light... this includes: melatonin, catalase, B12, folic acid, dopamine and of course melanopsin - so, because dopamine is destroyed, you need that dopamine hit you get from your phone... and your skin and eyes will go to shit... your ears feel full (think glue ear) because that is one way the body excretes free retinol... I have only experienced this once when I was on stage doing a chamber concert - my kids are always talking about "glue ear" and they use hydrogen peroxide to break it up - you can buy something called Debrox which is peroxide in a probably safer form...  So, what organ in your body is closest to your phone? Your lips... your nose... and your eyes... if you want to surf, get a computer - not a laptop - get a BenQ monitor - it supposedly is flicker free - run iris (iristech.com) for the flicker/blue light - run RJ45 cables - no wifi - and get some blue blockers - cover your lips - I would guess zinc oxide might work - when you surf... have the monitor 2 feet away from you - you can use google voice to transfer all texts to the computer I would guess - it would be seemless to other people - I use Discord to text my son who has a cell phone - my father used to tell me "life is a series of choices my friend, choose wisely" - only you can change you... instead of going home after work, go walk the beach - see how it is being around people again... Change all your bulbs to incandescent and run a U/V ligtht or open a window whenever you are inside - also, sunrise creates dopamine/serotonin/insulin if you need more motivation to see it and JK says 80% of his healing modalities is tied up in the sunrise - I get up when I hear the birds chirping... then I take a walk and forage for pine needles... it is very motivating actually And it gives me something to do outside in the A.M. - and for more inspiration, you increase testosterone by 300% by getting your junk in the sun... you can buy tan through shorts (kiniki - I forget the other one) and shirt for your morning walks - or go to Red Rock Beach next to Stinson - it is a nude beach... you can google all the nude beaches in your area When you are in your 30s, you make hormones more in your adrenals than your prostrate - so in order to make testosterone/vitamin d, you must have retinol - so that is another reason why many people who are in the "soup" can't make vitamin d (the last hormone made in the adrenals) - you can do a test - get DHEA and vitamin D - if either is low, you have a melanopsin problem...

[https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-018-28254-8/](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-018-28254-8/)

### Marius — 2021-07-16T16:05:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-new-here-sf-bay-area.25983/post-301212>

> [Dean6789 said:](/goto/post?id=299875)
>
> Hey Marius, How's Dripping Springs Texas? Never been there -- Are you feeling good there?

Felling great here, we first moved near downtown Austin, EMF levels in the city are crazy. I started having heart rate issues due to high EMF levels. Other EHS symptoms started during that two years. My girlfriend started having some heart rate issues too. We discovered Jacks work through a Luke Storey podcast and dove in deep from there. We threw away all sunglasses, watched the sun rise everyday, swam in any cold water we find, ice baths in tub and on and on.
I came upon a rental on 5 acres in Dripping Springs 30 miles out of Austin, we jumped on it 1st of march 2020. Best thing we ever did.. We got ourselves a freezer converted, thank you Luke Storey, we keep it cold about 35-40 and will go in multiple times a day.. Sunrise is a little late 10-15 because we live a little low but our view is across a creek looking into 350 acres of land with lots of trees and cows..

# Thread: Want to live in Tulum?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-live-in-tulum.26140/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jesilyn — 2021-07-11T21:23:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-live-in-tulum.26140/post-300983>

Hey all, wanted to share this opportunity for anyone looking for an extended stay in the Yucatán! I’m looking for a roommate here in Tulum (I’ve been here for a month getting to know the area) starting August 15 or potentially even sooner. The current renter’s lease ends September 15. From there it would be working directly with the owner, so probably a six month lease. It’s a great two bedroom in the la valeta area. Ground floor. Pool. Extremely low RF measurements in the bedrooms even with the WiFi router on (which we would of course set up to use as a wired connection). Rent total to be split is around $1500, plus electric, which we could keep pretty low by being strategic with the AC.

I’ve had quite severe health struggles for the past few years, and while I want to explore other areas as well, I feel that setting myself up with a stable environment for a period of time with a roommate interested in the same health perspectives and applications is a good next move. I feel really good about this place! Let me know if you want to learn more about the situation or me!

Link:[https://www.facebook.com/marketplace/item/319924966462102/](https://www.facebook.com/marketplace/item/319924966462102/)

My WhatsApp: +15053778067

> Thanks, we’re in a low population density, but it’s changing, people are coming here compared to other areas land is cheap, I’ll have to move away, to the forest, or further out and drive more which i I don’t want to do.

Good to know. Not surprised.

I'm kind of enamored with that region because of the forest. Seems that something has been calling me there. Just need to find viable income and I'd most likely be on the road pronto.
