# Thread: Hello from Finland!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-finland.26142/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Aleksi — 2021-07-11T23:34:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-finland.26142/post-300992>

Hi all! Long time reader of this forum and blogs here making his first post  I am a 30 year old male born in Northern Finland and now living a bit more south but still on a high latitude (65.01). My family line has been living around this area for at least 500 years. So harsh, long winters and efficient, sun-lit summers are the name of the game. I have done 23andme genetic test a couple years ago and my ancestry is 100% northern European, and also 100% FInnish. My haplotype is N-L550, so that also is centered on the northern latitude quite much. I have never had problems with cold climate and kind of enjoy the winters, except the non-existent sunlight during that time. What I understand from all the reading here, is that the latitude is not the perfect one comparatively, but not as bad for me personally because my biology has been formed through the hunders and thousands of year in this kind of climate.

I put my health information in my signature as adviced on the forum. Something more to elaborate: I am an active, healthy male with a fond interest in human health for about 10 years. I am quite of a student and always try to learn more. I stumbled on Dr Jack Kruse in 2017 through Ben Greenfield's podcast and immediately listened to more of his stuff. What appealed to me is the logic in his thinking. I have been a "evolution based" thinker for a long time and before even finding Dr Jack's stuff, I had a lot of "controversial" thoughts about many of the conveniencies of modern life, for example sunscreens, medications, vaccines, food, lifestyle etc. My brain always reverts back to nature-based thinking, trying to look at things through the lense of evolution and nature. When I heard Jack say in one of his podcast appearences, and also his Epi-Paleo RX book (which I've read twice so far), that a lot of what he teaches "makes sense in your primal, instinctual thinking", I knew there is wisdom to this. So in a way, what I have learned through him, reinforced and showed the science for a lot of stuff that I had an opinion already but didn't have any science backed information.

I never was a sickly child and always have had quite a robust immune system. No allergies or anything. I had a good upbringing and one of the biggest gifts that I have had is my family's holiday home (more like a ranch) in the middle of nowhere, by big lakes, old forests and lots of hunting and gathering opportunities. The biggest parts of my free time has been spent here exploring the outdoors and nowadays with the wisdom I now have, the place has risen to even higher value. I am a very passionate hunter, fisherman, gatherer and outdoorsman. I am also a black belt in Brazilian Jiu Jitsu and even though it is an inside sport, the camaraderie and social aspects of it outweigh the indoor problems, at least I think so. I also have been lifting a lot of weights through the years but lately have been investing this time more to MovNat as it makes that "primal sense" to me and also Jack supports it a lot. The training is also fun and supports my other endeavors.

I have read a lot of the posts here about other people's health challenges and I have to say that I may be a bit outlier here, shamefully having to say that I don't have and never have had big health challenges or mental challenges. I am a bit stubborn, optimistic and outgoing guy and I like people a lot in general. I am more on a journer to my optimal self, as you all call it here. As I try to think, maybe the biggest health challenge that I have had to work on through the years is a problem with my left SI joint that goes out time to time. It started in 2012 and I have done a lot of rehab and studying to alleviate the problem and it has went better, but still i have work to do with it. Maybe it will be like this always. The club foot birth defect I wrote on my signature has a place in this problem for sure, but I refuse to lie all the blame on that as it doesn't help anything.

What else? I have done the Leptin RX at the start of this year. Sunlight with as little clothes as possibe whenever I can. My job is not optimal in any way, as it is a basic indoor office job where EMF and indoor air is a big part of unfortunately. I try to take outside movement snacks whenever I can. More sunlight has for sure made a good impact on my general wellbeing, no doubt about that. For cold environment adaptation, the winters and not-too-much clothing make their impact. Also, I take cold showers every day year-round and have been ice swimming for a couple years already, couple times a week. I am lean, around 11-12% body fat and dabbling with IMF and Keto for about 2 years. Nowadays I try to eat seasonally as Jack suggests in his book. I have to focus on more seafood though. Bitcoin is also a thing and I am looking to invest more into it, but got a good start. I meditate daily and I really think meditation is a very important tool for overall happiness and life in general, and especially now in today's world where your attention is being ripped apart almost constantly.

I will add more stuff here as I remember and as (possibly) you guys request

One question I do have though, is that even though the Post Leptin RX suggests never to skip brekfast, I feel my body and brain works best with 10/14 - 8/16 IMF eating, with the feeds starting 12-14 a clock. Dr Jack says never to skip breakfast but I wonder if that is a big problem after all to a non-leptin resistant person, which I am quite sure I am. I can do with eating breakfast, don't get me wrong, but as I work 10-18, the later eating window suits me more well and I am more alert and active in my experience by eating like this. Any further advice here? I understand the breakfast is important because of the circadian system, but IMF makes that "primal sense" here to me, as I have understood humans quite much evolved by eating whenever they got food and usually that meant gathering/hunting it first at the start of the day and then preparing and eating it, which of course pushes the first bite later on the day.

I am very happy to start my journey here on this forum. Please tell me what other information you may want to hear and I will do my best to add more  Till that moment, I wish everyone good health and happiness!

### Penny — 2021-07-12T04:36:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-finland.26142/post-301010>

Any further advice here? >
> I am very happy to start my journey here on this forum. Please tell me what other information you may want to hear and I will do my best to add more  Till that moment, I wish everyone good health and happiness!

It's just that digestion is better in the sunlight and melatonin works better on an empty stomach (don't eat within 4 hours of bedtime) - the post leptin RX has most of the protein at night I vaguely recall from his book "The Leptin RX" - if you have energy and can sleep and can think clearly - I think you're good to go really...  Welcome! One guy on this forum went to Finland and lost 30lbs in one month by doing IR sauna and jumping into the cold lake/ocean whatever it was - they also have those cool islands around there - my son married a Finnish girl and her grandfather went to the island during covid where there was no technology or even electricity - he is very healthy... just try to do the best you can in our wifi soup world and get into nature/outside as much as possible and what else can we all do?

### caroline — 2021-07-12T05:28:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-finland.26142/post-301014>

Hi Alexsi ...and a big welcome!

Please start a journal in the optimal journal section and give Dr. K. your Mom's and Gran's health history too.

Congratulations on all the positive stuff you do.

The leptin reset isn't really about food ....it is about resetting your circadian rhythm.

Can't wait to hear more about your lifestyle......

### Aleksi — 2021-07-12T17:49:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-finland.26142/post-301024>

Thank you for your replies Penny and Caroline!

Penny, I have tried skipping dinner before (so last meal is at 16-17 from the usual 20), and even though my nighttime RHR is a tad lower, personally I don't find any big difference in sleep quality. I think it may have to do with my strong digestion/metabolism. I really can quite much get away eating almost anything, even though I don't do that. I gravitate towards meat and fatty foods in general and it is very natural for me to eat fewer and bigger meals.

I am a big sauna lover and go to regular Finnish sauna at least 4 times a week. We have an electric sauna at my apartment here, but in our holiday home it is the real deal, wood stove sauna. In between sauna sessions I take long dips in the lake nearby. And during winter of course, ice swimming

I also think that even though the idea of jumping off the grid for good sounds romantic and great, the best option at least for now is to try to mitigate all the damage from modern living. If my income stops as I would like to run away from all the computers and phones, that doesn't support my wife and soon-to-born child very well. Trying to do our very best as you said Penny  I have to admit though that one day living at our holiday home in the middle of nowhere is somewhat on my goal-list but that takes some work before becoming possible...

You mentioned Penny about your son's girlfriend's grandfather being very healthy living off the grid; my paternal grandfather is 92 at the moment and in good shape considering his age. He has lived a life outdoors taking care of his land and properties, which all lie in the forest. His siblings are all long-living and his mother (= my great grandmother) lived to 100 years and showed no signs of cognitive decline ever. I got good genetics from that side.

Forgot to mention before that blue blockers are in daily use and got Iris on my computers. One thing I also didn't mention but got on my signature is that unfortunately I had eye laser surgery done in 2017 before I knew any of this stuff. I don't know how f'd up I am because of it (change in sunlight coming into the eye) but living without the glasses has been a very welcome addition regarding my sport and outdoor endeavors and I recuperated very quicly from the operation. But if you guys got any tips regarding this, I am open to hear!

Caroline, I mentioned before in this post about my genetic makeup from my paternal side but you asked about my mother's side. One thing that comes from my mother's side is the very common Finnish "peoples' disease" of predisposition to obesity and heart and arteries related diseases. My mom is 61 at the moment and actually quite in a good shape (health wise) even though she works like crazy in the health care business. She has never had any big health hurdles, at least what I know of, no medications before but now having to take blood pressure medication, which is not wise of course. Obesity has come to the picture in these later years and I would love to get her on the Leptin Reset when all that damn work stops. The stress plays a big role here. Her mother, my maternal grandmother, was quite healthy until 70 (no medications or anything in her younger years) but has had a steep decline in her later years of lots of medications for different purposes, surgeries, movement problems and now possibly some cognitive problems. But I will possibly try to write furthermore in my Optimal Journal.

# Thread: Hello
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.26171/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scott klein — 2021-07-17T00:04:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.26171/post-301232>

Hello all,

I am so happy to be apart of this community now. Found Dr.Jack on clubhouse and never looked back..

Preface:
I have had really bad IBD since I was a young kid. I have tried Remicade, Humira, entyvio, xeljanz, and then back to Remicade. I have had two DVTS (at age 13 and 17), reaccuring CDIFF, fecal transplants, etc. This past summer I really started looking for answers, as I was in the midst of a 2 year long flare. I went down the carnivore rabbit hole and became a semi carnivore, eating quality meat and food. I then started seeing a holistic (which I know jack is not a fan of) and she helped me get a decent bit better. I then scheduled a colonoscopy with my GI, hoping for good results. Little did I know that day my colon would be perforated by a resident doctor (didnt know she would be doing the procedure). I had always had trouble with colposcopy as my colon developed a stricture early on, preventing most doctors from ever going higher than the stricture. My colon had been beaten up since I first got diagnosed, so it was already fragile, especially while in a flare (at the time flare was mild). Anways I woke up that day in extreme pain after the conloscopy. That night I got my colon removed, and left with an colectamy.

Now:
My current situation is a bit tricky. Now I am a huge health freak. I workout almost everyday, and do manual labor outside. I follow almost all of jacks protocols such as, sun as much as possible, I filter my water (shower and drinking), eat super quality food (meat sourced from a local ranch, pole caught canned tuna, raw milk, eggs, cheese, starting try oysters, etc.), blue blockers and IRIS, etc. I take this all very seriously. After trying every single health protocol since getting sick Dr Jack has been the only one to connect every single dot and make total sense, and give actual results to feeling great. Nature is the best medicine. However, I want to be reconnected. I can get a j-pouch or be connected to by rectum. My colon's damage seeped all the way past the wall, which is not usual for UC (which I was diagnosed with originally). My pathology came back inconclusive for UC or Chrons. I have had a bunch of other tests done as well, all the same. That being said I have never felt better since following these protocols. I dont want to be stuck with a J-pouch, and I dont think the surgeons would recommend getting one considering I could have chrons. I want to be reconnected to my rectum, but they will try and shill me immune suppressants. I want nothing to do with any of that fiat medicine ever again. So, my question is, if I opt to be reconnected to my rectum without any fiat drugs, is that smart? What should I do? Again, all of this can be solved by living as humans were intended to, alongside nature. But, I really am seeking advice.

I hope this short and brief overview helps for an answer,

Thanks,

Scott

### caroline — 2021-07-17T11:12:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.26171/post-301246>

Hi Scott and welcome.....

Start a journal in the optimal journal section and give Dr.K your Mom's and Gran's health history as well.

Where do you live? work?

What do you eat? drink?

What level of membership are you? You may want to consider a "gold" membership and then you can ask Jack questions on the monthly pow wows.

You may also want to consider a consult with Jack ...if and when he has time..

You have taken the first important step ....keep going

### JanSz — 2021-07-17T18:05:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.26171/post-301256>

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/aging-is-not-a-disease-damn-it.25242/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/aging-is-not-a-disease-damn-it.25242/)

[@Dennis Clark](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/12267/)

Possibly may have some suggestions for you [@Scott klein](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/25660/)

### JanSz — 2021-07-17T18:09:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.26171/post-301257>

[@Dennis Clark](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/12267/)

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/after-9-months.20448/page-38#post-264312](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/after-9-months.20448/page-38#post-264312)

> [Dennis Clark said:](/goto/post?id=264312)
>
> Put your medico-talk hat on: My surgery was due to long-term ulcerative colitis (20+ years). The final solution was to remove it, leading to a procedure called ileo-anal anastomosis (aka, J-pouch surgery: [https://www.mayoclinic.org/tests-procedures/j-pouch-surgery/about/pac-20385069](https://www.mayoclinic.org/tests-procedures/j-pouch-surgery/about/pac-20385069)). The surgery removed my entire colon, plus rectum. I now have a "J-pouch" - so-called because of the shape of the end of my small intestine, now connected directly to the anus, to form an internal ostomy "bag" of sorts. My fellow J-pouchers and I are in such a special group that we have our own forum: [https://www.j-pouch.org/](https://www.j-pouch.org/). It is mediated by a nurse, so the information is pretty mainstream ... i.e., not all that useful. No information on long-term consequences. No surprise there. With perfect hindsight (a redundancy, I know), I have no doubt that I could have solved my colitis and prevented the surgery had I known what I know now. The whole episode contributed to, among other things, me discovering the Jack Kruse site in 2015. My search for real biological health solutions continues.

# Thread: Hello from the South of France :)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-south-of-france.26404/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Yac — 2021-09-08T13:49:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-south-of-france.26404/post-303476>

Hello everyone ! I finally decided to create an account, sorry if my english is not that great.

My name is Yacine and I just turned 30. I currently live in Toulouse, in the South of France (43°36'15.34"N, 1°26'37.21"E) and hope to move even closer to a region with more sunlight in the future.

I was unfortunately vegan for 2 1/2 years until 3 years ago so I guess I'm still slowly recovering. I also escaped this prison called Paris 2 years ago  to start a master's degree elsewhere in... urban planning (ironically enough) and I just finished.

I'm here because I would love to learn more, exchange tips and views but more importantly, meet new people from France... The past 2 years away from family (+ multiple lockdowns) have been difficult and I'm getting more and more exhausted by the general ideology of people being alienated (but that's not their fault) by masks, "vaccines", lockdowns, social distancing, 5g, and so on (getting looks wearing blue blockers in the subway haha )

So I try to compensate as much as possible with what I can afford and what I can do in terms of my own psychological triggers and state of isolation. For example I'm starting to become a nudist but I'm still addicted to sugar  I try to buy healthy animal fats but I live in social housing with a lot of nnemf radiation...
"Eating well", nnemf protection, clean water... they all cost money, and I don't have any at the moment. So I just keep reading, listening, learning, to the day I can afford living where I want, and protecting myself the way I need.

More importantly, I realized I need to be around people that share the same values and that arrived at the same conclusions than me... It's the thing I'm lacking the most and is the more important I think.

I did implement stuff though ! I religiously wear blue blocking 45% and 100% and sleep 8h-8.5h a night, cold showers and oysters sometimes, grounding  little by little.

Anyway I hope everyone has a great day and please send me a message if you want to chat.

Yac

### caroline — 2021-09-09T05:39:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-south-of-france.26404/post-303517>

Hi Yac ...and welcome!

# Thread: From Toronto, Canada. Looking to connect with locals. Also, where to start?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-toronto-canada-looking-to-connect-with-locals-also-where-to-start.26415/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Discalced — 2021-09-11T19:54:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-toronto-canada-looking-to-connect-with-locals-also-where-to-start.26415/post-303592>

Hey! I'm David from Toronto,

I learned about Kruse like 10 years ago, but wasn't ready for it until now, especially after recent health issues (fatigue/apathy & stress/overwhelm, which I'm guessing a lot of it is due to MTHFR mutation I have). Starting doing the morning light due to Huberman's recommendations, but Kruse takes it much further. Also working on the grounding and EMF stuff too.

I started making a habit of running down to the beach each morning & going into the lake.

I'm quite rationally minded, so I'm quite skeptical about all this, but the great thing is that it's not that much of a risk to start trying & applying. It's also inspiring me to learn quantum physics so I can get a good understanding of the mechanisms and check his work.

Personally, I was quite into human movement science & spent quite a long time learning about strength & conditioning and physical rehabilitation, especially developmental kinesiology. A number of years ago, I tried mindfulness meditation to help with the stress, and eventually that blossomed into full on interest into (mystical/esoteric) religion, like indo-Tibetan Buddhism, Apostolic Christianity etc.

Would be great to connect with people in the area who are living the lifestyle, to share resources, tips etc.

Hey everyone I’m Adriel 29 from Sydney Australia.

I’m joining to figure out answers to weird autoimmune condition(s) that I’ve been dealing with since 2019. Undiagnoseable looks like hashimotos. I’ve had in under control through diet for a while but recent wild flair ups have motivated me to seek better answers and long term solutions. (I think you guys have logs here I’ll drop all the information in that)

I’m already into the keto/carnivore life. I’m very active when my body isn’t falling apart. Been into crypto for ages. I’m ethnically Indian lots of information of different races having different nutrition responses. I came here through a strong recommendation from a friend, seeing Dr Kruses thread on leaving Australia, sealed the deal that I want to participate in this community!

There’s a lot of info out here. What’s the best threads to start with?

### Sharon Coste — 2022-04-21T09:15:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/n-1.26964/post-312343>

I want to know what is the meaning of "your n equals 1"?

### Sue-UK — 2022-04-21T09:54:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/n-1.26964/post-312344>

> [Sharon Coste said:](/goto/post?id=312343)
>
> I want to know what is the meaning of "your n equals 1"?

It means an experiment with a sample of one, my n=1 is my experience, my result. Your experience or result - your n=1 - for the same thing may be different.

### Sharon Coste — 2022-04-21T11:00:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/n-1.26964/post-312346>

Ahhh, okay, I get it! Thank you for explaining. I am swimming in unchartered waters as I acclaimate myself to learning all the science involved in your body's systems and even something as simple as the scientific terms n=1. It is easier for me when things are put in more simplistic answers of a shorter version of here is what is going on and here is what you need to do to fix it. It is a struggle for me!

# Thread: Not Sure Where To Start - Hi, I'm Steve
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-where-to-start-hi-im-steve.27130/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Steven LaBella — 2022-08-09T06:52:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-where-to-start-hi-im-steve.27130/post-314936>

Steve from Fontana, CA here.

I have been overweight since birth, born iron deficient. I would be very thin if I had no elbows.

Now for the reason I am here. Male, 55, 330lbs. Diabetic, CHF patient, CPAP wearer, can't walk a decent 15 seconds before my back feels like I just want to lay down. I have tried everything and I mean everything, B-12 shots, Jenny Craig, Weight watchers, Nutri-System, Paleo, Keto, starvation/fasting, Electrolytes, in the work of Robb Wolf, recently discovered his book, I AM BROKEN.

I look in the mirror now and see Leptin Resistant, overweight, ready to stop everything and just let the LORD take me home. My Bride would hate that and she would not survive this world without me, really. I have lived in California all 55 years. Never really travelled due to the weight, heart, diabetes, back pain. I blame no one but myself for the millions of bad calories I have digested and turned my body into the destructive mess it is in. I know now that the healing will be a long duration of intense starts and fill in the gap of what I can do for myself. I have no one to go along this journey with as everyone around me looks to be in good health (probably not) and does not need "old fatty" telling them what they are doing is killing them one cell at a moment. Since the Scamdemic I have spent my days answering "NO" to the question - Do you want fries with that?. Ugh so terrible. I have eaten Dirty Keto for most of the last 5 years, to no avail.

Labs - want to shoot myself. HBA1c=-8.4, glucose 168, I know should be lower by half. Cholesterol - Total 175, Good needs help, bad needs more help. More on this later.

At my wits end and need some real good encouragement, no more listening to all the bad news about the world, country, state, county, city(X50,000) and everything.

I am armed with the books, diet, workout, etc. and not knowing where to start. I am sunbathing, sticking my feet in the GROUND and experienced a setback that I guess I just need to leave this GOD awful place I live and start fresh.

Sorry Jack I tried not to write this in the Dennis Downer tone but, hey guess what, the truth is what matters. Oh yeah and my step-daughter (25) HATES me with every fiber of her being even though she has had a really good time screwing up her and everybody's lives in the process.

Any advice, not psycho-babbel but, real advice I can get for not wanting to just give up.

### 5G Canary — 2022-08-09T18:20:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-where-to-start-hi-im-steve.27130/post-314944>

Hi Steve... Sorry to hear you are going through a difficult time. Since you posted... I hope you don’t mind if I share some feelings.

You are **_not_** responsible for your wife or your stepdaughter’s happiness... they are responsible for their own happiness.

When my daughter was little and she liked to threaten to run away. I would shock her by always packing her bag for her. Did she take her bag and leave... sometimes. She would go hide in the yard where she thought I couldn’t see her. She would lay in the sunshine in the grass and after a little while she would come back in feeling better. She realized at a very young age that she was responsible for her own happiness. As Mothers we are only as happy as are saddest child. Why? Because we are wirelessly connected to one another but everyone has to learn happiness/love on their own. Which makes that connection tougher at times.

When we leave this earth there is a great possibility the pain you leave with goes with you. It might not just be a do over. It’s a choice to learn from the lessons life throws at you and die with a smile on your face knowing what “Love” really is. Love isn’t all rainbows and sunshine and it takes hard work in this technological world. In nature unconditional love is much easier to experience because it’s in its purest state.

Sometimes we have to be buried first in order to grow. I like to think “they” tried to turn us into coach potatoes but what they didn’t count on is if you bury a potato in the right light environment... it will not just grow but thrive! In a microwave it will expand before eventually exploding. We definitely don’t want to be that potato. You sound like you are living in a microwave... Zip code matters.

I also was a child born with supposedly low iron. I also lived in California for 30 years. My husband was also told he had diabetes a few years back. Your body will do whatever it needs to do to survive in a bad light environment. We both have improved our health immensely but we did not focus on pills, food or exercise. We completely cleaned up our light environment and we learned to reconnect with nature. Not just laying in the sun but really working within nature. You should have a bright red neck if your connecting right. What you’ve shared shows you definitely need a new zip code and new habits when it comes to artificial light at night. Try turning all the power of to the house after 8pm. Also, if you’re in a bad place I would definitely ditch the mind controlling tv, books and news for a while(Hear no evil, see no evil, and speak no evil). You need to detox your environment before you worry about your weight or what your eating.

On earth it’s about mind, body, spirit. In the end it’s not about mind and body but just spirit. You seem like you have a beautiful spirit. Mother Nature is calling... will you answer? Do me a favor and lay in the grass while you listen...

### TaylorF — 2022-08-09T19:59:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-where-to-start-hi-im-steve.27130/post-314945>

Hi Steve,

I am going to speak strictly about weight and cravings.

I have struggled with poor eating or what I consider bad eating habits and diet mentality plenty over the last 4-5 years as I gained 90 lbs. The 2 things that have helped me this year since I took a turn are my light, water, magnetism AND:
1) Having decaf (water processed) coffee with saturated fat (1-2 tbs of grass fed butter and 1-2 tbsp of caprylic acid (c8 or brain octane) oil) blended in the mornings during fasting (what people call “bulletproof” but I think is fairly common sense as saturated fat heals our gut, our hormones, cravings, and more). This has really removed my cravings but it also allows you to slightly eat outside of a low carb paleo style while still maintaining ketones (in a 5G blue lit world).
2) Read the book “Intuitive Eating” and remove yourself from the diet mentality completely. It will heal your soul and allow you to use food as nourishment.

I am chronically ill and working on healing, too. We all need to stick together.

### Marko Pollo — 2022-08-09T22:54:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-where-to-start-hi-im-steve.27130/post-314948>

congrats on reaching your breaking/healing point of inflection. If you were getting by with your lifestyle you would be destined for mediocrity eternally. Lucky for you have the leptin reset ready to go, you are not hoping veganism, crossfit, supplements and diet are gonna cure you, and you are here in an awesome community to help you. Keep your chin up and keep going. You have a lot of life and happiness left if you don't piss it away. Think about the things you still want to do in life and start taking steps in that direction. If not you, who? If not now, when? good luck!

### EWO — 2022-08-10T06:50:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-where-to-start-hi-im-steve.27130/post-314949>

> [Steven LaBella said:](/goto/post?id=314936)
>
> real advice I can get for not wanting to just give up

IMO you have to know what your purpose is; if you are clear that you have one, then you can cling to that and it can help you pull yourself up. If you don't know what it is yet, maybe your temporary purpose becomes to find one. Wake up everyday and ask, What is my purpose? Why am I here? Keep asking till you get an answer. Until you've got your answer your purpose is asking and listening. Once you've got your answer, then live that purpose.

Also, what Marco said above is astute. You are at an inflection point. There's a saying about insight that just before you get it you get to a place of feeling like giving up. Total stuckness. Exhaustion even. And that's when the breakthrough or insight happens. So, instead of giving in to the temptation to give up, recognize that you are exactly where a stuck person is, just before getting unstuck. Sit with the discomfort and the frustration long enough to let the insight emerge.

### EWO — 2022-08-10T19:21:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-where-to-start-hi-im-steve.27130/post-314963>

Every body has to find what works for them.

Sometimes if you have never fasted, or tried whatever method it can be helpful, just to know that hunger itself is a transitory state. We often eat out of habit, and get "hungry" when we have trained ourselves to expect to eat.

In my experience, different foods also induce particular experiences...hunger, anger, depression, to name a few. If you never try letting go of some things, and replacing them with others, you won't get that many sensations and emotions have nothing to do with you personally, but they are coming from what you are allowing into your body.

This can be as true for what you allow in, in terms of light, emfs, information etc...as food. Taking a break from whatever you allow in habitually, gives you an opportunity to experience what it is like to not have those inputs.

Many paths can lead one to know what is like to be free of physical hunger. In my experience, fasting is one, HFLC keto is another, and finding foods that agree with you and help you feel full and satiated is another.

Just like there are many paths to blunt hunger, there are also many paths that can result in weighing less.

At the same time, deviating too far from what you are going to be able to reasonably sustain over time will result in temporary changes.

Gaining and losing, gaining and losing, gaining and losing is a real PITA. Having done both multiple times (since the age of 12!), I would rather find a middle path of gradual changes and tweaks that eventually helps me arrive at my goal.

The middle path is a slower one and difficult to see the small (often invisible) changes from day to day. It's also not as exciting (!) as picking extreme measures (that deviate far from what I currently practice or will likely be able to maintain over time) and dropping lots of weight quickly.

Let's admit it. If we struggle with weight, it's FUN to make some significant changes and see the numbers drop quickly. But the true test is what happens over time. How many fat people keep weight off over time? Not that many.

I'm 50 pounds lighter than my highest weight 13 years ago and have to remind myself of this success as I work to gradually reduce another 15-20 pounds. Stepping on the scale every day, putting the number down on graph paper, keeps me accountable. It also allows me to see patterns over time, in black and white.

Following a middle path, one must be able to zoom out, and look at the larger picture...over weeks, months, and years. With the above example, smoothing the day to day number into a 30 day moving average helps identify the trajectory over time.

6 pounds reduced each year over 5 years is 30 pounds. 12 pounds reduced each year over 5 years is 60 pounds. Most of us who have stuggled with weight over decades would be grateful to embrace a future in 5 years of carrying 30-60 fewer pounds, or a future in 10 years of carrying 60-120 fewer pounds, especially if we knew we could reasonably maintain that lower weight within a tight margin. But the (perceived) quick fix is so much more alluring!

What ends up being sustainable for one is not for another, and vice versa. And ususally we only know by giving it a go, and finding out for ourselves.

It is often only in looking back, we realize that something didn't work for us and have a chance to self correct going forward.

### caroline — 2022-08-10T22:41:59+0300

### EWO — 2022-08-23T04:12:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-where-to-start-hi-im-steve.27130/post-315206>

One of the most important qualaities to have is open-mindedness. It comes from realizing there is a lot we don't know, and don't know we don't know. Then from that place a willingness to open up to new ideas, and trying out new things. I've been to this rodeo many times (up and down weight), and posting in this thread, got my gears turning. Dropped 9 pounds in a few weeks. When we have been to this rodeo, we know it's not fat. Inflammation, maybe. But the bottom line is if you tune to your body, and what it needs, and give it that, you can see changes unfold. A little of this and a little of that, and voila, it happens. Stay open, stay creative, stay the course.

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2023-03-20T22:44:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-uk-fellow-mitochondriacs.27526/post-320107>

Hi My name is Rohen and Im here to learn as much as I can.
I was pointed to Nourish Vermont 2016 by Clare who I think must be here.
I was fascinated by 40 minutes in. Carlos Delgado. It explained why so many just blindly obeyed the tv.
So after that I watched Nourish Vermont 2017.
The Deuterium podcast is hard going though.
But Ive found everything resonated with my biochemistry background and my prior dermatological knowledge because often melanomas are found on big toenails and in retinas.
I researched Vitamin D3 in 2009 and realised that the statin drugs were depriving people of cholesterol lowering their immunity and wrecking their memory. I had my father as a prime example.
Id been a member of Weston A Price from 2009 onwards in the UK and I recognised the disconnect with food.

I'm a retired psychiatrist and I once worked in a unit that was slap bang near an electricity pylon in the UK. I said that the placement of the psychiatric unit was useless next to that thing. And that was back in 2000 I had studied feng shui and chi. Plus I had had a friend whose daughter had been badly affected by power lines. I always felt awful in that building.

I already knew that UHF transmitters would cause four leaved clovers to grow. I noticed this in 1991 in a hill station in india, around the base of the transmitter there were tens of four leaved clovers.

Then I lived in Dundee for university and a patch of clover was in a direct line to the tv transmitter. where I lived for a few years. I still have about 4-15 dried four leaf clovers pressed in my university medical books. I used to go out and pick them.

I was aware that it was causing a mutation in the plant life. Purely through observation.

Im currently working my way through the patreon blogs and filling in my gaps and also have read the Epi Paleo RX the other day.

Looking forward to getting more knowledge and fixing myself so that I can get a bit younger. I want my grey hair gone. Dad didnt go grey until he was 70. But then he spent lots of time in east africa.

Due to chronic fatigue Im already taking most of what Dr Kruse recommends. I did research on how to support my mitochondria.

I also have a rare genetic disease due to a deficiency of the electron carrier reductase enzyme tetrahydrobiopterin.

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2023-03-23T12:46:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-uk-fellow-mitochondriacs.27526/post-320174>

Thankfully I'm being assessed at Sheffield for this to make sure of which of the six Tetrahydrobiopterin deficiencies I have. But a diet of fruit and certain vegetables and animal fat and enough protein from certain sources would fix it mostly but in order for that to fit I'd really have to go back to tropical shores.
Living in the UK doesn't really have much in the way of 5G respite. Or UV or fruit that's in season all the year round.

I've always loved shellfish especially lobsters and I remember that in Australia I once had 19 oysters for lunch and then skipped around Melbourne quite happily but it didn't really fully do the job and I don't own an oyster farm ( yet ) so that would be as expensive as the Sapropterin. I brighten up best in St Lucia in a resort with local organic food and a massage every day but that place is somewhat expensive but I do make the pilgrimage every couple of years . I noticed once that I could do ten times the amount of exercise there. Plus you're not indoors all the time and I would go out to the beach every morning and walk barefoot at the water's edge . I was grounding and taking in the early morning light by instinct. I started doing that in June 2015
And at the end of the three weeks I was able to sprint across the beach for the first time in 6-7 years without getting tired . I haven't been able to repeat the sprint since though and I've tried it on two occasions since. I haven't been for four years thanks to government interference in our lives, 5G Starlink and restrictions. But by last October I figured well why am I saving so much if I can't use it to feel better. I'm going for three weeks in May June . I'm planning winter sun elsewhere.

I'm confident Sheffield will come up with a dietary plan , and I know I sleep better with the Sapropterin. I tried it out plus I got a source of raw human breast milk and I have 6 Oz of colostrum to take when I need it. I missed out of being breastfed as a kid .

They're thinking I might have Segawa syndrome. Which of course is very rare

I've kept searching and finally I got some answers. I did a full genome when I could finally afford one. Including the Mitochondrial DNA and I already had the NHS do a mitochondrial disease sweep and find nothing. Helps to have a professor of Neurology and Gluten ataxia to persuade

Right I've got three hours of February Powwow to listen to. See you on Sunday Jack and everyone else.

I'll look for answers on the forum too

Time to breakfast like a king. Just been out with feet on the ground and looking at the morning light.

Little steps. It's the front garden so it's not really private. But I know it has no cables or things underneath. I watched the house being built. For a year.

[EMR-TeK Inferno Red and Near infrared 770/1400 watt Infrared Device – EMR-TEK](https://emr-tek.com/collections/red-light-therapy/products/inferno)
Many hours exposure/day. Put it next to your desk at home, if that is where you spend most time.

[Amazon.com: Sperti Fiji Sun Home Tanning Lamp - Face and Body Sunlamp : Beauty & Personal Care](https://www.amazon.com/Sperti-Fiji-Home-Tanning-Lamp/dp/B001ID8P78/ref=sr_1_2?crid=F1DL1X8WIJDY&keywords=sperti&qid=1679570129&sprefix=sperti%2Caps%2C111&sr=8-2&ufe=app_do%3Aamzn1.fos.5137e923-c7be-4142-979c-7c68b6c26f63)

one full timer, 8 minutes front, 7 minutes back

Lobster is ok, but maybe overrated.
2-4 cans/week
(Omega-3, vitamin A, Vitamin D (the most natural you can get as food)
In my place if I buy them in the store, then they are at the end of their expiration day. That is currently expiration around dec 2023. And the price is high.
When I buy at amazon, expiration 21/7/2027 (produced 21/7/2022), and cost per can little less than in the store.
So in the store I would buy them when they are about 4 years old or real close to 5.

### Sue-UK — 2023-04-16T18:06:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-uk-fellow-mitochondriacs.27526/post-320571>

> [Dr Rohen Kapur said:](/goto/post?id=320174)
>
> I haven't been for four years thanks to government interference in our lives, 5G Starlink and restrictions. But by last October I figured well why am I saving so much if I can't use it to feel better. I'm going for three weeks in May June . I'm planning winter sun elsewhere.

The restrictions we had were a disaster, keeping people indoors when we already have a latitude disadvantage for UV. I really missed my usual 2 winter breaks in the Canaries, This winter its been the fuel crisis - on the 60+ estate I moved to in April 2021, I have rarely seen even a window open, whereas we haven't been putting the heating on and having windows and doors open as much as possible, a source of photons as well as fresh air. I started sunbathing in tan thru beachwear on good days in March. Photon scavenging.  Our neighbours think we are nuts, but the day they think we're not is probably when we should start to worry.

Because of the restrictions and oversea travel limitations we moved to an area with high sunshine, and we are less than a minute's walk from the sea.

The combination of change of location and other "low hanging fruit" hacks have meant I haven't felt desperate for an overseas break this winter, but it looks like I'll be going to Egypt for two weeks in December this year so it will be interesting to see how that addition changes my n=1......

### anac1991 — 2023-05-03T15:24:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-aus.27608/post-320799>

Hi everyone,

Jack’s recent podcast with Huberman was mind blowing. I’m a naturopath and realized within first year what I was learning was mostly rubbish, and was proven right as being bed ridden with CFS in second year had little to do with a supplement stack for recovery.

I got into Pall and Pollack’s work some years ago, and now just starting to get more acquainted with Jack's insights recently. I’ve come to realise in the past several months that understanding physics is pivotal to understanding health/reality. Very much looking forward to learning and applying more of these principles in life & in practice.

Cheers,
Ana

# Thread: Hi there! Newbie who just discovered Jack.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-there-newbie-who-just-discovered-jack.27624/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Thlas1971! — 2023-05-05T02:32:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-there-newbie-who-just-discovered-jack.27624/post-320891>

Hi all! Happy to be here. I found Jack in a round-about way this week. An Instragram friend liked something he posted and I thought, she’s pretty cool so I need to check him out. Then spent several hours listening to the discussion with Andrew Huberman and Rick Rubin. I started appreciating the sun in a new way about 2 years back. Need to quit my corporate job to see more of it!! I’m here to learn more in hopes of becoming mitochondriac. I’m 51, at a healthy weight (5’4” and 115lbs) but would like to optimize my sleep and health. Health issues I’m managing are Hashimotos, which I’ve been stepping down meds slowly and antibodies are now normal. I’ve had vitiligo since 5 but a fair bit of freckling in the past few years so that is about 50% repigmented. I also have a diagnosis of corneal ectasia which in my case is a severe side effect of lasik which requires scleral contact lenses to see clearly. However, I’ve been avoiding wearing them and doing my best to adapt to not seeing well and being outside in hopes of improving vision. I’ve also had anterior uveitis in the past and had shingles at 36 so my immune system has some struggles. My hubs and I are building a 38-acre homestead and recently added pigs, chickens, and geese and are planning a large garden this year - mostly to feed the animals so they can feed me!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Mellanie is tired of being tired!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mellanie-is-tired-of-being-tired.27632/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mellanie Parrish — 2023-05-06T18:42:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mellanie-is-tired-of-being-tired.27632/post-321001>

Hi! I've been reading and reading to figure out what I want to say for my first post, and there's so much that I just decided to put figurative pen to paper and just start.

I'm a 43yo female (44 at the end of August) who has been battling with chronic fatigue that for the last few months? Years? It seems like forever. My height is about 5'5" and I weigh about 181 lbs right now. I'm considered obese but I still have an hourglass figure...for the most part. Some days I have more energy than others. (today seems to be a low day.) I suspect I have adrenal fatigue--moderate--and leptin resistance, hormonal imbalance (low). I started my journey back to full health by using the Leptin Rx reset. I began on 4/15/2023 and have been eating Epi-Paleo for 3 weeks now. I saw an uptick in my energy levels after the first week til now, but this week seems like I've been taking a step backwards, as far as energy level goes. Sleep improved drastically and then got less restful this week. Life has been a more hectic this week (extra rehearsals and performances), which could definitely account for the drain on my body.

My BAB usually consists of more than 75g of protein. I typically need a 10 oz steak or larger, or a pound of ground beef/bison to feel full in the mornings. I add veggies (non-starchy) to help balance all the meat. (is that normal? Seems like so much meat! Why would I need so much protein?) I consume about 50-100g of starchy veggies per day, usually green plantains or sweet potato.

I use blue-light blocking glasses at night after the sun goes down and until the sun comes up. I try to see the sunrise and sunset every day, but sometimes I can't to it. I do feel sleepy around sunset and try to get to sleep within an hour of the sun going down.

As far as supplements go, I take:
Trizomal glutathione 5ml
Prenatal multivitamin (I'm still breastfeeding)
Vitamin D3 (10k IU) +K2
Buffered Vitamin C 1000mg
Omega-3s
Zinc picolinate 50mg
Tri-Magnesium 300mg
Electrolytes (Ultima brand, grape)
Seaweed (nori sheets) for iodine
Flax seeds for added Omega-3s (1-2 tablespoons)
Licorice root (tea made from root pieces)

Now, that I have some background info on me... Here's where I have questions. I know the Rx page said it could take 4-6 weeks to see the changes for leptin sensitivity, but I started seeing the signs after the first week. With my increase in energy levels, I've included more activities in my day. I havent officially started any exercise or weight training, more than gentle yoga poses to stretch my muscles and warm up my body for the day. I intend to continue what I'm doing, but I'm concerned about how much meat/protein I'm consuming daily. I'm starting to feel tired again on this current Epi-Paleo diet (no grains, refined or added sugars, legumes, dairy, alcohol, nightshades, or nuts). Oh, I also have a lot of food sensitivities, and this way of eating follows the Autoimmune Protocol (AIP) so I feel like I should be getting better, or rather seeing more consistent results.

# Thread: Hello from London, Kyiv and Warsaw
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-london-kyiv-and-warsaw.27637/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kurilo — 2023-05-07T22:52:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-london-kyiv-and-warsaw.27637/post-321059>

**Intro**
Hello! Nick Rubin podcast changed my perception of reality even though I loved the sun and light all my life and it fixed my obesity a few years ago. I bing watched 20+ hours of Jack's content.

I live between London, Kyiv and Warsaw (±50 lat) with the skin type 2 and grounding and setting as much sun as I can. I was born in Belarus and my genes are Eastern/Northern European.

**Challenge**
The main health challenge I'm trying to figure out is that my eyes are not aligned correctly, esp. when I gaze to the right - 4th nerve palsy diagnosed. Doctors in London are pushing for the surgery but i'm trying to exhaust as many alternative options as possible and joined this community to see if I can conquer my final health issue frontier.

Excited to start the protocol to push my health and well-being of my family even further.

**Fun fact about me**
I built a mobile iOS app for biomarker tracking (and ML models which analyse them later) which is loved and used by over 20K people, not sure if I plug it in here though so I skip the link/name for now (also I know Jack hates technology).

Why? I got into bloodwork a few years ago when I realised my PRL (prolactin) is through the roof and was even taking synthetic dopamine (cabergoline) to adjust it a bit (should NOT have done it). Considering all my health issues I got a huge dataset of bloodwork (I've done over 40+ tests!) on my hands for which I raised funds and built the application - first for myself, then for others. I'm now thinking on how to best incorporate the \_light therapy\_ into the app too as I invested so much money and time into the company/app that it could be a next platform for educating people about the light too.

# Thread: J LeBlanc N=1
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/j-leblanc-n-1.27650/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-05-09T22:49:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/j-leblanc-n-1.27650/post-321152>

Hi everyone! I've never been great with introductions, but here goes my attempt:

I have been a member for only a week or two, although I have been around these forums as a guest for a couple months now.

I learned about Dr. Kruse from Phil on the We All Know Nothing (WAKN) podcast. He is ultimately the reason I am here.

I have lived my entire life in or very close to Baton Rouge, Louisiana. As a kid, I was always playing outside, but I would spend probably an equal amount of time indoors playing video games. As I grew older, the gaming time only increased and the outdoor time decreased.

I never really cared much about health until my mid twenties, when I collapsed on the ground during leg day at a friend's home gym. My blood sugar had plummeted and I slowly lost motor function during the workout. All I could do was lay helplessly on the floor as my vision starting fading to back like a shade being slowly pulled over my eyes. Thankfully, I had a workout partner that day that was able to get me help. I had orange juice brought to me and very soon after drinking it, I was able to get up.

Up to that point, I have never really had a regular doctor. I've never really trusted the medical industry, even from a young age. And still to this day, I am doctorless and do not trust the current medical paradigm. Also, up to that point, I never really had an issue with blood sugar like that. At this time, though, I regularly worked LSU football games to raise money for the youth group of my church. During these games, I would consume massive amounts of coke, which I suspected played a role in my blood sugar getting out of whack. Part of the gig of working LSU games was unhindered access to unlimited fountain drinks, unfortunately. I will note that I have never really had a weight issue, but from the things I understand now, I believe I was what they refer to as "skinny fat" during this period. Note that my weight has always stayed between 115-120 lbs ever since I was in high school.

The day I collapsed changed everything though. From that point, I have been hyper-focused on regaining and staying healthy. I began scouring the internet, trying to find answers. Knowing what I know now, a _lot of the info I initially found and followed was half-truth_ (some info zero truth). At the time, I knew blood sugar problems related to carb consumption, so I became anti-carb. Also, during That was the thing for me for about a year. Then, I stumbled upon the carnivore diet and the whole notion that veggies contain plant toxins. Did that for about another year. Towards the end of my carnivore kick, I started having really bad sleep issues. Looked deeper and learned about the glycine/methionine ratio and how too much methionine can hinder sleep. Tried to remedy this with more bone broth and cartilage consumption. Didn't work. Sleep issues persisted. I started working out more to see if I could just exhaust myself more to make the sleep better. Still didn't work. Then, I read somewhere that carb consumption before bed can help sleep. I backed off on my restrictive diet and started regularly eating carbs again. I tried to focus on a general whole food diet. This seemed to work initially, but after about a month or so, the sleep issues returned. **_Note that during the course of events in this paragraph, I was living a densely populated apartment complex life and was heavily addicted to video games, spending lots of time indoors._**

It was at this point that I began giving up on trying to sleep better. I figured I was just destined for a life of poor sleep. Then, I stumbled upon Andrew Huberman. I watched the episode that he did with Dr. Samer Hattar. I learned of the importance of circadian rhythm and how the viewing of morning sunlight entrains this rhythm. I began putting that into practice. This helped my sleep tremendously. I learned to love getting sunlight. Before this point, I held the belief that you should only get 30 min of sun per day and then get out of it (bc I thought UV was dangerous :/). I also gained the general knowledge that blue light at night was bad. There were clearly pieces to this puzzle that I was still missing though, bc while there was a general improvement in sleep, I still had mornings here and there where I woke up groggy and was fatigued the whole day. At this point I understood the importance of light, but still believed that diet and exercise were still more important. **_Note that shortly after this point in the timeline, I moved to a house in the suburbs._** This further improved my sleep, which now finally makes sense after studying Jack's teachings.

About another year or so, I was hooked on Huberman's work, following his teachings to the T. Then, one day (about 2-3 months ago) I discovered the WAKN podcast. I decided to watch an episode. This was the first time I had heard of this idea of light being the most important factor in health. I'll be honest, initially I thought Phil was a quack. I didn't buy in to what he was saying, but I was strangely interested, bc everything he was saying was so counter to what I believed. I thought Phil had an unhealthy obsession with blocking blue light. I continued following Phil down this wacky rabbit hole to his last podcast episode where he mentioned Jack Kruse. He said this was the guy to follow if you really want to connect all these dots. So I searched up Jack Kruse and began watching podcasts he was featured in. My first podcast was his first appearance on the Life Stylist with Luke Storey. I've been hooked on Jack's teachings ever since. I'm am now fully bought in to this mindset of Light, Water, and Magnetism being of utmost importance to health.

And YES, my sleep has been the best it has ever been in years and I feel incredible. These practices actually DO work. Unlike the half-truths I followed before. I feel like I am in a very healthy state currently. I have a fairly dark tan, which I began acquiring after learning the importance of sunlight from Huberman. After discovering Jack though, I now understand the detriment from indoor lighting at ALL TIMES OF THE DAY (not just at night). I have recently turned from my gaming addiction. I still do play, but not nearly as much as I used to. And when I do play, it generally happens between 10AM - 2PM with blue blockers on and with windows open. I also don't play any longer than about an hour. Making this change to my gaming habits and the reduction of indoor time in general has further improved my sleep.

As far as my career, I am a chemistry teacher at a densely populated school, which I know is not ideal (wifi, 5G, and blue light blasting me from every direction). I do take as many sun breaks as possible throughout the day and leave my classroom window open all day. My classroom window faces East btw

Anyways, I am a Kruse follower for life and happy to be part of this community! I know I still have many things left to learn on my journey of being a mitochondriac, but I am eager to learn and have thoroughly enjoyed this journey thus far!

Thank you Dr. Kruse for your many years of persistence in bringing these life changing teachings to the world! Also, thank you to Phil for starting your WAKN podcast and posting those videos to Youtube. I wouldn't be here without you!

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-05-11T18:42:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/j-leblanc-n-1.27650/post-321215>

> [ND Hauf said:](/goto/post?id=321201)
>
> Hey J…..check out BenQ gaming monitors if you haven’t already. All my programmers are now on these. The eye protection zero flicker model with Iris app. installed for the win.

Thanks for the recommendation! Unfortunately, I haven't joined the PC master race yet. I game on console on a TV :/

> [ND Hauf said:](/goto/post?id=321201)
>
> We need good teachers fighting the good fight. You’re shaping developing minds and the seeds you plant today will grow with time.

True. I am trying to brainstorm ways to bring Jack's teachings to the students at my school. I guarantee they are not hearing this stuff in the classes they take. Health class is pretty much all about diet/exercise. In biology, the most they get is that mitochondria are the powerhouses of cells. They also learn the basics of photosynthesis. In my class (chemistry), they learn the concept that electrons emit light when they fall to the ground state from an excited state and the barebones basics of quantum numbers. That's it though. We don't even have redox reactions in our curriculum, which I believe is a huge disservice to these students. We used to teach a unit on redox, but we have recently switched to what is referred to as a block schedule, which means I have students for one semester (about 4 months) and then they are gone. No longer a full year with students. And after covering topics required by the state of LA (which doesn't include redox), there is no time to fit redox in.

# Thread: Hi from 5g hell
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-5g-hell.27711/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jeremiasbtome — 2023-05-22T02:09:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-5g-hell.27711/post-321568>

My name is Jeremias. I am 19 years old born in Portugal. My mother is german and my dad Portuguese. I have a sibling who is born in Portugal too.
We moved to Dublin when I was 6 years old for a better life which in the past months I've come to realise was the worst unknown mistake.

My mother's mitochondria is pretty bad in my opinion as she has thyroid issues and migraines. Migraines I got from her but I haven't had one since 2022 when I started seeing sunrise and being outside for 2 hours a day.

I searched all over the Internet in 2021 looking for help for my eyes as they were getting worse (blurry vision, fuzzy, shit vision basically and damaged) and got migraines from every few months to every month. Working evenings at a restaurant with fluorescen lights made it worse.

A mitochondriac reached out to me after weeks of searching and told me it was cuz of the screens and not enoguh sun. He gave me a protocol to follow consisting of seeing sunrise, being outside for 2 hours and eating seafood. I ignored the seafood part and still within weeks my vision got better.

That was Janaury 2022. I still had no idea then about 5g, blue light and the entire world being a scam. Never heard of Jack Kruse.

After a few months I stopped seeing sunrise but still got 2 hours sun everyday and never got migraines. I also mid 2022 began eating once a day a vegan diet because I reaearched a lot of David Sinclair. Who I found out now was wrong.

At the time my passion was youtube, becoming a youtuber had been my dream for years. And in mid 2022 i finally quit my restaurant job and got a job with a youtuber. Working was now at home infront of a laptop for over 10 hours a day. I was still getting sun exposure for 2 hours so i had no issues from the surface.

In late 2022 something really bad happened in my family which will forever change my life. Not talking about that but just to give context.

My sibling this entire time has been very sick with mental health diagnoses and autism. Before 2023 I always believed the medical world would help her. I was so wrong.

In early 2023 i asked that mitochondriac that helped me how he knew these stuff as I was always still asking him stuff. He told me about Jack kruse and my entire perspective on life has changed. I have probably listened to an hour of jack kruse everyday now for months, my motivation started as my sister's health is very bad. I knew after a few podcasts that the medical paradigm was a scam and that my sister probably would not recover if it was in their hands, I had to help her understand the truth about the world and why following convential medicine wouldn't work. His knowledge and information blew my mind and opened my brain. I began going down multiple rabbit holes which kept leading into other ones.

I never knew blue light was so bad and why my sister got autism. All these stuff would help me as I wanted to make my sibling's life healthier.

I kept learning everyday. The world I knew was gone and I couldn't take off this new lens.

Everything I ever knew was built for profit margins. I hate this society.

Eventually I learned about Victoria Dunckley, a psychiatrist who had very famous work on screens and its affect on child's brains. I listened to some podcasts and then I found out that some of her patients were able to reverse conditions one of which Autism. This then became my new goal. Could I reverse my sibling's autism? And the other diagnoses my sibling had I hope can too be Reversed.

From the stuff jack kruse has talked about I think it is possible as my sibling is still a teenager and brain not myelinated. This is what drives me, i want to help my sibling whose health issues are a direct result of modernisation. I am working now on a new business still via laptop and we are moving out of a Dublin and going to Portugal. 5G is still in Portugal but in dublin where we live it is way worse. I started learning more about 5g since April and it is so scary. I continue learning everyday and I continue questioning everything.

My goal is to get to a place where we can live in nature and almost no indoor. I know we can get there, just need money.

It was sad at first learning about the truth about the world. But I know now that despite that we can still achieve a better life. Thank you Jack Kruse for the free knowledge it has changed my life. I hope to meet you one day and I will continue being a student.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Hi Tribe - My name is Richard a UK based Orthodontist. I've been a member since 2018 and have successfully instigated Jacks knowledge and protocols to control my own Auto-inflammatory condition. Some of you may have heard the April 2023 Q&A where I introduced a toothbrush I've been working on which utilises red (635nm) and UVA (410nm) light to improve oral health:

2 speed setting for vibration and a manual setting
Red light 635nm for anti-inflammatory effect
UV 410nm for photolysis (lightening effect) and bactericidal (snaps the tails of the P.Gingivalis bacteria associated with periodontal disease)
Dual light mode (both frequencies)
Removable bristles for use as light wand (soft tissue ulceration/ wound healing)
Bristle is also the only replaceable part
USB-c charging
Waterproof

### Annemcnulty — 2024-04-27T22:32:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-and-uv-light-toothbrush.27737/post-334030>

Hi Rich - a bit of a weird question but does anyone use this on their dogs? We have a senior dog with bad teeth and I’m trying to avoid putting him under for cleaning. Thanks!

### Gibiani — 2024-04-28T12:07:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-and-uv-light-toothbrush.27737/post-334038>

Hi. Yep I don’t think you’d be the first. Obviously it’s a bit more expensive than the average doggy brush but it works well. There is a manual setting with no vibration which may be best. You can also remove the bristles and use it as a light wand for topical treatment for small cuts etc. it’s worth understanding that any established calculus won’t be removed by the brush. Hope that helps
Rich

To allow your body to produce NO, Nitric Oxide (which makes your cardiovascular system's volume flexible and highly variable), you must have mouth bacteria and sufficient acid in your stomach.
If one has GERD, that is usually due to too low stomach acid, that in turn is due to not enough potassium in your body.
Check (Potassium RBC) if you have GERD.

When you start thinking about how much potassium you really need (It is a lot)

That tells you that humans should eat lots of carbs. (and minimal amounts of animal proteins and fat).
Still a problem, carbs cause diabetes.
Solution is, we are talking about eating low glycemic index carbs, that are digesting slowly.

The category of carbohydrates is rather unfortunate, it should be divided into few sub-cathegories.

Stay away of fructose
and anything that is prepared in such a way that speeds up digestion.
Preferably check your blood's Uric Acid
Worry if more than 4.

clean arteries-----> ($100), Coronary Calcium Score, Agatston Score (either zero or in low single digits)
low blood pressure
body temperature in the midday 36.6 to 37C,

mostly outdoors but indoor with open windows also counts

### Annelieuk — 2024-07-10T15:39:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-and-uv-light-toothbrush.27737/post-336911>

> [Gibiani said:](/goto/post?id=321799)
>
> Hi Tribe - My name is Richard a UK based Orthodontist. I've been a member since 2018 and have successfully instigated Jacks knowledge and protocols to control my own Auto-inflammatory condition. Some of you may have heard the April 2023 Q&A where I introduced a toothbrush I've been working on which utilises red (635nm) and UVA (410nm) light to improve oral health:
> [View attachment 24218](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/24218/)[View attachment 24219](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/24219/)
> We've incorporated the following features:
> Sonic vibration (similar to the Phillips sonicare brush) to achieve the cavitation effect
> 2 speed setting for vibration and a manual setting
> Red light 635nm for anti-inflammatory effect
> UV 410nm for photolysis (lightening effect) and bactericidal (snaps the tails of the P.Gingivalis bacteria associated with periodontal disease)
> Dual light mode (both frequencies)
> Removable bristles for use as light wand (soft tissue ulceration/ wound healing)
> Bristle is also the only replaceable part
> USB-c charging
> Waterproof
>
> Non US customers please use website: [https://bristlscience.co.uk/](https://bristlscience.co.uk/)
> US customers please use [https://bristlscience.com/](https://bristlscience.com/)
>
> Follow us on instagram under @BRISTLUK
> Love feedback and hope you like the product
> Rich

### Heerink — 2024-07-10T18:44:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-and-uv-light-toothbrush.27737/post-336919>

Richard,
You mention that the brush uses UVA (410nm). 410nm is in the VIS spectrum and is not UVA. Which is a good thing for your company since you would have to get it certified by the FDA as a Class 2 medical device if it uses any UV light. I suggest you change that marketing before the FDA shuts you down and you could face criminal charges and fines. Just trying to help a fellow member here since I'm going through the process of getting FDA certification for a UV radiating Class 2 medical device and it is a pain-in-the-ass process

# Thread: Red and UV light toothbrush
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-and-uv-light-toothbrush.27738/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gibiani — 2023-05-28T18:17:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-and-uv-light-toothbrush.27738/post-321800>

Hi Tribe - My name is Richard a UK based Orthodontist. I've been a member since 2018 and have successfully instigated Jacks knowledge and protocols to control my own Auto-inflammatory condition. Some of you may have heard the April 2023 Q&A where I introduced a toothbrush I've been working on which utilises red (635nm) and UVA (410nm) light to improve oral health:
[View attachment 24218](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/24218/)[View attachment 24219](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/24219/)
We've incorporated the following features:
Sonic vibration (similar to the Phillips sonicare brush) to achieve the cavitation effect
2 speed setting for vibration and a manual setting
Red light 635nm for anti-inflammatory effect
UV 410nm for photolysis (lightening effect) and bactericidal (snaps the tails of the P.Gingivalis bacteria associated with periodontal disease)
Dual light mode (both frequencies)
Removable bristles for use as light wand (soft tissue ulceration/ wound healing)
Bristle is also the only replaceable part
USB-c charging
Waterproof

Non US customers please use website: [https://bristlscience.co.uk/](https://bristlscience.co.uk/)
US customers please use [https://bristlscience.com/](https://bristlscience.com/)

Follow us on instagram under @BRISTLUK
Love feedback and hope you like the product
Rich


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Hello from Vancouver Island, BC Canada
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-vancouver-island-bc-canada.27740/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gary Hamer — 2023-05-28T23:35:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-vancouver-island-bc-canada.27740/post-321812>

Hi tribe,

I’ve seen Jack’s work over the last couple years but finally dove into trying to find a greater understanding after listening to the Feb and Mar 2023 Pow wows.

My partner Andrea and I had made plans to begin building a house here on Vancouver island this year and to start a family. After going through a lot of podcasts, listening to Q&A’s and asking questions in the last Pow Wow we decided to put our house plans on hold and travel to Central America this fall and stay until Spring. With the intention to work on our mitochondrial/circadian health.

We are planning to spend some time in Costa Rica, Educador and El Salvador. We would like to make solid plans for December now so we can find accommodations and we would love any recommendations from those of you in El Salvador or who have visited about which sites to book through (AirBNB or is there other popular ones used down there?)

Also are there any other members who will be down there this winter? We would love to meet up and we are open to traveling around to meet fellow tribe members.

Any insights, recommendations or thoughts are welcome.

Thanks
Gary

### Laudy Cincotta — 2023-05-29T01:37:39+0300

We would like to make solid plans for December now so we can find accommodations and we would love any recommendations from those of you in El Salvador or who have visited about which sites to book through (AirBNB or is there other popular ones used down there?)
>
> Also are there any other members who will be down there this winter? We would love to meet up and we are open to traveling around to meet fellow tribe members.
>
> Any insights, recommendations or thoughts are welcome.

Gary great decision!

We are Australians based in Costa del Sol which is the Honduran side of the country and close to the airport, others are in Shalpa or Atami.

Personally speaking we look forward to meet you guys in your travels.

Cheers Laudy

Hi All.
I have been away from Jacks info for now 10 years or so.
Result: no diets has fixed my issues and I was retarded enough to forget the importance of what Jack is teaching.
Last 2 days I have been in the sun again due to finding some new interviews with him.(huberman) Already feel better than I have the last 10 years. duh.

Anyway, this forum is HUGE and need some help if possible.

I apparently need to be schooled by Jack in a continuous basis to not forget how important the Sun is. Its crazy how fast we slip into ignorance again and again.

I appreciate you all in here. This forum is an explosion of educational awesomeness. THANK YOU

Josh

### Awakenotwoke — 2023-06-08T08:27:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-lamps.27761/post-322181>

Do we need a UV lamp if we are very tan and in Florida?

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-06-08T19:14:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-lamps.27761/post-322207>

> [Awakenotwoke said:](/goto/post?id=322181)
>
> Do we need a UV lamp if we are very tan and in Florida?

No. UV from the sun in Florida should be plenty.

A UV lamp for you would only be useful if you were in a situation where you are stuck inside and cannot get sun for whatever reason. In that scenario, it would be no match to the sun, but better than nothing. Artificial light should only ever be thought of as supplemental in suboptimal conditions. Never a main source of benefit.

### JanSz — 2023-11-16T01:55:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-lamps.27761/post-328328>

> [TANNER said:](/goto/post?id=328327)
>
> How do you like this lamp? Do you just put your face towards it? or your front side? belly especially?
> I want to buy these for my kids for Christmas.
> we live in Mich. I hear some Quantum providers say to just build up the IV R and melatonin instead, but.. I would think we still need the D. right?
> we build up our Vit D in the Summer, but it may not be enough.

I like it very much.
I keep the middle of the lamp at the level of my navel.
The full timer is almost 16 minutes.
After a while I am able to stand in front of the lamp, sometimes touching lamp, for 8 minutes,
the other 8 minutes are on my back.

Eventually, having enough of the time, one can do this twice daily.

Start carefully, every day, first, think of the results from the day before then figure out if you want to extend exposure time.
I look at the lamp with naked eyes.

//////////
