### Amethyst — 2023-11-23T05:47:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-lamps.27761/post-328561>

EMRtec lights no longer seems to show up, gives an error message on two search engines. Any other suggestions. I'm good in the summer, but winter could use more light support. Also recommendations on good IR/R lamps would be welcome. Newbie question, can I read in bed with them?

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-11-28T20:03:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-lamps.27761/post-328741>

If you need a light at night specifically for visibility to read, I would go with fire over any artificial lamp.

Look into olive oil candles/lanterns. They are pretty easy to make.

Ideally you will want as little fire as possible, while still being able to see to read.

Just know that **even this can negatively impact circadian function,** but it is the best possible option though if you insist on reading after sunset.

# Thread: Hello, I´m a full-time traveller / health coach
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-i%C2%B4m-a-full-time-traveller-health-coach.27763/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2

### Anni — 2023-06-02T03:37:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-i%C2%B4m-a-full-time-traveller-health-coach.27763/post-321979>

Hi everyone. I´m here to introduce myself to you all I Finally managed to get access to this forum and have already spent nearly 2h reading everything. So interesting. Anyway, I´m a health coach originally from Finland but escaped the climate to Australia where I spent nearly 7 years. Now we are currently in the US but looking into Nicaragua to set up a base with my husband and 2 daughters. What led me here is my constant hunger to learn and educate myself on all the matters of health and human biology. I work as a nutrition-based health coach and I am, what Jack would call a "food guru", but the more I´ve discovered and seen my clients struggle with their weight and hormonal issues over and over again, no matter how "good" their diet is, the more I see the problem lays within their environment and light. Especially as most of my clients live in Scandinavia with a notoriously well-known low-UV light environment and massive amounts of ALAN. So I´m here to learn and connect with like-minded humans.

### ND Hauf — 2023-07-06T15:44:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-i%C2%B4m-a-full-time-traveller-health-coach.27763/post-323318>

Hey Anni…….if I can say anything about the core for my journey it’s…get the light right in order to optimize your internal refinery…after that ..the remainder becomes clear.

I’ve become a migratory mammal…..the sun is my guiding light.

# Thread: Spinal Surgery Hardware
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spinal-surgery-hardware.27791/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mike117 — 2023-06-08T12:07:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spinal-surgery-hardware.27791/post-322184>

Hey Jack,

One of my friend had a spinal surgery for multiple traumatic fractures from T9-L1 about two years ago.

She is wondering if she should get the screws and rods removed. They protrude through her skin and cause her pain.

I know this is hugely impacted by multiple factors but I wanted your opinion if possible.

She is worried about EMF and the impact of a foreign material in her body. She’s 38 and fit and is willing to do what’s necessary after the removal.

The main negative point about the surgery is that her surgeon said there’s a big chance she would start bending where the screws were and might need to get them reinstalled later.

And if she gets them removed, any suggestion to speed up the healing after in addition to sun, grounding and CT?

Thanks a lot!

Mike

# Thread: Hello from Tampa, FL!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-tampa-fl.27795/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ghopper@srbp.com — 2023-06-09T20:41:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-tampa-fl.27795/post-322249>

My name is Gary Hopper. I have followed Dr. Jack from a distance for many years (since about 2012) but didn't decide to take the red pill until recently. I signed up for a Gold membership last month and have been listening to the Q & A's, and my mind is blown! I have been missing out all these years and didn't know it. I started my health journey in 2011. I was 41 yrs old, 5'11", weighed 255 lbs, and had so much brain fog I could barely think straight most of the time. Dave Asprey and Mark Sisson initially guided me, and then I found Jack through Asprey when he started talking about CT. I liked Jack, but my brain wasn't ready for that level yet, and I stuck with Asprey and the food gurus for years. Then my wife's fatigue worsened, and food wasn't fixing it, and I couldn't understand why. Food generally worked well for me because I didn't have a condition like hers. So I've been trying to help my wife with her condition for the past four years. This caused me to dig deeper, and I ended up back with Jack and boy, am I glad I did! Since following Jack and adding some of his protocols to my daily life in the past year, my health has improved dramatically, and my wife is on the mend slowly but surely.
I am looking forward to going deeper and learning more every day! I am also looking forward to making friends with some of you in the tribe. Hit me up if you are local!
Gary

### caroline — 2023-06-09T23:41:02+0300

### Mar — 2023-06-15T22:14:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-murcia-spain.27822/post-322442>

Hi everyone, hello Dr. Kruse,

My name is María, I am 52, and live in the southeast of Spain (37 latitude).
I am EHS. In 12 years, this is the first time that I have hope of getting better.

The technical mindset and the medical approach to EHS have consisted of avoiding and shielding nnEMFs in the range of RF, low electric, and magnetic fields, together with supplementation and ecological food. But nothing about light.

Of course, when at home, I feel much better than when I had no shielding at all. But it is not enough to ameliorate and being able to have a regular life. I cannot be around smartphones or anything wireless. I go to the market round the corner for like 15’ 20’ max. That’s it. Fortunately, I live near a decently natural area and can safely go for walks. Also, the beach is not far away -for the winter.

I already have the blue blockers and your book is in the way.
I am all in.

Jack, thank you so much, and best to all!

# Thread: Hello from Aus
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-aus.27851/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### miss_maryanne — 2023-06-24T03:41:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-aus.27851/post-322751>

I’m Maryanne, a 39 yo from Sydney. I’ve just discovered Jack this past April (blown away!) and while still in the midst of reading voraciously, I have been watching the daily sunrise and removed my sunglasses and sunscreen. This is huge for me as I had only ever seen a single sunrise before this and had wearing spf and sunnies for decades!

My question is this: I have developed moderate reactions when exposed to either extreme heat (pink skin very quickly, even in lieu of sun) or cold (worse - rash, welts, dizzy, nauseous). This makes me nervous starting CT but I am eager to do it. Any precautions I should take? I’d also like to know why this is happening and how long before I can get rid of it. I have myopia but my eyes have felt incredible since I started, otherwise no health conditions that I know of

So excited to keep learning

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-06-24T18:20:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-aus.27851/post-322775>

Blood comes to the surface outdoors for 2 reasons I can think of:
\- to get the UVA light from the sun (UVA doesn't penetrate skin very deeply, so blood has to come up to get it)
\- to cool down (your body can cool down more efficiently with blood coming to the surface releasing heat)

This blood surfacing (and making your skin pinker) is a normal natural response. Nothing to fear.

For the explanations with cold, pretty sure it is nothing to worry about also. Just make sure your skin temp doesn't drop too low (I forget the exact temp to stay above)

I am still new to CT stuff (currently reading through CT blogs at the moment for the first time)

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-06-24T18:21:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-aus.27851/post-322776>

I think all that^^^ is correct. Hopefully someone will correct me if not

### caroline — 2023-06-24T18:55:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-aus.27851/post-322779>

Hi and welcome.

there is a C/T protocol - please read

start with face dunks

### miss_maryanne — 2023-06-25T12:29:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-aus.27851/post-322798>

Thank you both, I’ll get on it!

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fredrick347 — 2023-07-03T22:54:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-austin-tx.27908/post-323196>

Hi everyone, I've been following Dr Kruse through his blogs. Currently, going through his blogs, and books again. Every time I read it again, I learn something new and it's very exciting. I'm having a fantastic time readings these out in Sun.

### fredrick347 — 2023-07-03T22:57:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-austin-tx.27908/post-323197>

Also Dr Kruse mention something about ceiling fan being bad? Is there more to this? Ceiling fans are quite common here in Texas.

### caroline — 2023-07-03T23:11:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-austin-tx.27908/post-323199>

Hi and welcome Fredrick.
Actually we are wondering about ceiling fans too ......I am pretty sure we would be better off not using one .....but we are in the tropics and I can't even imagine trying to sleep without one!
We try not using the A/C but it has been so humid........ so we usually succumb and turn it on eventually

### Anthony Smith — 2023-07-03T23:26:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-austin-tx.27908/post-323201>

#1 The fan is putting out a magnetic field possibly several feet. #2 If it is AC then you have the 60Hz (50Hz) AC electric field extending 6 - 8 feet so with a high ceiling you may be out of (or on the edge of) that field. The problem is what else that circuit is doing to the space and should it be on at all. Think about a DC operated fan, recharge during the day, let in run at night IF you have to have it.

### fredrick347 — 2023-07-03T23:38:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-austin-tx.27908/post-323203>

oh interesting! thanks [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) that does make sense!
[@caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) same here, it's quite hot even during nights which is the troublesome part

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-07-04T02:31:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-austin-tx.27908/post-323206>

It is so hard to sleep without a fan. Especially in summer

# Thread: Greetings from an Australian based out of Santiago Chile!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-an-australian-based-out-of-santiago-chile.27914/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ateale — 2023-07-05T23:48:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-an-australian-based-out-of-santiago-chile.27914/post-323294>

Greetings to you all from an Australian based out of Santiago Chile!

I was fortunate enough to get to hear Dr Kruse speak on Clubhouse from time to time in previous years as well as recently on both Rick Rubin's Tetragrammaton podcast and Max Gulhane's Regenerative Health podcast.
Although most of it flew over my head I believe I got enough of a gist of was Dr Kruse' was saying and it was enough for me to want to dig a little further and begin to experiment on myself. So here I am!

It's winter down here and has been raining a lot but i've been trying to catch that morning sun as much as I possible (It feels like the last time i really looked at the sun itself was when I was a kid and my mum told me not to do so!), taking a cold shower before i do so (quite a challenge in the winter here!), sticking to cooking/eating meat + fat and no carbs as much as possible, no seed oils (using coconut oil and olive oil), not much processed foods where possible. Also trying to cook with garlic a lot more than i have in the past as i heard that is good too.

I also purchased a pair of blublocker glasses to wear at night ([https://www.blublocker.com/collections/original-aviators/products/black-nylon-blublocker](https://www.blublocker.com/collections/original-aviators/products/black-nylon-blublocker)) but i believe i will need something better suited to using at inside haha. Any recommendations?

Down here in Chile I feel that I am seeing a lot more sick people than ever before, people almost addicted to getting their moderna vaccines (for flu or whatever the media tells them it is that they need to get), eating shitty food, obese, staring at phones/screens, wearing masks, defending mask mandates (including on our young children in schools) and all relying on the government to tell them what is healthy and what is not (amongst many other things) etc etc....
It's just not right.
Even the younger healthy looking gym goer folks I work with are always getting sick. It just doesn't make sense but at the same time after hearing Dr Kruse speak and reading some of the material on this site it is actually starting to make sense.
I believe that Dr Kruse has a lot of the answers to these issues after hearing him speak on these podcasts as well as some of the reading I have been able to do on this site.

Very happy to be here. Thank you!

Adam

### ateale — 2023-07-05T23:49:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-an-australian-based-out-of-santiago-chile.27914/post-323295>

I forgot to mention that i'm ditching the airpods too

Thanks so much Caroline, yes its a great place to be!! if anyone needs any advice for the Bahamas would be happy to help!

### caroline — 2023-07-06T20:37:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-nassau-greece.27921/post-323337>

> [stel said:](/goto/post?id=323335)
>
> Thanks so much Caroline, yes its a great place to be!! if anyone needs any advice for the Bahamas would be happy to help!

we always wanted to go there to live - but couldn't figure out how to make a living there!

We used to be great friends with a group called the King and knights and go to their club all the time.
Our whole family has such great memories.

In the old days it was a playground for the rich and famous. The Shah\[Peacock Throne\] and his family used to vacation there all the time. He spent some time in exile there on paradise Island. We vividly remember all the guards with military weapons closing off part of paradise island
.I have read so many books on King Edward the 7th - when he abdicated the throne and he and Wallis were sent to the Bahamas to be governor.
We used to go to a wonderful restaurant called the Buena Vista - I wonder if it is still there?
David and Wallace stayed there while they renovated government house to Wallace's liking.

### The.Naked.Doctor — 2023-08-23T06:29:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-nassau-greece.27921/post-325448>

> [stel said:](/goto/post?id=323333)
>
> Hello everyone , I'm Stel 40 years old from Greece, moved to the Bahamas about 10 years ago for work. I came across Dr Kruse through Ryan Carter's newsletter with his top recommended books. I read the epi-paleo book and started implementing some of the strategies and connecting the dots. I am still in the beginning of my journey but I have already seen many benefits by following epi-paleo rx. Very happy to be part of the community and grateful for Dr Kruse work. Looking forward to connecting with you.

Hi Stel! im a Bahamian living in New Zealand! Are you in Nassau?

### stel — 2023-08-23T19:50:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-nassau-greece.27921/post-325486>

> [The.Naked.Doctor said:](/goto/post?id=325448)
>
> Hi Stel! im a Bahamian living in New Zealand! Are you in Nassau?

Hey !! yes I have been in Nassau, Cable beach area for around 10 years now! WOW New Zealand- how is life there? definitely enjoying The Bahamas, but we make an effort to go to Greece at least once a year in the summer!

# Thread: Hi from NYC with chronic psoriasis... Asking for advices!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-nyc-with-chronic-psoriasis-asking-for-advices.27947/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 26
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sabosha — 2023-07-12T05:06:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-nyc-with-chronic-psoriasis-asking-for-advices.27947/post-323563>

Hi people! Im glad to be here among you, so I can communicate and learn better!
I'm from Ukraine and Russia (equal amount of time in both countries and both of them are native to me). I'm 39 now and live for almost 9 years in NYC. I'n dying here. I constantly don't have sustainable energy throughout the day, and sick with flu often. My psoriasis is constantly on, very rare do I see that skin would get not so inflamed. I tried to eat differently but it didn't help with anything (that's now I know what is the way of eating). I simply can not be physically active. I have psoriasis since I was 15. And I gained weight very rapidly since the pandemic started (4 years almost) and it's not going away.
Help me, please, with advice what should I do to see the result, this city doesn't help at all. I want to move and planning on it.

### JanSz — 2023-07-12T17:27:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-nyc-with-chronic-psoriasis-asking-for-advices.27947/post-323580>

[@sabosha](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26865/)
**Welcome sabosha**
I was born in Poland. 1940 (83 yo, plus some months).
In the place that now is Ukraine.
At the time Ukrainians used to carry Polish passports.
At the time, most of them were happy about it, few were not.
My best friend was Metrunio. Wonder what happens with him.
My best uncle was Lemko.
Poland is a conglomerate of many tribes. Ukrainians are few other of those tribes.
My family is intermarried with Ukrainians. I hate to say intermarried; we are the same people.
Three religions, Roman Catholic, Eastern Orthodox, Prawoslaw (not sure about spelling).
I am glad that Poland now is so active helping in current war.
But have a huge misgiving about the big picture part of the story.
Now I live in NJ USA 07054 since 1971
\=================================
In Poland I had big problems with my sinuses.
Glad that I avoided surgery.
When I immigrated to USA I have spent few years in Manhattan NYC.
To me the air there was great, my health problems went away.
Have a difficulty to understand complains about dirty air there.
You are 39yo.
Got psoriasis at 15. So, 24 years ago.
Now you have 9 years in NYC
So, you are with psoriasis 15 years in Ukraine or Russia and 9 years in NYC.
\----------------------------------
**I am not a doctor.**

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-204182](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-204182)

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-177276](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-177276)

Looking at my notes on psoriasis above
1---It looks like you have messed up Fatty Acids.
2---Others are trying to solve this problem with narrow band UVB
3---alpha-MSH may be lacking.

So, there are these **three ideas that come to my mind.**
The easiest to try are the bottom two.
\=================
First is this $20 light, dr Jack recommended.
**380**\-405nm UVA wavelength
he was underscoring the 380nm wavelength
Latter there was discussion about the 405nm wavelength
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

This people will do something similar just charge you more $$

### [Proven Eczema & Psoriasis Light Treatment | ZERIGO Health](https://www.clarifymed.com/)

The latest in handheld narrow band UVB light therapy

 www.clarifymed.com

\===========================================
**Second, alpha-MSH**
I use it for many years. Look at my picture.

When buying, make sure that it is
**MT-2**
There is also MT-1 and PT-141, but for me they were less helpful.

Make sure that you also have:
very small gage insulin syringes
bigger syringes for mixing
Bacteriostatic water
I mix like this:
10mg MT2
add 2cc water=10/200=0.05mg/unit=0.5mg/**10units**

**Make sure** that you very first injection is 2 units only
see how it make you feel.
Mostly you worry about sexual effects.
I got extreme boner that I had to dispose of immediately (wife was not around).
"Unfortunately", following injections acted only on my skin.
You (girl) may act differently.
Not sure how to figure frequency of injections.
I use it once every week until I get sufficient tan. Latter less often.

\======================================
messed up Fatty Acids

Starting it easy,
Be aware that
pigs, cows, chicken, farmed fish
anything, any animal that is not wild but man raised and that we eat
is fed with humongous amounts of seed oils.
The bad part of oils it is **Linoleic Acid (omega-6)**
The rest is also bad.
Additionally, it tastes delicious, so it is easy for farmers to use.
The "good" part is that it makes you grow instantly
son is taller than his father
chicken from the egg to the slaughter in five weeks
piglet to bacon in five months

Animals, birds, fish that we eat do not live long enough to get sick.
Different with humans.

I do check my fatty acids yearly or twice yearly using this test:
Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

Unfortunately, it tests only even carbons.
You may try this at first.

Better analysis and professional advice on this topic you may get at
Kennedy Krieger Institute

### [Kennedy Krieger Institute](https://www.kennedykrieger.org/)

Kennedy Krieger Institute improves the lives of children and adults with special health challenges through interdisciplinary hospital services and outpatient, community, education and research programs.

 www.kennedykrieger.org

////////////////////////

///

### caroline — 2023-07-12T20:56:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-nyc-with-chronic-psoriasis-asking-for-advices.27947/post-323602>

Hi Sabosha and welcome ......

Please start a journal in the optimal journal section and give Dr. K some health history ....and your Mom's and gran's too.

New York City is devastating for you ....

What do you do for work?

I strongly suggest you become a gold member and then you can ask Jack questions live on the monthly pow wows.

But we all know what he will tell you.......you have low redox and you will never fix your problems in NYC.

You need to move somewhere south - closer to the equator - where you can get lots and lots of sun.

The July pow wow was great and you would see how all kinds of issues all stem from the same problem - low melanin.

Have you listened to the Dr. Huberman and Dr. Jack podcasts? they are exceptional.

All the podcasts are a great way to get info faster. Jack doesn't pull any punches on the podcasts or pow wows.

I have masters degree in social psychology. But I was working as a personal fashion buyer for several years. And I was disappointed in psychology a lot, there is a base of knowledge about our nervous system and how it operates.Whatever became of this psychology field is more main people arrogant, closed-minded, wounded and basically disabled.

You deserve a better life than that for yourself and your son.

### sabosha — 2023-07-12T22:15:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-nyc-with-chronic-psoriasis-asking-for-advices.27947/post-323611>

Thank you, Caroline!

Usually I go to sleep around 10, I'm falling asleep with my son. But lately it's difficult to put him to sleep earlier and I want to work as well a bit after he is going to sleep, so several days already 12 am. But if I go to sleep at 10, even like that I'm not waking up easy in the morning.
I do have a lot of exposure to the screen (phone, laptop), all work is mostly communicating digitally and I need to strictly adjust it.
I'm eating eggs, sweet potatoes, rice sometimes, not a lot fruits as they are not tasty here at all, cucumber, tomato, peppers (I need to try to eliminate them as well as they nightshades) sour cream, grass-fed ground beef/lamb, sometimes chicken. And I love salmon, but not often, it's so expensive if it's wild. I'm being good quality food, grass-fed, raw dairy, butter. But very often I'm confused what to eat at all.
I do not eat oysters regularly, only rare. I didn't like them, but sometime forcing myself.
I would eat lamb chops and good salad, sweet potato - I can eat it all the time.
Every single source that is natural and good quality – cost a fortune here!

I do have love with good real sourdough bread, that I'm trying to eliminate.

It is expensive to live here, it's true. And all money go to daily existence. It happens that I do not have any savings, that's why it's difficult to move. I was looking for different options but didn't find. I'm the one who makes money and I don't have any financial support from my sons father as of now.

### JanSz — 2023-07-12T23:33:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-nyc-with-chronic-psoriasis-asking-for-advices.27947/post-323613>

Please stay on just one thread, otherwise it will be hard to follow your story.

Please describe, show pictures of your psoriasis.
Where it is located.

Everyday sun must see maximum of your skin and eyes.
Directly, no sunscreens, no eyeglasses.
Especially the skin with psoriasis.
It is ok if it is cloudy day.

Low energy.
Likely you have low cortisol, likely also low DHEAs.
Must get tested.

\====================
NYC is expensive.
Some people these days are working remotely using computers or cellphones.
A girl I know, her sons still work for USA companies but doing it remotely.
One moved to Lisbon Portugal, the other to North Carolina.
Another guy works remotely for a bank in UK but stay in his house in NJ 30 miles west of NYC.

How do you live in NYC?
Do you live in multi-apartment multi-story building or single-family home?

..

Welcome! Traditional sourdough bread made with your own starter with native yeast and local, seasonal flour would not be high on my priority list for giving up if you love it, and only eat it along with some type of fat in the morning for 25-30 grams of carbohydrate. I'm at about 41 degrees North to the West of you and our neighborhood farmers just harvested their winter wheat. You should be able to find some of your own out there.

### sabosha — 2023-07-13T00:09:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-nyc-with-chronic-psoriasis-asking-for-advices.27947/post-323619>

I do not use any sunscreens, etc. I do not use any pharmaceuticals ever. And I did not go to treat my skin to conventional doctors. I went once to the dermatology doctor to do light therapy with specific laser machine and they said that I have to pay around 5K for the course and insurance of course is not covering it.

I have scalp psoriasis, also patches of it on my breast area, a bit on my brows, patch on my bellybutton and a patch on my back.

I started to rapidly gain the weight after in 2019 I was few month folowing plant-based fat free diet. Basically salads with no fat dressing, some root vegetables, and fruits. Fell under impression that it would help me. And at first it was helping. And I had good energy. But it's probably due to elimination of a lot of stuff and detoxification. Then I got tired of it and it couldn't be sustainable so I started to eat real food and very soon I was twice bigger then before.

We live on 22 floor.

I'm working remotely on my own projects, but as I'm transitioning from my previous occupation to new one it's not stable yet at all. I'm not working for any company.

Pictures are below

### sabosha — 2023-07-13T00:11:59+0300

>
> I am surprised that your son doesn't go to bed until 10pm. What time does he get up?
> What about TV and computer games etc?
> You must be spending a fortune on groceries if you are eating grass-fed etc.

It's still very light at 8, even 9. In winter time I was putting him to sleep at 8.
As he goes to sleep later of course he is waking up late. I just need to wake him up once very early and not let him sleep.

### JanSz — 2023-07-13T01:26:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-nyc-with-chronic-psoriasis-asking-for-advices.27947/post-323624>

> [sabosha said:](/goto/post?id=323619)
>
> I do not use any sunscreens, etc. I do not use any pharmaceuticals ever. And I did not go to treat my skin to conventional doctors. I went once to the dermatology doctor to do light therapy with specific laser machine and they said that I have to pay around 5K for the course and insurance of course is not covering it.
>
> I have scalp psoriasis, also patches of it on my breast area, a bit on my brows, patch on my bellybutton and a patch on my back.
>
> I started to rapidly gain the weight after in 2019 I was few month folowing plant-based fat free diet. Basically salads with no fat dressing, some root vegetables, and fruits. Fell under impression that it would help me. And at first it was helping. And I had good energy. But it's probably due to elimination of a lot of stuff and detoxification. Then I got tired of it and it couldn't be sustainable so I started to eat real food and very soon I was twice bigger then before.
>
> We live on 22 floor.

>
> Pictures are below

Living on 22 floor is not going to help.
Especially that you not only live there but also work from there.
You need long daylight exposure to all those places that bother you.

What was your life condition in Ukraine/Russia as far as exposure to a daylight?

Consider moving to NJ to some place 10 - 20 miles from Manhattan.
Place that you could spend many hours outdoors with all your skin, (breasts) exposed to a daylight. You need to have a place to lay down and let the sun see your breasts from the bottom.

Right away spend $20 and get that light I mentioned on post #3 on this thread.
You can keep that light for many hours around you.

Think also about getting Red Sperti light.

You can use this light right away **but start slowly.**
Eventually you want to get to a situation where you are close to the lamp and can withstand 16 minutes (one full timer turn) every day.
But that will take you a month or more.
Be very careful, but do not use any sunscreens or any skin covering.
But
if you would burn your skin (by mistake) do not worry about any cancers, it is only about unnecessary pain.
Take few days to heal and get back to using that light, just shorten the exposure time.

Using MT-2 injections would speed up the process.
On post #3 I wrote:
Bacteriostatic water
I mix like this:
10mg MT2
add 2cc water=10/200=0.05mg/unit=0.5mg/10units

MT-2 comes as a powder in the bottle, there is 10mg of that powder.
You add 2cc of bacteriostatic water to that bottle.
Syringes have a marks on them.
1cc=1cubic centimeter=1 milliliter=100units
When you work up to have full MT-2 injections they have a volume of 10 units.
That 10units contains 0.5mg of powder.
You can get 20 injections from one bottle.

The navel on the picture above
sits on too much fat,
We have to work on that.

//

### JanSz — 2023-07-14T03:57:21+0300

1.  Dematologists have recommended Sunlight as a natural home-treatment for **Psoriasis.** [https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33811728/](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33811728/)
2.  Studies used Heliotherapy in **Psoriasis** and Atopic Dermatitis.

### digital — 2023-11-21T21:22:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-nyc-with-chronic-psoriasis-asking-for-advices.27947/post-328512>

> [sabosha said:](/goto/post?id=323563)
>
> Hi people! Im glad to be here among you, so I can communicate and learn better!
> I'm from Ukraine and Russia (equal amount of time in both countries and both of them are native to me). I'm 39 now and live for almost 9 years in NYC. I'n dying here. I constantly don't have sustainable energy throughout the day, and sick with flu often. My psoriasis is constantly on, very rare do I see that skin would get not so inflamed. I tried to eat differently but it didn't help with anything (that's now I know what is the way of eating). I simply can not be physically active. I have psoriasis since I was 15. And I gained weight very rapidly since the pandemic started (4 years almost) and it's not going away.
> Help me, please, with advice what should I do to see the result, this city doesn't help at all. I want to move and planning on it.

Hi Sabosha,

I used to live in New York City and had psoriasis. I resolved it by staying in the Sun- UV light from the Sun remedies the issue. Do not bother using LED lights, just move or take multiple to Florida (or even more South: Central America.)

Once I and my family realized the importance of Sun and UV light intensity, we moved to Florida. You should do well here, IMO.

\--- Shri

### AlanaVint — 2023-11-24T15:41:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-nyc-with-chronic-psoriasis-asking-for-advices.27947/post-328609>

Simply to say hello as a fellow new yorker, brooklyn born, seemingly bred to conform but, we have to find a new light to exist among this false light....

Just as nothing can replace the sun, nothing can replace face to face human contact I am here if you ever would. like to grow friendship near by.

### Evee — 2023-12-02T15:59:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-nyc-with-chronic-psoriasis-asking-for-advices.27947/post-328849>

> [sabosha said:](/goto/post?id=323563)
>
> Hi people! Im glad to be here among you, so I can communicate and learn better!
> I'm from Ukraine and Russia (equal amount of time in both countries and both of them are native to me). I'm 39 now and live for almost 9 years in NYC. I'n dying here. I constantly don't have sustainable energy throughout the day, and sick with flu often. My psoriasis is constantly on, very rare do I see that skin would get not so inflamed. I tried to eat differently but it didn't help with anything (that's now I know what is the way of eating). I simply can not be physically active. I have psoriasis since I was 15. And I gained weight very rapidly since the pandemic started (4 years almost) and it's not going away.
> Help me, please, with advice what should I do to see the result, this city doesn't help at all. I want to move and planning on it.

I think leaving is the answer, just hold on as best you can until you can pull that off, but that should be the ultimate goal. I have been through this too, with autoimmune diagnosis and fatigue. Years before I found Jack Kruse, I would call myself a drained battery, and now I understand how that is not just a metaphorical, but actually quite true. I still haven’t figured out how to fully get away, I just spent six weeks in Mexico and I’m dreading heading back to New Jersey, but take whatever steps you can, I don’t have the big picture figured out yet either I just know I’m taking every step I can figure out in the meantime until I can fully change my life. I’ve stopped spending money on anything except travel away from the north east, while you’re stuck there, minimizing carbs, and trying to get on a sleep cycle with the natural light system, and seeing as much natural light as you can helps, but it’s not enough. I feel like a healthy person in Mexico and a sick person in New Jersey and that is the bottom line so for me the answer is leaving New Jersey. Unfortunately I can’t economically do it tomorrow, but every decision I make in life from here on out drives towards that goal. Maybe if you can take a trip for whatever you can manage down to the tropics, and prove it to yourself that changing your environment can make you feel better

### Evee — 2023-12-02T16:10:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-nyc-with-chronic-psoriasis-asking-for-advices.27947/post-328851>

> [sabosha said:](/goto/post?id=323607)
>
> Oh yes, I learned already a lot from Dr. K! And I watch d the podcast, I am not eating seed oils. I understand the whole protocol but again I can no grab myself at 5.30 in the morning to be able to get up. There is simply no energy in me.
> The same with physical exercise.
> I have masters degree in social psychology. But I was working as a personal fashion buyer for several years. And I was disappointed in psychology a lot, there is a base of knowledge about our nervous system and how it operates.Whatever became of this psychology field is more main people arrogant, closed-minded, wounded and basically disabled.
> Now I want to go back to psychology to interlace it with quantum biology, with physics of vibration, sound, light, electromagnetism.
> I became stagnant in NYC, energetically and physically.

I totally understand, I went from New York to New Jersey, and I can still sort of feel OK in New Jersey in the summer months, but winter is a killer for me. I don’t have psoriasis, but I do have an ankylosing spondylitis diagnosis which is probably a cousin to it. Doctors will claim it’s genetic and I do have the HLAb27 marker, but I swear that’s nothing more than a predisposition to inflammation not that you necessarily have to have it, because I can completely make my inflammation go away with enough sunlight and sleep.
Like at my worst when my vitamin D was low, and I was not sleeping and working crazy hours I would get uveitis and joint pain so bad that I was taking Advil every four hours (when I was 33 and got diagnosed.) Now 41, after trying everything that I read online for years, I can say that that if I’m getting sleep and sun, I’m completely fine, I have not so much as thought of an Advil, the entire time I’ve been in Mexico. I also rarely take them even when I need them in New Jersey, because of side effects, I usually just suffer, but I can’t tell you how amazing it is to feel like a different person here. I am seriously dreading returning home on Tuesday. And I don’t mean to be flippant, I know life, circumstances and economics, makes us do things that we don’t want to. I am in the same boat, I can just say that all of my energy is going into figuring out how to change my boat, so to speak.

### NikkiYork — 2023-07-19T04:25:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikki-here-aloha-and-hello-to-all.27983/post-323941>

Hi everybody,
I'm so glad I made it here, so I don't have to hang out on social media to bug Dr. Kruse.
I'm 51, live with my amazing team (that's my family, David and James (9)), on the beach north of Santa Monica, and I am so glad that a coworker of my husband sent us the Huberman podcast.
This might just really be it, and Dr. Jack is a genius. I have so many things that I hope I can deduct from what I am learning...Yay!
I was sick in my life multiple times, but I can't let it define me, so I am hoping to eradicate the majority of it now and the underlying causes. I'm on it. I'm looking forward to meeting a bunch of cool people in this forum. Thank you, for engaging here, I love it!

### ChristineH — 2023-07-21T00:40:39+0300

# Thread: Hello from UAE
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-uae.28002/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tourqstrong — 2023-07-23T22:42:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-uae.28002/post-324199>

Hello everyone, glad to be part of this beautiful community.

My name is Temour, i've been following Dr. Jack for over 5 years now, i live in the UAE, i've been building sun callus for over 4 years in the scorching heat of Dubai, rotating from a carnivore to a paleo-ish diet and feeling great so far!

A lot of information i see from Dr Jack that might not apply to where i live, so i am curious to learn more.

Some of the things that i would love to learn more about:

\- We have a ton of sunlight, but no local fruits/vegetables, and nothing grows here locally naturally other than dates..
\- I am thinking about moving to Canada, but also wondering it that's to the better or to the worse when it comes to longevity and sunlight exposure.

cheers~

### magnetic — 2023-08-09T21:46:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-uae.28002/post-324891>

British Columbia has fairly mild winters, gets very hot in the central part of the province and lots of sun especially around Kamloops. Rest of the country is very cold and low sunlight in winter but fairly hot and humid in summer. Nothing like UAE of course. Southern Ontario might be ok. Toronto/Gta is a 5g nightmare however.

Hope that helps

### coho3131 — 2023-08-10T01:03:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-uae.28002/post-324895>

> [tourqstrong said:](/goto/post?id=324199)
>
> Hello everyone, glad to be part of this beautiful community.
>
> My name is Temour, i've been following Dr. Jack for over 5 years now, i live in the UAE, i've been building sun callus for over 4 years in the scorching heat of Dubai, rotating from a carnivore to a paleo-ish diet and feeling great so far!
>
> A lot of information i see from Dr Jack that might not apply to where i live, so i am curious to learn more.
>
> Some of the things that i would love to learn more about:
>
> \- We have a ton of sunlight, but no local fruits/vegetables, and nothing grows here locally naturally other than dates..
> \- I am thinking about moving to Canada, but also wondering it that's to the better or to the worse when it comes to longevity and sunlight exposure.
>
> cheers~

Welcome.
I wonder if you would find some plants to eat by reading about native plants that are edible.
Also, are there any local sea vegetables?

Students are often performers, such as actors, dancers, musicians, athletes and public speakers, people who work on computers, or those who are in frequent pain for other reasons. Instructors observe their students, and provide both verbal and gentle manual guidance to help students learn how to move with better poise and less strain.
\===========================
chronic fatigue, fibromyalgia, brain fog

If you are still poppoing DUTCH test
get quick:

Portuguese dehydrated cod is not a jerky. It's heavily salted for preservation and can sit in cardboard at room temp. It has to soaked in water and the water pitched a few times. Cod has an amazing ability to reconstitute to a fleshy texture.

I'm Portuguese decent and eat alot of cod. Where I live there's market that has a big room of dehydrated cod from around the world

I bought frozen bacalao in Panama and then rehydrated it over a few days in the fridge with several water changes,. I probably needed to do a little bit more because it was still pretty salty when I eventually fried it. But it was delicious.. I havent ever felt so happy when eating cod like that before. And it was only 5-6 dollars for a reasonable sized piece. [@DLO](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19314/) I bought it in the Rey at Coronado.

# Thread: Hello from DC
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-dc.28104/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### RitaC — 2023-08-23T02:44:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-dc.28104/post-325429>

Hello everyone,

I am truly thrilled to join this community. I have Dr. Kruse’s “Epi-Paleo Rx” book and have avidly listened to many of his recent and older podcasts. In fact, I’ve gone through the Rubin-Kruse-Huberman podcasts three times each! My learning journey has been transformative, and I’m eager to delve into the workshops and Q&A sessions.

While I plan to read the blogs, I must admit that reading on the screen strains my eyes. I’m on a mission to correct my myopia and, by following Uncle Jack’s advice, have already reduced it by 2 diopters. My objective is to continue my learning here, apply the recommendations personally, and share the insights with my clients.

Today, I came across a picture of the new clinic in El Salvador on Facebook. The sight of it made me instantly register as a member. I am not only keen on visiting but am also genuinely interested in contributing to the new project on medical decentralization. Being fluent in Spanish might come in handy!

Thank you for welcoming me into this community.

Rita

# Thread: Newly Graduated Interventional Spine and Rehabilitation Physician Seeking decentralized training and network opportunities
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newly-graduated-interventional-spine-and-rehabilitation-physician-seeking-decentralized-training-and-network-opportunities.28109/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 34
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### dr.g — 2023-08-23T20:45:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newly-graduated-interventional-spine-and-rehabilitation-physician-seeking-decentralized-training-and-network-opportunities.28109/post-325489>

Looking for other physicians/fellow explorers interested in the decentralization process specifically as it pertains to health, but also more broadly. I am a newly graduated interventional spine and rehabilitation physician. Starting off private practice, hope to never step foot in a hospital again. Want to create a web of healers that I can connect to help my patients. Washington has low light levels through winter, horrendous metal health challenges, and the typical metabolic syndrome issues. My world is minimally invasive spine and musculoskeletal medicine. hope to connect with others who want to collaborate and form decentralized healing networks. Cheers!

### Kathirf — 2023-08-29T15:35:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newly-graduated-interventional-spine-and-rehabilitation-physician-seeking-decentralized-training-and-network-opportunities.28109/post-325693>

Hi Gavin, my son is a PM&R doc currently doing his fellowship at Barrow Brain and Spine in Phoenix. He is actively looking to relocate and start his own practice as a decentralized physician. His wife is a physician as well, they are both on the same page. Would you be willing to share more? I would love to put you in touch.

### dr.g — 2023-08-29T16:53:28+0300

Do you know if your house/apt has wiring errors and net currents? If so you'll need a certified EMRS consultant, none located in Louisiana but a few in Texas and one in Georgia:

### [Electromagnetic Radiation Specialists™ - Building Biology Institute](https://buildingbiologyinstitute.org/find-an-expert/certified-consultants/electromagnetic-radiation-specialists/)

Electromagnetic Radiation Specialists complete a rigorous training regimen that includes hands-on training in advanced electromagnetic radiation and natural healthy building subjects but is not BBI-certified to address indoor air quality issues unless IAQ-certified by an authorized third party...

 buildingbiologyinstitute.org

Shielding for RF if you want to achieve 'no harm' levels is very time, labor, and money intensive something I have more experience doing than I ever wanted but if you live close to a macro cell tower moving would be the best solution as you'd be getting blasted even if you were to shield when you step outside your home. Same goes for high voltage power lines if close proximity for E and M fields.

I just realized the four-way and two-way lights switches I have for my foyer and dining lights are run with just two conductor wires no third traveler. I'm not going to have the walls ripped open to fix it I'm just keeping those breakers off and using sockets for lamps which are luckily on separate balanced circuits. Sad that most homes in the country have wiring errors and people are getting blasted hard completely clueless since their circuits still function.

Everyone of this forum should own the Safe and Sound Pro II at the very minimum. RF levels have been seriously cranked up in many places since the FEMA test this month.

I've owned Anthony's remote kill switch for years and consider it essential.

A doctor of osteopathy (DO) is a fully trained and licensed physician.
They employ holistic practices for preventative health and healing known as **osteopathic medicine.**
DOs aim to improve people’s overall health and wellness by treating the whole person.
This includes osteopathic manipulative medicine,
which involves stretching, massaging, and moving the musculoskeletal system.

### JanSz — 2023-10-27T16:54:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newly-graduated-interventional-spine-and-rehabilitation-physician-seeking-decentralized-training-and-network-opportunities.28109/post-327584>

Our bodies are made of bones and joints.
They are expecting certain loads.
When we are at rest, we have certain (stationary) weight.
When we move the load on bones and joints is dynamic. **impact force**
Just walking exerts dynamic load on our joints.
One hundred pounds of stationary weight becomes 600 pounds of dynamic.
Just when walking, more when running.
So, if person whose bodies expects to carry 150# actually has 200 or 300 pounds
multiply that six time (make sure that you do not lose your hairs seeing result).

Actually, there is a better way to visualize this situation.
Run into rigid wall and hit it with your body. (Do that only in imaginary way, not real).
But our joints see all that in their daily life all the time when we are moving.
..
**What is good weight and what is not.**

### dr.g — 2023-11-09T18:50:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newly-graduated-interventional-spine-and-rehabilitation-physician-seeking-decentralized-training-and-network-opportunities.28109/post-328083>

> [Dr Rohen Kapur said:](/goto/post?id=327575)
>
> Retired ex psychiatrist here with lumbar disc disease. Got ideas.
>
> Get in touch ( [rohens@gmail.com](mailto:rohens@gmail.com))
>
> Full title Dr Rohen Kapur graduated 1994 from Dundee University

The pathophysiology of Lumbar DDD is still debated and its exact cause is not known. The disc has no blood supply and it traditionally has been taught that it receives its nutrients via passive diffusion from the endplates of the vertebral bodies. There are mitochondria within the disc. Overall, since the loss of water within the proteoglycan network is what leads to disc dehydration, desiccation and ultimately height loss and structural loss of integrity, maintaining hydration within the disc seems a reasonable place to start. Since the mitochondria make water, I have a feeling that loss of functional mitochondria leads to loss of hydration within the disc. As far as treatments are concerned, IR light can penetrate to the disc and there are some places that can offer light treatments for the spine. Unfortunately where I practice, this is mainly done by chiropractors, and I not sure of the efficacy or the dose. Studies are limited. Don't think it would hurt however.

From an interventional perspective, there are disc treatments ranging from platelet rich plasma injections, bone or fat derived stem cell concentrate injections (BMAC), and allograft nucleus pulpous injections (company Via disc) that can be done. The evidence is mixed. However, there is recent data showing the mesenchymal stem cells (MSCs) can release exosomes, and also mitochondria where they are placed, which mechanistically would lead me to believe that bone marrow derived stem cell injections into the disc would help increase energy to the disc at least temporarily. It is unknown how long the stem cells stay within the disc and how long they are active for.

There are also treatments of methylene blue into the disc which have shown good results in asian studies, but this is not mainstream or conventional treatment. Would work to increase metabolism at the mitochondrial level.

Viadisc is a new product in phase 3 trials which have good results and rehydrate the discs.

There are also non regenerative treatments for disc/endplate disease call intercept which a basivertebral nerve ablation, which effectively eliminates the pain signal from the nerve that innervates the vertebral endplates, but functionally will cause osteoporosis at that level given enough time. Treatment is palliative in nature and done for intractable pain.

Happy to answer any other questions. Lumbar DDD is a massive problem for humans and the the research on how it actually functions is just getting started. A decentralized view is required. There is also something going on in the endplates either from energy failure at the bone and or from lack of proper blood supply. Could be light and or inflammation related. There are weird studies showing certain antibiotics also can delay or reverse DDD in some cases.

Still looking for a unified theory of disc disease from a decentralized perspective if anyone else wants to chime in.

### dr.g — 2023-11-09T19:12:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newly-graduated-interventional-spine-and-rehabilitation-physician-seeking-decentralized-training-and-network-opportunities.28109/post-328089>

> [NikkiYork said:](/goto/post?id=326734)
>
> What’s your “specialty” or field of interest, or alternative approach, however you want to put it?

Primary Specialty is Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation. Fellowship training is in Interventional Spine.

My personal field of interest is "regenerative medicine" or orthobiologics as it pertains to the spine mainly, but also to the peripheral joints, shoulder, knees, tendons, bones etc. Concept of taking blood or bone marrow and concentrating the cells to reinject into places that are low energy diseased tissue to help regeneration. Becker's work on regeneration is some of the bedrock for these treatments. However I have not been able to find much data on treating bone marrow derived mesenchymal stem cells with weak electric currents pre injection to help their regenerative potential which I think needs to be explored. Working to build a regenerative practice is my long term goal. Of course these treatment's are not covered by insurance and most people cannot afford cash so it's a business model that is tenuous to build in a centralized framework.

I also have an interest in neuromodulation, peripherally and centrally. Providing electrical energy to the cells to help with neuropathic conditions has a lot of potential and the field is exploding.

My approach now is to learn how to view the diseases I have been trained to treat from a decentralized view so that I can offer full patient centered approaches that are whole health. I want to get a light bed that is programmable so that my patients also have access to alternative therapies. In Seattle there is very low light in the winter and TONS of mood and metabolic disruption as a result. As a specialist, I generally focus on the issue at hand (mainly back pain) but the actual person's overall health can get lost in the weeds. Primary Care does a horrible job actually treating underlying chronic co-morbidities.

If you are anyone you know has a decentralized hypothesis for chronic musculoskeletal conditions and degenerative spine conditions, I'd love to connect.

Of course he should address leptin sensitivity, eat epi paleo, mitochondriac it up as much as possible. That said, I would highly emphasize the need for good quality sleep, emphasizing the need for deep sleep to maximize glymphatic repair and waste product clearance from the brain. Of course, proper light hygiene, sun exposure, etc can be introduced into the conversation under that rubric.

I have often found that mechanistic explanations of exactly why lifestyle behaviors affect the very anatomy and physiology of the brain are actually useful for inspiring behavioral change with male patients.

Of course, others in this forum more experienced than I may be able to suggest other things to address potential underlying causes of why his vascular wall became weak. I suspect this has much to do with overall vascular elasticity, nitric oxide, etc.

### JanSz — 2023-11-12T22:49:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newly-graduated-interventional-spine-and-rehabilitation-physician-seeking-decentralized-training-and-network-opportunities.28109/post-328205>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=328137)
>
> [@dr.g](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/29574/)
> @[JeffRouseMD](https://forum.jackkruse.com/goto/post?id=327314)
> I am not a doctor.
> Two days ago, happy positive day, happy stress going to buy new puppy, someone in my neighborhood, 50yo, while his kid was driving, he is sitting in passenger seat, developed what turns out to be a brain aneurism.
> Rather immediately ended up in the hospital.
> Whatever they did there, he was transferred to nearby specialty hospital, there they opened his cranium and did some preliminary work. Some hours later real surgery happened.
> Doctor was able to accomplish whatever he was planning to accomplish.
> Next day, that is yesterday (11/10/2023 say 8AM EST) they woke him up.
> Yesterday and continuing today he is doing fine.
> Hopefully it will be good outcome, eventually.
>
> **I am looking for any pointers how he should be dealt with, after he gets home?**
>
> When it would be a good time to visit him in the hospital?
> ///////////

**Do you always need a shunt after aneurysm surgery?**
No, a shunt is not always necessary after successful aneurysm surgery.
[The need for a shunt is typically determined by the presence and severity of hydrocephalus, a condition where there is an accumulation of cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) in the brai](https://www.hydroassoc.org/predicting-the-need-for-a-shunt-after-a-brain-bleed/)n.

[After an aneurysm surgery, patients often develop hydrocephalus and require placement of an external ventricular drain (EVD) to temporarily drain CSF and decrease intracranial pressure](https://www.hydroassoc.org/predicting-the-need-for-a-shunt-after-a-brain-bleed/)[1](https://www.hydroassoc.org/predicting-the-need-for-a-shunt-after-a-brain-bleed/). [Once the period of risk for vasospasm (narrowing of blood vessels leading to reduced blood flow to the brain) has ended, the EVD is clamped to stop external drainage of CSF](https://www.hydroassoc.org/predicting-the-need-for-a-shunt-after-a-brain-bleed/).

[Doctors then determine if the hydrocephalus has improved and if the patient can tolerate removal of the drain](https://www.hydroassoc.org/predicting-the-need-for-a-shunt-after-a-brain-bleed/)[1](https://www.hydroassoc.org/predicting-the-need-for-a-shunt-after-a-brain-bleed/). [If the hydrocephalus is not improved or gets worse after the EVD has been clamped, the patient will require placement of a permanent ventriculoperitoneal (VP) shunt](https://www.hydroassoc.org/predicting-the-need-for-a-shunt-after-a-brain-bleed/).

[In some cases, a patient passes the clamp trial, the EVD is removed, and no VP shunt is placed, but later, the patient suffers from delayed cognitive and motor recovery](https://www.hydroassoc.org/predicting-the-need-for-a-shunt-after-a-brain-bleed/). [If the doctors believe that hydrocephalus is causing the delay in recovery, they may decide that a VP shunt is required](https://www.hydroassoc.org/predicting-the-need-for-a-shunt-after-a-brain-bleed/).

[However, it’s important to note that not all patients who undergo aneurysm surgery will develop hydrocephalus or require a shunt](https://www.hydroassoc.org/predicting-the-need-for-a-shunt-after-a-brain-bleed/).
[The decision to place a shunt is made on a case-by-case basis, taking into account the patient’s individual circumstances and the judgment of the medical team](https://www.hydroassoc.org/predicting-the-need-for-a-shunt-after-a-brain-bleed/).

\======================================

**Life with a cerebrospinal fluid (csf) shunt after aneurism surgery**
[After an aneurysm surgery, a cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) shunt may be placed to divert CSF from the brain, usually to the abdominal cavity](https://www.bmj.com/content/355/bmj.i5209).
[The shunt should place no or few restrictions on driving, travel, sexual intercourse, sports, and pregnancy once stable after the procedure](https://www.bmj.com/content/355/bmj.i5209). However, there are some concerns that patients may have about their shunt.
[For example, UK guidance states that patients must not drive for six months after an operation involving the ventricular end of the shunt](https://www.bmj.com/content/355/bmj.i5209).

\========================================

**The timeline for determining the need for a shunt** after aneurysm surgery can vary based on individual circumstances. However, here is a general timeline:

-   [**Immediately after surgery**: Patients often develop hydrocephalus and require placement of an external ventricular drain (EVD) to temporarily drain cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) and decrease intracranial pressure](https://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/brain-aneurysm/diagnosis-treatment/drc-20361595).
-   [**After the period of risk for vasospasm has ended**: The EVD is clamped to stop external drainage of CSF](https://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/brain-aneurysm/diagnosis-treatment/drc-20361595). [This is called a clamp trial](https://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/brain-aneurysm/diagnosis-treatment/drc-20361595).
-   [**During the clamp trial**: Doctors determine if the hydrocephalus has improved and if the patient can tolerate removal of the drain](https://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/brain-aneurysm/diagnosis-treatment/drc-20361595).
-   [**If hydrocephalus is not improved or gets worse after the EVD has been clamped**: The patient will require placement of a permanent ventriculoperitoneal (VP) shunt](https://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/brain-aneurysm/diagnosis-treatment/drc-20361595).
-   **If a patient passes the clamp trial**: The EVD is removed, and no VP shunt is placed. [However, if the patient suffers from delayed cognitive and motor recovery, doctors may decide that a VP shunt is required](https://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/brain-aneurysm/diagnosis-treatment/drc-20361595).

It’s important to note that these timelines can vary based on the individual patient’s condition and recovery.
[Regular follow-ups with the surgeon’s office are recommended, **typically within 2 weeks** of being discharged from the hospital](https://lisafoundation.org/what-to-expect/).
[If a CSF shunt was placed after aneurysm surgery, **regular follow-ups** are needed to ensure it functions well](https://lisafoundation.org/what-to-expect/).

////////////

### markthesunbather — 2023-10-19T17:06:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-st-augustine.28263/post-327418>

Long time lurker here. Ten years ago -around the time I turned 25- I had some pretty scary health issues and somehow, someway, I discovered Jack and his work. Even ordered his book on Amazon back then, though I have no idea who turned me onto it (not sure anyone even did; it's like the universe offered it up to me or something). Been listening to every podcast he's done that I can find; gotten to where I'm also searching his name on YouTube regularly for new offerings. Stuff gets more interesting with each passing year. Also trying to go back and listen to the Q&As/powows from yesteryear whenever opportunity presents. I find the information endlessly fascinating, even though I'm nowhere near as sharp as I was half my life ago and so much of it seems beyond me. Much love to you all.

# Thread: Just FYI for Travel to Playa del Carmen, regarding EMF
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-fyi-for-travel-to-playa-del-carmen-regarding-emf.28278/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 47
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Evee — 2023-10-26T23:24:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-fyi-for-travel-to-playa-del-carmen-regarding-emf.28278/post-327564>

Hi all, I'm staying in Playa del Carmen for awhile and have my Trifield. Just FYI, magnetic fields, I'm assuming from how various electrical wires are grounded, seem to have wild variations here. Walking around I can get low readings in some spots and then some of the highest I've ever seen in others. If I was to do this again, I might book a place for a week or few days, and then check out where I was trying to stay longer in person. I've walked under hot spots that send the meter into the 40s and above  Where I'm staying seems to average .7, so not great, but not horrid and trying to focus on the positive that I'm not at one of those hotspots. (For context bed at home averages .2) Beach is of course zeros so headed back out there soon. Just figured I post this info though in case it's helpful for anyone else.

(For context bed at home averages .2) Beach is of course zeros so headed back out there soon. Just figured I post this info though in case it's helpful for anyone else.

I guess it shouldn't be surprising that there are big variations. It's a great idea to rent short term or be able to go in and test a place ahead of time.
I'm on the coast in Colombia, not as dense as PDC but I didn't bring my meter, I'm moving outside the city on Sunday so it will be better.
What are you finding for prices on short term rentals there?

### Ronald Waters — 2023-10-28T03:21:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-fyi-for-travel-to-playa-del-carmen-regarding-emf.28278/post-327590>

I'm renting a house next to the beach in a gated community 3 bedroom 2 bath 65 a night. I was surprised to see the prices in PDC are rather reasonable. How are you finding the environment and beach? Food and cost?
Transportation? Can you see yourself staying there for 3 months?

### Evee — 2023-10-29T04:02:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-fyi-for-travel-to-playa-del-carmen-regarding-emf.28278/post-327620>

> [Ronald Waters said:](/goto/post?id=327590)
>
> I'm renting a house next to the beach in a gated community 3 bedroom 2 bath 65 a night. I was surprised to see the prices in PDC are rather reasonable. How are you finding the environment and beach? Food and cost?
> Transportation? Can you see yourself staying there for 3 months?

I've only been here 4 days but so far my impression are: it's very touristy, weather is amazing, people are nice. The Centro beach is very resort/club vibes with narrow public access, but if you walk up to like 77 (I think or ish about) there's a beautiful wild beach. I've only taken 1 cab so far but it was reasonable. Food is also reasonable, even eating in touristy places it's still coming in a bit less than eating out in the north eastern United States. There's a Walmart if you want really cheap groceries etc.. I'm down here for 7 weeks but given this MG thing, I don't think I'd stay longer. Walked a couple blocks with meter at 33 today. (Over by the highway & Ave Constituyentes) Unfortunately I have to work as a means of being down here so I'm stuck inside part of the day and it's bumming me out that I'm sitting in levels worse than what I left. Other than that though, it's great and I'd have no complaints. And for context, I'd say I'm pretty easy going about most stuff. I'm living out of a carry on and backpack for 7 weeks BUT I get a little fanatical about health stuff so that's why I'm hung up on the MG issue
Thoughts on Columbia?

> [Ronald Waters said:](/goto/post?id=327863)
>
> What will w causing these fields to be present on the beach? Are you referring to cell signal? Military communication? Marine activities? If I'm in the water isn't that creating a faraday cage? What's the best meter to measure these RF signals?

Water's only a faraday if you're scuba diving. If you're swimming or bathing in the ocean your head's above water and the micros are going straight through your head into rest of body. Anyone who's built a faraday room on land knows any little gap in continuity lets in a huge amount of micros and can never achieve safe levels unless the background levels weren't over a 100 micros in the first place.

I do CT in far Northern California mostly deserted beach where the closest cell tower's over a mile away and the dunes separate the beach a good distance from highway 101 and on some parts of the beach I'll measure up to 500 micros.

I just posted the best meters are Gigahertz Solution and SLT's meter. Trifield, Cornet, Acoustimeter, and all the cheap ones are worthless in giving actual readings. When SLT's meter first came on the market I was shocked to see it reading up to 15 times what my Trifield was showing.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-11-06T22:27:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-fyi-for-travel-to-playa-del-carmen-regarding-emf.28278/post-327954>

Fiber optic cables transport LIGHT not electricity . . . That said - when they lay them in the ground they put a bunch of other things/wires in that do carry electricity . . . And THAT’s the problem, not the actual fiber optics. Mute point I guess! But the Fiber Optic infrastructure is something else: This from Jeromy Jonson - [https://www.emfanalysis.com/fiber-optics-increasing-electrical-sensitivity/](https://www.emfanalysis.com/fiber-optics-increasing-electrical-sensitivity/)

There can be a significant problem with the high-speed fiber optic converters out at the street (or in the home with some newer fiber systems) that create these electrical data signals. This is because the converters are not designed with low-EMI emissions in mind. Thus, their power supplies and operation can generate high amounts of wide-spectrum EMI (electromagnetic interference). This inadvertent EMI then piggybacks on the copper cable and phone lines into our homes where it can radiate from every copper wire. This EMI from the fiber optic infrastructure is a primary reason why electrical sensitivity is increasing when high-speed internet is installed in our communities.

### coho3131 — 2023-11-13T05:06:34+0200

### Saichi — 2023-11-14T20:23:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-fyi-for-travel-to-playa-del-carmen-regarding-emf.28278/post-328278>

> [coho3131 said:](/goto/post?id=328216)
>
> What is "SLT's meter" called?
> Is it Safe and Sound Pro II RF meter?

Yes, it's essential for everyone here. The most recent version measures up to 3.18 million micros, the GS meter with a 20 dB attenuator only measures up to 2 million. I was going to purchase the GS meter but GS doesn't offer a 30 dB attenuator to measure up to 20 million. Of course once you max out at 2 million you should leave that area immediately, but since I still spend time at ground zero in NYC I'd like to be able to measure up to 10 or 20 million so I know what the actual peak levels are for apts in close proximity to macro cells.

I no longer fly through LAX after their renovations since some of their gates maxes out the SLT meter.

If for some reason you need a directional antenna to locate an RF source then you'd want to get the GS meter along with the SLT.

# Thread: Hi From Flagstaff
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-flagstaff.28284/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Emily Burgoon D.O. — 2023-10-31T00:00:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-flagstaff.28284/post-327694>

Hello, I'm happy to be here finally. Been listening and reading for a few months now and putting the light changes and Wim Hof breathing into practice, seeing significant changes in energy levels that took a solid 2 months to kick in.
The thing that has me so excited about all this is that it's taking me back to what I have always loved: nature. I have always had an alternative, outsider's view of the centralized system, and as a teen and college student I read constantly, eventually finding Tom Brown Jr's books. This lead me to his Tracker School, where in the 2000s I spent countless weeks/months taking classes, volunteering, interning etc.. in primitive wilderness survival skills, tracking, nature observation and spirituality/awareness training. Foundational to the nature observation, spiritual awareness and tracking I learned there was always two things that are central here as well: 1. morning "sit spot" out in nature to watch the sunrise, prayer and thanksgiving, set intentions for the day, nature awareness in wide angle vision as a doorway to spirit and 2. getting out in the elements away from the insulated comfort zone of society, including entering cold water as a practice.
I've always known how hopelessly corrupt medicine is, and this is partly what lead me to osteopathy as a specialty. It was the only place I felt I could train and maintain my integrity and conscience. Since Tracker School I've been through osteopathic medical school, residency, starting my own practice in osteopathy, health crisis with lyme, autoimmunity and trauma, raising my daughter in spite of her father etc.. In the insanity of survival I lost track of spending time in nature, started wearing sunglasses and keeping my corrective lenses on out on the trail... Now I'm learning the science of why I need to get out again with the glasses off for my health and survival, and am so glad to be integrating this into all the other skills I've pursued in alternative medicine over the past 10 years.
My question is for other practitioners here - tips on integrating it into your practice, creating educational guidelines for patients etc..

### JanSz — 2023-10-31T00:50:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-flagstaff.28284/post-327698>

I love Chiropractors and Doctors of Osteopathy.
Greatly benefited, past and present, from those expertise's.
They saved me for numerous unnecessary surgeries.
Please help us here.
............................

### calebthomas — 2023-10-31T01:11:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-flagstaff.28284/post-327700>

welcome! lots of great folks here continually engaging.

### Emily Burgoon D.O. — 2023-10-31T01:25:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-flagstaff.28284/post-327701>

> I love Chiropractors and Doctors of Osteopathy.
> Greatly benefited, past and present, from those expertise's.
> They saved me for numerous unnecessary surgeries.
> Please help us here.

Glad to hear. I love osteopathy as well. I feel like I'm part of a beautiful and sacred lineage.
One of my favorite quotes from A.T. Still:
“An Osteopath is taught that Nature is to be trusted to the end.”
also:
“To find health should be the object of the doctor. Anyone can find disease.”
In biodynamic cranial osteopathy we make a lifelong study of interfacing therapeutically, perceptually with all the subtle rhythms and rates and forces in the human organism, and its relationship to the environment. In service to the patient and as students of Health or Source. I wonder sometimes how much of that will someday be explained by biophysics.
I love looking at the old pictures of Still - always studying his books out in the yard, outdoor cadaver dissection etc.. They lived a very different life from our modern lives.

Speaking of turmeric onions garlic etc
and thinking of inflammation:

For the last about 150 years, farmers are feeding whatever living creatures they are feeding
them with remnants of seeds from which seed oil was taken out.
That goes for the conventional food and also for organic. That goes for venison that supposed to be meat from wild animals, but those animals are also fed by farmers.
Many know to not eat seed oils, most do not know that farmed animals, birds, fish, pigs etc. are fed with seed.
So, meats from them have very high content of LA, Linoleic Acid.
That causes inflammation in our bodies.

Not sure if knowing above or by coincidence, supercentenarians eat much less meat.
Which may among any other considerations
lessen the extremely high load of Linoleic Acid (Omega-6)
